*Actor references created using this function are *not* removed upon cell reset unless killed and in that case will be fully removed after 3 days and not bloat saves (Unless flagged as a quest item on the actor baseform). *Projectile references created using this function are *not* removed upon cell reset. *Misc. items, weapons, armor, and possibly other playable items created using this function are also unloaded/destroyed if picked up. *Explosion references created using this function are unloaded after their animation plays once.
==Notes== *Any variables declared in an object or actor script which has been created with PlaceAtMe is non persistent, and any variables in that object or actor script will be reset to 0 any time the object is reloaded or on game load. *Trying to run script commands on objects created in the same frame may not work, since it takes some time to place the object in the world. It is advised to wait a few frames or use a timed pause before attempting to manipulate these objects through scripts. *The placed object gets the same XYZ angle values as the calling object. Note that world objects placed at the player will be tilted if the player is looking up or down (player X angle not zero ... object X angle not zero). *This function can be used with leveled creature lists. *This function can NOT be used with leveled items. Leveled items are not supposed to be placed into the world - you can use them exclusively inside containers. Trying to create an item from a leveled list this way will just cause the yellow exclamation mark (Marker_Error.NIF) to appear. *The ''Distance'' and ''Direction'' parameters do not work. No matter what is specified for them, the created reference will be placed in safe location nearby the calling reference.
==Bugs== *Objects with destruction data placed with this command that have the "Disable Object" flag will often still be in the disabled state even if you load a save before the destruction occurred. *If used to spawn a dead body (An actor with 0 health) the actor will spawn alive, use [[Kill]] to prevent this.
==See Also== *[[GetAngle]], [[SetAngleEx]] *[[GetHeadingAngle]] *[[Rotate]] *[[GetPos]], [[SetPosEx]] *[[MoveToCell]], [[MoveTo]] *[[PlaceAtMeHealthPercent]], [[PlaceAtCell]], [[PlaceAtReticle]], [[PlaceLeveledActorAtMe]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Places a damaged actor or item in the world. |name = PlaceAtMeHealthPercent |referenceType = [actor|container:ref] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = placedObject |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = healthPercent |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = count |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = distance |Type = float |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = direction |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = buddyref.PlaceAtMeHealthPercent testraider .75 512 1 A raider is placed near Buddy's location at 75% health. }} ==Notes== *Direction is: **0 = front **1 = back **2 = left **3 = right *When using this to create an actor or item from a leveled list, note that the specified health percent overrides the health percentage set by the leveled list.
==See Also== *[[PlaceAtMe]] <!-- =='''Reference'''== None provided. <!-- [[PlaceAtMeHealthPercent_Reference|List of functions that use PlaceAtMeHealthPercent]] (wiki) -->
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = Places the given object at the spot the crosshair is pointing at. |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ObjectID |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = count |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = minDistance(unused) |Type = float |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = maxDistance |Value = 1-50000 |Type = float |Optional = y }} }} ==Examples== PlaceAtReticle TeddyBear01
PlaceAtReticle EuclidTargetPointer 1 0 EuclidPointerRange
player.PlaceAtReticle EuclidTarget 1 0 20000
==See Also== *[[PlaceAtCell]] *[[PlaceLeveledActorAtMe]] *[[PlaceAtMe]] *[[PlaceAtMeHealthPercent]] *[[PlaceAtReticleAlt]]
[[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Crosshair Functions]]

revision.comment: clarify that it generates a leveled actor, and document return val
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Generates and places a leveled actor reference from an [[Actor]] baseform, with the same parameters that are allowed in placing a leveled actor in the editor.
Returns a null form for failure, otherwise returns the newly placed reference. |name = PlaceLeveledActorAtMe |referenceType = [locationRefr] |returnType = reference |returnVal = placedActor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = actor |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = levelMod |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = encounterZoneID |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = markerref.PlaceLeveledActorAtMe LvlRaider 2 A raider is placed at the marker's location, set to Hard difficulty. }} ==Notes== *LevelMod values correspond to the Level Modifier dropdown on a leveled actor reference: **0 = Easy **1 = Medium **2 = Hard **3 = Boss **4 = None *LevelMod defaults to 4 ("None"). *If not specified, the Encounter Zone will default to whatever encounter zone the actor is being created in.
==See Also== *[[PlaceAtMe]] <!-- =='''Reference'''== None provided. <!-- [[PlaceLeveledActorAtMe_Reference|List of functions that use PlaceLeveledActorAtMe]] (wiki) -->
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Leveled List Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = '''PlayBink''' opens a Bink video file. |name = PlayBink |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = fileName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = interruptable |Type = bool(default=0) |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = muteAudio |Type = bool(default=1) |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = pauseMusic |Type = bool(default=1) |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = letterboxed |Type = bool(default=1) |Optional = y }} |example = PlaceAtMe Apple 1 Player.PlaceAtMe Apple 3 |example = 
PlayBink "MyNew.bik"
(above example plays Fallout3\data\video\MyNew.bik) ==Example== 
PlayBink "Mymod\MyNew.bik"
(above example plays Fallout3\data\video\Mymod\MyNew.bik) ==Example== 
PlayBink "..\sound\videos\Mynew.bik"
(above example plays Fallout3\sound\videos\MyNew.bik) ==Example== 
PlayBink "MyNew.bik" 0 1
(above example plays Fallout3\data\video\MyNew.bik, non-interruptable, with sounds muted) ==Example== 
PlayBink "MyNew.bik" 1 1 1 1
(above example plays Fallout3\data\video\MyNew.bik, interruptable with ESC key, game sound muted, game music paused, and video in letterbox style) }} ==Notes== *Playing a video via the PlayBink command in any way appears to stop game time, even if the flags are set to keep ingame noises going. *The Bink files need to be on your harddisk, they can't be packaged into a BSA File. *The filename needs to be in quotes. *The path to the filename is relative to Fallout3\Data\Video\ *If Interruptable is non-zero, the user can interrupt the Bink by hitting the escape key. Otherwise, they have to wait for it to play all the way to the end. *If Mute Audio is set to zero, the game's world sounds will continue to play during the Bink movie, otherwise they will be muted. *If Pause Music is set to zero, the game's music will continue to play while the movie is running, otherwise it will be paused. *If Letterboxed is set to zero, the bink will not be letterboxed if it is widescreen and is showing on a 4:3 screen. [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |name = PlayGroup |origin = GECK1 |summary = '''PlayGroup''' plays an animation group on the calling actor. See [[AnimGroups]] for a list of possible animations.
A flag must be used to control initialization of the group.
InitFlag :0 = Normal - The current animation will finish it's full cycle, and the new animation will start from its beginning. :1 = Immediate Start - The current animation will stop regardless of the frame it is on, and the new animation will start from its beginning. :2 = Immediate Loop -The current animation will stop regardless of the frame it is on, and the new animation will start at the beginning of its loop cycle. |referenceType = Actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = AnimGroup |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = InitFlag |Type = int |Value = 0, 1, 2 }} |example = PlayGroup FastForward 0 PlayGroup AttackPower 1 }} ==Notes== *Animations may be viewed in the GECK by navigating to the animation tab of the NPC window. Clicking on a .kf file will play the desired animation group. *Playing some animations on the Player or other NPCs can cause the actor to engage in abnormal behavior. These may be characterized by freezing in place, running backwards, walking through solid objects, being unable to attack, etc. Using ''PlayGroup Idle 1'' will return the actor to normal user function. *Some animation groups are only available under certain conditions. In particular, combat animations like Recoil will only be played if the actor has a weapon drawn. *The Stagger group is listed in [[AnimGroups]] but no .kf files are associated with it in the editor, meaning that it does not function. Instead, use PlayIdle to play a ''hit reaction'' idle. *When using this function, you need to ensure that the reference you are calling it on has an uppercase Anim Group (SpecialIdle) for instance, otherwise it will fail with a script error.
==See Also== * [[AnimGroups]] * [[PlayIdle]] * [[LoopGroup]] * [[SkipAnim]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Animation Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

revision.comment: replaced dead link with archived version
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = [[PlayIdle]] causes the calling actor to play a specified idle animation. It is recommended to use [[ForcePlayIdle]] or [[PlayIdleEx]] instead of this function. |referenceType = [Actor] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = IdleName |Type = EditorID }} |Example = FreddyRef.PlayIdle DanceMe FreddyRef will play the ''DanceMe'' idle animation }} == Notes ==
  • Calling PlayIdle on the player directly is bugged (but works in [[ForcePlayIdle]]), but you can use it on the player if the player's the implied reference of an actor effect's script.
    • begin ScriptEffectUpdate
    playidle someIdle
End
  • Playidle takes the actual [[Glossary#EditorID|EditorID]] string of the idle as parameter, not the converted formID, so will take a string literal as parameter, not a ref variable.
  • When called on the player directly, [[PlayIdle]] will only work under very specific circumstances: *When the game is in "Flying Camera" mode, as triggered by [[ToggleFlyCam]] *During the frame in which the player activated the scripted reference, and only if the scripted reference's default activation is not triggered. For example, the following code will cause the player to play the appropriate ''Brahmin Push'' when they activate the scripted reference: 
    Begin OnActivate player
	if IsPC1stPerson
		player.PlayIdle Loose1stPBrahminPush
	else
		player.Playidle Loose3rdPBrahminPush
	endif
End
  • The above limitations can be overcome by placing the animation outside of the characters folder. This requires usage of 3rd party tools, such as [[FO3Edit]], and is demonstrated [https://web.archive.org/web/20090504122735/http://www.project-valhalla.org/Adding_custom_animations#Adding_the_Idle_animations here]. When using vanilla animations in this manner, their file names must be changed, even if they are placed in a new folder. Note that adding a prefix to the file name works, but adding a suffix does not.
==See Also== *[[PlayGroup]] *[[PlayIdleEx]] *[[LoopGroup]] *[[SkipAnim]] *[[StopIdle]] *[[RefreshIdle]] *[[ForcePlayIdle]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Animation Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Plays the effect visuals on the calling reference. Visuals will play at least one cycle, regardless of duration, and if no duration is specified they play indefinitely or until the art stops animating. |name = PlayMagicEffectVisuals |alias = PME |referenceType = [Reference] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = effectID |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = duration |Type = float |Optional = y }} |example = 
PlayMagicEffectVisuals FIDG
pme FTHR 10
}} ==Notes== * The duration does not need to be specified for this command; it will play once unless you specify a longer duration. ==See Also== *[[PlayMagicShaderVisuals]] *[[StopMagicEffectVisuals]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category: Functions]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Plays the [[EffectShader]] visuals on the calling reference. Visuals will play at least one cycle, regardless of duration, and if no duration is specified they play indefinitely or until the art stops animating. |name = PlayMagicShaderVisuals |alias = PMS |referenceType = [Reference] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = effectShaderID |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = duration |Type = float |Optional = y }} |example = 
PlayMagicShaderVisuals SmokeFlameBurn
pms PlasmaGooificationGlow 10
}} ==Notes== * When playing a shader on the player, the effect will be applied to the current perspective state -- if the player is in first person when the shader is started, the effect will not be visible if the player switches to third person, and vice versa. * If no calling reference is provided, this will implicitly be called on the player. ==See Also== *[[PlayMagicEffectVisuals]] *[[StopMagicShaderVisuals]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category: Functions]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Plays the specified [[Music Type]] form. |name = PlayMusic |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = musicName |Type = baseform }} |example = 
PlayMusic DefaultExplore
}} ==Notes== * Will not stop the currently playing music of a music marker. NV Only. ==See Also== *[[IsMusicPlaying]] *[[SetMusicState]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Music]] [[Category:Sound Functions]] [[Category:Engine_Differences_(Fallout)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Plays the specified [[Sound]] form, non-locational.
If the ''SystemSound'' flag is set to a non-zero value, the sound will play even in [[MenuMode]]. Otherwise, the sound may be queued and not played until returning to [[GameMode]]. |referenceType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = sound |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = SystemSound |Type = int |Optional = optional }} |example = PlaySound AMBCeilingCrumble The game will play the AMBCeilingCrumble sound; if in MenuMode, it may not play until return to GameMode. PlaySound UIItemTakeAll 1 The UIItemTakeAll sound will be played even in MenuMode. }} ==Bugs== * This function will not work when called on an ambient sound that consists of one or more .ogg files.
==See Also== *[[IsSoundPlaying]] *[[PlayMusic]] *[[PlaySound3D]] *[[StopSound]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions]] [[Category:Sound Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Plays the specified [[Sound]] form, at the location of the calling reference. |name = PlaySound3D |referenceType = [Reference] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = soundID |Type = baseform }} |example = 
Player.PlaySound3D BigExplosion
}} ==Notes== *Function will not work if the reference has no model defined. *The sound file needs to be mono to play 3D properly. Stereo will revert to 2D playback. ==See Also== *[[IsSoundPlaying]] *[[PlayMusic]] *[[PlaySound]] *[[StopSound]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category: Functions]] [[Category: Miscellaneous Functions]] [[Category:Sound Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = This function will queue up the particles for a spell effect, if they aren't already loaded. The function will do nothing if the particles are already loading. It will be helpful only when a non-actor reference casts a spell, as actors preload their own spells automatically. |name = PreloadMagicEffect |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = effectID |Type = baseform }} |example = 
PreloadMagicEffect BACU
}} [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category: Functions]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Forcibly unloads all unattached cells in cell buffers. |name = PurgeCellBuffers |alias = PCB }}
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Cell Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = '''PushActorAway''' Knocks down (ragdolls) one actor and pushes it away from another with a given force. |name = PushActorAway |referenceType = [source:ref] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = target |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = force |Type = int }} |example = 
BuddyRef.PushActorAway FredRef 10
Fred would move away from Buddy with a Havok force of 10. 
BuddyRef.PushActorAway FredRef -50
Fred will be pulled towards Buddy with a Havok force of 50. If they are standing close enough, Fred will slide past Buddy, pushing Buddy away in the process. }} ==Notes== *Does not work on dead actors. *Does not work on actors flagged as "immobile" on their baseform animation data. *Force is an integer value. *An actor that has been pushed can have its inventory accessed by the player until the actor gets up again. *Will have no effect on actors that have the "No Knockdowns" flag set on their baseform. *If no calling REF is specified then the target will be launched straight up in the air with the force specified. ==See Also== *[[PushActorAwayAlt]] *[[PushActorNoRagdoll]] *[[PushObject]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Movement Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Call this command to allow the weather to resume normal dynamic operation, after using SetWeather or ForceWeather with the override flag set. |name = ReleaseWeatherOverride }} ==See Also== *[[SetWeather]] *[[ForceWeather]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Weather Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Replaces all instances of one water type with another water type |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = OldWaterType |Type = WaterType }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = NewWaterType |Type = WaterType }} |example = RemapWaterType DLC03TBPotomacWater DLC03TBCleanWater }} ==Notes== *This function will compile with any type of form specified for either parameter. It is currently unknown what effect other types of forms will have.
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions]]

{{Function |CSWikiPage = RemoveAllItems |origin = GECK1 |summary = Removes all items from the calling reference's inventory, <u>except for the following:</u>: *Biped forms marked as unplayable ([[LNIsPlayable]]), *If the calling reference is the player, Quest Items ([[IsQuestItem]]).
If a target container is specified, the items are moved to the target container with all equipment items retaining their current health; otherwise the items are destroyed as they would be via [[RemoveItem]].
If the ''retain ownership'' flag is set to a non-zero value, the items' original ownership is retained; otherwise the ownership is cleared. |returnType = void |referenceType = sourceContainer |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = targetContainer |Type = ref |Optional = optional }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = retainOwnershipFlag |Type = int |Optional = optional }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = hideMessages |Type = int |Optional = optional }} |example = RemoveAllItems RemoveAllItems TreasureChestREF Does nothing, since no source container has been specified.
player.RemoveAllItems Deletes every item in the player's inventory, with the exceptions of Quest items and Unplayable Biped items. For example, since the Pip-Boy is marked as Unplayable and it is considered to be Armor, it survives this mass removal.
player.RemoveAllItems EnclaveSoldierREF 1 Dumps the player's inventory into ''EnclaveSoldierREF'', with the exceptions of Quest items and Unplayable Biped items. The original ownership for each item is retained.
ContainerREF.RemoveAllItems player 1 0 Removes all items from ''ContainerREF'' and transfers them to the Player, except for Unplayable Biped items. Messages for each item are shown, and the original ownership for each transferred item is retained.
ContainerREF.RemoveAllItems player 0 1 Removes all items from ''ContainerREF'' and transfers them to the Player, except for Unplayable Biped items. Messages for each item are hidden, and the original ownership is cleared for each transferred item. }}
==Bugs== *Using RemoveAllItems on the player can cause the game to crash under unknown conditions in ''Fallout: New Vegas''
==See Also== *[[DropMe]] *[[RemoveAllTypedItems]] *[[RemoveAllItemsShowOff]] *[[RemoveItem]] *[[RemoveItemTarget]] *[[RemoveMe]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Object Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = An extended version of [[RemoveAllItems]]. Removes all [[Form Type IDs|typed items]] (except those listed in the exemption [[FormList]]) from the calling reference's inventory, <u>except for the following:</u> *Biped forms marked as unplayable ([[LNIsPlayable]]), *If the calling reference is the player, Quest Items ([[IsQuestItem]]).
==Arguments== If a target container is specified, the typed items are moved to the target container with all equipment items retaining their current health; otherwise the items are destroyed as they would be via [[RemoveItem]].
If the ''retain ownership'' flag is set to a non-zero value, the items' original ownership is retained; otherwise the ownership is cleared. This is especially notable in the case of retaining ownership on items owned by someone else when transferring them to the player, as this may be counted as stealing.
If the ''suppress messages'' flag is set to 1, the standard "Item Added" pop-up will not be displayed when this command is used to transfer items to the player.
If the ''typeCode'' flag is omitted or set to -1, will try to remove all items, essentially doing the same thing as [[RemoveAllItems]]. If set to 0, will do nothing. Otherwise, this determines the [[Form Type ID]] of items that will be removed.
If ''exceptionFormlist'' is specified, any form in that [[FormList]] will not be removed. {{:Inventory Item Type Codes}} |returnType = void |referenceType = sourceContainer |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = targetContainer |Type = reference |Optional = optional }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = retainOwnershipFlag |Type = (0/1) |Optional = optional }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = suppressMessages |Type = (0/1) |Optional = optional }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = typeCode |Type = int |Optional = optional }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = exceptionFormlist |Type = baseForm |Optional = optional }} |example = player.RemoveAllTypedItems vGOMPlayerEquipmentContainer 1 0 40 NVImprovedHoldoutWeapons Every item with FormType 40 ([[Weapon]]s) will be removed from the player and transferred to ''vGOMPlayerEquipmentContainer'', except for forms in ''NVImprovedHoldoutWeapons'' or which are unplayable Biped forms, or which are quest items. The original ownership of each item is retained, and no message is displayed.
TopsCompanionLocker2REF.RemoveAllTypedItems Player 1 0 Every item will be removed from the Tops Locker (''TopsCompanionLocker2REF'') and given to the player, except for unplayable Biped forms. Each item keeps its original ownership, and messages are displayed for each item being transferred. }}
==See Also== *[[Form Type IDs]] - a list of all formtypes. *[[RemoveItem]] *[[RemoveItemTarget]] *[[RemoveAllItems]] *[[RemoveAllItemsShowOff]]
[[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Removes all factions from the referenced actor. |name = RemoveFromAllFactions |referenceType = [actor:ref] }} ==See Also== *[[AddToFaction]] *[[RemoveFromFaction]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Faction Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Removes an actor (only the reference not the base object) from a faction. It is called on the Reference and has one parameter: the faction. |name = RemoveFromFaction |referenceType = [actor:ref] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = faction |Type = baseform }} |example = BuddyRef.RemoveFromFaction AntFaction BuddyRef is now removed from the AntFaction but all other Buddys are still in the AntFaction. }} ==Notes== * This does not work the same as [[SetFactionRank]], which is called on the base form of the actor.
==See Also== *[[AddToFaction]] *[[RemoveFromAllFactions]]
<!-- =='''Reference'''== None provided. [[RemoveFromFaction_Reference|List of functions that use RemoveFromFaction]] (wiki) -->
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Faction Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = '''RemoveImageSpaceModifier''' will remove the specified [[ImageSpace Modifiers|ImageSpace Modifier]]. |name = RemoveImageSpaceModifier |alias = rimod |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ImageSpaceModifierID |Type = baseform }} |example = RemoveImageSpaceModifier AntNectarISFX Removes the AntNectar FX from the screen. }} ==See Also== *[[ApplyImageSpaceModifier]] *[[IsImageSpaceActive]] *[[ImageSpace Modifier]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Imagespace Functions]]

revision.comment: does not run OnUnequip :sadge:
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Removes and destroys ''Count'' items of object ''ObjectID'' from the calling container's inventory. It has two required parameters (the object to remove and the quantity) and one optional parameter (whether to display a message on screen). |referenceType = <nowiki>ActorID|ContainerID</nowiki> |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ObjectID |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Count |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = HideMessageFlag |Type = int |Optional = y }} }} ==Examples==
buddyref.RemoveItem Stimpack 11 Buddy loses 11 stimpacks player.RemoveItem SuperStimpak 10 The player loses 10 super stimpacks and message displays like normal player.RemoveItem SuperStimpak 10 1 10 super stimpacks are removed from the player's inventory and no message would be displayed
==Notes== *You can use a FormList as the item parameter - in this case, the function removes the count for each item in the list. *The items are destroyed - despite the name, they are not simply "removed" from the container and placed nearby. *If the removed item was equipped, will NOT run the [[OnUnequip]] block. *If [[lStewieAl's Tweaks]] is installed then the command can be used in the console without specifying a quantity. This does not apply to scripts made in the GECK however. ==See Also== *[[AddItem]] *[[AddItemAlt]] *[[DropMe]] *[[DropMeAlt]] *[[GetItemCount]] *[[RemoveAllItems]] *[[RemoveAllTypedItems]] *[[RemoveItemTarget]] *[[RemoveMeIRAlt]] *[[RemoveMe]] *[[RemoveNote]]
<!--==Reference== None provided. [[RemoveItem_Reference|List of functions that use RemoveItem]] (wiki) -->
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Object Functions]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]]

revision.comment: clarify my note
revision.text:
{{Function |CSWikiPage = RemoveMe |origin = GECK1 |summary = Removes an object of the object's type from the inventory of the container that it is in (if applicable). If ''TargetContainerID'' is specified, a copy of the object is moved to the target container. |name = RemoveMe |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = TargetContainerID |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = 
short dispose ; 1 = Dispose of this object

Begin GameMode
   if dispose == 0
      set dispose to 1
      removeMe
   endif
End
| }} ==Notes== *In Fallout3.esm the only usage of this function is inside [[OnAdd]] blocks. If used outside of this block, RemoveMe may crash the game. Use [[DropMe]] instead. *This function acts as a "return" when called -- the lines of script following it will NOT be executed. *If there are multiple objects of the same type in the container (it is in a stack), the object that actually gets removed may be first one in the stack instead of the one that actually called the function. *This function does not display the "xxx has been removed." message. * If the ''removeMe TargetContainerID'' version of this function is used, the object placed in the target container will be a copy of the original object. The script variables of the copy will not be the same as the original. *This function preserves health, and charges associated with the object. *If this is called in the [[OnEquip]] block, then it will NOT run the [[OnUnequip]] block. ==Bugs== *If RemoveMe is called by an item in a non-persistent container, RemoveMe crashes the game. Use [[DropMe]] instead. If the calling item is inside a persistent container, the item will be removed, except if this container is the player. It does not cause crashes when used in the [[OnAdd]] block. *The removed object's [[GameMode]] block will continue to run for an undetermined amount of time after the RemoveMe function has been run. * The removed object will continue to think it is in its original container for an undetermined amount of time after the RemoveMe function has been run. I.e. If the object runs [[GetContainer]], it will continue to receive a reference to the last container it was in. *If this function is called from within a [[GameMode]] block, the game will go into an infinite loop and freeze. This can be corrected by guarding the RemoveMe call with a variable that ensures the call will only be made once. 
short dispose; 1 = Dispose of this object

BEGIN gameMode
   if dispose == 1
      set dispose to 0
      removeMe
   endif
END
{{Error|The following note may not apply to the GECK.}} *When this function is called inside the barter-menu (e.g. within an [[OnAdd]] Block) then the game will crash when the item with the script is stackable, you sell more than one of them and then switch from "sell" to "buy". To prevent this, either wait until the barter-screen closes (Menumode=1009) before removing the item or don't use such a script on stackable non-unique items. ==See Also== *[[DropMe]] *[[RemoveItem]] *[[RemoveMeIRAlt]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Object Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = '''RemoveMerchantContainer''' clears an existing merchant container off of an actor. |name = RemoveMerchantContainer |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = actor |Type = ref }} }} ==See Also== *[[SetMerchantContainer]] *[[GetMerchantContainer]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Removes a note from the players note inventory. It is not called on a Reference and it takes one parameter, the note to remove. |name = RemoveNote |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = note |Type = baseform }} |example = RemoveNote BombNote The player now does not have the bomb note. }} ==Notes== * Unlike [[AddNote]], when using RemoveNote the game will not display a message to let the player know what was removed. If the player needs to be notified, a simple message can be called using a function such as [[ShowMessage]].
==See Also== *[[AddNote]] *[[AddNoteNS]] *[[GetHasNote]] *[[RemoveItem]] *[[RemoveMe]]
<!--=='''Reference'''== None provided. [[RemoveNote_Reference|List of functions that use RemoveNote]] (wiki) --> [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Note Functions]]

revision.comment: https
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Removes the specified [[Perk]] from the calling [[Actor]]. *When called on the [[Player]] character, this function will remove the perk from the player, unless the optional argument '''forTeammates''' is non-zero, in which case it will remove the perk from all teammates. *When called on any actor other than the player, this function will work correctly only if the '''uNPCPerks''' optional [https://www.nexusmods.com/newvegas/mods/58277 JIP LN] feature is toggled in '''Data\NVSE\plugins\jip_nvse.ini'''. |name = RemovePerk |referenceType = ActorRef |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = perk |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = forTeammates |Type = 1/0 |Optional = y }} |example = 
Player.RemovePerk LittleSlugger
Removes the LittleSlugger perk from the '''Player'''. 
Player.RemovePerk LittleSlugger 1
Removes the LittleSlugger perk from all the '''Player's teammates'''. 
ActorRef.RemovePerk LittleSlugger
Removes the LittleSlugger perk from '''ActorRef'''. }} ==Notes== *In ''Fallout: New Vegas'', the Player is implemented as a special Actor type that has additional capabilities, including having perks. The Player has two lists of perks, one list which applies to the Player and one list which is applied to all current Teammates ([[GetPlayerTeammate]] == 1). Perks for companions can be added to the Player via Player.AddPerk PerkEditorID 1. Perk entry points can be given conditions such as [[GetIsID]] if their effects should not apply to all companions. Perk requirements have no effect on companion perks and only apply to the players ability to take the perk at level up. Abilities in a perk should also be conditioned to check for GetPlayerTeammate == 1 or GetIsID. *Using [[RemovePerk]] in an Effect Script on a perk that has an "ability" type Perk Entry defined will crash ''Fallout 3'', so long as it has an ability specified. Instead, use [[AddItem]] to add a scripted token that removes the perk via RemovePerk and then calls [[RemoveMe]]: ref rContainer Begin OnAdd set rContainer to GetContainer rContainer.RemovePerk MyPerk RemoveMe End ==See Also== *[[AddPerk]] *[[HasPerk]] *[[GetPerkRank]] to check the rank of a perk on the player. *[[GetAltPerkRank]] to check the rank of a perk on all teammates. [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Perk Functions]]

{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = Removes the "Recurring" flag from a [[Challenge]] on the player's challenges screen, preventing it from restarting. If it was completed, it will now be greyed out on the user's challenges screen. |name = RemoveRecurringFromChallenge |returnVal = |returnType = void |referenceType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ChallengeID |Type = ref |Optional = n }} |example = RemoveRecurringFromChallenge NVChallengeUsingThat }} ==See Also== *[[SetChallengeFlags]] is also able to remove the "Recurring" flag.
[[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Challenge Functions]] [[Category:Challenge Functions (Vegas)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Removes the currently running script [[package]] from the calling [[actor]]. Only necessary if you added a script package with [[AddScriptPackage]] and it was set to run indefinitely. When the script package is removed, the actor will automatically choose a new package. |name = RemoveScriptPackage |returnType = void |referenceType = [ActorRefID] |example = ActorRef.RemoveScriptPackage }}
==Notes== * Removing a script package on an actor that has no script package can cause the package to stop running on all instances of that actor. It is preferable to use a package(s) attached to the actor in its AI definition with one or more conditions specified. ** Also, it will prevent the player starting or acquiring new packages or procedures which are triggered by functions. For instance, calling [[ForceFlee]] while [[RemoveScriptPackage]] is being called will prevent the fleeing behavior from triggering. ** Conversely, calling [[RemoveScriptPackage]] will stop and remove the actor's current package/procedure had it been triggered by a function. For an example, calling the function on a fleeing actor will cancel the flee procedure. * Parameter usage is accepted by the script compiler, but has no effect during run-time. * This function cannot be used to remove Editor-added packages.
==Bugs==
* Issuing an [[EvaluatePackage]] within a few frames of RemoveScriptPackage may cause the package to be re-added to the Actor.
==See Also== *[[AddScriptPackage]] *[[EvaluatePackage]] *[[RemoveAllPackages]] *[[RemovePackageAt]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:AI Functions]] [[Category:Package Functions]]

revision.comment: italics
revision.text:
{{Incomplete}} {{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Removes the [[Actor Effect]] from the calling actor. The only types of actor effects used in the Vanilla Game are ''addictions'' and ''abilities''. |name = RemoveSpell |returnType = void |referenceType = [ActorRefID] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = EffectID |Type = ref }} |example = RemoveSpell StealthBoyInvisibilitySpell }} ==Bugs== * As of ''Fallout: New Vegas'' patch 1.2.0.352, this function appears to work inconsistently. It is recommended to use [[Dispel]] instead.
==See Also== *[[Dispel]] *[[AddSpell]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

revision.comment: capitalization
revision.text: {{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Clears any saved data regarding a reference's animation/Havok state. Used primarily to reset traps after they've been triggered. Note that this does not reset the object's 3d position in the world if it has been moved. |name = Reset3DState |referenceType = ref }} ==See Also== *[[TrapUpdate]] *[[Update3D]] *[[UpdatePlayer3D]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Clears all current AI behaviors from an actor. Combat, pathing, and packages are re-evaluated. |name = ResetAI |referenceType = [actor:ref] }} ==Notes== * This is a severe command that affects all AI behavior. Usually, you just want to have an actor re-evaluate its current package. In that case, use [[EvaluatePackage]] instead. *Packages added via [[AddScriptPackage]] will stop running and are removed when this command is used.
==See Also== *[[EvaluatePackage]] *[[ResetHealth]] *[[ResetInventory]]
[[Category:Designer Debug Tools]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:AI Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Resets the fall damage timer. When the timer is reset, calling object will take damage as if it just started falling. |name = ResetFallDamageTimer |referenceType = ref }} ==Bugs== * Note: This function is broken. Use the JIP alternative [[ResetFallTime]] instead.
==See Also== *[[ResetFallTime]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Restores the actor's health and cures crippling damage. It is called on a reference. |name = ResetHealth |referenceType = [actor:ref] |example = BuddyRef.ResetHealth Buddy's health and limb damage are reset. }} ==See Also== *[[GetBaseHealth]] *[[GetCurrentHealth]] *[[SetCurrentHealth]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Marks the cell as expired. |name = ResetInterior |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = cellID |Type = baseForm }} |example = ResetInterior MyVault01 }}
==Notes== * This function only marks the cell as expired. It does not automatically clean up the cell. * Unlike a time-based cell reset, this scripted reset does NOT resurrect [[Actor]]s. ** To work around this, either use [[ResetInteriorAlt]] or set '''bResetInteriorResetsActors''' to 1 (true) in [[ShowOff NVSE]]'s INI file.
==See also== *[[IsCellExpired]] *[[OnReset]] *[[ResetInteriorAlt]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category: Functions]] [[Category: Miscellaneous Functions]] [[Category:Cell Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Resets inventory on the reference it's called on. All previously added, removed, or calculated objects will be lost. |name = ResetInventory |referenceType = ref }}
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]]

revision.comment: I'm back baby!
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Resets the state of everything connected to a specified quest. Specifically: * Clears the quest stage list * Clears the objective list * Clears all the quest variables * Resets the quest stage (on or off) to its default state
For [[NVSE]], [[array_var]]s and [[string_var]]s are also reset. |name = ResetQuest |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = quest |Type = baseform }} |example = ResetQuest FFEUniqueA }} ==Notes== *Say Once and Say Once Per Day flags on infos in the quest ''are not cleared''.
==See Also== *[[StopQuest]] *[[StartQuest]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Quest Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = '''[[RestoreActorValue]]''' is called on a Reference and takes two parameters, an [[Stats_List|ActorValue]] and a value. The Function alters the damage modifier of a given actor value. The damage modifier is always a negative number, or zero if there is no damage to the actor value. RestoreActorValue will increase the modifier by the given amount or to 0. |name = RestoreActorValue |alias = RestoreAV |referenceType = ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = actorValue |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = value |Type = float }} |example =BuddyRef.RestoreActorValue Health 20 If Buddy has 10 points of health, adds 0 to the damage modifier, because it cannot go above zero [[GetActorValue]] health returns 10. }} ==Notes== * Function also accepts floating point numbers.
==See Also== *[[DamageActorValue]] *[[ForceActorValue]] *[[GetActorValueInfo]] *[[GetActorValue]] *[[GetBaseActorValue]] *[[GetPermanentActorValue]] *[[ModActorValue]] *[[SetActorValue]] *[[Stats List]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]]

revision.comment: italics
Cannot be used in loops
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = '''Return''' is used to force a script to stop processing from that line on. It does not JUST terminate the current block, it terminates everything within that script that is placed below the line in which '''Return''' is called, including any blocks. |name = Return }} ==Notes== *If the script uses a block that runs for multiple frames, such as [[GameMode]], the script will be run again in the next frame. Return can be useful inside of If statements by allowing you to prevent the lines following the return from being processed. *Cannot be used inside a [[While]] / [[ForEach]] loop.
==See Also== *[[Continue]] *[[Break]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Awards/removes Karma Points to/from the player. It is not called on a reference and the parameter is an integer which represents the amount of Karma to add to the player. If set to a negative value, will remove Karma. The function accounts for ''iKarmaMax'' and ''iKarmaMin'' game settings, meaning that the resulting Karma will not go over or under their values. |name = RewardKarma |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = value |Type = int }} |example = RewardKarma 100 100 Karma is added to the player (Good Karma).
RewardKarma -75 75 Karma is removed from the player (Bad Karma). }}
==Notes== *A corner message is shown in game indicating the change when using this function.
[[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Karma Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = '''[[RewardXP]]''' Awards Experience Points (XP) to the player. It is not called on a reference and the parameter is an integer which represents the amount of XP to add to the player. |name = RewardXP |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = value |Type = int }} |example = RewardXP 100 100 XP is added to the player. }} ==Notes== * Done correctly, a message should appear in-game stating how much experience has been earned.
==See Also== *[[Experience Settings]] *[[RewardXPExact]] *[[GetKillXP]] *[[GetXPForNextLevel]] *[[ResetXP]]
[[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Player Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Rotates the calling object relative to the world on the specified axis (X, Y, or Z) at a given speed in degrees/sec. |name = Rotate |referenceType = [Object] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Axis |Type = char |Value = X, Y, Z }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = DegreesPerSec |Type = float }} |example = GlobeObject.Rotate X 33 }} ==Notes== *Because of the method used for rotation, this function is not reliable for complex rotations: rotating an object which has already been rotated may not work as you expect. The animation functions should be used for repetitive or complex rotations, as they are both faster and more reliable. *A reference can only be rotated around one axis per frame. If [[Rotate]] is called on the same reference multiple times in one frame, only the last call will be applied. *Collision is not updated with rotation until the cell is reloaded or [[Update3D]] is used.
==Bugs== *Often causes the object this command was run on to be removed from the game when loading a save from ''before'' this command was used or in other cases changes will persist.
==See Also== *[[GetAngle]] *[[SetAngle]] *[[SetAngleEx]] *[[GetHeadingAngle]] *[[MoveTo]] *[[MoveToCell]] *[[MoveToContainer]] *[[SetPos]] *[[SetPosEx]] *[[GetPos]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Movement Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns 1 if the calling actor is in the same faction as the target actor -- i.e. they are both members of at least one faction in common. |name = SameFaction |returnType = bool |referenceType = actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = actor |Type = form }} |Example=if (player.SameFaction TargetActor.Ref == 1) }}
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor State Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Faction Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns 1 if the calling actor is in faction that the player also belongs to. |name = SameFactionAsPC |returnType = bool |referenceType = [actor:ref] }}
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor State Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Faction Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns 1 if the calling actor is the same race as the target actor. |name = SameRace |returnVal = 0, 1 |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = TargetActor |Type = ref }} }} ==See Also== *[[SameRaceAsPC]] *[[GetIsRace]] *[[GetPCIsRace]] *[[GetRace]] *[[IsRaceInList]] *[[MatchRace]] *[[SetRace]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor State Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns 1 if the calling actor is the same race as the player. |name = SameRaceAsPC |returnType = bool |referenceType = [actor:ref] }}
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor State Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns 1 if the calling actor is the same sex as the target actor. |name = SameSex |returnVal = 0, 1 |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = TargetActor |Type = ref }} }}
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor State Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns 1 if the calling actor is the same sex as the player. |name = SameSexAsPC |returnType = bool |referenceType = [actor:ref] }}
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor State Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

{{Function |CSWikiPage = Say |origin = GECK1 |summary = [[Say]] makes an actor speak a single line of dialogue. |referenceType = Actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Topic |Type = EditorID }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = ForceSubtitles |Type = int(0/1) |Optional = Optional }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Actor |Type = EditorID |Optional = Optional }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = is2D |Type = int(0/1) |Optional = Optional }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = isVoice |Type = int(0/1) |Optional = Optional }} |Example = JoeRef.Say SpeechTopic JoeRef will say SpeechTopic }}
==Bugs== * Using Say in a package result script will cause the package to hang if the player is not in the cell * Using Say in a dialogue result script may cause the game to hang
==See Also== *[[SayTo]] *[[SayToDone]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Dialogue Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |name = SayTo |summary = '''SayTo''' makes an actor say a single line of dialogue to another actor. The function returns 0, so setting a variable equal to the return value will not do anything. To set up an action for after the line is spoken, see [[SayToDone]], which is a block that is triggered when the SAYTO is complete. |referenceType = Actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = targetActor |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Topic |Type = EditorID }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = forceSubtitleFlag |Type = int |Optional = Optional }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = noTargetLookFlag |Type = int |Optional = Optional }} |example = 
SayTo JoeRef SecretTopic
SayTo player SecretTopic 1
force subtitles 
SayTo JoeRef SecretTopic 0 1
Joe is not forced to look at the speaker during the SayTo }} ==Bugs== * Using SayTo in a package result script will cause the package to hang if the player is not in the cell ==See Also== *[[Say]] *[[SayToDone]] *[[SayTo_GetTopic]] / [[SayTo_GetTopicInfo]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Dialogue Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Available only in Effect Scripts. Returns seconds elapsed since the last magic effect update. This will only return a value greater than 0 inside a [[ScriptEffectUpdate]] block ('''but it will compile without error in other block types'''). |name = ScriptEffectElapsedSeconds |returnVal = elapsedSeconds |returnType = float |example = 
float fTimer
 set fTimer to fTimer + ScriptEffectElapsedSeconds
}} [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category: Functions]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]] [[Category:Time Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Manually creates an assault crime. There are two optional parameters: an actor reference (the specific victim, if applicable - if not applicable, use player) and a faction reference. |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = OffendedActor |Type = ref |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = OffendedFaction |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |referenceType = ref |example = 
Player.SendAssaultAlarm FlashRef
Flash will react as if he is a victim of an assault crime. 
SendAssaultAlarm Player BigTown
Because the player is not a valid target for an assault alarm, it will pick an NPC in the BigTown faction to use instead. }} ==Notes== *This command CANNOT be used to have ai actors attack other ai, The [[player]] is always considered the one committing the crime. *Causes actors to yell out the "Assault" [[dialogue]] topic even if they are already in combat with the target. ==Bugs== *Using this command without a calling REF can sometimes lead to a CTD. ==See Also== *[[ClearActorsFactionsPlayerEnemyFlag]] *[[ClearFactionPlayerEnemyFlag]] *[[GetActorFactionPlayerEnemy]] *[[GetActorCrimePlayerEnemy]] *[[SendTrespassAlarm]] *[[SendStealingAlarm]] *[[StartCombat]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Crime Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Sends out a trespass alarm as if someone had reported the player trespassing. |name = SendTrespassAlarm |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = reportedTo |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |referenceType = [reportedTo:Ref] |example = 
SendTrespassAlarm SunnyRef
Alerts Sunny that the player trespassed. 
SunnyRef.SendTrespassAlarm player
Alerts Sunny that the player trespassed. }} ==Notes== * Seems to only take the player as the criminal parameter. * Goes all the way to fully trespassed on one call, regardless of [[IGuardWarnings|iGuardWarnings]] GMST. ==See Also== *[[SendAssaultAlarm]] *[[SendStealingAlarm]] *[[SetCellPublicFlag]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Crime Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Sets the actor's alpha value (how transparent they are).
NOTE: Does not affect the weapon they carry. |name = SetActorAlpha |alias = SAA |referenceType = [actor:ref] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = alpha |Type = float }} |example = SetActorAlpha 0.5 }} ==See Also== *[[GetActorAlpha]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category: Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Changes the actor's name (display name) to the title (not the text) of the specified [[Message]]. Note that while the function is called on a reference, it actually changes the name of the base object, so all instances of that NPC/creature will have a new name.
|name = SetActorFullName |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = message |Type = baseform }} |example = ref.SetActorFullName NamedActorHarold }} ==Notes== *SetActorFullName can also be used on a [[TalkingActivator|talking activator]].
==See Also== *[[SetFullNameAlt]] *[[SetName]] *[[LNSetName]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Name Functions]]

{{Function |CSWikiPage = SetActorValue |origin = GECK1 |summary = SetActorValue sets the stat's base value to the new value.
See [[Stats List]] for a list of stats that can be accessed using SetActorValue.
See [[ActorValue]] to understand how Actor Values are calculated. |name = SetActorValue |alias = SetAV |referenceType = Actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = StatName |Type = actorValue }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Value |Type = int }} |example = Player.SetActorValue Health 3 SetActorValue Strength 6 }}
== See Also == *[[UnsetAV]] to undo this function's effects. *[[ForceActorValue]] *[[GetActorValue]] *[[GetActorValueInfo]] *[[GetBaseActorValue]] *[[GetPermanentActorValue]] *[[ModActorValue]] *[[RestoreActorValue]] *[[Stats List]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]]

revision.comment: capitalization
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = '''SetActorsAI ''' turns on and off the processing of an actor in the world. It is called on a reference and takes one parameter an integer. If the integer is 0, it turns off the processing and any other integer will turn it on. Scripts will still run on them but they will not process packages, detection, Havok or animation. |name = SetActorsAI |referenceType = [actor:ref] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = processAI |Type = bool }} |example = BuddyRef.SetActorsAI 0 Buddy will no longer process schedules, detection, Havok or animation. }} ==See Also== *[[ToggleActorsAI]] *[[SetRestrained]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:AI Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Sets whether the actor is in an "alert" state (weapon drawn). This is purely an animation state, and does not affect their actual combat status. |name = SetAlert |returnType = void |referenceType = actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = value |Type = bool}} }} ==Notes== *This function is savebaked and in addition is not cleared after any length of time or when a [[cell]] resets. *using [[UnEquipItem]] and it's variants at the same time you are setting the alert state to 1 OR 0 can cause the actor be stuck holding an invisible weapon.
==See Also== *[[GetIsAlerted]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:AI Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = This is one of 4 functions which can be used to increase the game's performance in situations where many actors are in combat or otherwise doing complex pathing. Note that the flag is cleared every frame, so this function needs to be called in a script running on a reference in the loaded area.
When the SetAllVisible flag is set, actors do not perform LineOfSight checks. When actors check to see if they can see something, they assume they can see its head. |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = enable |Type = int }} |example = SetAllVisible 1 }}
==See Also== *[[SetSceneIsComplex]] *[[SetAllReachable]] *[[SetNoAvoidance]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category: Functions]] [[Category: AI Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Sets a friend/ally relationship between two factions. It is not called on a reference. Will add the second argument faction to the first argument faction's reaction list if it is absent from the list. |name = SetAlly |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = faction1 |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = faction2 |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = F1toF2Flag |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = F2toF1Flag |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = 
SetAlly BrotherhoodSteelFaction RivetCityFaction
This makes the BrotherhoodSteelFaction and the RivetCityFaction allies with each other 
SetAlly BrotherhoodSteelFaction RivetCityFaction 0 1
This makes the BrotherhoodSteelFaction an Ally of RivetCityFaction RivetCityFaction a Friend to BrotherhoodSteelFaction }} ==Notes== *The values for the flag mean: **0 = Ally (default) **1 = Friend ==See Also== * [[SetEnemy]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Faction Functions]]

revision.comment: Works in degrees
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Sets the angle ('''in degrees''') of the calling object relative to the world on the specified axis (X, Y, or Z). |name = SetAngle |returnType = void |referenceType = [Object] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Axis |Type = char |Value = X, Y, Z }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Angle |Type = float }} |example = ActorRef.SetAngle Z 270 Player.SetAngle X 28.8 }} ==Notes== When called on the player and actors: *SetAngle Z rotates the player/actor as it rotates any object in game. It can be used to force the player or actor face a certain direction. E.g. In an exterior cells, "Player.SetAngle Z 45" forces the player face NE.
*Player and actors cannot be tilted forward, backward or sideways like most other objects, therefore changing their X and Y angles has no effect, with the following exception.
*SetAngle X on the player tilts the camera view up/down. It is possible to set the player's X angle beyond the limits of the mouse movement (-89 to 89). Note that the values are counterintuitive: negative angles force the player look up, positive angles, down.
*Some objects will 'forget' the X angle, setting it back to zero every few frames.
*Statics may not respond to SetAngle unless spawned dynamically via [[PlaceAtMe]].
==See Also== *[[SetAngleEx]] *[[GetAngle]] *[[GetHeadingAngle]] *[[GetPos]] / [[SetPos]] *[[MoveTo]] *[[Rotate]] *[[Turn]] *[[FaceObject]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Movement Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |name = SetBroadcastState |summary = Sets the broadcast state of a radio station. |referenceType = [radio:ref] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = broadcastState |Type = int }} |example = NPCRadio.SetBroadcastState 1 NPCRadio will now broadcast whatever audio is assigned to that station. }} ==Broadcast State== 
0	turns off continuous broadcast -- the station will play static except when playing scripted conversations using StartRadioConversation.
1	turns on continuous broadcast -- the station will generate and play radio conversations one after another.
==See Also== *[[GetBroadcastState]] *[[StartRadioConversation]] *[[:Category:Radio Stations|Radio Stations]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Radio Stations]] [[Category:Radio Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Changes the cell's display name to the '''title''' text of the specified [[message]]. This is what will appear on load doors leading into the cell, as well as on the local map. |name = SetCellFullName |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = cellID |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = messageID |Type = baseform }} |example = SetCellFullName HouseForSale HouseForSaleMsg }} ==Notes== * Only works with interior cells.
==See Also== *[[SetName]] *[[SetNameEx]] *[[LNSetName]] *[[SetFullNameAlt]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category: Functions]] [[Category: Miscellaneous Functions]] [[Category:Name Functions]] [[Category:Cell Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Changes the ImageSpace of a cell. |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Cell |Type = EditorID }}{{FunctionArgument |ImageSpace |Type = EditorID }} |example = SetCellImageSpace Vault101a VaultImageSpace01 Sets the ImageSpace of the cell ''Vault101a'' to ''VaultImageSpace01'' }} ==Notes== *After calling [[SetCellImageSpace]] on a cell that is currently loaded, the ImageSpace will not be refreshed until the cell is reloaded. This will happen if the player moves away from the cell then back to the cell, or if a save made in the cell (with the new ImageSpace) is loaded from the menu. This is fixed in [[ButcherPete FOSE]] for Fallout 3 and JohnnyGuitar NVSE version 5.18 for New Vegas. ==See Also== *[[GetCellImageSpace]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Cell Functions]] [[Category:Imagespace Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Sets the ownership of the specified [[Cell]] to the specified [[NPC]] or [[Faction]] BaseForm. If the second parameter is left blank, the cell will be set as owned by the [[Player]]. |name = SetCellOwnership |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = cell |Type = BaseForm }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newOwner |Type = BaseForm |Optional = y }} |example = 
SetCellOwnership WeynonPrioryHouse BladesFaction
WeynonPrioryHouse set to BladesFaction-owned. 
SetCellOwnership MyImperialCityHouse
MyImperialCityHouse set to player-owned. }} ==See Also== *[[GetOwnerOfCell]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Crime Functions]] [[Category:Cell Functions]] [[Category:Ownership Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Sets the cell's public flag (1 or 0). Cells which are public cannot have trespassing, so this is useful for changing the [[:Category:Crime|Crime]] behavior in cells. |name = SetCellPublicFlag |alias = SetPublic |returnType = traitValue:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = cellID |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = publicFlag |Type = int }} |example = SetCellPublicFlag MyCell 1 }} ==See Also== *[[IsTrespassing]] *[[SendTrespassAlarm]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Crime Functions]] [[Category:Cell Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Changes the [[Class|class]] of the actor to the parameter. An autocalc is performed, if and only if the checkbox is marked for the actor. It is called on a reference. |name = SetClass |referenceType = [actor:ref] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = class |Type = baseform }} |example = BuddyRef.SetClass VaultDweller }} ==See Also== *[[GetIsClass]] *[[GetClass]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Sets the calling actor reference's [[Combat_Style|combat style]] to the new style specified. |name = SetCombatStyle |referenceType = [actor:ref] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = CombatStyleID |Type = baseform }} }} ==Notes== *Does not change the combat style on the base form of the actor but instead only changes the combat style of the calling reference.
==See Also== *[[GetCombatStyle]] *[[SetCombatStyleFlag]] *[[SetCombatStyleTraitNumeric]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category: Functions]] [[Category: Combat Functions]]

revision.comment: capitalization
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Sets the reference to be in the given critical stage and applies its effects. |name = SetCriticalStage |referenceType = ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = eCritStage |Type = string }} |example = SetCriticalStage GooStart }} ==Notes== eCritStage values are: * None * GooStart -- change constraints in the ragdoll * GooEnd -- take ref out of the water system, cull its 3D, and remove its Havok data from the world * DisintegrateStart -- change constraints in the ragdoll * DisintegrateEnd -- take ref out of the water system, cull its 3D, and remove its Havok data from the world
==See Also== *[[IsInCriticalStage]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Sets the default open state for an object. (The equivalent of using the "Open By Default" checkbox on a reference.) |name = SetDefaultOpen |referenceType = ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = defaultOpen |Type = int }} |example = SetDefaultOpen 1 object's default state is now "open" }} ==See Also== * [[GetDefaultOpen]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = SetDestroyed takes an int parameter. A 1 parameter marks the ref as destroyed, a 0 parameter clears the destroyed flag on the ref. |name = SetDestroyed |referenceType = ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = destroyedFlag |Type = int }} |example = SetDestroyed 1 }} ==Notes== *This can be used on any door or activator to indicate that it is non-functional. Destroyed doors/activators will no longer give rollover text and cannot be activated. *This should not be used on temporary references as the flag appears to not be cleared if the formid is reused. *Using this function on actors is not recommended, use a script instead to make activating them do nothing. *Do not use this function on spawn markers, it will create various bugs including immunity to explosives.
==See Also== *[[GetDestroyed]] *[[SetInteractionDisabled]] *[[Destructible_Object_Data]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = [[SetEnemy]] sets an enemy/neutral relationship between two factions. It is not called on a reference. Will add the second argument faction to the first argument faction's reaction list if it is absent from the list. |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Faction1 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Faction2 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = F1toF2Flag |Value = 0,1 |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = F2toF1Flag |Value = 0,1 |Optional = y }} }} ==Examples==
SetEnemy BrotherhoodSteelFaction EnclaveFaction This makes the BrotherhoodSteelFaction and the EnclaveFaction Enemies of each other SetEnemy BrotherhoodSteelFaction EnclaveFaction 1 0 This makes the BrotherhoodSteelFaction Neutral to EnclaveFaction but EnclaveFaction will treat BrotherhoodSteelFaction as an Enemy
==Notes== *The values for the flag mean: **0 = Enemy (default) **1 = Neutral
==See Also== * [[SetAlly]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Faction Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |CSWikiPage = SetEssential |summary = Sets the essential state of the actor. |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = BaseID |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Flag |Type = boolean }} |example = SetEssential MyCompanionID 1
Sets the object, referred to by MyCompanionID, to essential. }} ==See Also== *[[IsEssential]] *[[SetActorRefEssential]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]]

revision.comment: Undo revision 30262 by [[Special:Contributions/Demorome|Demorome]] ([[User talk:Demorome|talk]])
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Sets the actor's rank in the specified [[:Category:Factions|faction]]. If the actor is not a member of the faction, adds them to the faction at the new rank. |name = SetFactionRank |referenceType = Actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = FactionID |Type = faction }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newRank |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = KarenSchenzyREF.SetFactionRank ArefuFaction 1 Sets Karen to rank one in the ArefuFaction faction. Player.SetFactionRank ArefuFaction -1 This will remove the player from the ArefuFaction faction. }} ==Notes== The faction rank is set on the actor's [[Glossary#B|base object]] as well as on the actor ([[reference]]) itself. Any actor spawned from the same base object (by a [[Leveled Creature|leveled list]], by [[PlaceAtMe]] and so on) will have this faction, at the same rank, already set. This is not a problem for named characters (of which there is usually just one in the world), but ''can'' be a problem for random, unnamed characters or creatures.
==See also== * [[GetFactionRank]] * [[GetFactionRankDifference]] * [[ModFactionRank]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Faction Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Set the specified faction's reaction towards the TargetFactionID to the specified amount. See [[:Category:Factions|Factions]] for more information on faction reaction. |name = SetFactionReaction |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = faction |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = targetFaction |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = modValue |Type = int }} |example = SetFactionReaction MegatonFaction playerFaction -20 }} ==See Also== *[[GetFactionReaction]] *[[ModFactionReaction]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category: Functions]] [[Category: Faction Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Turns force run on or off. |name = SetForceRun |referenceType = actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = forceRunFlag |Type = int }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetForceRun]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor State Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Turns force sneak on or off. |name = SetForceSneak |referenceType = actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = forceRunFlag |Type = int }} }} ==Notes== *This bug only seems to occur if the script is not attached to the actor that it is affecting:
If SetForceSneak 1 is called too soon after SetForceSneak 0, the calling ref will appear to not be sneaking, although [[IsSneaking]] and [[GetForceSneak]] will still return the correct values. The opposite occurs if SetForceSneak 0 is called too soon after SetForceSneak 1. *If SetForceSneak is set to 1 and used in conjunction with the [[FireWeapon]] function, then the character will stand when firing. This appears to be an apparent bug with animation groups not updating when using this function. If you wish to make a character shoot while sneaking, then it would be best to use a "Use Weapon" package. ==See Also== *[[GetForceSneak]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor State Functions]]

{{Function |CSWikiPage = SetGhost |origin = GECK1 |summary = Sets the [[actor]] [[reference]] to/from the "ghost" state. The changes are save-persistent. |name = SetGhost |returnType = void |referenceType = [Actor] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ghostState |Type = 0/1 }} |example = BuddyREF.SetGhost 1 BuddyREF is now in "ghost" state. BuddyREF.SetGhost 0 BuddyREF is no longer in "ghost" state (default state). }} ==Notes== *Cannot be harmed by a weapon or spell/magic effect in any way. [[Projectile]]s pass through them, *The stated chance to hit them in [[VATS]] is unchanged, despite them being impossible to hit (bug), *''Can'' inflict damage. So using ''player.SetGhost 1'' will not prevent the player from killing their enemies, *Is still able to talk and can be activated to talk, *Still collides normally with other actors and world objects, *Cannot be pickpocketed, despite the prompt still showing up. Instead, regular activation (dialog) occurs. *Though NPCs and creatures can not be damaged in this state, the player ''can''. To prevent the player from taking damage, use ToggleImmortalMode. *Enemies of this calling ref will not go into combat or detect an actor with this flag.
==See Also== *[[GetIsGhost]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor State Functions]]

revision.comment: Document keepSoundUnchanged opt arg
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = GECK1 |alias = sgtm |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = multiplier |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = keepSoundUnchanged |Type = bool |Value = 0 |Optional = y }} |summary = [[SetGlobalTimeMultiplier]] Sets the time multiplier. 1.0 is standard time, 0.5 is slow mo, and 0.25 is even slower.
If ''keepSoundUnchanged'' is set to 1 (true), then sounds will not be sped up/slowed down when the time mult is changed.
|example = sgtm .5 Now the world is in slow motion.
SetGlobalTimeMultiplier 2 1 Now the world is sped up, but sounds are not affected. }}
==Notes== *Don't pass a value of 0 to this function or you will break the game. {{Template:Time Mult Changes}} ==See Also== *[[GetGlobalTimeMultiplier]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Time Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Set to 1 so actor will ignore friendly hits. Set to 0 to clear the condition. |name = SetIgnoreFriendlyHits |referenceType = [actor:ref] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ignoreFriendlyHits |Type = int }} |example = BobREF.SetIgnoreFriendlyHits 1 }} ==See Also== *[[GetIgnoreFriendlyHits]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category: Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:AI Functions]]

revision.comment: Typo fix.
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Sets the player into a "character generation" state. While in this state, the player will not level up (although XP will accumulate as usual). When the player leaves this state, the appropriate number of levels will be immediately awarded. |name = SetInCharGen |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = iFlag |Type = bool |Value = 0/1 }} |example = SetInCharGen 1 }} ==See Also== * [[GetInCharGen]] * [[IsInCharGen]]
[[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Player Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Sets the item's base value to the specified amount. Note that while it is called on a reference, it actually changes the value of the reference's base object (and so will change the value of every reference of that base object). |name = SetItemValue |referenceType = [item:ref] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = value |Type = int }} }} == Notes == *The changes made by this function will be saved to save games.
==See Also== *[[SetBaseItemValue]] *[[GetItemValue]] *[[GetValueAlt]] *[[SetValueAlt]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category: Functions]] [[Category: Object Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Sets the last exterior door activated. |name = SetLastExtDoorActivated |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Door |Type = Reference }} |example = SetLastExtDoorActivated MyExteriorDoorREF Sets the last activated exterior door to MyExteriorDoorREF }} ==Notes== This function sets the last exterior door activated which has the effect of changing which worldspace map is currently shown in the Pip-Boy.
This function does NOT update the parent worldspace of the currently loaded cell. ==See Also== *[[GetPCLastExteriorDoor]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Door Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = In theory, if the LevelToPCFlag is set to 1 then the level provided is an offset to the player's level, which will not take their level above or below the provided maximum and minimum levels. In practice, the function does not work correctly when called on NPCs- SetLevel will often (if not always) set their level to the provided MinLevel, disregarding any level offset, maximum or anything else.
This function can be called on the player and will change the player's level, but it will not change not any of his stats (and the world will not "level up" with the player), so this is not recommended. Use [[RewardXP]] instead. |name = SetLevel |referenceType = [actor:ref] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = newLevel |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = levelToPCFlag |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = minLevel |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = maxLevel |Type = int |Optional = y }} }} ==Notes== Calling SetLevel on an actor other than the player will cause that actor's inventory to be regenerated, re-evaluating any leveled lists that may make up the actor inventory. You can witness this in action by finding a random raider and calling setlevel on them via the console. They will usually end up wearing a different piece of armor, because their inventory was repopulated. This repopulation effect takes effect even if the SetLevel command did not change the actor's level.
==See Also== *[[GetLevel]] *[[RewardXP]] *[[RewardXPExact]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category: Functions]] [[Category: Actor State Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = This puts the game into a state where only load screens specifically associated with the current cell will be displayed. |name = SetLocationSpecificLoadScreensOnly |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = flag |Type = int |Value = 0/1 }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetLocationSpecificLoadScreensOnly]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Loading Screen Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Assigns a merchant container to an actor. |name = SetMerchantContainer |referenceType = [actor:ref] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = container |Type = ref }} }} ==Notes== * This function will fail if the merchant container is a non-persistent reference.
==See Also== *[[RemoveMerchantContainer]] *[[GetMerchantContainer]] *[[Adding items to vendors]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = This sets the "minimal use" flag on a door. |name = SetMinimalUse |referenceType = [door:ref] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = minimalUseFlag |Type = int |Value = 0/1 }} }}
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]]
