'''RemoveReputation''' removes a certain amount of reputation to a faction, unlike [[RemoveReputationExact]], it only allows 1-5, whereas 1 is a small deed forgotten, and 5 a big deed.
The first integer determines if infamy (0) of fame (1) should be removed, whereas the second integer determines the amount (1-5)
If one wishes to remove an exact amount of reputation, use [[RemoveReputationExact]].
The amount of reputation points that these integers correspond to are determined by game settings.
*(Integer) = (Amount of reputation points actually removed) **1 = [[fReputationBumpVeryMinor]], Default Setting = 1 **2 = [[fReputationBumpMinor]], Default Setting = 2 **3 = [[fReputationBumpAverage]], Default Setting = 4 **4 = [[fReputationBumpMajor]], Default Setting = 7 **5 = [[fReputationBumpVeryMajor]], Default Setting = 12
=='''Examples'''==
RemoveReputation RepNVGoodsprings 0 5
The player has somehow gotten this faction to forget a very major '''bad''' deed against them, causing a big loss of infamy equal to [[fReputationBumpVeryMajor]].
RemoveReputation RepNVGoodsprings 1 1
The player has somehow gotten this faction to forget a very minor '''good''' deed, causing a small loss of fame equal to [[fReputationBumpVeryMinor]].
=='''See Also'''== *[[GetReputation]] *[[AddReputation]] *[[RemoveReputationExact]]
[[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Reputation Functions]]

{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = Returns the current reputation with a certain faction. Currently, when used in-game with the console or when used at all without an if, it fails to return a value, so it is best used as an if statement like in the examples.
The second arg determines if infamy (0) of fame (1) is checked.
|returnType = void |referenceType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = FactionID |Type = editorID |Optional = n }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Fame/Infamy |Type = int |Optional = n }} }} =='''Examples'''==
if GetReputation RepNVGoodsprings 1 > 10
Returns 1 if the player has more than 10 '''fame''' with the Goodsprings faction.
if GetReputation RepNVGoodsprings 0 < 30
Returns 1 if the player has less than 30 '''infamy''' with the Goodsprings faction.
=='''See Also'''==
*[[GetReputationThreshold]] *[[GetReputationPct]] *[[AddReputation]]
[[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Reputation Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = Adds a certain amount of reputation (infamy/fame) with a certain faction.
|returnType = void |referenceType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = FactionReputation |Type = baseform |Optional = n }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Infamy/Fame |Type = int |Optional = n }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Amount |Type = int |Optional = n }} }}
'''AddReputation''' adds a certain amount of reputation to a faction, unlike [[AddReputationExact]], it only allows 1-5, whereas 1 is a small deed done, and 5 is a big deed.
The first integer determines if infamy (0) of fame (1) should be added, whereas the second integer determines the exact amount (1-5)
If one wishes to add an exact amount of reputation, use [[AddReputationExact]].
The amount of reputation points that these integers correspond to are determined by game settings.
*(Integer) = (Amount of reputation points actually added) **1 = [[fReputationBumpVeryMinor]], Default Setting = 1 **2 = [[fReputationBumpMinor]], Default Setting = 2 **3 = [[fReputationBumpAverage]], Default Setting = 4 **4 = [[fReputationBumpMajor]], Default Setting = 7 **5 = [[fReputationBumpVeryMajor]], Default Setting = 12
=='''Examples'''==
AddReputation RepNVGoodsprings 0 5
The player has done a big deed ''against'' this faction, causing a big gain of infamy equal to [[fReputationBumpVeryMajor]].
AddReputation RepNVGoodsprings 1 1
The player has done a small deed ''for'' this faction, causing a small gain of fame equal to [[fReputationBumpVeryMinor]].
=='''Notes'''==
*This function can technically be used to remove reputation by having Amount be a negative number, however, it is better to use [[RemoveReputation]] for this purpose because with '''AddReputation''' the message will always say "gained" rather than "removed."
=='''See Also'''== *[[GetReputation]] *[[AddReputationExact]] *[[RemoveReputation]]
[[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Reputation Functions]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]]

revision.comment: Made it say 'Removes' instead of 'Adds' for what it does to reputation listed at the top.
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = Removes an exact amount of reputation (Fame/Infamy) with a certain faction.
|returnType = void |referenceType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = FactionID |Type = editorID |Optional = n }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Fame/Infamy |Type = int |Optional = n }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Amount |Type = float |Optional = n }} }}
'''RemoveReputationExact''' removes an ''exact'' amount of reputation to a faction, unlike [[RemoveReputation]], which only allows for preset values (small deed - big deed).
The first parameter determines if Infamy (0) or Fame (1) should be removed, whereas the second parameter determines the exact amount.
=='''Examples'''==
RemoveReputationExact RepNVGoodsprings 0 0.5
Removes 0.5 '''Infamy''' from the Goodsprings faction.
RemoveReputationExact RepNVGoodsprings 1 10
Removes 10 '''Fame''' from the Goodsprings faction.
=='''Notes'''==
*[[RemoveReputation]] and '''RemoveReputationExact''' are only used by default in cases where the player is granted forgiveness. It should not be used, for example, as a way to punish the player for bad deeds by "removing" positive reputation. For that, add infamy using the function [[AddReputation]].
=='''See Also'''== *[[GetReputation]] *[[AddReputationExact]] *[[RemoveReputation]]
[[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Reputation Functions]]

{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = '''EnableLoadingMenu''' forces a loading screen to appear for a few seconds. This loading screen does not use any loading screens specific to the current location. |name = EnableLoadingMenu 1 }}
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Loading Screen Functions]]

{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = Returns the player's current winnings level for the specified casino form. Used as a condition function in dialogue for the casino's npcs. |name = GetCasinoWinningStage |returnVal = 0-4 |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = casino |Type = baseform }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetCasinoWinningsLevel]] [[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Casino Functions]]

{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = Returns the player's current winnings level for the specified casino form. Used as a script function in the casino form's associated script to handle giving the Achievement as well as a dialogue package to an employee to tell the player they've been banned.
[[GetCasinoWinningStage]] does the same, but as a condition function. |name = GetCasinoWinningsLevel |returnVal = 0-4 |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = casino |Type = baseform }} |example = if GetCasinoWinningsLevel TheTopsCasinoData == 1 }} ==See Also== *[[GetCasinoWinningStage]] [[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Casino Functions]]

{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = Returns 1 if a [[Challenge]] has been completed. If the challenge is set to "Recurring", will return 1 if it was completed at least once. |returnVal = 0, 1 |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Challenge |Type = form }} |example = GetChallengeCompleted MyChallenge }} [[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Challenge Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Challenge Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns the actor's [[GetAV|current]] Fatigue [[Actor Value]] divided by their [[GetBaseAV|base]] Fatigue. |name = GetFatiguePercentage |alias = GetFatigue |returnType = float |referenceType = actorREFR |example = 
float fPerc
let fPerc := RaulRef.GetFatiguePercentage
}} ==See Also== *[[GetHealthPercentage]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]
{{Bc|Functions}} A list of function aliases. [[Category:Functions (All)]] [[Category:Functions]]

{{Incomplete}} {{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns true if calling actor has line of sight to the specified object. Can only be called on actors. |name = GetLineOfSight |alias = GetLOS |returnType = bool |referenceType = [actor:ref] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = target |Type = ref }} |example = testActor.GetLineOfSight TestMarker }} ==Notes== * When called on a non-player actor, the supplied ObjectID must be another actor or getLOS will return false, even when the object is within line of sight. * When called on the player... ** ...non-actors can be specified as an objectID. *** If the objectID parameter is not an actor, however, this function is quite slow and should not be run every frame. ** ...it returns true if the ''camera'' can see the object, '''not''' if the player-character can see the object. This means that in Third-Person, GetLOS may return true for objects ''behind'' the player, if they are close enough that they can be seen with the camera. * When called on a disabled actor, it crashes the game. May CTD if GetLOS is called by an actor that was disabled in the previous frame. A delay of a few frames after the actor is enabled will solve this issue. * The field of view for LOS is +/- 95 degrees from the 0 axis line in which the observer (non-player actor, player, or camera) is looking straight ahead. ** All creatures and non-player actors have the same field of view for LOS, regardless of eye placement, or if they even have heads (zombies) or eyes (will-o-wisps). *Will return true with trigger volumes and collision volumes if they are in the calling actors field of view even though they normally aren't visible in game. *Will not return true when checking if a disabled [[reference]] is in view.
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = Sets the [[Actor]] to always show subtitles. |returnType = void |referenceType = ActorREF |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Toggle |Type = int |Value = 0,1 }} |example = SunnyREF.AlwaysShowActorSubtitles 1 }} ==Notes== *This function is cleared every game load.
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions]]

revision.comment: Added to Category:Condition Functions
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = A condition function that returns true if a challenge has "Start disabled" flag. |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = challenge |Type = baseform }} |example = set iLocked to ChallengeLocked NVSomeChallenge }} ==See Also== *[[UnlockChallenge]] *[[GetChallengeFlags]]
[[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Challenge Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Challenge Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Detonates all C4 placed by the specified actor. |name = DetonatePlacedExplosives |alias = DPE |returnType = void |referenceType = [actor:ref] |example = Player.DetonatePlacedExplosives }}
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Projectile Functions]]

{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = Turns Hardcore mode on or off |name = SetHardcore |referenceType = [Actor] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = int |Value = 1/0 }} |example = ref.SetHardcore 1 }}
==Notes== *This function seems to do nothing when called with the player as the reference. Using the function in an [[OnTrigger]] script block is probably best, with the direct or implicit reference the trigger object itself.
==See Also== *[[DisableHardcoreTracking]] *[[IsHardcore]]
[[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns whether the [[SetInChargen]] state is set. |name = GetInCharGen |returnType = inCharGen:1/0 |example = set bInCharGen to GetInCharGen }} ==Notes== * This function is not available as a condition on actor effects. ==See Also== * [[SetInChargen]] * [[IsInCharGen]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]]

#REDIRECT [[Special Variables]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Time Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns 1 if the actor is currently in a force sneak state. |name = GetForceSneak |returnType = success:0/1 |referenceType = actor |example = if (actorRef.GetForceSneak == 1) ; in forced sneak state endif }}
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor State Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

revision.comment: italics
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Resets Experience Points (XP) of the player to zero. It is not called on a reference. |name = ResetXP |example = ResetXP Player XP is reset to zero. }} ==Notes== * This function is used in the character revision script that pops up as you leave Goodsprings in ''New Vegas''.
==See Also== *[[RewardXP]]
[[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Player Functions]]

revision.comment: Added to Category:Condition Functions
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns 1 if actor is ignoring friendly hits. |name = GetIgnoreFriendlyHits |returnVal = ignoringFriendlyHits |returnType = int |example = BobREF.GetIgnoreFriendlyHits Returns 1 if actor is ignoring friendly hits. }} ==See Also== *[[SetIgnoreFriendlyHits]]
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category: Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:AI Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns true if the specified ref is in the given cell. Same functionality as GetInCell, except that this function does not run on a reference -- the actor is supplied as a parameter. |name = GetInCellParam |returnVal = 0, 1 |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Cell |Type = EditorID }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Actor |Type = RefID }} |example = 
GetInCellParam Megaton InternJohnRef
Returns true if Intern John is currently is any Megaton cell.. }} ==Notes== *The CellName has to be a valid interior cell name. Exterior cells will not work. In some cases, it is necessary to create a "dummy" interior cell (e.g. Megaton) in order to use GetInCell for a set of related interior and exterior cells (e.g. all the MegatonXXX cells). ==See Also== *[[GetInSameCell]] *[[GetInWorldspace]] *[[GetInGrid]] (Useful for exteriors) *[[GetInGridInCell]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor State Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Cell Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

revision.text:
{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = Sets the essential state of a specific actor without touching the base, useful when there are multiple actors with the same base loaded in at once (Ghost People for example). This change is saved with the game. |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Flag |Type = boolean }} |example = ActorREF.SetActorRefEssential 1 Sets the referenced actor to essential. }} ==Notes== *SetActorRefEssential '''cannot''' set an actor to unessential if the actor's base is set essential through the GECK or by [[SetEssential]].
==See Also== *[[SetEssential]] *[[IsEssential]]
[[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Functions]]

[[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |name = GetTalkedToPCParam |summary = Returns 1 if the NPC has ever spoken to the player, 0 otherwise. Functionally identical to [[GetTalkedToPC]] but an NPC is passed as an argument instead of using a calling reference. |returnVal = 0, 1 |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Actor |Type = ref }} |example = GetTalkedToPCParam BuddyREF Will return a 1 if Buddy has ever talked to the player, and 0 otherwise. }}
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor State Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor State Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns weight class of a calling actor's equipped armor. Return values are as follows:
0: None
1: Light armor
2: Medium or Heavy armor
|returnVal = 0, 1, 2 |returnType = int |referenceType = Actor |example = Player.GetArmorRatingUpperBody }} ==See Also== *[[GetArmorRating]] *[[GetArmorClass]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Armor Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Opens calling [[Terminal]] reference with hacking minigame. |returnType = void |referenceType = [TerminalREF] |example = TerminalREF.ShowHackingMiniGame ==Notes== *If calling reference is not a Terminal (or missing) the game will crash.
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Terminal Functions]] }}

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Displays interface of a calling [[Terminal]] reference. If a Terminal is locked, hacking minigame will be bypassed. |returnType = void |referenceType = [TerminalREF] |example = TerminalREF.ShowComputersInterface ==Notes== *If calling reference is not a Terminal (or missing) the game will crash.
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Terminal Functions]] }}

{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = Used to set a Securitron [[reference]]'s expression on their monitor. Requires a reference and two strings. |name = SetSecuritronExpression |alias = SetSecExp |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Target |Type = reference }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Face |Type = String }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Expression |Type = String }} |example = SetSecuritronExpression Lucky38UpgradeSecuritron03 Infantry Alert SetSecExp VMQTopsSecMessengerREF Victor Neutral }}
==Notes== * This function persists across saves and game restarts. * This function works based off of the filename of the textures located in the Textures\Creatures\Securitron\ folder. If one wishes to use this function, any custom textures must be added to the aforementioned directory, and they must be named in a similar fashion to the vanilla textures, e.g. CustomTron_Sad.dds. This could then be called via script like so: SetSecuritronExpression SecuritronRef CustomTron Sad
==See Also== *[[SwapTextureOnRef]] - can be used as a non-persistent alternative to this function, see example.
[[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Texture Functions]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]]

{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = Used to open the recipe menu from an [[Actor]] or [[Activator]]. |name = ShowRecipeMenu |alias = Craft |referenceType = [Actor, Activator] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = RecipeCategory |Type = Form }} |example = EDE1REF.Craft AmmoSubRecipes }}
[[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Recipe Functions]]

{{Bc|Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)}} This page lists event handling functions added by the [http://www.nexusmods.com/newvegas/mods/66927 JohnnyGuitar NVSE Plugin].
==See Also== *[[Event Handling]] *[[:Category:Event Handlers (JIP)|JIP Event Handlers]] [[Category:Functions (All)]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Checks if an item that matches a [[Form Type IDs | Form Type]] is currently being used or activated, returning 1 if it does. The full name of the Form Type must be passed, '''NOT''' its numeric ID. |returnVal = 0/1 |returnType = bool |referenceType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = FormType |Type = string }} |example = GetIsUsedItemType Activator Returns 1 if an activator is being used. }} ==See Also== *[[GetIsUsedItemEquipType]] *[[GetIsUsedItem]] [[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Object Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

[[Player]] must meet Casino requirements for the menu to display, such as having Casino Chips in the inventory and not being banned from the casino. |alias = sslmp |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Casino |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = minBet |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = maxBet |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = maxWinnings |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = ShowSlotMachineMenuParams TheTopsCasinoData 1 50 500 }} [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Casino Functions]]

{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = Displays Roulette menu. Player must meet Casino requirements for the menu to display, such as having Casino Chips in the inventory and not being banned from the casino. |alias = srmp |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Casino |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = minBet |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = maxBet |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = maxWinnings |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = ShowRouletteMenuParams TheTopsCasinoData 1 50 500 }} [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Casino Functions]]

{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = Displays the [[MenuMode|BlackJack menu]]. Player must meet Casino requirements for the menu to display, such as having Casino Chips in the inventory and not being banned from the casino. |alias = sbjmp |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Casino |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = minBet |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = maxBet |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = maxWinnings |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = ShowBlackJackMenuParams TheTopsCasinoData 1 50 500 }} [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Casino Functions]]

{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = Returns 1 if hardcore mode has always been activated in the game. This is used to check if player fails requirements before adding achievement for hardcore mode. |name = IsAlwaysHardcore |alias = behemoth |returnVal = 0, 1 |returnType = int |referenceType = [Actor] |example = set bAlwaysHardcore to PlayerRef.IsAlwaysHardcore }} ==See Also== [[IsHardcore]] [[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

{{Bc|Functions}}
An xNVSE plugin that allows you to edit and save scripts in GECK and update them live into the game without needing to restart it.
It also allows scripts to be edited and saved with any Text Editor (with the GECK still open). A nice utility to have for any mod maker of Fallout New Vegas.
Download here: [https://www.nexusmods.com/newvegas/mods/70962?tab=description nexusmods.com]
[[Category:Functions (All)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:NVSE]] [[Category:Mod Tools]]

{{Function |origin = VEGAS1 |summary = A condition function that returns 1 if the actor is currently in the specified region. |name = PlayerInRegion |returnVal = 0, 1 |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Type = RegionID }} |example = PlayerInRegion VMapGoodspringsRegion }}
== Notes == *If you're in a sub-region of a larger region, it will return 1 for both.
==See Also== *[[GetInWorldspace]] *[[GetInCell]] *[[IsPlayerInRegion]] - cannot be used as a condition, but can be used in scripts.
[[Category:Functions (Vegas)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Player Functions]]

{{Bc|Functions}} This page lists functions added by the [https://www.nexusmods.com/newvegas/mods/73160 SUP NVSE Plugin]
[[Category:Functions (All)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:NVSE]]

revision.comment: italics
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = BookMenu |summary = Activates the [[Book]] [[calling reference]] manually, opening the restored book menu for the given Book. Optionally allows the "Take" button to be hidden. |name = ShowBookMenu |referenceType = bookReference |Optional = optional |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = showTakeButton |Type = 0/1 |Optional = optional }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = book |Type = baseform |Optional = optional }} }} ==Notes== * This function behaves similarly to doing 'bookref.activate player', with the exception of having an optional argument which allows the "Take" button to be hidden. * This ordinarily unused in ''Fallout 3''/''Fallout: New Vegas'', but can be restored with [[:Category:Functions (BookMenu)|Book Menu Restored]].
==See Also== *[[Activate]]
[[Category:Functions (BookMenu)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Book Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions]]

{{Function |origin = GECK1 |summary = Returns 1 if the calling actor belongs to at least one evil faction and no non-evil factions. |name = IsActorEvil |returnType = bool |referenceType = [actor:ref] |example = if BuddyRef.IsActorEvil ;do evil stuff }} ==Notes== * This function only works on NPCs, not creatures. No creatures are considered evil, regardless of which factions they belong to.
[[Category:Functions (GECK)]] [[Category: Functions]] [[Category:Faction Functions]] [[Category: Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Bc|Functions}}
kNVSE, or kNVSE Animation Plugin, is an [[NVSE]] plugin that enables support in the engine for custom weapon animations, custom walking animations and a lot more.
Despite being named similarly to xNVSE, which replaces NVSE, kNVSE is an add-on for xNVSE, and does not replace it.
It is available [https://www.nexusmods.com/newvegas/mods/71336?tab=description here].
==Text keys==
kNVSE supplies numerous custom text keys which can be placed in an animation's KF file or through kNVSE's script functions.
* '''interruptLoop''': AttackLoop will behave more like AttackLoopIS where the animation gets interrupted at the end. Makes for superior automatic weapon animations. It also allows AttackLoop and AttackLoopIS animations to finish before Aim or AimIS animations are played again. * '''burstFire''': Allows multiple “hit” and optionally “eject” keys to activate within a single animation. * '''respectEndKey''': First person animation’s text key won’t be tied to the third person animation text keys. * '''noBlend''': Disables blending into this animation. It is recommended to use this for first person animations but not third person animations. * '''Script''': MyUDFScript: A user defined function script will be called once the time value is reached. In this example the script has the editor ID MyUDFScript * '''blendToReloadLoop''': modifies the behavior of Reload(X/Y/Z/W)Start anim group so that it blends directly to ReloadLoop instead of blending first to Aim, then to - '''ReloadLoop''' which causes a significant pause. Requires both anims to have different priorities. * '''soundPath''': path: Plays sound (.wav file relative to Datafolder) at time specified in value * '''scriptLine''': print "This will print": Runs script line at time specified in value. %r may be used to specify newline for multiline scripts. * '''allowAttack''': Allow the player to attack after this time in reload or equip animations. * '''noFix''': kNVSE on load will try to fix some presumed errors in animation files. If kNVSE is something erroneously to your anim, place this text key in your anim and kNVSE will not try to fix it. * '''allowClampInLocomotion''': By default the game only allows LOOP anims to be registered as locomotion anims. If you need your animation to bypass this, add this text key.
[[Category:Functions (All)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:NVSE]] [[Category:Mod Tools]]

{{Bc|Functions}} This page lists functions added by Tale of Two Wastelands.
[https://taleoftwowastelands.com/ Tale of Two Wastelands] is a large mod that brings the entirety of Fallout 3 into the Fallout New Vegas engine, allowing play of both wastelands, with travel between the wastes. [[Category:Functions (All)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:NVSE]]

{{Bc|Functions}}
The MCM, or Mod Configuration Menu, is a mod that allows configuration of options via the game's main menu for any external mod that supports it, without the requirement for it being a master.
It also includes a plugin for [[NVSE]] to facilitate its features, although if installed, these [[:Category:Functions_(MCM)|functions]] are available to anyone.
The Fallout: New Vegas version is available [http://www.nexusmods.com/newvegas/mods/42507 here]. [[Category:Functions (All)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:NVSE]] [[Category:Mod Tools]]

{{Bc|Functions}} This page lists functions added by [[Book_Menu_Restored_Plugin|Book Menu Restored]] plugin for ([https://www.nexusmods.com/fallout3/mods/23794 Fallout 3]) and ([https://www.nexusmods.com/newvegas/mods/67278 Fallout: New Vegas]). [[Category:Functions (All)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:NVSE]]

page.title: Complete List of Functions in Fallout 3

revision.comment: italics
revision.text:
The following is a complete list of all registered functions available in ''Fallout 3'' and its [[FOSE]] extenders. Note that some may be [[Console Functions|console only]], and unavailable in the GECK. Some may also be [[Unused Functions|unused and non-functional]], while others may be undocumented.
'''See also:''' *[[:Category:Functions_(All)|Functions (All)]] *[[:Category:Scripting|Scripting]] *[[:Category:Functions|Functions (Category)]] *[[:Category:Function Types|Functions Grouped By Type]]

{{Function |name = GetSelfAlt |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.2.8 |summary = Returns the calling reference, or a null form if there is none. Only useful when this information is accessed externally, or when the scripted reference needs to be passed as a parameter to a function like [[PushActorAway]] Unlike [[GetSelf]], this will always return the calling reference, even if it happens to be a non-Persistent [[Item]]. If that check is not needed, then this is a strictly faster version of that function. |alias = ThisAlt |example = ref rSelf let rSelf := GetSelfAlt player.PushActorAway rSelf 5 }} ==Notes== While functions that take an implied reference can be cast without needing a variable or call to [[GetSelfAlt]], like so: 
CIOS someSpell
Doing so may not be clear, and makes the code less portable if moved to a different script that does not support the implied reference. For this reason, modders should prefer the more expressive syntax: 
ref rSelf
let rSelf := GetSelfAlt
rSelf.CIOS someSpell
Not only for [[CIOS]], but for all "reference methods" that require a reference, like CIOS. ==See Also== *[[GetSelf]] *[[GetContainer]] [[Category:Functions (NVSE)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Reference Variable Functions]] [[Category:ID Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.3.2 |summary = Disables some or all of the player's controls.
Unlike [[DisablePlayerControls]], the changes are NOT savebaked, and are cleared whenever a save is loaded. * Consider using [[GetGameLoaded]] to re-apply changes, or something similar.
Furthermore, disabling is stored on a per-mod basis, so if two mods disable a control at some point, it will be disabled until both of them re-enable it, or until the save is reloaded. *This makes it easier to keep mods that disable controls compatible with each other.
Default (no parameters) is equivalent to:
DisablePlayerControlsAlt 1 1 1 1 0 0 0 ; Movement, Pipboy, Fighting and POV controls are disabled |name = DisablePlayerControlsAlt |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = MovementFlag (0/1), |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = PipboyFlag (0/1), |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = FightingFlag (0/1), |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = POVFlag (0/1), |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = LookingFlag (0/1), |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = RolloverTextFlag (0/1), |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = SneakingFlag (0/1) |Optional = y }} |example = DisablePlayerControlsAlt Player's Movement, Pipboy, Fighting and POV controls are disabled, but everything else is unaffected.
DisablePlayerControlsAlt 0 0 1 0 0 Player's ability to equip or use a weapon is disabled; all other controls are unaffected. }}
===Flag Definitions===
; MovementFlag : Player movement is disabled. Activation of world objects is disabled. HUD is partially disabled: Action Points, Health, Crosshair.
; PipBoyFlag : Player cannot bring up the pip-boy interface. The "Wait" menu will also be disabled.
; FightingFlag : Player cannot bring up a weapon. If the player currently has a weapon out, the weapon will be holstered.
; POVFlag : Player cannot go into 3rd person view. If currently in 3rd person, the view is forced into first person.
; LookingFlag : Player looking is disabled -- the player's view is locked into its current position.
; RolloverTextFlag : No rollover text on world objects.
; SneakingFlag : Player cannot go into sneak mode. If currently sneaking, forces you out of sneak mode.
; There are custom new flags, but they are exclusive to [[DisablePlayerControlsAltEx]].
==See Also== *[[DisablePlayerControlsAltEx]] - Same as this but takes a single [[Bitmask]] argument instead, and has custom new flags. *[[DisablePlayerControls]] *[[EnablePlayerControls]] / [[EnablePlayerControlsAlt]] / [[EnablePlayerControlsAltEx]] *[[GetPlayerControlsDisabled]] / [[GetPlayerControlsDisabledAlt]] / [[GetPlayerControlsDisabledAltEx]] *[[DisableControl]] *[[ToggleVanityWheel]]
[[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Functions (NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.3.2 |summary = Enables the player's controls after use of [[DisablePlayerControlsAlt]] / [[DisablePlayerControlsAltEx]].
Disabling and enabling is stored on a per-mod basis, so if two mods disable a control at some point, it will be disabled until both of them re-enable it, or until the save is reloaded.
This will NOT override the controls disabled with the vanilla [[DisablePlayerControls]].
Default (no parameters) is equivalent to having *all* the Flag arguments at 1, meaning it will re-enable all controls. |name = EnablePlayerControlsAlt |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = MovementFlag (0/1), |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = PipboyFlag (0/1), |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = FightingFlag (0/1), |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = POVFlag (0/1), |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = LookingFlag (0/1), |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = RolloverTextFlag (0/1), |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = SneakingFlag (0/1) |Optional = y }} |example = EnablePlayerControlsAlt Player's controls are completely enabled, at least from the calling mod's point of view. If other mods are still disabling those controls, then they'll stay disabled until those other mods re-enable the control too.
On top of this, vanilla [[DisablePlayerControls]] can also leave controls disabled, and the only way to turn it back on at that point is to use vanilla [[EnablePlayerControls]]. However, it shouldn't typically be necessary to go override whatever vanilla scripts are currently doing, so this can safely be ignored.
EnablePlayerControlsAlt 1 1 0 Turns back on ability to move and use the pip-boy, but not to fight (assuming fighting was currently disabled). Also re-enables all other flags. }}
==Notes== *See [[DisablePlayerControlsAlt]] for flag definitions.
==See Also== *[[EnablePlayerControlsAltEx]] - Same as this but takes a single [[Bitmask]] argument instead. *[[EnablePlayerControls]] *[[DisablePlayerControls]] / [[DisablePlayerControlsAlt]] / [[DisablePlayerControlsAltEx]] *[[GetPlayerControlsDisabled]] / [[GetPlayerControlsDisabledAlt]] / [[GetPlayerControlsDisabledAltEx]] *[[DisableControl]] *[[ToggleVanityWheel]]
[[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Functions (NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.3.2 |summary = Returns true (1) if any of the specified player controls are currently disabled, and false (0) otherwise. Disabling and enabling is stored on a per-mod basis, so if two mods disable a control at some point, it will be disabled until both of them re-enable it, or until the save is reloaded. The ''disabledHow'' arg can be used to check how the controls were disabled. Here are its possible values and uses: 
 If 0, check if controls are disabled by calling mod (DisablePlayerControls*Alt/AltEx* only)
 If 1, ... disabled by any mod (DisablePlayerControls*Alt/AltEx* only)
 If 2, ... disabled by any mod OR disabled by vanilla DisablePlayerControls
Default (no parameters) is equivalent to having ''disabledHow'' at 0, and *all* the Flag arguments at 1, meaning it will check if any of the flags are disabled by the calling mod. |name = GetPlayerControlsDisabledAlt |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = disabledHow |Type = int |Optional = y |Value = 0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = MovementFlag (0/1), |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = PipboyFlag (0/1), |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = FightingFlag (0/1), |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = POVFlag (0/1), |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = LookingFlag (0/1), |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = RolloverTextFlag (0/1), |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = SneakingFlag (0/1) |Optional = y }} |example = GetPlayerControlsDisabledAlt Returns 1 if any of the player's controls are currently disabled by the calling mod. GetPlayerControlsDisabledAlt 0 1 0 0 0 Returns 1 if the player's Pip-Boy flag or if any flags starting from RolloverTextFlag are disabled by the calling mod. If you want to get really specific without having to specify a bunch of filler args, consider using [[GetPlayerControlsDisabledAltEx]]. DisablePlayerControlsAlt 0 1 0 1 0 ; will disable pipboy and POV switch. GetPlayerControlsDisabledAlt 0 1 0 0 0 ; returns 1 GetPlayerControlsDisabledAlt 0 0 0 1 0 ; returns 1 GetPlayerControlsDisabledAlt 0 1 0 1 0 ; returns 1 GetPlayerControlsDisabledAlt 1 1 1 1 1 ; returns 1 GetPlayerControlsDisabledAlt 0 0 0 0 0 ; returns 0 GetPlayerControlsDisabledAlt 1 0 1 0 1 ; returns 0 }} ==Notes== *See [[DisablePlayerControlsAlt]] for flag definitions. ==See Also== *[[GetPlayerControlsDisabledAltEx]] - Same as this but takes a single [[Bitmask]] argument instead. *[[GetPlayerControlsDisabled]] *[[EnablePlayerControls]] / [[EnablePlayerControlsAlt]] / [[EnablePlayerControlsAltEx]] *[[DisablePlayerControls]] / [[DisablePlayerControlsAlt]] / [[DisablePlayerControlsAltEx]] *[[DisableControl]] *[[ToggleVanityWheel]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Functions (NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.3.2 |summary = Disables some or all of the player's controls. Unlike [[DisablePlayerControls]], the changes are NOT savebaked, and are cleared whenever a save is loaded. * Consider using [[GetGameLoaded]] to re-apply changes, or something similar. Furthermore, disabling is stored on a per-mod basis, so if two mods disable a control at some point, it will be disabled until both of them re-enable it, or until the save is reloaded. *This makes it easier to keep mods that disable controls compatible with each other. Unlike [[DisablePlayerControlsAlt]], this function takes a single arg, ''flags''. It is a [[Bitmask]], with each bit representing a flag for a control to disable. See the '''Flag Definitions''' section for more info. Default (no parameters) is equivalent to: 
 DisablePlayerControlsAltEx ((1 << 0) | (1 << 2) | (1 << 3) | (1 << 4)) 
 ; Movement, Pipboy, Fighting and POV controls are disabled
If you are unfamiliar with the binary OR operator, "|", or the binary bit-shifting operator "<<", see the [[NVSE Expressions]] page. This example also makes use of an [[Inline Expression]]. |name = DisablePlayerControlsAltEx |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = flags |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = DisablePlayerControlsAltEx Player's Movement, Pipboy, Fighting and POV controls are disabled, but everything else is unaffected. DisablePlayerControlsAltEx (1 << 3) Player's ability to equip or use a weapon is disabled; all other controls are unaffected. Equivalent to using [[DisablePlayerControlsAlt]] like so: DisablePlayerControlsAlt 0 0 1 0 0 Note that the flag # used in [[DisablePlayerControlsAltEx]] and the arg position used in [[DisablePlayerControlsAlt]] do not match in this example; the Fighting flag is the fourth bit (bit #3, remember that bit #0 is the first), but it is the third arg in [[DisablePlayerControlsAlt]]. this is intentional, because the vanilla game itself has the bits ordered in a separate way than how the args were ordered for [[DisablePlayerControls]]. }} {{Template:DisablePlayerControlsAltEx_FLAGS}} ==See Also== *[[DisablePlayerControls]] / [[DisablePlayerControlsAlt]] *[[EnablePlayerControls]] / [[EnablePlayerControlsAlt]] / [[EnablePlayerControlsAltEx]] *[[GetPlayerControlsDisabled]] / [[GetPlayerControlsDisabledAlt]] / [[GetPlayerControlsDisabledAltEx]] *[[DisableControl]] *[[ToggleVanityWheel]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Functions (NVSE)]] [[Category:Bit Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.3.2 |summary = Enables the player's controls after use of [[DisablePlayerControlsAlt]] / [[DisablePlayerControlsAltEx]]. Disabling and enabling is stored on a per-mod basis, so if two mods disable a control at some point, it will be disabled until both of them re-enable it, or until the save is reloaded. This will NOT override the controls disabled with the vanilla [[DisablePlayerControls]]. Unlike [[EnablePlayerControlsAlt]], this function takes a single arg, ''flags''. It is a [[Bitmask]], with each bit representing a flag for a control to disable. See the '''Flag Definitions''' section for more info. Default (no parameters) is equivalent to having *all* the flag bits at 1, meaning it will re-enable all controls. |name = EnablePlayerControlsAltEx |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = flags |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = EnablePlayerControlsAltEx Player's controls are completely enabled, at least from the calling mod's point of view. If other mods are still disabling those controls, then they'll stay disabled until those other mods re-enable the control too. On top of this, vanilla [[DisablePlayerControls]] can also leave controls disabled, and the only way to turn it back on at that point is to use vanilla [[EnablePlayerControls]]. However, it shouldn't typically be necessary to go override whatever vanilla scripts are currently doing, so this can safely be ignored. 
EnablePlayerControlsAltEx ((1 << 0) | (1 << 1))
Re-enables the '''Movement''' and '''Looking''' flags, from the calling mod's point of view. }} {{Template:DisablePlayerControlsAltEx_FLAGS}} ==See Also== *[[EnablePlayerControls]] / [[EnablePlayerControlsAlt]] *[[DisablePlayerControls]] / [[DisablePlayerControlsAlt]] / [[DisablePlayerControlsAltEx]] *[[GetPlayerControlsDisabled]] / [[GetPlayerControlsDisabledAlt]] / [[GetPlayerControlsDisabledAltEx]] *[[DisableControl]] *[[ToggleVanityWheel]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Functions (NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.3.2 |summary = Returns true (1) if any of the specified player controls are currently disabled, and false (0) otherwise. Disabling and enabling is stored on a per-mod basis, so if two mods disable a control at some point, it will be disabled until both of them re-enable it, or until the save is reloaded. The ''disabledHow'' arg can be used to check how the controls were disabled. Here are its possible values and uses: 
 If 0, check if controls are disabled by calling mod (DisablePlayerControls*Alt/AltEx* only)
 If 1, ... disabled by any mod (DisablePlayerControls*Alt/AltEx* only)
 If 2, ... disabled by any mod OR disabled by vanilla DisablePlayerControls
Unlike [[GetPlayerControlsDisabledAlt]], this function takes a single arg, ''flags''. It is a [[Bitmask]], with each bit representing a flag for a control to disable. See the '''Flag Definitions''' section for more info. Default (no parameters) is equivalent to having ''disabledHow'' at 0, and *all* the flag bits at 1, meaning it will check if any of the flags are disabled by the calling mod. |name = GetPlayerControlsDisabledAltEx |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = disabledHow |Type = int |Optional = y |Value = 0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flags |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = GetPlayerControlsDisabledAltEx Returns 1 if any of the player's controls are currently disabled by the calling mod. 
GetPlayerControlsDisabledAlt 0 (1 << 2)
Returns 1 if the player's Pip-Boy is currently disabled by the calling mod. }} {{Template:DisablePlayerControlsAltEx_FLAGS}} ==See Also== *[[GetPlayerControlsDisabled]] / [[GetPlayerControlsDisabledAlt]] *[[EnablePlayerControls]] / [[EnablePlayerControlsAlt]] / [[EnablePlayerControlsAltEx]] *[[DisablePlayerControls]] / [[DisablePlayerControlsAlt]] / [[DisablePlayerControlsAltEx]] *[[DisableControl]] *[[ToggleVanityWheel]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Functions (NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Makes the [[Actor]] [[calling reference]] fire a specified [[Weapon]] [[BaseForm]]'s projectile(s) (equivalent to one regular shot, but some weapons like shotguns fire multiple pellets per shot). Unlike [[FireWeapon]], this has an optional argument to specify the ''Node'' that the shot should be spawned from. |name = FireWeaponEx |returnType = void |referenceType = actorRefr |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Weapon |Type = BaseForm }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Node |Type = string |Optional = y }} |example = 
let rWeapon := player.GetEquippedWeapon 
player.FireWeapon rWeapon "ProjectileNode2"
}} ==See Also== *[[FireWeapon]] *[[UseWeapon]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:Combat Functions]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Weapon Functions (JIP)]]

revision.text:
{{Bc|Functions}}
Overcharge is an NVSE plugin that serves as a framework for implementing new gameplay mechanics, with a primary focus on energy weapon systems.
It is available [https://www.nexusmods.com/newvegas/mods/95019 here].
== Usage ==
Overcharge provides a framework for both users and mod authors to configure existing weapons and weapons not included in the base game. It operates on a per-weapon basis, with behavior defined through INI configuration files loaded from the plugin’s config directory, (NVSE/OCWeapons/).
Key features of Overcharge include a fully functional weapon heat and overheat system, charge shots, stat modification that scales with heat and skill level, color customizable particles and meshes, and other configurable gameplay enhancements.
For a more detailed overview on how to configure your weapons using Overcharge, see [[Overcharge Configuration]].
[[Category:Functions (All)]] [[Category:Functions]] [[Category:NVSE]] [[Category:Mod Tools]]

{{Function |origin = hNVSE |originVersion = 1.2 |summary = Returns an exterior cell's region list as an array. |name = GetCellRegions |returnType = array |conditionFunc = Script |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = cell |Type = ref }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetPlayerRegions]]
==Notes== *Because exterior cells are not normally persistent, you may need to pass the cell's FormID as the argument rather than its EditorID.
[[Category:Functions (hNVSE)]] [[Category:Cell_Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = hNVSE |originVersion = 1.1 |summary = Sets the northwest and southeast cell coordinates of a worldspace's map. |name = SetWorldspaceMapBounds |condit..."
revision.text: {{Function |origin = hNVSE |originVersion = 1.1 |summary = Sets the northwest and southeast cell coordinates of a worldspace's map. |name = SetWorldspaceMapBounds |conditionFunc = Script |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = worldspace |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = xCoordNW |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = yCoordNW |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = xCoordSE |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = yCoordSE |Type = int }} }} [[Category:Functions (hNVSE)]] [[Category:World_Space_Functions]] [[Category:Map_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIPPPLN |summary = Attaches an entire form’s runtime model (all runtime nodes created via [[AttachModel]]/[[InsertNode]] or present in the base model) to a selected NiNode in the 3D model of the specified object form. This function is useful for transferring a set of attached models from one form to another. The function takes '''3(+) arguments''', as follows: *'''refOrForm''' – Either an '''object [[reference]]''' or a '''[[base form]]'''. In the former case, the models will be attached to the target node in the 3D model of that specific reference only, whereas in the latter case, they will be attached in the 3D model of any reference instance of the base form. *'''formToAttach''' – A reference or base form whose attached models will be used. All NiNodes and models that were previously attached to this form (via [[AttachModel]] or [[InsertNode]]) will be copied and attached to the target form. *'''addOrRemove''' – Either attach or remove the models. Use one of the following values: 
0    Remove; Pending reload
1    Attach; Pending reload
2    Remove; Instantaneous
3    Attach; Instantaneous
:*'''Pending reload''' – Changes will be applied once either the object's 3D is (re)loaded by the game, or [[Update3D]] is called on it. :*'''Instantaneous''' – Changes will be applied instantly. *'''targetAndSuffix''' – An '''[[String Formatting|optional formatted string]]''' containing the name ('''case-sensitive''') of an existing NiNode in the target form’s 3D model and, separated by a vertical bar '''"|"''', an optional suffix to append to the name of every block in the attached form. The suffix (maximum 15 characters) is enclosed by asterisks and is useful to prevent name collisions when multiple copies of the same form are attached. If omitted, the function attaches the models to the root NiNode of the target form. *'''formatVars (up to 20)''' – Optional variables used in the formatted string. |name = AttachFormModel |returnType = successful:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Target |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = ToAttach |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = addOrRemove |Type = int |Value = 0-3 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = targetAndSuffix |Type = formattedString |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = formatVars (up to 20) |Optional = y }} |example = 
AttachModel HoneyMesquitePickable 1 "NVHoneyMesquite|clutter\food\vodka01.nif"  ; attach a bottle to NVHoneyMesquite
AttachFormModel Player HoneyMesquitePickable 3 "Bip01 Head|*_MESQ*"
The first call attaches a vodka bottle NIF to the “NVHoneyMesquite” node in every instance of '''HoneyMesquitePickable'''. The second call transfers that entire model (including the bottle) to the ‘‘Bip01 Head’’ node of the '''Player''', appending the suffix ‘‘_MESQ’’ to all blocks. }} ==Notes== * Any changes made by this function remain in effect for the duration of the current game session, or until removed. * On successful attachment, the function returns '''1'''; otherwise it returns '''0'''. * Using [[InsertNode]]/AttachModel/AttachFormModel on dynamic references will only work with mode '''3'''. For instantaneous removal of attached nodes/models from temporary references, use [[RemoveNifBlock]]. * Suffixes are appended immediately before attaching; they do not change the names of existing nodes in the original form. ==See Also== * [[AttachModel]] * [[CopyRuntimeNodes]] * [[HasRuntimeNode]] * [[GetRuntimeNodes]] * [[EraseRuntimeNode]] * [[InsertNode]] * [[ModelHasBlock]] [[Category:Functions (JIP PP LN)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Model Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Attaches a complete copy of a model from a selected NIF file to an existing NiNode in the 3D model of the specified object form. The function takes 3(+) arguments, as follows: *'''refOrForm''' - Either an '''object [[reference]]''' or a '''[[base form]]'''. In the former case, the model will be attached to the target node in the 3D model of that specific reference only, whereas in the latter case, it will be attached in the 3D model of any reference instance of the base form. *'''addOrRemove''' - Either attach, or remove a previously attached model. Use one of the following values: 
0	Remove; Pending reload
1	Attach; Pending reload
2	Remove; Instantaneous
3	Attach; Instantaneous
:*'''Pending reload''' - Changes will be applied once either the object's 3D is (re)loaded by the game, or [[Update3D]] is called on it. :*'''Instantaneous''' - Changes will be applied instantly. *'''targetAndFile''' - A '''[[String Formatting|formatted string]]''' containing the name ('''case-sensitive''') of an existing NiNode in the object's 3D model and, separated by a vertical bar '''"|"''', the path of the '''NIF file''' containing the model (NOT case-sensitive). Note that the path must be relative to '''..\Data\Meshes\''' :*'''Block name suffixes''' - The function can optionally append a suffix to the name of every block in the attached NIF. This is useful when attaching multiple copies of the same NIF to a model, and/or for avoiding an outcome where the model contains multiple blocks with the same name (which may cause various issues). Suffixes have a character limit of 15. ::To specify a suffix, add the suffix string immediately before the NIF path, enclosed by asterisks. For example: '''"Bip01 R Thigh|*_CPY1*weapons\9mmSMG.nif"'''. |name = AttachModel |returnType = successful:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = refOrForm |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = addOrRemove |Type = int |Value = 0-3 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = targetAndFile |Type = formattedString }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = formatVars(up to 20) |Optional = y }} |example = 
AttachModel PlayerRef 3 "Bip01 Head|*_SNIG*clutter\junk\tincan01.nif"
The above will '''instantly''' ATTACH a copy of '''tincan01.nif''', with the suffix '''"_SNIG"''' appended to every block name, to the '''"Bip01 Head"''' node in the 3D model of the '''PlayerRef'''. 
AttachModel PlayerRef 2 "Bip01 Head|*_SNIG*clutter\junk\tincan01.nif"
The reverse of previous example. This will '''instantly''' REMOVE '''tincan01.nif''', with the suffix '''"_SNIG"''', from the '''"Bip01 Head"''' node. 
AttachModel HoneyMesquitePickable 1 "NVHoneyMesquite|clutter\food\vodka01.nif"
The above will ATTACH a copy of '''vodka01.nif''' to the '''"NVHoneyMesquite"''' node in the 3D model of every instance of '''HoneyMesquitePickable'''. 
AttachModel HoneyMesquitePickable 0 "NVHoneyMesquite|clutter\food\vodka01.nif"
The reverse of previous example. }} ==JIP PP LN== In '''version 57.45''' and above, the following enhancements are available with the JIP PP LN plugin: * '''Sparse Node Paths''': You can now specify a '''partial''' path to the target node. This allows node insertion under deeply nested or ambiguous paths by matching only key segments of the path. * This resolves issues where multiple nodes with the same name exist in different branches of the model hierarchy. * Partial path segments are delimited using backslashes (`\`), just like in [[AttachModel]]. ==JIP PP LN Example== 
AttachModel Player 3 "Weapon\##Clip|*Suffix*clutter\food\vodka01.nif"
This will search player model for a node named "##Clip" that is somewhere under a parent node named "Weapon", and attach the model there. This prevents misattachment when multiple identical node names exist. ==Notes== *Any changes made by this function remain in effect for the duration of the current game session, or until removed. *On successful insertion, the function returns 1, otherwise 0. *Using [[InsertNode]]/AttachModel on dynamic refs will only work with mode 3. For instantaneous removal of attached nodes/models from temporary refs, you'll need to use [[RemoveNifBlock]]. *The new sparse path matching logic can resolve nested nodes that previously could not be uniquely identified due to duplicate names in different branches of the model tree. *To detect if JIP PP LN is available, use [[GetPluginVersion]] "JIP PP LN". ==See Also== *[[InsertNode]] *[[AttachFormModel]] *[[HasRuntimeNode]] *[[ModelHasBlock]] *[[SetNifBlockTranslation]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions (JIP PP LN)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIPPPLN |summary = Moves the calling reference straight down to the nearest floor/collision surface beneath it — identical to pressing F in the GECK render window. Adjusts vertical position only; orientation and X/Y remain unchanged. |name = ClampToGround |referenceType = reference |example = 
MyMarkerRef.ClampToGround

let createRef := PlayerRef.PlaceAtMe SomeCrateStatic
createRef.ClampToGround
}} ==Examples== ; Drop a newly placed object to the ground 
let createRef := PlayerRef.PlaceAtMe SomeCrateStatic
createRef.ClampToGround
; Snap an existing marker to the floor beneath it 
MyMarkerRef.ClampToGround
==Notes== * Works only on '''references''' (not base forms). * The object will be moved directly downward until it collides with the floor or terrain below. * Rotation/orientation is not modified. ==See Also== * [[Update3D]] * [[MoveTo]] * [[SetPos]] * [[SetAngle]] [[Category:Functions (JIP PP LN)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Movement Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIPPPLN |summary = Removes all RuntimeNodes added by your mod from the specified object in one call. Nodes created by other mods are left intact. |name =..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIPPPLN |summary = Removes all RuntimeNodes added by your mod from the specified object in one call. Nodes created by other mods are left intact. |name = ClearRuntimeNodes |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = object |Type = form }} |example = ClearRuntimeNodes Player Remove all of your mod's runtime nodes from the player 
if ClearRuntimeNodes WeapNVServiceRifle > 0
    AttachModel WeapNVServiceRifle 3 "Weapon|LeftBarrelweapons\attachments\silencer01.nif"
endif
Clear, then rebuild a custom setup on a weapon }} ==Notes== *Affects only nodes your mod registered (e.g., via [[InsertNode]] and [[AttachModel]]). Other mods’ runtime nodes are not touched. *If you need to remove a specific node instead of everything, use [[InsertNode]] or [[AttachModel]]. ==See Also== *[[InsertNode]] *[[AttachModel]] *[[GetRuntimeNodes]] *[[CopyRuntimeNodes]] *[[HasRuntimeNode]] [[Category:Functions (JIP PP LN)]] [[Category:Model Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIPPPLN |summary = Copies the '''RuntimeNodes''' from one form to another. This is much faster than manually looping [[AttachModel]]/[[InsertNode]]/[[AttachFormModel]]. Use it to create an exact copy of a form’s runtime setup, or to concatenate another form’s RuntimeNodes onto the target. |name = CopyRuntimeNodes |returnType = successful:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = toForm |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = fromForm |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = copyMode |Type = int |Optional = y |Value = 0–1 }} }} ==Description== Copies the internal runtime node list (attachments and inserted nodes) from '''fromForm''' to '''toForm''' without loading or parsing NIFs. Preserves suffixes, sparse paths, and insertion types. ==Modes== * '''0 – Overwrite (default)''': Replace ''toForm'''s existing RuntimeNodes with a copy of ''fromForm'''s list. * '''1 – Concatenate''': Append ''fromForm'''s RuntimeNodes to ''toForm'''s existing list (cat). ==Example== ; Overwrite the player’s runtime nodes with those from a prepared template form 
set bSuccessful to CopyRuntimeNodes Player TemplateWeapon 0
if bSuccessful
   Update3D Player
endif
; Concatenate: stack another form’s attachments onto an existing setup 
CopyRuntimeNodes WeapNVServiceRifle RifleBase 0
CopyRuntimeNodes WeapNVServiceRifle SuppressorKit 1

; Optional: update immediately
Update3D WeapNVServiceRifleRef
Start with RifleBase’s runtime setup, then add SuppressorKit’s runtime parts ; Batch cloning: make multiple actors share the same runtime configuration 
CopyRuntimeNodes npcB npcA 0
CopyRuntimeNodes npcC npcA 0
npcA is already configured at runtime; copy to others efficiently ==Notes== * Operates on the in-memory '''RuntimeNodes''' lists (no full NIF scan), so it’s significantly faster than calling [[AttachModel]]/[[InsertNode]]/[[AttachFormModel]] repeatedly. * Works with either a specific reference or a base form. When a base form is used as '''toForm''', the copy affects all instances when their models are (re)built. * Changes take effect on the next 3D rebuild. Call [[Update3D]] on a reference to apply immediately. * Overwrite (mode 0) discards existing RuntimeNodes on '''toForm''' before copying. * Concatenate (mode 1) appends entries. If you want to avoid duplicates, pair with [[HasRuntimeNode]] checks. * Returns '''1''' on success, '''0''' otherwise. ==See Also== * [[Runtime Nodes]] * [[HasRuntimeNode]] * [[GetRuntimeNodes]] * [[ClearRuntimeNodes]] * [[AttachModel]] * [[InsertNode]] * [[AttachFormModel]] [[Category:Functions (JIP PP LN)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Model Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIPPPLN |summary = Returns a two-dimensional '''[[array_var]]''' containing detailed information about an actor’s current active effects. The array is specifically designed to work with [[SetActiveEffectsInfo]]. |name = GetActiveEffectsInfo |returnType = activeEffects:array_var |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = effectFilter |Type = int/ref |Optional = y |Description = Filters which types of effects are included. See [[#Effect Filters|Effect Filters]] below. }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = tempFilter |Type = bool |Optional = y |Description = Filters between temporary and permanent effects. See [[#Temp Filters|Temp Filters]] below. }} }} == Overview == This function lets you retrieve all currently active effects on a reference (actor). It returns a two-dimensional array, where each element contains key-value pairs describing one active effect. You can then use [[SetActiveEffectsInfo]] to modify certain entries. == Static Entries (Read-Only) == These entries are always present for each effect and cannot be modified. {| class="wikitable sortable" ! String Index !! Description !! Type |- | "Form" || The parent magic effect (the applied spell, ingestible, etc.) || ref |- | "BaseEffect" || The base magic effect record that defines the effect’s behavior || ref |- | "Caster" || The actor who applied the effect || ref |- | "MetaData" || Unique identifier for this specific active effect instance || int |} == Modifiable Entries == These entries can be adjusted with [[SetActiveEffectsInfo]]. {| class="wikitable sortable" ! String Index !! Description !! Type |- | "Magnitude" || The current magnitude (strength) of the effect || float |- | "TimeLeft" || Remaining duration in seconds. Cannot exceed total duration. || float |- | "Duration" || Maximum total duration of the effect || float |- | "Flags" || Effect flags (purpose unknown; experimental) || UInt32 |} == Effect Filter Types == Use these values with the ''effectFilter'' argument to control which effects are included. You can alternatively pass a [[Base Effect]] or [[Actor Effect]] in place of the ID, to filter by effect form. {| class="wikitable sortable" ! ID !! Includes |- | 0 || No Filter (Default) |- | 1 || Object Effects |- | 2 || Actor Effects |- | 3 || Ingestibles (e.g., chems, food) |- | ref || will return all matching active effects |} == Temp Filter Types == Use these values with the ''tempFilter'' argument to control whether temporary effects are returned. {| class="wikitable sortable" ! ID !! Description |- | 0 || Non-temporary effects only |- | 1 || Temporary effects only |- | 2 || Both (default) |} == Examples == 
let aAllFireDamageEffects := actorRef.GetActiveEffectsInfo FlamerFireDamage
This returns all effect entries that use the FlamerFireDamage as a base effect. 
let aAllEffects := player.GetActiveEffectsInfo
let iIndex := 0

while iIndex < ar_Size aAllEffects
    let rParentEffect := aAllEffects[iIndex]["Form"]
    let rBaseEffect   := aAllEffects[iIndex]["BaseEffect"]
    let rCaster       := aAllEffects[iIndex]["Caster"]
    let fMagnitude    := aAllEffects[iIndex]["Magnitude"]
    let fTimeLeft     := aAllEffects[iIndex]["TimeLeft"]
    let fDuration     := aAllEffects[iIndex]["Duration"]
    let iFlags        := aAllEffects[iIndex]["Flags"]
    let iIndex += 1
loop
This script extracts information about every active effect on the player. 
let aTempIngestibleEffects := actorRef.GetActiveEffectsInfo 3 1
This returns all ''temporary ingestible effects'' currently applied to actorRef. == See Also == * [[SetActiveEffectsInfo]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP PP LN)]] [[Category:Effect Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIPPPLN |summary = Calculates and returns how much condition a weapon would lose if fired, using the specified ammo if provided (otherwise the current ammo). |name = GetCalculatedWeaponDegradation |referenceType = reference |returnType = degradation:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = ammo |Type = ref |Optional = true }} }} ==Examples== 
float deg = Player.GetCalculatedWeaponDegradation
Returns the calculated degradation for the players currently held weapon, and currently equipped ammo. 
let deg := GetCalculatedWeaponDegradation WeapNV9mmPistol Ammo9mmJunkRounds
Returns the degradation WeapNV9mmPistol would experience if it fired a Ammo9mmJunkRound ammo form. ==Notes== * This works for Fallout New Vegas degradation as well as Fallout 3 degradation settings. * If a weapon has a "damage to weapon" override, this will also be factored into the calculation. [[Category:Functions (JIP PP LN)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an '''[[array_var]]''' containing the names ([[string_var]]s) of all the blocks that are descended from the specified parent node, in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. *The 1st argument, '''parentNode''', is optional. If omitted, or an empty string, the object's root node will be used. *The 2nd argument, '''pcModel''', is optional, and may be used when calling the function on the player. The player character has two, completely separate 3D models for 1st and 3rd person. This argument enables to explicitly target one of the models. 
1	3rd Person Model
2	1st Person Model
|name = GetChildBlocks |returnType = blockNames:array_var |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = parentNode |Type = string |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = pcModel |Type = 1/2 |Optional = y }} |example = let aChildBlocks := player.GetChildBlocks "Bip01 Spine" }} ==See Also== *[[GetNifBlockParentNodes]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:Functions (JIP PP LN)]]

{{Function |origin = JIPPPLN |summary = Returns the [[Template:DirectX_Scancodes|DirectX Scancodes]] for keyboard, or [[Template:Control_Codes|XBox Controller Codes]] assigned to a control, specified by its [[Template:Control_Codes|control code]]. |name = GetControlCodeByID |returnType = ControlCode:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = controlCode |Type = int |Value = 0–27 }} {{FunctionArgument |Name = controlType |Type = int |Value = 0–3 }} }} ==Control Types== {| class="wikitable" ! Value ! Type ! Description |- | 0 | Keyboard | DirectX keyboard scancode |- | 1 | Mouse | Mouse button scancode |- | 2 | Controller | XInput controller button code |- | 3 | Joystick | DirectInput joystick scancode |} ==Example== 
int iCode
set iCode to GetControlCodeByID 7 2   ; Returns the “Reload” control mask for a gamepad
{{Controller_Button_Codes}} ==See Also== * [[GetAltControl]] * [[GetControl]] * [[TapControl]] * [[IsKeyPressed]] * [[TapKey]] * [[HoldKey]] [[Category:Functions (JIP PP LN)]] [[Category:Input Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the value of the specified property (determined by valueID) in the '''NiMaterialProperty''' of the specified '''Nif Block''' of the calling object reference. ==Material Property Value IDs== 
0	Specular Color - Red
1	Specular Color - Green
2	Specular Color - Blue
3	Emissive Color - Red
4	Emissive Color - Green
5	Emissive Color - Blue
6	Glossiness
7	Alpha
8	Emit Mult.
|name = GetMaterialPropertyValue |alias = GetMatrPropVal |returnType = propValue:float |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = nifBlockName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = valueID |Type = int |Value = 0-8 }} |example = set fAlpha to NAFBTunnelDoorRef.GetMaterialPropertyValue "TrackPanelRight01:13" 7 This will return the Alpha value of the NiMaterialProperty, of the "TrackPanelRight01:13" block, in the Nif used by NAFBTunnelDoorRef. }} ==Notes== * Passing a path for a node name requires [[JIP PP LN]] ==See Also== *[[SetMaterialPropertyValue]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions (JIP PP LN)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns whether the specified flag is set on the specified NIF block in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. *The 3rd argument, '''pcModel''', is optional, and may be used when calling the function on the player. The player character has two, completely separate 3D models for 1st and 3rd person. This argument enables to explicitly target one of the models. 
1	3rd Person Model
2	1st Person Model
==Nif Block Flag IDs== 
0	Hidden
1	Selective Update
2	Selective Update: Transforms
3	Selective Update: Controller
4	Selective Update: Rigid
5	Display (UI?) Object
6	Disable Sorting
7	Selective Update: Transforms Override
8	???
9	Save External Geometry Data
10	No Decals
11	Always Draw
12	Mesh LOD
13	Fixed Bound
14	Top FadeNode
15	Ignore Fade
16	No Anim Sync (X)
17	No Anim Sync (Y)
18	No Anim Sync (Z)
19	No Anim Sync (S)
20	No Dismember
21	No Dismember Validity
22	Render Use
23	Materials Applied
24	High Detail
25	Force Update
26	Pre-Processed Node
27	???
28	(Reserved)
29	(Reserved)
30	(Reserved)
31	(Reserved)
|name = GetNifBlockFlag |alias = GetNifFlag |returnType = flagSet:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = blockName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagID |Type = int |Value = 0-31 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = pcModel |Type = 1/2 |Optional = y }} |example = set bHidden to objectRef.GetNifBlockFlag "Bucket01:0" 0 }} ==Notes== * Passing a path for a node name requires [[JIP PP LN]] ==See Also== *[[SetNifBlockFlag]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]] [[Category:Functions (JIP PP LN)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns a 3-element '''[[array_var]]''' containing the XYZ rotation (yaw, pitch and roll) angles (in degrees) of the specified NIF block in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. *The 2nd argument, '''whichRotation''', determines the type of rotation that is returned: 
0	(Default) EXTRINSIC (global) rotation, relative to parent node.
1	EXTRINSIC (global) rotation, relative to the world.
2	INTRINSIC (local), relative to parent node.
3	INTRINSIC (local) rotation, relative to the world.
*The 3rd argument, '''pcModel''', is optional, and may be used when calling the function on the player. The player character has two, completely separate 3D models for 1st and 3rd person. This argument enables to explicitly target one of the models. 
1	3rd Person Model
2	1st Person Model
*If the 3D model does not have the specified block, the function returns '''[[ar_Null]]'''. |name = GetNifBlockRotation |alias = GetNifRot |returnType = rotation:array_var |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = blockName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = whichRotation |Type = int |Value = 0-3 |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = pcModel |Type = 1/2 |Optional = y }} |example = let aRotation := objectRef.GetNifBlockRotation "Bucket01:0" let aRotation := player.GetNifBlockRotation "ProjectileNode" 1 1 }} ==Notes== * Passing a path for a node name requires [[JIP PP LN]] ==See Also== *[[GetNifBlockRotationAlt]] *[[SetNifBlockRotation]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions (JIP PP LN)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Movement Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the XYZ rotation (yaw, pitch and roll) angles (in degrees) of the specified NIF block in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. The result XYZ are placed into the 3 specified '''Script Variables'''. *The 5th argument, '''whichRotation''', determines the type of rotation that is returned: 
0	(Default) EXTRINSIC (global) rotation, relative to parent node.
1	EXTRINSIC (global) rotation, relative to the world.
2	INTRINSIC (local), relative to parent node.
3	INTRINSIC (local) rotation, relative to the world.
*The 6th argument, '''pcModel''', is optional, and may be used when calling the function on the player. The player character has two, completely separate 3D models for 1st and 3rd person. This argument enables to explicitly target one of the models. 
1	3rd Person Model
2	1st Person Model
*If the 3D model does not have the specified block, the function returns 0, and the script variables are unchanged. |name = GetNifBlockRotationAlt |alias = GetNifRotAlt |returnType = success:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = blockName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = resX |Type = ScriptVar }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = resY |Type = ScriptVar }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = resZ |Type = ScriptVar }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = whichRotation |Type = int |Value = 0-3 |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = pcModel |Type = 1/2 |Optional = y }} |example = objectRef.GetNifBlockRotationAlt "Bucket01:0" fRotX fRotY fRotZ player.GetNifBlockRotationAlt "Weapon\ProjectileNode" fRotX fRotY fRotZ 1 1 }} ==Notes== * Passing a path for a node name requires [[JIP PP LN]] ==See Also== *[[GetNifBlockRotation]] *[[SetNifBlockRotation]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions (JIP PP LN)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Movement Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the scale of the specified NIF block in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. *The 2nd argument, '''pcModel''', is optional, and may be used when calling the function on the player. The player character has two, completely separate 3D models for 1st and 3rd person. This argument enables to explicitly target one of the models. 
1	3rd Person Model
2	1st Person Model
|name = GetNifBlockScale |alias = GetNifScale |returnType = scale:float |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = blockName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = pcModel |Type = 1/2 |Optional = y }} |example = set fScale to objectRef.GetNifBlockScale "Bucket01:0" }} ==Notes== * Passing a path for a node name requires [[JIP PP LN]] ==See Also== *[[SetNifBlockScale]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions (JIP PP LN)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Scale Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns a 3-element '''array_var''' containing the XYZ translation of the specified NIF block in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. *The 2nd argument, '''getWorld''', is optional. If non-zero, the returned array will contain the XYZ world position of the block. *The 3rd argument, '''pcModel''', is optional, and may be used when calling the function on the player. The player character has two, completely separate 3D models for 1st and 3rd person. This argument enables to explicitly target one of the models. 
1	3rd Person Model
2	1st Person Model
*If the 3D model does not have the specified block, the function returns '''ar_Null'''. |name = GetNifBlockTranslation |alias = GetNifTrans |returnType = translation:array_var |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = blockName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = getWorld |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = pcModel |Type = 1/2 |Optional = y }} |example = let aTranslation := objectRef.GetNifBlockTranslation "Bucket01:0" let aTranslation := player.GetNifBlockTranslation "Weapon\##SightingNode" let aTranslation := player.GetNifBlockTranslation "ProjectileNode" 1 1 }} ==Notes== * Passing a path for a node name requires [[JIP PP LN]] ==See Also== *[[GetNifBlockTranslationAlt]] *[[SetNifBlockTranslation]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Movement Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:Functions (JIP PP LN)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the XYZ translation of the specified NIF block in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. The result XYZ are placed into the 3 specified '''Script Variables'''. *The 5th argument, '''getWorld''', is optional. If non-zero, the returned array will contain the XYZ world position of the block. *The 6th argument, '''pcModel''', is optional, and may be used when calling the function on the player. The player character has two, completely separate 3D models for 1st and 3rd person. This argument enables to explicitly target one of the models. 
1	3rd Person Model
2	1st Person Model
