*If the 3D model does not have the specified block, the function returns 0, and the script variables are unchanged. |name = GetNifBlockTranslationAlt |alias = GetNifTransAlt |returnType = success:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = blockName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = resX |Type = ScriptVar }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = resY |Type = ScriptVar }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = resZ |Type = ScriptVar }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = getWorld |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = pcModel |Type = 1/2 |Optional = y }} |example = objectRef.GetNifBlockTranslationAlt "Bucket01:0" fPosX fPosY fPosZ player.GetNifBlockTranslationAlt "Weapon\##SightingNode" fPosX fPosY fPosZ player.GetNifBlockTranslationAlt "ProjectileNode" fPosX fPosY fPosZ 1 1 }} ==Notes== * Passing a path for a node name requires [[JIP PP LN]] ==See Also== *[[SetNifBlockTranslation]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions (JIP PP LN)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Movement Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIPPPLN |summary = Fast check for nodes present in the runtime attachment lists of the specified object (either a '''base form''' or a '''reference'''). Unlike [[ModelHasBlock]], this function does not load or scan the full NIF; it operates on the runtime data created by [[AttachModel]] and [[InsertNode]]. It supports three modes: attached models, inserted nodes, and sparse node paths. |name = HasRuntimeNode |returnType = hasNode:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = object |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = nodeQuery |Type = formattedString }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = mode |Type = int |Optional = y |Value = 0–2 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = formatVars (up to 20) |Optional = y }} }} ==Modes== * '''2 – Attached Models''': Search for [[RuntimeNodes]] added via [[AttachModel]]. * '''1 – Inserted Nodes''': Search [[RuntimeNodes]] added via [[InsertNode]]. * '''0 – Sparse Node Path''': checks for nodes that contain this path. ==Example== ;-- Mode 2: Attached Models (checks strings registered from [[AttachModel]]) 
if eval HasRuntimeNode WeapNVServiceRifle "Weapon|*LeftBarrel*weapons\attachments\silencer01.nif" 2 == 0
   AttachModel WeapNVServiceRifle 3 "Weapon|*LeftBarrel*weapons\attachments\silencer01.nif"
endif
This checks if the silencer model (with suffix *LeftBarrel*) is already scheduled in the runtime model builder for the rifle. If it isn’t, it attaches it. Prevents duplicate attachments. ;-- Mode 1: Inserted Nodes (checks nodes created with [[InsertNode]]) 
InsertNode Player 3 "Weapon|Secondary_Weapon"
set bHasNode to HasRuntimeNode Player "Weapon|Secondary_Weapon" 1
Returns 1 if an inserted NiNode named "Secondary_Weapon" exists under the player model. ;-- Mode 0: Sparse Node Path (partial path match across runtime nodes; backslash-delimited) 
InsertNode Player 3 "Weapon\BarrelDevice\ProjectileNode|Second_Barrel"
set bHasNode to HasRuntimeNode Player "BarrelDevice" 0
Returns 1 if any runtime nodes contain "BarrelDevice" in their paths. ==Notes== * This function is much faster than [[ModelHasBlock]] for checking [[RuntimeNodes]] because it does not parse or load the full base NIF; it checks the current runtime attachment/insert lists only. * If you need to search the entire resolved model (including the base NIF), use [[ModelHasBlock]] instead. ==See Also== * [[AttachModel]] * [[InsertNode]] * [[AttachFormModel]] * [[AttachModel]] * [[GetRuntimeNodes]] * [[CopyRuntimeNodes]] * [[ClearRuntimeNodes]] [[Category:Functions (JIP PP LN)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Model Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Inserts a '''NiNode''' as either a child or as the parent of an existing NiNode in the 3D model of the specified object form. The function takes 3(+) arguments, as follows: *'''refOrForm''' - Either an '''object reference''' or a '''base form'''. In the former case, the NiNode will be inserted into the 3D model of that specific reference only, whereas in the latter case, it will be inserted into the 3D model of any reference instance of the base form. *'''addOrRemove''' - Either insert a new, or remove a previously inserted NiNode. Use one of the following values: 
0	Remove; Pending reload
1	Insert; Pending reload
2	Remove; Instantaneous
3	Insert; Instantaneous
:*'''Pending reload''' - Changes will be applied once either the object's 3D is (re)loaded by the game, or [[Update3D]] is called on it. :*'''Instantaneous''' - Changes will be applied instantly. *'''targetAndName''' - A '''[[String Formatting|formatted string]]''' containing the name of an existing NiNode in the object's 3D model and, separated by a vertical bar '''"|"''', the name of the new inserted NiNode. If the name of the new node is prefixed by a caret '''"^"''', it will be inserted as the parent of the target node. Otherwise, it will be inserted as the last child of the target node. Names are case-sensitive. |name = InsertNode |returnType = successful:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = refOrForm |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = addOrRemove |Type = int |Value = 0-3 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = targetAndName |Type = formattedString }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = formatVars(up to 20) |Optional = y }} |example = 
InsertNode PlayerRef 1 "Bip01 Head|^Head Parent Bone"
The above will INSERT a new NiNode named '''"Head Parent Bone"''' as the '''parent''' of '''"Bip01 Head"''' in the 3D model of the '''PlayerRef'''. 
InsertNode TeddyBear01 1 "Bone01|My Node"
The above will INSERT a new NiNode named '''"My Node"''' as the last '''child''' of '''"Bone01"''' in the 3D model of every instance of '''TeddyBear01'''. 
InsertNode PlayerRef 3 "Weapon|Secondary_Weapon"
The above will '''instantly''' INSERT a new NiNode named '''"Secondary_Weapon"''' as the last '''child''' of '''"Weapon"''' in the 3D model of the '''PlayerRef'''. 
InsertNode PlayerRef 2 "Weapon|Secondary_Weapon"
The above will '''instantly''' REMOVE '''"Secondary_Weapon"''' from the 3D model of the '''PlayerRef'''. }} ==JIP PP LN== In '''version 57.45''' and above, the following enhancements are available with the JIP PP LN plugin: * '''Sparse Node Paths''': You can now specify a '''partial''' path to the target node. This allows node insertion under deeply nested or ambiguous paths by matching only key segments of the path. * This resolves issues where multiple nodes with the same name exist in different branches of the model hierarchy. * Partial path segments are delimited using backslashes (`\`), just like in [[AttachModel]]. ==JIP PP LN Example== 
InsertNode Player 3 "Weapon\##Clip|Secondary_ClipNode"
This inserts a new child node named '''Secondary_ClipNode''' under the first node matching the partial path '''Weapon\##Clip''' in the player's 3rd person model. 
InsertNode Player 3 "Weapon\##Clip|^ClipParent"
This inserts a new parent node named '''ClipParent''' above the node resolved by the sparse path '''Weapon\##Clip'''. ==Notes== *Any changes made by this function remain in effect for the duration of the current game session, or until removed. *On successful insertion, the function returns 1, otherwise 0. *Using InsertNode/[[AttachModel]] on dynamic refs will only work with mode 3. For instantaneous removal of attached nodes/models from temporary refs, you'll need to use [[RemoveNifBlock]]. *To detect if JIP PP LN is available, use [[GetPluginVersion]] "JIP PP LN". ==See Also== *[[ModelHasBlock]] *[[AttachModel]] *[[AttachFormModel]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions (JIP PP LN)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Model Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns whether the 3D model of the specified object (which may be either a '''base form''', or a '''reference''') has a block with a name matching (case-sensitive) the specified [[String Formatting|formatted string]]. This includes any '''NiNodes''' that were inserted to the object using [[InsertNode]]. |name = ModelHasBlock |returnType = hasBlock:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = object |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = blockName |Type = formattedString }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = formatVars(up to 20) |Optional = y }} |example = set bHasBlock to ModelHasBlock PlayerRef "ProjectileNode" set bHasBlock to ModelHasBlock WeapNVServiceRifle "##CockingMech" }} ==JIP PP LN== In '''version 57.45''' and above, the following enhancements are available with the JIP PP LN plugin: * '''Sparse Node Paths''': You can now specify a '''partial path''' to a nested node. The search will traverse down the model tree and match based on key segments (e.g., "Weapon\##Clip" or "Weapon\BarrelDevice\ProjectileNode"). * '''Nested Model Support for Base Forms''': This function now correctly checks attached models (via [[AttachModel]]) even when the input is a '''base form'''. * Previously, this only worked if a specific reference was passed in, as base forms would not resolve attached model data. * Now it works for any reference or base form, provided the model has already been modified in memory. ==JIP PP LN Example== 
AttachModel WeapNVServiceRifle 3 "Weapon|weapons\attachments\silencer01.nif"

if eval ModelHasBlock WeapNVServiceRifle "Weapon\Barrel Device\ProjectileNode"
   Print "Found ProjectileNode!"
endif
This will return true if the silencer model attached to the "Weapon" node contains a "Barrel Device" node with a child named "ProjectileNode". This works even though WeapNVServiceRifle is a base form. ==Notes== * '''ModelHasBlock''' loads and searches the object’s current resolved 3D model in memory, including the original base NIF. This can be more expensive than a runtime-only check. * If you only need to know whether a node was added at runtime (via [[AttachModel]] or [[InsertNode]]), it is usually better to use [[HasRuntimeNode]]. That function works directly on the runtime node builder lists, is significantly faster, and avoids loading the full NIF. * Use ModelHasBlock if you specifically need to verify a node that originates from the base NIF or from attached models that have already been merged into the active render tree. ==See Also== *[[InsertNode]] *[[AttachModel]] *[[AttachFormModel]] *[[HasRuntimeNode]] *[[RemoveNifBlock]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions (JIP PP LN)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Moves the calling reference to the specified target reference, at the world position of the specified '''Node''' in the target's loaded 3D model.
*Arguments 3-5 are optional, and may be used for positioning the calling reference at the specified X/Y/Z offset, relative to the node. |name = MoveToNode |returnType = success:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = target |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = nodeName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = offsetX |Type = float |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = offsetY |Type = float |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = offsetZ |Type = float |Optional = y }} |example = objectRef.MoveToNode targetRef "Scene Root"
objectRef.MoveToNode targetRef "Scene Root" 0 48 96 }} ==See Also== *[[MoveTo]] *[[MoveToCell]] *[[MoveToReticle]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Movement Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:Functions (JIP PP LN)]]

{{Function |origin = JIPPPLN |summary = Forces the game to recalculate the carrying/inventory weight of the calling reference. Normally this refresh happens only after picking up or dropping an item; use this after scripted changes (e.g., [[SetWeight]] on an item already in inventory) so the displayed weight is updated immediately. This command is void and returns no value. |name = RefreshInventoryWeight |referenceType = reference }} ==Example== 
SetWeight WeapNVServiceRifle 3.5
PlayerRef.RefreshInventoryWeight
==Notes== * Intended for actors (Player/NPCs). Calling on non-actor refs has no effect. * Use after: ** [[SetWeight]] on items already present in inventory. ** Scripted [[AddItem]]/[[RemoveItem]] sequences where the UI or carry-weight readout did not auto-refresh. * Useful to keep Pip-Boy/menus in sync without requiring the player to pick up or drop an item. ==See Also== * [[SetWeight]] * [[AddItem]] * [[RemoveItem]] * [[Actor Values]] [[Category:Functions (JIP PP LN)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Removes the specified NIF block, along with all its child blocks (if any), from the calling reference's loaded 3D model. *The effect is '''instantaneous''' and does not require calling [[Update3D]]. *The 2nd argument, '''pcModel''', is optional, and may be used when calling the function on the player. The player character has two, completely separate 3D models for 1st and 3rd person. This argument enables to explicitly target one of the models. 
1	3rd Person Model
2	1st Person Model
|name = RemoveNifBlock |returnType = successful:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = blockName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = pcModel |Type = 1/2 |Optional = y }} |example = player.RemoveNifBlock "ProjectileNode" }} ==JIP PP LN== In '''version 57.45''' and above, the following enhancements are available with the JIP PP LN extension: * '''Sparse Node Paths''': You can now specify a '''partial''' path to the block you want to remove. This makes it easier to target blocks inside deeply nested structures or when nodes share the same name in different locations. * Path segments are delimited with backslashes (`\`) * The path is matched from root down, allowing for resolution of ambiguous or deeply nested nodes ==JIP PP LN Example== 
Player.RemoveNifBlock "Weapon\Barrel2\ProjectileNode" 1
This will remove the ProjectileNode inside of the Barrel2 node located within the weapon node of the players 3rd person model, along with any child blocks under it. ==Notes== *The changes are reset once the calling reference's parent cell is reloaded, or [[Update3D]] is called on it. *RemoveNifBlock should not be used on anything inserted or attached via [[AttachModel]] or [[InsertNode]], unless it's a dynamic reference. *Sparse path matching improves compatibility with complex or dynamically built models. *To detect if JIP PP LN is available, use [[GetPluginVersion]] "JIP PP LN". ==See Also== *[[AttachModel]] *[[InsertNode]] *[[ModelHasBlock]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions (JIP PP LN)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Model Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIPPPLN |summary = Bulk edits multiple active effects on the calling actor. Must be used with arrays created with [[GetActiveEffectsInfo]] |name = SetActiveEffectsInfo |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = EffectInfo |Type = array |Optional = y }} }} == Example == 
let aTempIngestibleEffects := player.GetActiveEffectsInfo 3 1

 let iIndex := 0
 while iIndex < ar_Size aTempIngestibleEffects 
 	aTempIngestibleEffects[iIndex]["Magnitude"] *= 2
 	let iIndex += 1
 loop

player.SetActiveEffectsInfo aTempIngestibleEffects
Doubles the Magnitude of all temporary ingestible effects on the player. 
let aAllFlameDamageEffects := SunnyRef.GetActiveEffectsInfo FlamerFireDamage
 let iIndex := 0
 while iIndex < ar_Size aAllFlameDamageEffects 
 	aAllFlameDamageEffects[iIndex]["Magnitude"] /= 2
 	let iIndex += 1
 loop

 SunnyRef.SetActiveEffectsInfo aAllFlameDamageEffects
Cuts the Magnitude in half for all FlamerFireDamage effects currently effecting SunnyRef. ==See Also== *[[GetActiveEffectsInfo]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP PP LN)]] [[Category:Effect Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Modifies the XYZ rotation (pitch, roll and yaw) angles (in degrees) of the specified NIF block in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. *The 5th argument, '''method''', determines how the rotation is modified: 
0	(Default) Set the EXTRINSIC (global) rotation, relative to parent node.
1	Rotate the INTRINSIC (local) rotation by the specified XYZ values.
2	Set the INTRINSIC (local) rotation, relative to parent node.
3	(Reserved)
4	Rotate the EXTRINSIC (global) rotation by the specified XYZ values.
5	Set the EXTRINSIC (global) rotation, relative to the world, such that the block will face the specified XYZ (given as COORDINATES - not angles!).
*The 6th argument, '''pcModel''', is optional, and may be used when calling the function on the player. The player character has two, completely separate 3D models for 1st and 3rd person. This argument enables to explicitly target one of the models. 
1	3rd Person Model
2	1st Person Model
|name = SetNifBlockRotation |alias = SetNifRot |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = blockName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = rotX |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = rotY |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = rotZ |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = method |Type = int |Value = 0-5 |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = pcModel |Type = 1/2 |Optional = y }} |example = objectRef.SetNifBlockRotation "Bucket01:0" 68 137 -25 objectRef.SetNifBlockRotation "Bucket01:0" 0 30 90 1 }} ==Notes== * Passing a path for a node name requires [[JIP PP LN]] *The changes are reset once the calling reference's parent cell is reloaded, or [[Update3D]] is called on it. ==See Also== *[[GetNifBlockRotation]] *[[GetNifBlockRotationAlt]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions (JIP PP LN)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Movement Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the scale of the specified NIF block in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. *The 3rd argument, '''pcModel''', is optional, and may be used when calling the function on the player. The player character has two, completely separate 3D models for 1st and 3rd person. This argument enables to explicitly target one of the models. 
1	3rd Person Model
2	1st Person Model
|name = SetNifBlockScale |alias = SetNifScale |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = blockName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = scale |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = pcModel |Type = 1/2 |Optional = y }} |example = objectRef.SetNifBlockScale "Bucket01:0" 0.25 }} ==Notes== *The changes are reset once the calling reference's parent cell is reloaded, or [[Update3D]] is called on it. * Passing a path for a node name requires [[JIP PP LN]] ==See Also== *[[GetNifBlockScale]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions (JIP PP LN)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Scale Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the XYZ translation of the specified NIF block in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. *The 5th argument, '''pcModel''', is optional, and may be used when calling the function on the player. The player character has two, completely separate 3D models for 1st and 3rd person. This argument enables to explicitly target one of the models. 
1	3rd Person Model
2	1st Person Model
|name = SetNifBlockTranslation |alias = SetNifTrans |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = blockName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = posX |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = posY |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = posZ |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = pcModel |Type = 1/2 |Optional = y }} |example = objectRef.SetNifBlockTranslation "Bucket01:0" 12 -7.4 0.8 player.SetNifBlockTranslation "##SightingNode" 25.4 0 6.25 2 }} ==JIP PP LN== In '''version 57.45''' and above, the following enhancements are available with the [[JIP PP LN]] plugin: * '''Sparse Node Paths''': You can now specify a '''partial''' path to the node you wish to modify. This is useful for models with deeply nested structures or duplicate node names. The sparse path will search for a matching chain of node names in the model hierarchy, not just a direct name match. * '''New Mode Argument''': An additional 6th argument allows you to change how the translation is applied. 
mode = 0  ; relative to local (default)
mode = 1  ; additive translation
mode = 2  ; world position
==JIP PP LN Example== 
Player.SetNifBlockTranslation "Weapon\##SightingNode" 128 0 48 1 3
This will find the "##SightingNode" under the "Weapon" node in the player's 3rd person model and move it to the world position (128, 0, 48). ==Notes== * The changes are reset once the calling reference's parent cell is reloaded, or [[Update3D]] is called on it. * Sparse path matching allows precise targeting of blocks in complex models and reduces errors due to duplicate block names. * To detect if JIP PP LN is available, use [[GetPluginVersion]] "JIP PP LN". ==See Also== *[[GetNifBlockTranslationAlt]] *[[AttachModel]] *[[InsertNode]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions (JIP PP LN)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Movement Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''UDF Script''' as a handler that will be invoked in the event the specified '''Dialog Topic'''/'''Topic Info''' is spoken by an actor. When invoked, the script is passed with the speaking '''Actor''' as the calling reference ("this"), and a single argument: the '''Topic''' or the '''Info''' (depending on what the event was set for) spoken. *When using a '''Topic Info''' for the 3rd argument, use the hexadecimal '''Form ID''' (Topic Infos have no Editor IDs). |name = SetOnDialogTopicEventHandler |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = topicOrInfo |Type = form }} |example = 
SetOnDialogTopicEventHandler OnDialogTopicUDF 1 FollowersHired
'''Sets''' the OnDialogTopicUDF script as a handler to be invoked whenever the '''FollowersHired''' topic is spoken. 
SetOnDialogTopicEventHandler OnDialogTopicUDF 0 FollowersHired
'''Removes''' the OnDialogTopicUDF script as a handler of the above. }} ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn	OnDialogTopicUDF

ref	rTopicOrInfo
ref	rSpeaker

begin Function {rTopicOrInfo}

	set rSpeaker to this

	(code)

end
==Notes== This function has been tweaked in [[JIP PP LN]] to not require a Topic filter. ==See Also== [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions (JIP PP LN)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Adds an ammo effect to an ammo type. |name = AddAmmoEffect |returnType = successful:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ammo |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = ammoEffect |Type = ref }} |example = AddAmmoEffect Ammo12Ga AmmoEffectBeanBagFatigue }} ==See Also== *[[GetNumAmmoEffects]] *[[GetNthAmmoEffect]] *[[RemoveAmmoEffect]] *[[GetAmmoEffectTraitNumeric]] *[[SetAmmoEffectTraitNumeric]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Ammo Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Ammo Effect Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |originVersion = 14.00 |summary = Adds a new [[Destructible Object Stage|destruction stage]] to the specified Object, at the specified index, and with the specified Health Percentage and Model Damage Stage. |name = AddDestructionStage |returnType = stageCount:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = object |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = index |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = healthPercent |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = damageStage |Type = int }} |example = AddDestructionStage TrapGrenadeBunch 2 80 3 }} ==Notes== *This function has two major limitations: 1. It will have no effect on existing references of the object - only on references created after it is called, and 2. The changes will not be saved with the game. *If the stage was successfully added, the function will return the new stage-count, and -1 otherwise. *Obviously, this function is only usable with object types that have Destruction Data. ==See Also== *[[GetDestructionStage]] *[[RemoveDestructionStage]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Destruction Data Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Adds quantity of the specified item to the calling reference's inventory. *Unlike the vanilla [[AddItem]]/[[AddItemHealthPercent]] functions, this does not cause the infamous "invisible weapon" glitch when used on actors. *The 1st argument '''item''' may be either an '''[[Item]]''' form, a '''[[Leveled Item]]''', or a '''[[Form List]]''' of items. *The 3rd argument '''condition''' is optional and sets the item(s) health percent (100% by default). *The 4th argument '''equipItem''' is optional. If non-zero, and the calling reference is an actor, the added item(s) will be immediately equipped. This is mainly intended to be used with leveled items, where it is not possible to reliably determine which item(s) was added. *The 5th argument '''noMessage''' is optional (1 by default). If non-zero, the function will not display corner messages. |name = AddItemAlt |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = item |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = quantity |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = condition |Type = float |Value = 0-100 |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = equipItem |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = noMessage |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }} |example = 
TargetRef.AddItemAlt Camera 10
Add 10 Camera items to TargetRef. 
TargetRef.AddItemAlt NVHoldoutWeapons 4 65
Add 4 of each item in the list NVHoldoutWeapons to TargetRef, each with 65% health. 
TargetRef.AddItemAlt LL2Tier3Guns 1 50 1
Add a random leveled weapon with 50% health and suitable ammo to TargetRef, and immediately equips both. }} ==See Also== *[[BaseAddItem]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Object Functions]] [[Category:Leveled List Functions]] [[Category:List Functions]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Adds a new effect-entry to the effect list of the specified [[ingestible]]/[[Object Effect]]/[[Actor Effect]], with the specified parameters. |name = AddNewEffect |returnType = effectCount:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = effectForm |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseEffect |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = duration |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = magnitude |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = range |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = area |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = append |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }} ==Effect Range Types== 
0	Self
1	Touch
2	Target
|example = AddNewEffect EnchReconArmor IncreaseCharisma 0 3 0 0 AddNewEffect RadAway IncreaseRadiationResistance 240 25 0 0 }} ==Notes== *'''range''' and '''area''' are optional, and are defaulted to 0. *'''append''' (7th argument) is optional. If non-zero, the new effect will be appended at the end of the effects list. Otherwise, it will be inserted at the top of the list. *If the effect-entry was successfully added, the function will return the new effect-count, and -1 otherwise. *This function is save baked. ==See Also== *[[RemoveNthEffect]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Effect Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Creates and attaches a geometric [[Creating Primitives|primitive]] to the calling reference, with the specified shape and dimensions. This function will only work on: 1. [[Activator]]-type objects - in which case the primitive will function as a cubic/spherical activator (trigger) - or 2. A CollisionMarker - in which case the primitive will function as a collision plane/box/sphere. |name = AddPrimitive |returnType = success:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = shapeType |Type = int |Value = 1-3 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = boundX |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = boundY |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = boundZ |Type = float }} ==Primitive Shape Types== 
1	Box
2	Sphere
3	Plane (collision only)
|example = activatorRef.AddPrimitive 1 300 500 200 set rMarker to PlaceAtMe CollisionMarker 1 rMarker.AddPrimitive 3 250 0 250 }} ==See Also== *[[HasPrimitive]] *[[SetPrimitiveBound]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Adds a new variable to either an object script (when called by an object reference), or a quest script (when called on a quest). |name = AddScriptVariable |returnType = added:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = varName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = quest |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = RaulRef.AddScriptVariable "NewVar"
AddScriptVariable "NewVar" VERNovac01 }} ==Notes== *The new variable has no explicit type, and can store either a numeric (float) value, or a reference. *To retrieve/set the value of the new variable, use any of [[GetVariable]]/[[GetRefVariable]]/[[SetVariable]]/[[SetRefVariable]]. *The new variable is saved with the game. ==See Also== *[[RemoveScriptVariable]] *[[RemoveAllAddedVariables]] *[[HasVariableAdded]] *[[GetVariable]] *[[GetRefVariable]] *[[SetVariable]] *[[SetRefVariable]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Script Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Script_Functions]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Erases the element at the specified index of the specified [[Auxiliary Variables|Auxiliary-Variable]]. If index is not specified, or equals -1, the variable, along with all its elements, will be erased. Returns the Aux Variable's new number of elements, '''NOT''' the amount of elements that were erased. {{Template:ModIndexOverride}} |name = AuxiliaryVariableErase |alias = AuxVarErase |returnType = numElements:int |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = varName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = index |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = RaulRef.AuxiliaryVariableErase "someVarName" AuxVarErase "someVarName" 2 ArmorCombat }} ==See Also== *[[AuxiliaryVariableEraseAll]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Auxiliary-Variable Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Aux Functions]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Erases all [[Auxiliary Variables]] of the specified class, owned by the specified Object. AuxVar Classes: 
0	Permanent-Private
1	Temporary-Private
2	Permanent-Public
3	Temporary-Public
When erasing '''Private''' Auxiliary Variables, only the ones belonging to the calling mod will be erased.
Otherwise, when erasing '''Public''' AuxVars, all of them will be erased. {{Template:ModIndexOverride}} |name = AuxiliaryVariableEraseAll |alias = AuxVarEraseAll |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = varClass |Type = int |Value = 0-3 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = RaulRef.AuxiliaryVariableEraseAll 0 AuxVarEraseAll 1 ArmorCombat }} [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Auxiliary-Variable Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Aux Functions]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns a StringMap matrix containing all [[Auxiliary Variables]] of the specified class, and their elements' values, owned by the specified Object. AuxVar Classes: 
0	Permanent-Private
1	Temporary-Private
2	Permanent-Public
3	Temporary-Public
{{Template:ModIndexOverride}} |name = AuxiliaryVariableGetAll |alias = AuxVarGetAll |returnType = array_var |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = varClass |Type = int |Value = 0-3 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = let aVarMap := RaulRef.AuxiliaryVariableGetAll 0 let aVarMap := AuxVarGetAll 1 ArmorCombat }} [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Auxiliary-Variable Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Aux Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an array_var containing all the values stored in the specified [[Auxiliary Variables|Auxiliary-Variable]]. {{Template:ModIndexOverride}} |name = AuxiliaryVariableGetAsArray |alias = AuxVarGetAsArr |returnType = values:array_var |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = varName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = let aValues := RaulRef.AuxiliaryVariableGetAsArray "someVarName"
let aValues := AuxVarGetAsArr "someVarName" ArmorCombat }} ==See Also== *[[AuxiliaryVariableGetAll]] *[[AuxiliaryVariableSetFromArray]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Auxiliary-Variable Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Aux Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the float value of the element at the specified index (optional; default is 0) of the specified [[Auxiliary Variables|Auxiliary-Variable]]. If index equals -1, the value of the last element in the [[Auxiliary Variables|Auxiliary-Variable]] is returned. {{Template:ModIndexOverride}} |name = AuxiliaryVariableGetFloat |alias = AuxVarGetFlt |returnType = value:float |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = varName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = index |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = set fValue to RaulRef.AuxiliaryVariableGetFloat "someVarName" set fValue to AuxVarGetFlt "someVarName" 2 ArmorCombat }} ==Notes== *The function will (presumably) return 0 if the variable is not defined/has no element at index/element is not of type float. When necessary, verify using [[AuxiliaryVariableGetType]]. ==See Also== *[[AuxiliaryVariableSetFloat]] *[[AuxiliaryVariableSetString]] / [[AuxiliaryVariableGetString]] *[[AuxiliaryVariableSetRef]] / [[AuxiliaryVariableGetRef]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Auxiliary-Variable Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Aux Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the [[Ref Variable|ref]] value of the element at the specified index (optional; default is 0) of the specified [[Auxiliary Variables|Auxiliary-Variable]]. If index equals -1, the value of the last element in the [[Auxiliary Variables|Auxiliary-Variable]] is returned. {{Template:ModIndexOverride}} |name = AuxiliaryVariableGetRef |alias = AuxVarGetRef |returnType = value:ref |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = varName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = index |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = set rValue to RaulRef.AuxiliaryVariableGetRef "someVarName" set rValue to AuxVarGetRef "someVarName" 2 ArmorCombat }} ==Notes== *The function will return 0 if the variable is not defined/has no element at index/element is not of type ref. When necessary, verify using [[AuxiliaryVariableGetType]]. ==See Also== *[[AuxiliaryVariableSetRef]] *[[AuxiliaryVariableSetFloat]] / [[AuxiliaryVariableGetFloat]] *[[AuxiliaryVariableSetString]] / [[AuxiliaryVariableGetString]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Auxiliary-Variable Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Aux Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the string value of the element at the specified index (optional; default is 0) of the specified [[Auxiliary Variables|Auxiliary-Variable]]. If index equals -1, the value of the last element in the [[Auxiliary Variables|Auxiliary-Variable]] is returned. {{Template:ModIndexOverride}} |name = AuxiliaryVariableGetString |alias = AuxVarGetStr |returnType = value:string_var |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = varName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = index |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = let sValue := RaulRef.AuxiliaryVariableGetString "someVarName" let sValue := AuxVarGetStr "someVarName" 2 ArmorCombat }} ==Notes== *The function will return a null string if the variable is not defined/has no element at index/element is not of type string. When necessary, verify using [[AuxiliaryVariableGetType]]. ==See Also== *[[AuxiliaryVariableSetString]] *[[AuxiliaryVariableSetFloat]] / [[AuxiliaryVariableGetFloat]] *[[AuxiliaryVariableSetRef]] / [[AuxiliaryVariableGetRef]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Auxiliary-Variable Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Aux Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the value-type of the element at the specified index (optional; default is 0) of the specified [[Auxiliary Variables|Auxiliary-Variable]]. If index equals -1, the value-type of the last element in the [[Auxiliary Variables|Auxiliary-Variable]] is returned. Value Types: 
0	None (no such index/variable not defined)
1	Float
2	Reference
4	String
{{Template:ModIndexOverride}} |name = AuxiliaryVariableGetType |alias = AuxVarType |returnType = type:int |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = varName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = index |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = set iType to RaulRef.AuxiliaryVariableGetType "someVarName" set iType to AuxVarType "someVarName" 2 ArmorCombat }} [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Auxiliary-Variable Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Aux Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the float value of the element at the specified index (optional; default is 0) of the specified [[Auxiliary Variables|Auxiliary-Variable]]. If index equals -1, the value will be appended as a new element, at the end. {{Template:ModIndexOverride}} |name = AuxiliaryVariableSetFloat |alias = AuxVarSetFlt |returnType = success:bool |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = varName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = value |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = index |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = RaulRef.AuxiliaryVariableSetFloat "someVarName" -307.485 AuxVarSetFlt "someVarName" 102031 -1 ArmorCombat }} ==See Also== *[[AuxiliaryVariableGetFloat]] *[[AuxiliaryVariableSetString]] / [[AuxiliaryVariableGetString]] *[[AuxiliaryVariableSetRef]] / [[AuxiliaryVariableGetRef]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Auxiliary-Variable Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Aux Functions]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Creates an [[Auxiliary Variables|Auxiliary-Variable]] containing all the values stored in the passed [[array_var]]. If the specified AuxVar already exists, all values stored in it are erased. {{Template:ModIndexOverride}} |name = AuxiliaryVariableSetFromArray |alias = AuxVarSetFromArr |returnType = numElements:int |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = varName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = sourceArray |Type = array_var }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = RaulRef.AuxiliaryVariableSetFromArray "someVarName" aSourceArr
AuxVarSetFromArr "someVarName" aSourceArr ArmorCombat }} ==Notes== *The function returns the size (number of elements) of the created AuxVar. *This creates a new array stored in the AuxVar that is no longer associated with the source array. *This function only grabs the values from the array; '''keys from [[Map Array]]s and [[StringMap]]s are ignored''', as AuxVars only use numeric keys, like regular arrays. *Using this function on a Multidimensional Array (an Array composed of Arrays) will '''NOT''' produce the expected results. It is suggested to '''only use single-dimension arrays''' with this function.
==See Also== *[[AuxiliaryVariableGetAsArray]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Auxiliary-Variable Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Aux Functions]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the [[Ref Variable|ref]] value of the element at the specified index (optional; default is 0) of the specified [[Auxiliary Variables|Auxiliary-Variable]]. If index equals -1, the value will be appended as a new element, at the end. {{Template:ModIndexOverride}} |name = AuxiliaryVariableSetRef |alias = AuxVarSetRef |returnType = success:bool |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = varName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = value |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = index |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = RaulRef.AuxiliaryVariableSetRef "someVarName" Ammo127mm AuxVarSetRef "someVarName" CraigBooneRef -1 ArmorCombat }} ==See Also== *[[AuxiliaryVariableGetRef]] *[[AuxiliaryVariableSetFloat]] / [[AuxiliaryVariableGetFloat]] *[[AuxiliaryVariableSetString]] / [[AuxiliaryVariableGetString]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Auxiliary-Variable Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Aux Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the string value of the element at the specified index (optional; default is 0) of the specified [[Auxiliary Variables|Auxiliary-Variable]]. If index equals -1, the value will be appended as a new element, at the end. {{Template:ModIndexOverride}} |name = AuxiliaryVariableSetString |alias = AuxVarSetStr |returnType = success:bool |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = varName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = value |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = index |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = RaulRef.AuxiliaryVariableSetString "someVarName" "Snivelling, dim-witted weasel." AuxVarSetStr "someVarName" "Toffee-nosed git." -1 ArmorCombat player.AuxVarSetStr "*someVarName" $SomeStringVariable -1 }} ==See Also== *[[AuxiliaryVariableGetString]] *[[AuxiliaryVariableSetFloat]] / [[AuxiliaryVariableGetFloat]] *[[AuxiliaryVariableSetRef]] / [[AuxiliaryVariableGetRef]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Auxiliary-Variable Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Aux Functions]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Clears the player's active quest, and any quest targets shown on the map/compass. |name = ClearActiveQuest |example = ClearActiveQuest }} [[Category:Quest Functions]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Quest Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Deletes all data of the specified types:
'''scriptVars''' - Variables created by the mod calling this function with [[AddScriptVariable]].
'''linkedRefs''' - Linked-refs set by the mod calling this function with [[SetLinkedReference]].
'''auxVars''' - 1: Permanent-Private (to the mod calling this function), or 2: Permanent-Public [[Auxiliary Variables]].
'''refMaps''' - 1: Permanent-Private (to the mod calling this function), or 2: Permanent-Public [[RefMap Array|RefMap Arrays]].
(Deleted data will not be saved with the game) |name = ClearJIPSavedData |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = scriptVars |Type = 0/1 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = linkedRefs |Type = 0/1 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = auxVars |Type = 0/1/2 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = refMaps |Type = 0/1/2 }} |example = ClearJIPSavedData 0 1 2 0 }} ==See Also== *[[ClearModNVSEVars]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Simulates the user clicking (one or more times) on the specified UI button. *This function is intended for UI buttons performing a particular, hardcoded menu function (Accept, Cancel, Select, Switch, etc.). For miscellaneous UI buttons, see [[ClickMenuTile]]. |name = ClickMenuButton |returnType = successful:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = componentPath |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = clickTimes |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = 
ClickMenuButton "StartMenu\NOGLOW_BRANCH\settings_container" 1
ClickMenuButton "RecipeMenu#2" 1
The 2nd example demonstrates an alternate (and the preferable) method for specifying the component path. A '''#''' symbol immediately following the menu name, then followed by a number matching the '''''' trait of the component. In this case, '''"RecipeMenu#2"''' is equivalent to '''"RecipeMenu\NOGLOW_BRANCH\RM_ItemsRect\RM_Items_RightFilterArrow"''' }} ==Notes== *The UI component must be click-able, and have an '''''' trait. ==See Also== *[[IsComponentLoaded]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Adds a weather to a climate's weather types, with the specified chance and global variable (optional). If the weather is already in the list, sets its chance and global. |name = ClimateAddWeatherType |returnType = successful:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = climate |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = weather |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = chance |Type = int |Value = 0-100 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = globalVar |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = ClimateAddWeatherType WastelandClimate NVHooverDamWeather 50 VNight }} ==See Also== *[[GetClimateNumWeatherTypes]] *[[GetClimateNthWeatherType]] *[[GetClimateNthWeatherChance]] *[[GetClimateNthWeatherGlobal]] *[[ClimateRemoveWeatherType]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Climate Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Removes a weather from a climate's weather types. |name = ClimateRemoveWeatherType |returnType = successful:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = climate |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = weather |Type = ref }} |example = ClimateRemoveWeatherType WastelandClimate WastelandClear }} ==See Also== *[[GetClimateNumWeatherTypes]] *[[GetClimateNthWeatherType]] *[[GetClimateNthWeatherChance]] *[[GetClimateNthWeatherGlobal]] *[[ClimateAddWeatherType]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Climate Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns 1 if the [[creature]]'s model-list includes the specified .nif file. |name = CreatureHasModel |returnType = hasModel:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = modelPath |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = set bHasModel to RexRef.CreatureHasModel "eyessetvicious.nif"
set bHasModel to CreatureHasModel "eyessetvicious.nif" Rex }} ==Notes== *The file must appear in the creature's meshes folder. *The string must include the ".nif" extension and is case-insensitive. ==See Also== *[[ToggleCreatureModel]] *[[GetCreatureModels]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Creature Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Disables the specified NavMesh. This function takes a [[Reference]]/[[Editor ID]] as parameter, unlike [[DisableNavmesh]], which takes a [[Form ID]] string. |name = DisableNavMeshAlt |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = NavMesh |Type = ref }} |example = DisableNavMeshAlt MOBarracksNavMesh }} ==Notes== *Interior navmeshes are persistent and an [[Editor ID]] can be passed directly to the function, like in the example above. *Exterior navmeshes are not persistent and you must pass the navmesh's [[FormID]] as a [[ref variable]] to the function. See the example below: 
let rNavmesh := GetFormFromMod "SomeMod.esm" NavMeshFormID
DisableNavMeshAlt rNavmesh
Note that the NavMeshFormID is the condensed form of its [[FormID]], meaning it does not include information about its position in the load order. This is important, since one cannot assume that the mod the form originates from will always be at the same load order index. ==See Also== *[[EnableNavMeshAlt]] *[[Navmesh Disabling]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:Navmesh Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = The calling actor/container will drop the specified number of the specified item(s) in the world, next to it. This function is an improved version of the vanilla [[Drop]]: *This function may be called on any container, and is not restricted to actors alone. *A '''[[FormList]]''' may be passed as parameter (in place of an item). *All stats/attributes (health, script, mods, ownership, etc.) of dropped items are preserved. *This function will trigger the '''[[OnDrop]]''' script event (where applicable). |name = DropAlt |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = item/list |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = quantity |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = clearOwnership |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }} |example = 
player.DropAlt WeapLaserRifle
The player will drop all WeapLaserRifle items. 
containerRef.DropAlt NVHoldoutWeapons 2 1
containerRef will drop 2 of each item in the list NVHoldoutWeapons. Ownership will be cleared off all dropped items. }} ==Notes== *If quantity is not specified or is <= 0, all instances of the item(s) will be dropped. ==See Also== *[[DropMeAlt]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Object Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Drops the calling '''[[Inventory Reference]]''' in the world, next to its container. *All stats/attributes (health, script, mods, ownership, etc.) of dropped items are preserved. *This function will trigger the '''[[OnDrop]]''' script event (where applicable). |name = DropMeAlt |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = quantity |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = clearOwnership |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }} |example = itemRef.DropMeAlt 5 }} ==Notes== *If quantity is not specified or is <= 0, the whole amount of the Inventory Reference will be dropped. ==See Also== *[[DropMe]] *[[DropAlt]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Object Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Reference Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Enables the specified NavMesh. This function takes a [[Reference]]/[[Editor ID]] as parameter, unlike [[EnableNavMesh]], which takes a [[Form ID]] string. |name = EnableNavMeshAlt |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = NavMesh |Type = ref }} |example = EnableNavMeshAlt MOBarracksNavMesh }} ==Notes== *Interior navmeshes are persistent and an [[Editor ID]] can be passed directly to the function, like in the example above. *Exterior navmeshes are not persistent and you must pass the navmesh's [[FormID]] as a [[ref variable]] to the function. See the example below: 
let rNavmesh := GetFormFromMod "SomeMod.esm" NavMeshFormID
DisableNavMeshAlt rNavmesh
Note that the NavMeshFormID is the condensed form of its [[FormID]], meaning it does not include information about its position in the load order. This is important, since one cannot assume that the mod the form originates from will always be at the same load order index. ==See Also== *[[DisableNavMeshAlt]] *[[Navmesh Disabling]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:Navmesh Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Similar to the vanilla [[EquipItem]] - forces the calling actor to equip the specified item. However, unlike EquipItem, this function will also trigger the '''OnEquip''' script event (where applicable). |name = EquipItemAlt |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = item |Type = baseForm }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = noUnequip |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = hideMessage |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }} |example = player.EquipItemAlt WeapLaserRifle 0 1 }} ==Notes== *This function is identical to the NVSE function [[EquipItem2]]. ==See Also== *[[UnequipItemAlt]] *[[EquipItemData]] *[[UnequipItems]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions (JIP)]] [[Category: Object Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = The calling actor will turn (playing the appropriate animation) to face the specified object-reference. |name = FaceObject |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = object |Type = ref }} |example = actorRef.FaceObject objectRef }} ==See Also== *[[Turn]] *[[IsTurning]] *[[SetAngle]] *[[SetAngleEx]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Movement Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

revision.comment: Undo revision 19475 by [[Special:Contributions/Demorome|Demorome]] ([[User talk:Demorome|talk]])
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = '''A)''' Forces and locks the calculated '''[[:Category:Detection|detection value]]''' (a value that indicates how '''visible''' an actor is to another actor) of the calling actor to any other actor, regardless of skill, cover, lighting, perception, etc. A very low, negative value (such as -100) will render the calling actor completely undetectable by other actors, while a high positive value will render it fully detectable by any actor. '''B)''' If the 2nd, optional argument is non-zero, the value of the 1st argument will instead be used as a modifier, and will be added/subtracted from the '''detection value''' every time it is calculated. Calling the function and ommiting the argument, or passing 0, will restore detection mechanics on the calling actor to normal. |name = ForceActorDetectionValue |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = detectionValue |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = asModifier |Type = 1/0 |Optional = y }} |example = 
actorRef.ForceActorDetectionValue -100
Renders actorRef completely undetectable by other actors. 
actorRef.ForceActorDetectionValue -15 1
Subtracts 15 from the '''detection values''' for actorRef, every time they are calculated. }} ==See Also== *[[IsActorDetected]] / [[GetDetected]] *[[GetDetectionValue]] / [[GetDetectionLevel]] / [[GetPCDetectionState]] *[[GetDetectingActors]] / [[GetDetectedActors]] *[[GetWeaponDetectionSoundLevel]] / [[SetWeaponDetectionSoundLevel]] *[[CreateDetectionEvent]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Detection Functions]] [[Category:Detection]]

revision.comment: k. You u had ur joke.
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Calls the heap manager to deallocate the player character, bringing the game to its inevitable conclusion. |name = FreePlayer |alias = GoodbyeWorldspace |example = FreePlayer }} ==Notes== *Because. *I can. *Seriously, don't use this.
==See Also== *[[DeleteReference]] / [[Zap]] can fulfill the same "goal" if called on the player.
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Troll Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an '''array_var''' containing all the effects currently active on the calling actor. The optional filter parameter may be used for specifying which types of effects are returned. |name = GetActiveEffects |returnType = activeEffects:array_var |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = filter |Type = int |Optional = y }} ==Effect-Type Filter Values== 
1	Object Effects
2	Actor Effects
4	Ingestibles
|example = 
let aEffects := actorRef.GetActiveEffects
Will return all effects, of all types, currently active on actorRef. 
let aEffects := actorRef.GetActiveEffects 2
Will return all Actor Effects currently active on actorRef. 
let aEffects := actorRef.GetActiveEffects 5
Will return all Object Effects and Ingestibles currently active on actorRef. }} ==See Also== *[[GetTempEffects]] *[[GetActiveEffectsInfo]] *[[SetActiveEffectsInfo]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Effect Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the menu code of the top active menu mode currently receiving input. These are the same codes used by the [[MenuMode]] function. |name = GetActiveMenuMode |returnType = menuCode:int |example = GetActiveMenuMode }} ==See Also== *[[GetActiveUIComponentName]] *[[GetActiveUIComponentFullName]] *[[GetActiveUIComponentID]] *[[GetMenuTargetRef]] *[[GetMenuItemFilter]] *[[ClickMenuButton]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Like [[GetActiveUIComponentName]], returns the name of the menu element currently highlighted by the mouse cursor, if any. However, the returned name is fully qualified with the names of the element's parents in a form suitable to pass to commands like [[GetUIFloat]]. |name = GetActiveUIComponentFullName |returnType = name:string_var |example = GetActiveUIComponentFullName }} ==See Also== *[[GetActiveMenuMode]] *[[GetActiveUIComponentName]] *[[GetActiveUIComponentID]] *[[GetMenuTargetRef]] *[[GetMenuItemFilter]] *[[ClickMenuButton]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the integer ID of the currently highlighted menu element, as defined by that element's <id> trait in the menu XML file. |name = GetActiveUIComponentID |returnType = id:int |example = GetActiveUIComponentID }} ==See Also== *[[GetActiveMenuMode]] *[[GetActiveUIComponentName]] *[[GetActiveUIComponentFullName]] *[[GetMenuTargetRef]] *[[GetMenuItemFilter]] *[[ClickMenuButton]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the name of the menu element currently highlighted by the mouse cursor, as defined in the menu XML file. |name = GetActiveUIComponentName |returnType = name:string_var |example = GetActiveUIComponentName }} ==See Also== *[[GetActiveMenuMode]] *[[GetActiveUIComponentFullName]] *[[GetActiveUIComponentID]] *[[GetMenuTargetRef]] *[[GetMenuItemFilter]] *[[ClickMenuButton]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the calling actor's current Alpha as a floating point number ranging from 0.0 to 1.0, with 0.0 corresponding to fully-transparent and 1.0 to fully-opaque. |name = GetActorAlpha |returnType = alpha:float |referenceType = reference |example = set fAlpha to actorRef.GetActorAlpha }} ==Notes== *Higher values indicate more light: the values appear to range from 0 for complete darkness, to 100 for very bright light. ==See Also== *[[GetActorFadeState]] *[[SetActorAlpha]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = If the calling [[actor]] is underwater, returns the time (in seconds) remaining until they run out of breath (and begin drowning). |name = GetActorDiveBreath |returnType = breathSeconds:float |referenceType = reference |example = set fBreathSeconds to actorRef.GetActorDiveBreath }} ==See Also== *[[SetActorDiveBreath]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Time Functions]]

revision.comment: Adding to original actor functions category
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the specified leveling data value of an actor. The value returned is determined by dataType: 
0	Fixed Level/Level Mult.
1	Calc. Min.
2	Calc. Max.
|name = GetActorLevelingData |returnType = dataValue:float |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = dataType |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = 
CraigBooneRef.GetActorLevelingData 0
This will return Craig Boone's base fixed level/level multiplier. 
GetActorLevelingData 1 CraigBoone
This will return Craig Boone's calculated minimum level. }} ==See Also== *[[SetActorLevelingData]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns a float describing the amount of light falling on the calling actor. |name = GetActorLightAmount |returnType = lightAmount:float |referenceType = reference |example = set fLightAmount to actorRef.GetActorLightAmount }} ==Notes== *Higher values indicate more light: the values range from 0.0 for complete darkness, to 100.0 for very bright light.
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]] [[Category:Detection Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the current AI/script processing-level of the calling actor. The returned value ranges from 0 to 3: *'''0 (High):''' The actor is processed every frame. *'''1 (Mid-High):''' The actor is processed every ~18 seconds. *'''2 (Mid-Low):''' Unknown at this time. *'''3 (Low):''' The actor is processed every ~120 seconds.
If the actor's Base Process isn't loaded, which can occur if it is [[Disable|disabled]], returns -1. |name = GetActorProcessingLevel |returnType = processingLvl:int |referenceType = reference |example = set iProcLevel to actorRef.GetActorProcessingLevel }} ==See Also== *[[GetActorsByProcessingLevel]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]]

revision.comment: Adding to original actor functions category
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an actor's template. |name = GetActorTemplate |returnType = baseForm:ref |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = ActorRef.GetActorTemplate
GetActorTemplate AngelaWilliams }} ==See Also== *[[GetLeveledActorBase]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the actor's Unarmed Attack effect. |name = GetActorUnarmedEffect |returnType = effect:ref |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = set rEffect to RexRef.GetActorUnarmedEffect set rEffect to GetActorUnarmedEffect NVCrGoldenGecko }} ==See Also== *[[SetActorUnarmedEffect]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Effect Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |originVersion = 36.00 |summary = Returns the calling actor's current '''Velocity''' on the (optional) specified '''Axis'''. If no axis is specified, the total, absolute velocity (product of all three axes) is returned. |name = GetActorVelocity |returnType = velocity:float |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = axis |Type = X/Y/Z |Optional = y }} |example = set fVelocity to actorRef.GetActorVelocity
set fVelocityZ to actorRef.GetActorVelocity Z }} ==Notes== *The sign of the returned value indicates direction: a negative Z velocity indicates the actor is speeding downwards. {{Template:Velocity Measurement}} ==See Also== *[[SetActorVelocity]] *[[GetObjectVelocity]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Movement Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

revision.comment: Adding to original actor functions category
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an actor's Voice Type. |name = GetActorVoiceType |returnType = voiceType:ref |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = set rVoice to RaulRef.GetActorVoiceType set rVoice to GetActorVoiceType AngelaWilliams }} ==See Also== *[[SetActorVoiceType]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an '''[[array_var]]''' containing all the actors in the game that are currently in the specified script/AI processing-level. |name = GetActorsByProcessingLevel |returnType = actors:array_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = processingLvl |Type = int |Value = 0-3 }} ==Processing-Levels== '''0 (High):''' Equivalent to all actors in the currently loaded interior cell or set of exterior cells adjacent to (and including) the player's current cell, excluding the player ref itself. Note that this is analogous to using [[GetRefs]] for actors, but is much faster and more efficient. '''1 (Mid-High):''' Equivalent to all actors in exterior cells that are not currently loaded, but which have recently been visited by the player and are still buffered in memory. For example, when entering one of the Strip casinos, actors in the Strip exterior are moved from High into Mid-High. '''2 (Mid-Low):''' Contains actors in interior cells that have not been visited, but are accessible through teleport doors within player's loaded cell grid. Once actor's level goes up to Mid-Low, it never seems to go back to Low. For example, upon entering the Goodsprings exterior, Chet's processing level goes up from Low to Mid-Low and stays on that level for the remainder of the session, unless the player visits his store. '''3 (Low):''' Equivalent to all actors in cells that are neither currently loaded nor buffered in memory. Note that this includes the vast majority of actors in the game (many thousands). Avoid using this unless you have a good reason. |example = let aActors := GetActorsByProcessingLevel 0 Will return all actors with high processing-level. }} ==See Also== *[[GetActorProcessingLevel]] *[[GetDetectingActors]] *[[GetDetectedActors]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the Addiction Chance of the specified [[Ingestible]] [[item]]. |name = GetAddictionChance |returnType = chance:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ingestible |Type = ref }} |example = set fChance to GetAddictionChance Morphine }} ==See Also== *[[SetAddictionChance]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Effect Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the Addiction effect of the specified [[Ingestible]] [[item]]. |name = GetAddictionEffect |returnType = actorEffect:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ingestible |Type = ref }} |example = set rEffect to GetAddictionEffect Morphine }} ==See Also== *[[SetAddictionEffect]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Effect Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an '''[[array_var]]''' containing '''[[Inventory Reference]]s''' of all the items in the calling reference's inventory. Four optional parameters may be passed for filtering the result: '''typeCode''' - Only include inventory references of items of this type ([[Inventory_Item_Type_Codes|type-codes]]). Passing 0 will include items of ANY type. '''noNonPlayable''' - Do not include items that are non-playable (only applicable to clothing, weapons and ammo). '''noQuestItems''' - Do not include [[Quest Item|quest items]]. '''noEquipped''' - Do not include inventory references that are currently equipped by the calling reference. |name = GetAllItemRefs |returnType = items:array_var |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = typeCode |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = noNonPlayable |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = noQuestItems |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = noEquipped |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }} |example = 
let aItems := objectRef.GetAllItemRefs
The returned array will include inventory references of ALL the items in the calling reference's inventory. 
let aItems := objectRef.GetAllItemRefs 40 1 0 1
The returned array will include inventory references of all the weapon-type items in the calling reference's inventory, excluding references that are equipped, or of non-playable weapons. }} ==Notes== *The size of this array is not necessarily the total amount of items the player has, as some item refs with identical health can be stacked. Only one representative ref is returned for a stack of item refs - use [[GetRefCount]] to check for this. ==See Also== *[[GetAllItems]] *[[GetContainerInventoryCount]] *[[GetInventoryWeight]] *[[RemoveMeIRAlt]] / [[RemoveMeIR]] *[[DropMeAlt]], [[DropAlt]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Reference Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an '''[[array_var]]''' containing the [[Base Form]]s of all the items in the [[calling reference]]'s inventory. Four optional parameters may be passed for filtering the result: '''typeCode''' - Only include items of this type (a full list of type-codes can be found [[Form_Type_IDs|here]]). Passing 0 will include items of ANY type. '''noNonPlayable''' - Do not include items that are non-playable (only applicable to clothing, weapons and ammo). '''noQuestItems''' - Do not include quest items. '''noEquipped''' - Do not include items that are currently equipped by the calling reference. |name = GetAllItems |returnType = items:array_var |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = typeCode |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = noNonPlayable |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = noQuestItems |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = noEquipped |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }} |example = 
let aItems := objectRef.GetAllItems
The returned array will include ALL the items in the calling reference's inventory. 
let aItems := objectRef.GetAllItems 40 1 0 1
The returned array will include all the weapon-type items in the calling reference's inventory, excluding weapons that are non-playable or equipped. }} ==See Also== *[[GetAllItemRefs]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns whether '''Always Run''' is currently toggled. |name = GetAlwaysRun |returnType = alwaysRun:0/1 |example = set bAlwaysRun to GetAlwaysRun }} ==See Also== [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the value of an ammo effect's numeric trait (determined by traitID). |name = GetAmmoEffectTraitNumeric |returnType = traitValue:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ammoEffect |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-2 }} ==Ammo Effect Numeric Trait IDs== 
0	Effect Type
1	Operation Type
2	Value
Effect Types: 
0	Damage Mod
1	DR Mod
2	DT Mod
3	Spread Mod
4	Weapon Condition Mod
5	Fatigue Mod
Operation Types: 
0	Add
1	Multiply
2	Subtract
|example = GetAmmoEffectTraitNumeric AmmoEffect5mmDTReduction 0 Will return the effect type of AmmoEffect5mmDTReduction. }} ==See Also== *[[SetAmmoEffectTraitNumeric]] *[[GetNumAmmoEffects]] *[[GetNthAmmoEffect]] *[[AddAmmoEffect]] *[[RemoveAmmoEffect]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Ammo Effect Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Ammo Functions]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an ammo type's projectile. |name = GetAmmoProjectile |returnType = projectile:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ammo |Type = ref }} |example = GetAmmoProjectile Ammo12GaSlug }} ==See Also== *[[SetAmmoProjectile]] *[[GetAmmoTraitNumeric]] *[[SetAmmoTraitNumeric]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Ammo Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions]] [[Category:Ammo Functions]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the value of an ammo type's numeric trait (determined by traitID). |name = GetAmmoTraitNumeric |returnType = traitValue:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ammo |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-2 }} ==Ammo Numeric Trait IDs== 
0	Speed
1	Projectiles Per Shot
2	Ammo Consumed Percent
3	Clip Rounds
|example = GetAmmoTraitNumeric Ammo12Ga 1 Will return the 12 Gauge Round's Projectiles Per Shot. }} ==See Also== *[[SetAmmoTraitNumeric]] *[[GetAmmoProjectile]] *[[SetAmmoProjectile]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Ammo Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Ammo Functions]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the '''Ragdoll Constraint Template''' RDT file path, of the specified '''[[Armor]]''' form. |name = GetArmorRCT |returnType = RCTPath:string_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = armor |Type = ref }} |example = let sRCTPath := GetArmorRCT ArmorMetal }} ==See Also== *[[SetArmorRCT]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Armor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Armor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = When called on an '''Ash/Goo Pile''', returns the actor it originated from. |name = GetAshPileSource |returnType = pileSource:ref |referenceType = reference |example = set rSource to pileRef.GetAshPileSource }} ==See Also== *[[AttachAshPileEx]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns whether '''Auto Move''' is currently toggled. |name = GetAutoMove |returnType = autoMove:0/1 |example = set bAutoMove to GetAutoMove }} ==See Also== [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the amount of money the merchant, with whom the player is currently trading, has available for bartering. This is the value displayed on the upper-right corner when in the barter menu. |name = GetBarterGoldAlt |returnType = merchantCash:int |example = set iMerchantCash to GetBarterGoldAlt }} ==Notes== *The vanilla function [[GetBarterGold]] is broken and will always return 0. ==See Also== *[[GetBarterPriceMult]] / [[SetBarterPriceMult]] *[[GetBarterItems]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an [[array]] of unique temporary [[Inventory Reference]]s to the items bartered in the current, active transaction. If the getSoldItems parameter is non-zero, the returned array will contain the items set to be sold by the player. Otherwise, the array will contain the items set to be bought by the player. |name = GetBarterItems |returnType = barterItemRefs:array_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = getSoldItems |Type = 0/1 }} |example = let aBarterItems := GetBarterItems 0 Returns an array with all the unique items being bought. Returns 0 if there are no items being bought. 
int iNumItems
int iIndex
ref rItem
array_var aBarterItems
array_var aSoldItems

let iNumItems := 0

let aBarterItems := GetBarterItems 0  ;bought items
let aSoldItems := GetBarterItems 1  ;sold items
if (aBarterItems == 0)
   let aBarterItems := ar_Construct "array"  ;Otherwise ar_Cat won't work.
endif
ar_Cat aBarterItems, aSoldItems  ;both bought and sold items, together

let iIndex := ar_size aBarterItems
while (iIndex -= 1) >= 0
   let rItem := aBarterItems[iIndex]
   let iNumItems += rItem.GetRefCount
loop

let aSoldItems := aBarterItems := ar_Null
Returns the total amount of items (both being bought and sold) in the transaction, including items in stacks. }} ==See Also== *[[GetSelectedItemRef]] *[[GetInvRefsForItem]] *[[GetFirstRefForItem]] *[[GetRefCount]] to get the amount of stacked items. [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = An alternate version of the vanilla function [[GetBaseActorValue]]. This version may be called on a [[reference]], as well as (optionally) on a [[base form]]. |name = GetBaseActorValueAlt |alias = GetBaseAVAlt |returnType = statValue:float |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = actorValue |Type = StatName }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = set fBaseEndurance to CraigBooneRef.GetBaseActorValueAlt Endurance
set fBaseEndurance to GetBaseActorValueAlt Endurance CraigBoone }} ==See Also== *[[ModBaseActorValue]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns whether or not the specified flag (determined by flagID) is set for the specified '''Base Effect'''. |name = GetBaseEffectFlag |returnType = isFlagSet:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = baseEffect |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagID |Type = int }} ==Base Effect Flag IDs== 
0	Hostile
1	Recover
2	Detrimental
3	Hidden
4	Self
5	Touch
6	Target
7	No Duration
8	No Magnitude
9	No Area
10	FX Persist
12	Gory Visuals
13	Display Name Only
19	Use Skill
20	Use Attribute
24	Painless
27	No Hit Effect
28	No Death Dispel
|example = set bIsFlagSet to GetBaseEffectFlag DamageStrength 2 }} ==See Also== *[[SetBaseEffectFlag]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Effect Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the associated '''Script''' of the specified script-type '''Base Effect'''. |name = GetBaseEffectScript |returnType = script:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = baseEffect |Type = ref }} |example = set rScript to GetBaseEffectScript CateyeBaseEffect }} ==See Also== *[[SetBaseEffectScript]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Effect Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the numeric value of the specified trait (determined by traitID), of the specified body-part type (determined by partID..."
revision.text:
