{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the numeric value of the specified trait (determined by traitID), of the specified body-part type (determined by partID) of the passed Body-Part data. |name = GetBodyPartTraitNumeric |returnType = traitValue:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = bodyPartData |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = partID |Type = int |Value = 0-14 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-18 }} ==Body-Part Type IDs== 
0	Torso
1	Head 1
2	Head 2
3	Left Arm 1
4	Left Arm 2
5	Right Arm 1
6	Right Arm 2
7	Left Leg 1
8	Left Leg 2
9	Left Leg 3
10	Right Leg 1
11	Right Leg 2
12	Right Leg 3
13	Brain
14	Weapon
==Body-Part Numeric Trait IDs== 
0	Actor Value Code
1	Damage Mult.
2	Health Percent
3	To Hit Chance
4	Tracking Max. Angle
5	Severable - Debris Count
6	Severable - Debris Scale
7	Severable - Decal Count
8	Explosion Chance
9	Limb Replacement Scale
10	Explodable - Debris Count
11	Explodable - Debris Scale
12	Explodable - Decal Count
13	Local Translate: X
14	Local Translate: Y
15	Local Translate: Z
16	Local Rotate: X
17	Local Rotate: Y
18	Local Rotate: Z
|example = set fVal to GetBodyPartTraitNumeric NVGecko 0 2 Will return the Health Percent of the Torso body-part of NVGecko. }} ==See Also== *[[SetBodyPartTraitNumeric]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Body-Part Data Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Tests and returns 1 if the specified cell (and all objects contained within) is currently buffered in memory. The cell parameter is optional and, if left out, the function will use the parent-cell of the calling reference. |name = GetCellBuffered |returnType = buffered:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = cell |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = set bBuffered to GetCellBuffered NovacMotelLobby set rExtrCell to GetExteriorCell WastelandNV -22 -19 set bBuffered to GetCellBuffered rExtrCell set bBuffered to objectRef.GetCellBuffered }} ==See Also== *[[GetBufferedCells]] *[[GetExteriorCell]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Cell Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Cell Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns a class' flag value (determined by flagID). |name = GetClassFlag |returnType = flagValue:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = class |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagID |Type = int |Value = 0-19 }} ==Class Flag IDs== 
0	Playable
1	Guard
2	Buys/Sells Weapons
3	Buys/Sells Armor
4	Buys/Sells Alcohol
5	Buys/Sells Books
6	Buys/Sells Food
7	Buys/Sells Chems
8	Buys/Sells Stimpaks
12	Buys/Sells Miscellaneous
18	Recharge
19	Repair
|example = GetClassFlag NCRRanger 4 }} ==See Also== *[[SetClassFlag]] *[[GetClassTraitNumeric]] *[[SetClassTraitNumeric]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Class Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the value of a class' numeric trait (determined by traitID). |name = GetClassTraitNumeric |returnType = traitValue:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = class |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-10 }} ==Class Numeric Trait IDs== 
0	Tag Skill #1
1	Tag Skill #2
2	Tag Skill #3
3	Tag Skill #4
4	Base Strength
5	Base Perception
6	Base Endurance
7	Base Charisma
8	Base Intelligence
9	Base Agility
10	Base Luck
*For Tag Skill values, see [http://fose.silverlock.org/fose_command_doc.html#Actor_Value_Codes here]. |example = GetClassTraitNumeric NCRRanger 2 Will return the Tag Skill #3 of NCRRanger. }} ==See Also== *[[SetClassTraitNumeric]] *[[GetClassFlag]] *[[SetClassFlag]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Class Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the Night Sky model path of a climate. |name = GetClimateNightSkyModel |returnType = modelPath:string_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = climate |Type = ref }} |example = GetClimateNightSkyModel WastelandClimate }} ==See Also== *[[SetClimateNightSkyModel]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Climate Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the chance of the Nth weather type of a climate. |name = GetClimateNthWeatherChance |returnType = chance:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = climate |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = weatherIndex |Type = int }} |example = GetClimateNthWeatherChance WastelandClimate 0 }} ==See Also== *[[GetClimateNumWeatherTypes]] *[[GetClimateNthWeatherType]] *[[GetClimateNthWeatherGlobal]] *[[ClimateAddWeatherType]] *[[ClimateRemoveWeatherType]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Climate Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the global variable (if any) of the Nth weather type of a climate. |name = GetClimateNthWeatherGlobal |returnType = globalVar:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = climate |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = weatherIndex |Type = int }} |example = GetClimateNthWeatherGlobal WastelandClimate 0 }} ==See Also== *[[GetClimateNumWeatherTypes]] *[[GetClimateNthWeatherType]] *[[GetClimateNthWeatherChance]] *[[ClimateAddWeatherType]] *[[ClimateRemoveWeatherType]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Climate Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the Nth weather type of a climate. |name = GetClimateNthWeatherType |returnType = weather:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = climate |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = weatherIndex |Type = int }} |example = GetClimateNthWeatherType WastelandClimate 0 }} ==See Also== *[[GetClimateNumWeatherTypes]] *[[GetClimateNthWeatherChance]] *[[GetClimateNthWeatherGlobal]] *[[ClimateAddWeatherType]] *[[ClimateRemoveWeatherType]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Climate Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the number of weather types of a climate. |name = GetClimateNumWeatherTypes |returnType = weatherTypes:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = climate |Type = ref }} |example = GetClimateNumWeatherTypes WastelandClimate }} ==See Also== *[[GetClimateNthWeatherType]] *[[GetClimateNthWeatherChance]] *[[GetClimateNthWeatherGlobal]] *[[ClimateAddWeatherType]] *[[ClimateRemoveWeatherType]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Climate Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the Sun Glare texture path of a climate. |name = GetClimateSunGlareTexture |returnType = texturePath:string_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = climate |Type = ref }} |example = GetClimateSunGlareTexture WastelandClimate }} ==See Also== *[[SetClimateSunGlareTexture]] *[[GetClimateSunTexture]] *[[SetClimateSunTexture]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Climate Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the Sun texture path of a climate. |name = GetClimateSunTexture |returnType = texturePath:string_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = climate |Type = ref }} |example = GetClimateSunTexture WastelandClimate }} ==See Also== *[[SetClimateSunTexture]] *[[GetClimateSunGlareTexture]] *[[SetClimateSunGlareTexture]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Climate Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the value of a climate's numeric trait (determined by traitID). |name = GetClimateTraitNumeric |returnType = traitValue:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = climate |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-6 }} ==Climate Numeric Trait IDs== 
0	Sunrise Begin (minutes)
1	Sunrise End (minutes)
2	Sunset Begin (minutes)
3	Sunset End (minutes)
4	Volatility
5	Moons (0 - None, 1 - Masser, 2 - Secunda, 3 - Both)
6	Phase Length (days)
|example = GetClimateTraitNumeric WastelandClimate 2 Will return the Sunset Begin time of WastelandClimate. }} ==See Also== *[[SetClimateTraitNumeric]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Climate Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = For an actor in combat, returns an '''[[array_var]]''' containing all [[Actor]]s which the game considers allies of the calling actor, at that particular moment. |name = GetCombatAllies |returnType = allies:array_var |referenceType = reference |example = let aAllies := actorRef.GetCombatAllies }} ==See Also== *[[GetCombatTargets]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

revision.comment: Adding to original actor functions category
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns 1 if combat is disabled for the calling actor. |name = GetCombatDisabled |returnType = combatDisabled:0/1 |referenceType = reference |example = set bCombatDisabled to actorRef.GetCombatDisabled }} ==See Also== *[[SetCombatDisabled]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns a combat style's flag value (determined by flagID). |name = GetCombatStyleFlag |returnType = flagValue:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = combatStyle |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagID |Type = int |Value = 0-8 }} ==Combat Style Flag IDs== 
0	Choose Attack Using % Chance
1	Melee Alert OK
2	Flee Based on Personal Survival
4	Ignore Threats
5	Ignore Damaging Self
6	Ignore Damaging Group
7	Ignore Damaging Spectators
8	Cannot Use Stealthboy
|example = GetCombatStyleFlag FollowersCombatStyleRanged 4 }} ==See Also== *[[SetCombatStyleFlag]] *[[GetCombatStyleTraitNumeric]] *[[SetCombatStyleTraitNumeric]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Combat Style Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the value of a combat style's numeric trait (determined by traitID). |name = GetCombatStyleTraitNumeric |returnType = traitValue:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = combatStyle |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-63 }} ==Combat Style Numeric Trait IDs== 
0	Cover Search Radius
1	Take Cover Chance
2	Wait Time Min.
3	Wait Time Max.
4	Wait to Fire Timer Min.
5	Wait to Fire Timer Max.
6	Fire Timer Min.
7	Fire Timer Max.
8	Ranged Weap Range Mult. Min.
9	Weapon Restrictions (0 - None, 1 - Melee Only, 2 - Ranged Only)
10	Ranged Weap Range Mult. Max.
11	Max Targeting FOV
12	Combat Radius
13	Semi-Auto Firing Delay Mult. Min.
14	Semi-Auto Firing Delay Mult. Max.
15	Dodge % Chance
16	Left/Right % Chance
17	Dodge L/R Timer Min.
18	Dodge L/R Timer Max.
19	Dodge Forward Timer Min.
20	Dodge Forward Timer Max.
21	Dodge Back Timer Min.
22	Dodge Back Timer Max.
23	Idle Timer Min.
24	Idle Timer Max.
25	Block % Chance
26	Attack % Chance
27	Recoil/Stagger Bonus to Attack
28	Unconscious Bonus to Attack
29	Hand-to-Hand Bonus to Attack
30	Power Attack % Chance
31	Recoil/Stagger Bonus to Power
32	Unconscious Bonus to Power Attack
33	Power Attack % Chance Normal
34	Power Attack % Chance Forward
35	Power Attack % Chance Back
36	Power Attack % Chance Left
37	Power Attack % Chance Right
38	Hold Timer Min.
39	Hold Timer Max.
40	Acrobatic Dodge % Chance
41	Rushing Attack % Chance
42	Rushing Attack Distance Mult.
43	Dodge Fatigue Mod Base
44	Dodge Fatigue Mod Mult.
45	Encumb. Speed Mod Base
46	Encumb. Speed Mod Mult.
47	Dodge While Under Attack Mult.
48	Dodge Not Under Attack Mult.
49	Dodge Back While Under Attack Mult.
50	Dodge Back Not Under Attack Mult.
51	Dodge Forward While Attacking Mult.
52	Dodge Forward Not Attacking Mult.
53	Block Skill Modifier Base
54	Block Skill Modifier Mult.
55	Block While Under Attack Mult.
56	Block Not Under Attack Mult.
57	Attack Skill Modifier Base
58	Attack Skill Modifier Mult.
59	Attack While Under Attack Mult.
60	Attack Not Under Attack Mult.
61	Attack During Block Mult.
62	Power Att. Fatigue Mod Base
63	Power Att. Fatigue Mod Mult.
|example = GetCombatStyleTraitNumeric FollowersCombatStyleRanged 12 Will return the Combat Radius of FollowersCombatStyleRanged. }} ==See Also== *[[SetCombatStyleTraitNumeric]] *[[GetCombatStyleFlag]] *[[SetCombatStyleFlag]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Combat Style Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = For an actor in combat, returns an '''[[array_var]]''' containing all [[Actor]]s which the game considers potential targets of the calling actor, at that particular moment. |name = GetCombatTargets |returnType = targets:array_var |referenceType = reference |example = let aTargets := actorRef.GetCombatTargets }} ==See Also== *[[GetCombatAllies]] *[[GetCombatTarget]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |originVersion = 36.00 |summary = Returns an '''[[array_var]]''' containing all [[Object]]/[[Actor]] [[reference]]s whose collision geometry the calling reference has '''physical contact''' with (i.e. is "touching"). |name = GetContactRefs |returnType = array_var |referenceType = reference |example = let aContactRefs := objectRef.GetContactRefs }} ==See Also== *[[GetHasContact]] *[[GetHasContactBase]] *[[GetHasContactType]] *[[GetObjectUnderFeet]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Object Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns 1 if the Respawns flag in enabled for the passed Container. |name = GetContainerRespawns |referenceType = referen..."
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns 1 if the Respawns flag in enabled for the passed Container. |name = GetContainerRespawns |referenceType = reference |returnType = respawns:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = set bRespawns to GetContainerRespawns CampSearchlightFootlocker set bRespawns to containerRef.GetContainerRespawns }} ==See Also== *[[SetContainerRespawns]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns a [[:Category:creatures|creatures]] melee attack damage. |name = GetCreatureDamage |returnType = damage:int |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = RexRef.GetCreatureDamage
GetCreatureDamage Rex }} ==Notes== *This attribute is only applicable to creatures that are unarmed and attack using parts of their skeleton. ==See Also== *[[SetCreatureDamage]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Creature Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an [[array_var]] containing the set of model-paths in the [[Creature]]'s model-list that are toggled ON. |name = GetCreatureModels |returnType = modelPaths:array_var |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = let aModelPaths := RexRef.GetCreatureModels
let aModelPaths := GetCreatureModels Rex }} ==See Also== *[[ToggleCreatureModel]] *[[CreatureHasModel]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Creature Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the [[Base Form]] of the '''[[Ammo]]''' currently held in the [[calling]] [[actor]]'s equipped [[weapon]]. |name = GetCurrentAmmo |returnType = ammo:ref |referenceType = reference |example = set rAmmo to actorRef.GetCurrentAmmo }} ==See Also== *[[GetCurrentAmmoRounds]] *[[GetPlayerCurrentAmmo]] *[[GetPlayerCurrentAmmoRounds]] *[[SetCurrentAmmoRounds]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Ammo Functions]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the current number of rounds in the clip/magazine of the calling actor's equipped weapon. |name = GetCurrentAmmoRounds |returnType = ammoRounds:int |referenceType = reference |example = set iAmmoRounds to actorRef.GetCurrentAmmoRounds }} ==See Also== *[[SetCurrentAmmoRounds]] *[[GetCurrentAmmo]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Ammo Functions]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the current climate. |name = GetCurrentClimate |returnType = climate:ref |example = set rClimate to GetCurrentClimate }} ==See Also== *[[SetCurrentClimate]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Climate Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions_(ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the stable '''[[AI Package]]''' currently in use by the calling actor. Unlike [[GetCurrentPackage]], this ignores any "interrupt" packages (combat, dialogue, etc.) running on the actor. |name = GetCurrentStablePackage |returnType = package:ref |referenceType = reference |example = set rPackage to actorRef.GetCurrentStablePackage }} ==See Also== *[[GetBasePackages]] *[[GetCurrentAIPackage]] *[[GetCurrentPackage]] *[[GetIsCurrentPackage]] *[[GetNthPackage]] *[[GetPackageCount]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:AI Functions]] [[Category:Package Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the current weather. |name = GetCurrentWeather |returnType = weather:ref |example = set rWeather to GetCurrentWeather }} [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Weather Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weather Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the current X/Y screen coordinate of the mouse cursor (in MenuMode only). |name = GetCursorPos |returnType = pos:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = axis |Type = X/Y }} |example = set fCursorX to GetCursorPos X
set fCursorY to GetCursorPos Y }} ==Notes== *The screen's (0, 0) is at the top-left corner. ==See Also== *[[SetCursorPos]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the time remaining until the next iteration of the quest's script. |name = GetDelayElapsed |returnType = seconds:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = quest |Type = ref }} |example = GetDelayElapsed vCCExplorerBark }} ==See Also== *[[SetDelayElapsed]] *[[GetQuestDelay]] *[[SetQuestDelay]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Script Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Script_Functions]] [[Category:Quest Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the Health value of the Destruction Data of the specified Object. |name = GetDestructionDataHealth |returnType = health:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = object |Type = ref }} |example = set iDstrHealth to GetDestructionDataHealth ElectricBox01 }} ==Notes== *Obviously, this function is only usable with object types that have Destruction Data. ==See Also== *[[SetDestructionDataHealth]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Destruction Data Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |originVersion = 11.00 |summary = Returns the number of destruction stages in the Destruction Data of the specified Object. |name = GetDestructionDataNumStages |returnType = stages:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = object |Type = ref }} |example = set iStages to GetDestructionDataNumStages ElectricBox01 }} ==Notes== *Obviously, this function is only usable with object types that have Destruction Data. ==See Also== *[[GetDestructionStage]] *[[OnDestructionStageChange]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Destruction Data Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns whether or not the Destruction Data of the specified Object is VATS Targetable. |name = GetDestructionDataTargetable |returnType = targetable:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = object |Type = ref }} |example = set bTargetable to GetDestructionDataTargetable ElectricBox01 }} ==Notes== *Obviously, this function is only usable with object types that have Destruction Data. ==See Also== *[[SetDestructionDataTargetable]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Destruction Data Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an '''[[array_var]]''' containing all [[Actor]]s currently '''[[:Category:Detection|detected]]''' by the calling actor. |name = GetDetectedActors |returnType = detectedActors:array_var |referenceType = reference |example = let aDetected := actorRef.GetDetectedActors }} ==See Also== *[[GetDetectingActors]] *[[GetActorLightAmount]] *[[GetActorsByProcessingLevel]] *[[GetRefs]] *[[GetFollowers]] *[[GetTeammates]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Detection Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an '''[[array_var]]''' containing all actors currently '''[[:Category:Detection|detecting]]''' the [[calling]] [[Actor]]. |name = GetDetectingActors |returnType = detectingActors:array_var |referenceType = reference |example = let aDetecting := actorRef.GetDetectingActors }} ==See Also== *[[GetDetectedActors]] *[[GetActorLightAmount]] *[[GetActorsByProcessingLevel]] *[[GetRefs]] *[[GetFollowers]] *[[GetTeammates]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Detection Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns a value indicating how [[:Category:Detection|visible]] the target actor is to the calling actor. A value lower than zero indicates the calling actor does not detect the target actor, whilst a value higher than zero indicates the target actor is detected by the calling actor. The higher the absolute value, the more the target actor is visible to/concealed from the calling actor. |name = GetDetectionValue |returnType = detectionValue:int |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = targetActor |Type = ref }} |example = set iDetectionValue to actorRef.GetDetectionValue player Returns how visible the player character is to actorRef. }} ==Notes== *This value stops updating after the calling actor has [[GetDead|died]]. It is only refreshed when exiting the actor's cell, where it will be set to -100. ==See Also== *[[GetDetected]] / [[IsActorDetected]] *[[GetDetectionLevel]] *[[GetPCDetectionState]] *[[GetWeaponDetectionSoundLevel]] / [[SetWeaponDetectionSoundLevel]] *[[CreateDetectionEvent]] *[[ForceActorDetectionValue]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Detection Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the time (seconds) remaining until the calling projectile detonates. |name = GetDetonationTimer |returnType = detonationTime:float |referenceType = reference |example = set fDetonationTime to projRef.GetDetonationTimer }} ==Notes== *This function returns -1 on projectiles without the "Explosion" and "Alt. Trigger" flags. *Used on a mine that hasn't been triggered yet, this function returns a very large integer, which can be equated to > 99999. ==See Also== *[[SetDetonationTimer]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions]] [[Category:Time Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an '''[[array_var]]''' containing the world references (as opposed to '''Inventory References''') of weapons dropped by the calling actor, either upon death or after being successfully targeted in VATS. Note that, while these weapons do appear in the calling actor's inventory, they are not visible to inventory functions ([[GetItemCount]], [[RemoveItem]], etc.). |name = GetDroppedRefs |returnType = droppedRefs:array_var |referenceType = reference |example = let aDroppedRefs := actorRef.GetDroppedRefs }} ==See Also== [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns whether or not the specified flag (determined by flagID) is set for the specified '''[[Actor Effect]]/[[Object Effect]]/[[Ingestible]] Item'''. |name = GetEffectFlag |returnType = isFlagSet:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = effectForm |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagID |Type = int }} ==Actor Effect Flag IDs== 
0	No Auto-Calc
2	PC Start Effect
4	Area Effect Ignores LOS
5	Script Effect Always Applies
6	Disallow Absorb/Reflect
7	Force Touch Explode
==Object Effect Flag IDs== 
0	No Auto-Calc
2	Hide Effect
==Ingestible Item Flag IDs== 
0	No Auto-Calc
1	Food Item
2	Medicine
|example = set bIsFlagSet to GetEffectFlag EnchClothingDuster 2 }} ==See Also== *[[SetEffectFlag]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Effect Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an array of all the references of which the calling reference is the enable parent. |name = GetEnableChildren |returnType = array_var |referenceType = reference |example = let aEnableChildren := objectRef.GetEnableChildren }} ==See Also== *[[GetLinkedChildren]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an explosion's flag value (determined by flagID). |name = GetExplosionFlag |returnType = flagValue:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = explosion |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagID |Type = int |Value = 1-6 }} ==Explosion Flag IDs== 
1	Always Use World Orientation
2	Knock Down - Always
3	Knock Down - By Formula
4	Ignore LOS Check
5	Push Explosion Source Ref Only
6	Ignore Image Space Swap
|example = GetExplosionFlag 40mmGrenadeExplosion 5 }} ==See Also== *[[SetExplosionFlag]] *[[GetExplosionTraitNumeric]] *[[SetExplosionTraitNumeric]] *[[GetExplosionTraitForm]] *[[SetExplosionTraitForm]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Explosion Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the value of an explosion's form-type trait (determined by traitID). |name = GetExplosionTraitForm |returnType = traitForm:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = explosion |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-4 }} ==Explosion Form Trait IDs== 
0	Light
1	Sound #1
2	Sound #2
3	Impact Data Set
4	Placed Impact Object
5	Enchantment
6	IS Mod
|example = GetExplosionTraitForm 40mmGrenadeExplosion 2 Will return the Sound #2 of 40mmGrenadeExplosion. }} ==See Also== *[[SetExplosionTraitForm]] *[[GetExplosionTraitNumeric]] *[[SetExplosionTraitNumeric]] *[[GetExplosionFlag]] *[[SetExplosionFlag]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Explosion Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the value of a numeric trait (determined by traitID) of the specified '''Explosion'''. |name = GetExplosionTraitNumeric |returnType = traitValue:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = explosion |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-7 }} ==Explosion Numeric Trait IDs== 
0	Force
1	Damage
2	Radius
3	IS Radius
4	RAD Level
5	RAD Dissipation Time
6	RAD Radius
7	Sound Level
|example = set fValue to GetExplosionTraitNumeric 40mmGrenadeExplosion 2 Will return the Radius of 40mmGrenadeExplosion. }} ==See Also== *[[SetExplosionTraitNumeric]] *[[GetExplosionTraitForm]] *[[SetExplosionTraitForm]] *[[GetExplosionFlag]] *[[SetExplosionFlag]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Explosion Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the cell at the specified (X, Y) grid coordinates in the specified worldspace. |name = GetExteriorCell |returnType = cell:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = worldspace |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = coordX |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = coordY |Type = int }} |example = set rCell to GetExteriorCell WastelandNV -22 -19 }}
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Cell Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Cell Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns a faction's reputation type. |name = GetFactionReputationType |returnType = reputation:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = faction |Type = ref }} |example = GetFactionReputationType NCRFactionNV }} ==See Also== *[[SetFactionReputationType]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Faction Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Faction Functions]] [[Category:Reputation Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the length of time the calling actor has been falling. |name = GetFallTimeElapsed |returnType = fallTimeElapsed:float |referenceType = reference |example = set fTime to actorRef.GetFallTimeElapsed }} ==See Also== *[[GetFallTimeRemaining]] *[[ResetFallTime]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Time Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the length of time remaining until the calling actor, if falling, will hit the ground. |name = GetFallTimeRemaining |returnType = fallTimeRemaining:float |referenceType = reference |example = set fTime to actorRef.GetFallTimeRemaining }} ==See Also== *[[GetFallTimeElapsed]] *[[ResetFallTime]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Time Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an [[array]] of all the [[Actor]]s that are currently following the calling reference. |name = GetFollowers |returnType = array_var |referenceType = reference |example = ActorRef.GetFollowers }} ==See Also== *[[GetTeammates]] (Same thing but for Teammates) *[[GetPlayerTeammate]] *[[SetPlayerTeammate]] *[[GetPlayerTeammateCount]] *[[OpenTeammateContainer]] *[[GetActorsByProcessingLevel]] *[[GetRefs]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:Follower Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the path to the source-file (.fnt) of the specified font type (1-8, 10-89). |name = GetFontFile |returnType = filePath:string_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = fontType |Type = int |Value = 1-8, 10-89 }} |example = let sFilePath := GetFontFile 2 }} ==See Also== *[[SetFontFile]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:String Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the '''Description''' text of the specified form (e.g. Note, Perk, Message, Terminal, Weapon Mod, Challenge, Loading Screen, etc.). |name = GetFormDescription |returnType = description:string_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref }} |example = let sDescr := GetFormDescription ActionBoy
let sDescr := GetFormDescription GSFixedRadioMsg
let sDescr := GetFormDescription ModNV127mmPistolSilencer }} ==See Also== *[[SetFormDescription]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Message Functions]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:String Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the current difficulty-level of the game. The returned value ranges from 0 for '''Very Easy''', to 4 for '''Very Hard'''. |name = GetGameDifficulty |returnType = difficulty:int |example = set iDifficulty to GetGameDifficulty }} ==See Also== *[[SetGameDifficulty]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the value of a [[Global Variable]]. |name = GetGlobalVariable |returnType = value:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = global |Type = ref }} |example = 
int	iModIndex
ref	rGlobalVar
float	fValue


set iModIndex to GetModIndex "LonesomeRoad.esm"
set rGlobalVar to BuildRef iModIndex 13842

set fValue to GetGlobalVariable rGlobalVar
}} ==Notes== *This function is meant to be used for retrieving the values of external global variables (i.e. from other mods). You first use [[BuildRef]] or [[GetFormFromMod]] and create a reference to the global, on which this function can then be called. ==See Also== *[[SetGlobalVariable]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Global Variable Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the model (Art File) path of the specified Grass form. |name = GetGrassModel |returnType = modelPath:string_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = grass |Type = ref }} |example = let sPath := GetGrassModel GrassWasteland01 }} ==See Also== *[[SetGrassModel]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Grass Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the value of a numeric trait (determined by traitID) of the specified Grass form. |name = GetGrassTraitNumeric |returnType = traitValue:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = grass |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-9 }} ==Grass Numeric Trait IDs== 
0	Density
1	Min. Slope
2	Max. Slope
3	Units from Water Amount
4	Units from Water Type (see below)
5	Position Range
6	Height Range
7	Color Range
8	Wave Period
9	Flags Bitmask (see below)
'''Units from Water Types:''' 
0	Above - At Least
1	Above - At Most
2	Below - At Least
3	Below - At Most
4	Either - At Least
5	Either - At Most
6	Either - At Most Above
7	Either - At Most Below
'''Flags:''' 
1	Vertex Lighting
2	Uniform Scaling
4	Fit to Slope
|example = set fPosRange to GetGrassTraitNumeric GrassWasteland01 5 Will return the Position Range of GrassWasteland01. }} ==See Also== *[[SetGrassTraitNumeric]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Grass Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |originVersion = 36.00 |summary = Returns whether the calling reference has '''physical contact''' (i.e. is "touching") with the collision geometry of the specified object/actor reference (or, if a '''Form List''' is passed, with any reference in the list). |name = GetHasContact |returnType = hasContact:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = objectRefOrList |Type = ref }} |example = set bHasContact to objectRef.GetHasContact someRef
set bHasContact to objectRef.GetHasContact RefList }} ==See Also== *[[GetHasContactBase]] *[[GetHasContactType]] *[[GetContactRefs]] *[[GetObjectUnderFeet]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns whether the calling reference has '''physical contact''' (i.e. is "touching") with the collision geometry of an object/actor reference with the specified '''base form''' (or, if a '''Form List''' is passed, with any base form in the list). |name = GetHasContactBase |returnType = hasContact:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = baseFormOrList |Type = ref }} |example = set bHasContact to objectRef.GetHasContactBase baseForm
set bHasContact to objectRef.GetHasContactBase baseFormList }} ==See Also== *[[GetHasContact]] *[[GetHasContactType]] *[[GetContactRefs]] *[[GetObjectUnderFeet]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns whether the calling reference has '''physical contact''' (i.e. is "touching") with the collision geometry of an object/actor reference whose base type matches the specified [[Form Type IDs|type code]]. |name = GetHasContactType |returnType = hasContact:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = typeID |Type = int }} |example = set bHasContact to objectRef.GetHasContactType 28 }} ==See Also== *[[GetHasContact]] *[[GetHasContactBase]] *[[GetContactRefs]] *[[GetObjectUnderFeet]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns whether or not the calling reference's model (NIF) has '''Phantom Shape'''-type collision geometry. |name = GetHasPhantom |returnType = hasPhantom:0/1 |referenceType = reference |example = set bHasPhantom to objectRef.GetHasPhantom }} ==Notes== This function will always return 1 when called on an actor. ==See Also== [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the loop times of the passed '''Idle Animation'''. If getMax is non-zero, returns the Max times, otherwise returns the Min times. |name = GetIdleLoopTimes |returnType = loopTimes:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = idleAnim |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = getMax |Type = 0/1 }} |example = set iLoopTimes to GetIdleLoopTimes RepairWall02 1 Returns the Max loop times of RepairWall02. }} ==See Also== *[[SetIdleLoopTimes]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Animation Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the model path of the specified '''[[Impact Data]]'''. |name = GetImpactDataModel |returnType = modelPath:string_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = impactData |Type = ref }} |example = let sModel := GetImpactDataModel DeathClawsFleshImpact }} ==See Also== *[[SetImpactDataModel]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Impact Data Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns a trait value (determined by traitID) of the specified '''Impact Data'''. |name = GetImpactDataTrait |returnType = trait..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns a trait value (determined by traitID) of the specified '''Impact Data'''. |name = GetImpactDataTrait |returnType = traitValue:float/ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = impactData |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-17 }} ==Impact Data Trait IDs== 
0	Duration
1	Orientation (0 - Surface Normal, 1 - Proj. Vector, 2 - Proj. Reflection)
2	Angle Threshold
3	Placement Radius
4	Sound Level (0 - Loud, 1 - Normal, 2 - Silent)
5	Texture Set
6	Sound 1
7	Sound 2
8	Min. Width
9	Max. Width
10	Min. Height
11	Max. Height
12	Depth
13	Shininess
14	Scale
15	Passes
16	Flag Bitmask (see below)
17	Color (RRRGGGBBB)
'''Impact Data Flags:''' 
1	No Decal Data
2	Has Parallax
4	Alpha - Blending
8	Alpha - Testing
|example = 
set fValue to GetImpactDataTrait DeathClawsFleshImpact 13
Will return the Shininess of DeathClawsFleshImpact. 
set rValue to GetImpactDataTrait DeathClawsFleshImpact 5
Will return the Texture Set of DeathClawsFleshImpact. }} ==See Also== *[[SetImpactDataTraitNumeric]] *[[SetImpactDataTraitForm]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Impact Data Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns whether interaction with the calling reference, or (optional) Base Form, has been disabled by [[SetInteractionDisabled]]. |name = GetInteractionDisabled |returnType = disabled:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = set bDisabled to objectRef.GetInteractionDisabled set bDisabled to GetInteractionDisabled CraigBoone }} ==See Also== *[[SetInteractionDisabled]] *[[GetInteractionDisabledType]] *[[SetInteractionDisabledType]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns whether interaction with objects of the specified [[Form Type IDs|type code]] has been disabled by [[SetInteractionDisabledType]]. |name = GetInteractionDisabledType |returnType = disabled:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = typeCode |Type = int }} |example = set bDisabled to GetInteractionDisabledType 28 }} ==See Also== *[[SetInteractionDisabledType]] *[[GetInteractionDisabled]] *[[SetInteractionDisabled]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]]

revision.comment: ACTUALLY fixed it?
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns 1 if the passed object is an [[Item]].
To clarify, this returns 1 if the object is of any [[Inventory_Item_Type_Codes|Formtype which can be stored in an inventory]]. |name = GetIsItem |returnType = isItem:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = set bIsItem to objectRef.GetIsItem set bIsItem to GetIsItem Ashtray }} ==See Also== *[[Inventory_Item_Type_Codes|Inventory Item Type Codes]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns 1 if the specified [[Ingestible]] [[item]] is considered a poison. |name = GetIsPoison |returnType = isPoison:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ingestible |Type = ref }} |example = set bPoison to GetIsPoison SilverSting }} ==Notes== *An Ingestible item is considered a poison only if all of its effects are detrimental and hostile. ==See Also== *[[GetIsPoisoned]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Effect Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns 1 if the calling actor reference is under the influence of a poison-type effect. |name = GetIsPoisoned |returnType = poisoned:0/1 |referenceType = reference |example = ActorRef.GetIsPoisoned }} ==Notes== *This function can be used as a condition function (where applicable). ==See Also== *[[Base Effect]] *[[GetIsPoison]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns whether the calling actor is in ragdoll mode (i.e. being knocked/pushed over by an explosion or by [[PushActorAway]], etc.). |name = GetIsRagdolled |returnType = ragdolled:0/1 |referenceType = reference |example = set bRagdolled to actorRef.GetIsRagdolled }} ==See Also== [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]] [[Category:Movement Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the health of the calling item. Unlike [[GetEquippedCurrentHealth]], this function can also called on an [[inventory reference]]. The calling item reference does not have to be equipped. The function can return the health value in one of three ways, depending on the optional argument '''percOrBase''': 
0	Current Health (default)
1	Current Health Percent (0.0 to 1.0)
2	Base Health (incl. bonuses from weapon mods)
|name = GetItemRefCurrentHealth |returnType = health:float |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = percOrBase |Type = 0/1/2 |Optional = y }} |example = set fCurrHealth to rItemRef.GetItemRefCurrentHealth set fHealthPerc to rItemRef.GetItemRefCurrentHealth 1 set fBaseHealth to rItemRef.GetItemRefCurrentHealth 2 }} ==See Also== *[[SetItemRefCurrentHealth]] *[[GetInvRefsForItem]] *[[GetFirstRefForItem]] *[[GetSelectedItemRef]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Reference Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the Nth potential grass type of a Land Texture. |name = GetLandTextureNthGrass |returnType = grass:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = landTexture |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = grassIndex |Type = int }} |example = GetLandTextureNthGrass LScrubDirtCanyon01Grass 0 }} ==See Also== *[[GetLandTextureNumGrasses]] *[[LandTextureAddGrass]] *[[LandTextureRemoveGrass]] *[[GetLandTextureTextureSet]] *[[SetLandTextureTextureSet]] *[[GetLandTextureTraitNumeric]] *[[SetLandTextureTraitNumeric]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Land Texture Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the number of potential grass types of a Land Texture. |name = GetLandTextureNumGrasses |returnType = grasses:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = landTexture |Type = ref }} |example = GetLandTextureNumGrasses LScrubDirtCanyon01Grass }} ==See Also== *[[GetLandTextureNthGrass]] *[[LandTextureAddGrass]] *[[LandTextureRemoveGrass]] *[[GetLandTextureTextureSet]] *[[SetLandTextureTextureSet]] *[[GetLandTextureTraitNumeric]] *[[SetLandTextureTraitNumeric]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Land Texture Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the Texture-Set of a Land Texture. |name = GetLandTextureTextureSet |returnType = textureSet:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = landTexture |Type = ref }} |example = GetLandTextureTextureSet LScrubDirtCanyon01Grass }} ==See Also== *[[SetLandTextureTextureSet]] *[[GetLandTextureTraitNumeric]] *[[SetLandTextureTraitNumeric]] *[[GetLandTextureNumGrasses]] *[[GetLandTextureNthGrass]] *[[LandTextureAddGrass]] *[[LandTextureRemoveGrass]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Land Texture Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the value of a Land Texture's numeric trait (determined by traitID). |name = GetLandTextureTraitNumeric |returnType = traitValue:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = landTexture |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-3 }} ==Land Texture Numeric Trait IDs== 
0	Material Type
1	Friction
2	Restitution
3	Texture Specular Exponent
|example = GetLandTextureTraitNumeric LScrubDirtCanyon01Grass 1 Will return the Friction of LScrubDirtCanyon01Grass. }} ==See Also== *[[SetLandTextureTraitNumeric]] *[[GetLandTextureTextureSet]] *[[SetLandTextureTextureSet]] *[[GetLandTextureNumGrasses]] *[[GetLandTextureNthGrass]] *[[LandTextureAddGrass]] *[[LandTextureRemoveGrass]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Land Texture Functions (JIP)]]

revision.comment: Adding to original actor functions category
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns a leveled actor's actual, root base form. |name = GetLeveledActorBase |returnType = baseForm:ref |referenceType = reference |example = ActorRef.GetLeveledActorBase }} ==See Also== *[[GetActorTemplate]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns a Light's flag value (determined by flagID). |name = GetLightFlag |returnType = flagValue:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = light |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagID |Type = int |Value = 0-31 }} ==Light Flag IDs== 
0	Dynamic
1	Can Be Carried
2	Negative
3	Flicker
5	Off By Default
6	Flicker Slow
7	Pulse
8	Pulse Slow
11	Colour Shift
12	Colour Shift Slow
|example = GetLightFlag BasementLightFillWarm 6 }} ==See Also== *[[SetLightFlag]] *[[GetLightTraitNumeric]] *[[SetLightTraitNumeric]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Light Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Light_Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the value of a Light's numeric trait (determined by traitID). |name = GetLightTraitNumeric |returnType = traitValue:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = light |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-6 }} ==Light Numeric Trait IDs== 
0	Radius
1	Red
2	Green
3	Blue
4	Falloff Exponent
5	FOV
6	Fade
|example = GetLightTraitNumeric BasementLightFillWarm 0 Will return the Radius of BasementLightFillWarm. }} ==See Also== *[[SetLightTraitNumeric]] *[[GetLightFlag]] *[[SetLightFlag]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Light Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Light_Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

revision.comment: Added link to array functions
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an array of all the references that are linked to the calling reference. |name = GetLinkedChildren |returnType = array_var |referenceType = reference |example = let aLinkedChildren := objectRef.GetLinkedChildren }} ==See Also== *[[GetLinkedRef]] *[[SetLinkedReference]] *[[GetEnableChildren]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Array_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns 1 if the load-order has changed since the time the current loaded save was created. |name = GetLoadOrderChanged |returnType = loadOrderChanged:0/1 |example = GetLoadOrderChanged }} ==See Also== *[[ValidateModIndex]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Utility Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:File Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |originVersion = 36.00 |summary = Returns the acceleration (in [[Units|Game Units]] per second squared) due to '''gravity''' in the specified '''axis''' for the current cell. |name = GetLocalGravity |returnType = gravity:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = axis |Type = X/Y/Z }} |example = set fGravityZ to GetLocalGravity Z }} ==Notes== *Unless [[SetLocalGravityVector]] has been used, the X and Y components will always be zero.
==See Also== *[[SetLocalGravityVector]] *[[GetActorGravityMult]] *[[SetActorGravityMult]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Movement Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the maximal out of up to five passed values. |name = GetMaxOf |returnType = maximal:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = value1 |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = value2 |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = value3 |Type = float |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = value4 |Type = float |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = value5 |Type = float |Optional = y }} |example = GetMaxOf fVar1 fVar2
GetMaxOf fVar1 fVar2 fVar3 fVar4 fVar5 }} ==See Also== *[[GetMinOf]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Utility Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Math Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns a code indicating the current item-filter option selected in the specified menu. If useMenuTarget is non-zero, the function returns the filter selected for the target reference (right side). Otherwise, it returns the filter selected for the player (left side). Filter codes: 
0	None
1	Weapons
2	Apparel
3	Aid
4	Misc
5	Ammo
|name = GetMenuItemFilter |returnType = filterCode:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = menuCode |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = useMenuTarget |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }} |example = 
GetMenuItemFilter 1002
This will return the item-filter selected in the pip-boy's inventory screen. 
GetMenuItemFilter 1008 1
This will return the item-filter selected for the target actor/container in the container menu. }} ==Notes== *This function is usable only with the inventory, container and barter menus (1002, 1008 and 1053, respectively). ==See Also== *[[GetActiveMenuMode]] *[[GetActiveUIComponentName]] *[[GetActiveUIComponentFullName]] *[[GetActiveUIComponentID]] *[[GetMenuTargetRef]] *[[ClickMenuButton]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the reference from which the specified menu is derived (if currently open). The returned reference varies, depending on the menu: 
1008	The container/actor inventory currently open.
1009	The actor being spoken to.
1014	The door/container being picked.
1016	The underlying, source item stack (Inventory Reference).
1023	The Map Marker on which the mouse is over.
1027	The Perk on which the mouse is over.
1035	The item being repaired (Inventory Reference).
1053	The actor being bartered with.
1055	The terminal being hacked.
1056	The current target in VATS target-selection.
1057	The terminal being used.
1058	The actor (vendor) whose skills are used for repairing.
1061	The weapon being modded (Inventory Reference).
1075	The actor whose companion-wheel is being used.
1077	The actor whose skills are used for crafting.
1083	The opponent actor in a Caravan game.
1084	The Perk on which the mouse is over.
|name = GetMenuTargetRef |returnType = menuTarget:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = menuCode |Type = int }} |example = set rMenuTarget to GetMenuTargetRef 1009 }} ==See Also== *Can return [[Inventory Reference]]s. *[[GetActiveMenuMode]] *[[GetActiveUIComponentName]] *[[GetActiveUIComponentFullName]] *[[GetActiveUIComponentID]] *[[GetMenuItemFilter]] *[[ClickMenuButton]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns 1 if the specified '''[[Message]]''' form was disabled by [[SetMessageDisabled]]. |name = GetMessageDisabled |returnType = messageDisabled:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = message |Type = ref }} |example = set bMsgDisabled to GetMessageDisabled AddictionRemovalMsg }} ==See Also== *[[SetMessageDisabled]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Message Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the flags state, as a bitmask, of the specified '''[[Message]]''' form. |name = GetMessageFlags |returnType = flagState:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = message |Type = ref }} '''Message Flags:''' 
1	Is Message Box
2	Auto-display
|example = set iMsgFlags to GetMessageFlags AddictionRemovalMsg }} ==See Also== *[[SetMessageFlags]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Message Functions]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the minimal out of up to five passed values. |name = GetMinOf |returnType = minimal:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = value1 |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = value2 |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = value3 |Type = float |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = value4 |Type = float |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = value5 |Type = float |Optional = y }} |example = GetMinOf fVar1 fVar2
GetMinOf fVar1 fVar2 fVar3 fVar4 fVar5 }} ==See Also== *[[GetMaxOf]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Utility Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Math Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

revision.comment: Added texture functions category.
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns a '''string_var''' with the texture path (relative to the '''Data''' folder) of the '''Moon''', specified by moonType: 
0	Full
1	Three Wan
2	Half Wan
3	One Wan
4	No Moon
5	One Wax
6	Half Wax
7	Three Wax
|name = GetMoonTexture |returnType = texturePath:string_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = moonType |Type = int }} |example = 
let sTexture := GetMoonTexture 3
Returns the texture path of the "One Wan" moon. }} ==See Also== *[[SetMoonTexture]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Texture Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the Nth [[Actor Effect]] of an actor. |name = GetNthActorEffect |returnType = actorEffect:ref |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = effectIndex |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = CraigBooneRef.GetNthActorEffect 0
GetNthActorEffect 0 CraigBoone }} ==See Also== *[[GetNumActorEffects]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Effect Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an ammo type's Nth ammo effect. |name = GetNthAmmoEffect |returnType = ammoEffect:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ammo |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = effectIndex |Type = int }} |example = GetNthAmmoEffect Ammo12GaMagnum 0 }} ==See Also== *[[GetNumAmmoEffects]] *[[AddAmmoEffect]] *[[RemoveAmmoEffect]] *[[GetAmmoEffectTraitNumeric]] *[[SetAmmoEffectTraitNumeric]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Ammo Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Ammo Effect Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Ammo Functions]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the Debris (if any) of the Nth destruction stage in the Destruction Data of the specified Object. |name = GetNthDestructionStageDebris |returnType = debris:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = object |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = stageIndex |Type = int }} |example = set rDebris to GetNthDestructionStageDebris ElectricBox01 0 Will return the Debris of the first (index 0) destruction stage of ElectricBox01. }} ==Notes== *Obviously, this function is only usable with object types that have Destruction Data. ==See Also== *[[SetNthDestructionStageDebris]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Destruction Data Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the Explosion (if any) of the Nth destruction stage in the Destruction Data of the specified Object. |name = GetNthDestructionStageExplosion |returnType = explosion:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = object |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = stageIndex |Type = int }} |example = set rExplosion to GetNthDestructionStageExplosion ElectricBox01 0 Will return the Explosion of the first (index 0) destruction stage of ElectricBox01. }} ==Notes== *Obviously, this function is only usable with object types that have Destruction Data. ==See Also== *[[SetNthDestructionStageExplosion]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Destruction Data Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the Replacement Model path (if any) of the Nth destruction stage in the Destruction Data of the specified Object. |name = GetNthDestructionStageReplacement |returnType = modelPath:string_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = object |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = stageIndex |Type = int }} |example = let sReplacement := GetNthDestructionStageReplacement ElectricBox01 0 Will return the Replacement Model path of the first (index 0) destruction stage of ElectricBox01. }} ==Notes== *Obviously, this function is only usable with object types that have Destruction Data. ==See Also== *[[SetNthDestructionStageReplacement]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Destruction Data Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the value of a numeric trait (determined by traitID) of the Nth destruction stage in the Destruction Data of the specified Object. |name = GetNthDestructionStageTrait |returnType = traitValue:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = object |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = stageIndex |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-4 }} ==Destruction Stage Trait IDs== 
0	Health Percentage
1	Self Damage per Second
2	Model Damage Stage
3	Flags Bitmask (see below)
4	Debris Count
'''Flags:''' 
1	Cap Damage
2	Disable Object
4	Destroy Object
|example = set iDamageSec to GetNthDestructionStageTrait ElectricBox01 0 1 Will return the Self Damage per Second of the first (index 0) destruction stage of ElectricBox01. }} ==Notes== *Obviously, this function is only usable with object types that have Destruction Data. ==See Also== *[[SetNthDestructionStageTrait]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Destruction Data Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the '''Base Effect''' of the Nth effect-entry of the specified [[ingestible]]/[[Object Effect]]/[[Actor Effect]]. |name = GetNthEffectBase |returnType = baseEffect:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = effectForm |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = effectIndex |Type = int }} |example = GetNthEffectBase Stimpak 2
GetNthEffectBase EnchPowerArmor 0
GetNthEffectBase WithdrawalPsycho 1 }} ==See Also== *[[SetNthEffectBase]] *[[GetNumEffects]] *[[GetNthEffectTraitNumeric]] *[[SetNthEffectTraitNumeric]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Effect Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the value of a numeric trait (determined by traitID) of the Nth effect-entry of the specified [[ingestible]]/[[Object Effect]]/[[Actor Effect]]. |name = GetNthEffectTraitNumeric |returnType = traitValue:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = effectForm |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = effectIndex |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-3 }} ==Effect Numeric Trait IDs== 
0	Magnitude
1	Area
2	Duration (Seconds)
3	Range (0 - Self, 1 - Touch, 2 - Target)
|example = GetNthEffectTraitNumeric Stimpak 0 2 Will return the Duration of the effect-entry at index 0 of Stimpak. }} ==See Also== *[[SetNthEffectTraitNumeric]] *[[GetNumEffects]] *[[GetNthEffectBase]] *[[SetNthEffectBase]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Effect Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the number of [[Actor Effect]]s of an actor. |name = GetNumActorEffects |returnType = effects:int |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = set iEffects to actorRef.GetNumActorEffects
set iEffects to GetNumActorEffects AngelaWilliams }} ==See Also== *[[GetNthActorEffect]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Effect Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an ammo type's number of ammo effects. |name = GetNumAmmoEffects |returnType = effects:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ammo |Type = ref }} |example = GetNumAmmoEffects Ammo12GaMagnum }} ==See Also== *[[GetNthAmmoEffect]] *[[AddAmmoEffect]] *[[RemoveAmmoEffect]] *[[GetAmmoEffectTraitNumeric]] *[[SetAmmoEffectTraitNumeric]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Ammo Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Ammo Effect Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Ammo Functions]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the number of effect-entries of the specified [[ingestible]]/[[Object Effect]]/[[Actor Effect]]. |name = GetNumEffects |returnType = effects:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = effectForm |Type = ref }} |example = GetNumEffects Stimpak
GetNumEffects EnchPowerArmor
GetNumEffects WithdrawalPsycho }} ==See Also== *[[GetTempEffects]] *[[GetNthEffectBase]] *[[SetNthEffectBase]] *[[GetNthEffectTraitNumeric]] *[[SetNthEffectTraitNumeric]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Effect Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns either the length(X), width(Y) or height(Z) (in game [[Units]]) of a physical game-object. |name = GetObjectDimensions |alias = GetObjDim |returnType = dimension:float |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = axis |Type = X/Y/Z }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = set fDimX to objectRef.GetObjectDimensions X
set fDimZ to GetObjectDimensions Z NukaVendingMachine }} ==Notes== *The dimensions are the same as those of the bounding-box encapsulating the object, in the GECK. ==See Also== [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Movement Functions]]

revision.comment: Adding to original actor functions category
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the object reference the calling actor is standing on. |name = GetObjectUnderFeet |returnType = objUnderFeet:ref |referenceType = reference |example = set rObject to actorRef.GetObjectUnderFeet }} ==See Also== *[[GetHasContact]] *[[GetHasContactBase]] *[[GetHasContactType]] *[[GetContactRefs]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an integer representing the Player character's current '''detection state'''. Possible return values and their meaning: 
-1	N/A (Main Menu / Load Screen)
 0	HIDDEN
 1	CAUTION
 2	DETECTED
 3	DANGER
Note that the player does not have to be sneaking/crouched for this function to return results such as 0 (HIDDEN). |name = GetPCDetectionState |returnType = detectionState:int |example = set iDetectionState to GetPCDetectionState }} ==Notes== *May not return the expected value if [[OnFire]] is used and the calling actor is hidden, the detection state might not be updated immediatly after the weapon is fired and the block is triggered, so it's best to use [[CallAfterFrames]] to check a few frames later so that GetPCDetectionState will return an expected value. ==See Also== *[[GetDetectionLevel]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]] [[Category:Detection Functions]] [[Category:Player Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns 1 if the player has fast-travelled since the last time this command was called from the calling script. |name = GetPCFastTravelled |returnType = fastTravelled:0/1 |example = set bFastTravelled to GetPCFastTravelled }} ==See Also== *[[GetPCMovedCell]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Player Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the last exterior door activated by the player. |name = GetPCLastExteriorDoor |returnType = lastExtDoor:ref |example = set rLastExtDoor to GetPCLastExteriorDoor }} ==See Also== *[[SetLastExtDoorActivated]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Door Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns 1 if the player has entered a new cell since the last time this command was called from the calling script, including transitioning from one exterior cell to another. |name = GetPCMovedCell |returnType = movedCell:0/1 |example = set bMovedCell to GetPCMovedCell }} ==See Also== *[[GetPCFastTravelled]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Cell_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Cell Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns 1 if the player is currently aiming with a ranged-weapon and playing any IS animation (1st or 3rd person). |name = GetPCUsingIronSights |returnType = usingIS:0/1 |example = set bUsingIS to GetPCUsingIronSights }} ==See Also== *[[GetPCUsingScope]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]] [[Category:Player Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns 1 if the player is currently zooming with a weapon scope. |name = GetPCUsingScope |returnType = usingScope:0/1 |example = set bUsingScope to GetPCUsingScope }} ==See Also== *[[GetPCUsingIronSights]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]] [[Category:Player Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an [[AI Package]]'s flag value (determined by flagID). |name = GetPackageFlag |returnType = flagValue:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = package |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagID |Type = int |Value = 0-28 }} ==Package Flag IDs== 
0	Offers Services
1	Must Reach Location
2	Must Complete
3	Lock Doors At Start
4	Lock Doors At End
5	Lock Doors At Location
6	Unlock Doors At Start
7	Unlock Doors At End
8	Unlock Doors At Location
9	Continue If PC Near
10	Once Per Day
11	Created
12	Skip Fallout Behaviour
13	Always Run
14	Script Package
17	Always Sneak
18	Allow Swimming
19	Allow Falls
20	Armor Unequipped
21	Weapons Unequipped
22	Defensive Combat
23	Weapons Drawn
24	No Idle Anims
25	Pretend In Combat
26	Continue During Combat
27	No Combat Alert
28	No Warn Attack Behaviour
|example = GetPackageFlag FollowersCassL38Package 18 }} ==See Also== *[[SetPackageFlag]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:AI Package Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:AI Functions]] [[Category:Package Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns whether or not the specified flag (determined by flagID) is set for the specified '''[[Perk]]'''. |name = GetPerkFlag |returnType = isFlagSet:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = perk |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagID |Type = int }} ==Perk Flag IDs== 
0	Is Trait;
1	Is Playable;
2	Is Hidden
|example = set bIsFlagSet to GetPerkFlag BloodyMess 2 }} ==See Also== *[[SetPerkFlag]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Perk Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Perk Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the radio station (object reference; Talking Activator) currently playing in the Pip-Boy (or 0 if the radio is turned off). When used as a Condition Function, returns either 0 if there is no radio station currently playing, or 1/0 depending on if the Talking Activator is the same as the reference passed to the function or not (respectively). |name = GetPipboyRadio |returnType = radioRef:ref |example = set radioRef to GetPipboyRadio }} [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Radio Functions]] [[Category:Pip-Boy Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the specified bound of the primitive of the calling reference. |name = GetPrimitiveBound |returnType = bound:float |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = axis |Type = X/Y/Z }} |example = set fPrimBoundX to objectRef.GetPrimitiveBound X }} ==Notes== *Objects with a primitive include cubic-activators, collision planes, etc. ==See Also== *[[SetPrimitiveBound]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the [[Projectile]]'s associated [[Explosion]] (if any). |name = GetProjectileExplosion |returnType = explosion:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = projectile |Type = ref }} |example = GetProjectileExplosion 40mmGrenadeProjectile }} ==See Also== *[[SetProjectileExplosion]] *[[GetProjectileTraitNumeric]] *[[SetProjectileTraitNumeric]] *[[GetProjectileFlag]] *[[SetProjectileFlag]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns a projectile's flag value (determined by flagID). |name = GetProjectileFlag |returnType = flagValue:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = projectile |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagID |Type = int |Value = 0-11 }} ==Projectile Flag IDs== 
0	Hitscan
1	Explosion
2	Alt. Trigger
3	Muzzle Flash
5	Can Be Disabled
6	Can Be Picked
7	Supersonic
8	Crit. Effect: Pins Limbs
9	Pass Through Small Transparent
10	Detonates
11	Rotation
|example = GetProjectileFlag 12GaSlugProjectile 7 }} ==See Also== *[[SetProjectileFlag]] *[[GetProjectileTraitNumeric]] *[[SetProjectileTraitNumeric]] *[[GetProjectileExplosion]] *[[SetProjectileExplosion]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the base damage that will be inflicted by the calling live-projectile, upon impact. By default, this equals the base damage of the source weapon. |name = GetProjectileRefDamage |returnType = damage:float |referenceType = reference |example = set fDamage to liveProjRef.GetProjectileRefDamage }} ==Finding Live-Projectile References== The only way to get a live-projectile reference is by "ref-walking" - that is, walking through projectile references using the [[GetFirstRef]] and [[GetNextRef]] functions. '''Example:''' 
set rProj to GetFirstRef 51 3
while rProj
	; Do something
	set rProj to GetNextRef
loop
==See Also== *[[SetProjectileRefDamage]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the total distance traveled by the calling live-projectile. This will return the correct value only for missile-type projectiles. |name = GetProjectileRefDistanceTraveled |returnType = distance:float |referenceType = reference |example = set fDistance to liveProjRef.GetProjectileRefDistanceTraveled }} ==Finding Live-Projectile References== The only way to get a live-projectile reference is by "ref-walking" - that is, walking through projectile references using the [[GetFirstRef]] and [[GetNextRef]] functions. '''Example:''' 
set rProj to GetFirstRef 51 3
while rProj
	; Do something
	set rProj to GetNextRef
loop
==See Also== *[[Get3DDistanceFromHitToNiNode]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions]] [[Category:Movement Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the real-time (in seconds) for which the calling live-projectile has existed. |name = GetProjectileRefLifeTime |returnType = lifeTime:float |referenceType = reference |example = set fLifeTime to liveProjRef.GetProjectileRefLifeTime }} ==Finding Live-Projectile References== The only way to get a live-projectile reference is by "ref-walking" - that is, walking through projectile references using the [[GetFirstRef]] and [[GetNextRef]] functions. '''Example:''' 
set rProj to GetFirstRef 51 3
while rProj
	; Do something
	set rProj to GetNextRef
loop
==See Also== [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions]] [[Category:Time Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the reference that fired the calling live-projectile. |name = GetProjectileRefSource |returnType = source:ref |referenceType = reference |example = set rProjSource to liveProjRef.GetProjectileRefSource }} ==Finding Live-Projectile References== The only way to get a live-projectile reference is by "ref-walking" - that is, walking through projectile references using the [[GetFirstRef]] and [[GetNextRef]] functions. '''Example:''' 
set rProj to GetFirstRef 51 3
while rProj
	; Do something
	set rProj to GetNextRef
loop
==See Also== *[[SetProjectileRefSource]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the speed multiplier of the calling live-projectile. The effective speed is equal to the base speed (as defined in the projectile's base-form) x speed multiplier. |name = GetProjectileRefSpeedMult |returnType = speedMult:float |referenceType = reference |example = set fSpeedMult to liveProjRef.GetProjectileRefSpeedMult }} ==Finding Live-Projectile References== The only way to get a live-projectile reference is by "ref-walking" - that is, walking through projectile references using the [[GetFirstRef]] and [[GetNextRef]] functions. '''Example:''' 
set rProj to GetFirstRef 51 3
while rProj
	; Do something
	set rProj to GetNextRef
loop
==See Also== *[[SetProjectileRefSpeedMult]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the weapon ([[Base Form]]) that fired the calling live-[[Projectile]]. |name = GetProjectileRefWeapon |returnType = weapon:baseForm |referenceType = reference |example = set rSourceWeapon to liveProjRef.GetProjectileRefWeapon }} ==Finding Live-Projectile References== One way to get a live-projectile reference is by "ref-walking" - that is, walking through projectile references using the [[GetFirstRef]] and [[GetNextRef]] functions. '''Example:''' 
set rProj to GetFirstRef 51 3
while rProj
	; Do something
	set rProj to GetNextRef
loop
Another is to retrieve the live-projectile reference provided by [[SetOnProjectileImpactEventHandler]], which is on the brink of expiring. ==See Also== *[[SetProjectileRefWeapon]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the value of a projectile's numeric trait (determined by traitID). |name = GetProjectileTraitNumeric |returnType = traitValue:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = projectile |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-14 }} ==Projectile Numeric Trait IDs== 
0	Projectile Type
1	Gravity
2	Speed
3	Range
4	Tracer Chance
5	Proximity (Explosion)
6	Timer (Explosion)
7	Muzzle Flash Duration
8	Fade Duration
9	Impact Force
10	Rotation X
11	Rotation Y
12	Rotation Z
13	Bouncy Mult.
14	Detection Sound Level
Projectile Types: 
1	Missile
2	Lobber
4	Beam
8	Flame
16	Continuous Beam
Detection Sound Levels: 
0	Loud
1	Normal
2	Silent
|example = GetProjectileTraitNumeric 12GaSlugProjectile 3 Will return the Range of 12GaSlugProjectile. }} ==See Also== *[[SetProjectileTraitNumeric]] *[[GetProjectileFlag]] *[[SetProjectileFlag]] *[[GetProjectileExplosion]] *[[SetProjectileExplosion]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the radiation level (rads/sec) at the calling actor's current position. Unlike the vanilla [[GetRadiationLevel]], which would only return a value when called on the player, this function may be called on any NPC. |name = GetRadiationLevelAlt |returnType = radLevel:float |referenceType = reference |example = set fRadLevel to actorRef.GetRadiationLevelAlt }} [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the main Recipe Category of the active Recipe Menu. That is the type of the Recipe Menu (Workbench, Reloading Bench, Campfire, etc.). |name = GetRecipeMenuCategory |returnType = recipeCategory:ref |example = set rCategory to GetRecipeMenuCategory }} ==See Also== *[[GetRecipeMenuSelection]] *[[GetMenuTargetRef]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Recipe Functions]] [[Category:Recipe_Functions_(JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the Recipe currently highlighted in the active Recipe Menu. |name = GetRecipeMenuSelection |returnType = recipe:ref |example = set rRecipe to GetRecipeMenuSelection }} ==See Also== *[[GetRecipeMenuCategory]] *[[GetSelectedItemRef]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Recipe Functions]] [[Category:Recipe_Functions_(JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the [[Form Type IDs|Type Code]] of the calling reference (unlike [[GetType]], which returns the type of the reference's Base Form). |name = GetRefType |returnType = refType:int |referenceType = reference |example = set iRefType to objectRef.GetRefType }} ==See Also== [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the form currently highlighted in the active menu.
