The function returns either a temporary [[Inventory Reference]], or a '''[[Base Form]]''', depending on the [[MenuMode|menu type]]: '''Inventory Reference''': *'''Inventory Menu''' (1002) *'''Container Menu''' (1008) *'''Pipboy Repair Menu''' (1035) *'''Barter Menu''' (1053) *'''Vendor Repair Menu''' (1058) *'''Item Mod Menu''' (1061) '''Base Form:''' *'''Pipboy DATA Menu''' (1023) - Quest/Note/Challenge *'''Pipboy STATS Menu''' (1003) - Perk |name = GetSelectedItemRef |returnType = selectedItem:ref |example = set rItemRef to GetSelectedItemRef }} ==Notes== *At least for the '''Inventory Menu''', this function does not work when called from console. For debugging, the following [[SetOnKeyDownEventHandler|OnKeyDown]] [[UDF]] can be used: 
scn GetSelectedItemRefUDF

; Enter this in console to enable it:
; SetOnKeyDownEventHandler GetSelectedItemRefUDF 1 43

; 43:    Back Slash key
; Feel free to use any other scancode.

int	iKeyID

begin Function { iKeyID }

	let ref rItemRef := GetSelectedItemRef
	let short isRef := IsReference rItemRef 
	TestExpr print "GetSelectedItemRefUDF: " + $rItemRef + ", isRef: " + $isRef + ", BaseForm: " + $(rItemRef.gbo)

end
**Other scancodes are listed [[DirectX Scancodes|here]]. ==See Also== *[[GetInvRefsForItem]] *[[GetFirstRefForItem]] *[[GetMenuTargetRef]] *[[SetOnMenuClickEventHandler]] *[[SetOnMouseoverChangeEventHandler]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Reference Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns a [[Sound]]'s flag value (determined by flagID). |name = GetSoundFlag |returnType = flagValue:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = sound |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagID |Type = int |Value = 0-13 }} ==Sound Flag IDs== 
0	Random Frequency Shift
1	Play At Random
2	Environment Ignored
3	Random Location
4	Loop
5	Menu Sound
6	2D
7	360 LFE
8	Dialogue Sound
9	Envelope Fast
10	Envelope Slow
11	2D Radius
12	Mute When Submerged
13	Start at Random Position
|example = GetSoundFlag EMTMedicalBeep01 6 }} ==Note== *If changing a sound in a persistent REF used in a script, it must be disabled and re-enabled for the changes to take effect ==See Also== *[[SetSoundFlag]] *[[GetSoundTraitNumeric]] *[[SetSoundTraitNumeric]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Sound Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Sound_Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an '''[[array_var]]''' containing all object references currently playing the specified '''[[Sound]]'''. |name = GetSoundPlayers |returnType = soundPlayers:array_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = sound |Type = ref }} |example = let aSoundPlayers := GetSoundPlayers FXBulletWhiz }} ==See Also== *[[IsSoundPlaying]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Sound Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Sound_Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

revision.comment: Adding to base sound functions category
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the value of a Sound's numeric trait (determined by traitID). |name = GetSoundTraitNumeric |returnType = traitValue:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = sound |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-12 }} ==Sound Numeric Trait IDs== 
0	Min. Attenuation Distance
1	Max. Attenuation Distance
2	Frequency Adjustment
3	Static Attenuation
4	Ends At (Minutes)
5	Starts At (Minutes)
6	Attenuation Curve 1
7	Attenuation Curve 2
8	Attenuation Curve 3
9	Attenuation Curve 4
10	Attenuation Curve 5
11	Reverb Attenuation Control
12	Priority
|example = GetSoundTraitNumeric EMTMedicalBeep01 3 Will return the Static Attenuation of EMTMedicalBeep01. }} ==See Also== *[[SetSoundTraitNumeric]] *[[GetSoundFlag]] *[[SetSoundFlag]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Sound Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Sound_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an [[array_var]] of all the actors currently toggled as player's Teammates. |name = GetTeammates |returnType = teammates:array_var |example = let aTeammates := GetTeammates }} ==See Also== *[[GetFollowers]] (Same thing but for Followers) *[[GetPlayerTeammate]] *[[SetPlayerTeammate]] *[[GetPlayerTeammateCount]] *[[OpenTeammateContainer]] *[[GetActorsByProcessingLevel]] *[[GetRefs]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:Follower Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns a two-dimensional '''[[array_var]]''' containing all temporary effect-entries (i.e. with limited duration) currently active on the calling actor, and their attributes. The elements in the main array are sub-arrays. Each sub-array contains the attribute values of one effect-entry, and has a total of 6 elements, ordered as follows: 
Index	Attribute
-----	---------
0	Parent Effect-Form (Object Effect/Actor Effect/Ingestible) : ref
1	Base Effect form : ref
2	Magnitude : float
3	Time Left : float
4	Effect "caster" (the actor who applied the effect) : ref
5	Duration : float
|name = GetTempEffects |returnType = tempEffects:array_var |referenceType = reference |example = let aTempEffects := actorRef.GetTempEffects let iIndex := 0 while iIndex < ar_Size aTempEffects let rParentEffect := aTempEffects[iIndex][0] let rBaseEffect := aTempEffects[iIndex][1] let fMagnitude := aTempEffects[iIndex][2] let fTimeLeft := aTempEffects[iIndex][3] let rCaster := aTempEffects[iIndex][4] let fDuration := aTempEffects[iIndex][5] let iIndex += 1 loop }} ==See Also== *[[RemoveNthTempEffect]] *[[ModNthTempEffectTimeLeft]] *[[GetActiveEffects]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Effect Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the height of the terrain mesh at the specified (x, y) coordinates, within the current exterior worldspace. |name = GetTerrainHeight |returnType = height:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = xCoord |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = yCoord |Type = float }} |example = set fTerrainHeight to GetTerrainHeight 22680.72 115090.25 }} ==Notes== *X and Y are worldspace coordinates (e.g. as returned by [[GetPos]]). *If the coordinates are outside of any loaded cells, 0 is returned. *This function does not check for collision with surfaces other than terrain. ==See Also== [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Movement Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns a Texture-Set's flag value (determined by flagID). |name = GetTextureSetFlag |returnType = flagValue:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = textureSet |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagID |Type = int |Value = 0-3 }} ==Texture-Set Flag IDs== 
0	No Specular Map
1	Parallax
2	Alpha - Blending
3	Alpha - Testing
|example = GetTextureSetFlag LandscapeDirtNV 1 }} ==See Also== *[[SetTextureSetFlag]] *[[GetTextureSetTexture]] *[[SetTextureSetTexture]] *[[GetTextureSetTraitNumeric]] *[[SetTextureSetTraitNumeric]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Texture-Set Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the texture path of the specified mapID of a Texture-Set. |name = GetTextureSetTexture |returnType = texturePath:string_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = textureSet |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = mapID |Type = int |Value = 0-5 }} ==Texture-Set Map IDs== 
0	Diffuse
1	Normal/Gloss
2	Environment Mask
3	Glow/Skin/Hair
4	Height
5	Environment
|example = GetTextureSetTexture 1stPersonSniperRifleTexture 2 Will return the Environment Mask texture path of 1stPersonSniperRifleTexture. }} ==See Also== *[[SetTextureSetTexture]] *[[GetTextureSetTraitNumeric]] *[[SetTextureSetTraitNumeric]] *[[GetTextureSetFlag]] *[[SetTextureSetFlag]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Texture-Set Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the value of a Texture-Set's numeric trait (determined by traitID). |name = GetTextureSetTraitNumeric |returnType = traitValue:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = textureSet |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-8 }} ==Texture-Set Numeric Trait IDs== 
0	Min. Width
1	Max. Width
2	Min. Height
3	Max. Height
4	Depth
5	Shininess
6	Parallax Scale
7	Parallax Passes
8	Color (RRRGGGBBB)
|example = GetTextureSetTraitNumeric LandscapeDirtNV 3 Will return the Max. Height of LandscapeDirtNV. }} ==See Also== *[[SetTextureSetTraitNumeric]] *[[GetTextureSetTexture]] *[[SetTextureSetTexture]] *[[GetTextureSetFlag]] *[[SetTextureSetFlag]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Texture-Set Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the [[string]] value of a UI trait. The trait name is of the format "MenuName/ElementName/ElementName/.../TraitName" as defined in the XML hierarchy for the desired menu, i.e. "MessageMenu/NoGlow_Branch/MM_Title/x". |name = GetUIString |returnType = string_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = componentPath |Type = string }} |example = let sStrVar := GetUIString "HUDMainMenu\HardcoreMode\Dehydration\string" }} ==See Also== *[[GetUIFloatAlt]] *[[GetUIFloatAlt]] *[[SetUIStringAlt]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:String Functions]]

revision.comment: add missing category
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Similar to the NVSE [[GetItemValue]] - returns the base value of the specified object or calling reference. This function, however, also works on ingestible-type items. |name = GetValueAlt |returnType = value:int |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = ItemRef.GetValueAlt
GetValueAlt Stimpak }} ==See Also== *[[SetValueAlt]] *[[SetBaseItemValue]] *[[GetItemValue]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category: Object Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the '''Noise Texture''' path of the specified '''Water Type'''. |name = GetWaterNoiseTexture |returnType = texturePath:string_v..."
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the '''Noise Texture''' path of the specified '''Water Type'''. |name = GetWaterNoiseTexture |returnType = texturePath:string_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = waterType |Type = ref }} |example = let sNoiseTex := GetWaterNoiseTexture NVCleanWater }} ==See Also== *[[SetWaterNoiseTexture]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Water Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the '''Open Sound''' of the specified '''[[WaterType|Water Type]]'''. |name = GetWaterSound |returnType = sound:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = waterType |Type = ref }} |example = set rSound to GetWaterSound NVCleanWater }} ==See Also== *[[SetWaterSound]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Water Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Sound Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns a weapon's detection sound level: 
0	Loud
1	Normal
2	Silent
|name = GetWeaponDetectionSoundLevel |returnType = soundLevel:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = ref }} |example = GetWeaponDetectionSoundLevel WeapNVSilenced22Pistol }} ==See Also== *[[SetWeaponDetectionSoundLevel]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Detection Functions]] [[Category:Sound Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the override [http://fose.silverlock.org/fose_command_doc.html#Reload_Animations reload animation] for weapon-mods (in the Mod Info tab) of the passed weapon. |name = GetWeaponModReloadAnim |returnType = reloadAnim:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = ref }} |example = GetWeaponModReloadAnim WeapNVAssaultCarbine }} ==See Also== *[[SetWeaponModReloadAnim]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Animation Functions]]

revision.comment: add missing categories
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns a bit-mask representing the calling weapon's currently installed weapon-mods. Unlike [[GetEquippedWeaponModFlags]], this function is called on an inventory reference. The calling weapon reference does not have to be equipped. |name = GetWeaponRefModFlags |returnType = modFlags:int |referenceType = reference |example = set iModFlags to rItemRef.GetWeaponRefModFlags gets the weapon mod flags for rItemRef. 
set iModFlags to rWorldRef.GetWeaponModFlags
This is short syntax added in [[JIP PP LN]]. }} ==Weapon Mod Flags== 
1	Weapon Mod #1
2	Weapon Mod #2
4	Weapon Mod #3
==Notes== *If you're looking for a compatible general use case function, consider using [[GetAllSlotMods]] instead, as it supports both the Vanilla Item Mod system, as well as the [[Extended Item Mods System]] introduced in [[VanillaPlugins+|Plugins+]] ==See Also== *[[SetWeaponRefModFlags]] *[[GetInvRefsForItem]] *[[GetFirstRefForItem]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Reference Functions]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the Shell Casing model path of the weapon. |name = GetWeaponShellCasingModel |returnType = modelPath:string_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = ref }} |example = let sModelPath := GetWeaponShellCasingModel WeapNVAssaultCarbine }} ==See Also== *[[SetWeaponShellCasingModel]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns a weapon's sound, determined by soundID: 
0	Attack Sound 3D
1	Attack Sound Dist.
2	Attack Sound 2D
3	Attack Loop
4	Attack Fail
5	Melee Block
6	Idle Sound
7	Equip Sound
8	Unequip Sound
9	Attack Sound 3D (Mod)
10	Attack Sound Dist. (Mod)
11	Attack Sound 2D (Mod)
12	Pickup Sound
13	Putdown Sound
|name = GetWeaponSound |returnType = sound:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = soundID |Type = int |Value = 0-13 }} |example = GetWeaponSound WeapNVAssaultCarbine 2 Will return the Assault Carbine's 2D attack sound. }} ==See Also== *[[SetWeaponSound]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Sound Functions]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns a weather's Image Space Modifier at the specified timeID. |name = GetWeatherImageSpaceMod |returnType = imageSpaceMod:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weather |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = timeID |Type = int |Value = 0-5 }} ==Weather Time IDs== 
0	Sunrise
1	Day
2	Sunset
3	Night
4	High Noon
5	Midnight
|example = GetWeatherImageSpaceMod NVHooverDamWeather 4 Will return the Image Space Modifier, at High Noon, of NVHooverDamWeather. }} ==See Also== *[[SetWeatherImageSpaceMod]] *[[GetWeatherTexture]] *[[SetWeatherTexture]] *[[GetWeatherPrecipitationModel]] *[[SetWeatherPrecipitationModel]] *[[GetWeatherTraitNumeric]] *[[SetWeatherTraitNumeric]] *[[GetWeatherRGBColor]] *[[SetWeatherRGBColor]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Weather Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weather Functions]] [[Category:Imagespace Functions]] [[Category:Imagespace Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the Precipitation model path of a weather. |name = GetWeatherPrecipitationModel |returnType = modelPath:string_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weather |Type = ref }} |example = GetWeatherPrecipitationModel NVHooverDamWeather }} ==See Also== *[[SetWeatherPrecipitationModel]] *[[GetWeatherImageSpaceMod]] *[[SetWeatherImageSpaceMod]] *[[GetWeatherTexture]] *[[SetWeatherTexture]] *[[GetWeatherTraitNumeric]] *[[SetWeatherTraitNumeric]] *[[GetWeatherRGBColor]] *[[SetWeatherRGBColor]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Weather Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weather Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the weather's RGB color of the specified typeID, at the specified timeID. |name = GetWeatherRGBColor |returnType = color:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weather |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = typeID |Type = int |Value = 0-9 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = timeID |Type = int |Value = 0-5 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = cloudLayer |Type = int |Value = 0-3 |Optional = y }} ==Weather Type IDs== 
0	Sky-Upper
1	Fog
2	Clouds-Lower
3	Ambient
4	Sunlight
5	Sun
6	Stars
7	Sky-Lower
8	Horizon
9	Clouds-Upper
==Weather Time IDs== 
0	Sunrise
1	Day
2	Sunset
3	Night
4	High Noon
5	Midnight
|example = GetWeatherRGBColor NVHooverDamWeather 3 4 Will return the Ambient color, at High Noon, of NVHooverDamWeather. }} ==See Also== *[[SetWeatherRGBColor]] *[[GetWeatherImageSpaceMod]] *[[SetWeatherImageSpaceMod]] *[[GetWeatherTexture]] *[[SetWeatherTexture]] *[[GetWeatherPrecipitationModel]] *[[SetWeatherPrecipitationModel]] *[[GetWeatherTraitNumeric]] *[[SetWeatherTraitNumeric]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Weather Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weather Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the texture path of the specified layer of a weather. |name = GetWeatherTexture |returnType = texturePath:string_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weather |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = layerIndex |Type = int |Value = 0-3 }} |example = GetWeatherTexture NVHooverDamWeather 2 }} ==See Also== *[[SetWeatherTexture]] *[[GetWeatherImageSpaceMod]] *[[SetWeatherImageSpaceMod]] *[[GetWeatherPrecipitationModel]] *[[SetWeatherPrecipitationModel]] *[[GetWeatherTraitNumeric]] *[[SetWeatherTraitNumeric]] *[[GetWeatherRGBColor]] *[[SetWeatherRGBColor]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Weather Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weather Functions]] [[Category:Texture Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the value of a weather's numeric trait (determined by traitID). |name = GetWeatherTraitNumeric |returnType = traitValue:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weather |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-20 }} ==Weather Numeric Trait IDs== 
0	Layer 0 Cloud Speed
1	Layer 1 Cloud Speed
2	Layer 2 Cloud Speed
3	Layer 3 Cloud Speed
4	Wind Speed
5	Trans Delta
6	Sun Glare
7	Sun Damage
8	Precipitation Begin Fade In
9	Precipitation End Fade Out
10	Lightning Begin Fade In
11	Lightning End Fade Out
12	Lightning Frequency
13	Weather Classification
14	Lightning Color (RRRGGGBBB)
15	Fog Distance Day Near
16	Fog Distance Day Far
17	Fog Distance Night Near
18	Fog Distance Night Far
19	Fog Distance Day Power
20	Fog Distance Night Power
Weather Classifications: 
0	None
1	Pleasant
2	Cloudy
4	Rainy
8	Snow
|example = GetWeatherTraitNumeric NVHooverDamWeather 4 Will return the Wind Speed of NVHooverDamWeather. }} ==See Also== *[[SetWeatherTraitNumeric]] *[[GetWeatherImageSpaceMod]] *[[SetWeatherImageSpaceMod]] *[[GetWeatherTexture]] *[[SetWeatherTexture]] *[[GetWeatherPrecipitationModel]] *[[SetWeatherPrecipitationModel]] *[[GetWeatherRGBColor]] *[[SetWeatherRGBColor]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Weather Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weather Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the Climate of the specified World Space. |name = GetWorldspaceClimate |returnType = climate:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = worldSpace |Type = ref }} |example = set rClimate to GetWorldspaceClimate FreesideWorld }} ==See Also== *[[SetWorldspaceClimate]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:World Space Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Climate Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:World_Space_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns whether the specified flag (determined by flagID) is set for the specified Worldspace. |name = GetWorldspaceFlag |returnType = flagValue:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = worldspace |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagID |Type = int |Value = 0-7 }} ==Worldspace Flag IDs== 
0	Small World
1	Can't Fast-Travel
4	No LOD Water
5	No LOD Noise
6	Disallow NPC Fall Damage
7	Needs Water Adjustment
|example = set bFlag to GetWorldspaceFlag FreesideWorld 4 }} ==See Also== *[[SetWorldspaceFlag]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Flag Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Checks if the specified [[Effect Shader]] is playing on the calling reference, and if so returns the number of instances of that shader playing on it. Otherwise returns zero. |name = HasEffectShader |returnType = instanceCount:int |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = effectShader |Type = ref }} |example = set iShaderInstances to someObjectRef.HasEffectShader GlowingOneFXShader }} ==Notes== *This function may return a value greater than 0 for a short period after calling [[StopMagicShaderVisuals]]. This is because the shader is not fully removed until its ending sequence has played out. ==NVSE Version== *This function is also added by NVSE 5.0.2, but this version just returns a boolean, 1 if the reference has the shader, or 0 if not, rather than the number of instances. ==See Also== *[[StopMagicShaderVisuals]] *[[PlayMagicShaderVisuals]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns 1 if the calling reference has a geometric [[Creating Primitives|primitive]]. |name = HasPrimitive |returnType = hasPrimitive:0/1 |referenceType = reference |example = set bHasPrimitive to objectRef.HasPrimitive }} ==See Also== *[[AddPrimitive]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]]

revision.comment: Adding to original actor functions category
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns 1 if the specified variable was added to the calling reference's/quest's script by [[AddScriptVariable]]. |name = HasVariableAdded |returnType = hasVariable:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = varName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = quest |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = RaulRef.HasVariableAdded "NewVar"
HasVariableAdded "NewVar" VERNovac01 }} ==Notes== *Use this instead of [[HasVariable]] with variables added by [[AddScriptVariable]]. ==See Also== *[[AddScriptVariable]] *[[RemoveScriptVariable]] *[[RemoveAllAddedVariables]] *[[GetVariable]] *[[GetRefVariable]] *[[SetVariable]] *[[SetRefVariable]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Script Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Script_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Injects the contents of a [[formatted string]] into a menu, at componentPath (see Notes, below). |name = InjectUIComponent |returnType = successful:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = componentPath |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = component |Type = formattedString }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = formatVars(up to 20) |Optional = y }} |example = InjectUIComponent "HUDMainMenu" "<_Var1>%g <_Var2> " intVar InjectUIComponent "StartMenu\NOGLOW_BRANCH" " " strVar }} ==Notes== *component supports format specifiers, and up to 20 variables may be used. *The new components are injected directly into the game's memory - no files on disk are modified (or even accessed). *Once injected, all UI functions can be used on the new components. *Most menus are loaded in memory only while open, and are unloaded when closed. This means: **New components will be injected successfully only if the target menu is currently loaded. **New components must be re-injected every time the target menu is re-loaded. The only exceptions are the following menus, which are always loaded (and are never re-loaded): HUDMainMenu, InventoryMenu, MapMenu, RepairMenu, StatsMenu, ItemModMenu. ==See Also== *[[IsComponentLoaded]] *[[InjectUIXML]] *[[UnloadUIComponent]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Injects the contents of an XML file into a menu, at componentPath (see Notes, below). |name = InjectUIXML |returnType = successful:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = componentPath |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = filePath |Type = string }} |example = InjectUIXML "HUDMainMenu" "data\menus\prefabs\list_box.xml" InjectUIXML "StartMenu\NOGLOW_BRANCH\main_container" "data\menus\my_file.xml" }} ==Notes== *filePath is relative to the game's installation path. *The XML file can either be loose, or packed in a BSA archive. *The new components are injected directly into the game's memory - no files on disk are modified (or even accessed). *Once injected, all UI functions can be used on the new components. *Most menus are loaded in memory only while open, and are unloaded when closed. This means: **New components will be injected successfully only if the target menu is currently loaded. **New components must be re-injected every time the target menu is re-loaded. The only exceptions are the following menus, which are always loaded (and are never re-loaded): HUDMainMenu, InventoryMenu, MapMenu, RepairMenu, StatsMenu, ItemModMenu. ==See Also== *[[IsComponentLoaded]] *[[InjectUIComponent]] *[[UnloadUIComponent]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

revision.comment: Adding to condition function category
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns 1 if the calling actor is currently attacking another actor. |name = IsAttacking |returnType = isAttacking:0/1 |referenceType = reference |example = set bAttacking to actorRef.IsAttacking }} ==See Also== [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns 1 if the UI component (which can be either a menu, an object-element, or a property-element) is currently loaded in memory. |name = IsComponentLoaded |returnType = loaded:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = componentPath |Type = string }} |example = IsComponentLoaded "InventoryMenu\GLOW_BRANCH\IM_HotKeyWheel"
IsComponentLoaded "HUDMainMenu\XPMeter\XPPointer\_x_min"
IsComponentLoaded "StartMenu" }} ==See Also== *[[GetActiveMenuMode]] *[[GetActiveUIComponentName]] *[[GetActiveUIComponentFullName]] *[[GetActiveUIComponentID]] *[[GetMenuTargetRef]] *[[GetMenuItemFilter]] *[[ClickMenuButton]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Checks whether the calling reference's equipped weapon is scoped. Returns: 
0	If the weapon is not scoped.
1	If the weapon is scoped by default.
2	If the weapon has a scope mod attached.
|name = IsEquippedWeaponScoped |returnType = scoped:0/1/2 |referenceType = reference |example = ActorRef.IsEquippedWeaponScoped }} ==Notes== *This function can be used as a condition function (where applicable). ==See Also== *[[IsEquippedWeaponSilenced]] *[[GetWeaponHasScope]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Checks whether the calling reference's equipped weapon is silenced. Returns: 
0	If the weapon is not silenced.
1	If the weapon is silenced by default.
2	If the weapon has a silencer mod attached.
|name = IsEquippedWeaponSilenced |returnType = silenced:0/1/2 |referenceType = reference |example = ActorRef.IsEquippedWeaponSilenced }} ==Notes== *This function can be used as a condition function (where applicable). ==See Also== *[[IsEquippedWeaponScoped]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |originVersion = 36.00 |summary = Returns whether the calling actor is currently in the air (i.e. not on solid ground). |name = IsInAir |returnType = inAir:0/1 |referenceType = reference |example = set bInAir to actorRef.IsInAir }} ==See Also== [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns 1 if the calling actor is currently in combat with the specified target reference. |name = IsInCombatWith |returnType = inCombat:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = target |Type = ref }} |example = set bInCombat to actorRef.IsInCombatWith targetRef }} ==See Also== *[[IsInCombat]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns 1 if the calling actor is at least partially submerged in water (i.e. wading/swimming/diving). |name = IsInWater |returnType = inWater:0/1 |referenceType = reference |example = set bInWater to actorRef.IsInWater }} ==Bugs== *Does not work with objects or dead actors. ==See Also== *[[GetWaterImmersionPerc]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Water Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns 1 if the specified '''[[Sound]]''' is currently being played by the passed objectRef (optional) or, if the latter was not specified, by ANY [[Object]]. |name = IsSoundPlaying |returnType = soundPlaying:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = sound |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = objectRef |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = set bSoundPlaying to IsSoundPlaying EMTMedicalBeep01 set bSoundPlaying to IsSoundPlaying NPCBarkScorpionClaws vGSBarkScorp01Ref }} ==See Also== *[[GetSoundPlayers]] *[[PlaySound3D]] *[[PlaySound]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Sound Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Sound_Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Adds a grass to the potential grass types of a Land Texture. |name = LandTextureAddGrass |returnType = successful:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = landTexture |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = grass |Type = ref }} |example = LandTextureAddGrass LScrubDirtCanyon01Grass GrassOasis01 }} ==See Also== *[[LandTextureRemoveGrass]] *[[GetLandTextureTextureSet]] *[[SetLandTextureTextureSet]] *[[GetLandTextureTraitNumeric]] *[[SetLandTextureTraitNumeric]] *[[GetLandTextureNumGrasses]] *[[GetLandTextureNthGrass]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Land Texture Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Removes a grass from the potential grass types of a Land Texture. |name = LandTextureRemoveGrass |returnType = successful:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = landTexture |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = grass |Type = ref }} |example = LandTextureRemoveGrass LScrubDirtCanyon01Grass GrassWasteland04 }} ==See Also== *[[LandTextureAddGrass]] *[[GetLandTextureTextureSet]] *[[SetLandTextureTextureSet]] *[[GetLandTextureTraitNumeric]] *[[SetLandTextureTraitNumeric]] *[[GetLandTextureNumGrasses]] *[[GetLandTextureNthGrass]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Land Texture Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = This function is an extended version of [[MessageEx]], and enables configuring both the display time, the icon of the message, and a played sound. The icon and played sound are both optional, and are passed as part of the formatted string, before the actual message text, each suffixed with a bar "|" symbol (see examples below). *The icon is a path to a DDS image file (relative to ..\Data\Textures). *The played sound is the '''Editor ID''' of a [[Sound]] form, and must be prefixed with a '''$'''. |name = MessageExAlt |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = displayTime |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = messageText |Type = formattedString }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = formatVars(up to 20) |Optional = y }} |example = MessageExAlt 4 "Interface\Icons\Message Icons\glow_message_vaultboy_angry.dds|$UIRepGood|Name is %n%rValue is %g" refVar intVar '''Other commonly used icons:''' *glow_message_vaultboy_in_pain.dds *glow_message_vaultboy_neutral.dds *glow_message_vaultboy_sad.dds *glow_message_vaultboy_surprised.dds *glow_message_vaultboy_thinking.dds *glow_message_vaultboy_very_happy.dds *glow_message_vaultboy_wasteland.dds }} ==Notes== *messageText supports [[String Formatting|string formatting]], and up to 20 variables may be used. *The interface icon path can be prefixed with # and then a number from 0-6 to show default icons. An example of such would be MessageExAlt 3 "#0|Test". The default icons are: {| class="wikitable" |- ! Number ! Icon Path
! Emotion |- | 0 | Interface\Icons\Message Icons\glow_message_vaultboy_sad.dds
| Sad
|- | 1 | Interface\Icons\Message Icons\glow_message_vaultboy_neutral.dds | Neutral/Happy |- | 2 | Interface\Icons\Message Icons\glow_message_vaultboy_angry.dds | Angry |- | 3 | Interface\Icons\Message Icons\glow_message_vaultboy_thinking.dds | Thinking |- | 4 | Interface\Icons\Message Icons\glow_message_vaultboy_very_happy.dds | Very Happy |- | 5 | Interface\Icons\Message Icons\glow_message_vaultboy_surprised.dds | Surprised |- | 6 | Interface\Icons\Message Icons\glow_message_vaultboy_in_pain.dds | Pain |} ==See Also== *[[ShowMessage]] *[[MessageBoxEx]] *[[MessageBoxExAlt]] *[[Print]] *[[ToString|ToString ($)]] *[[String Variable]] *[[MessageEx]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Message Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Modifies the <u>base</u> value of the specified [[Stats List|stat]], for the passed actor. This function differs from [[ModActorValue]], which modifies the <u>current</u> stat value. The modifier may be either positive or negative. |name = ModBaseActorValue |alias = ModBaseAV |referenceType = actorRef |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = actorValue |Type = statName }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = modifier |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = actorBase |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = CraigBooneRef.ModBaseActorValue Health 50
ModBaseAV Health -85 CrDeathClawMother }} ==Notes== *The changes will be applied on <u>all</u> instances of the actor's base form in the world. ==See Also== *[[GetBaseActorValueAlt]] / [[GetBaseActorValue]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Prints the passed [[String Formatting|formatted string]], as a new line, into a log file of the mod owning the script from which the function is called.
*The log file is found in '''..\Steam\SteamApps\common\Fallout New Vegas\Mod Logs''' and is named after the mod (i.e. '''ModName.esp.log'''). *If the file does not exist, it is created. *The '''indent''' argument determines the indent level of the appended line. |name = ModLogPrint |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = indent |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = textLine |Type = formattedString }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = formatVars(up to 20) |Optional = y }} |example = ModLogPrint 0 "Test Print :%rID = %i Name = %n" someForm someForm }} ==See Also== *[[Con_SCOF]] *[[PrintDebug]] *[[PrintF]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:File Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Moves the calling reference to the specified '''[[:Category:Cells|Cell]]''' or '''[[World Spaces|Worldspace]]''', at the specified (X, Y, Z) position. Unlike [[MoveTo]], this function does not require another object reference to mark the destination, allowing more flexibility. |name = MoveToCell |returnType = success:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = cellOrWorldspace |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = posX |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = posY |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = posZ |Type = float }} |example = objectRef.MoveToCell FreesideAtomicWrangler 2200 3200 13700
objectRef.MoveToCell WastelandNV -12000 98000 4300 }} ==See Also== *[[MoveTo]] *[[MoveToContainer]] *[[MoveToEditorPosition]] *[[MoveToMarkerWithFade]] *[[MoveToNode]] *[[MoveToReticle]] *[[MoveToQuestTarget]] *[[SetPosEx]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Cell_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Movement Functions]] [[Category:Cell Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Moves the calling reference to its original location (cell & XYZ coords), where it was placed in the editor. |name = MoveToEditorPosition |returnType = success:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = resetRotation |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }} |example = objectRef.MoveToEditorPosition
objectRef.MoveToEditorPosition 1 }} ==See Also== *[[GetEditorPosition]] *[[GetStartingPos]] *[[MoveTo]] *[[MoveToCell]] *[[MoveToNode]] *[[MoveToReticle]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Movement Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Reads data from the specified file and stores it in an [[array variable]]. If the file contains a single line of data, the function returns a standard, list-type array. Otherwise, it returns a matrix, the layout of which depends on the 2nd, optional argument, '''transpose''': *'''0''' (default) - The 1st index is the '''column''' number and the 2nd index is the '''row''' number (someArray[column][row]). *'''1''' - The 1st index is the '''row''' number and the 2nd index is the '''column''' number (someArray[row][column]). The file must be formatted accordingly:
  1. Values must be separated with tabs (one or more).
  2. When using non-numeric types as values:
    3. *Strings must be prefixed with '$'. Example: $Brave men run, in my family. *Forms must be prefixed with '@', followed by the source file name, then ':', and finally the six-digit formID (i.e. without the mod index). Example (for Craig Boone): @FalloutNV.esm:096BCE
|name = ReadArrayFromFile |returnType = array_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = filePath |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = transpose |Type = 1/0 |Optional = y }} |example = 496 -1.26 -12 0.514 0 2009 -100.8004 A file with the same contents as the above will return an array of numbers, with 7 elements. $they can lie $to my face $but not $to my heart The above will return an array of strings, with 4 elements. @FalloutNV.esm:135F19 @LonesomeRoad.esm:003E43 @DeadMoney.esm:00A7A9 The above will return an array of forms, with 3 elements. 0.514 $if we all -12 @DeadMoney.esm:00A7A9 @FalloutNV.esm:135F19 $stand together The above will return an array of mixed-type values, with 6 elements. 52 14 7 9 110 2 20 51 10 99 0 61 -5 37 39 45 35 17 496 -12 The above will return a matrix of numbers, with 4 columns and 5 rows. @FalloutNV.esm:135F19 496 $they can lie to my face @LonesomeRoad.esm:003E43 -1.26 $but not to my heart @DeadMoney.esm:00A7A9 -12 $if we all stand together @FalloutNV.esm:022108 0.514 $it will just be the start The above will return a matrix of mixed-type values, with 3 columns and 4 rows. }} ==Notes== *filePath is relative to the game's installation path. *Before calling this, make sure the file exists using [[FileExists]]. '''CAUTION''': FileExists is relative the Data folder, not the Game's installation path. ==See Also== *[[WriteArrayToFile]] *[[ReadStringFromFile]] *[[WriteStringToFile]] *[[FileExists]] *[[pReadFile]] *[[pWriteFile]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Utility Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Reads the contents of the specified file and stores them in a string variable. *If startAtLine is specified, the file will be read beginning from that line number (otherwise, it will be read from line 1). *If lineCount is specified, only that number of lines will be read. |name = ReadStringFromFile |returnType = string_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = filePath |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = startAtLine |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = lineCount |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = ReadStringFromFile "data_file.txt" ReadStringFromFile "data_file.txt" 10 8 }} ==Notes== *filePath is relative to the game's installation path. *Before calling this, make sure the file exists using [[FileExists]]. '''CAUTION''': FileExists is relative the Data folder, not the Game's installation path. *Maximum length of a file is 16384 characters. Any data over that point will not be read, even if lineCount and StartAtLine are specified. ==See Also== *[[WriteStringToFile]] *[[ReadArrayFromFile]] *[[WriteArrayToFile]] *[[FileExists]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Utility Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:File Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Erases the element with a Key matching the specified Object, in the specified [[RefMap Array]]. |name = RefMapArrayErase |alias = RefMapErase |returnType = arraySize:int |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = arrName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = RaulRef.RefMapArrayErase "someRefMap" RefMapErase "someRefMap" ArmorCombat }} ==Notes== *The function returns the new size (number of elements) of the array. ==See Also== [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:RefMap Array Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns a StringMap matrix containing all [[RefMap Array|RefMap Arrays]] of the specified class, and their keys. RefMap Classes: 
0	Permanent-Private
1	Temporary-Private
2	Permanent-Public
3	Temporary-Public
|name = RefMapArrayGetAll |alias = RefMapGetAll |returnType = array_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = refMapClass |Type = int |Value = 0-3 }} |example = let aAllKeys := RefMapArrayGetAll 0 let aAllKeys := RefMapGetAll 1 }} ==See Also== [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:RefMap Array Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an '''[[array_var]]''' with the first '''Key::Value''' pair in the specified [[RefMap Array]].
The first element in the returned array is the '''Key''' (reference type), and the second element is the '''Value''' (float/reference/string type). |name = RefMapArrayGetFirst |alias = RefMapFirst |returnType = array_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = arrName |Type = string }} |example = let aKeyValue := RefMapArrayGetFirst "someRefMap"
let aKeyValue := RefMapFirst "someRefMap" }} ==See Also== *[[TypeOf]] can be used to verify the type of the Value. *[[RefMapArrayGetNext]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:RefMap Array Functions (JIP)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the float value of the element with a Key matching the specified Object, in the specified [[RefMap Array]]. |name..."
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the float value of the element with a Key matching the specified Object, in the specified [[RefMap Array]]. |name = RefMapArrayGetFloat |alias = RefMapGetFlt |returnType = value:float |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = arrName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = set fValue to RaulRef.RefMapArrayGetFloat "someRefMap" set fValue to RefMapGetFlt "someRefMap" ArmorCombat }} ==Notes== *The function will return 0 if the RefMap is not defined/has no matching Key/element is not of type float. When necessary, verify using [[RefMapArrayGetType]]. ==See Also== [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:RefMap Array Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an array containing all of the Keys of the specified [[RefMap Array]]. |name = RefMapArrayGetKeys |alias = RefMapKeys |returnType = keys:array_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = arrName |Type = string }} |example = let aKeys := RefMapArrayGetKeys "someRefMap" let aKeys := RefMapKeys "someRefMap" }} ==See Also== *[[RefMapArrayGetAll]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:RefMap Array Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an '''[[array_var]]''' with the next '''Key::Value''' pair in the specified [[RefMap Array]]. This is the pair immediately following the one that was returned by the last call to this function, or to [[RefMapArrayGetFirst]]. If no pair follows, the function returns '''[[ar_Null]]'''. The first element in the returned array is the '''Key''' (reference type), and the second element is the '''Value''' (float/reference/string type). |name = RefMapArrayGetNext |alias = RefMapNext |returnType = array_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = arrName |Type = string }} |example = let aKeyValue := RefMapArrayGetNext "someRefMap" let aKeyValue := RefMapNext "someRefMap" 
array_var aArray
ref rKey
string_var s_Value

let aArray := RefMapArrayGetFirst "*SomeRefMap"
while (aArray != Ar_NULL)
   let rKey := aArray[0]
   let s_Value := aArray[1]  ;* Assumes the type of the value is a string here, i.e RefMapArraySetString was used all of the time for this RefMap.
   ;* If unsure, can check the type of the value using TypeOf
   
   ;* do stuff
   
   let aArray := RefMapArrayGetNext "*SomeRefMap"
loop
}} ==See Also== *[[RefMapArrayGetFirst]] *[[TypeOf]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:RefMap Array Functions (JIP)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the ref value of the element with a Key matching the specified Object, in the specified [[RefMap Array]]. |name =..."
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the ref value of the element with a Key matching the specified Object, in the specified [[RefMap Array]]. |name = RefMapArrayGetRef |alias = RefMapGetRef |returnType = value:ref |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = arrName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = set rValue to RaulRef.RefMapArrayGetRef "someRefMap" set rValue to RefMapGetRef "someRefMap" ArmorCombat }} ==Notes== *The function will return 0 if the RefMap is not defined/has no matching Key/element is not of type ref. When necessary, verify using [[RefMapArrayGetType]]. ==See Also== [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:RefMap Array Functions (JIP)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the string value of the element with a Key matching the specified Object, in the specified [[RefMap Array]]. |name..."
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the string value of the element with a Key matching the specified Object, in the specified [[RefMap Array]]. |name = RefMapArrayGetString |alias = RefMapGetStr |returnType = value:string_var |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = arrName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = let sValue := RaulRef.RefMapArrayGetString "someRefMap" let sValue := RefMapGetStr "someRefMap" ArmorCombat }} ==Notes== *The function will return a null string if the RefMap is not defined/has no matching Key/element is not of type string. When necessary, verify using [[RefMapArrayGetType]]. ==See Also== [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:RefMap Array Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the value-type of the element with a Key matching the specified Object, in the specified [[RefMap Array]]. Value Types: 
0	None (no such key/RefMap not defined)
1	Float
2	Form
4	String
|name = RefMapArrayGetType |alias = RefMapGetType |returnType = type:int |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = arrName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = set iType to RaulRef.RefMapArrayGetType "someRefMap" set iType to RefMapGetType "someRefMap" ArmorCombat }} ==See Also== [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:RefMap Array Functions (JIP)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the float value of the element with a Key matching the specified Object, in the specified [[RefMap Array]]. If no such..."
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the float value of the element with a Key matching the specified Object, in the specified [[RefMap Array]]. If no such element exists, it is created. |name = RefMapArraySetFloat |alias = RefMapSetFlt |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = arrName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = value |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = RaulRef.RefMapArraySetFloat "someRefMap" -307.485 RefMapSetFlt "someRefMap" 102031 ArmorCombat }} ==See Also== [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:RefMap Array Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the ref value of the element with a Key matching the specified Object, in the specified [[RefMap Array]]. If no such element exists, it is created. |name = RefMapArraySetRef |alias = RefMapSetRef |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = arrName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = value |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = RaulRef.RefMapArraySetRef "someRefMap" Ammo127mm RefMapSetRef "someRefMap" ArmorCombat CraigBoone }} ==Notes== * As with most JIP functions, this can be called either on a reference with only one parameter, or with a base form as the second parameter (which ignores the calling reference). * When using the base form syntax, be extra careful to note that the order is value-key, and not key-value. That is, ensure that your "key" is the ''second'' parameter and the value you want to assign that key is your ''first'' parameter. The above example, for instance, is conceptually equivalent to a dictionary of the form "someRefMap[CraigBoone] = ArmorCombat". [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:RefMap Array Functions (JIP)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the string value of the element with a Key matching the specified Object, in the specified [[RefMap Array]]. If no suc..."
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the string value of the element with a Key matching the specified Object, in the specified [[RefMap Array]]. If no such element exists, it is created. |name = RefMapArraySetString |alias = RefMapSetStr |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = arrName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = value |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = RaulRef.RefMapArraySetString "someRefMap" "Snivelling, dim-witted weasel." RefMapSetStr "someRefMap" "Toffee-nosed git." ArmorCombat }} ==See Also== [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:RefMap Array Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Deletes all invalid keys (i.e. of references which no longer exist) from the specified [[RefMap Array]]. |name = RefMapArrayValidate |alias = RefMapValidate |returnType = arraySize:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = arrName |Type = string }} |example = RefMapArrayValidate "someRefMap"
RefMapValidate "someRefMap" }} ==Notes== *The function returns the size (number of elements) of the cleaned array.
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:RefMap Array Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Generates a unique identifier string for a reference/object. The result string is intended to be used as a key in StringMap-type arrays. This makes up for the inability to use references as keys in arrays. |name = RefToString |returnType = string_var |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = anyForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = let svDocBase := RefToString DocMitchell ; "FalloutNV.esm:104C0C" let svDocRef := DocMitchellRef.RefToString ; "FalloutNV.esm:104C0F" let arrVar[objRef.RefToString] := Caps001 let refVar := arrVar[objRef.RefToString] let strVar := objRef.RefToString let intVar := arrVar[strVar] }} ==Notes== *The generated string identifier is load-order-independent, and remains valid even if the load order has changed. *For references, the syntax matters. Passing the reference as a parameter (base syntax) results in the string for the base form, not the reference. ==See Also== *[[StringToRef]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Utility Functions (JIP)]] [[Category: String Functions]] [[Category: ID Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Refreshes the current climate to reflect changes made by SetClimateXXX functions. |name = RefreshCurrentClimate |example = RefreshCurrentClimate }} ==Known Issues== There are some cases where this will not work unless [[SetWeather]] is called immediately before calling this function: 
SetWeather NVWastelandClear	; (any Weather can be used as the parameter)
RefreshCurrentClimate
==See Also== [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Climate Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions_(ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

revision.comment: Adding to original actor functions category
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Removes all variables added with [[AddScriptVariable]] to the calling reference's/quest's script by the mod calling this function. |name = RemoveAllAddedVariables |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = quest |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = RaulRef.RemoveAllAddedVariables
RemoveAllAddedVariables VERNovac01 }} ==See Also== *[[RemoveScriptVariable]] *[[AddScriptVariable]] *[[HasVariableAdded]] *[[GetVariable]] *[[GetRefVariable]] *[[SetVariable]] *[[SetRefVariable]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Script Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Script_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Removes an ammo effect from an ammo type. |name = RemoveAmmoEffect |returnType = successful:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ammo |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = ammoEffect |Type = ref }} |example = RemoveAmmoEffect Ammo12GaBeanBag AmmoEffectBeanBagFatigue }} ==See Also== *[[AddAmmoEffect]] *[[GetNumAmmoEffects]] *[[GetNthAmmoEffect]] *[[GetAmmoEffectTraitNumeric]] *[[SetAmmoEffectTraitNumeric]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Ammo Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Ammo Effect Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Ammo Functions]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |originVersion = 14.00 |summary = Removes the [[Destructible Object Stage|destruction stage]], at the specified index, from the specified Object. |name = RemoveDestructionStage |returnType = stageCount:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = object |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = index |Type = int }} |example = RemoveDestructionStage TrapGrenadeBunch 2 }} ==Notes== *This function has two major limitations: 1. It will have no effect on existing references of the object - only on references created after it is called, and 2. The changes will not be saved with the game. *If the stage was successfully removed, the function will return the new stage-count, and -1 otherwise. *Obviously, this function is only usable with object types that have Destruction Data. ==See Also== *[[AddDestructionStage]] *[[ClearDestruction]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Destruction Data Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Similar to the vanilla [[RemoveItem]] - removes quantity of the specified item from the calling reference's inventory. However, instead of being destroyed, the items are moved to the specified target container/actor. |name = RemoveItemTarget |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = item/list |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = target |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = quantity |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = clearOwnership |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }} |example = 
SourceRef.RemoveItemTarget Camera TargetRef
This will move all Camera items from SourceRef to TargetRef. 
SourceRef.RemoveItemTarget NVHoldoutWeapons TargetRef 4 1
This will move 4 of each item in the list NVHoldoutWeapons from SourceRef to TargetRef. Ownership will be cleared off all moved items. }} ==Notes== *All stats/attributes (health, script, mods, ownership, etc.) of removed items are preserved. *If quantity is not specified or is <= 0, all instances of the item are removed. *The function accepts a FormList as parameter (in place of an item) and also supports sub-lists. *If the removed item was equipped, does NOT run the [[OnUnequip]] block. ==See Also== *[[RemoveItem]] *[[RemoveAllItems]] *[[RemoveAllTypedItems]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Removes the effect-entry at the specified index from the effect list of the specified ingestible/object effect/actor effect. |name = RemoveNthEffect |returnType = effectCount:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = effectForm |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = index |Type = int }} |example = RemoveNthEffect EnchClothingDuster 0 RemoveNthEffect RadAway 2 }} ==Notes== *If the effect-entry was successfully removed, the function will return the new effect-count, and -1 otherwise. *If the effect-entry was successfully removed, indices of remaining entries in the list are shifted accordingly. To avoid potential mistakes when removing multiple effects, it's advised to remove them starting from the largest index. *You can not remove an effect-entry if the effect list only has one effect. The function will return -1. ==See Also== *[[AddNewEffect]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Effect Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Dispels and removes the Nth temporary effect-entry from the calling actor. [[GetTempEffects]] should be called <u>before</u> calling this function, and on the same frame, in order to determine the index of the effect-entry to be removed. |name = RemoveNthTempEffect |returnType = success:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = index |Type = int }} |example = actorRef.RemoveNthTempEffect 2 }} ==See Also== *[[ModNthTempEffectTimeLeft]] *[[RemoveNthEffect]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Effect Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]]

revision.comment: Adding to original actor functions category
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Removes the specified variable added to the calling reference's/quest's script by [[AddScriptVariable]]. |name = RemoveScriptVariable |returnType = removed:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = varName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = quest |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = RaulRef.RemoveScriptVariable "NewVar"
RemoveScriptVariable "NewVar" VERNovac01 }} ==See Also== *[[AddScriptVariable]] *[[RemoveAllAddedVariables]] *[[HasVariableAdded]] *[[GetVariable]] *[[GetRefVariable]] *[[SetVariable]] *[[SetRefVariable]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Script Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Script_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Resets the calling actor's fall timer. The final fall damage taken by the actor will be re-calculated, as if he had just started falling. Note that the vanilla function [[ResetFallDamageTimer]], which claims to have the same effect, is unfortunately broken. |name = ResetFallTime |referenceType = reference |example = actorRef.ResetFallTime }} ==See Also== *[[GetFallTimeElapsed]] *[[GetFallTimeRemaining]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Time Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = If the calling [[actor]] is underwater, sets the time (in seconds) remaining until they run out of breath (and begin drowning). |name = SetActorDiveBreath |returnType = breathSeconds:float |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = breathSeconds |Type = float }} |example = actorRef.SetActorDiveBreath 15 }} ==See Also== *[[GetActorDiveBreath]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Time Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = An alternate version of the vanilla functions [[SetActorFullName]] and [[SetCellFullName]]. Takes a '''[[String]]''', instead of a '''[[Message]]''' form, as argument.
The name change is <u>permanent</u> and is saved in the game. |name = SetFullNameAlt |alias = SetActorFullNameAlt |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = newName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = RexRef.SetFullNameAlt "Eek the Cat"
SetActorFullNameAlt "Eek the Cat" Rex
SetFullNameAlt "My Room" FreesideAtomicWranglerRoom }} ==Notes== *Even if called on a reference, the function actually changes the name of the '''[[Base Form]]''', meaning that any instance of that [[NPC]]/[[creature]] will have its name changed. *This function may also be called on a '''[[Talking Activator]]'''. *This function may also be called on Cells. *The changes made by this function are permanent. If you need a temporary name change consider using [[SetName]] or [[LNSetName]].
==See Also== *[[GetName]] *[[SetName]] *[[LNSetName]] *[[SetNameEx]] *[[SetActorFullName]] *[[SetCellFullName]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Name Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Name Functions]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]]

revision.comment: Adding to original actor functions category
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the specified leveling data value of an actor. The value that is set is determined by dataType: 
0	Fixed Level/Level Mult.
1	Calc. Min.
2	Calc. Max.
|name = SetActorLevelingData |returnType = dataValue:float |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = dataType |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = 
CraigBooneRef.SetActorLevelingData 0 0.75
This will set Craig Boone's level multiplier to 0.75. 
SetActorLevelingData 1 5 CraigBoone
This will set Craig Boone's calculated minimum level to 5. }} ==See Also== *[[GetActorLevelingData]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the actor's Unarmed Attack effect. |name = SetActorUnarmedEffect |returnType = success:0/1 |referenceType = reference |ar..."
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the actor's Unarmed Attack effect. |name = SetActorUnarmedEffect |returnType = success:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = newEffect |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = RexRef.SetActorUnarmedEffect GoldenGeckoRadiation SetActorUnarmedEffect BigHornerKnockback NVCrGoldenGecko }} ==See Also== *[[GetActorUnarmedEffect]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Effect Functions (JIP)]]

revision.comment: Adding to original actor functions category
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets an actor's Voice Type. |name = SetActorVoiceType |returnType = voiceType:ref |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = newVoice |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = RaulRef.SetActorVoiceType MaleAdult04 SetActorVoiceType FemaleGhoul02 AngelaWilliams }} ==See Also== *[[GetActorVoiceType]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the Addiction Chance of the specified [[Ingestible]] [[item]]. |name = SetAddictionChance |returnType = chance:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ingestible |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newChance |Type = float }} |example = SetAddictionChance Morphine 0.2 }} ==See Also== *[[GetAddictionChance]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Effect Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the Addiction effect of the specified [[Ingestible]] [[item]]. |name = SetAddictionEffect |returnType = actorEffect:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ingestible |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newEffect |Type = ref }} |example = SetAddictionEffect Morphine WithdrawalAlcohol }} ==See Also== *[[GetAddictionEffect]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Effect Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the value of an ammo effect's numeric trait (determined by traitID). |name = SetAmmoEffectTraitNumeric |returnType = traitValue:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ammoEffect |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-2 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = float }} ==Ammo Effect Numeric Trait IDs== 
0	Effect Type
1	Operation Type
2	Value
Effect Types: 
0	Damage Mod
1	DR Mod
2	DT Mod
3	Spread Mod
4	Weapon Condition Mod
5	Fatigue Mod
Operation Types: 
0	Add
1	Multiply
2	Subtract
|example = 
SetAmmoEffectTraitNumeric AmmoEffect5mmDTReduction 0 3
SetAmmoEffectTraitNumeric AmmoEffect5mmDTReduction 1 1
SetAmmoEffectTraitNumeric AmmoEffect5mmDTReduction 2 0.8
Will set AmmoEffect5mmDTReduction to reduce the weapon's spread by 20%. }} ==See Also== *[[GetAmmoEffectTraitNumeric]] *[[GetNumAmmoEffects]] *[[GetNthAmmoEffect]] *[[AddAmmoEffect]] *[[RemoveAmmoEffect]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Ammo Effect Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Ammo Functions]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets an [[ammo]] type's [[projectile]]. |name = SetAmmoProjectile |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ammo |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newProjectile |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = SetAmmoProjectile Ammo12GaSlug 12GaBeanBagProjectile SetAmmoProjectile Ammo12GaSlug ; (Sets the projectile to None) }} ==See Also== *[[GetAmmoProjectile]] *[[GetAmmoTraitNumeric]] *[[SetAmmoTraitNumeric]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Ammo Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions]] [[Category:Ammo Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the value of an ammo type's numeric trait (determined by traitID). |name = SetAmmoTraitNumeric |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ammo |Type = baseForm }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-2 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = float }} ==Ammo Numeric Trait IDs== 
0	Speed
1	Projectiles Per Shot
2	Ammo Consumed Percent
3	Clip Rounds
|example = SetAmmoTraitNumeric Ammo12Ga 1 3 Will set the 12 Gauge Round's Projectiles Per Shot to 3. }} ==See Also== *[[GetAmmoTraitNumeric]] *[[GetAmmoProjectile]] *[[SetAmmoProjectile]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Ammo Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Ammo Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the '''Ragdoll Constraint Template''' RDT file path, of the specified '''[[Armor]]''' form. |name = SetArmorRCT |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = armor |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = RCTPath |Type = string }} |example = SetArmorRCT ArmorMetal "MetalArmor.rdt" }} ==Notes== *The file path is relative to the '''\Data\Meshes\''' folder. ==See Also== *[[GetArmorRCT]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Armor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Armor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets/un-sets the specified flag (determined by flagID) for the specified '''Base Effect'''. |name = SetBaseEffectFlag |returnType = isFlagSet:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = baseEffect |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagID |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagSet |Type = 0/1 }} ==Base Effect Flag IDs== 
0	Hostile
1	Recover
2	Detrimental
3	Hidden
4	Self
5	Touch
6	Target
7	No Duration
8	No Magnitude
9	No Area
10	FX Persist
12	Gory Visuals
13	Display Name Only
19	Use Skill
20	Use Attribute
24	Painless
27	No Hit Effect
28	No Death Dispel
|example = SetBaseEffectFlag DamageStrength 2 0 }} ==See Also== *[[GetBaseEffectFlag]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Effect Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the associated '''Script''' of the specified script-type '''Base Effect'''. |name = SetBaseEffectScript |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = baseEffect |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = script |Type = ref }} |example = SetBaseEffectScript CateyeBaseEffect CateyeEffect }} ==See Also== *[[GetBaseEffectScript]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Effect Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the Ragdoll used by a [[Body-Part Data]]. |name = SetBodyPartDataRagdoll |returnType = ragDoll:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = bodyPartData |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newRagDoll |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = SetBodyPartDataRagdoll NVGecko RadroachRagdoll SetBodyPartDataRagdoll NVGecko ; (Sets the Ragdoll to None) }} ==See Also== *[[GetBodyPartDataRagdoll]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Body-Part Data Functions (JIP)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the Skeleton Model path of a Body-Part data. |name = SetBodyPartDataSkeleton |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = bodyPar..."
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the Skeleton Model path of a Body-Part data. |name = SetBodyPartDataSkeleton |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = bodyPartData |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = modelPath |Type = string }} |example = SetBodyPartDataSkeleton NVGecko "creatures\NVGecko\skeleton.nif" }} ==See Also== *[[GetBodyPartDataSkeleton]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Body-Part Data Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets/un-sets the specified flag (determined by flagID) for the specified body-part type (determined by partID) of the passed [[Body-Part Data]]. |name = SetBodyPartFlag |returnType = flagValue:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = bodyPartData |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = partID |Type = int |Value = 0-14 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagID |Type = int |Value = 0-6 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = 0/1 }} ==Body-Part Type IDs== 
0	Torso
1	Head 1
2	Head 2
3	Left Arm 1
4	Left Arm 2
5	Right Arm 1
6	Right Arm 2
7	Left Leg 1
8	Left Leg 2
9	Left Leg 3
10	Right Leg 1
11	Right Leg 2
12	Right Leg 3
13	Brain
14	Weapon
==Body-Part Flag IDs== 
0	Severable
1	IK Data
2	IK Data - Biped Data
3	Explodable
4	IK Data - Is Head
5	IK Data - Headtracking
6	Explodable - Absolute
|example = SetBodyPartFlag NVGecko 1 3 1 Will set the Explodable flag for the Head 1 body-part of NVGecko. }} ==See Also== *[[GetBodyPartFlag]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Body-Part Data Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the name of the specified body-part type (determined by partID) of the passed [[Body-Part Data]] to the specified [[string]]. |name = SetBodyPartName |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = bodyPartData |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = partID |Type = int |Value = 0-14 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newName |Type = string }} ==Body-Part Type IDs== 
0	Torso
1	Head 1
2	Head 2
3	Left Arm 1
4	Left Arm 2
5	Right Arm 1
6	Right Arm 2
7	Left Leg 1
8	Left Leg 2
9	Left Leg 3
10	Right Leg 1
11	Right Leg 2
12	Right Leg 3
13	Brain
14	Weapon
|example = SetBodyPartName RobobrainBodyPartData 0 "Chassis" Will set the name of the Torso body-part of RobobrainBodyPartData to "Chassis". }} ==See Also== *[[GetBodyPartName]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Body-Part Data Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Name Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Name Functions]] [[Category:String Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the Limb Replacement model path of the specified body-part type (determined by partID) of the passed Body-Part data. |name = SetBodyPartReplacementModel |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = bodyPartData |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = partID |Type = int |Value = 0-14 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = modelPath |Type = string }} ==Body-Part Type IDs== 
0	Torso
1	Head 1
2	Head 2
3	Left Arm 1
4	Left Arm 2
5	Right Arm 1
6	Right Arm 2
7	Left Leg 1
8	Left Leg 2
9	Left Leg 3
10	Right Leg 1
11	Right Leg 2
12	Right Leg 3
13	Brain
14	Weapon
|example = SetBodyPartReplacementModel NVGecko 0 "creatures\NVGiantRat\torso_rep.nif" Will set the Limb Replacement model path of the Torso body-part of NVGecko to "creatures\NVGiantRat\torso_rep.nif". }} ==See Also== *[[GetBodyPartReplacementModel]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Body-Part Data Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the form-type value of the specified trait (determined by traitID), of the specified body-part type (determined by partID<..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the form-type value of the specified trait (determined by traitID), of the specified body-part type (determined by partID) of the passed Body-Part data. |name = SetBodyPartTraitForm |returnType = traitValue:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = bodyPartData |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = partID |Type = int |Value = 0-14 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-5 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = ref |Optional = y }} ==Body-Part Type IDs== 
0	Torso
1	Head 1
2	Head 2
3	Left Arm 1
4	Left Arm 2
5	Right Arm 1
6	Right Arm 2
7	Left Leg 1
8	Left Leg 2
9	Left Leg 3
10	Right Leg 1
11	Right Leg 2
12	Right Leg 3
13	Brain
14	Weapon
==Body-Part Form Trait IDs== 
0	Severable - Explosion Type
1	Explodable - Explosion Type
2	Severable - Generic Debris
3	Explodable - Generic Debris
4	Severable - Blood Spurt / Decal Impact Data
5	Explodable - Blood Spurt / Decal Impact Data
|example = SetBodyPartTraitForm NVGecko 0 1 LimbExplosion Will set the Explosion Type (Explodable) of the Torso body-part of NVGecko to LimbExplosion. SetBodyPartTraitForm NVGecko 5 2 Will set the Generic Debris (Severable) of the Right Arm 1 body-part of NVGecko to None. }} ==See Also== *[[GetBodyPartTraitForm]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Body-Part Data Functions (JIP)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the numeric value of the specified trait (determined by traitID), of the specified body-part type (determined by partID
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the numeric value of the specified trait (determined by traitID), of the specified body-part type (determined by partID) of the passed Body-Part data. |name = SetBodyPartTraitNumeric |returnType = traitValue:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = bodyPartData |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = partID |Type = int |Value = 0-14 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-18 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = float }} ==Body-Part Type IDs== 
0	Torso
1	Head 1
2	Head 2
3	Left Arm 1
4	Left Arm 2
5	Right Arm 1
6	Right Arm 2
7	Left Leg 1
8	Left Leg 2
9	Left Leg 3
10	Right Leg 1
11	Right Leg 2
12	Right Leg 3
13	Brain
14	Weapon
==Body-Part Numeric Trait IDs== 
0	Actor Value Code
1	Damage Mult.
2	Health Percent
3	To Hit Chance
4	Tracking Max. Angle
5	Severable - Debris Count
6	Severable - Debris Scale
7	Severable - Decal Count
8	Explosion Chance
9	Limb Replacement Scale
10	Explodable - Debris Count
11	Explodable - Debris Scale
12	Explodable - Decal Count
13	Local Translate: X
14	Local Translate: Y
15	Local Translate: Z
16	Local Rotate: X
17	Local Rotate: Y
18	Local Rotate: Z
|example = SetBodyPartTraitNumeric NVGecko 0 2 75 Will set the Health Percent of the Torso body-part of NVGecko to 75. }} ==See Also== *[[GetBodyPartTraitNumeric]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Body-Part Data Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets a class' flag value (determined by flagID). |name = SetClassFlag |returnType = flagValue:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = class |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagID |Type = int |Value = 0-19 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = 0/1 }} ==Class Flag IDs== 
0	Playable
1	Guard
2	Buys/Sells Weapons
3	Buys/Sells Armor
4	Buys/Sells Alcohol
5	Buys/Sells Books
6	Buys/Sells Food
7	Buys/Sells Chems
8	Buys/Sells Stimpaks
12	Buys/Sells Miscellaneous
18	Recharge
19	Repair
|example = SetClassFlag NCRRanger 4 0 }} ==See Also== *[[GetClassFlag]] *[[GetClassTraitNumeric]] *[[SetClassTraitNumeric]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Class Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the value of a class' numeric trait (determined by traitID). |name = SetClassTraitNumeric |returnType = traitValue:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = class |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-10 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = int }} ==Class Numeric Trait IDs== 
0	Tag Skill #1
1	Tag Skill #2
2	Tag Skill #3
3	Tag Skill #4
4	Base Strength
5	Base Perception
6	Base Endurance
7	Base Charisma
8	Base Intelligence
9	Base Agility
10	Base Luck
*For Tag Skill values, see [http://fose.silverlock.org/fose_command_doc.html#Actor_Value_Codes here]. |example = SetClassTraitNumeric NCRRanger 2 34 Will set the Tag Skill #3 of NCRRanger to EnergyWeapons. }} ==See Also== *[[GetClassTraitNumeric]] *[[GetClassFlag]] *[[SetClassFlag]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Class Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the Night Sky model path of a climate. |name = SetClimateNightSkyModel |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = climate |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = modelPath |Type = string }} |example = SetClimateNightSkyModel WastelandClimate "Sky\Stars.nif" }} ==See Also== *[[GetClimateNightSkyModel]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Climate Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]]
