{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the Sun Glare texture path of a climate. |name = SetClimateSunGlareTexture |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = climate |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = texturePath |Type = string }} |example = SetClimateSunGlareTexture WastelandClimate "Sky\SunGlare.dds" }} ==See Also== *[[GetClimateSunGlareTexture]] *[[GetClimateSunTexture]] *[[SetClimateSunTexture]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Climate Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the Sun texture path of a climate. |name = SetClimateSunTexture |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = climate |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = texturePath |Type = string }} |example = SetClimateSunTexture WastelandClimate "Sky\Sun.dds" }} ==See Also== *[[GetClimateSunTexture]] *[[GetClimateSunGlareTexture]] *[[SetClimateSunGlareTexture]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Climate Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the value of a climate's numeric trait (determined by traitID). |name = SetClimateTraitNumeric |returnType = traitValue:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = climate |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-6 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = int }} ==Climate Numeric Trait IDs== 
0	Sunrise Begin (minutes)
1	Sunrise End (minutes)
2	Sunset Begin (minutes)
3	Sunset End (minutes)
4	Volatility
5	Moons (0 - None, 1 - Masser, 2 - Secunda, 3 - Both)
6	Phase Length (days)
|example = SetClimateTraitNumeric WastelandClimate 2 1050 Will set the Sunset Begin time of WastelandClimate to 5:30 PM. }} ==See Also== *[[GetClimateTraitNumeric]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Climate Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Disables (1) or enables (0) combat for the calling actor. Actors with combat disabled will <u>never</u> go into combat with other actors. However they can still be attacked by other actors. |name = SetCombatDisabled |returnType = combatDisabled:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = combatDisabled |Type = 0/1 }} |example = actorRef.SetCombatDisabled 1 }} ==Notes== *This is reset (combat is enabled) every time a saved game is loaded. *Does not prevent the usage of weapons from [[Use Weapon Package]] and [[UseWeapon (Function)]] which can be useful for unique behavior.
==See Also== *[[GetCombatDisabled]] *[[SetRestrained]] *[[StopCombat]] *[[ToggleAI]] *[[ToggleCombatAI]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets a [[Combat Style]]'s flag value (determined by flagID). |name = SetCombatStyleFlag |returnType = flagValue:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = combatStyle |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagID |Type = int |Value = 0-8 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = 0/1 }} ==Combat Style Flag IDs== 
0	Choose Attack Using % Chance
1	Melee Alert OK
2	Flee Based on Personal Survival
4	Ignore Threats
5	Ignore Damaging Self
6	Ignore Damaging Group
7	Ignore Damaging Spectators
8	Cannot Use Stealthboy
|example = SetCombatStyleFlag FollowersCombatStyleRanged 4 1 }} ==See Also== *[[GetCombatStyleFlag]] *[[GetCombatStyleTraitNumeric]] *[[SetCombatStyleTraitNumeric]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Combat Style Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the value of a combat style's numeric trait (determined by traitID). |name = SetCombatStyleTraitNumeric |returnType = traitValue:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = combatStyle |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-63 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = float }} ==Combat Style Numeric Trait IDs== 
0	Cover Search Radius
1	Take Cover Chance
2	Wait Time Min.
3	Wait Time Max.
4	Wait to Fire Timer Min.
5	Wait to Fire Timer Max.

9	Weapon Restrictions (0 - None, 1 - Melee Only, 2 - Ranged Only)
10	Ranged Weap Range Mult. Max.
11	Max Targeting FOV
12	Combat Radius
13	Semi-Auto Firing Delay Mult. Min.
14	Semi-Auto Firing Delay Mult. Max.
15	Dodge % Chance
16	Left/Right % Chance
17	Dodge L/R Timer Min.
18	Dodge L/R Timer Max.
19	Dodge Forward Timer Min.
20	Dodge Forward Timer Max.
21	Dodge Back Timer Min.
22	Dodge Back Timer Max.
23	Idle Timer Min.
24	Idle Timer Max.
25	Block % Chance
26	Attack % Chance
27	Recoil/Stagger Bonus to Attack
28	Unconscious Bonus to Attack
29	Hand-to-Hand Bonus to Attack
30	Power Attack % Chance
31	Recoil/Stagger Bonus to Power
32	Unconscious Bonus to Power Attack
33	Power Attack % Chance Normal
34	Power Attack % Chance Forward
35	Power Attack % Chance Back
36	Power Attack % Chance Left
37	Power Attack % Chance Right
38	Hold Timer Min.
39	Hold Timer Max.
40	Acrobatic Dodge % Chance
41	Rushing Attack % Chance

50	Dodge Back Not Under Attack Mult.
51	Dodge Forward While Attacking Mult.
52	Dodge Forward Not Attacking Mult.
53	Block Skill Modifier Base
54	Block Skill Modifier Mult.
55	Block While Under Attack Mult.
56	Block Not Under Attack Mult.
57	Attack Skill Modifier Base
58	Attack Skill Modifier Mult.
59	Attack While Under Attack Mult.
60	Attack Not Under Attack Mult.
61	Attack During Block Mult.
62	Power Att. Fatigue Mod Base
63	Power Att. Fatigue Mod Mult.
|example = SetCombatStyleTraitNumeric FollowersCombatStyleRanged 12 5000 Will set the Combat Radius of FollowersCombatStyleRanged to 5000. }} ==See Also== *[[GetCombatStyleTraitNumeric]] *[[GetCombatStyleFlag]] *[[SetCombatStyleFlag]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Combat Style Functions (JIP)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the Respawns flag for the passed Container. |name = SetContainerRespawns |referenceType = reference |returnType = r..."
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the Respawns flag for the passed Container. |name = SetContainerRespawns |referenceType = reference |returnType = respawns:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = respawns |Type = 0/1 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = SetContainerRespawns 1 CampSearchlightFootlocker containerRef.SetContainerRespawns 0 }} ==Notes== *The changes will be applied on all instances of the container's base form in the world. ==See Also== *[[GetContainerRespawns]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets a [[:Category:creatures|creatures]] melee attack damage. |name = SetCreatureDamage |returnType = damage:int |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = damage |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = RexRef.SetCreatureDamage 45
SetCreatureDamage 28 Rex }} ==Notes== *This attribute is only applicable to creatures that are unarmed and attack using parts of their skeleton. ==See Also== *[[GetCreatureDamage]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Creature Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the current climate. |name = SetCurrentClimate |returnType = climate:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = newClimate |Type = ref }} |example = SetCurrentClimate NVDLC02ZionValleyClimate }} ==Notes== *For the new Climate effects to apply immediately, call [[RefreshCurrentClimate]] after calling this function.
==Bugs== *After waiting for ~7 seconds after using this function, the active climate reverts back to the previous default. To block climate transitions, use [[ForceClimate]].
==See Also== *[[GetCurrentClimate]] *[[ForceClimate]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Climate Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions_(ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the time remaining until the next iteration of the quest's script. |name = SetDelayElapsed |returnType = seconds:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = quest |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = seconds |Type = float }} |example = SetDelayElapsed vCCExplorerBark 10 }} ==Notes== *This will temporarily override the script's processing delay. *A value of 0 will effectively force the script to re-process immediately on the next frame. ==See Also== *[[GetDelayElapsed]] *[[SetQuestDelay]] *[[GetQuestDelay]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Script Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Script_Functions]] [[Category:Quest Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the Health value of the Destruction Data of the specified Object. |name = SetDestructionDataHealth |returnType = health:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = object |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = int }} |example = SetDestructionDataHealth ElectricBox01 25 }} ==Notes== *Obviously, this function is only usable with object types that have Destruction Data. ==See Also== *[[GetDestructionDataHealth]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Destruction Data Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets/un-sets the Destruction Data of the specified Object as VATS Targetable. |name = SetDestructionDataTargetable |returnType = targetable:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = object |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = targetable |Type = 0/1 }} |example = SetDestructionDataTargetable ElectricBox01 1 }} ==Notes== *Obviously, this function is only usable with object types that have Destruction Data. ==See Also== *[[GetDestructionDataTargetable]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Destruction Data Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the time (seconds) remaining until the calling projectile detonates. |name = SetDetonationTimer |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = newTime |Type = float }} |example = projRef.SetDetonationTimer 4.5 ==Notes== *Using this function on a live mine that hasn't been yet triggered will initiate its detonation sequence, and thus will explode when the duration is up, no matter what value is used. }} ==See Also== *[[GetDetonationTimer]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions]] [[Category:Time Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets/un-sets the specified flag (determined by flagID) for the specified '''[[Actor Effect]]/[[Object Effect]]/[[Ingestible]] Item'''. |name = SetEffectFlag |returnType = isFlagSet:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = effectForm |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagID |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagSet |Type = 0/1 }} ==Actor Effect Flag IDs== 
0	No Auto-Calc
2	PC Start Effect
4	Area Effect Ignores LOS
5	Script Effect Always Applies
6	Disallow Absorb/Reflect
7	Force Touch Explode
==Object Effect Flag IDs== 
0	No Auto-Calc
2	Hide Effect
==Ingestible Item Flag IDs== 
0	No Auto-Calc
1	Food Item
2	Medicine
|example = SetEffectFlag EnchClothingDuster 2 1 }} ==See Also== *[[GetEffectFlag]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Effect Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the [[Actor Value Codes|Actor Value]] associated with the '''[[Weapons#Embedded_Weapon|Embedded Weapon]]''' setting of the specified weapon form. |name = SetEmbeddedWeaponAV |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = actorValue |Type = StatName }} |example = SetEmbeddedWeaponAV SentryBotGatling2HH BrainCondition }} ==See Also== *[[SetEmbeddedWeaponNode]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the name of the '''[[Weapons#Embedded_Weapon|Embedded Weapon]]''' node of the specified [[Weapon]] form. |name = SetEmbeddedWeaponNode |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = nodeName |Type = string }} |example = SetEmbeddedWeaponNode SentryBotGatling2HH "ProjectileNode_RGatling" }} ==See Also== *[[SetEmbeddedWeaponAV]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets an explosion's flag value (determined by flagID). |name = SetExplosionFlag |returnType = flagValue:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = explosion |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagID |Type = int |Value = 1-6 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = 0/1 }} ==Explosion Flag IDs== 
1	Always Use World Orientation
2	Knock Down - Always
3	Knock Down - By Formula
4	Ignore LOS Check
5	Push Explosion Source Ref Only
6	Ignore Image Space Swap
|example = SetExplosionFlag 40mmGrenadeExplosion 4 0 }} ==See Also== *[[GetExplosionFlag]] *[[GetExplosionTraitNumeric]] *[[SetExplosionTraitNumeric]] *[[GetExplosionTraitForm]] *[[SetExplosionTraitForm]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Explosion Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the value of an explosion's form-type trait (determined by traitID). |name = SetExplosionTraitForm |returnType = traitForm:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = explosion |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-4 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} ==Explosion Form Trait IDs== 
0	Light
1	Sound #1
2	Sound #2
3	Impact Data Set
4	Placed Impact Object
5	Enchantment
6	IS Mod
|example = SetExplosionTraitForm 40mmGrenadeExplosion 4 Baseball01 Will set the Placed Impact Object of 40mmGrenadeExplosion to Baseball01. SetExplosionTraitForm 40mmGrenadeExplosion 1 Will set the Sound #1 of 40mmGrenadeExplosion to None. }} ==See Also== *[[GetExplosionTraitForm]] *[[GetExplosionTraitNumeric]] *[[SetExplosionTraitNumeric]] *[[GetExplosionFlag]] *[[SetExplosionFlag]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Explosion Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the value of a numeric trait (determined by traitID) of the specified '''Explosion'''. |name = SetExplosionTraitNumeric |returnType = traitValue:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = explosion |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-7 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = float }} ==Explosion Numeric Trait IDs== 
0	Force
1	Damage
2	Radius
3	IS Radius
4	RAD Level
5	RAD Dissipation Time
6	RAD Radius
7	Sound Level
|example = SetExplosionTraitNumeric 40mmGrenadeExplosion 2 1500 Will set the Radius of 40mmGrenadeExplosion to 1500. }} ==See Also== *[[GetExplosionTraitNumeric]] *[[GetExplosionTraitForm]] *[[SetExplosionTraitForm]] *[[GetExplosionFlag]] *[[SetExplosionFlag]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Explosion Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets a [[faction]]'s reputation type. |name = SetFactionReputationType |returnType = reputation:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = faction |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newReputation |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = SetFactionReputationType FreedSlaveFaction RepNVNCR SetFactionReputationType FreedSlaveFaction ; (Sets the reputation type to None) }} ==See Also== *[[GetFactionReputationType]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Faction Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Faction Functions]] [[Category:Reputation Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the source-file (.fnt) of the specified font type (1-8, 10-89). This enables to dynamically manipulate the graphics of the game's fonts during run-time. Returns 1 (true) on success, 0 (false) otherwise. |name = SetFontFile |returnType = success:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = fontType |Type = int |Value = 1-8, 10-89 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = filePath |Type = string }} |example = SetFontFile 2 "textures\fonts\My_Custom_Font.fnt" }} ==Notes== *''filePath'' is relative to the game's '''Data''' folder.
==See Also== *[[GetFontFile]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:String Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the '''Description''' text of the specified form (e.g. [[Note]], [[Perk]], [[Message]], [[Terminal]], [[Weapon Mod]], [[Challenge]], [[Loading Screen]], etc.). |name = SetFormDescription |returnType = success:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = descriptionText |Type = formattedString }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = formatVars(up to 20) |Optional = y }} |example = SetFormDescription McCarranFieldNote01 "Put some text here" }} ==Notes== *descriptionText supports [[String Formatting|string formatting]], and up to 20 variables may be used. ==See Also== *[[GetFormDescription]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Message Functions]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:String Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the value of a [[Global Variable]]. |name = SetGlobalVariable |returnType = value:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = global |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = float }} |example = 
int	iModIndex
ref	rGlobalVar


set iModIndex to GetModIndex "LonesomeRoad.esm"
set rGlobalVar to BuildRef iModIndex 13842

SetGlobalVariable rGlobalVar 15.6
}} ==Notes== *This function is meant to be used for setting the values of external global variables (i.e. from other mods). You first use [[BuildRef]] or [[GetFormFromMod]] and create a reference to the global, on which this function can then be called. ==See Also== *[[GetGlobalVariable]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Global Variable Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the model (Art File) path of the specified Grass form. |name = SetGrassModel |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = grass |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = modelPath |Type = string }} |example = SetGrassModel GrassWasteland01 "Landscape\Grass\GrassLawn01.nif" }} ==See Also== *[[GetGrassModel]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Grass Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the value of a numeric trait (determined by traitID) of the specified Grass form. |name = SetGrassTraitNumeric |returnType = traitValue:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = grass |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-9 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = float }} ==Grass Numeric Trait IDs== 
0	Density
1	Min. Slope
2	Max. Slope
3	Units from Water Amount
4	Units from Water Type (see below)
5	Position Range
6	Height Range
7	Color Range
8	Wave Period
9	Flags Bitmask (see below)
'''Units from Water Types:''' 
0	Above - At Least
1	Above - At Most
2	Below - At Least
3	Below - At Most
4	Either - At Least
5	Either - At Most
6	Either - At Most Above
7	Either - At Most Below
'''Flags:''' 
1	Vertex Lighting
2	Uniform Scaling
4	Fit to Slope
|example = SetGrassTraitNumeric GrassWasteland01 5 20 Will set the Position Range of GrassWasteland01 to 20. }} ==See Also== *[[GetGrassTraitNumeric]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Grass Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Associates the calling Inventory Reference with the specified hotkey number. |name = SetHotkeyItemRef |returnType = success:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = hotkeyNum |Type = int |Value = 1, 3-8 }} |example = itemRef.SetHotkeyItemRef 5 This will associate itemRef with hotkey #5. }} ==See Also== *[[SetHotkeyItem]] / [[SetHotkey]] *[[GetHotkeyItem]] / [[GetHotkeyItemRef]] *[[SaveHotkeys]] + [[RestoreHotkeys]] *[[ClearAllHotkeys]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Hotkey Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Reference Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the Min and Max loop times of the passed '''[[Idle Animation]]'''. |name = SetIdleLoopTimes |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = idleAnim |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = minTimes |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = maxTimes |Type = int }} |example = SetIdleLoopTimes RepairWall02 1 4 }} ==See Also== *[[GetIdleLoopTimes]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Animation Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the model path (relative to '''data\meshes\''') of the specified '''[[Impact Data]]'''. |name = SetImpactDataModel |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = impactData |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = modelPath |Type = string }} |example = SetImpactDataModel DeathClawsFleshImpact "effects\ImpactBallisticBlood01.nif" }} ==See Also== *[[GetImpactDataModel]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Impact Data Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets a trait value (determined by traitID) of the specified '''Impact Data'''. |name = SetImpactDataTraitForm |returnType = trai..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets a trait value (determined by traitID) of the specified '''Impact Data'''. |name = SetImpactDataTraitForm |returnType = traitValue:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = impactData |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-2 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = ref }} ==Impact Data Form Trait IDs== 
0	Texture Set
1	Sound 1
2	Sound 2
|example = SetImpactDataTraitForm DeathClawsFleshImpact 0 DecalOrganicBallistic Will set the Texture Set of DeathClawsFleshImpact to DecalOrganicBallistic. }} ==See Also== *[[GetImpactDataTrait]] *[[SetImpactDataTraitNumeric]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Impact Data Functions (JIP)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets a trait value (determined by traitID) of the specified '''Impact Data'''. |name = SetImpactDataTraitNumeric |returnType = t..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets a trait value (determined by traitID) of the specified '''Impact Data'''. |name = SetImpactDataTraitNumeric |returnType = traitValue:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = impactData |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-14 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = float }} ==Impact Data Numeric Trait IDs== 
0	Duration
1	Orientation (0 - Surface Normal, 1 - Proj. Vector, 2 - Proj. Reflection)
2	Angle Threshold
3	Placement Radius
4	Sound Level (0 - Loud, 1 - Normal, 2 - Silent)
5	Min. Width
6	Max. Width
7	Min. Height
8	Max. Height
9	Depth
10	Shininess
11	Scale
12	Passes
13	Flag Bitmask (see below)
14	Color (RRRGGGBBB)
'''Impact Data Flags:''' 
1	No Decal Data
2	Has Parallax
4	Alpha - Blending
8	Alpha - Testing
|example = SetImpactDataTraitNumeric DeathClawsFleshImpact 10 3 Will set the Shininess of DeathClawsFleshImpact to 3. }} ==See Also== *[[GetImpactDataTrait]] *[[SetImpactDataTraitForm]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Impact Data Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Disables/enables interaction with the [[calling reference]], or (optional) [[Base Form]], by the [[player|player character]]. When interaction is disabled, the player will not be able to "activate" the object, and no rollover-text will be shown when the object is pointed at with the crosshair. |name = SetInteractionDisabled |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = disable |Type = 0/1 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = objectRef.SetInteractionDisabled 1 SetInteractionDisabled 1 CraigBoone }} ==Notes== *This function does not affect scripted activation: '''objectRef.[[Activate]] player''' will still work normally. *When passing a base form, the effects will be applied on ANY reference of that base form. *The function persists through the game session. ==See Also== *[[GetInteractionDisabled]] *[[GetInteractionDisabledType]] *[[SetInteractionDisabledType]] *[[SetDestroyed]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Disables/enables interaction with ANY object of the specified [[Form Type IDs|type code]], by the player character.
When interaction is disabled, the player will not be able to "activate" the object, and no rollover-text will be shown when the object is pointed at with the crosshair. |name = SetInteractionDisabledType |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = disable |Type = 0/1 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = typeCode |Type = int }} |example = SetInteractionDisabledType 1 28 }} ==Notes== *This function <u>does not</u> affect scripted activation: '''objectRef.Activate player''' will still work normally. ==See Also== *[[GetInteractionDisabledType]] *[[GetInteractionDisabled]] *[[SetInteractionDisabled]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the current health of the calling item. Unlike [[SetEquippedCurrentHealth]], this function is called on an [[inventory reference]]. The calling item reference does not have to be equipped. *If the 2nd argument, '''setPercent''', is non-zero, the function will set the calling item's health percent (0.0 to 1.0). |name = SetItemRefCurrentHealth |returnType = success:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = newHealth |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = setPercent |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }} |example = rItemRef.SetItemRefCurrentHealth 185 rItemRef.SetItemRefCurrentHealth 0.7 1 }} ==See Also== *[[GetItemRefCurrentHealth]] *[[GetInvRefsForItem]] *[[GetFirstRefForItem]] *[[SetCurrentHealth]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Reference Functions]]

revision.comment: Added FO3 and ButcherPete categories.
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the Texture-Set of a Land Texture. |name = SetLandTextureTextureSet |returnType = textureSet:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = landTexture |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newTextureSet |Type = ref }} |example = SetLandTextureTextureSet LScrubDirtCanyon01Grass LandscapeRocksNV }} ==See Also== *[[GetLandTextureTextureSet]] *[[GetLandTextureTraitNumeric]] *[[SetLandTextureTraitNumeric]] *[[GetLandTextureNumGrasses]] *[[GetLandTextureNthGrass]] *[[LandTextureAddGrass]] *[[LandTextureRemoveGrass]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Land Texture Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the value of a Land Texture's numeric trait (determined by traitID). |name = SetLandTextureTraitNumeric |returnType = traitValue:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = landTexture |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-3 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = int }} ==Land Texture Numeric Trait IDs== 
0	Material Type
1	Friction
2	Restitution
3	Texture Specular Exponent
|example = SetLandTextureTraitNumeric LScrubDirtCanyon01Grass 1 20 Will set the Friction of LScrubDirtCanyon01Grass to 20. }} ==See Also== *[[GetLandTextureTraitNumeric]] *[[GetLandTextureTextureSet]] *[[SetLandTextureTextureSet]] *[[GetLandTextureNumGrasses]] *[[GetLandTextureNthGrass]] *[[LandTextureAddGrass]] *[[LandTextureRemoveGrass]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Land Texture Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets a Light's flag value (determined by flagID). |name = SetLightFlag |returnType = flagValue:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = light |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagID |Type = int |Value = 0-31 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = 0/1 }} ==Light Flag IDs== 
0	Dynamic
1	Can Be Carried
2	Negative
3	Flicker
5	Off By Default
6	Flicker Slow
7	Pulse
8	Pulse Slow
11	Colour Shift
12	Colour Shift Slow
|example = SetLightFlag BasementLightFillWarm 6 1 }} ==See Also== *[[GetLightFlag]] *[[GetLightTraitNumeric]] *[[SetLightTraitNumeric]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Light Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Light_Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the value of a Light's numeric trait (determined by traitID). |name = SetLightTraitNumeric |returnType = traitValue:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = light |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-6 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = float }} ==Light Numeric Trait IDs== 
0	Radius
1	Red
2	Green
3	Blue
4	Falloff Exponent
5	FOV
6	Fade
|example = SetLightTraitNumeric BasementLightFillWarm 0 500 Will set the Radius of BasementLightFillWarm to 500. }} ==See Also== *[[GetLightTraitNumeric]] *[[GetLightFlag]] *[[SetLightFlag]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Light Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Light_Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the calling reference's linked reference to targetRef or, if the latter is not specified, resets its linked reference to the original value. |name = SetLinkedReference |alias = SetLinkedRef |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = targetRef |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = ObjectRef.SetLinkedReference linkedRef }} ==See Also== *[[GetLinkedRef]] *[[GetLinkedChildren]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |originVersion = 36.00 |summary = Sets the X, Y, and Z components of local acceleration (in [[Units|Game Units]] per second squared) due to '''gravity''' for the current cell.
X and Y accelerations in the game are always zero, by default. Modifying them to non-zero values may, theoretically, be used to cause gravity to act in a non-vertical direction.
Negative values are the norm, causing objects to accelerate downward, but positive values are also acceptable.
Changing the gravity for an exterior cell changes it for <u>all exterior cells</u> until it is reset by another call to this function. Changes to gravity in an interior cell affect <u>only that cell</u>, and the change will be reset once the cell is reloaded. |name = SetLocalGravityVector |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = xGravity |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = yGravity |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = zGravity |Type = float }} |example = SetLocalGravityVector 0 0 -45 }} ==See Also== *[[GetLocalGravity]] *[[GetActorGravityMult]] *[[SetActorGravityMult]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Movement Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the value of the specified property (determined by valueID) in the '''[[NiMaterialProperty]]''' of the specified '''Nif Block''' of the calling object reference. ==Material Property Value IDs== 
0	Specular Color - Red
1	Specular Color - Green
2	Specular Color - Blue
3	Emissive Color - Red
4	Emissive Color - Green
5	Emissive Color - Blue
6	Glossiness
7	Alpha
8	Emit Mult.
|name = SetMaterialPropertyValue |alias = SetMatrPropVal |returnType = propValue:float |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = nifBlockName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = valueID |Type = int |Value = 0-8 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = float }} |example = NAFBTunnelDoorRef.SetMaterialPropertyValue "TrackPanelRight01:13" 7 0.6 This will set the Alpha value of the NiMaterialProperty, of the "TrackPanelRight01:13" block, in the Nif used by NAFBTunnelDoorRef, to 0.6. }} ==Notes== This function does not work on inventory references, for ex Weapons. It will modify the property on the base form. ==See Also== *[[GetMaterialPropertyValue]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Disables/enables the specified '''[[Message]]''' form. A disabled message will never be displayed by the game. Either a single '''[[Message]]''' or a '''[[Form List]]''' may be passed as the first parameter (in the latter case, all messages contained in the list will be disabled/enabled). |name = SetMessageDisabled |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = messageOrList |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = disable |Type = 0/1 }} |example = SetMessageDisabled AddictionRemovalMsg 1
SetMessageDisabled HelpManual 1 }} ==See Also== *[[GetMessageDisabled]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Message Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the display time (in seconds) of the specified '''[[Message]]''' form. |name = SetMessageDisplayTime |returnType = displayTime:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = message |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = displayTime |Type = int }} |example = SetMessageDisplayTime AddictionRemovalMsg 3 }} ==See Also== [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Message Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]]

revision.comment: Added FO3 and ButcherPete categories.
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the flags state of the specified '''[[Message]]''' form to the specified bitmask. |name = SetMessageFlags |returnType = flagState:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = message |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newState |Type = int }} '''Message Flags:''' 
1	Is Message Box
2	Auto-display
|example = SetMessageFlags AddictionRemovalMsg 2 This will disable the Is Message Box flag, and enable the Auto-display flag for AddictionRemovalMsg. }} ==See Also== *[[GetMessageFlags]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Message Functions]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]]

revision.comment: Added texture functions category.
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the texture path of the '''Moon''', specified by moonType: 
0	Full
1	Three Wan
2	Half Wan
3	One Wan
4	No Moon
5	One Wax
6	Half Wax
7	Three Wax
|name = SetMoonTexture |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = moonType |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newPath |Type = string }} |example = 
SetMoonTexture 3 "textures/sky/one_wan_alt.dds"
Sets the texture path of the "One Wan" moon. }} ==See Also== *[[GetMoonTexture]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Texture Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the Debris of the Nth destruction stage in the Destruction Data of the specified Object. |name = SetNthDestructionStageDebris |returnType = debris:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = object |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = stageIndex |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newDebris |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = SetNthDestructionStageDebris ElectricBox01 0 debrisFlameMesh Will set the Debris of the first (index 0) destruction stage of ElectricBox01 to debrisFlameMesh. SetNthDestructionStageDebris ElectricBox01 2 Will set the Debris of the third (index 2) destruction stage of ElectricBox01 to None. }} ==Notes== *Obviously, this function is only usable with object types that have Destruction Data. ==See Also== *[[GetNthDestructionStageDebris]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Destruction Data Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the Explosion of the Nth destruction stage in the Destruction Data of the specified Object. |name = SetNthDestructionStageExplosion |returnType = explosion:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = object |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = stageIndex |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newExplosion |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = SetNthDestructionStageExplosion ElectricBox01 0 MissileExplosion Will set the Explosion of the first (index 0) destruction stage of ElectricBox01 to MissileExplosion. SetNthDestructionStageExplosion ElectricBox01 2 Will set the Explosion of the third (index 2) destruction stage of ElectricBox01 to None. }} ==Notes== *Obviously, this function is only usable with object types that have Destruction Data. ==See Also== *[[GetNthDestructionStageExplosion]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Destruction Data Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the Replacement Model path of the Nth destruction stage in the Destruction Data of the specified Object. |name = SetNthDestructionStageReplacement |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = object |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = stageIndex |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = modelPath |Type = string }} |example = SetNthDestructionStageReplacement ElectricBox01 0 "clutter\ElectricBox\ElectricBox1Hulk.nif" Will set the Replacement Model path of the first (index 0) destruction stage of ElectricBox01. }} ==Notes== *Obviously, this function is only usable with object types that have Destruction Data. ==See Also== *[[GetNthDestructionStageReplacement]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Destruction Data Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the value of a numeric trait (determined by traitID) of the Nth destruction stage in the Destruction Data of the specified Object. |name = SetNthDestructionStageTrait |returnType = traitValue:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = object |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = stageIndex |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-4 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = int }} ==Destruction Stage Trait IDs== 
0	Health Percentage
1	Self Damage per Second
2	Model Damage Stage
3	Flags Bitmask (see below)
4	Debris Count
'''Flags:''' 
1	Cap Damage
2	Disable Object
4	Destroy Object
|example = SetNthDestructionStageTrait ElectricBox01 0 1 10 Will set the Self Damage per Second of the first (index 0) destruction stage of ElectricBox01 to 10. SetNthDestructionStageTrait ElectricBox01 2 3 5 Will set the Flags Bitmask of the third (index 2) destruction stage of ElectricBox01 to 5, thus enable Cap Damage (1) and Destroy Object (4), and disable Disable Object (2). }} ==Notes== *Obviously, this function is only usable with object types that have Destruction Data. ==See Also== *[[GetNthDestructionStageTrait]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Destruction Data Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the '''Base Effect''' of the Nth effect-entry of the specified [[ingestible]]/[[Object Effect]]/[[Actor Effect]]. |name = SetNthEffectBase |returnType = baseEffect:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = effectForm |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = effectIndex |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newBaseEffect |Type = ref }} |example = SetNthEffectBase Stimpak 2 ChemIncSTMorphine
SetNthEffectBase EnchPowerArmor 0 IncreaseDamageResistance
SetNthEffectBase WithdrawalPsycho 1 ReduceEndurance }} ==See Also== *[[GetNthEffectBase]] *[[GetNumEffects]] *[[GetNthEffectTraitNumeric]] *[[SetNthEffectTraitNumeric]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Effect Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the value of a numeric trait (determined by traitID) of the Nth effect-entry of the specified [[ingestible]]/[[Object Effect]]/[[Actor Effect]]. |name = SetNthEffectTraitNumeric |returnType = traitValue:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = effectForm |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = effectIndex |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-2 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = int }} ==Effect Numeric Trait IDs== 
0	Magnitude
1	Area
2	Duration (Seconds)
|example = SetNthEffectTraitNumeric Stimpak 0 2 45 Will set the Duration of the effect-entry at index 0 of Stimpak to 45. }} ==See Also== *[[GetNthEffectTraitNumeric]] *[[GetNumEffects]] *[[GetNthEffectBase]] *[[SetNthEffectBase]] *[[GetTempEffects]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Effect Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the [[Object Effect]] of the specified [[BaseForm]] (which can be either a [[Weapon]], an [[Armor]], or an [[Explosion]]). If ''objectEffect'' isn't specified, will remove the object effect from the form. |name = SetObjectEffect |returnType = success:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = form |Type = BaseForm }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = objectEffect |Type = baseForm |Optional = y }} |example = SetObjectEffect WeapNVAssaultCarbine EnchEuclidEffect SetObjectEffect ArmorCombatHelmet EnchPowerArmorHelmet }} ==See Also== *[[GetObjectEffect]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Effect Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''UDF Script''' as a handler that will be invoked in the event the specified '''Actor''' starts performing the specified '''Animation Action''' (see [[GetAnimAction]]). When invoked, the script is passed with the '''Actor''' as the calling reference ("this"), and a single argument: the performed '''Animation Action''' (0-13). |name = SetOnAnimActionEventHandler |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = actorOrList |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = animAction |Type = int |Value = 0-13 }} |example = 
SetOnAnimActionEventHandler OnAnimActionUDF 1 SomeActor 2
'''Sets''' the OnAnimActionUDF script as a handler to be invoked when '''SomeActor''' starts performing the '''Attack''' action. 
SetOnAnimActionEventHandler OnAnimActionUDF 0 SomeActor 2
'''Removes''' the OnAnimActionUDF script as a handler of the above. }} ==Notes== *As the 3rd argument, either a single reference, or a '''Form List''' of references may be passed. *If the 3rd argument is an empty '''Form List''', the handler will be invoked for '''ANY''' actor performing the specified '''Animation Action'''. ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn	OnAnimActionUDF

int	iAnimAction
ref	rActorRef

begin Function {iAnimAction}

	set rActorRef to this

	(code)

end
==See Also== *[[SetOnPlayGroupEventHandler]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Animation Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''UDF Script''' as a handler that will be invoked in the event the specified '''Actor''' has one of his limbs crippled. When invoked, the script is passed with the '''Actor''' as the calling reference ("this"), and a single argument: an integer indicating which limb was crippled: 
0	Head
1	Torso
2	Left Hand
3	Right Hand
4	Left Leg
5	Right Leg
6	Brain
|name = SetOnCrippledLimbEventHandler |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = actorOrList |Type = ref }} |example = 
SetOnCrippledLimbEventHandler OnCrippledLimbUDF 1 SomeActor
'''Sets''' the OnCrippledLimbUDF script as a handler to be invoked when '''SomeActor''' becomes crippled. 
SetOnCrippledLimbEventHandler OnCrippledLimbUDF 0 SomeActor
'''Removes''' the OnCrippledLimbUDF script as a handler of the above. }} ==Notes== *As the third argument, either a single reference, or a '''FormList''' of references may be passed. ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn	OnCrippledLimbUDF

int	iWhichLimb
ref	rActorRef

begin Function {iWhichLimb}

	set rActorRef to this

	(code)

end
==See Also== *[[SetOnHealthDamageEventHandler]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

revision.comment: Event works with linked marker if any, not necessarily the map marker proper
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''UDF Script''' as a handler that will be invoked in the event the player fast-travels to a '''Map Marker'''. When invoked, the script is passed with a single argument: the Marker reference traveled to, which may or may not be an actual Map Marker base object (hardcoded form ID 10). |name = SetOnFastTravelEventHandler |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }} |example = 
SetOnFastTravelEventHandler OnPCFastTravelUDF 1
'''Sets''' the OnPCFastTravelUDF script as a handler. 
SetOnFastTravelEventHandler OnPCFastTravelUDF 0
'''Removes''' the OnPCFastTravelUDF script as a handler. }} ==Handler Script== This skeleton handler script illustrates how to retrieve what would be the ''real'' Map Marker reference that the player clicked in the Pip-Boy map, in the event it has a linked marker where the player is set to appear: 
scn	OnPCFastTravelUDF

ref	rMapMarkerRef
ref	rMapMarkerReal
array_var	aMapMarkerLinks

begin Function {rMapMarkerRef}

if rMapMarkerRef.GetBaseObject != MapMarker ;the marker we get sent to is not the real marker from the Pip-Boy
	aMapMarkerLinks = rMapMarkerRef.GetLinkedChildren
	eval (ar_Size aMapMarkerLinks) > 0 && (rMapMarkerReal = aMapMarkerLinks[0]) ;assumes the first or only reference linked to this marker is the real map marker we traveled to.
else
	rMapMarkerReal = rMapMarkerRef
endif

end
==See Also== [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''[[UDF|UDF Script]]''' as a handler that will be invoked in the event the specified '''[[Actor]]''' fires a round with a ranged-weapon. *The 3rd argument, '''actorOrList''', is optional. Either a single reference, or a '''[[FormList]]''' of [[reference]]s may be passed. If omitted, the script will be invoked when '''ANY''' actor fires a weapon. When invoked, the script is passed with the '''[[Actor]]''' as the [[calling reference]] ("this"), and a single argument: the '''[[Weapon]]''' (base form) that was fired. |name = SetOnFireWeaponEventHandler |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = actorOrList |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = 
SetOnFireWeaponEventHandler OnFireWeaponUDF 1 SomeActor
'''Sets''' the OnFireWeaponUDF script as a handler to be invoked when '''SomeActor''' fires a weapon. 
SetOnFireWeaponEventHandler OnFireWeaponUDF 0 SomeActor
'''Removes''' the OnFireWeaponUDF script as a handler of the above. 
SetOnFireWeaponEventHandler OnFireWeaponUDF 1
'''Sets''' the OnFireWeaponUDF script as a handler to be invoked when '''ANY''' actor fires a weapon. }} ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn	OnFireWeaponUDF

ref	rWeapon
ref	rActorRef

begin Function {rWeapon}

	set rActorRef to this

	(code)

end
==Notes== *Does not run if the weapon is jammed as it tries to fire. Weapon jam is controlled by the [[fWeaponConditionJamXX]] gamesettings. ==Bugs== *This event will fail to trigger on the player when god mode is enabled but still works in demigod mode. ==See Also== *[[SetOnReloadWeaponEventHandler]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

revision.comment: fDamageAmount is a negative val
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''UDF Script''' as a handler that will be invoked in the event the specified '''Actor''' takes Health damage. When invoked, the script is passed with the '''Actor''' as the calling reference ("this"), and two arguments: the amount of damage taken and, if the damage resulted from being hit by another actor, the reference of that actor, otherwise (if the damage resulted from falling, for example) 0. The amount of damage taken is a negative value. |name = SetOnHealthDamageEventHandler |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = actorOrList |Type = ref }} |example = 
SetOnHealthDamageEventHandler OnHealthDamageUDF 1 SomeActor
'''Sets''' the OnHealthDamageUDF script as a handler to be invoked when '''SomeActor''' takes Health damage. 
SetOnHealthDamageEventHandler OnHealthDamageUDF 0 SomeActor
'''Removes''' the OnHealthDamageUDF script as a handler of the above. }} ==Notes== *As the third argument, either a single reference, or a '''FormList''' of references may be passed. *If the damage was taken from a poison Actor Effect, the source will be the victim. *If taking damage from collision objects, the source will be 0. *This event will run on damaging an actor's Health Actor Value via [[DamageAV]]. ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn	OnHealthDamageUDF

float	fDamageAmount
ref	rSourceRef
ref	rActorRef

begin Function {fDamageAmount, rSourceRef}

	set rActorRef to this

	(code)

end
==See Also== *[[SetOnCrippledLimbEventHandler]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''[[UDF|UDF Script]]''' as a handler that will be invoked in the event the specified '''UI Tile''' is clicked/pressed. The '''tilePath''' argument may specify a menu name and target a specific tile by its '''''' trait: 
"InventoryMenu#8"
Note the menu name, immediately followed by a '''#''' symbol, then followed by a number matching the '''''' trait of the tile. This is equivalent to "InventoryMenu/GLOW_BRANCH/IM_MainRect/IM_ButtonRect/IM_RepairButton". The '''tilePath''' argument may be the full-path of a tile, such as: 
"InventoryMenu/GLOW_BRANCH/IM_Tabline/Aid" (clicking on Aid tab)
"InventoryMenu/GLOW_BRANCH/IM_Tabline/Apparel" (clicking on Apparel tab)
"InventoryMenu/GLOW_BRANCH/IM_Tabline/Weapons" (clicking on Weapons tab)
"InventoryMenu/GLOW_BRANCH/IM_MainRect/IM_InventoryList/IM_InventoryListTemplateRect" (clicking on any inventory item)
In the above cases the handler will be invoked only when the specific tile is clicked. It may be a partial path, such as: 
"InventoryMenu/GLOW_BRANCH/IM_Tabline"
In which case the handler will be invoked when ANY tile beginning with that path is clicked. Note that the path, either full or partial, must begin with a valid menu name. When invoked, the script is passed with three arguments: *'''(int) Menu Mode ID''' - 1002/1009/1075 etc. *'''(int) Tile ID''' - Only for tiles with a property. Otherwise -1 is passed. *'''(string_var) Tile Name''' - Without the full-path, and in lower-case (for example: "apparel"). For template ListBox tiles, the name is suffixed with the tile's index in the list (for example: "stats_list_template_item:26") |name = SetOnMenuClickEventHandler |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = tilePath |Type = string }} |example = 
SetOnMenuClickEventHandler OnMenuClickUDF 1 "ContainerMenu/NOGLOW_BRANCH/CM_ButtonRect/CM_ExitButton"
'''Sets''' the OnMenuClickUDF script as a handler to be invoked when any UI Tile(s) matching the string is clicked. 
SetOnMenuClickEventHandler OnMenuClickUDF 0 "ContainerMenu/NOGLOW_BRANCH/CM_ButtonRect/CM_ExitButton"
'''Removes''' the OnMenuClickUDF script as a handler of the above. }} ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn	OnMenuClickUDF

int	iMenuID
int	iTileID
string_var	sTileName

begin Function {iMenuID, iTileID, sTileName}

	(code)

	sv_Destruct sTileName

end
If using "InventoryMenu/GLOW_BRANCH/IM_MainRect/IM_InventoryList/IM_InventoryListTemplateRect" to detect inventory item clicks, the function [[GetSelectedItemRef]] can be used to retrieve the inventory item reference, the function [[GetBaseObject]] (GBO) can be used to get the base object from that reference, and the base object can be checked against an object category or a specific object with the functions [[GetType]] and/or [[GetIsID]] respectively. ==See Also== *[[GetSelectedItemRef]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''[[UDF|UDF Script]]''' as a handler that will be invoked in the event the specified [[MenuMode]] is closed (MUST be a ''Specific menu'', NOT a ''Menu Type''). If the menuID parameter is omitted, the script will be invoked when ANY menu mode closes. When invoked, the script is passed with a single argument: the Menu Mode ID. |name = SetOnMenuCloseEventHandler |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = menuID |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = 
SetOnMenuCloseEventHandler OnMenuCloseUDF 1 1002
'''Sets''' the OnMenuCloseUDF script as a handler to be invoked when the '''Inventory Menu''' is closed. 
SetOnMenuCloseEventHandler OnMenuCloseUDF 0 1002
'''Removes''' the OnMenuCloseUDF script as a handler of the above. }} ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn	OnMenuCloseUDF

int	iMenuID

begin Function {iMenuID}

(code)

end
==Warning== If passing the optional menu arg, a specific MenuMode must be provided (rather than a menu type). For example, trying to pass the "main four (the character interface: stats, magic, inventory, quest log)" Menu Type will result in errors/crashes: SetOnMenuOpenEventHandler OnMenuOpenUDF 1 1 ==See Also== *[[SetOnMenuOpenEventHandler]] *[[SetJohnnyOnSettingsUpdateEventHandler]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''[[UDF|UDF Script]]''' as a handler that will be invoked in the event the specified [[MenuMode]] is opened (MUST be a ''Specific menu'', NOT a ''Menu Type''). If the menuID parameter is omitted, the script will be invoked when ANY menu mode opens. When invoked, the script is passed with a single argument: the Menu Mode ID. |name = SetOnMenuOpenEventHandler |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = menuID |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = 
SetOnMenuOpenEventHandler OnMenuOpenUDF 1 1061
'''Sets''' the OnMenuOpenUDF script as a handler to be invoked when the '''Item Mod Menu''' is opened. 
SetOnMenuOpenEventHandler OnMenuOpenUDF 0 1061
'''Removes''' the OnMenuOpenUDF script as a handler of the above. }} ==Notes== *Where applicable, you can use [[GetMenuTargetRef]] in the handler script. ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn	OnMenuOpenUDF

int	iMenuID

begin Function {iMenuID}

(code)

end
==Warning== A specific MenuMode must be specified. For example, trying to pass the "main four (the character interface: stats, magic, inventory, quest log)" Menu Type will result in errors/crashes: SetOnMenuOpenEventHandler OnMenuOpenUDF 1 1 ==See Also== *[[SetOnMenuCloseEventHandler]] *[[SetJohnnyOnSettingsUpdateEventHandler]] *[[ShowOff:OnMenuCreate]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''[[UDF|UDF Script]]''' as a handler that will be invoked in the event the mouseover-ed, or highlighted selection in the specified '''[[Menu Mode]]''', is changed. If the menuID parameter is omitted, the script will be invoked in ANY menu mode. When invoked, the script is passed with a single argument: the Menu Mode ID. |name = SetOnMouseoverChangeEventHandler |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = menuID |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = 
SetOnMouseoverChangeEventHandler OnMouseoverChangeUDF 1 1002
'''Sets''' the OnMouseoverChangeUDF script as a handler to be invoked when the highlighted selection in the '''Inventory Menu''' is changed. 
SetOnMouseoverChangeEventHandler OnMouseoverChangeUDF 0 1002
'''Removes''' the OnMouseoverChangeUDF script as a handler of the above. }} ==Notes== *Use any of [[GetActiveUIComponentID]], [[GetActiveUIComponentName]], or [[GetActiveUIComponentFullName]] to determine which UI component is currently selected. *Where applicable, use [[GetSelectedItemRef]] to determine which item is currently selected. ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn	OnMouseoverChangeUDF

int	iMenuID

begin Function {iMenuID}

(code)

end
==See Also== *[[MenuMode]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''UDF Script''' as a handler that will be invoked in the event the actor reference under the reticle (i.e. the actor the player is aiming at) is changed. When invoked, the script is passed with a single argument: the target actor (which may be null if there is none). |name = SetOnPCTargetChangeEventHandler |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }} |example =
