{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets an [[AI Package]]'s flag value (determined by flagID). |name = SetPackageFlag |returnType = flagValue:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = package |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagID |Type = int |Value = 0-28 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = 0/1 }} ==Package Flag IDs== 
0	Offers Services
1	Must Reach Location
2	Must Complete
3	Lock Doors At Start
4	Lock Doors At End
5	Lock Doors At Location
6	Unlock Doors At Start
7	Unlock Doors At End
8	Unlock Doors At Location
9	Continue If PC Near
10	Once Per Day
12	Skip Fallout Behaviour
13	Always Run
17	Always Sneak
18	Allow Swimming
19	Allow Falls
20	Armor Unequipped
21	Weapons Unequipped
22	Defensive Combat
23	Weapons Drawn
24	No Idle Anims
25	Pretend In Combat
26	Continue During Combat
27	No Combat Alert
28	No Warn Attack Behaviour
|example = SetPackageFlag FollowersCassL38Package 18 1 }} ==See Also== *[[GetPackageFlag]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:AI Package Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:AI Functions]] [[Category:Package Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets/un-sets the specified flag (determined by flagID) for the specified '''[[Perk]]'''. |name = SetPerkFlag |returnType = isFlagSet:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = perk |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagID |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagSet |Type = 0/1 }} ==Perk Flag IDs== 
0	Is Trait;
1	Is Playable;
2	Is Hidden
|example = SetPerkFlag BloodyMess 2 0 }} ==See Also== *[[GetPerkFlag]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Perk Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Perk Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets/un-sets the persistent flag for the calling [[reference]]. |name = SetPersistent |returnType = persistent:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = persistent |Type = 0/1 }} |example = ObjectRef.SetPersistent 1 }} ==See Also== *[[IsPersistent]] *[[GetReferenceFlag]] / [[SetReferenceFlag]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the specified bound of the primitive of the calling reference. |name = SetPrimitiveBound |returnType = bound:float |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = axis |Type = X/Y/Z }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = float }} |example = objectRef.SetPrimitiveBound Z 500 }} ==Notes== *Objects with a primitive include cubic-activators, collision planes, etc. ==See Also== *[[GetPrimitiveBound]] *[[HasPrimitive]] *[[AddPrimitive]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the projectile's explosion. |name = SetProjectileExplosion |returnType = explosion:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = projectile |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newExplosion |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = SetProjectileExplosion 40mmGrenadeProjectile MissileExplosion SetProjectileExplosion 40mmGrenadeProjectile ; (Sets the explosion to None) }} ==See Also== *[[GetProjectileExplosion]] *[[GetProjectileTraitNumeric]] *[[SetProjectileTraitNumeric]] *[[GetProjectileFlag]] *[[SetProjectileFlag]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets a projectile's flag value (determined by flagID). |name = SetProjectileFlag |returnType = flagValue:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = projectile |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagID |Type = int |Value = 0-11 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = 0/1 }} ==Projectile Flag IDs== 
0	Hitscan
1	Explosion
2	Alt. Trigger
3	Muzzle Flash
5	Can Be Disabled
6	Can Be Picked
7	Supersonic
8	Crit. Effect: Pins Limbs
9	Pass Through Small Transparent
10	Detonates
11	Rotation
|example = SetProjectileFlag 12GaSlugProjectile 7 1 }} ==See Also== *[[GetProjectileFlag]] *[[GetProjectileTraitNumeric]] *[[SetProjectileTraitNumeric]] *[[GetProjectileExplosion]] *[[SetProjectileExplosion]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the base damage that will be inflicted by the calling live-projectile, upon impact. By default, this equals the base damage of the source weapon. |name = SetProjectileRefDamage |returnType = damage:float |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = newDamage |Type = float }} |example = liveProjRef.SetProjectileRefDamage 35 }} ==Finding Live-Projectile References== The only way to get a live-projectile reference is by "ref-walking" - that is, walking through projectile references using the [[GetFirstRef]] and [[GetNextRef]] functions. '''Example:''' 
set rProj to GetFirstRef 51 3
while rProj
	; Do something
	set rProj to GetNextRef
loop
==See Also== *[[GetProjectileRefDamage]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the reference that is the "source" of the calling live-projectile - that is, who/what fired it. Whoever is hit by the projectile will consider that reference as his attacker. |name = SetProjectileRefSource |returnType = void |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = newSource |Type = ref }} |example = liveProjRef.SetProjectileRefSource someObjectRef }} ==Finding Live-Projectile References== The only way to get a live-projectile reference is by "ref-walking" - that is, walking through projectile references using the [[GetFirstRef]] and [[GetNextRef]] functions. '''Example:''' 
set rProj to GetFirstRef 51 3
while rProj
	; Do something
	set rProj to GetNextRef
loop
==See Also== *[[GetProjectileRefSource]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the speed multiplier of the calling live-projectile. The effective speed is equal to the base speed (as defined in the projectile's base-form) x speed multiplier. |name = SetProjectileRefSpeedMult |returnType = speedMult:float |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = newMult |Type = float }} |example = liveProjRef.SetProjectileRefSpeedMult 0.75 }} ==Finding Live-Projectile References== The only way to get a live-projectile reference is by "ref-walking" - that is, walking through projectile references using the [[GetFirstRef]] and [[GetNextRef]] functions. '''Example:''' 
set rProj to GetFirstRef 51 3
while rProj
	; Do something
	set rProj to GetNextRef
loop
==See Also== *[[GetProjectileRefSpeedMult]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the weapon that fired the calling live-[[Projectile]]. This will have an effect on Impact Data Set, Object Effect, Critical Effect, and possibly more - all of which will be "inherited" from the new weapon. |name = SetProjectileRefWeapon |returnType = weapon:ref |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = newWeapon |Type = ref }} |example = liveProjRef.SetProjectileRefWeapon WeapNV44Revolver }} ==Finding Live-Projectile References== One way to get a live-projectile reference is by "ref-walking" - that is, walking through projectile references using the [[GetFirstRef]] and [[GetNextRef]] functions. '''Example:''' 
set rProj to GetFirstRef 51 3
while rProj
	; Do something
	set rProj to GetNextRef
loop
Another is to retrieve the live-projectile reference provided by [[SetOnProjectileImpactEventHandler]], which is on the brink of expiring. ==See Also== *[[GetProjectileRefWeapon]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the value of a projectile's numeric trait (determined by traitID). |name = SetProjectileTraitNumeric |returnType = traitValue:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = projectile |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-14 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = float }} ==Projectile Numeric Trait IDs== 
0	Projectile Type
1	Gravity
2	Speed
3	Range
4	Tracer Chance
5	Proximity (Explosion)
6	Timer (Explosion)
7	Muzzle Flash Duration
8	Fade Duration
9	Impact Force
10	Rotation X
11	Rotation Y
12	Rotation Z
13	Bouncy Mult.
14	Detection Sound Level
Projectile Types: 
1	Missile
2	Lobber
4	Beam
8	Flame
16	Continuous Beam
Detection Sound Levels: 
0	Loud
1	Normal
2	Silent
|example = SetProjectileTraitNumeric 12GaSlugProjectile 0 4 Will set the Projectile Type of 12GaSlugProjectile to Beam. }} ==See Also== *[[GetProjectileTraitNumeric]] *[[GetProjectileFlag]] *[[SetProjectileFlag]] *[[GetProjectileExplosion]] *[[SetProjectileExplosion]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets a Sound's flag value (determined by flagID). |name = SetSoundFlag |returnType = flagValue:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = sound |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagID |Type = int |Value = 0-13 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = 0/1 }} ==Sound Flag IDs== 
0	Random Frequency Shift
1	Play At Random
2	Environment Ignored
3	Random Location
4	Loop
5	Menu Sound
6	2D
7	360 LFE
8	Dialogue Sound
9	Envelope Fast
10	Envelope Slow
11	2D Radius
12	Mute When Submerged
13	Start at Random Position
|example = SetSoundFlag EMTMedicalBeep01 6 1 }} ==Note== *If changing a sound in a persistent REF used in a script, it must be disabled and re-enabled for the changes to take effect ==See Also== *[[GetSoundFlag]] *[[GetSoundTraitNumeric]] *[[SetSoundTraitNumeric]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Sound Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Sound_Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the value of a Sound's numeric trait (determined by traitID). |name = SetSoundTraitNumeric |returnType = traitValue:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = sound |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-12 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = float }} ==Sound Numeric Trait IDs== 
0	Min. Attenuation Distance
1	Max. Attenuation Distance
2	Frequency Adjustment
3	Static Attenuation
4	Ends At (Minutes)
5	Starts At (Minutes)
6	Attenuation Curve 1
7	Attenuation Curve 2
8	Attenuation Curve 3
9	Attenuation Curve 4
10	Attenuation Curve 5
11	Reverb Attenuation Control
12	Priority
|example = SetSoundTraitNumeric EMTMedicalBeep01 3 -4.85 Will set the Static Attenuation of EMTMedicalBeep01 to -4.85. }} ==Note== *Changing a trait will have no effect on sounds currently playing when this command is called, only sounds that begin to play after this command was called with inherit the changes. *If changing a sound in a persistent REF used in a script, it must be disabled and re-enabled for the changes to take effect ==See Also== *[[GetSoundTraitNumeric]] *[[GetSoundFlag]] *[[SetSoundFlag]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Sound Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Sound_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the calling actor's '''Speed Multiplier''' to the specified value and applies the changes on movement animations. If the speedMult argument is omitted, the function applies the changes using the current value. |name = SetSpeedMult |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = speedMult |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = actorRef.SetSpeedMult 125 actorRef.SetSpeedMult }} ==Notes== *Setting the SpeedMult for an actor to a value below 0 seems to stop them from moving entirely. *Changing the SpeedMult [[Stats List|Actor Value]] with this function is save-baked. Consider using "SomeActor.[[GetAV]] SpeedMult" if you need to undo that change in the future. ==See Also== *[[GetAV]] (since SpeedMult is an [[Stats List|Actor Value]]) *[[Movement Settings]] *[[GetRunSpeed]] *[[GetWalkSpeed]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Works exactly as [[SetStage]]. The only difference is that this function will set the specified quest's highest completed stage (i.e. the value returned by [[GetStage]]) to the specified number, even if it is lower than the current stage. |name = SetStageAlt |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = quest |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = stage |Type = int }} |example = SetStageAlt Generic 20 }} ==See Also== *[[SetStage]] [[Category:Quest Functions]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Quest Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets a Texture-Set's flag value (determined by flagID). |name = SetTextureSetFlag |returnType = flagValue:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = textureSet |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagID |Type = int |Value = 0-3 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = 0/1 }} ==Texture-Set Flag IDs== 
0	No Specular Map
1	Parallax
2	Alpha - Blending
3	Alpha - Testing
|example = SetTextureSetFlag LandscapeDirtNV 1 0 }} ==See Also== *[[GetTextureSetFlag]] *[[GetTextureSetTexture]] *[[SetTextureSetTexture]] *[[GetTextureSetTraitNumeric]] *[[SetTextureSetTraitNumeric]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Texture-Set Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

revision.comment: Added FO3 and ButcherPete categories.
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the texture path of the specified mapID of a Texture-Set. |name = SetTextureSetTexture |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = textureSet |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = mapID |Type = int |Value = 0-5 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = texturePath |Type = string }} ==Texture-Set Map IDs== 
0	Diffuse
1	Normal/Gloss
2	Environment Mask
3	Glow/Skin/Hair
4	Height
5	Environment
|example = SetTextureSetTexture 1stPersonSniperRifleTexture 2 "Weapons\2HandRifle\SniperRifle_m.dds" Will set the Environment Mask texture path of 1stPersonSniperRifleTexture to "Weapons\2HandRifle\SniperRifle_m.dds". }} ==See Also== *[[GetTextureSetTexture]] *[[GetTextureSetTraitNumeric]] *[[SetTextureSetTraitNumeric]] *[[GetTextureSetFlag]] *[[SetTextureSetFlag]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Texture-Set Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Texture Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the value of a Texture-Set's numeric trait (determined by traitID). |name = SetTextureSetTraitNumeric |returnType = traitValue:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = textureSet |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-8 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = float }} ==Texture-Set Numeric Trait IDs== 
0	Min. Width
1	Max. Width
2	Min. Height
3	Max. Height
4	Depth
5	Shininess
6	Parallax Scale
7	Parallax Passes
8	Color (RRRGGGBBB)
|example = SetTextureSetTraitNumeric LandscapeDirtNV 3 32 Will set the Max. Height of LandscapeDirtNV to 32. }} ==See Also== *[[GetTextureSetTraitNumeric]] *[[GetTextureSetTexture]] *[[SetTextureSetTexture]] *[[GetTextureSetFlag]] *[[SetTextureSetFlag]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Texture-Set Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Similar to the NVSE [[SetBaseItemValue]] - sets the base value of the specified item. This function, however, also works on ingestible-type items. |name = SetValueAlt |returnType = oldValue:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = baseItem |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = int }} |example = SetValueAlt Stimpak 120 }} ==See Also== *[[GetValueAlt]] *[[GetItemValue]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions (JIP)]] [[Category: Object Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the '''Spell Effect''' of the specified '''Water Type'''. |name = SetWaterFormEffect |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = waterType |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = actorEffect |Type = ref }} |example = SetWaterFormEffect NVCleanWater WaterHeal1Purified }} ==See Also== *[[GetWaterFormEffect]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Water Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the '''Noise Texture''' path of the specified '''Water Type'''. |name = SetWaterNoiseTexture |arguments = {{FunctionArgument..."
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the '''Noise Texture''' path of the specified '''Water Type'''. |name = SetWaterNoiseTexture |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = waterType |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = texturePath |Type = string }} |example = SetWaterNoiseTexture NVCleanWater "Data\Textures\Water\WastelandWaterPotomac.dds" }} ==Notes== *The path is relative to the game's main folder, '''NOT''' to \Data\Textures\. ==See Also== *[[GetWaterNoiseTexture]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Water Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the '''Open Sound''' of the specified '''[[Water Type]]'''. |name = SetWaterSound |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = waterType |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = sound |Type = ref }} |example = SetWaterSound NVCleanWater AMBWaterLakeLP }} ==See Also== *[[GetWaterSound]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Water Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Sound Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets a weapon's detection sound level: 
0	Loud
1	Normal
2	Silent
|name = SetWeaponDetectionSoundLevel |returnType = soundLevel:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = int |Value = 0-2 }} |example = SetWeaponDetectionSoundLevel WeapNVAssaultCarbine 2 }} ==See Also== *[[GetWeaponDetectionSoundLevel]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Detection Functions]] [[Category:Sound Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets a [[weapon]]'s mod form at the specified index. |name = SetWeaponItemMod |returnType = itemMod:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = index |Type = int |Value = 1-3 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newItemMod |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = SetWeaponItemMod WeapNVAssaultCarbine 2 ModNV10mmPistolSilencer Will set the #2 mod item of WeapNVAssaultCarbine to ModNV10mmPistolSilencer. SetWeaponItemMod WeapNVAssaultCarbine 3 Will set the #3 mod item of WeapNVAssaultCarbine to None. }} ==See Also== *[[GetWeaponItemMod]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets a weapon's mod effect ID at the specified index. Mod Effect IDs: 
0	None
1	Increase Weapon Damage
2	Increase Clip Capacity
3	Decrease Spread
4	Decrease Weight
6	Regenerate Ammo
7	Decrease Equip Time
8	Increase Rate of Fire
9	Increase Projectile Speed
10	Increase Max. Condition
11	Silence
12	Split Beam
13	VATS Bonus
14	Increase Zoom (Scope)
|name = SetWeaponItemModEffect |returnType = effectID:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = index |Type = int |Value = 1-3 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newEffectID |Type = int |Value = 0-14 }} |example = SetWeaponItemModEffect WeapNVAssaultCarbine 2 3 Will set the Assault Carbine's #2 mod effect to Decrease Spread. }} ==See Also== *[[GetWeaponItemModEffect]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets a weapon's mod effect value at the specified index. |name = SetWeaponItemModValue |returnType = effectValue:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = index |Type = int |Value = 1-3 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newEffectValue |Type = float }} |example = SetWeaponItemModValue WeapNVAssaultCarbine 2 6.0 Will set the Assault Carbine's #2 mod effect value to 6.0. }} ==See Also== *[[GetWeaponItemModValue1]] *[[GetWeaponItemModValue2]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the override [http://fose.silverlock.org/fose_command_doc.html#Reload_Animations reload animation] for weapon-mods (in the Mod Info tab) of the passed weapon. |name = SetWeaponModReloadAnim |returnType = reloadAnim:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newAnim |Type = int }} |example = SetWeaponModReloadAnim WeapNVAssaultCarbine 8 }} ==See Also== *[[GetWeaponModReloadAnim]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Animation Functions]]

revision.comment: add missing categories
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the bit-mask representing the calling weapon's currently installed weapon-mods. Unlike [[SetEquippedWeaponModFlags]], this function is called on an inventory reference. The calling weapon reference does not have to be equipped. |name = SetWeaponRefModFlags |returnType = success:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = newFlags |Type = int }} ==Weapon Mod Flags== 
1	Weapon Mod #1
2	Weapon Mod #2
4	Weapon Mod #3
|example = rItemRef.SetWeaponRefModFlags 5 This will enable mods #1 and #3 (1 + 4). 
rWorldRef.SetWeaponModFlags 1
This is short syntax added in [[JIP PP LN]] }} ==Notes== *Setting the flags to 0 on a weapon without any possible mods results in a bugged display. Unverified *Note that using this function to apply a durability altering weapon mod will not set the weapon's durability correctly afterwards. Unverified *[[JIP PP LN]] allows this function to be used on weapons placed in the world. Previously this function only worked on inventory references. *If you're looking for a compatible general use case function, consider using [[AttachMod]] instead, as it supports both the Vanilla Item Mod system, as well as the [[Extended Item Mods System]] introduced in [[VanillaPlugins+|Plugins+]] ==See Also== *[[AttachMod]] *[[GetWeaponRefModFlags]] *[[GetInvRefsForItem]] *[[GetFirstRefForItem]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Reference Functions]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the Shell Casing model path of the weapon. |name = SetWeaponShellCasingModel |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = modelPath |Type = string }} |example = SetWeaponShellCasingModel WeapNVAssaultCarbine "weapons\2handautomatic\assaultrifleshellcasing.nif" }} ==See Also== *[[GetWeaponShellCasingModel]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets a weapon's sound, determined by soundID: 
0	Attack Sound 3D
1	Attack Sound Dist.
2	Attack Sound 2D
3	Attack Loop
4	Attack Fail
5	Melee Block
6	Idle Sound
7	Equip Sound
8	Unequip Sound
9	Attack Sound 3D (Mod)
10	Attack Sound Dist. (Mod)
11	Attack Sound 2D (Mod)
12	Pickup Sound
13	Putdown Sound
|name = SetWeaponSound |returnType = sound:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = soundID |Type = int |Value = 0-13 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newSound |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = SetWeaponSound WeapNVAssaultCarbine 2 WPN10mmSMGFire2D Will set the Attack Sound 2D of WeapNVAssaultCarbine to WPN10mmSMGFire2D. SetWeaponSound WeapNVAssaultCarbine 7 Will set the Equip Sound of WeapNVAssaultCarbine to None. }} ==See Also== *[[GetWeaponSound]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Sound Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets a weather's Image Space Modifier at the specified timeID. |name = SetWeatherImageSpaceMod |returnType = imageSpaceMod:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weather |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = timeID |Type = int |Value = 0-5 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newImageSpaceMod |Type = ref |Optional = y }} ==Weather Time IDs== 
0	Sunrise
1	Day
2	Sunset
3	Night
4	High Noon
5	Midnight
|example = SetWeatherImageSpaceMod NVHooverDamWeather 4 NVJacobstownIS Will set the Image Space Modifier of NVHooverDamWeather, at High Noon, to NVJacobstownIS. SetWeatherImageSpaceMod NVHooverDamWeather 2 Will set the Image Space Modifier of NVHooverDamWeather, at Sunset, to None. }} ==See Also== *[[GetWeatherImageSpaceMod]] *[[GetWeatherTexture]] *[[SetWeatherTexture]] *[[GetWeatherPrecipitationModel]] *[[SetWeatherPrecipitationModel]] *[[GetWeatherTraitNumeric]] *[[SetWeatherTraitNumeric]] *[[GetWeatherRGBColor]] *[[SetWeatherRGBColor]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Weather Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weather Functions]] [[Category:Imagespace Functions]] [[Category:Imagespace Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the Precipitation model path of a weather. |name = SetWeatherPrecipitationModel |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weather |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = modelPath |Type = string }} |example = SetWeatherPrecipitationModel NVHooverDamWeather "Weather\NVRain.nif" }} ==See Also== *[[GetWeatherPrecipitationModel]] *[[GetWeatherImageSpaceMod]] *[[SetWeatherImageSpaceMod]] *[[GetWeatherTexture]] *[[SetWeatherTexture]] *[[GetWeatherTraitNumeric]] *[[SetWeatherTraitNumeric]] *[[GetWeatherRGBColor]] *[[SetWeatherRGBColor]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Weather Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weather Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the weather's RGB color of the specified typeID, at the specified timeID. |name = SetWeatherRGBColor |returnType = color:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weather |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = typeID |Type = int |Value = 0-9 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = timeID |Type = int |Value = 0-5 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newColor |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = cloudLayer |Type = int |Value = 0-3 |Optional = y }} ==Weather Type IDs== 
0	Sky-Upper
1	Fog
2	Clouds-Lower
3	Ambient
4	Sunlight
5	Sun
6	Stars
7	Sky-Lower
8	Horizon
9	Clouds-Upper
==Weather Time IDs== 
0	Sunrise
1	Day
2	Sunset
3	Night
4	High Noon
5	Midnight
|example = SetWeatherRGBColor NVHooverDamWeather 3 4 128032224 Will set the Ambient color, at High Noon, of NVHooverDamWeather, to R:128, G:32, B:224. }} ==See Also== *[[GetWeatherRGBColor]] *[[GetWeatherImageSpaceMod]] *[[SetWeatherImageSpaceMod]] *[[GetWeatherTexture]] *[[SetWeatherTexture]] *[[GetWeatherPrecipitationModel]] *[[SetWeatherPrecipitationModel]] *[[GetWeatherTraitNumeric]] *[[SetWeatherTraitNumeric]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Weather Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weather Functions]]

revision.comment: Bulleted notes section.
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the texture path of the specified layer of a weather. |name = SetWeatherTexture |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weather |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = layerIndex |Type = int |Value = 0-3 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = texturePath |Type = string }} |example = SetWeatherTexture NVHooverDamWeather 2 "sky\NVCloudlight.dds" }} ==Notes== *If the weather affected is currently loaded, changes will not be visible until the weather is reloaded, either by entering an interior cell or changing the currently loaded weather to a different weather. ==See Also== *[[GetWeatherTexture]] *[[GetWeatherImageSpaceMod]] *[[SetWeatherImageSpaceMod]] *[[GetWeatherPrecipitationModel]] *[[SetWeatherPrecipitationModel]] *[[GetWeatherTraitNumeric]] *[[SetWeatherTraitNumeric]] *[[GetWeatherRGBColor]] *[[SetWeatherRGBColor]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Weather Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weather Functions]] [[Category:Texture Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the value of a weather's numeric trait (determined by traitID). |name = SetWeatherTraitNumeric |returnType = traitValue:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weather |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-20 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = float }} ==Weather Numeric Trait IDs== 
0	Layer 0 Cloud Speed
1	Layer 1 Cloud Speed
2	Layer 2 Cloud Speed
3	Layer 3 Cloud Speed
4	Wind Speed
5	Trans Delta
6	Sun Glare
7	Sun Damage
8	Precipitation Begin Fade In
9	Precipitation End Fade Out
10	Lightning Begin Fade In
11	Lightning End Fade Out
12	Lightning Frequency
13	Weather Classification
14	Lightning Color (RRRGGGBBB)
15	Fog Distance Day Near
16	Fog Distance Day Far
17	Fog Distance Night Near
18	Fog Distance Night Far
19	Fog Distance Day Power
20	Fog Distance Night Power
Weather Classifications: 
0	None
1	Pleasant
2	Cloudy
4	Rainy
8	Snow
|example = SetWeatherTraitNumeric NVHooverDamWeather 4 0.75 Will set the Wind Speed of NVHooverDamWeather to 0.75. }} ==See Also== *[[GetWeatherTraitNumeric]] *[[GetWeatherImageSpaceMod]] *[[SetWeatherImageSpaceMod]] *[[GetWeatherTexture]] *[[SetWeatherTexture]] *[[GetWeatherPrecipitationModel]] *[[SetWeatherPrecipitationModel]] *[[GetWeatherRGBColor]] *[[SetWeatherRGBColor]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Weather Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weather Functions]]

revision.comment: Removed empty see also section.
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the amount of time (in real-time seconds) it takes for the game's '''[[Weather]]''' to make a full transition to a new Weather type. This overrides the Trans Delta setting of all Weather types (which effectively limits the transition time to 30 seconds max), allowing for smoother, more gradual transitions. Calling this function with no arguments (or with 0) removes the override. |name = SetWeatherTransitionTimeOverride |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = transTime |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = 
SetWeatherTransitionTimeOverride 90
Sets the transition time of all Weathers to 90 seconds. 
SetWeatherTransitionTimeOverride
Removes the Weather transition time override. }} [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Weather Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weather Functions]] [[Category:Time Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the Climate of the specified World Space. |name = SetWorldspaceClimate |returnType = climate:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = worldSpace |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newClimate |Type = ref }} |example = SetWorldspaceClimate FreesideWorld NVDLC02ZionValleyClimate }} ==See Also== *[[GetWorldspaceClimate]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:World Space Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Climate Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:World_Space_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets/removes the specified flag (determined by flagID) for the specified Worldspace. |name = SetWorldspaceFlag |returnType = flagValue:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = worldspace |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagID |Type = int |Value = 0-7 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = 0/1 }} ==Worldspace Flag IDs== 
0	Small World
1	Can't Fast-Travel
4	No LOD Water
5	No LOD Noise
6	Disallow NPC Fall Damage
7	Needs Water Adjustment
|example = SetWorldspaceFlag FreesideWorld 1 1 }} ==See Also== *[[GetWorldspaceFlag]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Flag Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Opens a text input message box, enabling typing in text (using the keyboard, obviously). The function takes four arguments: *'''callbackScript''' - A '''[[UDF|UDF Script]]''' that shall be invoked once the OK button is pressed and the message box is closed. The script is passed with a single argument - a '''[[string_var]]''' containing the typed in text (see example script, below). *'''msgBoxWidth''' - The pixel '''width''' of the message box. *'''msgBoxHeight''' - The pixel '''height''' of the message box. *'''msgBoxTitle''' - A '''[[String Formatting|formatted string]]''' that shall be used as the title text of the message box, displayed at its top. |name = ShowTextInputMenu |returnType = success:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = callbackScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = msgBoxWidth |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = msgBoxHeight |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = msgBoxTitle |Type = formattedString }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = formatVars(up to 20) |Optional = y }} |example = ShowTextInputMenu TextInputUDF 700 500 "Please type in text below:" }} ==Callback Script== A skeleton script for a callback: 
scn	TextInputUDF

string_var	sTextInput

begin Function {sTextInput}

	(code)

	sv_Destruct sTextInput

end
==Notes== *By default, the color of the input text is white, but it can be changed by using the following code after calling the menu: 
SetUIFloatAlt "TextEditMenu/NOGLOW_BRANCH/TEM_TextInputBox/TEM_InputText/red" iRed
SetUIFloatAlt "TextEditMenu/NOGLOW_BRANCH/TEM_TextInputBox/TEM_InputText/green" iGreen
SetUIFloatAlt "TextEditMenu/NOGLOW_BRANCH/TEM_TextInputBox/TEM_InputText/blue" iBlue
==See Also== *[[SetTextInputExtendedProps]] *[[SetTextInputString]] *[[ShowQuantityMenu]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Message Functions]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:String Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Stops any '''Idle Animation''' currently playing on the calling actor. |name = StopIdle |referenceType = reference |example = actorRef.StopIdle }} ==See Also== *[[PlayIdle]] *[[PlayIdleEx]] *[[RefreshIdle]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Animation Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]]

revision.comment: Removed See Also. Three functions listed are linked in the summary.
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Rebuilds a reference from a string generated by [[RefToString]] or [[GetFormIDString]], or [[GetRawFormIDString]]. |name = StringToRef |returnType = form:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = refString |Type = string }} |example = StringToRef $strVar }} ==Notes== *The function will first resolve the mod index from the string, therefore changes in the load-order will not invalidate the reference ID.
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Utility Functions (JIP)]] [[Category: String Functions]] [[Category: ID Functions]]

revision.comment: https
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns whether the specified '''[[string_var]]''' matches the specified [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Regular_expression Regular Expression] pattern. |name = sv_RegexMatch |returnType = match:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = targetSeq |Type = string_var }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = regEx |Type = formattedString }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = formatVars(up to 20) |Optional = y }} |example = 
let sTargetSeq := "Whatchamacallit"
let bMatch := sv_RegexMatch sTargetSeq "(What)(.*)"
In the above example, sTargetSeq does begin with the sequence "What", therefore bMatch equals 1. }} ==Notes== *regEx supports [[String Formatting|string formatting]], and up to 20 variables may be used. *Will crash the game if the Regex pattern is invalidly formatted. If it cannot be verified before the script runs, use [[ValidateRegex]]. ==See Also== *[[sv_RegexSearch]] *[[sv_RegexReplace]] *To test Regex expressions, consider using this website: [https://regex101.com/ Regex101] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:String Functions (JIP)]]

revision.comment: https
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns a copy of the specified '''string_var''', with all matches to the specified [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Regular_expression Regular Expression] pattern replaced by the specified string. The replacement and pattern are both passed as a single [[String Formatting|formatted string]], separated by a '''|''', i.e. '''replacement|pattern''' (see example, below). |name = sv_RegexReplace |returnType = modifiedSeq:string_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = targetSeq |Type = string_var }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = replacement|pattern |Type = formattedString }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = formatVars(up to 20) |Optional = y }} |example = 
let sTargetSeq := "This subject has a submarine as a subsequence"
let sModifiedSeq := sv_RegexReplace sTargetSeq "blip!|\b(sub)([^ ]*)"
In the above example, every whole word in sTargetSeq that begins with the sequence "sub" is replaced by "blip!". The result sModifiedSeq is assigned the string "This blip! has a blip! as a blip!". }} ==Notes== *Will crash the game if the Regex pattern is invalidly formatted. If it cannot be verified before the script runs, use [[ValidateRegex]]. ==See Also== *[[Sv_Replace]] *[[sv_RegexSearch]] *[[sv_RegexMatch]] *To test Regex expressions, consider using this website: [https://regex101.com/ Regex101] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:String Functions (JIP)]]

revision.comment: https
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an '''[[array_var]]''' containing all matches in the specified '''[[string_var]]''' to the specified [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Regular_expression Regular Expression] pattern. |name = sv_RegexSearch |returnType = matches:array_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = targetSeq |Type = string_var }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = regEx |Type = formattedString }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = formatVars(up to 20) |Optional = y }} |example = 
let sTargetSeq := "This subject has a submarine as a subsequence"
let aMatches := sv_RegexSearch sTargetSeq "\b(sub)([^ ]*)"
In the above example, aMatches will contain every whole word in sTargetSeq that begins with the sequence "sub" ("subject", "submarine" and "subsequence"). }} ==Notes== *regEx supports [[String Formatting|string formatting]], and up to 20 variables may be used. *Will crash the game if the Regex pattern is invalidly formatted. If it cannot be verified before the script runs, use [[ValidateRegex]]. ==See Also== *[[sv_Find]] *[[sv_RegexMatch]] *[[sv_RegexReplace]] *To test Regex expressions, consider using this website: [https://regex101.com/ Regex101] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:String Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

revision.comment: Removed empty 'see also' section
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Similar to the vanilla function [[SwapTextureOnRef]] - swaps a texture used by a particular '''Nif Block''' on the specified object reference. However, unlike the vanilla version, which only allows swapping the main texture (diffuse map), this function allows swapping any of the six possible texture layers. This function takes the same parameters as the vanilla version, with one extra parameter for specifying the texture slot index (0-5). Both versions are otherwise functionally identical. |name = SwapTextureEx |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = objectRef |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = nifBlockName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = texturePath |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = texSlotIndex |Type = int |Value = 0-5 }} |example = SwapTextureEx objectRef "SodaBottle01:0" "clutter\junk\SodaBottleClosed01_n" 1 }} ==Notes== *Please also refer to the documentation of [[SwapTextureOnRef]]. [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Toggles a model within a [[creature]]'s model-list ON or OFF. |name = ToggleCreatureModel |returnType = success:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = modelPath |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = enableModel |Type = 0/1 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = RexRef.ToggleCreatureModel "eyessetvicious.nif" 1
ToggleCreatureModel "eyessetvicious.nif" 1 Rex }} ==Notes== *The model path must be relative to the creature's model folder and include the ".nif" extension. *When called on a reference which is currently being rendered, the reference must be disabled and then re-enabled (alternatively, [[Update3D]] can be called on the reference) before the change is visible. ==See Also== *[[CreatureHasModel]] *[[GetCreatureModels]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Creature Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Toggles the player's POV to 1st or 3rd person. Passing 1 enables 1st person view, whereas 0 enables 3rd person view. |name = ToggleFirstPerson |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = firstPerson |Type = 0/1 }} |example = ToggleFirstPerson 1 }} ==See Also== *[[IsPC1stPerson]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Disables/enables '''Collision''' geometry (if any) on the calling object reference. |name = ToggleObjectCollision |alias = TOC |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = toggleState |Type = 0/1 }} |example = objectRef.ToggleObjectCollision 0 }} ==Notes== *Can remove a Static's shader effects, such as ones added via [[PlayMagicShaderVisuals]].
==See Also== *[[ToggleCollision|ToggleCollision (TCL)]] *[[Con_TCL]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = The calling actor will turn (playing the appropriate animation) for the specified degrees to the right (deg. > 0), or to the left (deg. < 0). |name = Turn |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = turnDegrees |Type = float }} |example = 
actorRef.Turn 30
actorRef will turn 30 deg. to the right. 
actorRef.Turn -112
actorRef will turn 112 deg. to the left. }} ==See Also== *[[FaceObject]] *[[IsTurning]] *[[SetAngle]] *[[SetAngleEx]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Movement Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Similar to the vanilla [[UnequipItem]] - forces the calling actor to unequip the specified item. However, unlike UnequipItem, this function will also trigger the '''[[OnUnequip]]''' script event (where applicable). |name = UnequipItemAlt |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = item |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = noEquip |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = hideMessage |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }} |example = player.UnequipItemAlt WeapLaserRifle 0 1 }} =='''Parameters'''== *If NoEquipFlag = 1, actor (including the player) will be unable to equip the item. Once the item is equipped with the [[EquipItem]] function, the item can be equipped and unequipped as normal. *If HideEquipMessage = 1, the item unequipped message will be hidden from the player. =='''Notes'''== *The ''NoEquipItem'' flag is effectively ignored by Companions. Calling "UnequipItemAlt 1" on a Companion will ''not'' prevent them from re-equipping the item if it is the best equipment available. *If UnequipItemAlt is called on the player while they're viewing their inventory, whether or not the visual "equipped" indicator is updated immediately depends on the HideEquipMessage flag. If it's set to 0, the graphic will update immediately, but if it's set to 1 the graphic won't update until another graphic updates. Running the following code will prevent this bug: UnequipItemAlt ObjectID 0 1 AddItem ObjectID 1 1 RemoveItem ObjectID 1 1 ==See Also== *[[DropAlt]] *[[EquipItemAlt]] *[[UnequipItems]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions (JIP)]] [[Category: Object Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Attempts to resolve a mod index which was stored during a previous game session to account for any changes to the user's load order. Given the stored mod index for a mod, returns the mod index currently associated with that same mod, or -1 if the index could not be resolved (for instance, because the mod is no longer loaded). |name = ValidateModIndex |returnType = validatedIndex:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = modIndex |Type = int }} |example = 
Suppose ExampleMod.esp was at index 6 at the time the current save was made, but has since been moved, and its index is now 9. Loading the save and calling:
 
ValidateModIndex 6
 
with 6 being ExampleMod.esp last index, will return 9 - its current index.
}} ==See Also== *[[GetLoadOrderChanged]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Utility Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Similar to [[ReadArrayFromFile]], only this function writes data from an [[array variable]] into a file. *If the file does not exist, it is created. *If append is non-zero, the new data is appended to any existing data in the file, otherwise all existing data is erased and overwritten. *In cases where the passed array is '''a matrix''' (2D array), each sub-array is treated, and is written into the file as '''a column''', '''unless''' the 4th, optional argument, '''transpose''', is non-zero, in which case each sub-array is treated and written as '''a row'''. *Only the values from the array are written; keys are ignored. |name = WriteArrayToFile |returnType = successful:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = filePath |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = append |Type = 0/1 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = sourceArray |Type = array_var }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = transpose |Type = 1/0 |Optional = y }} |example = WriteArrayToFile "data_file.txt" 1 arrVar }} ==Notes== *filePath is relative to the game's installation path. ==See Also== *[[ReadArrayFromFile]] *[[ReadStringFromFile]] *[[WriteStringToFile]] *[[FileExists]] *[[pReadFile]] *[[pWriteFile]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Utility Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:Debugging]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Similar to [[ReadStringFromFile]], only this function writes a string into a file. *If the file does not exist, it is created. *If append is non-zero, the new string is appended at the end of the file, otherwise '''ALL''' existing data is erased and overwritten. |name = WriteStringToFile |returnType = successful:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = filePath |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = append |Type = 0/1 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = sourceString |Type = formattedString }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = formatVars(up to 20) |Optional = y }} |example = WriteStringToFile "data_file.txt" 1 "%z" strVar WriteStringToFile "data_file.txt" 1 "Two times four is %g and no more.%rIts name is %n" intVar refVar }} ==Notes== *filePath is relative to the game's installation path. *sourceString supports format specifiers, and up to 20 variables may be used. ==See Also== *[[GetStringHash]] *[[ReadStringFromFile]] *[[ReadArrayFromFile]] *[[WriteArrayToFile]] *[[FileExists]] *[[pWriteFileAlt]] *[[pWriteFile]] *[[PrintF]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Utility Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:File Functions]]

revision.comment: /* Properties */
revision.text:
The Auxiliary-Variables interface is a utility added by the [https://www.nexusmods.com/newvegas/mods/58277 JIP LN NVSE Plugin] in Fallout: New Vegas and [https://www.nexusmods.com/fallout3/mods/23682 Command Extender] in Fallout 3, and is an alternative method for dynamically storing and managing game-data. Auxiliary-Variables (abbreviated as AuxVars) are a special type of variables that, unlike generic script variables, exist in the game's memory and are not part of, and are not pre-declared in any game-script. AuxVars are created, accessed, modified and deleted using a set of script commands. ==Properties== *An AuxVar is defined by a Name (string, case-insensitive, limit of 79 characters including any prefix) and an Object (object reference/base form) that "owns" it. This Object:Name pair must, therefore, be unique. Note that different Objects may "own" a variable with the same Name and still count as a unique pair. Any Object may "own" any number of variables. *AuxVars can store either numeric, reference/form, or string values. They have no explicit, fixed value-type - their type changes dynamically, according to the type of value assigned to them. *Every AuxVar is an array by default, and can be used for storing either a single, or multiple values (of mixed types). *AuxVar functions '''REQUIRE passing a form''' so that the value can be "owned". ==Duration & Accessibility== '''Duration:''' An AuxVar's duration setting determines its "life-span", i.e. whether it is permanent or temporary. Permanent AuxVars, and any values they store, are saved with the game. In contrast, temporary AuxVars are not saved with the game. Instead, they are persistent (retain their values) for the duration of the current game session (much like constant global variables) but are discarded when the game is exited. Consider using [[File Functions|file functions]] such as [[SetINIFloat]] to save information across multiple sessions. '''Accessibility:''' The accessibility setting determines which mods can "see" and manipulate the AuxVar. A private AuxVar is "private" to the mod it was created by, and can only be accessed/modified by scripts originating in that mod. This allows different mods to use the same variable names, associated with the same objects, without overwriting each other. A public AuxVar, on the other hand, can be accessed/modified by any mod. This is an effective method for inter-mod communication and exchange of data, without any master-dependencies. Enabling each property is done by adding a special prefix to the AuxVar's name. The two properties, and their combinations, define four classes of AuxVars, as summarized in the following table: {|class="wikitable centercells" |- !Class !Prefix !Example !Saved w/ Game !Persistent in Session !Accessible By |- |Temporary-Public |*_ |"*_VarName" |No |Yes |Any mod |- |Permanent-Public |_ |"_VarName" |Yes |No |Any mod |- |Temporary-Private |* |"*VarName" |No |Yes |Creating mod |- |Permanent-Private |None |"VarName" |Yes |No |Creating mod |} '''Caveats:''' {{Template:ModIndexOverride}} ==Examples== Suppose we want to save if an actor has hit the player, and be able to request this at a later point.
We can use the '''"HasHitPlayer"''' permanent-private AuxVar name as part of the "key" required to get this information; the other "key" being the requested actor. Then we can use the following code in an [[OnHit]] [[UDF]] to set a value on the attacking actor, using [[AuxiliaryVariableSetFloat]]: 
ref rAttacker
;* ... other UDF code, need to initialize rAttacker

if (rAttacker == player)
   rAttacker.AuxiliaryVariableSetFloat "HasHitPlayer" 1
endif
We may later request the value using [[AuxiliaryVariableGetFloat]]: 
ref rSomeReference
;* ... other code, need to initialize rSomeReference

int hasHit = rSomeReference.AuxiliaryVariableGetFloat "HasHitPlayer"
if (hasHit)
   ;* Do stuff
endif
The above code works as expected even if no value was previously attributed to the calling reference, because [[AuxiliaryVariableGetFloat]] returns 0 if no value was attributed to that form with that AuxVar key. ==See Also== *[[RefMap Array]] (a specialized form of Auxiliary Variables) [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Variables]] [[Category:Aux Functions]]

The Reference-Map array interface is a utility added by the [http://www.nexusmods.com/newvegas/mods/58277 JIP LN NVSE Plugin]. A RefMap is a special type of array variable, where the <u>keys are [[Ref Variable|ref]]s</u> (object [[reference]]s/[[base form]]s). This is intended for cases where it is necessary/more convenient to use [[Ref Variable|ref]]-type (instead of numeric- or [[string]]-type) values as keys - which is not possible when using the NVSE [[Array Variable|array_var]] interface.
(Note that RefMap variables are not related, in any way, to [[array_var]]s, and can only be manipulated using the [[RefMap Array Functions (JIP)|RefMap set of script commands]])
==Properties== A RefMap variable is not an ordinary script variable - it is a special case of an [[Auxiliary Variables|Auxiliary-Variable]], and has the same general properties (see article), with two main differences: *A RefMap is not "owned" by any Object - it is defined by a Name only, which must be unique. *The Key of each element in a RefMap array is (obviously) a ref, instead of a numeric index. Internally, the elements in a RefMap are ordered by FormID (ascending).
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Variables]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the value of a numeric trait (determined by traitID) of the specified '''Imagespace''' form. |name = GetImageSpaceTrait |returnType = traitValue:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = imageSpace |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-32 }} ==Imagespace Trait IDs==

1	HDR: Blur Radius
2	HDR: Blur Passes

4	HDR: Target LUM
5	HDR: Upper LUM Clamp
6	HDR: Bright Scale
7	HDR: Bright Clamp

10	HDR: LUM Ramp Max

20	Get Hit: Damping Contrast

22	Night Eye: Tint: Green
23	Night Eye: Tint: Blue
24	Night Eye: Brightness

26	Cinematic: Contrast: Avg Lum Value

28	Cinematic: Brightness: Value

32	Cinematic: Tint: Value
|example = set fBlurRadius to GetImageSpaceTrait HazmatImageSpace 15 }} ==See Also== *[[SetImageSpaceTrait]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Imagespace Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Imagespace Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''UDF Script''' as a handler that will be invoked in the event the specified [[DirectX Scancode|KeyID]] is pressed. When invoked, the script is passed with a single argument: the pressed Key ID. |name = SetOnKeyDownEventHandler |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = keyID |Type = int }} |example = 
SetOnKeyDownEventHandler OnKeyDownUDF 1 14
'''Sets''' the OnKeyDownUDF script as a handler to be invoked whenever '''Backspace''' is pressed. 
SetOnKeyDownEventHandler OnKeyDownUDF 0 14
'''Removes''' the OnKeyDownUDF script as a handler of the above. }} ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn	OnKeyDownUDF

int	iKeyID

begin Function {iKeyID}

(code)

end
==Notes== *This event still runs even if the key has been disabled using [[DisableKey]]. ==See Also== *[[SetOnKeyUpEventHandler]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JIP)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''UDF Script''' as a handler that will be invoked in the event the specified [[DirectX Scancode|KeyID]] is released. When invoked, the script is passed with a single argument: the released Key ID. |name = SetOnKeyUpEventHandler |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = keyID |Type = int }} |example = 
SetOnKeyUpEventHandler OnKeyUpUDF 1 14
'''Sets''' the OnKeyUpUDF script as a handler to be invoked whenever '''Backspace''' is released. 
SetOnKeyUpEventHandler OnKeyUpUDF 0 14
'''Removes''' the OnKeyUpUDF script as a handler of the above. }} ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn	OnKeyUpUDF

int	iKeyID

begin Function {iKeyID}

(code)

end
==See Also== *[[SetOnKeyDownEventHandler]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JIP)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''[[UDF|UDF Script]]''' as a handler that will be invoked in the event the specified [http://fose.silverlock.org/fose_command_doc.html#ControlCodes '''Control ID'''] is pressed. When invoked, the script is passed with a single argument: the pressed Control ID. |name = SetOnControlDownEventHandler |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = controlID |Type = int }} |example = 
SetOnControlDownEventHandler OnControlDownUDF 1 4
'''Sets''' the OnControlDownUDF script as a handler to be invoked whenever the '''Attack''' control is pressed. 
SetOnControlDownEventHandler OnControlDownUDF 0 4
'''Removes''' the OnControlDownUDF script as a handler for the above. }} ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn	OnControlDownUDF

int	iControlID

begin Function {iControlID}

(code)

end
==See Also== *[[SetOnControlUpEventHandler]] *[[OnControlUp]] / [[OnControlDown]] (uses the [[NVSE]] event handling system) [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JIP)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''[[UDF|UDF Script]]''' as a handler that will be invoked in the event the specified [http://fose.silverlock.org/fose_command_doc.html#ControlCodes '''Control ID'''] is released. When invoked, the script is passed with a single argument: the released Control ID. |name = SetOnControlUpEventHandler |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = controlID |Type = int }} |example = 
SetOnControlUpEventHandler OnControlUpUDF 1 4
'''Sets''' the OnControlUpUDF script as a handler to be invoked whenever the '''Attack''' control is released. 
SetOnControlUpEventHandler OnControlUpUDF 0 4
'''Removes''' the OnControlUpUDF script as a handler for the above. }} ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn	OnControlUpUDF

int	iControlID

begin Function {iControlID}

(code)

end
==See Also== *[[SetOnControlDownEventHandler]] *[[OnControlUp]] / [[OnControlDown]] (uses the [[NVSE]] event handling system) [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JIP)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

revision.comment: Add SGMLC alias
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Registers/removes the specified '''UDF Script''' as a callback that will be repeatedly and independently called by the game, with the specified delay between calls. The function takes four arguments: *1. '''callbackScript''' - A UDF script. The script should take no arguments (empty braces {}). *2. '''setORremove''' - (1) Register, or (0) un-register the callback. *3. '''callDelay''' (optional) - The delay time between calls, in '''frames'''. The default is 1 (every frame). *4. '''modeFlag''' (optional) - A bitmask; enables to restrict the calls to a specific mode, etc. The default is 3. 
1	Run on GameMode
2	Run on MenuMode
8	Auto-remove on load game/main menu
If the function is '''called on a reference''' (implicitly/explicitly), the UDF script, whenever called, will be called on that '''same reference''' (passed as "this"). Otherwise, it will be called on '''the player''' (see examples, below). |name = SetGameMainLoopCallback |alias = SGMLC |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = callbackScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = callDelay |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = modeFlag |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = 
CraigBooneRef.SetGameMainLoopCallback MyCallbackUDF 1 30 9
Registers the MyCallbackUDF script to be called every '''half a second''' on '''CraigBooneRef''', while in '''GameMode''' only. The callback will be auto-removed when loading a game/exiting to the main menu. 
CraigBooneRef.SetGameMainLoopCallback MyCallbackUDF 0
Un-registers the MyCallbackUDF script as a callback. 
SetGameMainLoopCallback MyCallbackUDF 1
Registers the MyCallbackUDF script to be called '''every frame''' on '''the Player''', while in either '''GameMode''' or '''MenuMode'''. 
SetGameMainLoopCallback MyCallbackUDF 0
Un-registers the MyCallbackUDF script as a callback. }} ==Callback Script== A skeleton script for a callback: 
scn	MyCallbackUDF

ref	rCallingRef

begin Function {}

	set rCallingRef to GetSelfAlt  ; Can also use GetSelf, but it won't work on non-persistent items

	(code)

end
==See Also== *[[Auxiliary Timers]] may be used for CallingRef-specific timers. *Script-type [[Base Effect]]s can serve a similar role once [[CastImmediate|casted]] on an Actor. *[[CallWhile]] *[[CallLoop]] *[[CallAfterSeconds]] *[[CallForSeconds]] *[[CallFunctionNextFrame]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JIP)]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the value stored in the specified '''[[Global]]''' variable as a '''[[reference]]''' (rather than a float/int). A reference must previously been stored in the global, using [[SetGlobalRef]]. |name = GetGlobalRef |returnType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = global |Type = ref }} |example = set rRef to GetGlobalRef SomeGlobalVar }} ==See Also== *[[SetGlobalRef]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Global Variable Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Stores a '''[[reference]]'''-type value in a '''[[Global]]''' variable (which normally would accept only numeric values). In order to retrieve a reference from a global, [[GetGlobalRef]] must be used. |name = SetGlobalRef |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = global |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = anyForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = ObjectRef.SetGlobalRef SomeGlobalVar
SetGlobalRef SomeGlobalVar SomeRefOrForm }} ==See Also== *[[GetGlobalRef]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Global Variable Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]]

revision.comment: Jazzisparis moved page [[World Space Functions (JIP)]] to [[Category:World Space Functions (JIP)]]
revision.text: {{Bc|Functions (JIP)}} [[Category:Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the '''Water Noise Texture''' path of the specified '''World Space'''. |name = GetWorldspaceNoiseTexture |returnType = texturePath:string_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = worldSpace |Type = ref }} |example = let sNoiseTex := GetWorldspaceNoiseTexture FreesideWorld }} ==See Also== *[[SetWorldspaceNoiseTexture]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:World Space Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:World_Space_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the '''Water Noise Texture''' path of the specified '''World Space'''. |name = SetWorldspaceNoiseTexture |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = worldSpace |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = texturePath |Type = string }} |example = SetWorldspaceNoiseTexture FreesideWorld "Data\Textures\Water\WastelandWaterPotomac.dds" }} ==Notes== *The path is relative to the game's main folder, '''NOT''' to \Data\Textures\. ==See Also== *[[GetWorldspaceNoiseTexture]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:World Space Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:World_Space_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the '''Water''' type form of the specified '''World Space'''. |name = GetWorldspaceWaterType |returnType = waterType:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = worldSpace |Type = ref }} |example = set rWaterType to GetWorldspaceWaterType FreesideWorld }} ==See Also== *[[SetWorldspaceWaterType]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Water Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:World Space Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:World_Space_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the '''Water''' type form of the specified '''World Space'''. |name = SetWorldspaceWaterType |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = worldSpace |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = waterType |Type = ref }} |example = SetWorldspaceWaterType FreesideWorld RadioactiveWater }} ==See Also== *[[GetWorldspaceWaterType]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Water Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:World Space Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:World_Space_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the '''[[Image Space]]''' of the specified '''[[World Space]]'''. |name = GetWorldspaceImagespace |returnType = imageSpace:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = worldSpace |Type = ref }} |example = set rImageSpace to GetWorldspaceImagespace FreesideWorld }} ==See Also== *[[SetWorldspaceImagespace]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:World Space Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:World_Space_Functions]] [[Category:Imagespace Functions]] [[Category:Imagespace Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the '''[[Image Space]]''' of the specified '''[[World Space]]'''. |name = SetWorldspaceImagespace |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = worldSpace |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = imageSpace |Type = ref }} |example = SetWorldspaceImagespace FreesideWorld AtomicWranglerImageSpace }} ==See Also== *[[GetWorldspaceImagespace]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:World Space Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:World_Space_Functions]] [[Category:Imagespace Functions]] [[Category:Imagespace Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the '''Water''' type of the specified '''Cell'''. |name = SetCellWaterForm |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = cell |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = waterForm |Type = ref }} |example = SetCellWaterForm Gomorrah00 NVMurkyWater }} ==See Also== *[[GetCellWaterForm]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Cell Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Cell Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the '''Climate''' of the specified '''Cell'''. |name = GetCellClimate |returnType = climate:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = cell |Type = ref }} |example = set rClimate to GetCellClimate Gomorrah00 }} ==See Also== *[[SetCellClimate]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Cell Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Cell Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the '''Climate''' of the specified '''Cell'''. |name = SetCellClimate |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = cell |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = climate |Type = ref }} |example = SetCellClimate Gomorrah00 NVInteriorClimate }} ==See Also== *[[GetCellClimate]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Cell Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Cell Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the '''Water Noise Texture''' path of the specified '''Cell'''. |name = GetCellNoiseTexture |returnType = texturePath:string_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = cell |Type = ref }} |example = let sNoiseTex := GetCellNoiseTexture Gomorrah00 }} ==See Also== *[[SetCellNoiseTexture]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Cell Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Cell Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the '''Water Noise Texture''' path of the specified '''Cell'''. |name = SetCellNoiseTexture |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = cell |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = texturePath |Type = string }} |example = SetCellNoiseTexture Gomorrah00 "Data\Textures\Water\WaterFlowRippleNoise01.dds" }} ==Notes== *The path is relative to the game's main folder, '''NOT''' to \Data\Textures\. *Adding a water noise texture to a cell without water will cause a crash to desktop. ==See Also== *[[GetCellNoiseTexture]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Cell Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Cell Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the '''Lighting Template''' of the specified '''Cell'''. |name = GetCellLightingTemplate |returnType = lightingTemplate:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = cell |Type = ref }} |example = set rLgtTemp to GetCellLightingTemplate Gomorrah00 }} ==See Also== *[[SetCellLightingTemplate]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Cell Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Light_Functions]] [[Category:Cell Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the '''Lighting Template''' of the specified '''Cell'''. |name = SetCellLightingTemplate |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = cell |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = lightingTemplate |Type = ref }} |example = SetCellLightingTemplate Gomorrah00 NovacInterior }} ==See Also== *[[GetCellLightingTemplate]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Cell Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Light_Functions]] [[Category:Cell Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns whether or not the specified flag (determined by flagID) is set for the specified '''Cell'''. |name = GetCellFlag |returnType = isFlagSet:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = cell |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagID |Type = int |Value = 0-16 }} ==Cell Flag IDs== 
0	Is Interior Cell
1	Has Water
2	Invert Fast Travel Behavior
3	No LOD Water
5	Public Place
6	Hand Changed
7	Behave Like Exterior
8	Ambient Color
9	Directional Color
10	Fog Color
11	Fog Near
12	Fog Far
13	Directional Rot
14	Directional Fade
15	Clip Distance
16	Fog Power
|example = set bIsFlagSet to GetCellFlag Gomorrah00 2 }} ==See Also== *[[Cell Common Data]] - explains some of these flags. *[[SetCellFlag]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Cell Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Cell Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets/un-sets the specified flag (determined by flagID) for the specified '''Cell'''. |name = SetCellFlag |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = cell |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagID |Type = int |Value = 0-16 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = doSet |Type = 0/1 }} ==Cell Flag IDs== 
0	Is Interior Cell
1	Has Water
2	Invert Fast Travel Behavior
3	No LOD Water
5	Public Place
6	Hand Changed
7	Behave Like Exterior
8	Ambient Color
9	Directional Color
10	Fog Color
11	Fog Near
12	Fog Far
13	Directional Rot
14	Directional Fade
15	Clip Distance
16	Fog Power
|example = SetCellFlag Gomorrah00 2 1 }} ==See Also== *[[GetCellFlag]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Cell Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Cell Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the '''[[Image Space]]''' of the specified '''[[Cell]]'''. |name = GetCellImageSpace |returnType = imageSpace:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = cell |Type = ref }} |example = set rImageSpace to GetCellImageSpace Gomorrah00 }} ==See Also== *[[SetCellImageSpace]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Cell Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Cell Functions]] [[Category:Imagespace Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Imagespace Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]]

revision.comment: Added note about Stewie's Tweaks changes to the function.
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Forces the current '''Menu Mode''' to reload, so that any changes made by previously-called, inventory-manipulating functions (which either add, remove, drop, or modify items' stats) are reflected in the list of displayed items. |name = RefreshItemsList |example = RefreshItemsList }} ==Notes== *Stewie's Tweaks patches this function to also work with the weapon modding menu and merchant repair menus. [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Removes the calling '''Inventory Reference''' from its containing object. *If quantity is not specified, or is <= 0, the whole count of the Inventory Reference will be removed. *If clearOwnership is not specified, or is not 0, ownership of the item will be cleared. *If targetRef is specified, the Inventory Reference will be moved to the target container, preserving all its stats/attributes (health, script, mods, etc.). Otherwise, it will be destroyed. |name = RemoveMeIRAlt |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = quantity |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = clearOwnership |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = targetRef |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = itemRef.RemoveMeIRAlt itemRef.RemoveMeIRAlt 1 0 ContainerRef }} ==Notes== *This function will invoke the [[OnDrop]]/[[OnAdd]] script-events (where applicable). ==See Also== *[[DropMeAlt]] *[[RemoveMe]] *[[RemoveMeIR]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Object Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Reference Functions]]

revision.comment: Adding to original actor functions category
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns whether or not the specified '''Script''' has been disabled by [[SetScriptDisabled]]. |name = GetScriptDisabled |returnType = disabled:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = script |Type = ref }} |example = set bDisabled to GetScriptDisabled RoseOfSharonCassidyScript }} ==See Also== *[[SetScriptDisabled]] *[[GetScriptEventDisabled]] *[[SetScriptEventDisabled]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Script Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Script_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Disables/re-enables the specified '''Script''' (or, alternatively, every script in the specified '''[[Form List]]'''). *While disabled, the script will '''not''' be processed by the game (including any and all block-types). *'''[[Variables]]''' defined in the script remain accessible, and may still be read/modified remotely, from another script. |name = SetScriptDisabled |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = scriptOrList |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = disable |Type = 0/1 }} |example = 
SetScriptDisabled RoseOfSharonCassidyScript 1
'''Disables''' the RoseOfSharonCassidyScript script, preventing it from being processed. 
SetScriptDisabled DisableScriptsList 1
'''Disables''' every script in the DisableScriptsList '''[[Form List]]'''. }} ==See Also== *[[GetScriptDisabled]] *[[GetScriptEventDisabled]] *[[SetScriptEventDisabled]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Script Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Script_Functions]]

revision.comment: Adding to original actor functions category
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns whether or not the specified script-'''Event Block''' (i.e. OnActivate, OnAdd, OnTrigger, etc.) has been disabled (by [[SetScriptEventDisabled]]) in the '''Script''' (if any) of the specified form (which may be a reference or a baseForm). |name = GetScriptEventDisabled |returnType = disabled:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = anyForm |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = eventName |Type = string }} |example = set bDisabled to GetScriptEventDisabled RoseOfSharonCassidyRef "OnHit" set bDisabled to GetScriptEventDisabled WeapNV9mmPistol "OnEquip" }} ==See Also== *[[SetScriptEventDisabled]] *[[GetScriptDisabled]] *[[SetScriptDisabled]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Script Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Script_Functions]]

revision.comment: Adding to original actor functions category
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Disables/re-enables the specified script-'''Event Block''' (i.e. OnActivate, OnAdd, OnTrigger, etc.) in the '''Script''' (if any) of the specified form(s). *A disabled '''Event Block''' will not run when its matching game-event occurs and gets registered in the containing '''Script'''. There are three options for the first argument (anyFormOrList): *An '''Object Reference''' - In which case, the effects will apply only on that one, specific reference. *A '''Base Form''' - In which case, the effects will apply on every instance of that form in the world. *A '''Form List''' of object references and/or base forms. The second argument (eventNames) is a string containing the event names. Multiple event names may be specified, separated by a space. |name = SetScriptEventDisabled |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = anyFormOrList |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = eventNames |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = disable |Type = 0/1 }} |example = SetScriptEventDisabled RoseOfSharonCassidyRef "OnHit OnStartCombat" 1 SetScriptEventDisabled TrapTripwire01 "OnActivate OnTriggerEnter OnLoad" 1 SetScriptEventDisabled WeapNV9mmPistol "OnEquip OnAdd" 0 SetScriptEventDisabled EventDisabledList "OnOpen OnReset" 1 }} ==Notes== *This function has no effect on, and does not interfere with NVSE's event-handling system, which will function normally. ==See Also== *[[GetScriptEventDisabled]] *[[GetScriptDisabled]] *[[SetScriptDisabled]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Script Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Script_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = "Fakes" the specified script-event on the calling reference, forcing the matching [[Begin|Event Block]] in its script (if one exists) to run. |name = FakeScriptEvent |returnType = success:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = eventName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = filterForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = actorRef.FakeScriptEvent "OnActorEquip" SomeWeaponForm doorRef.FakeScriptEvent "OnOpen" inventoryRef.FakeScriptEvent "OnUnequip" player objectRef.FakeScriptEvent "OnLoad" }} ==Notes== *This function is not affected by [[SetScriptEventDisabled]], and will work even if the specified '''Event Block''' is disabled. *Does not trigger NVSE event-handlers. *Cannot be used to fake '''OnActivate''' event. Use the vanilla [[Activate]]. *This cannot be used to run a code block on a [[quest]] script. ==See Also== *[[Call]] *[[GetScriptEventDisabled]] *[[SetScriptEventDisabled]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Script Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Script_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Adds a '''[[Map Marker]]''' showing the location of the [[calling reference]], much like map markers of followers. The calling reference may be of any type, except pick-able objects (inventory items). '''Note: For safety reasons, any map markers added by this function are removed (and, therefore, must be re-added) once a saved-game is loaded.''' |name = AddRefMapMarker |referenceType = reference |example = objectRef.AddRefMapMarker }} ==See Also== *[[RefHasMapMarker]] *[[RemoveRefMapMarker]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Map Functions]] [[Category:Map_Marker_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]]
