{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Removes a '''[[Map Marker]]''' of the calling reference added by [[AddRefMapMarker]]. |name = RemoveRefMapMarker |referenceType = reference |example = objectRef.RemoveRefMapMarker }} ==See Also== *[[RefHasMapMarker]] *[[AddRefMapMarker]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Map Functions]] [[Category:Map_Marker_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns whether the calling reference has a '''[[Map Marker]]''' added by [[AddRefMapMarker]]. |name = RefHasMapMarker |returnType = hasMarker:0/1 |referenceType = reference |example = set bHasMarker to objectRef.RefHasMapMarker }} ==See Also== *[[AddRefMapMarker]] *[[RemoveRefMapMarker]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Map Functions]] [[Category:Map_Marker_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = An alternate version of the vanilla function [[IsSpellTarget]], that actually works reliably and as advertised. Returns 1 if the calling reference is currently being affected by the specified magic item (an [[Actor Effect]] (spell), an [[Object Effect]] (enchantment), or an [[Ingestible]] (food, drink, chem)). |name = IsSpellTargetAlt |returnType = spellTarget:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = effectForm |Type = ref }} |example = set bSpellTarget to actorRef.IsSpellTargetAlt WithdrawalAlcohol }} ==See Also== *[[IsSpellTargetList]] *[[ActorHasEffect]] *[[HasMagicEffect]] *[[OnMagicEffectHit]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Effect Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the total, calculated attack damage of a '''[[Weapon]]''', adjusted by any bonuses/penalties from stats, perks, etc.
*If called on either an [[Inventory Reference]], or a weapon [[reference]] placed in the world, the damage will further be adjusted by the weapon's condition, and by any weapon mods attached to it. *In the case of an '''inventory reference''', if the weapon is in the inventory of an '''actor''', the damage will be adjusted by the actor's stats/perks. In all other cases, the player's stats/perks will be used. *If the weapon is equipped and uses ammo, the damage will be adjusted by any effects of the equipped ammo type. |name = GetCalculatedWeaponDamage |returnType = weaponDamage:float |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weaponForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = set fTotalDamage to GetCalculatedWeaponDamage WeapGatlingLaser
set fTotalDamage to weaponRef.GetCalculatedWeaponDamage }} ==See Also== *[[GetHitBaseWeaponDamage]] *[[GetCalculatedWeaponDPS]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Enables/disables special, optional '''Game Patches''' provided by '''JIP LN''', and ranging from certain game features which, while appearing in the game, are in fact broken or not fully-implemented; Other patches overhaul and alter the functionality of more major game features, making them more balanced and more suitable for some players' game-style. |name = SetOptionalPatch |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = patchName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = enable |Type = 0/1 }} ==Optional Game Patches== '''Note:''' All patches may be toggled ON/OFF via '''jip_nvse.ini''' (\Data\NVSE\Plugins\), in which case, if enabled, they will be applied automatically when the game launches. *'''bIgnoreDTDRFix''' - Fixes the '''Ignore DT/DR''' effect (mostly found in some melee/unarmed weapons), which is completely broken in the game. *'''bEnableFO3Repair''' - Brings back the FO3-style item repair menu to FNV. The max repair amount of items will be capped to the player's '''Repair''' skill. For example, with 75 skill, items can only be repaired for up to 75% of their max health, as opposed to 100% as before, regardless of skill level. *'''bEnableBigGunsSkill''' - Restores '''Big Guns''' as a fully-functional, playable skill. *'''bProjImpactDmgFix''' - Fixes an engine bug where weapons which fire projectiles that explode upon impact (i.e. Missile Launcher, Grenade Launcher, etc.) would only apply the damage from the explosion, even on direct hit, ignoring the weapon's hit damage. *'''bGameDaysPassedFix''' - Fixes an engine bug where the '''GameDaysPassed''' and '''GameHour''' global timers would "freeze" and stop increasing in game real-time (they would only be increased when sleeping, waiting or fast traveling). This issue directly affected hardcore needs build-up, which would also freeze in game real-time. *'''bHardcoreNeedsFix''' - Fixes an issue where some hardcore needs could, for undetermined reasons, end up having negative values. This resulted in hardcore needs not increasing at the correct rate/at all. *'''bNoFailedScriptLocks''' - Fixes an engine behavior where a script that has failed (due to any reason) at some point during execution will be effectively disabled by the game and will no longer be processed again until the game is restarted. *'''bDoublePrecision''' - Modifies the game's code such that arithmetic/relational operations in scripts are calculated/evaluated with double-precision floating-point accuracy (instead of single-precision). This was causing various issues, where relational operators (==, !=, <=, <, >=, >) were not evaluating correctly, and numeric calculations ended with inaccurate results (this was especially observed with relatively high absolute values). Additionally, this patch also guarantees no script errors/crashes in cases of division by zero. *'''bQttSelectShortKeys''' - If enabled, (a) when selecting an item stack in either the inventory, container, or barter menus, holding '''SHIFT''' will transfer/drop the <u>entire</u> stack, whilst holding '''CTRL''' will transfer/drop a <u>single</u> item from the stack - thus skipping the quantity-select menu. (b) Picking up from a container a stack of items that are weightless (such as ammo on non-hardcore mode) will bring up the quantity-select menu, instead of automatically picking the entire stack. *'''bMultiProjectileFix''' - Fixes an engine bug where using weapons which fire multiple projectiles per shot could often cause brief lags and FPS drops when hitting live targets. *'''bLocalizedDTDR''' - When enabled, a distinction will be made between head-armor and body-armor when applying damage reduction from DR/DT. Head hits will benefit only from DT/DR gained from worn head-armor (if any), whereas body hits, in similar fashion, only from DT/DR gained from worn body-armor. (Note: Requires bIgnoreDTDRFix to be enabled). *'''bVoiceModulationFix''' - Adds voice modulation (a slight distortion effect) for talking activators and holotapes. *'''bSneakBoundingBoxFix''' - Fixes a longstanding Bethesda games' bug where the dimensions of the collision bounding box encapsulating the player (as well as all NPCs) remained fixed and did not scale to correspond to body posture. This, effectively, had made it impossible to crawl through breaches and spaces when crouched, despite being able to easily fit through them. This patch fixes this issue for the player character and human companions. *'''bEnableNVACAlerts''' - If NVAC is installed, enable this setting to receive in-game alerts in the event an exception has occurred in the game's code that was successfully handled by NVAC. You will be notified by a corner message, and the error's details will be printed to the console. This should make it a lot easier to identify the precise circumstances/location in which the error has occurred. *'''bLoadScreenFix''' - Forces the load menu to give priority to location-specific load screens (if any are found to match current location) when selecting a load screen to display. *'''bNPCWeaponMods''' - When enabled, all NPCs will have a chance for their main weapon to include weapon mods. The chance is based on each NPC's level - the higher the level, the greater the chance. The type of mod(s) is selected randomly from the ones available for the particular weapon. Weapons have a chance to include multiple mods, though 2 mods is rare, and a fully-modded weapon is uncommon. *'''uNPCPerks''' - Unlocks perks for NPCs (perks will no longer be restricted to the player character and player teammates). Script commands such as AddPerk/RemovePerk/HasPerk/etc. will work on ANY NPC in the game. Optionally, perks will automatically be added to human NPCs. *'''bCreatureSpreadFix''' - Fixes a bug where all non-human actors suffer a massive penalty to weapon-spread (equal to the fUnaimedSpreadPenalty game setting) due to not having weapon aiming animations, and therefore not technically being able to aim. Note that although this is almost certainly a bug and an oversight of the game devs, this fix will make certain enemies much deadlier and may affect game balance. '''There will likely be more patches in future releases.''' |example = SetOptionalPatch "bIgnoreDTDRFix" 1
SetOptionalPatch "bEnableFO3Repair" 0 }} ==See Also== *[[GetOptionalPatch]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Utility Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:File Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns whether or not an optional '''Game Patch''' provided by '''[[JIP NVSE|JIP LN]]''', is currently enabled. Please see [[SetOptionalPatch]] for more in-depth details. |name = GetOptionalPatch |returnType = patchEnabled:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = patchName |Type = string }} |example = set bPatchEnabled to GetOptionalPatch "bIgnoreDTDRFix"
set bPatchEnabled to GetOptionalPatch "bEnableFO3Repair" }} ==See Also== *[[SetOptionalPatch]] *[[GetJohnnyPatch]] - JohnnyGuitar NVSE equivalent of this function. *[[ReadStringFromFile]] - Could also be used to read setting values from JIP LN's config file.
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Utility Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:File Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the XYZ position of the calling object reference. This function sets the position on all three axes, and is faster than calling [[SetPos]] for each axis, individually. It also ignores the FormType limitations imposed on '''SetPos'''. *If the 4th, optional argument, '''translate''', is non-zero, instead of flat-setting the object's position, the function will move the object to the specified XYZ point, relative to its '''current position and local rotation'''. |name = SetPosEx |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = posX |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = posY |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = posZ |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = translate |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }} |example = 
objectRef.SetPosEx 2340 7650 970
objectRef.SetPosEx 128 0 0 1
Moves the object 128 units along its local (+)X axis. }} ==See Also== *[[GetPos]] *[[GetPosEx]] *[[SetAngle]] *[[SetAngleEx]] *[[GetAngle]] *[[GetAngleEx]] *[[ClampToGround]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Movement Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an '''[[array_var]]''' containing the XYZ coordinates (indexed in that order) of the position the reticle (crosshair) is pointing at. If the position is not a valid one, the function returns '''[[ar_Null]]'''. |name = GetReticlePos |returnType = coords:array_var |example = let aReticlePos := GetReticlePos }} ==See Also== *[[GetReticleRange]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:Crosshair Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the distance (in game units) between the player and the position pointed at by the reticle (crosshair). If the position is not a valid one, the function returns -1. |name = GetReticleRange |returnType = range:float |example = set fReticleRange to GetReticleRange }} ==See Also== *[[GetReticlePosAlt]] *[[GetReticlePos]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Crosshair Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Moves the calling reference to the position pointed at by the reticle (crosshair). *If '''maxRange''' is passed, the reference will be moved to within a distance from the player that shall not exceed the value specified (10,000 by default; a higher value is not recommended). *If any of the three optional '''offsetX/Y/Z''' arguments is passed, similar to [[MoveTo]], the reference will be moved to the specified offset from the reticle position. |name = MoveToReticle |returnType = success:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = maxRange |Type = float |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = offsetX |Type = float |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = offsetY |Type = float |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = offsetZ |Type = float |Optional = y }} |example = objectRef.MoveToReticle 4000 objectRef.MoveToReticle 8000 0 0 128 }} ==See Also== *[[MoveTo]] *[[MoveToCell]] *[[MoveToNode]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Movement Functions]] [[Category:Crosshair Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the name of the [[calling reference]]. Unlike [[SetName]]/[[SetNameEx]]/[[LNSetName]], all of which change the name of every instance of the '''[[Base Form]]''', this function assigns a name '''unique''' only to the reference. *If the '''newName''' argument is omitted, the reference's name is reset to the default one. *To retrieve the unique name of a reference (if any), [[LNGetName]] should be used; [[GetName]] etc will only return the base form's name. |name = SetRefName |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = newName |Type = string |Optional = y }} |example = objectRef.SetRefName "Gussie the Newt Fancier" }} ==Notes== *This function is only session-persistent. [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Name Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Name Functions]] [[Category:String Functions]]

revision.comment: Add Notes (about Skill Books) and See Also
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the net modifier of the specified [[Actor Value Codes|Actor Value]], gained from '''[[Effects]]''' (of any type) currently applied on the calling actor. The 2nd argument '''typeFilter''' is optional, and may be used to instruct the function to return: *Only the '''permanent''' modifier (1) *Only the '''temporary''' modifier (2) *Both (3; default) |name = GetActorValueModifier |alias = GetAVMod |returnType = avMod:float |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = actorValue |Type = StatName }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = typeFilter |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = set fHealthModifier to CraigBooneRef.GetActorValueModifier Health }} ==Notes== *"Effects" granted by skill [[Book]]s are not applied as [[Effect]]s, meaning this function will not account for permanent modifiers brought by them. ==See Also== *[[GetPermanentActorValue]] *[[GetBaseActorValue]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Effect Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''UDF Script''' as a handler that will be invoked in the event '''"[[SetStage]] quest stageIdx"''' is called from any script. The '''skipScript''' argument is optional, and if non-zero will effectively prevent the '''result script''' (if any) of the specified stage from being executed. When invoked, the script is passed with two arguments: the '''Quest''' and the '''Stage''' index. |name = SetOnQuestStageEventHandler |returnType = success:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = quest |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = stageIdx |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = skipScript |Type = 1/0 |Optional = y }} |example = 
SetOnQuestStageEventHandler OnQuestStageUDF 1 vDialogueEDE 10 1
'''Sets''' the OnQuestStageUDF script as a handler to be invoked when '''"SetStage vDialogueEDE 10"''' is called. The result script of the stage will not be executed. 
SetOnQuestStageEventHandler OnQuestStageUDF 0 vDialogueEDE 10
'''Removes''' the OnQuestStageUDF script as a handler of the above. }} ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn	OnQuestStageUDF

ref	rQuest
int	iStageIdx

begin Function {rQuest, iStageIdx}

(code)

end
==See Also== *[[SetJohnnyOnCompleteQuestEventHandler]] *[[SetJohnnyOnFailQuestEventHandler]] *[[SetJohnnyOnStartQuestEventHandler]] *[[SetJohnnyOnStopQuestEventHandler]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Script Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JIP)]] [[Category:Script_Functions]] [[Category:Quest Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Modifies the XYZ rotation angles ('''in degrees''') of the calling reference. This function sets the angles of all three axes, and is faster (and possibly less glitchy) than calling [[SetAngle]] for each axis, individually. *The 4th argument, '''method''', is optional, and can be used for specifying how the rotation is modified: 
0	(Default) Set the EXTRINSIC (global) rotation.
1	Set the INTRINSIC (local) rotation.
2	Rotate the INTRINSIC (local) rotation by the specified XYZ values.
3	Rotate the EXTRINSIC (global) rotation by the specified XYZ values.
4	Set the EXTRINSIC (global) rotation, such that the reference will face the specified XYZ (given as COORDINATES - not angles!).
|name = SetAngleEx |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = angleX |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = angleY |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = angleZ |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = method |Type = int |Value = 0-4 |Optional = y }} |example = objectRef.SetAngleEx 34.5 0 126.7 objectRef.SetAngleEx 0 90 0 2 }} ==See Also== *[[GetAngle]] *[[GetAngleEx]] *[[SetPos]] *[[SetPosEx]] *[[Turn]] *[[FaceObject]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Movement Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = When called on a '''[[Door]]''' reference which teleports to another [[cell]], returns the Door [[reference]] teleported to in the other cell. |name = GetTeleportDoor |returnType = linkedDoor:refr |referenceType = reference |example = set rLinkedDoor to doorRef.GetTeleportDoor }} ==See Also== *[[GetTeleportCell]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Door Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the '''item price''' modifier (a multiplier applied to the base price) in the current '''Barter Menu''' transaction. The '''buyOrSell''' argument determines which modifier is returned: *0 - Bought items *1 - Sold items |name = GetBarterPriceMult |returnType = priceMult:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = buyOrSell |Type = 0/1 }} |example = set fSellPriceMult to GetBarterPriceMult 1 }} ==See Also== *[[SetBarterPriceMult]] *[[GetBarterItems]] *[[GetBarterGoldAlt]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the '''item price''' modifier (a multiplier applied to the base price) in the current '''Barter Menu''' transaction. The '''buyOrSell''' argument determines which modifier is set: *0 - Bought items (must be >= 1.0) *1 - Sold items (must be <= 1.0) |name = SetBarterPriceMult |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = buyOrSell |Type = 0/1 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = priceMult |Type = float }} |example = SetBarterPriceMult 0 1 Makes bought item prices go to their base value. 
float fMult

let fMult := (GetBarterPriceMult 1) * 1.25
SetBarterPriceMult 1 fMult
Makes sold item prices 25% greater. 
float fMult

let fMult := (GetBarterPriceMult 0) * 0.75
SetBarterPriceMult 0 fMult
Makes sold item prices 25% lesser (discount). }} ==Notes== *No matter how high/low the PriceMult, an item cannot be sold for higher than its base value, and it cannot be bought for lower than its base value. ==See Also== *[[GetBarterPriceMult]] *[[Calculate Buy Price (Perk Entry Point)]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Fakes a controller '''Button''' press for one frame. Multiple buttons may be specified at once. |name = TapButton |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = button |Type = int }} |example = TapButton 16384 }} {{Controller_Button_Codes}} ==See Also== *[[TapTrigger]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Controller Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Fakes a controller '''Trigger''' press for one frame. |name = TapTrigger |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = leftOrRight |Type = 0/1 }} |example = TapTrigger 1 Fakes a '''R-Trigger''' press. }} ==See Also== *[[TapButton]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Controller Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''UDF Script''' as a handler that will be invoked in the event the specified controller '''Trigger''' is pressed. When invoked, the script is passed with a single argument: the pressed '''Trigger''' (0: Left; 1: Right). |name = SetOnTriggerDownEventHandler |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = leftOrRight |Type = 0/1 }} |example = 
SetOnTriggerDownEventHandler OnTriggerDownUDF 1 0
'''Sets''' the OnTriggerDownUDF script as a handler to be invoked whenever '''L-Trigger''' is pressed. 
SetOnTriggerDownEventHandler OnTriggerDownUDF 0 0
'''Removes''' the OnTriggerDownUDF script as a handler of the above. }} ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn	OnTriggerDownUDF

int	bIsRTrigger

begin Function {bIsRTrigger}

(code)

end
==See Also== *[[SetOnTriggerUpEventHandler]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JIP)]] [[Category:Controller Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''UDF Script''' as a handler that will be invoked in the event the specified controller '''Trigger''' is released. When invoked, the script is passed with a single argument: the released '''Trigger''' (0: Left; 1: Right). |name = SetOnTriggerUpEventHandler |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = leftOrRight |Type = 0/1 }} |example = 
SetOnTriggerUpEventHandler OnTriggerUpUDF 1 0
'''Sets''' the OnTriggerUpUDF script as a handler to be invoked whenever '''L-Trigger''' is released. 
SetOnTriggerUpEventHandler OnTriggerUpUDF 0 0
'''Removes''' the OnTriggerUpUDF script as a handler of the above. }} ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn	OnTriggerUpUDF

int	bIsRTrigger

begin Function {bIsRTrigger}

(code)

end
==See Also== *[[SetOnTriggerDownEventHandler]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JIP)]] [[Category:Controller Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''UDF Script''' as a handler that will be invoked in the event an '''Actor''' is struck by a '''Critical Hit'''. Any combination of the three optional arguments may be used for filtering events: *'''target''' - The target '''Actor''' (taking the hit). *'''source''' - The source '''Reference''' (striking the hit). *'''weapon''' - The '''Weapon''' dealing the hit. '''Note:''' Due to GECK limitations, the function cannot accept '''0'''-valued arguments. As a workaround, when required, pass '''XMarker''' to indicate a filter should be ignored (see examples, below). When invoked, the script is passed with the struck '''Actor''' as the calling reference ("this"), and two arguments: the source '''Reference''' and the '''Weapon'''. |name = SetOnCriticalHitEventHandler |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = target |Type = ref |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = source |Type = ref |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = 
SetOnCriticalHitEventHandler OnCriticalHitUDF 1
'''Sets''' the OnCriticalHitUDF script as a handler to be invoked whenever any actor takes a critical hit (unfiltered). 
SetOnCriticalHitEventHandler OnCriticalHitUDF 1 FortCaesarRef
...whenever '''FortCaesarRef''' takes a critical hit. 
SetOnCriticalHitEventHandler OnCriticalHitUDF 1 FortCaesarRef PlayerRef
...whenever '''FortCaesarRef''' takes a critical hit from the '''Player'''. 
SetOnCriticalHitEventHandler OnCriticalHitUDF 1 XMarker PlayerRef
...whenever any actor takes a critical hit from the '''Player'''. 
SetOnCriticalHitEventHandler OnCriticalHitUDF 1 XMarker XMarker Weap10mmPistol
...whenever any actor takes a critical hit from a '''Weap10mmPistol'''. 
SetOnCriticalHitEventHandler OnCriticalHitUDF 1 XMarker PlayerRef Weap10mmPistol
...whenever any actor takes a critical hit from the '''Player''', using a '''Weap10mmPistol'''. }} ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn	OnCriticalHitUDF

ref	rTarget
ref	rSource
ref	rWeapon
int	iHitLocation

begin Function {rSource, rWeapon}

	set rTarget to this
	set iHitLocation to GetHitLocation	; (Optional)

	(code)

end
==Notes== When explosions trigger this event it can fire up to three times. To detect which of the three possible times use [[GetHitProjectile]] and [[GetType]] on the returned projectile. The function will return 0 on the first firing. On the second firing it will either return the Projectile that hit the actor or the Explosion that hit the actor if it was not a direct hit. On the third firing it will return the Explosion that hit the actor if a Projectile hit the actor on the second firing. ==See Also== *[[SetOnHitEventHandler]] *[[GetHitExtendedFlag]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

revision.comment: Removed link to FOSE docs, added control codes template
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Fakes a '''Control''' press indefinitely, or until [[ReleaseControl]] is called. |name = HoldControl |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ctrlID |Type = int }} |example = HoldControl 6 }} {{Control_Codes}} ==See Also== *[[ReleaseControl]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Input Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Input Functions]]

revision.comment: Removed link to FOSE docs, added control code table template
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Releases a '''Control''' which was held down by a prior call to [[HoldControl]]. |name = ReleaseControl |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ctrlID |Type = int }} |example = ReleaseControl 6 }} {{Control_Codes}} ==See Also== *[[HoldControl]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Input Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Input Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = An alternate, extended version of the vanilla function [[PlayIdle]] - causes the calling actor to play a specified '''[[Idle Animation]]'''. Unlike the former, which takes a string argument comprising the name of the '''Idle Animation''' to be played, this function takes an '''[[Editor ID]]'''. Moreover, the behavior of this function is determined by the passed argument: *If a "parent" animation (i.e. grouping one or more "child" animations, or sub-parents), the function will randomly pick and play the first animation whose conditions (if any) evaluate as true. *If a "child" animation (i.e. not grouping other animations), the function will force-play that animation, ignoring conditions. *If the argument is entirely omitted, the function behaves exactly the same as [[PickIdle]]. |name = PlayIdleEx |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = idleAnim |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = 
actorRef.PlayIdleEx MTHitHeadBForce
Force-plays the '''MTHitHeadBForce''' on actorRef (conditions, if any, are ignored). 
actorRef.PlayIdleEx 3rdPersonHitReactions
Randomly picks the first child animation of '''3rdPersonHitReactions''' whose conditions evaluate as true, and plays it on actorRef. }} ==See Also== *[[ForcePlayIdle]] *[[GetPlayedIdle]] *[[IsIdlePlayingEx]] *[[StopIdle]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Animation Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the '''NIF File''' path for a '''Model''' of the specified '''Armor''' form. The file path is relative to '''..\Data\Meshes\'''. Which model path will be set is determined by the '''modelSlot''' argument: 
0	Biped Model - Male
1	Biped Model - Female
2	World (ground) Model - Male
3	World (ground) Model - Female
'''Note:''' This function is identical to [[SetBipedModelPathEx]] which, at the time this was written, is partially/utterly broken. |name = SetBipedModelPathAlt |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = armor |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = modelSlot |Type = int |Value = 0-3 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = filePath |Type = formattedString }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = formatVars(up to 20) |Optional = y }} |example = SetBipedModelPathAlt ArmorCombat 1 "Armor\CombatArmor\F\OutfitF.nif" }} ==Notes:== *'''filePath''' supports [[String Formatting|string formatting]], and up to 20 variables may be used. ==See Also== *[[ReloadEquippedModels]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Armor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Armor Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]]

revision.comment: Added Slot IDs list.
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Reloads the 3D models of the [[calling]] [[Actor]]'s equipped weapon and any worn armor/clothing items, to reflect any changes made to the models/textures of those items via other functions. *The function takes an optional argument which, if not omitted, enables to specify a single equipment slot to reload. Otherwise, the function reloads ALL slots. Using this function, for the said purpose, is preferable to, less intrusive and more "visually appealing" than calling [[Update3D]], and will also work properly on the player character. |name = ReloadEquippedModels |alias = ReloadModels |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = equipSlot |Type = int |Value = 0-19 |Optional = y }} |example = actorRef.ReloadEquippedModels actorRef.ReloadEquippedModels 5 }} == Equipment Slot IDs == 0: head 1: hair 2: upper body (usual slot for armor and clothing) 3: left hand 4: right hand 5: weapon 6: pip boy 7: backpack 8: necklace 9: headband 10: hat 11: eyeglasses 12: nosering 13: earrings 14: mask 15: choker 16: mouth object 17: body addon 1 18: body addon 2 19: body addon 3 ==See Also== *[[Update3D]] *[[UpdatePlayer3D]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Armor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Armor Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the calculated '''experience points (XP)''' the player character will gain by killing the calling actor. Note that the XP is also gained if a [[GetPlayerTeammate|player companion]] kills the calling actor, unless `bNoCompanionKillXP` is enabled in [[lStewieAl's Tweaks]]. |name = GetKillXP |returnType = killXP:float |referenceType = reference |example = set fKillXP to actorRef.GetKillXP }} ==Notes== *This function does not account for the "Adjust Experience Points" perk [[Entry Point]]. When necessary, use `[[GetPerkModifier]] 9` to adjust the count. For example: 
float fKillXP
float fCalcKillXP

let fKillXP := SunnyREF.GetKillXP
let fCalcKillXP := Player.GetPerkModifier 9 fKillXP
==See Also== *[[RewardXP]] *[[RewardXPExact]] *[[ResetXP]] *[[GetXPForNextLevel]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the actor (if any) by whom the calling actor was killed. |name = GetKiller |returnType = killer:ref |referenceType = reference |example = set rKiller to actorRef.GetKiller }}
==Notes==
*If the actor has been disabled, they will lose the killer data.
==See Also== *[[IsKiller]] - Used to check if a certain NPC is a ref's killer. *[[GetKillXP]] *[[OnDeath]] *[[SetJohnnyOnDyingEventHandler]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Delays the processing of the script code immediately following this function call, in the current '''BEGIN''' block. While waiting, the script will skip processing the waiting '''BEGIN''' block (the rest of the script will be processed normally); When the time expires, the script will continue from the wait point. *The '''waitTime''' is represented in 1 wait unit = 1 processing cycle, which is once every frame (60 times/second) for Object-type scripts, and determined by the '''Script Processing Delay''' setting for Quest-type scripts. *Any running script may only have one active waiting block at one time; calling this function from a '''BEGIN''' block while another block is waiting will stop the latter from waiting. *This function cannot be used in Effect-type scripts, UDF scripts, or result scripts. |name = ScriptWait |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = waitTime |Type = int }} |example = 
BEGIN 

	

	ScriptWait 10

	

END

BEGIN 

	

END

(...)
}} ==See Also== *[[IsScriptWaiting]] *[[StopScriptWaiting]] *[[CallAfterSeconds]] *[[CallAfterFrames]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Script Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Script_Functions]]

revision.comment: Adding to original actor functions category
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns whether any block in the script run by the calling reference/quest is waiting (via the [[ScriptWait]] command). |name = IsScriptWaiting |returnType = isWaiting:1/0 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = quest |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = set bScriptWaiting to objectRef.IsScriptWaiting
set bScriptWaiting to IsScriptWaiting SomeQuest }} ==See Also== *[[ScriptWait]] *[[StopScriptWaiting]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Script Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Script_Functions]]

revision.comment: Adding to original actor functions category
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = If there is any block in the script run by the calling reference/quest that is currently waiting (via the [[ScriptWait]] command), calling this function will stop it. |name = StopScriptWaiting |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = quest |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = objectRef.StopScriptWaiting
StopScriptWaiting SomeQuest }} ==See Also== *[[ScriptWait]] *[[IsScriptWaiting]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Script Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Script_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Constructs, compiles and runs the script source in the [[String Formatting|formatted string]]. *The script is executed immediately or, if the '''delayTime''' argument is non-zero, is deferred for the specified number of frames. *The script should follow the same structure rules as '''Result Scripts''' (of quest stages, dialogue topics, etc.), and may include local '''variables''' and '''if/else''' statements. *If the function is '''called on a reference''' (implicitly/explicitly), the script will be called on that '''same reference''' (passed as "this" and returned by '''GetSelf'''). Otherwise, it will be called on '''the player''' (see examples, below). *When used skillfully, this function can generate and run fully customizable scripts. |name = RunScriptSnippet |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = delayTime |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = scriptSource |Type = formattedString }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = formatVars(up to 20) |Optional = y }} |example = 
set rItem to Ammo12GaSlug
set iCount to 50

CraigBooneRef.RunScriptSnippet 5 "ref rItem%rint iCount%rset rItem to %q%i%q%rset iCount to %g%rif GetItemCount rItem < iCount%rAddItem rItem iCount%rendif" rItem iCount
Will generate the following script, executed after a 5 frames delay, using '''CraigBooneRef''' as the calling reference: 
ref rItem
int iCount

set rItem to "0013E447"
set iCount to 50

if GetItemCount rItem < iCount
	AddItem rItem iCount
endif
'''Notes:''' *The '''%r''' format specifier must be used to indicate a new line. *When using forms/references, the '''Form ID''' must be passed (not the '''Editor ID'''), enclosed in double quotation marks (using the '''%q''' specifier). }} [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Script Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Script_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the '''Idle Animation''' currently playing on the calling actor. |name = GetPlayedIdle |returnType = idleAnim:ref |referenceType = reference |example = set rCurrIdle to actorRef.GetPlayedIdle }} ==See Also== *[[IsIdlePlayingEx]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Animation Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns whether the specified [[Idle Animations|Idle Animation]] is currently being played on the calling actor. |name = IsIdlePlayingEx |returnType = idlePlaying:1/0 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = idleAnim |Type = baseform }} |example = set bPickingNose to actorRef.IsIdlePlayingEx LoosePickingNose }} ==See Also== *[[PlayIdleEx]] *[[IsIdlePlaying]] to detect if a special Idle Anim is playing, in general. *[[GetPlayedIdle]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Animation Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

revision.comment: Adding to base Quest Functions category
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the Nth quest target linked to the specified '''Objective''' of the specified '''Quest'''. |name = SetObjectiveNthTarget |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = quest |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = objectiveIndex |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = targetIndex |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = targetRef |Type = ref }} |example = SetObjectiveNthTarget VCG02 30 0 targetRef }} ==See Also== *[[GetObjectiveTargets]] *[[GetObjectiveHasTarget]] *[[AddObjectiveTarget]] *[[RemoveObjectiveTarget]] [[Category:Quest Functions]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Quest Functions (JIP)]]

revision.comment: Adding to base Quest Functions category
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns whether the specified reference is a quest target linked to the specified '''Objective''' of the specified '''Quest'''. |name = GetObjectiveHasTarget |returnType = hasTarget:1/0 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = quest |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = objectiveIndex |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = targetRef |Type = ref }} |example = set bHasTarget to GetObjectiveHasTarget VCG02 30 targetRef }} ==See Also== *[[GetObjectiveTargets]] *[[SetObjectiveNthTarget]] *[[AddObjectiveTarget]] *[[RemoveObjectiveTarget]] [[Category:Quest Functions]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Quest Functions (JIP)]]

revision.comment: Adding to base Quest Functions category
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Adds the specified reference as a quest target linked to the specified '''Objective''' of the specified '''Quest'''. |name = AddObjectiveTarget |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = quest |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = objectiveIndex |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = targetRef |Type = ref }} |example = AddObjectiveTarget VCG02 30 targetRef }} ==See Also== *[[GetObjectiveTargets]] *[[SetObjectiveNthTarget]] *[[GetObjectiveHasTarget]] *[[RemoveObjectiveTarget]] [[Category:Quest Functions]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Quest Functions (JIP)]]

revision.comment: Adding to base Quest Functions category
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Removes the specified reference from the quest target(s) linked to the specified '''Objective''' of the specified '''Quest'''. |name = RemoveObjectiveTarget |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = quest |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = objectiveIndex |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = targetRef |Type = ref }} |example = RemoveObjectiveTarget VCG02 30 targetRef }} ==See Also== *[[GetObjectiveTargets]] *[[SetObjectiveNthTarget]] *[[GetObjectiveHasTarget]] *[[AddObjectiveTarget]] [[Category:Quest Functions]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Quest Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an '''[[array_var]]''' containing all the quest target references linked to the specified '''Objective''' of the specified '''[[Quest]]'''. |name = GetObjectiveTargets |returnType = objTargets:array_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = quest |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = objectiveIndex |Type = int }} |example = let aObjTargets := GetObjectiveTargets VCG02 30 }} ==See Also== *[[SetObjectiveNthTarget]] *[[GetObjectiveHasTarget]] *[[AddObjectiveTarget]] *[[RemoveObjectiveTarget]] [[Category:Quest Functions]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Quest Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the '''Display Text''' of the specified '''Objective''' of the specified '''Quest'''. |name = SetObjectiveText |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = quest |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = objectiveIndex |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = displayText |Type = formattedString }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = formatVars(up to 20) |Optional = y }} |example = SetObjectiveText VCG02 30 "Kill %g Cazadores, then report back to %n." iCount rTheBoss }} ==See Also== *[[GetObjectiveText]] [[Category:Quest Functions]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Quest Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:String Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the '''Display Text''' of the specified '''Objective''' of the specified '''Quest'''. |name = GetObjectiveText |returnType = objText:string_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = quest |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = objectiveIndex |Type = int }} |example = let sObjText := GetObjectiveText VCG02 30 }} ==See Also== *[[SetObjectiveText]] [[Category:Quest Functions]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Quest Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:String Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the '''Armor Form''' used as an '''Audio Template''' by the specified '''Armor Form'''. |name = GetArmorAudioTemplate |returnType = audioTemplate:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = armorForm |Type = ref }} |example = set rAudioTemplate to GetArmorAudioTemplate ArmorCombat }} ==See Also== *[[SetArmorAudioTemplate]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Armor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Armor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the '''Armor Form''' used as an '''Audio Template''' by the specified '''Armor Form'''. If the second argument is omitted, the audio template will be set to null. |name = SetArmorAudioTemplate |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = armorForm |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = audioTemplate |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = SetArmorAudioTemplate ArmorCombat ArmorLightAudioTemplate }} ==See Also== *[[GetArmorAudioTemplate]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Armor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Armor Functions]]

revision.comment: da pronoun
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Forces the calling actor to either draw (1) or holster (0) their equipped weapon. |name = SetWeaponOut |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weaponOut |Type = 1/0 }} |example = actorRef.SetWeaponOut 1 }} ==See also== * [[IsWeaponOut]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the UI art file that will be used for the specified '''[[Terminal]]''' in the computers/hacking menu.
*The first argument may be either a '''[[Terminal]]''' form, or a '''[[Form List]]''' of terminal forms. *If the second argument is omitted, the art file will be reset to the default one. |name = SetTerminalUIModel |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = terminalOrList |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = modelPath |Type = string |Optional = y }} |example = SetTerminalUIModel GunRunnerTerminal "Terminals\TerminalInterface02.NIF" }} ==Notes== *The file path should be relative to '''data\meshes\''' [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]]

revision.comment: italics
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Opens an instance of the '''Quantity Menu'''. The function takes three arguments: *'''callbackScript''' - A UDF script that shall be invoked once the Quantity Menu is closed. The script is passed with a single argument - the selected quantity (see example script, below). If '''Cancel''' was selected, -1 is passed. *'''maxQuantity''' - The maximum selected quantity in the menu. *'''initQuantity''' - The initial selected quantity when the menu is opened. |name = ShowQuantityMenu |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = callbackScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = maxQuantity |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = initQuantity |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = ShowQuantityMenu QuantityMenuUDF 123 ShowQuantityMenu QuantityMenuUDF 1000 250 }} ==Callback Script== A skeleton script for a callback: 
scn	QuantityMenuUDF

int	iQuantity

begin Function {iQuantity}

(code)

end
==Notes== *To change the text shown by the quantity menu, the [[sHowMany]] string gamesetting can be modified by using [[SetStringSetting]] or other means. ==See Also== *[[ShowTextInputMenu]] *[[FakeQuantityMenu]] - For the ''Fallout 3'' equivalent. [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Adds the specified '''Actor Effect''' to the effect list attached to the <u>base form</u> of the specified base actor or calling reference. |name = AddBaseEffectListEffect |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = actorEffect |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseActor |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = RexRef.AddBaseEffectListEffect DeathclawDT
AddBaseEffectListEffect DeathclawDT Rex }} ==See Also== *[[RemoveBaseEffectListEffect]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Removes the specified '''Actor Effect''' (if present) from the effect list attached to the <u>base form</u> of the specified base actor or calling reference. |name = RemoveBaseEffectListEffect |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = actorEffect |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseActor |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = RexRef.RemoveBaseEffectListEffect DeathclawDT
RemoveBaseEffectListEffect DeathclawDT Rex }} ==See Also== *[[AddBaseEffectListEffect]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]]

revision.comment: You used the contracted form of its. "It's" is a contraction for "it is". Its is the possessive form which is more appropriate here.
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = An improved version of the NVSE function [[MessageBoxEx]]. *Does not require using [[GetButtonPressed]] for message boxes with multiple buttons, but instead passes the selected button index via a UDF script, which is invoked when a button is pressed. *Allows up to '''255''' buttons (instead of 10 max). *Allows setting the message box title (optional). The title text must be passed at the beginning of the formatted string, enclosed by circumflexes: '''^'''title'''^''' (see example, below). The function takes two(+) arguments: *'''callbackScript''' - A '''UDF Script''' (as explained above. Also see example script, below). *'''msgStrings''' - A '''[[String Formatting|formatted string]]''' containing the message box title, text and buttons (all optional). Up to 20 variables may be passed for format specifiers. |name = MessageBoxExAlt |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = callbackScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = msgStrings |Type = formattedString }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = formatVars(up to 20) |Optional = y }} |example = 
MessageBoxExAlt MsgButtonPressUDF "^Awesome Menu^Select an option:|Option #1|Option #2|Option #3|Option #4|Option #5"
Will display the message box shown below: [[Image:MessageBoxExAlt.jpg|200px]] }} ==Callback Script== A skeleton script for a callback: 
scn	MsgButtonPressUDF

int	iButton

begin Function {iButton}

(code)

end
==Notes== *Clicking a button returns its integer value, it is 0 based. *In the example above: **Option #1 will set iButton to 0 **Option #2 will set iButton to 1 ==See Also== *[https://cs.elderscrolls.com/index.php?title=MessageBoxEx MessageBoxEx (OBSE)] - Additional information can be found in this article, that is also applicable to this function. *[[MessageExAlt]] *[[DynamicMenuScript]] - Template script to use conditions. [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Message Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an '''[[array_var]]''' containing all targetable references in the current [[VATS]] mode. |name = GetVATSTargets |returnType = VATSTargets:array_var |example = let aTargets := GetVATSTargets }} ==See Also== *[[SetVatsTarget]]
[[Category:VATS Functions]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

revision.comment: Removed a duplicate word.
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns true if the script on the specified calling reference contains the matching [[Begin|Event Block]]. The optional baseForm may be a script '''or''' scripted object. |name = HasScriptBlock |returnType = success:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = eventID |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseObject |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = actorRef.HasScriptBlock 10 ; OnDeath
doorRef.HasScriptBlock 31 ; OnOpen
HasScriptBlock 13 SomeScriptUDF ; NVSE UDF
HasScriptBlock 21 SomeActivator ; OnLoad }} ==Notes== This function is the only way to detect if a script is a [[User Defined Function]] by checking for type 13 ==See Also== *[[FakeScriptEvent]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Script Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Script_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = An alternate version of the vanilla [[CastImmediateOnSelf]]. Instantly invokes the specified '''[[Game Effect]]''' ([[Actor Effect]], [[Object Effect]] or [[Ingestible]]) on the calling [[actor]] [[reference]]. If the second (optional) argument '''casterRef''' is not omitted, the effects being applied on the calling [[reference]] will be registered as if they were inflicted by casterRef. *If any of the '''Effect Items''' of the effect are considered hostile, this will count as an assault. *If the effect causes the death of the calling actor, and the caster is the player character, XP will be gained for the kill. '''Note:''' For the above to work properly, all the "hostile" '''Effect Items''' must have their '''Range''' set to '''Touch'''. *If the target is not a [[GetIsGhost|Ghost]], the spell's type is NOT Disease, Ability or Addiction, and a ''casterRef'' is specified, calling this function can trigger [[OnHit]] events for each applicable Effect Item. *If the target reference is not currently loaded, does nothing. |name = CastImmediate |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = gameEffect |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = casterRef |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = SunnyRef.CastImmediate CazadorPoison player ;* Only if "hostile" Effect Items have "Touch" range! ;* SunnyRef will now be affected by the CazadorPoison effect, and will turn hostile against the player. player.CastImmediate FlamerCritEffect ;* Only if "hostile" Effect Items have "Self" range! ;* The player will suffer the (mostly visual) effects of FlamerCritEffect. }} ==See Also== *[[AddSpell]] *[[AddSpellNS]] *[[Dispel]] *[[IsSpellTargetAlt]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Effect Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns whether the CharGenMenu, SpecialBookMenu, LoveTesterMenu, or TraitMenu is open. |name = IsInCharGen |returnType = inCharGen:1/0 |example = set bInCharGen to IsInCharGen }} ==See also== * [[GetInCharGen]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = When called from a '''[[OnHit]]'''/'''[[OnHitWith]]''' script block (or the corresponding NVSE event handlers), or from a '''[[ScriptEffectStart]]''' block in an '''[[Ammo]]''' form '''Impact Script''', will return the health damage caused by the current hit. |name = GetHitHealthDamage |returnType = healthDmg:float |referenceType = reference |example = set fHealthDmg to targetRef.GetHitHealthDamage }} ==Notes== *In order to determine if [[GetHitHealthDamage]] will return an accurate and viable result, one should check what type of OnHit event is running by using [[GetHitProjectile#Notes|GetHitProjectile]].
==See Also== *[[GetHitBaseWeaponDamage]] *[[GetCalculatedWeaponDamage]] *[[GetHitLimbDamage]] *[[GetHitAttacker]] *[[GetHitProjectile]] *[[GetHitWeapon]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]]

revision.comment: Note about Explosion dmg bug
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = When called from a '''OnHit'''/'''OnHitWith''' script block (or the corresponding NVSE event handlers), or from a '''ScriptEffectStart''' block in an '''Ammo''' form '''Impact Script''', will return the limb damage caused by the current hit.
To determine which limb was hit, use [[GetHitLocation]].
If the hit is the result of an [[Explosion]], will always return 0, even if the explosion dealt limb damage. |name = GetHitLimbDamage |returnType = limbDmg:float |referenceType = reference |example = set fLimbDmg to targetRef.GetHitLimbDamage }} ==See Also== *[[GetHitHealthDamage]] *[[GetHitAttacker]] *[[GetHitProjectile]] *[[GetHitWeapon]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = When called from a '''OnHit'''/'''OnHitWith''' script block (or the corresponding NVSE event handlers), or from a '''ScriptEffectStart''' block in an '''Ammo''' form '''Impact Script''', will return the actor reference (if any) by which the current hit was struck. |name = GetHitAttacker |returnType = attacker:ref |referenceType = reference |example = set rAttacker to targetRef.GetHitAttacker }} ==See Also== *[[GetHitHealthDamage]] *[[GetHitLimbDamage]] *[[GetHitProjectile]] *[[GetHitWeapon]] *[[SetOnHitEventHandler]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]]

revision.comment: multiple OnHit events per pellets only happen with JIP OnHit
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = When called from a '''[[OnHit]]'''/'''[[OnHitWith]]''' script block (or the corresponding NVSE event handlers), or from a '''[[ScriptEffectStart]]''' block in an '''[[Ammo]]''' form '''Impact Script''', will return the '''[[Projectile]]''' OR '''[[Explosion]]''' [[reference]] (if any) of the current hit. |name = GetHitProjectile |returnType = projectile/Explosion:ref |referenceType = reference |example = set rProjectileRef to targetRef.GetHitProjectile }} ==Notes== *The OnHit event from [[SetOnHitEventHandler]] can run multiple times for what seems to be a singular hit. This function can return up to three different results for the different instances of the OnHit event. These return values categorize the type of OnHit event that has occurred: 
1: No valid reference 
2: Projectile-type reference
3: Explosion-type reference
[[GetHitTypeUDF]] can be used to retrieve the type of OnHit event that is occurring. Note that these types of OnHit events are not necessarily in order of possible frame-time execution; for example, when an explosion directly hits an actor, first the ''Projectile-type reference'' OnHit event runs, then ''No valid reference'', and finally ''Explosion-type reference''. *Case 1 ('''''No valid reference'''''): **If the attack is a regular melee hit, this will be the only time the OnHit event will fire. In this case, [[GetHitHealthDamage]] returns a reliable result. **This may also happen right before an ''Explosion-type reference'' OnHit event occurs. In this case, [[GetHitHealthDamage]] does NOT return a reliable result; damage should only be checked when the ''Explosion-type reference'' OnHit event occurs. **For flamethrower-type hits, the OnHit event will run a couple times with this case. For all these cases, [[GetHitHealthDamage]] does NOT seem to be reliable to check. Instead, it is more accurate to check for a ''Projectile-type reference'' hit, which will run once, and then check the health damage. *Case 2 ('''''Projectile-type reference'''''): **For throwing-type weapons like the Throwing Spear, this is the only OnHit event-type that will be executed (it is executed only once), and [[GetHitHealthDamage]] is safe to check here. **For shotgun-type weapons, this is also the only OnHit event-type that will be executed, except it is executed multiple times for [[SetOnHitEventHandler]], equal to the amount of pellets the weapon has. [[GetHitHealthDamage]] will return the amount of damage each pellet is dealing. *Case 3 ('''''Explosion-type reference'''''): **To check damage from explosions, checking the damage from this type of event (+ possibly checking the ''Projectile-type reference'' type in order to account for direct projectile hit damage) is all that is needed. ***Caveat: if the player fires a grenade launcher right next to a mine that they're next to, the collective detonation will fire this type of event twice, but the total damage retrieved by [[GetHitHealthDamage]] will be inaccurate. ==See Also== *[[GetHitHealthDamage]] *[[GetHitLimbDamage]] *[[GetHitAttacker]] *[[GetHitWeapon]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Projectile Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions]] [[Category:Explosion Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Explosion Functions]]

revision.comment: Added "base" in front of "Weapon Form" to clarify things for myself, even if it may be a bit redundant.
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = When called from a '''OnHit'''/'''OnHitWith''' script block (or the corresponding NVSE event handlers), or from a '''ScriptEffectStart''' block in an '''Ammo''' form '''Impact Script''', will return the base '''Weapon''' form (if any) with which the current hit was struck. |name = GetHitWeapon |returnType = weapon:ref |referenceType = reference |example = set rWeapon to targetRef.GetHitWeapon }} ==See Also== *[[GetHitHealthDamage]] *[[GetHitLimbDamage]] *[[GetHitAttacker]] *[[GetHitProjectile]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the precise number of game days passed since the specified YYYYMMDD date. *If the optional arguments are omitted, the function returns the number of days passed since 10/13/81 (October 13, 2281). *Using this function is preferable to using the '''[[GameDaysPassed]]''' global variable, which is highly unreliable. |name = GetGameDaysPassed |returnType = daysPassed:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = year |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = month |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = day |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = set fDaysPassed to GetGameDaysPassed
set fDaysPassed to GetGameDaysPassed 2282 2 10 }} ==Notes== *''month'' uses standard month numbering (1 - 12), but the ''GameMonth'' global variable numbers the months from 0 to 11. *When using mods that change the start date such as TTW, you need to amend the starting year to ''2277 08 17'' Otherwise, this function may return negative numbers.
==See Also== *[[GetDayOfWeek]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Time Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the '''Base [[Actor]]''''s rank in the specified '''[[Faction]]'''. If the base actor is not in the faction, returns -1. |name = GetBaseFactionRank |returnType = factionRank:int |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = faction |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseActor |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = set iFactionRank to CraigBooneRef.GetBaseFactionRank NCRFactionNV
set iFactionRank to GetBaseFactionRank NCRFactionNV CraigBoone }} ==See Also== *[[SetBaseFactionRank]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Faction Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Faction Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the '''Base [[Actor]]''''s rank in the specified '''[[Faction]]'''. |name = SetBaseFactionRank |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = faction |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = rank |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseActor |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = CraigBooneRef.SetBaseFactionRank NCRFactionNV 1
SetBaseFactionRank NCRFactionNV 1 CraigBoone }} ==See Also== *[[GetBaseFactionRank]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Faction Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Faction Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns a value in the range of 0.0 to 1.0, indicating the percent of the calling actor's body currently immersed in water.
A value greater than 0.875 indicates the calling actor is diving (holding breath). |name = GetWaterImmersionPerc |returnType = immersionPerc:float |referenceType = reference |example = set fImmersionPerc to rActorRef.GetWaterImmersionPerc }} ==See Also== *[[IsInWater]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Water Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Dismembers the calling actor's specified limb. If the second, optional argument is non-zero, the limb will explode; otherwise, it will be severed (default). |name = DismemberLimb |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = bodyPartID |Type = int |Value = 0-13 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = explode |Type = 1/0 |Optional = y }} ==Body Part IDs== 
0	Torso
1	Head 1
2	Head 2
3	Left Arm 1
4	Left Arm 2
5	Right Arm 1
6	Right Arm 2
7	Left Leg 1
8	Left Leg 2
9	Left Leg 3
10	Right Leg 1
11	Right Leg 2
12	Right Leg 3
13	Brain
|example = rActorRef.DismemberLimb 7 rActorRef.DismemberLimb 3 1 }} ==Notes== *Limb dismemberment is normally a death effect. While calling this function on a live actor will work, doing so is ill advised and may cause any number of glitches ==See Also== *[[CrippleLimb]] *[[IsLimbGone]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]]

revision.comment: Jazzisparis moved page [[Activator Functions (JIP)]] to [[Category:Activator Functions (JIP)]]
revision.text: {{Bc|Functions (JIP)}} [[Category:Functions (JIP)]]

revision.comment: link to activator page
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the activation prompt of the specified [[Activator]] form. |name = GetActivatorPrompt |returnType = prompt:string_var |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = activator |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = let sPrompt := rActivatorRef.GetActivatorPrompt
let sPrompt := GetActivatorPrompt BlackjackTableGomorrah }} ==See Also== *[[SetActivatorPrompt]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Activator Functions (JIP)]]

revision.comment: Link to Activator page
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the activation prompt of the specified [[Activator]] form. |name = SetActivatorPrompt |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = prompt |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = activator |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = rActivatorRef.SetActivatorPrompt "Play Blackjack"
SetActivatorPrompt "Play Blackjack" BlackjackTableGomorrah }} ==See Also== *[[GetActivatorPrompt]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Activator Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the [[Radio_Stations|radio station]] ([[Talking Activator]]) attached to the specified [[Activator]] form. |name = GetActivatorRadioStation |returnType = radioStation:ref |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = activator |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = set rRadioStation to rActivatorRef.GetActivatorRadioStation
set rRadioStation to GetActivatorRadioStation GoodspringsRadio }} ==See Also== *[[SetActivatorRadioStation]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Activator Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Radio_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Radio_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the [[Radio_Stations|radio station]] ([[TalkingActivator|Talking Activator]]) attached to the specified [[Activator]] form. |name = SetActivatorRadioStation |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = radioStation |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = activator |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = rActivatorRef.SetActivatorRadioStation vCountryRadio
SetActivatorRadioStation vCountryRadio GoodspringsRadio }} ==See Also== *[[GetActivatorRadioStation]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Activator Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Radio_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Radio_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the Activate [[Sound]] of the specified [[Activator]] form. |name = GetActivatorSoundActivate |returnType = activateSound:ref |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = activator |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = set rActivateSound to rActivatorRef.GetActivatorSoundActivate set rActivateSound to GetActivatorSoundActivate BananaYuccaPickable }} ==See Also== *[[SetActivatorSoundActivate]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Activator Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Sound Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the ''Activate'' [[Sound]] of the specified [[Activator]] form. |name = SetActivatorSoundActivate |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = sound |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = activator |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = rActivatorRef.SetActivatorSoundActivate OBJPickPlant
SetActivatorSoundActivate OBJPickPlant BananaYuccaPickable }} ==See Also== *[[GetActivatorSoundActivate]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Activator Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Sound Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the Looping [[Sound]] of the specified [[Activator]] form. |name = GetActivatorSoundLooping |returnType = loopingSound:ref |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = activator |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = set rLoopingSound to rActivatorRef.GetActivatorSoundLooping set rLoopingSound to GetActivatorSoundLooping TrapGasValve }} ==See Also== *[[SetActivatorSoundLooping]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Activator Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Sound Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the ''Looping'' [[Sound]] of the specified [[Activator]] form. |name = SetActivatorSoundLooping |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = sound |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = activator |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = rActivatorRef.SetActivatorSoundLooping EMTWaterPressure
SetActivatorSoundLooping EMTWaterPressure TrapGasValve }} ==See Also== *[[GetActivatorSoundLooping]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Activator Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Sound Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the '''[[Water Type]]''' of the specified [[Activator]] or [[PlaceableWater]] [[BaseForm]]. If a reference is passed, its base form is used. |name = GetActivatorWaterType |returnType = waterType:baseForm |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = activatorOrPlaceableWater |Type = baseForm |Optional = y }} |example = set rWaterType to rActivatorRef.GetActivatorWaterType
set rWaterType to GetActivatorWaterType DrinkSink01Avg }} ==See Also== *[[SetActivatorWaterType]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Activator Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Water Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the '''Water Type''' of the specified [[Activator]] form. |name = SetActivatorWaterType |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = waterType |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = activator |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = rActivatorRef.SetActivatorWaterType NVCleanWater
SetActivatorWaterType NVCleanWater DrinkSink01Avg }} ==See Also== *[[GetActivatorWaterType]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Activator Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Water Functions (JIP)]]

revision.comment: Don't compare it to IsSpellTarget, it does something else entirely
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns whether or not the calling actor has the specified '''Base Effect''' applied. |name = ActorHasEffect |returnType = hasEffect:1/0 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = baseEffect |Type = form }} |example = set bHasEffect to rActorRef.ActorHasEffect ChemIncSTAlcohol }} ==See Also== *[[HasMagicEffect]] *[[IsSpellTargetAlt]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Copies all visual appearance properties from the specified source '''[[NPC]]''' form to the calling actor. *The function copies race, sex, face geometry (FaceGen data), skin tone, hair (style, length and color), eyes and miscellaneous head/face parts. *The function modifies the calling actor's base form, thus changes will be applied on any instance of the same base form in the game. *When called on the player character, any changes made using this function will be recorded in the game's save file, and will apply on the current game only, whereas changes made to other NPCs' appearance will <u>not</u> be saved, will remain in effect for the duration of the current game session, and will be reset once the game is restarted. *Visual update is already performed by the function, and is therefore not required separately. '''Undoing changes:''' *The function keeps an internal backup of the pre-modified properties. *To undo changes, simply call the function omitting the '''sourceNPC''' argument. |name = CopyFaceGenFrom |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = sourceNPC |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = actorRef.CopyFaceGenFrom CarlitosWayne
actorRef.CopyFaceGenFrom ; Undo changes }}
==See Also== *[[ModifyFaceGen]] *[[DuplicateNPCStats]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:FaceGen Functions]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]]

revision.comment: triggers OnEquip events
revision.text: {{Function |origin = LU |summary = Similar to [[EquipItem]], but allows specification of health and weapon mod flags. Also triggers [[OnEquip]] events. *You can pass -1 as the itemHealth argument to ignore it when matching weapon mod flags. |name = EquipItemData |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = item |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = itemHealth |Type = float |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = modFlags |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = noUnequip |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = noMessage |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }} |example = rActorRef.EquipItemData Weap10mmSubmachineGun -1 5 }} ==See Also== *[[GetInvRefsForItem]] (to check each inventory ref for an Item). *[[GetItemRefCurrentHealth]] (goes well with the above). *[[EquipItemAlt]] / [[UnequipItemAlt]] *[[RemoveMeIRAlt]] / [[RemoveMeIR]] [[Category:Inventory Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category: Object Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the state of a '''Template Data''' flag in an actor base form. |name = GetActorTemplateFlag |returnType = flagSet:1/0 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = flagID |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = actorBase |Type = form |Optional = y }} ==Template Data Flag IDs== 
1	Use Traits
2	Use Stats
4	Use Factions
8	Use Actor Effect List
16	Use AI Data
32	Use AI Packages
64	Use Model/Animation
128	Use Base Data
256	Use Inventory
512	Use Script
|example = set bFlagSet to rActorRef.GetActorTemplateFlag 4 set bFlagSet to GetActorTemplateFlag 4 CarlitosWayne }} ==Notes== This function works on both actors and creatures. ==See Also== *[[SetActorTemplateFlag]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the state of a '''Template Data''' flag in an actor base form. |name = SetActorTemplateFlag |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = flagID |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newState |Type = 0/1 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = actorBase |Type = form |Optional = y }} ==Template Data Flag IDs== 
1	Use Traits
2	Use Stats
4	Use Factions
8	Use Actor Effect List
16	Use AI Data
32	Use AI Packages
64	Use Model/Animation
128	Use Base Data
256	Use Inventory
512	Use Script
|example = rActorRef.SetActorTemplateFlag 4 0 SetActorTemplateFlag 4 0 CarlitosWayne }} ==See Also== *[[GetActorTemplateFlag]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the base karma value of an actor base form. |name = GetBaseKarma |returnType = baseKarma:float |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = actorBase |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = set fBaseKarma to rActorRef.GetBaseKarma
set fBaseKarma to GetBaseKarma CarlitosWayne }} ==See Also== *[[SetBaseKarma]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Karma Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the base karma value of an actor base form. |name = SetBaseKarma |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = baseKarma |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = actorBase |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = rActorRef.SetBaseKarma 400
SetBaseKarma 400 CarlitosWayne }} ==See Also== *[[GetBaseKarma]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Karma Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the '''Body Part Data''' form of a '''Creature''' form.
With later [[:Category:Functions_(JIP)|JIP]] developments, this can also be used with '''NPC''' forms (i.e. humans). In this case, it will always return the '''DefaultBodyPartData''' (formID '''1D'''). |name = GetBodyPartData |returnType = bodyPartData:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = creature |Type = form }} |example = set rBodyPartData to GetBodyPartData CrDogVicious }} ==Notes== *As you can see, this uses base forms, do not run it on actor references. *If using the player's base object (formID '''7'''), this also returns the DefaultBodyPartData. The player reference (formID '''14''') actually uses a special one, '''PlayerBodyPartData''' (formID '''1C'''). ==See Also== *[[SetBodyPartData]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Body-Part Data Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the current [[CombatStyle|'''Combat Style''']] used by an actor. |name = GetCombatStyle |returnType = combatStyle:ref |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = actorBase |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = set rCombatStyle to rActorRef.GetCombatStyle
set rCombatStyle to GetCombatStyle CarlitosWayne }} ==See Also== *[[GetCombatStyleFlag]] *[[GetCombatStyleTraitNumeric]] *[[SetCombatStyle]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the '''Creature Type''' ID of a '''[[Creature]]''' form or a reference. *Returns -1 when called on a NPC. |name = GetCreatureType |returnType = creatureType:int |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = creatureBase |Type = form |Optional = y }} {{Creature_Types}} |example = set iCreatureType to rActorRef.GetCreatureType
set iCreatureType to GetCreatureType CrCentaurEvolved }} ==See Also== *[[GetIsCreatureType]] *[[GetIsCreature]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Creature Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Similar to [[GetEquippedObject]], but returns an '''[[array_var]]''' containing the item form, its health, and weapon mod flags (where applicable). |name = GetEquippedData |alias = GetEqData |returnType = equippedData:array_var |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = equipSlot |Type = int |Value = 0-19 }} |example = let aEquippedData := rActorRef.GetEquippedData 5 }} ==See Also== *[[SetEquippedData]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]
