{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Equips an [[item]] on the [[calling]] [[actor]] using an '''Equipped Data [[array_var]]''' returned by [[GetEquippedData]]. |name = SetEquippedData |alias = SetEqData |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = equippedData |Type = array_var }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = noUnequip |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = noMessage |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }} |example = 
rActorRef.SetEquippedData aEquippedData 0 1
With NVSE '''[[Compiler Override]]''' mode: 
set iArrayID to aEquippedData
rActorRef.SetEquippedData iArrayID 0 1
}} ==See Also== *[[GetEquippedData]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns whether or not the specified '''NPC''' form has the specified buy/sell/service flag set. |name = GetServiceFlag |returnType = flagSet:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = NPC |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagID |Type = int }} ==Service Flag IDs== 
1	Weapons
2	Armor
4	Alcohol
8	Books
16	Food
32	Chems
64	Stimpacks
1024	Misc. Items
131072	Repair
|example = set bFlagSet to GetServiceFlag CliffBriscoe 16 }} ==See Also== *[[SetServiceFlag]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets/un-sets the specified buy/sell/service flag for the specified '''NPC''' form. |name = SetServiceFlag |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = NPC |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagID |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = doSet |Type = 1/0 }} ==Service Flag IDs== 
1	Weapons
2	Armor
4	Alcohol
8	Books
16	Food
32	Chems
64	Stimpacks
1024	Misc. Items
131072	Repair
|example = SetServiceFlag CliffBriscoe 16 1 }} ==See Also== *[[GetServiceFlag]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns whether or not the calling actor's '''[[Race]]''' is in the specified '''[[Form List]]'''. |name = IsRaceInList |returnType = raceInList:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = formList |Type = form }} |example = set bRaceInList to rActorRef.IsRaceInList SomeFormList }} [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the '''Aggro Radius''' of a '''NPC/Creature''' form or a reference. |name = LNGetAggroRadius |returnType = aggroRadius:int |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = actorBase |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = set iAggroRadius to rActorRef.LNGetAggroRadius
set iAggroRadius to LNGetAggroRadius CrDogVicious }} ==See Also== *[[LNSetAggroRadius]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the '''Aggro Radius''' of a '''NPC/Creature''' form or a reference. |name = LNSetAggroRadius |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = aggroRadius |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = actorBase |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = rActorRef.LNSetAggroRadius 1000
LNSetAggroRadius 1000 CrDogVicious }} ==See Also== *[[LNGetAggroRadius]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the '''Template: ActorBase''' of a '''NPC/Creature''' form or a reference. |name = LNSetActorTemplate |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = actorTemplate |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = actorBase |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = rActorRef.LNSetActorTemplate CrDog
LNSetActorTemplate CrDog CrDogVicious }} ==See Also== *[[GetActorTemplate]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the specified '''[[NPC]]''' form's '''[[Race]]'''. *Visual update of loaded references, via [[Update3D]], is required. *This function may have unanticipated side effects. |name = SetRace |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = NPC |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = race |Type = form }} |example = SetRace Benny HispanicOldAged }} ==Notes== *The function doesn't work on the Player. ==See Also== *[[GetRace]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Return the '''Idle Animation''' of the specified '''Anim Object'''. |name = GetAnimObjectIdle |alias = GetANIOIdle |returnType = idleAnim:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = animObject |Type = form }} |example = set rIdleAnim to GetAnimObjectIdle RepairWall01Wrench }} ==See Also== *[[SetAnimObjectIdle]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Animation Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the '''Idle Animation''' of the specified '''Anim Object'''. |name = SetAnimObjectIdle |alias = SetANIOIdle |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = animObject |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = idleAnim |Type = form }} |example = SetAnimObjectIdle RepairWall01Wrench RepairWall02 }} ==See Also== *[[GetAnimObjectIdle]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Animation Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the path to the animation '''.KF''' file of the specified '''Idle Animation'''. |name = GetIdleAnimPath |returnType = filePath:string_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = idleAnim |Type = form }} |example = let sFilePath := GetIdleAnimPath RepairWall02 }} ==See Also== *[[SetIdleAnimPath]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Animation Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the path to the animation '''.KF''' file of the specified '''Idle Animation'''. |name = SetIdleAnimPath |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = idleAnim |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = filePath |Type = string }} |example = SetIdleAnimPath RepairWall02 "Characters\_Male\IdleAnims\3rdP_SpecialIdle_RepairWall01.kf" }} ==See Also== *[[GetIdleAnimPath]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Animation Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the armor class of the specified '''[[Armor]]''' form or, if omitted, of the UpperBody armor of the calling actor. |name = GetArmorClass |alias = GetAC |returnType = armorClass:int |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = armor |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = set iArmorClass to GetArmorClass ArmorMetal set iArmorClass to rActorRef.GetArmorClass }} ==Armor Class== 
0	None
1	Light Armor
2	Medium Armor
3	Heavy Armor
==Notes== * '''None''' (0) is not a valid armor class. It's only returned if a calling actor does not have an UpperBody armor equipped, or if an error occurred while the function was running, i.e. invalid form was passed. ==See Also== *[[SetArmorClass]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Armor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Armor Functions]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the '''Biped Model List''' of the specified '''[[Armor]]''' or '''[[Weapon]]''' form. |name = GetBipedModelList |alias = GetBMList |returnType = modelList:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = armor |Type = form }} |example = set rBPModelList to GetBipedModelList ArmorMetal }} ==See Also== *[[SetBipedModelList]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Armor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Armor Functions]] [[Category:Weapon Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the '''Biped Model List''' of the specified '''[[Armor]]''' or '''[[Weapon]]''' form. |name = SetBipedModelList |alias = SetBMList |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = armor |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = formList |Type = form }} |example = SetBipedModelList ArmorMetal ArmorLeatherList }} ==See Also== *[[GetBipedModelList]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Armor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Armor Functions]] [[Category:Weapon Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]]

revision.comment: Added alias
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Concatenates the elements of the second '''[[array_var]]''' to the first. If either the first or second array are invalid, will do nothing. Will also do nothing if both arrays have different [[TypeOf|types]]. For '''Map''' or '''StringMap'''-type arrays, if '''overrideOldKeys''' is set to non-zero, then the values for the keys from the '''baseArray''' can be overwritten if the '''toAddArray''' has matching keys. |name = ar_Cat |alias = pinto_Cat |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = baseArray |Type = array_var }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = toAddArray |Type = array_var }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = overrideOldKeys |Type = int |Optional = y |Value = Default:0 }} |example = 
ar_Cat aBaseArr aToAddArr
With NVSE '''[[Compiler override| Compiler Override]]''' mode: 
set iBaseArrID to aBaseArr
set iToAddArrID to aToAddArr
ar_Cat iBaseArrID iToAddArrID
}} ==Notes== *Used to only support [[TypeOf|"Array"-type]] arrays, but JIP NVSE v56.34 has fixed this and introduced the ''overrideOldKeys'' argument. ==See Also== *[[Array Variables]] *[[Ar_Append]] will append a single element to an array. *[[Ar_Copy]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns whether or not a dealer stands on a soft 17 in a '''Casino''' form. |name = GetCasinoBJ17Stand |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = casino |Type = form }} |example = set bStand to GetCasinoBJ17Stand TheTopsCasinoData }} ==See Also== *[[SetCasinoBJ17Stand]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Casino Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Casino Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets whether or not a dealer stands on a soft 17 in a '''Casino''' form. |name = SetCasinoBJ17Stand |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = casino |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = value |Type = 0/1 }} |example = SetCasinoBJ17Stand TheTopsCasinoData 1 }} ==See Also== *[[GetCasinoBJ17Stand]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Casino Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Casino Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the blackjack payout ratio of a '''Casino''' form. |name = GetCasinoBJPayout |returnType = payout:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = casino |Type = form }} |example = set fPayout to GetCasinoBJPayout TheTopsCasinoData }} ==See Also== *[[SetCasinoBJPayout]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Casino Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Casino Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the blackjack payout ratio of a '''Casino''' form. |name = SetCasinoBJPayout |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = casino |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = payout |Type = float }} |example = SetCasinoBJPayout TheTopsCasinoData 2.5 }} ==See Also== *[[GetCasinoBJPayout]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Casino Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Casino Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the max winnings value of a '''Casino''' form. |name = GetCasinoMaxWinnings |returnType = maxWinnings:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = casino |Type = form }} |example = set iMaxWinnings to GetCasinoMaxWinnings TheTopsCasinoData }} ==See Also== *[[SetCasinoMaxWinnings]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Casino Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Casino Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the max winnings value of a '''Casino''' form. |name = SetCasinoMaxWinnings |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = casino |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = maxWinnings |Type = int }} |example = SetCasinoMaxWinnings TheTopsCasinoData 12000 }} ==See Also== *[[GetCasinoMaxWinnings]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Casino Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Casino Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the number of decks used for blackjack in a '''Casino''' form. |name = GetCasinoNumDecks |returnType = numDecks:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = casino |Type = form }} |example = set iNumDecks to GetCasinoNumDecks TheTopsCasinoData }} ==See Also== *[[SetCasinoNumDecks]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Casino Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Casino Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the number of decks used for blackjack in a '''Casino''' form. |name = SetCasinoNumDecks |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = casino |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = numDecks |Type = int }} |example = SetCasinoNumDecks TheTopsCasinoData 3 }} ==See Also== *[[GetCasinoNumDecks]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Casino Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Casino Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the number of times a symbol (numbered 1-7) appears on a slot machine reel in a '''Casino''' form. |name = GetCasinoReelStops |returnType = numStops:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = casino |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = symbol |Type = int }} |example = set iNumStops to GetCasinoReelStops TheTopsCasinoData 4 }} ==Notes== *The "W" or winning symbol is #7. The total number of stops on a slot machine reel must equal 14. ==See Also== *[[SetCasinoReelStops]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Casino Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Casino Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the number of times a symbol (numbered 1-7) appears on a slot machine reel in a '''Casino''' form. |name = SetCasinoReelStops |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = casino |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = symbol |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = numStops |Type = int }} |example = 
SetCasinoReelStops TheTopsCasinoData 4 1
Remove 1 stop from symbol 4. 
SetCasinoReelStops TheTopsCasinoData 7 3
Add 1 stop to symbol 7. }} ==Notes== *The "W" or winning symbol is #7. The total number of stops on a slot machine reel must equal 14. ==See Also== *[[GetCasinoReelStops]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Casino Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Casino Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the percentage of the decks to exhaust before shuffling in a '''Casino''' form. |name = GetCasinoShufflePercent |returnType = percToShuffle:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = casino |Type = form }} |example = set fPercToShuffle to GetCasinoShufflePercent TheTopsCasinoData }} ==Notes== *Percentages are a decimal value: 1.0 is 100%. ==See Also== *[[SetCasinoShufflePercent]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Casino Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Casino Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the percentage of the decks to exhaust before shuffling in a '''Casino''' form. |name = SetCasinoShufflePercent |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = casino |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = percToShuffle |Type = float }} |example = SetCasinoShufflePercent TheTopsCasinoData 0.5 }} ==Notes== *Percentages are a decimal value: 1.0 is 100%. ==See Also== *[[GetCasinoShufflePercent]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Casino Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Casino Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the flags of the specified '''[[Challenge]]''' form. |name = GetChallengeFlags |returnType = flags:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = challenge |Type = form }} ==Challenge Flags== 
1	Start Disabled
2	Recurring
4	Show Zero Progress
|example = set iFlags to GetChallengeFlags NVChallengeAnimalControl }} ==See Also== *[[SetChallengeFlags]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Challenge Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Challenge Functions]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the flags of the specified '''[[Challenge]]''' form. |name = SetChallengeFlags |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = challenge |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flags |Type = int }} ==Challenge Flags== 
1	Start Disabled
2	Recurring
4	Show Zero Progress
|example = 
SetChallengeFlags NVChallengeAnimalControl 5
(Sets flags 1 & 4) }} ==See Also== *[[GetChallengeFlags]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Challenge Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Challenge Functions]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

revision.comment: added limitation - does not work properly for dirs inside BSAs
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns whether or not a file (or a folder) with the specified path (relative to the '''Data''' folder) exists. *If the file exists as a '''loose''' file, the function returns '''1'''; If the file is '''packed''' in a BSA archive, the function returns '''2'''. *If the second argument '''isFolder''' is non-zero, the function checks for a folder, otherwise for a file. *A '''[[string_var]]''' may be passed, prefixed with the '''[[ToString|$]]''' operator. *'''Does not work for directories packed inside a BSA archive and will always return 0 when attempting to check for those.''' |name = FileExists |returnType = exists:0/1/2 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = filePath |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = isFolder |Type = 1/0 |Optional = y }} |example = set bExists to FileExists "meshes\ammo\10mmammo.nif" set bExists to FileExists "Textures\jazzisparis" 1 set bExists to FileExists $sFilePath }} ==See Also== *[[GetFilesInFolder]] *[[GetFoldersInFolder]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Utility Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Executes the '''script source''' in the specified file. *The source file path should be relative to the game's root folder (..\Fallout New Vegas\). *The script is executed immediately; The calling script will be halted until the invoked script is processed. *The script source should follow the same structure rules as '''Result Scripts''' (of quest stages, dialogue topics, etc.), and may include local '''variables''' and '''if/else''' statements. *When using forms/references in the script source, the '''Form ID''' must be specified (not the '''Editor ID'''), enclosed in double quotation marks. Example: 
ref rItem
set rItem to "00020420"          ; ArmorCombat

AddItem "00E3778" 1 1            ; WeapNV9mmPistol

if "0010D8DF".GetPlayerTeammate  ; RexREF
endif
*If the function is '''called on a reference''' (implicitly/explicitly), the script will be called on that '''same reference''' (passed as "this" and returned by '''GetSelf'''). Otherwise, it will be called on '''the player''' (see examples, below). *If the source file was successfuly opened and read, and the script was compiled and executed with no errors, the function returns 1. |name = RunBatchScript |returnType = success:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = filePath |Type = string }} |example = RunBatchScript "my_script_source.txt" CraigBooneRef.RunBatchScript "my_script_source.txt" }} ==See Also== *[[RunScriptSnippet]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Script Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Script Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns whether the specified '''Base Item''' form is an '''Armor Addon'''. *The unique type-code 96 reserved for armor addons is actually unused by the game. Armor Addon forms share the same type-code with '''Armor''' forms (24). This function enables to distinguish between the two forms. |name = IsArmorAddon |returnType = armorAddon:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = BaseItem |Type = form }} |example = set bArmorAddon to IsArmorAddon SomeBaseItem }} [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Armor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Armor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the full, extended '''NVSE''' version (version, revision and beta) loaded in the current game session. *This function can also safely be used as an NVSE check, since it is registered in an unused vanilla function opcode that otherwise returns zero. If the user does not have NVSE and JIP LN running, it will simply return 0. If JIP LN and NVSE are running, the full NVSE version is returned, rather than having to concatenate GetNVSEVersion and GetNVSERevision. |name = GetNVSEVersionFull |returnType = fullVersion:float |example = if GetNVSEVersionFull >= 5.02 }} ==Notes== * This function was broken prior to version 57.05. Existing scripts will need to be recompiled. ==See Also== *[[GetNVSEVersion]] *[[GetNVSEBeta]] *[[GetNVSERevision]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:File Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the current difficulty-level of the game, ranging from 0 for '''Very Easy''', to 4 for '''Very Hard'''. |name = SetGameDifficulty |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = difficulty |Type = int |Value = 0-4 }} |example = SetGameDifficulty 3 }} ==See Also== *[[GetGameDifficulty]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Stops/resumes the build up of hardcore needs (food, water and sleep) as game time passes. |name = ToggleHardcoreTracking |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = toggleON |Type = 1/0 }} |example = ToggleHardcoreTracking 0 }} ==See Also== *[[GetHardcoreTracking]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Player Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns whether the calling actor is either fleeing from combat, or is running a Flee-type AI package. |name = IsFleeing |returnType = isFleeing:0/1 |referenceType = reference |example = set bFleeing to actorRef.IsFleeing }} =='''See Also'''== *[[Flee not Combat Package]] *[[ForceFlee]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an '''[[Inventory Reference]]''' of the [[item]] currently equipped by the [[calling]] [[actor]] and using the specified '''Equipment Slot'''. |name = GetEquippedItemRef |returnType = itemRef:ref |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = equipSlotID |Type = int |Value = 0-19 }} ==Equipment Slot IDs== 
0	Head
1	Hair
2	Upper Body
3	Left Hand
4	Right Hand
5	Weapon
6	Pip-Boy
7	Backpack
8	Necklace
9	Headband
10	Hat
11	Eye Glasses
12	Nose Ring
13	Earrings
14	Mask
15	Choker
16	Mouth Object
17	Body Addon 1
18	Body Addon 2
19	Body Addon 3
|example = set rItemRef to actorRef.GetEquippedItemRef 5 }} ==See Also== *[[GetEquippedObject]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Reference Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the current number of rounds in the clip/magazine of the calling actor's equipped weapon. |name = SetCurrentAmmoRounds |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ammoRounds |Type = int }} |example = actorRef.SetCurrentAmmoRounds 1 }} ==See Also== *[[GetCurrentAmmoRounds]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Ammo Functions]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = An advanced version of the vanilla function [[IsAnimPlaying]]. Unlike the latter, which checks whether the calling reference is currently playing a specific [[AnimGroups|Animation Group]], this function allows checking whether '''any''' animation group of a certain category, sub-type and variation is playing. |name = IsAnimPlayingEx |returnType = animPlaying:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = category |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = subType |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = variationFlags |Type = int |Optional = y }} ==1st Argument: Animation Categories== 
1	Miscellaneous
2	Movement
3	Attack
4	Reload
5	Weapon Jam
==2nd Argument (Optional): Animation Sub-Types== '''Miscellaneous:''' 
0	Any
1	Idle
2	(Un-)Equip/Holster
3	Block
4	Handgrip
'''Movement:''' 
0	Any
1	Forward
2	Backward
3	Left
4	Right
5	Jump
'''Attack:''' 
0	Any
1	AttackLeft
2	AttackRight
3	Attack3
4	Attack4
5	Attack5
6	Attack6
7	Attack7
8	Attack8
9	Attack9
10	AttackLoop
11	AttackSpin
12	AttackSpin2
13	AttackThrow
14	AttackThrow2
15	AttackThrow3
16	AttackThrow4
17	AttackThrow5
18	AttackThrow6
19	AttackThrow7
20	AttackThrow8
21	PlaceMine
22	PlaceMine2
23	AttackPower
'''Reload''': N/A '''Weapon Jam''': N/A ==3rd Argument (Optional): Variation Flags== '''Miscellaneous:''' N/A '''Movement:''' 
1	Fast
2	Dodge
4	Turn
'''Attack:''' 
1	Up
2	Down
4	IS
'''Reload''': N/A '''Weapon Jam''': N/A |example = 
set bAnimPlaying to objectRef.IsAnimPlayingEx 2
Returns whether '''objectRef''' is playing any '''Movement''' animation group. 
set bAnimPlaying to objectRef.IsAnimPlayingEx 2 1
Returns whether '''objectRef''' is playing a '''Movement''' animation group of the '''Forward''' sub-type. 
set bAnimPlaying to objectRef.IsAnimPlayingEx 3 10
Returns whether '''objectRef''' is playing an '''Attack''' animation group of the '''AttackLoop''' sub-type. 
set bAnimPlaying to objectRef.IsAnimPlayingEx 3 0 4
Returns whether '''objectRef''' is playing an '''Attack''' animation group of any sub-type, and of the '''IS''' (iron-sights) variation. 
set bAnimPlaying to objectRef.IsAnimPlayingEx 5
Returns whether '''objectRef''' is playing any '''Weapon Jam''' animation group. }} [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Animation Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Attaches an '''Ash Pile''' to the calling actor. Unlike the vanilla [[AttachAshPile]], this function allows specifying an '''Activator''' form to be used for the ash pile model/properties, thus making it possible to create and use custom-made ash piles, in addition to the only two available in the game. *In order to use a custom '''Activator''' form as an ash pile, the base form must first be marked as one, using [[MarkActivatorAshPile]]. |name = AttachAshPileEx |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = activator |Type = form }} |example = actorRef.AttachAshPileEx MyCustomAshPile }} ==See Also== *[[MarkActivatorAshPile]] *[[IsInCriticalStage]] *[[SetCriticalStage]]
[[Category:Savebaked Functions]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Marks the specified '''Activator''' base form(s) as '''Ash Pile'''-type, so that the game treats and handles it accordingly. The form can..."
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Marks the specified '''Activator''' base form(s) as '''Ash Pile'''-type, so that the game treats and handles it accordingly. The form can then be attached to actor references using [[AttachAshPileEx]]. *This remains in effect for the duration of the current game session. Forms must be re-marked once the game was restarted. *The function's argument may be either a single '''Activator''' form, or a '''Form List''' of activator forms. |name = MarkActivatorAshPile |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = activator/list |Type = form }} |example = MarkActivatorAshPile MyCustomAshPile }} ==See Also== *[[AttachAshPileEx]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Activator Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the calling actor's current '''Velocity''' vector in the specified '''Axis'''. *The change applies only to the current frame. *The sign of the velocity value indicates direction: A negative Z velocity equals speeding downwards. *Actors on the ground may be impossible to move horizontally, due to friction. *'''Gravity''' is also applied to the actor: you must supply a velocity equal to at least ([[GetLocalGravity]] '''axis''') in order to cancel it out. |name = SetActorVelocity |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = axis |Type = X/Y/Z }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = velocity |Type = float }} |example = actorRef.SetActorVelocity Z 0 }} ==Notes== {{Template:Velocity Measurement}} ==See Also== *[[GetActorVelocity]] *[[GetActorVelocityAlt]] *[[PushActorNoRagdoll]] *[[GetObjectVelocity]] *[[SetAngularVelocity]] *[[SetLinearVelocity]] *[[SetVelEx]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Movement Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns a value in the range of 0-100, indicating the '''Mass''' of the calling object. The '''Mass''' determines how much the object mov..."
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns a value in the range of 0-100, indicating the '''Mass''' of the calling object. The '''Mass''' determines how much the object moves when hit by a projectile/explosion, or bumped into. A value of 100 translates as no movement. |name = GetRigidBodyMass |returnType = bodyMass:float |referenceType = reference |example = set fBodyMass to objectRef.GetRigidBodyMass }} ==Notes== This function will always return 0 when called on an actor. ==See Also== *[[SetRigidBodyMass]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Similar to [[PushActorAway]], but intended for non-actor, movable objects. "Pushes" the calling reference in a vector-like force and direction specified by the passed arguments. *By default, the '''origin''' of the vector is the calling reference's world position. The 1st through 3rd arguments allow to adjust the origin relative to that position. *The 4th argument specifies the Havok force applied on the object. A '''positive''' value pushes the object <u>away</u> from the origin, whereas a '''negative''' value pulls the object <u>towards</u> the origin. *The 5th argument is optional. If not omitted, the specified reference will be used as the vector '''origin''', instead of the calling reference. |name = PushObject |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = posXmod |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = posYmod |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = posZmod |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = force |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = originRef |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = objectRef.PushObject 100 -50 -200 800 To push the object away from the player by a force of 100, do this: objectRef.PushObject 0 0 0 100 player }} ==See Also== *[[PushActorNoRagdoll]] *[[PushActorAwayAlt]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Movement Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the '''Body Part Data''' of the specified '''Creature''' form. *In order for the visual change to be apparent on loaded references, [[Update3D]] must be used. |name = SetBodyPartData |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = creature |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = bodyPartData |Type = form }} |example = SetBodyPartData CrBrahmin BrahminBodyPartData }} ==See Also== *[[GetBodyPartData]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Body-Part Data Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the '''Skeleton''' model path of the specified '''NPC/Creature''' form. |name = GetSkeletonModel |returnType = modelPath:string_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = actorBase |Type = form }} |example = let sModelPath := GetSkeletonModel CrDogVicious }} ==See Also== *[[SetNPCSkeletonModel]] *[[SetCRESkeletonModel]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the '''Skeleton''' model path of the specified '''NPC''' form. *The 2nd argument '''modelPath''' is optional. If omitted, the model path is reset to the default one defined in the sNPCModelMale\sNPCModelFemale game settings. *In order for the visual change to be apparent on loaded references, [[Update3D]] must be used. |name = SetNPCSkeletonModel |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = npc |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = modelPath |Type = string |Optional = y }} |example = SetNPCSkeletonModel Veronica "Characters\_Male\SkeletonAlt.nif" }} ==See Also== *[[SetCRESkeletonModel]] *[[GetSkeletonModel]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the '''Skeleton''' model path of the specified '''Creature''' form. *In order for the visual change to be apparent on loaded references, [[Update3D]] must be used. |name = SetCRESkeletonModel |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = creature |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = modelPath |Type = string }} |example = SetCRESkeletonModel CrDogVicious "Creatures\Brahmin\Skeleton.nif" }} ==See Also== *[[SetNPCSkeletonModel]] *[[GetSkeletonModel]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns whether the calling actor is currently "stuck" and unable to find a path for reaching the target of their AI package. |name = GetTargetUnreachable |returnType = unreachable:0/1 |referenceType = reference |example = set bUnreachable to actorRef.GetTargetUnreachable }} [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

revision.comment: Link to "NIF NiAVObject Flags" page instead of duplicating (incorrect) data.
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets/Un-sets the specified flag on the specified NIF block in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. *The 4th argument, '''pcModel''', is optional, and may be used when calling the function on the player. The player character has two, completely separate 3D models for 1st and 3rd person. This argument enables to explicitly target one of the models. 
1	3rd Person Model
2	1st Person Model
You can see available flags in [[NIF NiAVObject Flags]] |name = SetNifBlockFlag |alias = SetNifFlag |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = blockName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagID |Type = int |Value = 0-31 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = doSet |Type = 0/1 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = pcModel |Type = 1/2 |Optional = y }} |example = objectRef.SetNifBlockFlag "Bucket01:0" 0 1 }} ==Notes== *The changes are reset once the calling reference's parent cell is reloaded, or [[Update3D]] is called on it. ==See Also== *[[GetNifBlockFlag]] *[[NIF NiAVObject Flags]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an '''array_var''' containing all the '''Cells''' currently buffered in memory. If '''getInteriors''' is non-zero, the array will contain all buffered interiors, otherwise all buffered exteriors. |name = GetBufferedCells |returnType = buffCells:array_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = getInteriors |Type = 0/1 }} |example = let aExteriors := GetBufferedCells 0 let aInteriors := GetBufferedCells 1 }} ==See Also== *[[GetCellBuffered]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Cell Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:Cell Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the calling reference's current '''Velocity''' on the (optional) specified '''Axis'''. If no axis is specified, the total, absolute velocity (product of all three axes) is returned. |name = GetObjectVelocity |returnType = velocity:float |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = axis |Type = X/Y/Z |Optional = y }} |example = set fVelocity to objectRef.GetObjectVelocity
set fVelocityZ to objectRef.GetObjectVelocity Z }} ==Notes== *The sign of the returned value indicates direction: a negative Z velocity indicates the object is speeding downwards. {{Template:Velocity Measurement}} ==See Also== *[[GetLinearVelocity]] / [[GetLinearVelocityAlt]] *[[SetLinearVelocity]] *[[GetActorVelocity]] / [[SetActorVelocity]] *[[SetVelEx]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Movement Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Marks the calling actor as VATS targetable/non-targetable. *Non-tagetable actors will not be selectable in VATS mode. |name = SetVATSTargetable |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = isTargetable |Type = 0/1 }} |example = actorRef.SetVATSTargetable 0 }} ==Notes== *Actors marked as un-targetable will automatically become targetable again once a saved game is loaded.
==See Also== *[[SetVatsTarget]] *[[GetVATSTargets]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:VATS Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the '''Weapon List''' ([[FormList]]) of a '''[[Creature]]''' form. |name = GetCreatureWeaponList |returnType = weaponList:ref |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = baseActor |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = set rWeaponList to LilyRef.GetCreatureWeaponList
set rWeaponList to GetCreatureWeaponList CrSuperMutant1GunA }} [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Creature Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an angle (in degrees) indicating the current direction to which the wind in blowing. 
North	0
East	90
West	-90
South	+/-180
SE	135
NW	-45
(etc.)
|name = GetWindDirection |returnType = windAngle:float |example = set fWindAngle to GetWindDirection }} ==See Also== *[[SetWindDirection]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weather Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weather Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the direction angle to which the wind in blowing. 
North	0
East	90
West	-90
South	+/-180
SE	135
NW	-45
(etc.)
|name = SetWindDirection |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = windAngle |Type = float }} |example = SetWindDirection -120 }} ==See Also== *[[GetWindDirection]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weather Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weather Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Triggers a lightning flash in the game's atmosphere, and plays the associated visual and audial effects. *This function will only work when the player is outdoors and it is raining. |name = TriggerLightningFX |example = TriggerLightningFX }} ==See Also== *[[IsRaining]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weather Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weather Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Similar to the vanilla function [[PlaceAtMe]]. Unlike the latter, does not require a calling reference to use as the spawn location, but instead allows specifying either a '''Cell''', or a '''Worldspace''', and the X/Y/Z position. |name = PlaceAtCell |returnType = placedObj:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = baseObject |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = count |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = cellOrWorld |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = posX |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = posY |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = posZ |Type = float }} |example = set rPlacedObj to PlaceAtCell CrFeralGhoulGlowingOne 2 GSProspectorSaloonInterior 150 -450 3500
set rPlacedObj to PlaceAtCell EncWastelandNVTough 1 WastelandNV 28000 33000 5000 }} [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Cell Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Cell Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the angular velocity, on the specified '''Axis''', of the collision object (if any) attached to the specified node in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. *The velocity is in units of '''2π rad/s''' (essentially, '''revolutions/second'''). |name = GetAngularVelocity |alias = GetAngVel |returnType = velocity:float |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = nodeName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = axis |Type = X/Y/Z }} |example = set fVelocityAngZ to objectRef.GetAngularVelocity "TumbleweedNV" Z }} ==See Also== *[[SetAngularVelocity]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Movement Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the angular velocity, on the specified '''Axis''', of the collision object (if any) attached to the specified node in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. *The velocity is in units of '''2π rad/s''' (essentially, '''revolutions/second'''). |name = SetAngularVelocity |alias = SetAngVel |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = nodeName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = axis |Type = X/Y/Z }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = velocity |Type = float }} |example = objectRef.SetAngularVelocity "TumbleweedNV" Z 20 }} ==See Also== *[[SetAngularVelocityEx]] *[[GetAngularVelocityAlt]] *[[GetAngularVelocity]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Movement Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Immediately stops any instances of the specified '''Sound''' form that are currently playing. |name = StopSound |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = sound |Type = form }} |example = StopSound DRSGateChainLink01Close }} ==See Also== *[[IsSoundPlaying]] *[[PlaySound]] *[[PlaySound3D]] *[[StopSoundAlt]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Sound Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Sound Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the playback state of the game's current background music: 
0	Not Playing
1	Paused
2	Playing
The '''musicType''' argument may be either a '''Music Type''' form, a '''Media Location Controller''' form, or it may be entirely omitted. *If specified, the function will return the playback state of that particular music type. *If omitted, the function will return the state of the currently playing music (if any). |name = IsMusicPlaying |returnType = musicState:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = musicType |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = set iMusicState to IsMusicPlaying set iMusicState to IsMusicPlaying DefaultExplore set iMusicState to IsMusicPlaying musCtrlGnrcSubtleRural }} ==See Also== *[[SetMusicState]] *[[PlayMusic]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Sound Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Sound Functions]] [[Category:Music]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the playback state of the game's current background music: 
0	Stop Playback
1	Pause Playback
2	Resume Playback
*'''Note:''' The '''Stop Playback''' state will stop music played by [[PlayMusic]], otherwise it will replay the current music from the beginning. |name = SetMusicState |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = musicState |Type = int |Value = 0, 1, 2 }} |example = SetMusicState 1 }} ==See Also== *[[IsMusicPlaying]] *[[PlayMusic]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Sound Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Sound Functions]] [[Category:Music]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the '''Frequency''' of the specified '''NiControllerSequence''' in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. |name = GetAnimSequenceFrequency |alias = GetAnimSeqFreq |returnType = frequency:float |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = sequenceName |Type = string }} |example = set fFrequency to objectRef.GetAnimSequenceFrequency "Forward" }} ==See Also== *[[SetAnimSequenceFrequency]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Animation Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the '''Frequency''' (animation play speed) of the specified '''NiControllerSequence''' in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. *For actor animations, including [[Idle Animations | idle animations]], the sequence name should be a path to the animation's .kf file, including the '''Meshes\''' sub-folder. *Passing "*" as the '''sequenceName''' argument will set the frequency of '''all''' the sequences to the specified value. |name = SetAnimSequenceFrequency |alias = SetAnimSeqFreq |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = sequenceName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = frequency |Type = float }} |example = objectRef.SetAnimSequenceFrequency "Forward" 1.5 objectRef.SetAnimSequenceFrequency "*" 1.5 EasyPeteRef.SetAnimSequenceFrequency "Meshes\Characters\_Male\Locomotion\Male\mtfastforward.kf" 1.5 The following is an example on how one might double an idle animation's speed: string_var svIdleAnimPath ref rIdleAnimation int bDoOnce begin GameMode let rIdleAnimation := MTHitHeadA if SunnyREF.IsIdlePlayingEx rIdleAnimation == 0 SunnyRef.PlayIdleEx rIdleAnimation elseif bDoOnce == 0 ; Okay, idle is being played, we can change the speed now. let svIdleAnimPath := "Meshes\" + GetIdleAnimPath rIdleAnimation SunnyRef.SetAnimSequenceFrequency $svIdleAnimPath 2.0 Sv_Destruct svIdleAnimPath let bDoOnce := 1 endif end }} ==Notes== *For this function to work on [[Idle Animations | idle animations]], it must be called ''while'' the idle is being played. Nothing will happen if you call it before the actor plays the specified idle animation. When the idle animation has stopped, the flag is cleared so you will have to call the function again when the animation is played the next time. *For animations on actors, if the animation does not loop it is limited by the animation's ''stop time''. For example a sped up animation will result in the actor freezing on the last frame until the stop time has been reached. ==See Also== *[[GetAnimSequenceFrequency]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Animation Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Moves the mouse cursor to the specified (X, Y) screen coordinates (in MenuMode only). |name = SetCursorPos |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = coordX |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = coordY |Type = float }} |example = SetCursorPos 300 550 }} ==Notes== *The screen's (0, 0) is at the top-left corner. ==See Also== *[[GetCursorPos]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]]

revision.comment: add missing categories
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = When called on an '''[[Inventory Reference]]''' of a currently equipped item, returns 1 if the item cannot be unequipped by normal means, only via script commands. |name = GetNoUnequip |returnType = noUnequip:0/1 |referenceType = reference |example = 
set rEqBodyArmorRef to actorRef.GetEquippedItemRef 2
if rEqBodyArmorRef
	set bNoUnequip to rEqBodyArmorRef.GetNoUnequip
endif
}} ==See Also== *[[SetNoUnequip]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Reference Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = When called on an '''[[Inventory Reference]]''' of a currently equipped item, sets whether or not the item may be unequipped by normal means, or only via script commands. The effect of this function is save-baked. |name = SetNoUnequip |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = noUnequip |Type = 0/1 }} |example = 
set rEqBodyArmorRef to actorRef.GetEquippedItemRef 2
if rEqBodyArmorRef
	rEqBodyArmorRef.SetNoUnequip 1
endif
}} ==Notes== *The message that plays when the player tries unequipping the "NoUnequip" item is controlled by the [[sCantUnequipGeneric]] gamesetting. *This function also prevents the player from dropping the equipped item by normal means. **The message that plays once this occurs is controlled by the [[sCantRemoveWornItem]] gamesetting. ==See Also== *[[GetNoUnequip]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions (JIP)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the '''Death Item''' form of a '''NPC/Creature''' form. |name = GetDeathItem |returnType = deathItem:form |referenceType = ref..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the '''Death Item''' form of a '''NPC/Creature''' form. |name = GetDeathItem |returnType = deathItem:form |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = baseActor |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = set rDeathItem to LilyRef.GetDeathItem
set rDeathItem to GetDeathItem CrSuperMutant1GunA }} ==See Also== *[[SetDeathItem]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the '''Death Item''' form of a '''NPC/Creature''' form. |name = SetDeathItem |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{Functio..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the '''Death Item''' form of a '''NPC/Creature''' form. |name = SetDeathItem |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = itemID |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseActor |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = LilyRef.SetDeathItem StealthBoy
SetDeathItem DeathItemSupermutant100 CrSuperMutant1GunA }} ==See Also== *[[GetDeathItem]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Given a baseValue, returns it modified by the calling actors' active perk effects of a given entryFunction, as per [[GetNthPerkEntryFunction]]. The calling actor is taken as the reference on which the conditions for the 'Perk Owner' tab are run onto.
Optionally up to two additional forms are to be supplied, for entry functions which use additional condition tabs. The final result is thus the base value modified by all the actors' active perk effects of that entry function which can apply to the forms provided. |name = GetPerkModifier |referenceType = reference |returnType = perkModifier:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = entryFunction |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseValue |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = form1 |Type = ref |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = form2 |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = 
let fPerkModifier := PlayerRef.GetPerkModifier 10 5
10 is the entry function for 'Adjust Gained Skill Points', fPerkModifier would thus normally be 5. If the player has the Educated perk for example, which adds 2 extra skill points per level up, fPerkModifier would then be 7. 
let rWeapon := PlayerRef.GetEquippedObject 5
let fBaseSpread := 1
let fPerkModifier := PlayerRef.GetPerkModifier 34 fBaseSpread rWeapon
34 is the entry function for 'Calculate Gun Spread'. Assuming all perks with such an effect on the player act as multipliers for that value, fPerkModifier would then return the player's spread multiplier due to perks with his current weapon. It could be better to calculate a more proper value for fBaseSpread beforehand, in case some of the player's perks are directly adding or substracting to that value. 
let fPerkModifier := PlayerRef.GetPerkModifier 28 0
28 is the entry function for 'Mysterious Stranger', which can only be enabled if perks are setting it to 1, so the modifier is calculated from a base of 0. ==Notes== *form1 and form2 are necessary on a case by case basis, whether the entry function uses more than one condition tab can be quickly consulted in the GECK. Even if they are unimportant for the result, any dummy reference should be supplied. *As illustrated in the examples, what to use as baseValue and how to interpret the result can depend on the entry function: some are somewhat straightforward, others require gathering some base value beforehand, or to assume a base value for the actor of 0 or 1 in absence of perks. *Typically only the player character can hold perks, so this function is of no use when called on NPCs. However, it does work on active companions, returning adjusted values as per the player's companion perk list. *Additional arguments according to [[Perk Entry Codes]] that accept them are as follows: 
0: Weapon, Target
1: Weapon, Target
2: Weapon, target
3: Weapon
4: Item
5: Weapon
6: Attacker, Attacker's weapon
7: Weapon
8: Weapon, Target
14: Target
15: Attacker
16: Attacker, Attackee
17: Item
21: Target
27: Target
34: Weapon
35: Weapon, Target
36: Weapon, Target
37: Weapon
38: Weapon
39: Weapon
40: Weapon
43: Weapon
49: Weapon
50: Weapon
52: Weapon
53: Weapon
54: Weapon
56: Attacker, Attacker's weapon
57: Weapon
58: Weapon, Target
59: Weapon
60: Weapon
72: Weapon
}} [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Perk Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Perk Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the '''Leveled List''' form from which the calling actor reference was spawned. If not a leveled actor, the function returns 0...."
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the '''Leveled List''' form from which the calling actor reference was spawned. If not a leveled actor, the function returns 0. |name = GetActorLeveledList |returnType = srcLvlList:form |referenceType = reference |example = set rSrcLvlList to actorRef.GetActorLeveledList }} [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an '''[[array_var]]''' containing all the non-hidden '''[[Perk]]s''' currently owned by the player. |name = GetPlayerPerks |returnType = pcPerks:array_var |example = let aPlayerPerks := GetPlayerPerks }} [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Perk Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:Perk Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Unloads and deletes the specified UI component, <u>along with all its children components</u>.
'''This function is only safe to use with UI components dynamically added using [[InjectUIXML]] or [[InjectUIComponent]].''' |name = UnloadUIComponent |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = componentPath |Type = string }} |example = UnloadUIComponent "HUDMainMenu\MyMenuExtension\SomeComponent" }} ==See Also== *[[IsComponentLoaded]] *[[InjectUIComponent]] *[[InjectUIXML]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an '''[[array_var]]''' containing the names ([[string_vars]]) of all the parent nodes of the specified NIF block in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. The first element of the array is the name of the direct parent of the NIF block, the second is its grandparent, and so on, all the way to the top root node. *The 2nd argument, '''pcModel''', is optional, and may be used when calling the function on the player. The player character has two, completely separate 3D models for 1st and 3rd person. This argument enables to explicitly target one of the models. 
1	3rd Person Model
2	1st Person Model
|name = GetNifBlockParentNodes |returnType = parentNames:array_var |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = blockName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = pcModel |Type = 1/2 |Optional = y }} |example = let aParentNames := player.GetNifBlockParentNodes "ProjectileNode" }} ==See Also== *[[GetChildBlocks]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Causes a limb crippling event on the target, damaging the corresponding condition AV to 0, with an optional reference as the responsible for it. |name = CrippleLimb |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = limb |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = source |Type = ref |Optional = y }} The limb codes are the corresponding [[Actor_Value_Codes]] minus 25, that is: 
0	Head
1	Torso
2	Left Arm
3	Right Arm
4	Left Leg
5	Right Leg
6	Brain/Frenzy
|example = 
actorRef.CrippleLimb 0
Cripples the actor's head. 
actorRef.CrippleLimb 1 PlayerRef
Cripples the actor's torso. The message notification will appear on screen, as in "actorRef's torso crippled". }} ==Notes== *Does not increment crippling challenges, use [[IncrementScriptedChallenge]] to handle them. ==See Also== *[[DismemberLimb]] *[[IsLimbGone]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the object reference under the crosshair. Unlike the NVSE [[GetCrosshairRef]], this function is not limited to objects within activation range. |name = GetCrosshairRefEx |returnType = crosshairRef:ref |example = set rCrosshairRef to GetCrosshairRefEx }} ==Notes== *'''This command has been found to introduce a persistent frame stutter/skip when used. ObjectUnderReticle is recommended instead.''' *If the crosshair ref is Cell Water, this function returns an invalid reference.
==See also== * [[GetCrosshairRef]] * [[ObjectUnderReticle]] * [[CrosshairRefInList]] * [[GetCrosshairWater]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Crosshair Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

revision.comment: Bulleted note and removed empty see also section.
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns whether or not the calling reference can move, or be moved (responds to '''Havok''' physics). |name = IsMobile |returnType = isMobile:0/1 |referenceType = reference |example = set bIsMobile to objectRef.IsMobile }} ==Notes== *This function will always return 1 when called on an actor.
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns whether or not the calling reference can be "grabbed" by the player character. |name = IsGrabbable |returnType = grabbable:0/1..."
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns whether or not the calling reference can be "grabbed" by the player character. |name = IsGrabbable |returnType = grabbable:0/1 |referenceType = reference |example = set bGrabbable to objectRef.IsGrabbable }} [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''UDF Script''' as a handler that will be invoked in the event the player discovers a new location (and unlocks a '''Map Marker'''). When invoked, the script is passed with a single argument: the '''Map Marker''' reference of the discovered location. |name = SetOnLocationDiscoverEventHandler |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }} |example = 
SetOnLocationDiscoverEventHandler OnLocationDiscoverUDF 1
'''Sets''' the OnLocationDiscoverUDF script as a handler. 
SetOnLocationDiscoverEventHandler OnLocationDiscoverUDF 0
'''Removes''' the OnLocationDiscoverUDF script as a handler. }} ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn	OnLocationDiscoverUDF

ref	rMapMarkerRef

begin Function {rMapMarkerRef}

(code)

end
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''UDF Script''' as a handler that will be invoked in the event an '''Item''' is crafted in the '''Recipe Menu'''. When invoked, the script is passed with two arguments: the crafted item's '''Base Form''', and the quantity. |name = SetOnCraftingEventHandler |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = setOrRremove |Type = 1/0 }} |example = 
SetOnCraftingEventHandler OnCraftingUDF 1
'''Sets''' the OnCraftingUDF script as a handler for this event. 
SetOnCraftingEventHandler OnCraftingUDF 0
'''Removes''' the OnCraftingUDF script as a handler of the above. }} ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn	OnCraftingUDF

ref	rItemForm
int	iQuantity

begin Function {rItemForm, iQuantity}

	(code)

end
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns whether a '''Kill Camera''' cutscene is currently being played. |name = IsInKillCam |returnType = inKillCam:0/1 |example = set bInKillCam to IsInKillCam }} ==See Also== *[[GetVATSMode]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = If an enemy's health bar is currently visible in the HUD, this function will return that actor's reference. Otherwise returns null. |name = GetEnemyHealthTarget |returnType = actor:ref |example = 
if GetEnemyHealthTarget
	let rHealthBarActor := GetEnemyHealthTarget
elseif rHealthBarActor
	let rHealthBarActor := 0
endif
}} ==See Also== *[[GetCombatTarget]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Ran on a [[projectile]] [[reference]], returns 1 if the projectile is considered a mine and is currently armed, capable of detonation when an actor comes nearby. In any other case it returns 0. |name = GetMineArmed |returnType = isArmed:bool |referenceType = reference |example = set bMineArmed to projectileRef.GetMineArmed ==Notes== *A mine is any projectile that is explosion-capable, with the alt-trigger flag, and with a detonation radius greater than 0. *This function always returns 0 on [[player]]-placed mines. *To disarm a mine, consider using [[Activate]] on it.
}} [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the global '''RGB colour''' of the specified interface. 
1	HUD - Main
2	HUD - Alt.
3	Terminal
4	Pip-Boy
5	Main/Pause Menu
|name = SetSystemColor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = interfaceID |Type = int |Value = 1-5 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = red |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = green |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = blue |Type = float }} |example = SetSystemColor 1 223 47 223 }} == See Also == *[[GetSystemColor]] *[[GetSystemColorAlt]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the '''Impact Material''' type used when the specified actor gets hit. |name = GetImpactMaterialType |returnType = materialType..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the '''Impact Material''' type used when the specified actor gets hit. |name = GetImpactMaterialType |returnType = materialType:int |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = baseActor |Type = form |Optional = y }} ==Material Type IDs== 
0	Stone
1	Dirt
2	Grass
3	Glass
4	Metal
5	Wood
6	Organic
7	Cloth
8	Water
9	Hollow Metal
10	Organic Bug
11	Organic Glow
|example = set iImpactMat to RexRef.GetImpactMaterialType set iImpactMat to GetImpactMaterialType Rex }} ==See Also== *[[SetImpactMaterialType]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the '''Impact Material''' type used when the specified actor gets hit. |name = SetImpactMaterialType |referenceType = reference |..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the '''Impact Material''' type used when the specified actor gets hit. |name = SetImpactMaterialType |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = materialType |Type = int |Value = 0-11 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseActor |Type = form |Optional = y }} ==Material Type IDs== 
0	Stone
1	Dirt
2	Grass
3	Glass
4	Metal
5	Wood
6	Organic
7	Cloth
8	Water
9	Hollow Metal
10	Organic Bug
11	Organic Glow
|example = RexRef.SetImpactMaterialType 10 SetImpactMaterialType 10 Rex }} ==See Also== *[[GetImpactMaterialType]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the '''Impact Data''' form assigned for the specified '''Impact Data Set''' in the specified '''Material Type''' slot. |name = G..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the '''Impact Data''' form assigned for the specified '''Impact Data Set''' in the specified '''Material Type''' slot. |name = GetImpactDataSetForm |returnType = impactData:form |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = impactDataSet |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = materialType |Type = int |Value = 0-11 }} ==Material Type IDs== 
0	Stone
1	Dirt
2	Grass
3	Glass
4	Metal
5	Wood
6	Organic
7	Cloth
8	Water
9	Hollow Metal
10	Organic Bug
11	Organic Glow
|example = set rImpactData to GetImpactDataSetForm BigHornerDataSet 6 }} ==See Also== *[[SetImpactDataSetForm]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Impact Data Functions (JIP)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the '''Impact Data''' form assigned for the specified '''Impact Data Set''' in the specified '''Material Type''' slot. '''Note:'''..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the '''Impact Data''' form assigned for the specified '''Impact Data Set''' in the specified '''Material Type''' slot. '''Note:''' The 3rd argument (impactData) is optional. If omitted, the specified material slot will be set to null. |name = SetImpactDataSetForm |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = impactDataSet |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = materialType |Type = int |Value = 0-11 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = impactData |Type = form |Optional = y }} ==Material Type IDs== 
0	Stone
1	Dirt
2	Grass
3	Glass
4	Metal
5	Wood
6	Organic
7	Cloth
8	Water
9	Hollow Metal
10	Organic Bug
11	Organic Glow
|example = SetImpactDataSetForm BigHornerDataSet 6 BigHornerFleshImpact }} ==See Also== *[[GetImpactDataSetForm]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Impact Data Functions (JIP)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = When called from a '''OnHit'''/'''OnHitWith''' script block (or the equivalent NVSE event handlers), or from a '''ScriptEffectStart''' bl..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = When called from a '''OnHit'''/'''OnHitWith''' script block (or the equivalent NVSE event handlers), or from a '''ScriptEffectStart''' block in an '''Ammo''' form '''Impact Script''', returns whether the total damage delivered by the current hit had penetrated the actor's '''DT''', or was fully-absorbed by it.
Note that the maximum damage reduction, after deducting DT/DR is, by default, 80% of the total damage, which effectively means that even if a hit was fully-absorbed, a certain fraction of the damage will always pass through. |name = GetArmourPenetrated |returnType = hitPenetrated:1/0 |referenceType = reference |example = set bHitPenetrated to targetRef.GetArmourPenetrated }} ==See Also== *[[GetHitHealthDamage]] *[[GetHitLimbDamage]] *[[GetHitAttacker]] *[[GetHitWeapon]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |originVersion = 54.50 |summary = Returns whether or not the specified flag (determined by flagID) is toggled for the specified '''[[Quest]]''' form. |name = GetQuestFlag |returnType = flagValue:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = quest |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagID |Type = int |Value = 0-7 }} ==Quest Flag IDs== 
0	Is running
1	Is completed
2	Allow repeated conversation topics
3	Allow repeated stages
4	Set running on reset
5	Is shown in Pip-Boy
6	Is failed
7	Unknown
|example = set bIsFlagSet to GetQuestFlag VDeadSea 3 }} ==Notes== *'''Start Game Enabled''' in the GECK or xEdit equals flags 0 and 4. ==See Also== *[[SetQuestFlag]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Quest Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Quest Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |originVersion = 54.50 |summary = Sets/un-sets the specified flag (determined by flagID) for the specified '''[[Quest]]''' form. |name = SetQuestFlag |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = quest |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagID |Type = int |Value = 0-7 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = 0/1 }} ==Quest Flag IDs== 
0	Is running
1	Is completed
2	Allow repeated conversation topics
3	Allow repeated stages
4	Set running on reset
5	Is shown in Pip-Boy
6	Is failed
7	Unknown
|example = SetQuestFlag VDeadSea 3 1 }} ==Notes== *Because quest flags are "baked-in" the save file on the first time the quest has been loaded in the game, changing the flags later on (in the GECK) will have no effect on existing games, thus can potentially cause backward compatibility issues with the mod. This function is the only way to change quest flags after they have been baked-in. *'''Start Game Enabled''' in the GECK or xEdit equals flags 0 and 4. ==See Also== *[[GetQuestFlag]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Quest Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Quest Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |originVersion = 54.50 |summary = An alternate version of the vanilla [[PushActorAway]]: Knocks down (ragdolls) the calling actor, and either pushes it away from, or pulls it towards a 3D coordinate point of '''origin''', relative to the actor's world position. *The 1st argument specifies the Havok force applied on the actor. A '''positive''' value pushes the actor <u>away</u> from the '''origin''', whereas a '''negative''' value pulls it <u>towards</u> the '''origin'''. *Arguments 2 through 4 (origin X/Y/Z modifiers) are all optional and default to 0. |name = PushActorAwayAlt |referenceType = actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = force |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = posXmod |Type = float |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = posYmod |Type = float |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = posZmod |Type = float |Optional = y }} |example = actorRef.PushActorAwayAlt 30 -100 50 -20
actorRef.PushActorAwayAlt -45 0 0 500 }} ==Notes== *Does not work on dead actors
==See Also== *[[PushActorNoRagdoll]] *[[PushObject]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Movement Functions]]

revision.comment: For shotguns, runs each time a pellet hits.
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''[[UDF|UDF Script]]''' as a handler that will be invoked in the event an '''[[Actor]]''' is struck by a '''Hit'''. The optional argument may use an '''[[Actor]]''' or '''[[Form List]]''' for filtering events: *'''target''' - The target '''[[Actor]]''' (taking the hit). *'''target''' - The target '''[[Form List]]''' (taking the hit). When invoked, the script is passed with the struck '''[[Actor]]''' as the [[calling reference]] ("this"). |name = SetOnHitEventHandler |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = target |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = 
SetOnHitEventHandler OnHitUDF 1
'''Sets''' the OnHitUDF script as a handler to be invoked whenever any actor takes a hit (unfiltered). 
SetOnHitEventHandler OnHitUDF 1 FortCaesarRef
...whenever '''FortCaesarRef''' takes a hit. 
SetOnHitEventHandler OnHitUDF 1 AndaleResidentList
...whenever any actor in '''AndaleResidentList''' takes a hit. }} ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn	OnHitUDF

ref	rTarget
int	iHitLocation

begin Function { }

	set rTarget to this
	set iHitLocation to GetHitLocation	; (Optional)

	(code)

end
==Notes== *When explosions trigger this event it can fire up to three times. To detect which of the three possible times use [[GetHitProjectile]] and [[GetType]] on the returned projectile. The function will return 0 on the first firing. On the second firing it will either return the Projectile that hit the actor or the Explosion that hit the actor if it was not a direct hit. On the third firing it will return the Explosion that hit the actor if a Projectile hit the actor on the second firing. *This event runs before the actor takes damage from the hit. It also runs before the on-hit console message from [[Verbose]] occurs. *Unlike the [[OnHit]] blocktype, in the case of Shotgun hits, this event runs for each pellet. ==See Also== *[[SetOnProjectileImpactEventHandler]] *[[GetHit3DData]] *[[GetHitAttacker]] *[[GetHitExtendedFlag]] *[[GetHitHealthDamage]] *[[GetHitLimbDamage]] *[[GetHitLocation]] *[[GetHitProjectile]] *[[GetHitWeapon]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Provides a safe and convenient way to set the '''[[Special Variables|GameHour]]''' global and/or advance game time, without the risk of corrupting the other game time globals. |name = SetGameHour |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = gameHour |Type = float }} |example = 
SetGameHour 13
Sets the current game hour to 1pm. 
SetGameHour 104
Advances 4 days and sets the current game hour to 8am (24 * 4 + 8). }} == Bugs == * This function can freeze or crash the game if the user's TimeScale global value is changed to anything other than the default value of 30. == See Also == *[[ToggleHardcoreTracking]] - to prevent the player from dying from the Hardcore Needs buildup that might happen after using this function. [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Time Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the level (0-100) of the specified game volume type. |name = GetGameVolume |returnType = volumeLevel:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = volumeType |Type = int |Value = 0-5 }} ==Game Volume Types== 
0	Master Volume
1	Footsteps
2	Voice
3	Effects
4	Music
5	Radio
|example = set fMusicVolume to GetGameVolume 4 }} ==See Also== *[[SetGameVolume]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Sound Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Sound Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the level (0-100) of the specified game volume type. *The 2nd argument '''volumeLevel''' is optional and may be omitted, in which case the specified volume type's level will be reset to the value defined in the game's INI file. |name = SetGameVolume |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = volumeType |Type = int |Value = 0-5 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = volumeLevel |Type = int |Value = 0-100 |Optional = y }} ==Game Volume Types== 
0	Master Volume
1	Footsteps
2	Voice
3	Effects
4	Music
5	Radio
|example = SetGameVolume 4 50 }} ==See Also== *[[GetGameVolume]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Sound Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Sound Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the '''Ingestible''' item (if any) applied as '''Poison''' on the weapon currently equipped by the calling actor. |name = GetEquippedWeaponPoison |returnType = ingestible:form |referenceType = reference |example = set rPoisonChem to actorRef.GetEquippedWeaponPoison }} [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Forces the calling actor to move away from the specified reference (1st argument), to a valid position within the specified distance (2nd argument) in game units. The 3rd argument '''flags''' is optional and may be used to toggle movement settings: 
1	Always Run
2	Always Sneak
3	Always Run AND Sneak
|name = MoveAwayFrom |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = target |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = maxDistance |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flags |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = actorRef.MoveAwayFrom rTargetRef 500 actorRef.MoveAwayFrom rTargetRef 500 1 }} ==See Also== *[[TravelToRef]] *[[ForceFlee]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Forces the calling actor to travel (as in a '''Travel''' AI package) to the specified destination reference. The reference must be direct..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Forces the calling actor to travel (as in a '''Travel''' AI package) to the specified destination reference. The reference must be directly reachable to the calling actor, with no teleport doors in-between. The 2nd argument '''flags''' is optional and may be used to toggle movement settings: 
1	Always Run
2	Always Sneak
3	Always Run AND Sneak
|name = TravelToRef |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = target |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flags |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = actorRef.TravelToRef rTargetRef actorRef.TravelToRef rTargetRef 1 }} ==See Also== *[[MoveAwayFrom]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

revision.comment: Reverted edits by [[Special:Contributions/Korri123|Korri123]] ([[User talk:Korri123|talk]]) to last revision by [[User:Jazzisparis|Jazzisparis]]
revision.text: {{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns an approximation (accurate to at least 6 decimal digits) of the '''cosine''' of the specified angle (expressed in degrees). *This function is '''significantly faster''' than either [[cos]] or [[dCos]]. |name = fcos |returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = angle |Type = float }} |example = set fCosine to fcos 71.8027891 }} ==See Also== *[[fsin]] *[[ftan]] [[Category:Math Functions]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Math Functions (JIP)]]

revision.comment: Reverted edits by [[Special:Contributions/Korri123|Korri123]] ([[User talk:Korri123|talk]]) to last revision by [[User:Jazzisparis|Jazzisparis]]
revision.text: {{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns an approximation (accurate to at least 6 decimal digits) of the '''sine''' of the specified angle (expressed in degrees). *This function is '''significantly faster''' than either [[sin]] or [[dSin]]. |name = fsin |returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = angle |Type = float }} |example = set fSine to fsin 71.8027891 }} ==See Also== *[[fcos]] *[[ftan]] [[Category:Math Functions]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Math Functions (JIP)]]
