revision.comment: Reverted edits by [[Special:Contributions/Korri123|Korri123]] ([[User talk:Korri123|talk]]) to last revision by [[User:Jazzisparis|Jazzisparis]]
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an approximation (accurate to at least 6 decimal digits) of the '''tangent''' of the specified angle (expressed in degrees). *This function is '''significantly faster''' than either [[tan]] or [[dTan]]. |name = ftan |returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = angle |Type = float }} |example = set fTangent to ftan 71.8027891 }} ==See Also== *[[fsin]] *[[fcos]] [[Category:Math Functions]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Math Functions (JIP)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an approximation (accurate to at least 6 decimal digits) of the principal value of the '''arc sine''' (expressed in degrees) of t..."
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an approximation (accurate to at least 6 decimal digits) of the principal value of the '''arc sine''' (expressed in degrees) of the specified value. *This function is '''significantly faster''' than [[dASin]]. |name = fasin |returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = value |Type = float }} |example = set fArSine to fasin -0.945518575 }} ==See Also== *[[facos]] *[[fatan]] *[[fatan2]] [[Category:Math Functions]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Math Functions (JIP)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an approximation (accurate to at least 6 decimal digits) of the principal value of the '''arc cosine''' (expressed in degrees) of..."
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an approximation (accurate to at least 6 decimal digits) of the principal value of the '''arc cosine''' (expressed in degrees) of the specified value. *This function is '''significantly faster''' than [[dACos]]. |name = facos |returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = value |Type = float }} |example = set fArCosine to facos 0.945518575 }} ==See Also== *[[fasin]] *[[fatan]] *[[fatan2]] [[Category:Math Functions]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Math Functions (JIP)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an approximation (accurate to at least 6 decimal digits) of the principal value of the '''arc tangent''' (expressed in degrees) o..."
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an approximation (accurate to at least 6 decimal digits) of the principal value of the '''arc tangent''' (expressed in degrees) of the specified value. *This function is '''significantly faster''' than [[dATan]]. *'''Note:''' Due to the sign ambiguity, this function cannot determine with certainty in which quadrant the angle falls only by its tangent value. See [[fatan2]] for an alternative that takes a fractional argument instead. |name = fatan |returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = value |Type = float }} |example = set fArTangent to fatan 2.90421087767 }} ==See Also== *[[fasin]] *[[facos]] *[[fatan2]] [[Category:Math Functions]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Math Functions (JIP)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an approximation (accurate to at least 6 decimal digits) of the principal value of the '''arc tangent''' (expressed in degrees) o..."
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an approximation (accurate to at least 6 decimal digits) of the principal value of the '''arc tangent''' (expressed in degrees) of the specified '''y/x'''. *This function is '''significantly faster''' than [[dATan2]]. *To compute the value, the function takes into account the sign of both arguments in order to determine the quadrant. |name = fatan2 |returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = y |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = x |Type = float }} |example = set fArTangent to fatan2 11.965348816 4.12 }} ==See Also== *[[fasin]] *[[facos]] *[[fatan]] [[Category:Math Functions]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Math Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = *Checks whether the game's executable has the LAA (Large Address Aware) flag enabled, which indicates whether or not the game is patched to be able to use up to 4GB of memory. *Performs an allocation test to determine whether the game's process can actually handle large addresses. Returns: 
0	Executable is NOT LAA enabled
1	Executable is LAA enabled; allocation test FAILED
2	Both tests successful
|name = GetIsLAA |returnType = isLAA:0/1/2 |example = set bIsLAA to GetIsLAA }} [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Utility Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Debugging]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

revision.comment: Jazzisparis moved page [[Troll Functions (JIP)]] to [[Category:Troll Functions (JIP)]]
revision.text: {{Bc|Functions (JIP)}} [[Category:Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the [[ImpactDataSet]] of a weapon form. |name = GetImpactDataSet |returnType = impactSet:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = form }} |example = set rImpactSet to GetImpactDataSet Weap10mmPistol }} ==See Also== *[[SetImpactDataSet]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Marks the calling actor's '''corpse''' as eaten/not eaten (with the '''Cannibal''' perk). *Marking a corpse as eaten will block feeding on it. Conversely, marking it as not eaten will enable feeding on it again. |name = DonnerReedKuruParty |alias = TheOtherOtherWhiteMeat |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = isEaten |Type = 0/1 }} |example = actorRef.DonnerReedKuruParty 1 }} ==See Also== *[[HasBeenEaten]] *[[GetCannibal]] *[[IsAnimPlaying]] *[[HasPerk]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the '''armor class''' of the specified '''[[Armor]]''' form. |name = SetArmorClass |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = armor |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = class |Type = int |Value = 1-3 }} ==Armor Class== 
1	Light Armor
2	Medium Armor
3	Heavy Armor
|example = SetArmorClass ArmorMetal 2 }} ==See Also== *[[GetArmorClass]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Armor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Armor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the first item found in the specified '''[[Form List]]''' that is currently equipped by the calling actor (or 0 if none are equipped). |name = GetEquippedEx |returnType = item:form |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = itemList |Type = form }} |example = set rPickEquipped to actorRef.GetEquippedEx ArmorList }} [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:List Functions]]

revision.comment: Character-only note
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns whether or not a '''Biped slot''' "conflict" exists between the specified '''Armor''' form and one of the armor items currently equipped by the calling Character actor (player/NPC). *A "conflict" in this context means both items use overlapping Biped slots. |name = TestEquippedSlots |returnType = slotConflict:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = armor |Type = form }} |example = set bSlotConflict to actorRef.TestEquippedSlots HatParty }} [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the '''On Hit''' dismemberment setting for the specified '''Weapon''' form. '''On Hit''' setting values: 
0	Normal formula behavior
1	Dismember Only
2	Explode Only
3	No Dismember/Explode
|name = GetWeaponOnHitSetting |returnType = onHitSetting:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = form }} |example = set iOnHitSetting to GetWeaponOnHitSetting WeapNV44Revolver }} ==See Also== *[[SetWeaponOnHitSetting]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the '''On Hit''' dismemberment setting for the specified '''Weapon''' form. '''On Hit''' setting values: 
0	Normal formula behavior
1	Dismember Only
2	Explode Only
3	No Dismember/Explode
|name = SetWeaponOnHitSetting |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = onHitSetting |Type = int |Value = 0-3 }} |example = SetWeaponOnHitSetting WeapNV44Revolver 2 }} ==See Also== *[[GetWeaponOnHitSetting]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the minimum or maximum semi-auto fire delay of the specified [[Weapon]] form. |name = GetWeaponSemiAutoFireDelay |returnType = fireDelay:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = getMax |Type = 0/1 }} |example = set fMinFireDelay to GetWeaponSemiAutoFireDelay WeapNV44Revolver 0 }} ==See Also== *[[SetWeaponSemiAutoFireDelay]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the minimum or maximum semi-auto fire delay of the specified [[Weapon]] form. |name = SetWeaponSemiAutoFireDelay |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = setMax |Type = 0/1 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = fireDelay |Type = float }} |example = SetWeaponSemiAutoFireDelay WeapNV44Revolver 1 0.5 }} ==See Also== *[[GetWeaponSemiAutoFireDelay]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns whether or not the left/right controller stick is disabled. |name = IsStickDisabled |returnType = stickDisabled:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = rightStick |Type = 0/1 }} |example = set bLeftStickDisabled to IsStickDisabled 0 }} ==See Also== *[[SetStickDisabled]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Controller Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Input Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Disables/enables the left/right controller stick. |name = SetStickDisabled |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = rightStick |Type = 0/1 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = setDisabled |Type = 0/1 }} |example = SetStickDisabled 1 1 }} ==See Also== *[[IsStickDisabled]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Controller Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Input Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the dead-zone (0.0 - 1.0) configured for the controller left stick. |name = GetDeadZoneLS |returnType = deadZone:float |example = set fLSDeadZone to GetDeadZoneLS }} ==See Also== *[[SetDeadZoneLS]] *[[GetDeadZoneRS]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Controller Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the dead-zone (0.0 - 1.0) configured for the controller right stick. |name = GetDeadZoneRS |returnType = deadZone:float |example = set fRSDeadZone to GetDeadZoneRS }} ==See Also== *[[SetDeadZoneRS]] *[[GetDeadZoneLS]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Controller Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the dead-zone range for the controller left stick. |name = SetDeadZoneLS |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = deadZone |Type = float |Value = 0.1-0.9 }} |example = SetDeadZoneLS 0.3 }} ==See Also== *[[GetDeadZoneLS]] *[[SetDeadZoneRS]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Controller Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the dead-zone range for the controller right stick. |name = SetDeadZoneRS |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = deadZone |Type = float |Value = 0.1-0.9 }} |example = SetDeadZoneRS 0.3 }} ==See Also== *[[GetDeadZoneRS]] *[[SetDeadZoneLS]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Controller Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an '''[[array_var]]''' containing all currently active [[ImageSpace Modifiers]]. |name = GetActiveIMODs |returnType = activeIMODs:array_var |example = let aActiveIMODs := GetActiveIMODs }} ==See Also== *[[IsImageSpaceActive]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Imagespace Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:Imagespace Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Marks/un-marks an item form (or a list of item forms) as "unique". *Unique items will not be available as optional repair parts sources in the item repair menu. |name = ToggleItemUnique |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = item/list |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = doToggle |Type = 1/0 }} |example = ToggleItemUnique WeapNVMinigunUnique 1
ToggleItemUnique UniqueWeaponsList 1 }} ==See Also== *[[IsItemUnique]] *[[HideItemBarterEx]] - Similar function for barter menu. [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the numeric [[Actor Value Codes|Actor Value Code]] associated with the specified '''Actor Value''' name string. *If the string does not match a name of any valid actor value, the function returns -1. |name = StringToActorValue |returnType = avCode:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = avString |Type = string }} |example = set iAVCode to StringToActorValue "DamageThreshold"
set iAVCode to StringToActorValue $sAVString }} ==See Also== *[[ActorValueToStringC]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Simulates the user clicking on the specified interactable '''UI tile'''. *Unlike [[ClickMenuButton]], which only works with UI buttons performing a particular, hardcoded menu function, this function should work with '''any''' interactable UI tile. |name = ClickMenuTile |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = componentPath |Type = string }} |example = ClickMenuTile "SleepWaitMenu\NOGLOW_BRANCH\SWM_MainRect\SWM_Scrollbar\scrollbar_horiz_left" }} [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the text string in the currently active '''[[ShowTextInputMenu|Text Input Menu]]''' to the specified '''[[String Formatting|formatted string]]'''. Any text already in the menu will be discarded. |name = SetTextInputString |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = textInput |Type = formattedString }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = formatVars(up to 20) |Optional = y }} |example = SetTextInputString "Candide, expelled from the earthly paradise, walked for a long time, weeping, not knowing where..." }} ==See Also== *[[ShowTextInputMenu]] *[[SetTextInputExtendedProps]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:String Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets various extended properties of the currently active '''[[ShowTextInputMenu|Text Input Menu]]'''. The function takes up to five arguments: *'''offsetX''' - Override the horizontal (x) origin of the text input window to the specified value. If zero, use the default origin (center of the screen). *'''offsetY''' - Override the vertical (y) origin of the text input window to the specified value. If zero, use the default origin (center of the screen). *'''minLength (optional)''' - The minimum number of characters the input text must contain before the '''OK''' button may be pressed; '''defaults to 1'''. *'''maxLength (optional)''' - The maximum number of characters the input text may contain, after which further input is ignored. If omitted, or if zero, '''defaults to 32,767'''. *'''switches (optional)''' - A bit-mask of switches, as specified below; '''defaults to 0'''. 
1	Digits only (including point)
2	Pressing 'Enter' closes menu (instead of new line)
|name = SetTextInputExtendedProps |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = offsetX |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = offsetY |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = minLength |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = maxLength |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = switches |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = 
SetTextInputExtendedProps 300 200
Position the text input window at x(300), y(200). 
SetTextInputExtendedProps 0 0 1 10 3
Use default screen position; set minimum length to 1, maximum length to 10; allow digits only, pressing 'Enter' closes window. }} ==See Also== *[[ShowTextInputMenu]] *[[SetTextInputString]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an '''[[array_var]]''' containing all the '''[[faction]]s''' an '''[[Actor]]''' is a member of. *If the optional '''baseActor''' argument is specified, the returned array will contain the default factions of the '''base actor'''. *If the argument is omitted, the returned array will contain all the factions the calling '''actor reference''' is currently a member of. |name = GetFactions |returnType = factions:array_var |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = baseActor |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = let aFactions := RoseOfSharonCassidyRef.GetFactions let aFactions := GetFactions RoseOfSharonCassidy }} ==See Also== *[[GetInFaction]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Faction Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Faction Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an '''[[array_var]]''' of the '''Item List''' of a base form ('''Container'''/'''Actor'''). |name = GetBaseItems |returnType = baseItems:array_var |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = let aBaseItems := GetBaseItems HollowRockContainer
let aBaseItems := containerRef.GetBaseItems
let aBaseItems := GetBaseItems Boomer00F
let aBaseItems := actorRef.GetBaseItems }} ==See Also== *[[BaseGetItemCount]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Switches the Pip-Boy light ON/OFF. *By omitting the argument, the function can be used to return whether the pip-boy light is on, without changing its state. |name = TogglePipBoyLight |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = switchON |Type = 1/0 |Optional = y }} |example = TogglePipBoyLight 1
set bPipBoyLight to TogglePipBoyLight }} [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Light_Functions]] [[Category:Pip-Boy Functions]]

revision.comment: Added to Category:Condition Functions
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns whether '''Hardcore Tracking''' (the gradual build up of hunger, dehydration and sleep deprivation) is enabled or suspended. |name = GetHardcoreTracking |returnType = hcTracking:0/1 |example = set bHardcoreTracking to GetHardcoreTracking }} ==See Also== *[[ToggleHardcoreTracking]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the file path of one of the optional models of the specified '''Weapon''' form. The second argument '''modBitmask''' determines which model is to be returned: 
0	Default model (no mods)
1	Mod 1 attached
2	Mod 2 attached
3	Mods 1+2 attached
4	Mod 3 attached
5	Mods 1+3 attached
6	Mods 2+3 attached
7	Mods 1+2+3 attached
|name = GetWeaponModel |returnType = modelPath:string_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = modBitmask |Type = int |Value = 0-7 }} |example = let sModelPath := GetWeaponModel WeapNVAssaultCarbine 0 let sModelPath := GetWeaponModel WeapGatlingLaser 3 }} ==See Also== *[[SetWeaponModel]] *[[GetWeapon1stPersonModel]] / [[SetWeapon1stPersonModel]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the file path of one of the optional models of the specified '''Weapon''' form. The second argument '''modBitmask''' determines which model is to be set: 
0	Default model (no mods)
1	Mod 1 attached
2	Mod 2 attached
3	Mods 1+2 attached
4	Mod 3 attached
5	Mods 1+3 attached
6	Mods 2+3 attached
7	Mods 1+2+3 attached
|name = SetWeaponModel |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = modBitmask |Type = int |Value = 0-7 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = modelPath |Type = string }} |example = SetWeaponModel WeapGatlingLaser 3 "weapons\2handhandle\lasergatlingFocusOpticsCFFrameGO.nif" }} ==See Also== *[[GetWeaponModel]] *[[GetWeapon1stPersonModel]] / [[SetWeapon1stPersonModel]] *[[SetModelPath]] *[[SetWeaponWorldModelPath]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]]

revision.comment: explosion hit note
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = When called from a '''[[OnHit]]'''/'''[[OnHitWith]]''' script block (or the corresponding NVSE event handlers), or from a '''[[ScriptEffectStart]]''' block in an '''[[Ammo]]''' form '''Impact Script''', will return an '''[[array_var]]''' containing the exact world X/Y/Z coords and/or angles where the target was hit. The second argument '''dataType''' determines which values are returned in the array: 
0	X/Y/Z coords (elements 0-2)
1	X/Y/Z angles (elements 0-2)
2	X/Y/Z coords (elements 0-2) AND angles (elements 3-5)
|name = GetHit3DData |returnType = hit3DData:array_var |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = dataType |Type = int |Value = 0-2 }} |example = 
let aHit3DData := targetRef.GetHit3DData 0
let fHitPosX := aHit3DData[0]
let fHitPosY := aHit3DData[1]
let fHitPosZ := aHit3DData[2]
let aHit3DData := targetRef.GetHit3DData 2
let fHitPosX := aHit3DData[0]
let fHitPosY := aHit3DData[1]
let fHitPosZ := aHit3DData[2]
let fHitAngX := aHit3DData[3]
let fHitAngY := aHit3DData[4]
let fHitAngZ := aHit3DData[5]
}} ==Notes== *For all hits that originated from an [[Explosion]], all array elements will be 0. ==See Also== *[[GetHitHealthDamage]] *[[GetHitLimbDamage]] *[[GetHitAttacker]] *[[GetHitWeapon]] *[[GetHitProjectile]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Movement Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |originVersion = 54.91 |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''[[UDF|UDF Script]]''' as a handler that will be invoked in the event an instance of the specified '''[[Projectile]]''' form hits an object (including terrain). When invoked, the script is passed with the '''Projectile''' [[reference]] as the calling reference ("this"), and no arguments. *Additional data on the projectile reference may be retrieved via a set of functions (see below). |name = SetOnProjectileImpactEventHandler |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = projectileOrList |Type = form }} |example = 
SetOnProjectileImpactEventHandler OnProjectileImpactUDF 1 308BulletProjectile
'''Sets''' the OnProjectileImpactUDF script as a handler to be invoked when an instance of '''308BulletProjectile''' hits an object. 
SetOnProjectileImpactEventHandler OnProjectileImpactUDF 0 308BulletProjectile
'''Removes''' the OnProjectileImpactUDF script as a handler of the above. }} ==Notes== *As the third argument, either a single, or a '''[[Form List]]''' of '''[[Projectile]]''' forms may be passed. ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn	OnProjectileImpactUDF

ref	rProjectileRef
ref	rProjectileBase

; OPTIONAL
ref	rImpactRef
ref	rWeapon
ref	rAttacker

; OPTIONAL
float	fPosX
float	fPosY
float	fPosZ

begin Function {}

	set rProjectileRef to this
	set rProjectileBase to GetBaseObject

	; OPTIONAL - Additional data
	set rImpactRef to GetProjectileRefImpactRef
	set rWeapon to GetProjectileRefWeapon
	set rAttacker to GetProjectileRefSource

	; OPTIONAL - Impact 3D position
	set fPosX to GetPos X
	set fPosY to GetPos Y
	set fPosZ to GetPos Z

	(code)

end
==See Also== *[[GetProjectileRefImpactRef]] *[[GetProjectileRefSource]] *[[GetProjectileRefWeapon]] *[[GetProjectileRefDamage]] *[[SetOnHitEventHandler]] *[[ShowOff:OnProjectileImpact]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |originVersion = 54.91 |summary = Returns the object [[reference]] (if any) that was hit by the calling live-projectile. *This function is designed to be used in a '''[[SetOnProjectileImpactEventHandler|OnProjectileImpact]]''' event-handler script, otherwise it is unreliable. |name = GetProjectileRefImpactRef |returnType = impactObj:ref |referenceType = reference |example = set rImpactRef to projectileRef.GetProjectileRefImpactRef }} ==Notes== *Before using the result from this function, make sure to verify that it passed a valid reference by using [[IsReference]].
==See Also== *[[SetOnProjectileImpactEventHandler]] *[[GetProjectileRefSource]] *[[GetProjectileRefWeapon]] *[[GetProjectileRefDamage]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the required level of the specified '''Perk''' form. |name = SetPerkLevel |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = perk |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = reqLevel |Type = int }} |example = SetPerkLevel Cannibal 8 }} ==See Also== *[[GetPerkLevel]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Perk Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Perk Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns whether the specified '''Note''' has been read in the Pip-Boy. |name = GetNoteRead |returnType = noteRead:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = note |Type = form }} |example = set bNoteRead to GetNoteRead PrimmBisonSteveRansom }} ==Notes== *[[GetHasNote]] *[[SetNoteRead]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Note Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''UDF Script''' as a handler that will be invoked in the event a new '''Note''' is added to the player. When invoked, the script is passed with a single argument: the '''Note''' form that was added. |name = SetOnNoteAddedEventHandler |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }} |example = 
SetOnNoteAddedEventHandler OnNoteAddedUDF 1
'''Sets''' the OnNoteAddedUDF script as a handler. 
SetOnNoteAddedEventHandler OnNoteAddedUDF 0
'''Removes''' the OnNoteAddedUDF script as a handler. }} ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn	OnNoteAddedUDF

ref	rNote

begin Function {rNote}

(code)

end
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JIP)]] [[Category:Note Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''[[UDF|UDF Script]]''' as a handler that will be invoked in the event an '''[[Actor]]''' consumes an '''[[Ingestible]]'''-type item. When invoked, the script is passed with the '''Actor''' as the calling reference ("this"), and a single argument: the item form that was used. |name = SetOnUseAidItemEventHandler |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = itemOrList |Type = form }} |example = 
SetOnUseAidItemEventHandler OnUseAidItemUDF 1 Stimpak
'''Sets''' the OnUseAidItemUDF script as a handler to be invoked when an actor consumes a '''Stimpak'''. 
SetOnUseAidItemEventHandler OnUseAidItemUDF 0 Stimpak
'''Removes''' the OnUseAidItemUDF script as a handler. }} ==Notes== *As the third argument, either a single, or a '''[[Form List]]''' of '''[[Ingestible]]''' item forms may be passed. ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn	OnUseAidItemUDF

ref	rAidItem
ref	rActor

begin Function {rAidItem}

	set rActor to this

	; (Code)

end
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JIP)]] [[Category:Object Functions]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Toggles the menu mode cursor for the HUDMain menu. While toggled, mouse motion will control the cursor position, instead of the camera direction. Just as in true menu mode, the cursor is capable of activating and interacting with UI components (click/mouseover/scroll/drag etc.) that are part of the HUDMain menu. *Cursor mode is automatically switched off upon opening any game menu. *By omitting the argument, the function can be used to return whether cursor mode is active, without making any changes. |name = ToggleHUDCursor |returnType = isToggled:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = toggle |Type = 1/0 |Optional = y }} |example = ToggleHUDCursor 1 set bCursorMode to ToggleHUDCursor }} ==See Also== *[[SetOnMenuClickEventHandler]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets whether mouse wheel scrolling adjusts camera distance from the player character. *By omitting the argument, the function can be used to return whether vanity wheel is enabled, without making any changes. |name = ToggleVanityWheel |returnType = isToggled:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = toggle |Type = 1/0 |Optional = y }} |example = ToggleVanityWheel 0
set bVanityWheel to ToggleVanityWheel }} ==See Also== *[[DisablePlayerControls]] *[[EnablePlayerControls]] *[[GetPlayerControlsDisabled]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Input Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Input Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the threshold value of the specified hardcore needs (dehydration/hunger/sleep deprivation) or radiation stage. |name = GetHardco..."
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the threshold value of the specified hardcore needs (dehydration/hunger/sleep deprivation) or radiation stage. |name = GetHardcoreStageThreshold |returnType = threshold:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = hcStage |Type = form }} |example = set iThreshold to GetHardcoreStageThreshold Water2 }} ==See Also== *[[SetHardcoreStageThreshold]] *[[GetHardcoreStageEffect]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the threshold value for the specified hardcore needs (dehydration/hunger/sleep deprivation) or radiation stage. |name = SetHardcore..."
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the threshold value for the specified hardcore needs (dehydration/hunger/sleep deprivation) or radiation stage. |name = SetHardcoreStageThreshold |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = hcStage |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = threshold |Type = int }} |example = SetHardcoreStageThreshold Water2 300 }} ==See Also== *[[GetHardcoreStageThreshold]] *[[SetHardcoreStageEffect]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the sickness '''Actor Effect''' associated with the specified hardcore needs (dehydration/hunger/sleep deprivation) or radiation stage. |name = GetHardcoreStageEffect |returnType = sicknessEff:form |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = hcStage |Type = form }} |example = set rSicknessEff to GetHardcoreStageEffect Water2 }} ==See Also== *[[SetHardcoreStageEffect]] *[[GetHardcoreStageThreshold]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the sickness '''Actor Effect''' associated with the specified hardcore needs (dehydration/hunger/sleep deprivation) or radiation stage. |name = SetHardcoreStageEffect |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = hcStage |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = sicknessEff |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = SetHardcoreStageEffect Water2 Dehydration300 }} ==See Also== *[[GetHardcoreStageEffect]] *[[SetHardcoreStageThreshold]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the '''[[Creature]]''' form (if any) used as the creature sounds template for the specified '''Creature''' form. |name = GetCreatureSoundsTemplate |returnType = soundsTemplate:form |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = baseCreature |Type = form }} |example = set rSoundsTemplate to GetCreatureSoundsTemplate CrBarkScorpion }} ==See Also== *[[SetCreatureSoundsTemplate]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Creature Functions]] [[Category:Sound Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the '''[[Creature]]''' form used as the creature sounds template for the specified '''Creature''' form. *The second argument soundsTemplate may be omitted, for clearing the sounds template. |name = SetCreatureSoundsTemplate |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = baseCreature |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = soundsTemplate |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = SetCreatureSoundsTemplate CrBarkScorpion AudioTemplateBarkScorpion }} ==See Also== *[[GetCreatureSoundsTemplate]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Creature Functions]] [[Category:Sound Functions]]

revision.comment: document how it prevents stuff from loading in
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Toggles a special game mode, where: *Game-time ceases to advance. *'''All''' animations and physics are suspended, except for the player character. The following effects were observed while this mode is enabled: *Game-time does advance when sleeping/waiting, or when fast-travelling. *Actor Effects/ingestibles with a duration are suspended. *Hardcore needs are not incremented. *Quest-/Object-type scripts continue to run as normal; Effect-type script, however, are suspended. *Weapons with an ammo regen rate will still have their ammo regenerate. See [[FreezeAmmoRegen]] to counter this. *Neither lighting settings not IMODs are updated when traversing between cells. '''Additional effects are likely, pending further testing.''' |name = FreezeTime |returnType = timeFrozen:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = toggle |Type = 1/0 |Optional = y }} |example = FreezeTime 1 set bTimeFrozen to FreezeTime }} ==Notes== * By omitting the argument, the function can be used to return whether this mode is enabled, without making any changes. * This function is session-persistent. ==Bugs== * If time is unfrozen during the NVSE '''LoadGame''' event, the game will crash. Unfreezing time can also cause the console menu to crash. * Prevents things from loading properly when entering a new cell while time is frozen. [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Time Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the '''[[Fallout Havok Materials|material]] type''' of the solid body that was hit by the calling '''live-projectile'''. *This function is designed to be used in a '''[[SetOnProjectileImpactEventHandler|OnProjectileImpact]]''' event-handler script, and is otherwise unreliable. |name = GetProjectileRefImpactMaterial |returnType = materialType:int |referenceType = reference |example = set iMaterialType to projectileRef.GetProjectileRefImpactMaterial }} ==Material Types== 
0	Stone
1	Dirt
2	Grass
3	Glass
4	Metal
5	Wood
6	Organic
7	Cloth
8	Water
9	Hollow Metal
==See Also== *[[SetOnProjectileImpactEventHandler]] *[[GetProjectileRefImpactRef]] *[[GetProjectileRefSource]] *[[GetProjectileRefWeapon]] *[[GetProjectileRefDamage]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Overrides the hardcoded '''Multiplier''' (0.67, by default) defining the rate and maximum penalty to weapon damage when item condition percent falls below the 75% threshold. Above the 75% threshold, this formula is ignored and the '''PenaltyMult''' is just 1.0. The formula: 
PenaltyMult = 1.0 - (0.75 - CondPercent[0, 1.0]) * Multiplier
|name = SetConditionDamagePenalty |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = multiplier |Type = float }} |example = 
SetConditionDamagePenalty 1
The maximum penalty mult, at 0% condition, is 0.25 (i.e. EffectiveDamage = 0.25 * BaseDamage). }} ==See Also== *[[SetWeaponDegradationMult]] *[[SetArmorConditionPenalty]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

revision.comment: passes Weapon baseform
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''[[UDF|UDF Script]]''' as a handler that will be invoked in the event any '''[[Actor]]''' reloads their '''[[Weapon]]'''. When invoked, the script is passed with the '''[[Actor]]''' as the [[calling reference]] ("this"), and a single argument: the weapon [[BaseForm]] that was reloaded. |name = SetOnReloadWeaponEventHandler |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }} |example = 
SetOnReloadWeaponEventHandler OnReloadWeaponUDF 1
'''Sets''' the OnReloadWeaponUDF script as a handler to be invoked when an actor reloads his weapon. 
SetOnReloadWeaponEventHandler OnReloadWeaponUDF 0
'''Removes''' the OnReloadWeaponUDF script as a handler. }} ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn	OnReloadWeaponUDF

ref	rWeapon
ref	rActor

begin Function {rWeapon}

	set rActor to this

	; (Code)

end
==See Also== *[[SetOnFireWeaponEventHandler]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Toggles the visibility of the specified '''Armor Biped Slot''' when in 1st person POV. Normally, '''UpperBody''', '''Left/RightHand''' and '''PipBoy''' are the only biped slots that are visible in 1st person, the rest being hidden. This function enables to override this limitation. *Calling this function will have no immediate effect on items that are already equipped. To apply the changes, simply call [[ReloadEquippedModels]] on the player: '''player.ReloadEquippedModels''' *The 2nd argument may be omitted, in which case the function will only return the visibility state of the specified slot, without making any changes. |name = ToggleBipedSlotVisibility |returnType = isToggled:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = slotID |Type = int |Value = 0-19 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = toggle |Type = 1/0 |Optional = y }} |example = ToggleBipedSlotVisibility 17 1 ToggleBipedSlotVisibility 2 0 set bSlotVisible to ToggleBipedSlotVisibility 10 }} ==Biped Slot IDs== 
0	Head
1	Hair
2	UpperBody
3	LeftHand
4	RightHand
5	Weapon
6	PipBoy
7	Backpack
8	Necklace
9	Headband
10	Hat
11	EyeGlasses
12	NoseRing
13	Earrings
14	Mask
15	Choker
16	MouthObject
17	BodyAddon1
18	BodyAddon2
19	BodyAddon3
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Armor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Armor Functions]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = A specialized version of [[Update3D]] designed specifically for the player character. This function will fully reload the player's 3D, including skeleton model, collision and animations. |name = UpdatePlayer3D |example = UpdatePlayer3D }} ==See Also== *[[Update3D]] *[[ReloadEquippedModels]] *[[Reset3DState]] *[[AttachModel]] *[[InsertNode]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |originVersion = 55.26 |summary = An alternate version of [[PushActorAway]]/[[PushActorAwayAlt]]. Pushes the calling actor in a specified direction, <u>'''without'''</u> knocking it down or ragdoll-ing it.
The function takes 3 arguments: *The 1st argument specifies the Havok '''force''' applied on the actor. A '''positive''' value pushes the actor <u>away</u>, whereas a '''negative''' value pulls it <u>towards</u> the opposite direction. *The 2nd argument specifies the '''direction''', as a global '''Z-angle'''. *The 3rd argument (optional) may alternatively be used to specify the direction as relative to the position of an '''object reference'''. |name = PushActorNoRagdoll |referenceType = actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = force |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = directionAng |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = originRef |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = actorRef.PushActorNoRagdoll 10 -60
actorRef.PushActorNoRagdoll 15 0 player }}
==See Also== *[[PushActorAwayAlt]] *[[PushObject]] *[[SetActorVelocity]] / [[GetActorVelocity]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Movement Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |originVersion = 55.26 |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''UDF Script''' as a handler that will be invoked in the event any '''Actor''' is "ragdolled" (i.e. upon death, paralysis, knockdown, low fatigue, or unconsciousness). To check if an actor is unconscious, use [[GetLifeState]]. When invoked, the script is passed with the '''Actor''' as the calling reference ("this"). |name = SetOnRagdollEventHandler |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }} |example = 
SetOnRagdollEventHandler OnRagdollUDF 1
'''Sets''' the OnRagdollUDF script as a handler to be invoked when an actor is ragdolled. 
SetOnRagdollEventHandler OnRagdollUDF 0
'''Removes''' the OnRagdollUDF script as a handler. }} ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn	OnRagdollUDF

ref	rActor

begin Function {}

	set rActor to this

	; (Code)

end
==Notes== *Event must be registered in [[GameMode]] block (not [[MenuMode|MenuMode 4]]) as it is flushed every game load. This also means [[GetGameRestarted]] is NOT a viable option to set this event for the session. Instead consider using [[GetGameLoaded]] to (re)set the event handler. [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Gradually changes the value of the specified UI component trait, first setting it to '''startValue''', then gradually increases/decreases it toward '''endValue''', in '''duration''' seconds (unaffected by Time Mult ([[GetGlobalTimeMultiplier]])). This enables creating simple animations using UI tiles. *The 1st argument '''componentPath''' should be of the format "MenuName\TileName\...\TraitName", as defined in the XML hierarchy for the particular menu. *The last (5th) argument '''changeMode''' is optional, and may be used to toggle "special" change modes, as follows: 
0	(Default) From startVal to endVal in duration.
1	From startVal to endVal in duration/6, endVal for duration*2/3, back to startVal in duration/6.
2	From startVal to endVal back to startVal, in duration, repeated perpetually.
3	From startVal to endVal, in duration, repeated perpetually.
|name = SetUIFloatGradual |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = componentPath |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = startValue |Type = float |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = endValue |Type = float |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = duration |Type = float |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = changeMode |Type = int |Value = 0-3 |Optional = y }} |example = 
SetUIFloatGradual "HUDMainMenu\Example_Tile\width" 200 50 4
Sets the '''width''' trait to 200; gradually decreases it to 50 over 4 seconds. 
SetUIFloatGradual "ContainerMenu\Example_Tile\alpha" 0 255 6 1
Sets the '''alpha''' trait to 0; gradually increases to 255 over 1 second; remains at 255 for 4 seconds; gradually decreases to 0 over 1 second. 
SetUIFloatGradual "RecipeMenu\Example_Tile\alpha" 0 255 4 2
Sets the '''alpha''' trait to 0; gradually increases to 255, then gradually back to 0, over a total of 4 seconds; repeats perpetually. 
SetUIFloatGradual "LockpickMenu\Example_Tile\rotateangle" 0 6.2831853 5 3
Sets the '''rotateangle''' trait to 0; gradually increases to '''2Π''' over 5 second; repeats perpetually. When using modes 2 or 3 (both of which repeat perpetually), in order to stop the animation, call the function again while omitting all but 1st argument '''componentPath''': 
SetUIFloatGradual "RecipeMenu\Example_Tile\alpha"
Alternatively, do include the 2nd argument '''startValue''', in which case in addition to stopping the animation, the trait will also be set to that value: 
SetUIFloatGradual "LockpickMenu\Example_Tile\rotateangle" 0
}} ==See Also== *[[SetUIFloatAlt]] *[[GetUIFloatAlt]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Force-closes the current, topmost active menu or, if the optional '''closeAll''' argument is non-zero, closes '''ALL''' currently open menus. *Unlike [[Con_CloseAllMenus]], which is partially broken, this function should be considered safe to use. |name = CloseActiveMenu |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = closeAll |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }} |example = CloseActiveMenu CloseActiveMenu 1 }} ==Notes== * This function is bugged in JIP LN 57.30. Calling this function while the Pip-Boy is open will prevent the Pip-Boy from reopening. This issue was patched in JonnyGuitar NVSE 5.19 and [[JIP PP LN]] 57.51 and later. ==See Also== *[[MenuTapKey]] can also be used to close menus. *[[Con_CloseAllMenus]] *[[CloseAllMenus]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Prevents the [[Player]] character from taking health/limb damage, '''and/or''' from dying. The function has 2 modes: 
0	(Disabled)
1	Prevents health from dropping below 1 HP.
2	Prevents taking any health/limb damage.
*By omitting the argument, the function can be used to return current immortal mode, without making any changes. |name = ToggleImmortalMode |returnType = currMode:0/1/2 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = setMode |Type = 0/1/2 |Optional = y }} |example = ToggleImmortalMode 1 set iImmortalMode to ToggleImmortalMode }} [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the linear velocity on each of the 3 X/Y/Z '''global axes''', of the collision object (if any) attached to the specified node in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. *The velocity is in units of '''0.1 Meter/sec''', or '''7 game units/sec'''. *If the '''optional''' 5th argument, '''setLocal''', is non-zero, the function sets the velocities relative to the object's '''local axes'''. *If the 3D model does not have the specified node, or the node does not have a mobile collision object, the function returns 0. |name = SetLinearVelocity |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = nodeName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = velX |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = velY |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = velZ |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = setLocal |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }} |example = objectRef.SetLinearVelocity "TumbleweedNV" -95 0 70 1 }} ==See Also== *[[GetLinearVelocityAlt]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Movement Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Enables/disables the collision box of the player's camera. *By omitting the argument, the function can be used to return whether camera c..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Enables/disables the collision box of the player's camera. *By omitting the argument, the function can be used to return whether camera collision is enabled, without making any changes. |name = ToggleCameraCollision |returnType = isToggled:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = toggle |Type = 1/0 |Optional = y }} |example = ToggleCameraCollision 0
set bCameraCollision to ToggleCameraCollision }} [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Forces the game to use the specified '''Climate''' type, blocking transitions to other climate types. *Calling either this function with the climate argument omitted, or calling [[SetCurrentClimate]], will unlock and restore normal climate transitions. |name = ForceClimate |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = climate |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = ForceClimate NVDLC02ZionValleyClimate ForceClimate ; Argument omitted - climate transition unlocked. }} ==Notes== *For the new Climate effects to apply immediately, call [[RefreshCurrentClimate]] after calling this function. ==See Also== *[[SetCurrentClimate]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Climate Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns whether the calling actor reference is in at least one of the '''factions''' in the specified '''Form List'''. |name = Ge..."
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns whether the calling actor reference is in at least one of the '''factions''' in the specified '''Form List'''. |name = GetInFactionList |returnType = inFaction:0/1 |referenceType = actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = factionList |Type = FormList }} |example = set bInFaction to actorRef.GetInFactionList MyFactionList }} [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Faction Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns whether the specified form (2nd argument) is found in the specified '''[[Leveled List]]''' (1st argument), including any deep sub-lists contained in it. |name = LeveledListHasFormDeep |alias = LListHasFormDeep |returnType = inList:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = leveledList |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = form }} |example = set bInLvlList to LeveledListHasFormDeep ClutterHouseholdJunkNV TeddyBear01 }} [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Leveled List Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Leveled List Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the calling actor's '''tilt angle''' on the specified '''Axis'''. *'''X''' - Front/Back tilt. *'''Y''' - Left/Right tilt. |name = GetActorTiltAngle |returnType = angle:float |referenceType = actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = axis |Type = X/Y }} |example = set fTiltLeftRight to actorRef.GetActorTiltAngle Y }} ==See Also== *[[SetActorTiltAngle]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Movement Functions]]

revision.comment: Change Math category to Movement category.
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the calling actor's '''tilt angle''' on the specified '''Axis'''. *'''X''' - angle > 0 : Front; angle < 0 : Back. *'''Y''' - angle > 0 : Left; angle < 0 : Right. |name = SetActorTiltAngle |referenceType = actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = axis |Type = X/Y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = angle |Type = float }} |example = actorRef.SetActorTiltAngle X 35
actorRef.SetActorTiltAngle Y -60 }} ==See Also== *[[GetActorTiltAngle]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Movement Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Similar to the vanilla function [[GetRandomPercent]], except allows specifying a range. Returns a random integer in the interval '''min <= x < max'''. |name = GetRandomInRange |returnType = randNum:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = min |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = max |Type = int }} |example = 
set iRandInt to GetRandomInRange -40 121
Returns a random integer between -40 and 120 (inclusive). }} ==See Also== *[[Rand]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Utility Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Math Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the [[Fallout Havok Materials|material]] type of the object/terrain upon which the calling actor is standing. |name = GetGroundMaterial |returnType = materialID:int |referenceType = actor |example = set iMaterial to actorRef.GetGroundMaterial ==Material Type IDs== 
0	Stone
1	Dirt
2	Grass
3	Glass
4	Metal
5	Wood
6	Organic
7	Cloth
8	Water
9	Hollow Metal
}} [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

revision.comment: add missing category
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the effective '''Damage Threshold''' (taking into account item condition) of an armor reference, which may be either an [[Inventory Reference]], or an armor item placed in the world. |name = GetArmorEffectiveDT |returnType = effectiveDT:float |referenceType = reference |example = set fEffectiveDT to armorRef.GetArmorEffectiveDT }} ==See Also== *[[GetArmorEffectiveDR]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Reference Functions]] [[Category:Armor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Armor Functions]]

revision.comment: add missing category
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the effective '''Damage Resistance''' (taking into account item condition) of an [[armor]] reference, which may be either an [[Inventory Reference]], or an armor item placed in the world. |name = GetArmorEffectiveDR |returnType = effectiveDR:float |referenceType = reference |example = set fEffectiveDR to armorRef.GetArmorEffectiveDR }} ==See Also== *[[GetArmorEffectiveDT]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Reference Functions]] [[Category:Armor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Armor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = A modified form of [[AuxiliaryVariableGetFloat]] that works as a [[:Category:Conditions|Condition]]. Returns the float value of the element at index 0 of the specified [[Auxiliary Variables|Auxiliary-Variable]] for the calling ref. The Aux Var's Name is specified in the Parameter window, where one must select a [[:Category:Quests|Quest]] which has a [[Quest scripts|Quest Script]] that contains a [[String Variable]]. The contents of this String will be used as the Name. The Integer field indicates on what index this string variable has in the script. {{Template:ModIndexOverride}} |name = AuxVarGetFltCond |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Quest |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Integer |Type = int }} |conditionFunc = Condition }} ==Notes== *Integer in the parameters is The variable's index (visible in xEdit, NOT directly based on where the variable is line-wise). *The function will return 0 if the variable is not defined / has no element at index = 0 / element is not of type [[float]]. [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Auxiliary-Variable Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Aux Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the calling actor's gravity multiplier (1.0 by default). This multiplier affects drop acceleration when falling. |name = GetActorGravityMult |returnType = gravityMult:float |referenceType = reference |example = set fGravityMult to actorRef.GetActorGravityMult }} ==See Also== *[[SetActorGravityMult]] *[[GetLocalGravity]] *[[SetLocalGravityVector]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Movement Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the calling actor's gravity multiplier (1.0 by default). This multiplier affects drop acceleration when falling. |name = SetActorGravityMult |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = gravityMult |Type = float }} |example = actorRef.SetActorGravityMult 0.2 }} ==See Also== *[[GetActorGravityMult]] *[[GetLocalGravity]] *[[SetLocalGravityVector]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Movement Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Used to check if a specific debug mode is toggled on or off. Previously undocumented. Accepts a value from 0-4, which specifies the debug mode to check: 
0	ToggleGodMode / TGM
1	ToggleAI / TAI
2	TCAI
3	ToggleCollision / TCL
4	TB
|arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = debugMode |Type = int |Value = 0-4 }} |example = GetDebugModeState 3 ;will check if TCL is enabled. }} =='''Notes'''== *None at this time. =='''Reference'''== -lStewieAl, who looked at the function's code and provided these answers. ==See Also== *[[GetDebugMode]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Utility Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Debugging]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Similar to the vanilla [[RewardXP]]. This function rewards the exact specified amount of '''Experience Points''', unaffected by modifiers. |name = RewardXPExact |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = xpAmount |Type = int }} |example = RewardXPExact 50 }} ==See Also== *[[Experience Settings]] *[[RewardXP]] *[[GetKillXP]] *[[ResetXP]] *[[GetXPForNextLevel]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Player Functions]]

revision.comment: added note about using the function with caution.
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Clears <u>all</u> dead actors from the current loaded area, <u>with the exception</u> of actors carrying one or more quest items in their inventories, or actors whose base forms are marked as quest items. |name = ClearDeadActors |example = ClearDeadActors }} ==Notes== *This function should be used with caution as it may disable NPCs that are not normally meant to be disabled such as those that respawn (e.g. Yes Man in the Tops). [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an [[Inventory Reference]] of the item that is assigned to the specified hotkey. |name = GetHotkeyItemRef |returnType = itemRef:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = hotkey |Type = int |Value = 1-8 }} |example = set rHotkey4ItemRef to GetHotkeyItemRef 4 }} ==See Also== *[[GetHotkeyItem]] *[[SetHotkeyItem]] / [[SetHotkey]] / [[SetHotkeyItemRef]] *[[SaveHotkeys]] + [[RestoreHotkeys]] *[[ClearAllHotkeys]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Hotkey Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Reference Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = When called from a '''[[OnHit]]'''/'''[[OnHitWith]]''' script block (or the corresponding NVSE event handlers), or from a '''[[ScriptEffectStart]]''' block in an '''[[Ammo]]''' form '''Impact Script''', will return whether the specified extended flag is set for the current hit. |name = GetHitExtendedFlag |returnType = flagSet:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = flagID |Type = int |Value = 0-8 }} ==Extended Flag IDs== 
0	Target Was Blocking
1	Critical Hit
2	Fatal Hit (target killed, or knocked out if Essential)
3	Limb Dismembered
4	Limb Exploded
5	Limb Crippled
6	Target's Weapon Broke
7	Sneak Attack
8	Explosion Hit
|example = set bFatalHit to targetRef.GetHitExtendedFlag 2 }} ==Notes== *When an automatic melee weapon hits a limb that has been severed at any point, GetHitExtendedFlag 3 will always return 1. ==See Also== *[[GetHitHealthDamage]] *[[GetHitLimbDamage]] *[[GetHitAttacker]] *[[GetHitWeapon]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an '''[[array_var]]''' containing the file paths ([[string_var]]s) of the textures in the texture set of the specified NIF block, in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. *If the specified block is not found, or does not have a texture set, the function returns a null array. Otherwise, the function returns an array of 6 elements, as the number of slots in a texture set. |name = GetBlockTextureSet |returnType = textures:array_var |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = blockName |Type = string }} |example = let aTextures := player.GetBlockTextureSet "9mmSMG:0" }} [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |originVersion = 56.08 |summary = Returns whether or not the calling reference's equipped weapon has a weapon mod, of the specified effect type, attached. |name = EquippedWeaponHasModType |returnType = hasModType:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = modType |Type = int |Value = 1-16 }} ==Weapon Mod Type IDs== 
1	Increase Damage
2	Increase Clip Capacity
3	Decrease Spread
4	Decrease Weight
5	Regenerate Ammo (Shots)
6	Regenerate Ammo (Seconds)
7	Decrease Equip Time
8	Increase Rate of Fire
9	Increase Projectile Speed
10	Increase Max Condition
11	Silencer
12	Split Beam
13	VATS Bonus
14	Increase Zoom (Scope)
16	Suppressor
|example = set bWeaponSilenced to actorRef.EquippedWeaponHasModType 11 }} ==Notes== *This function can be used as a condition function (where applicable). ==See Also== *[[WeaponHasModType]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |originVersion = 56.08 |summary = Returns the name of the '''NiNode''' pointed at by the reticle (crosshair). |name = GetReticleNode |returnType = nodeName:string_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = maxRange |Type = float |Optional = y }} |example = let sReticleNode := GetReticleNode }} ==See Also== *[[GetReticleTargetLimb]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:Crosshair Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Outputs the XYZ position values of the calling reference into the 3 specified '''script variables'''. |name = GetPosEx |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = resPosX |Type = ScriptVar }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = resPosY |Type = ScriptVar }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = resPosZ |Type = ScriptVar }} |example = 
float fPosX
float fPosY
float fPosZ
objectRef.GetPosEx fPosX fPosY fPosZ
}} ==See Also== *[[GetPos]] *[[SetPosEx]] *[[GetAngleEx]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Movement Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Outputs the XYZ rotation angles ('''in degrees''') of the calling object reference into the 3 specified '''script variables'''. *By default, the function outputs the object's '''extrinsic''' (global) rotation, unless the 4th, optional argument, '''getLocal''', is non-zero, on which case it outputs the object's '''intrinsic''' (local) rotation. |name = GetAngleEx |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = resAngleX |Type = ScriptVar }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = resAngleY |Type = ScriptVar }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = resAngleZ |Type = ScriptVar }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = getLocal |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }} |example = objectRef.GetAngleEx fAngleX fAngleY fAngleZ objectRef.GetAngleEx fAngleX fAngleY fAngleZ 1 }} ==See Also== *[[GetAngle]] *[[SetAngleEx]] *[[GetPosEx]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Movement Functions]]

revision.comment: Updated example.
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Toggles the corner messages that display when crafting items. |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Type = int |Value = 0/1 }} |example = 
ToggleCraftingMessages 0 // Disables the crafting messages.
ToggleCraftingMessages 1 // Re-eanbles the crafting messages.
}} [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Creates a new UI tile from a pre-defined [[Template]], returning 1 on success. Function argument uses the following format:
"componentPath|templateName|altTileName(optional)"
|returnVal = 0, 1 |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = "componentPath|templateName|''altTileName''" }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = formatVars(up to 20) |Optional = y }} |example = let iResult := AddTileFromTemplate "HUDMainMenu/myRect|myTemplate|myNewItem%g" 0 Example XML (for purposes of the example, injected in HUDMainMenu using UIO): 

(...)
Running the example above will add a new child rect under '''myRect''' using '''myNewItem''' definition, and named '''myNewItem0'''. 
UnloadUIComponent "HUDMainMenu/myRect/myNewItem0"
The above will unload newly added component. }} ==See Also== *[[Template]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Disables/enables horizontal and/or vertical mouse movement. <pre>0	Both disabled 1	Horizontal only 2	Vertical only 3	Both..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Disables/enables horizontal and/or vertical mouse movement. 
0	Both disabled
1	Horizontal only
2	Vertical only
3	Both enabled
*By omitting the argument, the function can be used for returning the current mouse movement enable state, without making any changes. |name = ToggleMouseMovement |returnType = movementFlag:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = movementFlag |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = ToggleMouseMovement 1 }} [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Input Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Input Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the ratio between the [[calling reference|calling]] [[actor]]'s inventory weight and their [[Stats List|max carry weight]] as a percentage float (i.e. xx.xx). If the actor's [[CarryWeight]] Actor Value is somehow below 1, this function considers their max carry weight as 1 instead. |name = GetEncumbranceRate |referenceType = [[reference]] |returnType = float |conditionFunc = Both |example = RaulRef.GetEncumbranceRate }} ==Notes== *The player becomes overencumbered when their encumbrance rate is > 100.66%. *This function accounts for the "Modify Light Items" [[Perk Entry Point]]. *This function does '''NOT''' account for the "Get Max Carry Weight" [[Perk Entry Point]]. **This is fixed in version 56.28
==See Also== *[[GetInventoryWeight]] *[[GetActorValue]] *[[IsPlayerOverencumbered]] *[[GetPerkModifier]] (since some perk entries modify carry weight)
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Forces the calling actor reference to holster their weapon, if it was out. |name = HolsterWeapon |returnType = void |referenceType = actorREFR |example = }} ==Notes== *On the player, this function has a temporary effect at best, since the player character goes right back to having the weapon out. *This issue is not present when calling `[[SetWeaponOut]] 0`, so consider using that instead.
==See Also== *[[SetWeaponOut]] *[[IsWeaponOut]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Incomplete}} {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = |name = IsPCInCombat |returnType = int }}
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Attaches an extra scene camera at the specified '''NiNode''' of the calling reference's loaded 3D model. The scene, as viewed from the camera, can then be projected onto either another model block, or an '''image'''-type UI tile, using '''[[ProjectExtraCamera]]'''. The function takes 3 arguments, as follows: *'''cameraName''' - Any unique name that will be used for identifying and referencing the camera. *'''addOrRemove''' - Pass '''1''' to attach, or '''0''' to remove the camera. *'''parentNode''' - A name (case-sensitive) of a '''NiNode''' in the calling reference's 3D model, to which the camera will be attached. ==Notes== *The camera's XYZ position/rotation can be modified using '''[[SetNifBlockTranslation]] / [[SetNifBlockRotation]]''' (the block name is the camera name). *If the camera was successfully attached, the function returns 1 (otherwise, 0). *The function will '''fail''' if the calling reference's 3D model isn't loaded. *The camera will be removed, '''a)''' if the calling reference's 3D is un(re)loaded, or '''b)''' when loading a game. *This function should be considered '''EXPERIMENTAL''', and may change considerably (or be scrapped, altogether) in the future. |name = AttachExtraCamera |returnType = successful:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = cameraName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = addOrRemove |Type = 1/0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = parentNode |Type = string |Optional = y }} |example = set bAttached to objectRef.AttachExtraCamera "mySnigCam" 1 "SnigCamNode" objectRef.AttachExtraCamera "mySnigCam" 0 }} ==See Also== *[[ProjectExtraCamera]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Projects the current scene frame, as viewed from the specified camera, onto either a '''NiTri'''-based geometry block, or an '''image'''-type UI tile. The function takes up to 5 arguments, as follows: *'''cameraName''' - The name of a camera previously attached using [[AttachExtraCamera]]. *'''targetGeometry''' - Either **'''a)''' A name (case-sensitive) of a '''NiTriShape/Strips''' block in the calling reference's 3D model. The block '''MUST''' have either '''BSShaderNoLightingProperty''' or '''BSShaderPPLightingProperty''' property; or **'''b)''' The full path of an '''image'''-type UI tile, prefixed with an asterisk. *'''FoV''' - The camera's '''field-of-view''' angle. The lower this value, the more zoomed-in the rendered image will be (it is recommended not to use FoVs lower than 1, or greater than 10). *'''pixelSize''' (optional) - The resolution (and hence quality) of the rendered image. 256x256, by default. It is not recommended (and, in most cases, not necessary) to use resolutios greater than 1024x1024, especially when calling this function every frame. *'''grayscale''' (optional) - If present and non-zero, the rendered image will be generated in grayscale, otherwise (default) in full-colour. ==Notes== *The camera '''MUST''' be within the current loaded cells grid, or in the same interior cell as the player. *If an image was successfully rendered from the camera and projected onto the target, the function returns 1 (otherwise, 0). *A calling reference is not required when projecting onto a UI tile. *When projecting onto a NiTri block, the image will be wraped according to the mesh UV map. *This function should be considered '''EXPERIMENTAL''', and may change considerably (or be scrapped, altogether) in the future. |name = ProjectExtraCamera |returnType = successful:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = cameraName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = targetGeometry |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = FoV |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = pixelSize |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = grayscale |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }} |example = objectRef.ProjectExtraCamera "mySnigCam" "ProjectScreen" 4.2 ProjectExtraCamera "mySnigCam" "*HUDMainMenu/Cam_Project_Screen" 3.4 512 }} ==See Also== *[[AttachExtraCamera]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Generates UI components from an XML file and attaches them at the specified '''NiNode''' of the calling reference's loaded 3D model. ==N..."
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Generates UI components from an XML file and attaches them at the specified '''NiNode''' of the calling reference's loaded 3D model. ==Notes== *The name of the target '''NiNode''' (1st argument) is <u>case-sensitive</u>. *The '''XML file''' path (2nd argument) is relative to the game's installation path. *If the components were successfully generated and attached, the function returns 1 (otherwise, 0). *The attached components can be manipulated using any of the available (and applicable) UI functions. *Internally, the components are injected into '''HUDMainMenu''' menu root. *The XML must be composed of a single '''rect''' parent, with all the other components as its children. *To remove the attached components, use [[UnloadUIComponent]]. *This function should be considered '''<u>EXPERIMENTAL</u>''', and may change considerably (or be scrapped, altogether) in the future. |name = AttachUIXML |returnType = successful:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = targetNode |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = filePath |Type = string }} |example = objectRef.AttachUIXML "AttachXMLNode" "data\menus\my_file.xml" }} ==See Also== *[[AttachUIComponent]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Generates UI components from a [[formatted string]] and attaches them at the specified '''NiNode''' of the calling reference's loaded 3D..."
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Generates UI components from a [[formatted string]] and attaches them at the specified '''NiNode''' of the calling reference's loaded 3D model. ==Notes== *The name of the target '''NiNode''' (1st argument) is <u>case-sensitive</u>. *If the components were successfully generated and attached, the function returns 1 (otherwise, 0). *The attached components can be manipulated using any of the available (and applicable) UI functions. *Internally, the components are injected into '''HUDMainMenu''' menu root. *The component must be composed of a single '''rect''' parent, with all the other components as its children. *To remove the attached components, use [[UnloadUIComponent]]. *This function should be considered '''<u>EXPERIMENTAL</u>''', and may change considerably (or be scrapped, altogether) in the future. |name = AttachUIComponent |returnType = successful:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = targetNode |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = component |Type = formattedString }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = formatVars(up to 20) |Optional = y }} |example = objectRef.AttachUIComponent "AttachXMLNode" "<rect name=%qAttach_Rect%q><text name=%q%z%q></text></rect>" sTextName }} ==See Also== *[[AttachUIXML]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]]

revision.comment: Inform about Show Off's cached functions
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the value of an INI key found in a file (relative to <u>"Data\Config"</u>) as a float. |name = GetINIFloat |returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = keystring |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = filename |Type = string |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== GetINIFloat "GamePlay:fMeleeDamageMultiplier" "MyFolder\MyFile.ini" Will return the value from the INI file located in "Data\Config\MyFolder\MyFile.ini"
Below is the appropriate INI format that should be in MyFile.ini: [GamePlay] fMeleeDamageMultiplier=3 In this case GetINIFloat would have returned a 3
==Notes== * Reading multiple INI settings with this function can be slow and inefficient, especially during gameplay. If you need to read multiple INI settings, or access them in-game, it's recommended to use [[GetINIFloatOrDefault_Cached]].
* INI files are read from and written to in the Data\config\ folder (as in MCM's INI functions). [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/INI_file#Example Format Example]
* If no filename is specified, the name of the mod the function is called from is used. Subdirectories can be defined in the path, e.g. "subDir\nameOfFile"
* It is likely that if you include a subdirectory, you will go over the parser limit. If this occurs, the script will compile but will not work in-game. This can be solved by passing a string variable instead.
* Writing to an INI file that does not exist will create it along with any non-existent subdirectories in its path.
* A keystring is a string containing both the section and key in the format: "Section:Key" ('\' and '/' may also be used as the delimiter.)
==See Also== *[[GetINIString]] *[[SetINIString]] *[[SetINIFloat]] *[[ReadINIFloatFromFile]] can get INI values for INIs placed outside of the "Data\config" folder. *[[GetINIFloat_Cached]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:INI_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:INI Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

revision.comment: https
revision.text:
