{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the numerical value of an INI key. |name = SetINIFloat |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = keystring |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = value |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = filename |Type = string |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== SetINIFloat "GamePlay:fMeleeDamageMultiplier" 2 "MyFolder\MyFile.ini" Once this function is called it should automatically make an INI file (If not created already) named MyFile.ini in "Data\Config\MyFolder"
The INI file should now contain the following: [GamePlay] fMeleeDamageMultiplier=2
==Notes== * INI files are read from and written to in the Data\config\ folder (as in MCM's INI functions). [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/INI_file#Example Format Example]
* If no filename is specified, the name of the mod the function is called from is used. Subdirectories can be defined in the path, e.g. "subDir\nameOfFile"
* It is likely that if you include a subdirectory, you will go over the parser limit. If this occurs, the script will compile but will not work in-game. This can be solved by passing a string variable instead.
* Writing to an INI file that does not exist will create it along with any non-existent subdirectories in its path.
* A keystring is a string containing both the section and key in the format: "Section:Key" ('\' and '/' may also be used as the delimiter.)
==See Also== *[[GetINIString]] *[[SetINIString]] *[[GetINIFloat]] *[[SetINIValue_Cached]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:INI_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:INI Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]]

revision.comment: I don't think these notes make sense here at all, they're just confusing... This function doesn't use a key-string, nor does it write anything.
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns a '''StringMap''' of all keys in the specified INI section, and their values, with 64k buffer. *INI files are read from and written to in the ..\Data\Config\ folder (as in MCM's INI functions). [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/INI_file#Example Format Example] *The 2nd argument '''filename''' is optional. If omitted, the name of the mod the function is called from is used. *The 3rd argument '''getNumeric''' is optional. If non-zero, the '''values''' of the keys in the section will be returned as floats (rather than as strings). |name = GetINISection |returnType = array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = section |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = filename |Type = string |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = getNumeric |Type = 1/0 |Optional = y }} |example = let aINISection := GetINISection "SectionName"
let aINISection := GetINISection "SectionName" "FilePath\FileName.ini" 1 }} ==See Also== *[[SetINISection]] *[[GetINISectionNames]] *[[GetFilesInFolder]] *[[GetFoldersInFolder]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:INI_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:INI Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

revision.comment: https
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the values of multiple keys in an INI section using a stringmap [[array]]. JIP NVSE Plugin v40 merged Lutana NVSE plugin v19. '''As of JIPv40, This function's syntax was changed from Lutana v19, requiring that you pass the arrays via their IDs (set to integers) if you use the script compiler override (CO).''' |name = SetINISection |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = section |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = keysValuesArrayID |Type = array }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = filename |Type = string |Optional = y }} }}
== Example == array_var arrKeysValues
SetINISection "SectionName", arrKeysValues, "MySubFolder/MyFileName" (or) SetINISection "SectionName", arrKeysValues, "MySubFolder/MyFileName.ini"
== Example with CO == array_var arrKeysValues int iArrKeysValuesID ... set iArrKeysValuesID to arrKeysValues SetINISection section:string iArrKeysValuesID:int filename:string
==Notes== * INI files are read from and written to in the Data\config\ folder (as in MCM's INI functions). [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/INI_file#Example Format Example]
* Subdirectories can be defined in the path, e.g. "subDir\nameOfFile". Note that the .ini file does not need to have the .ini extension in the filepath. This was the case in Lutana and is the case in JIP from version 41.10. Both forward and backward slashes are accepted in the filepath string.
* Writing to an INI file that does not exist will create it along with any non-existent subdirectories in its path.
==See Also== *[[GetINISection]] *[[GetINISectionNames]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:INI_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:INI Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

revision.comment: add funcs to "see also"

revision.text:
{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the Full Name of [[Base Form]] to the provided [[string]]. Works with all form types that have names. If a reference is passed, its BaseForm is used. |name = LNSetName |returnType = void |referenceType = [reference] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = name |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = object |Type = baseForm |Optional = y }} |example = 
TerminalRef.LNSetName "Vault 21 Terminal"
Changes the reference's name to "Vault 21 Terminal." 
LNSetName "Goodsprings Saloon" GSProspectorSaloonInterior
Changes the base form's name to "Goodsprings Saloon." }} ==See Also== *[[LNGetName]] *[[GetName]] *[[SetName]] *[[SetNameEx]] *[[SetActorFullName]] *[[SetFullNameAlt]] *[[SetCellFullName]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Name Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Name Functions]] [[Category:String Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Adds an output form entry to a recipe form. |name = AddRecipeOutputForm |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = recipe |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = output |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = count |Type = int }} |example = AddRecipeOutputForm RecipeCaravanLunch Beer 1 The recipe for Caravan Lunch will now also create 1 beer in addition to 1 Caravan Lunch. }} [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Recipe_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Recipe Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns an [[array]] of [[Item]]s that can be made using a given item. |name = GetFormRecipeOutputs |returnType = createdItemForms:array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = item |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = recipeCategoryToFilterBy |Type = baseform |Optional = y }} |example = GetFormRecipeOutputs SpareParts This will return an array containing NVRepairKit and Lead.
GetFormRecipeOutputs SpareParts ReloadingBenchRecipes This will return an array containing Lead. }} [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Recipe_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:Recipe Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Re-enables button input disabled via DisableButton. Multiple buttons can be specified at once. |name = EnableButton |returnType = none |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Button |Type = int }} |example = EnableButton 15 ; enable the d-pad EnableButton 63487 ; enable all buttons }} {{Controller_Button_Codes}} ==See Also== *[[DisableButton]] *[[IsButtonPressed]] *[[IsButtonDisabled]] *[[GetPressedButtons]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Controller Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Prevents the game from registering input from a button. (Script functions will still register the input.) Multiple buttons can be specified at once. |name = DisableButton |returnType = none |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Button |Type = int }} |example = DisableButton 15 ; disable the d-pad DisableButton 63487 ; disable all buttons }} {{Controller_Button_Codes}} ==See Also== *[[EnableButton]] *[[IsButtonPressed]] *[[GetPressedButtons]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Controller Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

revision.comment: clarified example and added 2 more
revision.text: {{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns a bitfield of buttons currently pressed. |name = GetPressedButtons |returnType = int |example = if (GetPressedButtons == 768) ; Both shoulder buttons pressed (exclusively) endif Executes if both shoulder buttons are pressed, and no other buttons are pressed. if (GetPressedButtons & 768) == 768 ; Both shoulder buttons pressed (and possibly others) endif Executes if both shoulder buttons are pressed, regardless of other buttons. if (GetPressedButtons & 768) ; At least one shoulder button pressed (and possibly others) endif Executes if at least one of the shoulder buttons is pressed, regardless of other buttons. }} {{Controller_Button_Codes}} ==See Also== *[[EnableButton]] *[[DisableButton]] *[[IsButtonPressed]] *[[IsButtonDisabled]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Controller Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Bit_Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns an [[array]] of recipes that use a given [[item]]. |name = GetFormRecipes |returnType = array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ingredientItem |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = recipeCategoryToFilterBy |Type = baseform |Optional = y }} |example = GetFormRecipes SpareParts This will return an array containing RecipeWeaponRepairKit and RecipeBreakdownScrapMetalLead.
example = GetFormRecipeOutputs SpareParts ReloadingBenchRecipes This will return an array containing RecipeBreakdownScrapMetalLead. }} ==See Also== *[[GetFormRecipesAlt]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Recipe_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:Recipe Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = An alternative to [[BuildRef]], this function takes two strings with the plugin name and the hexadecimal formID (without the mod index, i.e. the first two numbers).
As opposed to [[BuildRef]] or [[StringToRef]], this function does not resolve unloaded non-persistent references. |name = GetFormFromMod |alias = GFFM |returnType = form |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = pluginName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = hexFormID |Type = string }} |example = ref rForm
if IsModLoaded "Some Plugin.esp" set rForm to GetFormFromMod "Some Plugin.esp" "AED" ;* "AED" = "000AED", leading zeros can be removed endif }} ==See Also== *[[BuildRef]] *[[GetVariable]] *[[AddScriptVariable]] *[[SetVariable]] *[[SetRefVariable]] *[[EditorIDToFormID]] - Can be used to achieve similar results to this function if the form has an Editor ID.
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:ID Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the category of a recipe form. |name = GetRecipeCategory |returnType = category:baseform |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = recipe |Type = baseform }} |example = GetRecipeCategory RecipeBreakdownScrapMetalLead This will return ReloadingBenchRecipes. }} [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Recipe_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Recipe Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the reputation form assigned to a [[map marker]] [[reference]]. |name = GetMapMarkerRep |returnType = form |returnVal = reputation |referenceType = markerRef |example = set rReputation to rMarker.GetMapMarkerRep Set the check as a variable. 
PowderGangerPlaceMarkerRef.GetMapMarkerRep != RepNVPowderGanger
PowderGangerPlaceMarkerRef.GetMapMarkerRep == RepNVPowderGanger
Checks the reputation as a boolean. }} ==See Also== *[[SetMapMarkerRep]] *[[Reference#Map_Marker|Map Marker Data]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Map_Marker_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Map Functions]] [[Category:Reputation Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the type ID of a [[map marker]] [[reference]], which determines the icon that it displays. |name = GetMapMarkerType |returnType = int |returnVal = typeID |referenceType = markerRef |example = set iType to rMarker.GetMapMarkerType }}
==Map Marker Type IDs== 0	None 1	City 2	Settlement 3	Encampment 4	Natural Landmark 5	Cave 6	Factory 7	Memorial 8	Military 9	Office 10	Town Ruins 11	Urban Ruins 12	Sewer Ruins 13	Metro 14	Vault
==See Also== *[[SetMapMarkerType]] *[[Reference#Map_Marker|Map Marker Data]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Map_Marker_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Map Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the number of entries in a perk's entry list. |name = GetPerkEntryCount |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = perk |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = set count to GetPerkEntryCount ActionBoy
Returns 2 since this perk has 2 ranks. }} ==See Also==
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Perk Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Perk Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the amount of an item required for a recipe. |name = GetRecipeInputCount |returnType = count:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = recipe |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = input |Type = baseform }} |example = GetRecipeInputCount RecipeCaravanLunch Lunchbox Will return 1. }} [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Recipe_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Recipe Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns whether or not the button is currently pressed. |name = IsButtonPressed |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = BitMask |Type = int }} |example = 
if (IsButtonPressed 4)
	; Dpad Left pressed, do something
endif
}} {{Controller_Button_Codes}} ==See Also== *[[EnableButton]] *[[DisableButton]] *[[GetPressedButtons]] *[[IsKeyPressed]] - Similar function, but for keyboard keys. [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Controller Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the '"Show All" Hidden' flag of a [[map marker]], which makes it remain hidden when [[ShowAllMapMarkers]] is called. |name = SetMapMarkerHidden |returnType = void |referenceType = markerRef |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = isHidden |Type = bool }} |example = rMarker.SetMapMarkerHidden 1 }}
==Notes== *If [[ShowMap]] or [[ShowAllMapMarkers]] has been called on the map marker, this function alters its permanent state. Otherwise, the effect expires when the game exits.
==See Also== *[[ShowAllMapMarkers]] *[[ShowMap]] *[[GetMapMarkerVisible]] *[[GetMapMarkerHidden]] *[[Reference#Map_Marker|Map Marker Data]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Map_Marker_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Map Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns an [[array]] of [[item]]s requried for a [[recipe]]. |name = GetRecipeInputForms |returnType = inputBaseForms:array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = recipe |Type = baseform }} |example = GetRecipeInputForms RecipeCaravanLunch Will return an array containing InstaMash, PorkNBeans, Cram, and Lunchbox. }} [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Recipe_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:Recipe Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the [[Reputation]] form assigned to a [[map marker]] [[reference]]. |name = SetMapMarkerRep |returnType = void |referenceType = markerRef |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = reputation |Type = form }} |example = rMarker.SetMapMarkerRep RepNVCaesarsLegion }}
==Notes== * If the reputation is omitted then the current reputation will be removed from the map marker.
==See Also== *[[GetMapMarkerRep]] *[[Reference#Map_Marker|Map Marker Data]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Map_Marker_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Map Functions]] [[Category:Reputation Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the "Can Travel" flag of a map marker. |name = SetMapMarkerTravel |returnType = void |referenceType = markerRef |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = canTravel |Type = bool }} |example = rMarker.SetMapMarkerTravel 0 }}
==Notes== *If [[ShowMap]] or [[ShowAllMapMarkers]] has been called on the map marker, this function alters its permanent state. Otherwise, the effect expires when the game exits.
==See Also== *[[GetMapMarkerTravel ]] *[[Reference#Map_Marker|Map Marker Data]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Map_Marker_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Map Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the type ID of a [[map marker]] [[reference]], which determines the icon that it displays. |name = SetMapMarkerType |returnType = void |referenceType = markerRef |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = typeID |Type = int }} |example = rMarker.SetMapMarkerType 14 }}
==Map Marker Type IDs== 0	None 1	City 2	Settlement 3	Encampment 4	Natural Landmark 5	Cave 6	Factory 7	Memorial 8	Military 9	Office 10	Town Ruins 11	Urban Ruins 12	Sewer Ruins 13	Metro 14	Vault
==See Also== *[[GetMapMarkerType]] *[[Reference#Map_Marker|Map Marker Data]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Map_Marker_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Map Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the "Visible" flag on a [[map marker]]. |name = SetMapMarkerVisible |returnType = void |referenceType = markerRef |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = visible |Type = bool }} |example = rMarker.SetMapMarkerVisible 1 }}
==Notes== *Although the name suggests similar behaviour to [[GetMapMarkerVisible]], this function does not enable fast travel when passed an argument value of 2. **Use [[ShowMap]] or [[SetMapMarkerTravel]] to enable a marker for fast travel. *If ShowMap or [[ShowAllMapMarkers]] has been called on the map marker, this function alters its permanent state. Otherwise, the effect expires when the game exits.
==See Also== *[[GetMapMarkerVisible]] *[[ShowAllMapMarkers]] *[[ShowMap]] *[[Reference#Map_Marker|Map Marker Data]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Map_Marker_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Map Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the amount of a form produced by a recipe. |name = GetRecipeOutputCount |returnType = count:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = recipe |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = output |Type = baseform }} |example = GetRecipeOutputCount RecipeCaravanLunch CaravanLunch Will return 1. }} [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Recipe Functions]] [[Category:Recipe_Functions_(JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns an [[array]] of items produced by a recipe. |name = GetRecipeOutputForms |returnType = outputBaseForms:array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = recipe |Type = baseform }} |example = GetRecipeOutputForms RecipeCaravanLunch Will return an array containing CaravanLunch. }} [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Recipe Functions]] [[Category:Recipe_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the [[Actor_Value_Codes|actor value code]] of a recipe's required skill. |name = GetRecipeRequiredSkill |returnType = actorValueCode:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = recipe |Type = baseform }} |example = GetRecipeRequiredSkill RecipeCaravanLunch Will return 44 (Survival). }} [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Recipe Functions]] [[Category:Recipe_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the skill level required by a recipe. |name = GetRecipeRequiredSkillLevel |returnType = skillLevel:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = recipe |Type = baseform }} |example = GetRecipeRequiredSkillLevel RecipeCaravanLunch Will return 30. (The player needs a Survival skill of 30). }} [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Recipe Functions]] [[Category:Recipe_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the subcategory of a recipe form. |name = GetRecipeSubcategory |returnType = recipeSubCategory:baseform |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = recipe |Type = baseform }} |example = GetRecipeSubcategory RecipeCaravanLunch Will return FoodSubRecipes. }} [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Recipe Functions]] [[Category:Recipe_Functions_(JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Removes an input form entry from a recipe form. |name = RemoveRecipeInputForm |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = recipe |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = input |Type = baseform }} |example = RemoveRecipeInputForm RecipeCaravanLunch Lunchbox The Caravan Lunch recipe will no longer require a Lunchbox to create. }} [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Recipe Functions]] [[Category:Recipe_Functions_(JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Removes an output form entry from a recipe form. |name = RemoveRecipeOutputForm |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = recipe |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = output |Type = form }} |example = RemoveRecipeOutputForm RecipeBreakdown556mm PowderRifle Removes the output form ''PowderRifle'' from recipe form ''RecipeBreakdown556mm''. }} [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Recipe Functions]] [[Category:Recipe_Functions_(JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Replaces an item required for a recipe with another item. |name = ReplaceRecipeInputForm |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = recipe |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = old |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = new |Type = baseform }} |example = ReplaceRecipeInputForm RecipeCaravanLunch InstaMash Bubblegum Caravan Lunch will require Bubblegum instead of InstaMash in the recipe. }} [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Recipe Functions]] [[Category:Recipe_Functions_(JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Replaces an item produced by a recipe with another item. |name = ReplaceRecipeOutputForm |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = recipe |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = old |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = new |Type = baseform }} |example = ReplaceRecipeOutputForm RecipeCaravanLunch CaravanLunch Bubblegum The recipe for Caravan Lunch now creates Bubblegum instead of CaravanLunch. }} [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Recipe Functions]] [[Category:Recipe_Functions_(JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the category of a recipe form. |name = SetRecipeCategory |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = recipe |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = category |Type = baseform }} |example = SetRecipeCategory RecipeCaravanLunch WorkbenchRecipes Caravan Lunch will now be in the Workbench instead of the Campfire. }} [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Recipe Functions]] [[Category:Recipe_Functions_(JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the amount of an item required for a recipe. |name = SetRecipeInputCount |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = recipe |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = input |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = count |Type = int }} |example = SetRecipeInputCount RecipeCaravanLunch Cram 2 Caravan Lunch now requires 2 Crams to create instead of 1 Cram. }} [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Recipe Functions]] [[Category:Recipe_Functions_(JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the amount of an item produced by a recipe. |name = SetRecipeOutputCount |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = recipe |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = output |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = count |Type = int }} |example = SetRecipeOutputCount RecipeCaravanLunch CaravanLunch 2 The Caravan Lunch recipe now creates 2 CaravanLunch instead of 1 CaravanLunch. }} [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Recipe Functions]] [[Category:Recipe_Functions_(JIP)]]

This page lists functions used to create [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/INI_file INI files] for mods. INI files are stored in data/config.
[[Category:INI Functions]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Recipe Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the [[Actor_Value_Codes|actor value code]] of a recipe's required skill. |name = SetRecipeRequiredSkill |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = recipe |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = actorValueCode |Type = int }} |example = SetRecipeRequiredSkill RecipeCaravanLunch 40 Caravan Lunch now requires Science skill to create instead of Survival skill. }} [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Recipe Functions]] [[Category:Recipe_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the skill level required by a recipe. |name = SetRecipeRequiredSkillLevel |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = recipe |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = skillLevel |Type = int }} |example = SetRecipeRequiredSkillLevel RecipeCaravanLunch 45 Caravan Lunch now requires 45 skill points to create instead of 30 skill points. }} [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Recipe Functions]] [[Category:Recipe_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the subcategory of a recipe form. |name = SetRecipeSubcategory |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = recipe |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = category |Type = baseform }} |example = SetRecipeSubcategory RecipeCaravanLunch ChemsSubRecipes Caravan Lunch is now in the Chems sub-menu instead of the Food sub-menu. }} [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Recipe Functions]] [[Category:Recipe_Functions_(JIP)]]
[[Category:Map Functions]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "[[Category:Functions (JIP)]]"
revision.text: [[Category:Functions (JIP)]]

revision.comment: Reverted edits by [[Special:Contributions/Demorome|Demorome]] ([[User talk:Demorome|talk]]) to last revision by [[User:Trooper|Trooper]]
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = LU |summary = [[Event_Handling|Event]] block type that calls the [[User_Defined_Function|UDF]] when the gamepad button is pressed. The pressed button code (int) is passed to the UDF.
Use any Button code after the ":" delimiter. Button code int can be a bitmask of more than one button. |name = SetEventHandler "OnButtonDown:int" |returnVal = buttonID |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = myUDF |Type = userDefinedFunction }} }}
== Example using either "A" or "B" == SetEventHandler "OnButtonDown:12288" MyUDF
scn MyUDF
int keyCode
begin function {keyCode}
end function
== Example using "A" button == SetEventHandler "OnButtonDown:4096" MyUDF
scn MyUDF
int keyCode
begin function {keyCode}
end function
{{Controller_Button_Codes}}
==See Also== * [[OnButtonUp]] * [[Event_Handling]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JIP)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

revision.comment: Reverted edits by [[Special:Contributions/Demorome|Demorome]] ([[User talk:Demorome|talk]]) to last revision by [[User:Trooper|Trooper]]
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = LU |summary = [[Event_Handling|Event]] block type that calls the [[User_Defined_Function|UDF]] when the gamepad button is released. The released button code (int) is passed to the UDF.
Use any Button code after the ":" delimiter. Button code int can be a bitmask of more than one button. |name = SetEventHandler "OnButtonUp:int" |returnVal = buttonID |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = myUDF |Type = userDefinedFunction }} }}
== Example using either "A" or "B" == SetEventHandler "OnButtonUp:12288" MyUDF
scn MyUDF
int keyCode
begin function {keyCode}
end function
== Example using "A" button == SetEventHandler "OnButtonUp:4096" MyUDF
scn MyUDF
int keyCode
begin function {keyCode}
end function
{{Controller_Button_Codes}}
==See Also== * [[OnButtonDown]] * [[Event_Handling]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JIP)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Passes the entered cell to the UDF when the player changes cells. |name = OnCellEnter |returnVal = cell |returnType = form |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = UDFForm |Type = UserDefinedFunction }} |example = SetEventHandler "OnCellEnter" MyUDF SetEventHandler "OnCellEnter" MyUDF "first"::Cell SetEventHandler "OnCellEnter" MyUDF "first"::FormListOfCells }} ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn	 MyUDF
ref	 rEnteredCell
BEGIN Function {rEnteredCell}
(code)
END
==Notes== *Does trigger for exterior cells when crossing cell borders but exterior cell IDs cannot be provided as an argument as it will not trigger. ==See Also== *[[OnCellExit]] *[[Event_Handling]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Cell Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JIP)]] [[Category:Cell Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

revision.comment: fix example
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Passes the exited cell to the UDF when the player changes cells. |name = OnCellExit |returnVal = cell |returnType = form |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = UDFForm |Type = UserDefinedFunction }} |example = SetEventHandler "OnCellExit" MyUDF SetEventHandler "OnCellExit" MyUDF "first"::Cell SetEventHandler "OnCellExit" MyUDF "first"::FormListOfCells }} ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn	 MyUDF
ref	 rExitedCell
BEGIN Function {rExitedCell}
(code)
END
==See Also== *[[OnCellEnter]] *[[Event_Handling]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Cell Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JIP)]] [[Category:Cell Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = [[Event_Handling|Event]] block type that calls the [[User_Defined_Function|UDF]] when the control is pressed, even if the control has been disabled with [[DisableControl]]. The pressed control code (int) is passed to the UDF. Use any control code after the ":" delimiter. |name = SetEventHandler "OnControlDown:int" |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = myUDF |Type = userDefinedFunction }} }} == Example using "attack" control (4) == 
SetEventHandler "OnControlDown:4" MyUDF

scn MyUDF
 
int controlCode
 
begin function {controlCode}
 
end
{{Control_Codes}} ==See Also== * [[OnControlUp]] * [[SetOnControlDownEventHandler]] / [[SetOnControlUpEventHandler]] (uses JIP's event handling system) * [[Event_Handling]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JIP)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = [[Event_Handling|Event]] block type that calls the [[User_Defined_Function|UDF]] when the current ref is no longer in the crosshair. Crosshair (ref) is passed to the UDF. Can be filtered with a form or list, or [[Form_Type_IDs|Form Type]]. OnCrosshairOn and OnCrosshairOff can also be filtered by form type via "OnCrosshairOff:25" etc. |name = SetEventHandler "OnCrosshairOff" |returnVal = crosshairRef |returnType = ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = myUDF |Type = userDefinedFunction }} }}
== Example with no filtering == SetEventHandler "OnCrosshairOff" MyUDF
scn MyUDF
ref rCrosshairRef
begin function {rCrosshairRef}
end function
== Example with filtering by a form == SetEventHandler "OnCrosshairOff" ""::someForm MyUDF
scn MyUDF
ref rCrosshairRef
begin function {rCrosshairRef}
end function
== Example with filtering by formlist == SetEventHandler "OnCrosshairOff" ""::someFormList MyUDF
scn MyUDF
ref rCrosshairRef
begin function {rCrosshairRef}
end function
== Example with filtering by form type 24 (armor) == SetEventHandler "OnCrosshairOff:24" MyUDF
scn MyUDF
ref rCrosshairRef
begin function {rCrosshairRef}
end function
==See Also== * [[OnCrosshairOn]] * [[Event_Handling]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers]] [[Category:Crosshair Functions]]

revision.comment: pass "first" for filter key
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = LU |summary = [[Event_Handling|Event]] block type that calls the [[User_Defined_Function|UDF]] when the a new ref is found in the crosshair. Crosshair (ref) is passed to the UDF. Can be filtered with a form or list. Can also be filtered by [[Form_Type_IDs|Form Type]] via "OnCrosshairOn:25" etc.
This event functions similarly to the [[GetCrosshairRef]] function - it only tracks refs within activation distance. |name = SetEventHandler "OnCrosshairOn" |returnVal = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = myUDF |Type = userDefinedFunction }} }}
== Example with no filtering == SetEventHandler "OnCrosshairOn" MyUDF
scn MyUDF
ref rCrosshairRef
begin function {rCrosshairRef}
end function
== Example with filtering by a form == SetEventHandler "OnCrosshairOn" MyUDF "first"::someForm
scn MyUDF
ref rCrosshairRef
begin function {rCrosshairRef}
end function
== Example with filtering by formlist == SetEventHandler "OnCrosshairOn" MyUDF "first"::someFormList
scn MyUDF
ref rCrosshairRef
begin function {rCrosshairRef}
end function
== Example with filtering by form type 24 (armor) == SetEventHandler "OnCrosshairOff:24" MyUDF
scn MyUDF
ref rCrosshairRef
begin function {rCrosshairRef}
end function
==See Also== * [[OnCrosshairOff]] * [[Event_Handling]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers]] [[Category:Crosshair Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = [[Event_Handling|Event]] block type that calls the [[User_Defined_Function|UDF]] when the key is pressed. The pressed key code (int) is passed to the UDF.
Use any DirectX scancode after the ":" delimiter. |name = SetEventHandler "OnKeyDown:int" |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = myUDF |Type = userDefinedFunction }} }}
== Example using "L" key == SetEventHandler "OnKeyDown:38" MyUDF
scn MyUDF
int keyCode
begin function {keyCode}
end function
{{DirectX Scancodes}}
==See Also== *[[OnKeyUp]] *[[SetOnKeyDownEventHandler]] *[[SetOnKeyUpEventHandler]] *[[DisableKey]] *[[EnableKey]] *[[GetKeyPress]] *[[HoldKey]] *[[IsKeyDisabled]] *[[IsKeyPressed]] *[[MenuHoldKey]] *[[MenuReleaseKey]] *[[MenuTapKey]]
*[[ReleaseKey]] *[[TapKey]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JIP)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = [[Event_Handling|Event]] block type that calls the [[User_Defined_Function|UDF]] when the when the player picks up ([[Template:Control_Codes|Control Code 27]] ) the form or form in the formlist. Grabbed (ref) is passed to the UDF. Can be filtered with a form or formlist.
|name = SetEventHandler "OnPlayerGrab" |returnVal = grabbedRef |returnType = ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = myUDF |Type = userDefinedFunction }} }}
== Example with no filtering == SetEventHandler "OnPlayerGrab" MyUDF
scn MyUDF
ref rGrabbedRef
begin function {rGrabbedRef}
end function
== Example with filtering by a form == SetEventHandler "OnPlayerGrab" ""::someForm MyUDF
scn MyUDF
ref rGrabbedRef
begin function {rGrabbedRef}
end function
== Example with filtering by formlist == SetEventHandler "OnPlayerGrab" ""::someFormList MyUDF
scn MyUDF
ref rGrabbedRef
begin function {rGrabbedRef}
end function
==See Also== *[[Event_Handling]] *[[OnPlayerRelease]] *[[ShowOff:OnPlayerJump]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JIP)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = [[Event_Handling|Event]] block type that calls the [[User_Defined_Function|UDF]] when the when the player releases (drops the grabbed ref) ([[Template:Control_Codes|Control Code 27]] ) the form or form in the formlist. Released (ref) is passed to the UDF. Can be filtered with a form or formlist.
|name = SetEventHandler "OnPlayerRelease" |returnVal = grabbedRef |returnType = ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = myUDF |Type = userDefinedFunction }} }}
== Example with no filtering == SetEventHandler "OnPlayerRelease" MyUDF
scn MyUDF
ref rReleasedRef
begin function {rReleasedRef}
end function
== Example with filtering by a form == SetEventHandler "OnPlayerRelease" ""::someForm MyUDF
scn MyUDF
ref rReleasedRef
begin function {rReleasedRef}
end function
== Example with filtering by formlist == SetEventHandler "OnPlayerRelease" ""::someFormList MyUDF
scn MyUDF
ref rReleasedRef
begin function {rReleasedRef}
end function
==See Also== * [[OnPlayerGrab]] * [[Event_Handling]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JIP)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the value of a numeric trait (determined by traitID) of the specified '''Water Type''' form. |name = GetWaterTrait |returnType = traitValue:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = waterType |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-50 }} ==Water Trait IDs== 
0	Water property: Opacity
1	Flags bitmask (1 - Causes damage; 2 - Reflective)
2	Unknown
3	Unknown
4	Unknown
5	Unknown
6	Water property: Sun power
7	Water property: Reflectivity
8	Water property: Fresnel amount
9	Unknown
10	Fog property: Above water - Fog distance (near plane)
11	Fog property: Above water - Fog distance (far plane)
12	Shallow color (RRRGGGBBB)
13	Deep color (RRRGGGBBB)
14	Reflection color (RRRGGGBBB)
15	Unknown
16	Rain sim: Force
17	Rain sim: Velocity
18	Rain sim: Falloff
19	Rain sim: Dampener
20	Displacement sim: Starting size
21	Displacement sim: Force
22	Displacement sim: Velocity
23	Displacement sim: Falloff
24	Displacement sim: Dampener
25	Rain sim: Starting size
26	Noise property: Normals - Noise scale
27	Noise property: Layer 1 - Wind direction
28	Noise property: Layer 2 - Wind direction
29	Noise property: Layer 3 - Wind direction
30	Noise property: Layer 1 - Wind speed
31	Noise property: Layer 2 - Wind speed
32	Noise property: Layer 3 - Wind speed
33	Noise property: Normals - Depth falloff start
34	Noise property: Normals - Depth falloff end
35	Fog property: Above Water - Fog amount
36	Nosie property: Normals - UV scale
37	Fog property: Under Water - Fog amount
38	Fog property: Under Water - Fog distance (near Plane)
39	Fog property: Under Water - Fog distance (far Plane)
40	Water property: Distortion amount
41	Water property: Shininess
42	Water property: Reflection HDR multiplier
43	Water property: Light radius
44	Water property: Light brightness
45	Water property: Layer 1 - UV scale
46	Water property: Layer 2 - UV scale
47	Water property: Layer 3 - UV scale
48	Water property: Layer 1 - Amplitude scale
49	Water property: Layer 2 - Amplitude scale
50	Water property: Layer 3 - Amplitude scale
|example = set fShininess to GetWaterTrait NVCleanWater 41 }} ==See Also== *[[SetWaterTrait]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Water Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the value of a numeric trait (determined by traitID) of the specified '''Water Type''' form. |name = GetWaterTrait |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = waterType |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-50 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = float }} ==Water Trait IDs== 
0	Water property: Opacity
1	Flags bitmask (1 - Causes damage; 2 - Reflective)

13	Deep color (RRRGGGBBB)

20	Displacement sim: Starting size

23	Displacement sim: Falloff
24	Displacement sim: Dampener

26	Noise property: Normals - Noise scale
27	Noise property: Layer 1 - Wind direction
28	Noise property: Layer 2 - Wind direction
29	Noise property: Layer 3 - Wind direction
30	Noise property: Layer 1 - Wind speed
31	Noise property: Layer 2 - Wind speed
32	Noise property: Layer 3 - Wind speed
33	Noise property: Normals - Depth falloff start
34	Noise property: Normals - Depth falloff end
35	Fog property: Above Water - Fog amount
36	Nosie property: Normals - UV scale
37	Fog property: Under Water - Fog amount
38	Fog property: Under Water - Fog distance (near Plane)
39	Fog property: Under Water - Fog distance (far Plane)

41	Water property: Shininess
42	Water property: Reflection HDR multiplier
43	Water property: Light radius
44	Water property: Light brightness
45	Water property: Layer 1 - UV scale
46	Water property: Layer 2 - UV scale
47	Water property: Layer 3 - UV scale
48	Water property: Layer 1 - Amplitude scale
49	Water property: Layer 2 - Amplitude scale
50	Water property: Layer 3 - Amplitude scale
|example = SetWaterTrait NVCleanWater 41 500 }} ==See Also== *[[GetWaterTrait]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Water Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the interval value of a [[Challenge]] form, which determines at which intervals of progress that a progress notification will show up. |name = GetChallengeInterval |returnType = interval:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = challenge |Type = baseform }} |example = GetChallengeInterval NVChallengeBug Will return 10. }} ==See Also== *[[SetChallengeInterval]] *[[GetChallengeThreshold]] / [[SetChallengeThreshold]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Challenge_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Challenge Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the threshold value of a [[Challenge]] form, which determines the amount of progress the player must make on a challenge before it is completed. |name = GetChallengeThreshold |returnType = threshold:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = challenge |Type = baseform }} |example = GetChallengeThreshold NVChallengeBug Will return 50. }} ==See Also== *[[SetChallengeThreshold]] *[[SetChallengeInterval]] / [[GetChallengeInterval]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Challenge_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Challenge Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the interval value of a [[Challenge]] form, which determines at which intervals of progress that a progress notification will show up. |name = SetChallengeInterval |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = challenge |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = interval |Type = int }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetChallengeInterval]] *[[SetChallengeThreshold]] / [[GetChallengeThreshold]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Challenge_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Challenge Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the threshold value of a [[Challenge]] form, which determines the amount of progress the player must make on a challenge before it is completed. |name = SetChallengeThreshold |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = challenge |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = threshold |Type = int }} }} ==Notes== *Setting the Threshold to a lower value than the player's current progress will '''not''' automatically complete the challenge. For that to happen, the challenge must increment naturally right after.
==See Also== *[[GetChallengeThreshold]] *[[SetChallengeInterval]] / [[GetChallengeInterval]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Challenge_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Challenge Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Removes all of the player's current hotkeys. |name = ClearAllHotkeys }} ==See Also== *[[GetHotkeyItem]] / [[GetHotkeyItemRef]] *[[SetHotkeyItem]] / [[SetHotkey]] / [[SetHotkeyItemRef]] *[[SaveHotkeys]] + [[RestoreHotkeys]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Hotkey Functions]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]]

{{Deprecated | As of version 56.38 of JIP LN the plugin will now automatically remember and restore your hotkeys.}}
{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Restores the player's hotkeys from memory or from an array_var. JIP NVSE Plugin v40 merged Lutana NVSE plugin v19. '''As of JIPv40, This function's syntax was changed from Lutana v19, requiring that you pass the arrays via their IDs (set to integers) if you use the script compiler override (CO).''' |name = RestoreHotkeys |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = hotKeysArrayID |Type = array }} }}
== Example == array_var arrHotkeys
RestoreHotkeys arrHotkeys
== Example with Compiler Override == array_var arrHotKeys int iArrHotKeysID ... set iArrHotKeysID to arrHotKeys RestoreHotkeys iArrHotKeysID
== Old Syntax (Lutana standalone) == RestoreHotkeys arrHotKeys
==See Also== *[[ClearAllHotkeys]] *[[GetHotkeyItem]] *[[SaveHotkeys]] *[[SetHotkey]] *[[SetHotkeyItem]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Hotkey Functions]] [[Category:Deprecated Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Similar to SetHotkeyItem, but allows specification of health and weapon mod flags. You can pass -1 as the 'health' parameter to ignore it when matching modflags. |name = SetHotkey |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = index |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = item |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = health |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = modflags |Type = int }} }} ==See Also== *[[ClearAllHotkeys]] *[[ClearAllHotkeys]] *[[GetHotkeyItem]] *[[RestoreHotkeys]] *[[SaveHotkeys]] *[[SetHotkeyItem]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Hotkey Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Prevents the game from registering input from a trigger. (Script functions will still register the input.) |name = DisableTrigger |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = trigger |Type = int }} }} {{Controller_Trigger_Codes}}
==See Also== *[[EnableTrigger]] *[[EnableButton]] *[[DisableButton]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Controller_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns 1 if a gamepad controller is plugged-in and enabled. |name = GetController |returnType = enabled:1/0 }} [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Controller Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns a value ranging from -1.00 to 1.00 for the y-axis position of the left joystick. |name = GetLeftStickY |alias = GetLSY |returnType = float |returnVal = -1.00 to 1.00 }}
==See Also== *[[GetLeftStickX]] *[[GetRightStickX]] *[[GetRightStickY]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Controller Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns a value ranging from -1.00 to 1.00 for the x-axis position of the left joystick. |name = GetLeftStickX |alias = GetLSX |returnType = float |returnVal = -1.00 to 1.00 }}
==See Also== *[[GetLeftStickY]] *[[GetRightStickX]] *[[GetRightStickY]] *[[GetPressedButtons]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Controller Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns a value ranging from -1.00 to 1.00 for the x-axis position of the right joystick. |name = GetRightStickX |alias = GetRSX |returnType = float |returnVal = -1.00 to 1.00 }}
==See Also== *[[GetLeftStickX]] *[[GetLeftStickY]] *[[GetRightStickY]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Controller Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns a value ranging from 0.00 to 1.00 for how far the left trigger is depressed. |name = GetLeftTrigger |alias = GetLTr |returnType = float |returnVal = 0.00 to 1.00 }}
==See Also== *[[GetRightTrigger]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Controller Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns a value ranging from -1.00 to 1.00 for the y-axis position of the right joystick. |name = GetRightStickY |alias = GetRSY |returnType = float |returnVal = -1.00 to 1.00 }}
==See Also== *[[GetLeftStickX]] *[[GetLeftStickY]] *[[GetRightStickX]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Controller Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns a value ranging from 0.00 to 1.00 for how far the right trigger is depressed. |name = GetRightTrigger |alias = GetRTr |returnType = float |returnVal = 0.00 to 1.00 }}
==See Also== *[[GetLeftTrigger]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Controller Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns whether or not a button is held by HoldButton. |name = IsButtonHeld |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = button |Type = int }} }} ==Notes== This function can also be used as a condition. {{Controller_Button_Codes}} ==See Also== *[[HoldButton]] *[[ReleaseButton]] *[[HoldTrigger]] *[[ReleaseTrigger]] *[[IsTriggerHeld]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Controller Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns whether or not a button is disabled. |name = IsButtonDisabled |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = button |Type = int }} }} ==Notes== This function can also be used as a condition. {{Controller_Button_Codes}} ==See Also== *[[EnableTrigger]] *[[DisableTrigger]] *[[EnableButton]] *[[DisableButton]] *[[IsTriggerDisabled]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Controller Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Emulates a button press indefinitely. |name = HoldButton |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = button |Type = int }} }} {{Controller_Button_Codes}} ==See Also== *[[ReleaseButton]] *[[HoldTrigger]] *[[ReleaseTrigger]] *[[IsButtonHeld]] *[[IsTriggerHeld]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Controller Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Releases a button held by HoldButton. |name = ReleaseButton |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = button |Type = int }} }} {{Controller_Button_Codes}} ==See Also== *[[HoldButton]] *[[HoldTrigger]] *[[ReleaseTrigger]] *[[IsButtonHeld]] *[[IsTriggerHeld]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Controller Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Releases a trigger held by HoldTrigger |name = ReleaseTrigger |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = trigger |Type = int }} }} {{Controller_Trigger_Codes}} ==See Also== *[[HoldButton]] *[[ReleaseButton]] *[[HoldTrigger]] *[[IsButtonHeld]] *[[IsTriggerHeld]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Controller Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Emulates holding a trigger indefinitely. |name = HoldTrigger |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = trigger |Type = int }} }} {{Controller_Trigger_Codes}} ==See Also== *[[HoldButton]] *[[ReleaseButton]] *[[ReleaseTrigger]] *[[IsButtonHeld]] *[[IsTriggerHeld]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Controller Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns whether or not a [[Perk]] is a trait. |name = IsTrait |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = perk |Type = form }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetPerkFlag]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Perk_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Perk Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the player's active quest. |name = GetActiveQuest |returnType = quest:ref }} ==See Also== *[[ForceActiveQuest]] *[[SetCurrentQuest]]
[[Category:Quest Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Quest_Functions_(JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Very fast floating-point square-root calculation. |name = fsqrt |returnType = outputValue:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = inputValue |Type = float }} |example = set fResult to fsqrt 11.965348816 }} ==See Also== *[[Sqrt]] [[Category:Math_Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Math_Functions_(JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Faster version of [[GetDistance]]. Ignores the z-axis. Can also be used as a condition. Unlike GetDistance, this does not cause crashes if the reference(s) are not in the current cell/worldspace. |name = GetDistance2D |returnType = float |referenceType = [Actor, Activator] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ref |Type = ref }} |example = 
Player.GetDistance2D Molerat01Ref
Returns the distance between the player and the Molerat }} ==Notes== *If the reference(s) are not in the current cell/worldspace, or the references are invalid, returns '''FLT_MAX''' (3.402823466e+38F). *Return value is measured in game [[Units]]. ==See Also== *[[GetDistance]] *[[GetDistance3D]] [[Category:Math_Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Math_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]] [[Category:Movement Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Faster version of [[GetDistance]]. Can also be used as a condition. Unlike GetDistance, this does not cause crashes if the reference(s) are not in the current cell/worldspace. |name = GetDistance3D |returnType = float |referenceType = [Actor, Activator] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ref |Type = ref }} |example = 
Player.GetDistance3D Molerat01Ref
Returns the distance between the player and the Molerat }} ==Notes== *If the reference(s) are not in the current cell/worldspace, or the references are invalid, returns '''FLT_MAX''' (3.402823466e+38F). *Return value is measured in game [[Units]]. ==See Also== *[[GetDistance]] *[[GetDistance2D]] [[Category:Math_Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Math_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]] [[Category:Movement Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the radius of a [[reference]] (any type) or [[Light]] [[BaseForm]]. Also works on Radiation Markers references. |name = GetRadius |returnType = float |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = light |Type = baseform |Optional = optional }} }} ==See Also== *[[SetRadius]] *[[GetLightTraitNumeric]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Light_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Light_Functions]] [[Category:ExtraData Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the radius of a [[reference]] (any type) or [[Light]] BaseForm.
If used on a reference, this will modify that reference's Radius [[ExtraData]]. If it does not exist, it is created. Note that it may not be saved with the reference, depending on the reference's type. |name = SetRadius |returnType = void |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = radius |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = light |Type = baseform |Optional = optional }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetRadius]] *[[SetLightTraitNumeric]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Light_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Light_Functions]] [[Category:ExtraData Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the water type of a cell form. If no cell is specified, the player's current cell is used. Will return the parent worldspace's water form for exterior cells. |name = GetCellWaterForm |returnType = ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = cell |Type = form |Optional = y }} }} ==See Also== *[[SetCellWaterForm]] *[[GetCellWaterHeight]] *[[SetCellWaterHeight]] *[[GetWaterFormEffect]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Cell Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Water_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Cell Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the water height of a cell. If the cell parameter is omitted, the player's current cell is used. |name = GetCellWaterHeight |returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = cell |Type = form |Optional = y }} }} ==See Also== *[[SetCellWaterHeight]] *[[GetCellWaterForm]] *[[GetWaterFormEffect]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Cell Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Water_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Cell Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the spell effect attached to a water form. |name = GetWaterFormEffect |returnType = actorEffect:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = water |Type = ref |Optional = y }} }} ==See Also== *[[SetWaterFormEffect]] *[[GetCellWaterHeight]] *[[SetCellWaterHeight]] *[[GetCellWaterForm]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Water_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the water height of a cell. If the cell parameter is omitted, the player's current cell is used.(NEEDS TESTING) |name = SetCellWaterHeight |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = height |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = cell |Type = form |Optional = y }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetCellWaterHeight]] *[[GetCellWaterForm]] *[[GetWaterFormEffect]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Cell Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Water_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Cell Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the pickup sound form of a form. |name = GetPickupSound |returnType = sound:baseform |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = object |Type = form }} }} ==See Also== *[[SetPickupSound]] *[[GetPutdownSound]] *[[SetPutdownSound]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Sound_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Sound_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the putdown [[Sound]] form of a form. |name = GetPutdownSound |returnType = sound:baseform |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = object |Type = form }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetPickupSound]] *[[SetPickupSound]] *[[SetPutdownSound]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Sound_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Sound_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the pickup sound form of a form. |name = SetPickupSound |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = object |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = sound |Type = form }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetPickupSound]] *[[GetPutdownSound]] *[[SetPutdownSound]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Sound_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Sound_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the putdown sound of a form. |name = SetPutdownSound |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = object |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = sound |Type = form }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetPickupSound]] *[[SetPickupSound]] *[[GetPutdownSound]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Sound_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Sound_Functions]]
