revision.text: {{Bc|Functions (JIP)}} [[Category:Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the broadcast type of a [[Radio_Stations|radio]] reference. |name = GetRadioBroadcastType |returnType = broadcastType:int |referenceType = [talkingAcivator:reference] |example = RNVTARef.GetRadioBroadcastType Will return 1. (Everywhere) }} ==Broadcast Types== 
0	Radius
1	Everywhere
2	Worldspace and Linked Interiors
3	Linked Interiors
4	Current Cell
==See Also== *[[SetRadioBroadcastType]] [[Category:Radio Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Radio_Functions_(JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the broadcast type of a [[Radio_Stations|radio]] reference. (See below for values.) |name = SetRadioBroadcastType |returnType = void |referenceType = [talkingActivator:reference] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = broadcastType |Type = int }} |example = RNVTARef.SetRadioBroadcastType 0 Will change Radio New Vegas to a broadcast type of 0 (Radius). }} ==Broadcast Types== 
0	Radius
1	Everywhere
2	Worldspace and Linked Interiors
3	Linked Interiors
4	Current Cell
==See Also== *[[GetRadioBroadcastType]] [[Category:Radio Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Radio_Functions_(JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the exterior position reference of a radius-type [[Radio_Stations|radio]] broadcast. |name = SetRadioPosRef |returnType = void |referenceType = [talkingActivator:reference] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = positionReference |Type = ref }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetRadioPosRef]] [[Category:Radio Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Radio_Functions_(JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the exterior position reference of a radius-type [[Radio_Stations|radio]] broadcast. If a radius-type radio reference is in an exterior cell, it might not have an exterior position reference set. In this case, it uses itself implicitly, and this function will not return a valid reference. Use [[IsFormValid]] to verify the return value. |name = GetRadioPosRef |returnType = reference |referenceType = [talkingActivator:reference] }} ==See Also== *[[SetRadioPosRef]] [[Category:Radio Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Radio_Functions_(JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns whether or not a reference is a [[Radio_Stations|radio]]. |name = IsRadioRef |returnType = bool |referenceType = [talkingActivator:reference] }} ==Notes== *Does not return true for an actor broadcasting due to [[SetNPCRadio]], or an activator playing a radio. It's meant for querying the ''Radio Station'' checkbox on a TalkingActivator. [[Category:Radio Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Radio_Functions_(JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the radius of a radius-type [[Radio_Stations|radio]] broadcast. |name = GetRadioRadius |returnType = float |referenceType = [talkingActivator:reference] |example = AlienRadioBeaconREF.GetRadioRadius Will return 6000. }} ==See Also== *[[SetRadioRadius]] [[Category:Radio Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Radio_Functions_(JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the radius of a radius-type [[Radio_Stations|radio]] broadcast. |name = SetRadioRadius |returnType = void |referenceType = [talkingActivator:reference] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = radius |Type = float }} |example = AlienRadioBeaconREF.GetRadioRadius 2000 Will set the radius to 2000. }} ==See Also== *[[GetRadioRadius]] [[Category:Radio Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Radio_Functions_(JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the static percentage of a radius-type [[Radio_Stations|radio]] broadcast. |name = GetRadioStatic |returnType = staticPercentage:float |referenceType = [talkingActivator:ref] |example = AlienRadioBeaconREF.GetRadioStatic Will return 15.00. }} ==See Also== *[[SetRadioStatic]] [[Category:Radio Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Radio_Functions_(JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the static percentage of a radius-type [[Radio_Stations|radio]] broadcast. |name = SetRadioStatic |returnType = void |referenceType = [talkingActivator:reference] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = percent |Type = float }} |example = AlienRadioBeaconREF.SetRadioStatic 37.3 Will set the static to 37.3% }} ==See Also== *[[GetRadioStatic]] [[Category:Radio Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Radio_Functions_(JIP)]]

revision.comment: He's right.
revision.text: {{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns whether or not an ingestible form is a skill magazine. If an ingestible has an effect with a condition of HasPerk=Retention or HasPerk=Comprehension, it will be considered a magazine. |name = IsSkillMagazine |returnType = isMagazine:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ingestible |Type = baseform }} |example = set bIsMagazine to IsSkillMagazine MagazineNVExplosives }} [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Effect Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets an ingestible flag on an ingestible form. "Food" determines if the effects are affected by Survival skill, while "Medicine" flag determines if the effects are affected by Medicine skill. They are not mutually exclusive. |name = SetIngestibleFlag |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ingestible |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flag |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = value |Type = bool }} |example = SetIngestibleFlag JalapenoPepper 2 0 Will remove the food flag from Jalapenos. ==Ingestible Flags== 
2	Food
4	Medicine
}} ==See Also== *[[SetEffectFlag]] *[[GetIngestibleFlag]] *[[Ingestible Settings]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Effect Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns whether or not an ingestible has an ingestible flag. "Food" determines if the effects are affected by Survival skill, while "Medicine" flag determines if the effects are affected by Medicine skill. They are not mutually exclusive. |name = GetIngestibleFlag |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ingestible |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flag |Type = int }} |example = GetIngestibleFlag JalapenoPepper 2 Will return 1 (true) for having the Food flag. ==Ingestible Flags== 
2	Food
4	Medicine
}} ==See Also== *[[GetEffectFlag]] *[[SetIngestibleFlag]] *[[Ingestible Settings]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Effect Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

revision.comment: italics
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Outputs information about all forms that match the provided string to the console. (This is intended to partially emulate the way the 'help' command works in the ''Skyrim'' console.) * The '''optional''' type parameter filters output by [[Form Type IDs]]. * The type parameter can be the four character code of the type, as output in the Search command (AMMO, ARMO, NPC_, etc.) * Use quotation marks around strings that contain a space. |name = search |returnType = void |consoleOnly = y |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = name |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = type |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = 
search "power armor"
Find anything with '''power armor''' in the name. 
search "power armor" 86
Find the '''PowerArmorTraining''' perk (and any other with that string in the name). 
search 10mm
Find anything with '''10mm''' in the name. 
search 10mm 40
search 10mm WEAP
Find weapons with '''10mm''' in the name. }} ==Notes== * This function will search for forms with matching [[EditorID]]s if the [[JohnnyGuitar NVSE]] plugin is installed. ==See Also== *[[Help]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Console_Functions]] [[Category:Debugging]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns 1 if the reference under the crosshair is in the [[Formlist]], 2 if the reference's [[Base Form]] is in the list. This function can also be used as a condition. |name = CrosshairRefInList |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = list |Type = formlist }} |example = set bCrosshairIsInList to CrosshairRefInList NPCFollowersLIST }} ==See Also== *[[GetCrosshairRef]] *[[GetCrosshairRefEx]] *[[ObjectUnderReticle]] *[[OnCrosshairOn]] *[[OnCrosshairOff]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Crosshair Functions]]

revision.comment: unlike GetUserTime, this doesn't actually return year, day or month at all https://github.com/jazzisparis/JIP-LN-NVSE/blob/5a30ac4356ea0e93b9ff357b5031b1e420240a4d/internal/utility.cpp#L2896
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns a [[string_var]] containing the user's current local time in the following format: "14-50-30" (HH-mm-SS). |name = GetTimeStamp |returnType = dateTimeStamp:string }} ==See Also== *[[GetUserTime]] *[[GetTimePlayed]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Time Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the value of a numeric global variable. Returns the previous value. |name = SetGlobalValue |alias = SGV |returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = global |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = value |Type = float }} |example = SetGlobalValue CaravanJunk1 50 }} ==See Also== *[[GetGlobalValue]] *[[SetGlobalVariable]] *[[GetGlobalVariable]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Global Variable Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the rank of the specified [[Perk]] on the calling actor, or adds it at a specified rank if they don't have it. *When called on the '''player character''', this function will set/add the perk rank on the player, unless the optional argument '''forTeammates''' is non-zero, in which case it will set/add the perk rank for teammates. *When called on any actor other than the player, this function will work correctly only if the '''uNPCPerks''' optional JIP LN feature is toggled in '''Data\NVSE\plugins\jip_nvse.ini'''. |name = SetPerkRank |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = perk |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = rank |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = forTeammates |Type = 1/0 |Optional = y }} |example = actorRef.SetPerkRank DemolitionExpert 2 player.SetPerkRank DemolitionExpert 2 1 }} ==See Also== *[[AddPerk]] *[[GetPerkRank]] *[[GetAltPerkRank]] *[[HasPerkRank]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Perk Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Perk Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]]

revision.comment: Like GetINISection - these notes don't belong here, the function doesn't use a key-string and nothing is written.
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns an [[array]] containing the names of all sections in an INI file with 64k buffer. |name = GetINISectionNames |returnType = array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = filename |Type = string |Optional = y }} |example=GetINISectionNames "FilePath\FileName.ini" }} ==Notes== * INI files are read from and written to in the Data\config\ folder (as in MCM's INI functions). [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/INI_file#Example Format Example]
* If no filename is specified, the name of the mod the function is called from is used. Subdirectories can be defined in the path, e.g. "subDir\nameOfFile"
==See Also== *[[GetINISection]] *[[SetINISection]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:INI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:INI Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

revision.comment: Inform about Show Off's cached functions
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the value of an INI key as a string. |name = GetINIString |returnType = string |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = keystring |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = filename |Type = string |Optional = y }} }} ==Notes== * Reading multiple INI settings with this function can be slow and inefficient, especially during gameplay. If you need to read multiple INI settings, or access them in-game, it's recommended to use [[GetINIStringOrDefault_Cached]]. * INI files are read from and written to in the Data\config\ folder (as in MCM's INI functions). [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/INI_file#Example Format Example]
* If no filename is specified, the name of the mod the function is called from is used. Subdirectories can be defined in the path, e.g. "subDir\nameOfFile"
* Writing to an INI file that does not exist will create it along with any non-existent subdirectories in its path.
* A keystring is a string containing both the section and key in the format: "Section:Key" ('\' and '/' may also be used as the delimiter.)
==See Also== *[[SetINIString]] *[[GetINIFloat]] *[[SetINIFloat]] *[[GetINIString_Cached]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:INI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:INI Functions]]

revision.comment: https
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Deletes a key from an INI file. |name = RemoveINIKey |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = keystring |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = filename |Type = string |Optional = y }} }} ==Notes== *INI files are read from and written to in the Data\config\ folder (as in MCM's INI functions). [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/INI_file#Example Format Example] *If no filename is specified, the name of the mod the function is called from is used. Subdirectories can be defined in the path, e.g. "subDir\nameOfFile" *Writing to an INI file that does not exist will create it along with any non-existent subdirectories in its path. *A keystring is a string containing both the section and key in the format: "Section:Key" ('\' and '/' may also be used as the delimiter.)
==See Also== *[[RemoveINISection]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:INI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:INI Functions]]

revision.comment: https
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Deletes a section from an INI file. |name = RemoveINISection |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = section |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = filename |Type = string |Optional = y }} }} ==Notes== *INI files are read from and written to in the Data\config\ folder (as in MCM's INI functions). [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/INI_file#Example Format Example] *If no filename is specified, the name of the mod the function is called from is used. Subdirectories can be defined in the path, e.g. "subDir\nameOfFile" *Writing to an INI file that does not exist will create it along with any non-existent subdirectories in its path. *A keystring is a string containing both the section and key in the format: "Section:Key" ('\' and '/' may also be used as the delimiter.)
==See Also== *[[RemoveINIKey]] *[[GetFilesInFolder]] *[[GetFoldersInFolder]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:INI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:INI Functions]]

revision.comment: https
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the string value of an INI key. |name = SetINIString |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = keystring |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = value |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = filename |Type = string |Optional = y }} }} ==Notes== * INI files are read from and written to in the Data\config\ folder (as in MCM's INI functions). [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/INI_file#Example Format Example]
* If no filename is specified, the name of the mod the function is called from is used. Subdirectories can be defined in the path, e.g. "subDir\nameOfFile"
* Writing to an INI file that does not exist will create it along with any non-existent subdirectories in its path.
* A keystring is a string containing both the section and key in the format: "Section:Key" ('\' and '/' may also be used as the delimiter.)
==See Also== *[[GetINIString]] *[[GetINIFloat]] *[[SetINIFloat]] *[[SetINIValue_Cached]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:INI_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:INI Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns whether or not the player can equip power armor items. |name = GetPCCanUsePowerArmor |returnType = canUse:bool }} ==Notes== * The perk '''PowerArmorTraining''' is just a dummy for display on the pipboy. Internally, the perk is actually setting '''SetPCCanUsePowerArmor 1''' through stage 80 of the '''Generic''' quest. ==See Also== *[[SetPCCanUsePowerArmor]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Armor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Renames the specified NIF block in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. *The 3rd argument, '''pcModel''', is optional, and may be used when calling the function on the player. The player character has two, completely separate 3D models for 1st and 3rd person. This argument enables to explicitly target one of the models. 
1	3rd Person Model
2	1st Person Model
|name = RenameNifBlock |returnType = successful:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = blockName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = pcModel |Type = 1/2 |Optional = y }} |example = player.RenameNifBlock "ProjectileNode" "ProjectileNode_1" }} ==Notes== *The changes are reset once the calling reference's parent cell is reloaded, or [[Update3D]] is called on it. [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Model Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the count of an item in a form's base container. |name = BaseGetItemCount |returnType = count:int |referenceType = [container:reference] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = item |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = container |Type = baseform |Optional = y }} |example = 
rRef.BaseGetItemCount BookSkillUnarmed
BaseGetItemCount BookSkillUnarmed MailboxDestroyedUnarmed
}} ==See Also== *[[GetBaseItems]] *[[GetItemCount]] *[[BaseAddItem]] *[[BaseAddItemHealth]] *[[BaseRemoveItem]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Adds an item to a form's base container. |name = BaseAddItem |returnType = void |referenceType = [container:reference] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = itemToAdd |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = count |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseContainerForm |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = 
BaseAddItem BookSkillScience 1 MailboxDestroyedUnarmed
rRef.BaseAddItem BookSkillScience 1
}} ==See Also== *[[BaseGetItemCount]] *[[BaseAddItemHealth]] *[[BaseRemoveItem]] *[[BaseGetItemCount]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]]

{{Deprecated |The function doesn't work properly when called on base forms.
To add something with specific health value use [[AddItemAlt]]}} {{Function |origin = LU |summary = Adds an item with the specified health percentage (0.0 - 1.0) to a form's base container. (NEEDS TESTING) |name = BaseAddItemHealth |returnType = void |referenceType = [container:reference] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = itemToAdd |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = count |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = health |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseContainerForm |Type = baseform |Optional = y }} |example = 
BaseAddItemHealth Weap10mmPistol 1 .5 MailboxDestroyedUnarmed
rRef.BaseAddItemHealth Weap10mmPistol 1 .5
}} ==Notes== * In previous versions, it appears to add the item with '''full health/condition''' regardless of the float parameter. As of JIP LN 56.78, this seems to have been fixed. * This function will work if the itemToAdd is a leveled list, although the health of all the items inside that list will be overwritten by the health argument. * While the float parameter will take an int, the call will fail to work silently. Use a float that is less than or equal to 0.99. ==See Also== *[[BaseGetItemCount]] *[[BaseAddItem]] *[[BaseRemoveItem]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Removes an item from a form's base container. |name = BaseRemoveItem |returnType = void |referenceType = [container:reference] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = itemToRemove |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = count |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseContainerForm |Type = baseform }} |example = 
BaseRemoveItem BookSkillScience 1 MailboxDestroyedUnarmed
rRef.BaseRemoveItem BookSkillScience 1
}} ==See Also== *[[BaseGetItemCount]] *[[BaseAddItem]] *[[BaseAddItemHealth]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the password note of a terminal. |name = SetPasswordNote |returnType = void |referenceType = [terminal:reference] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = note |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = terminal |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = 
rRef.SetPasswordNote BMMarcusNote
SetPasswordNote BMMarcusNote NewVegasSteelTerminal
}} ==See Also== *[[SetOpenKey]] *[[GetPasswordNote]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Terminal_Functions]] [[Category:Misc_Reference_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Note Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the "locked out" state of an item through failed hacking/picking attempts. Doors, Containers, and Terminals can be locked. |name = SetLockedOut |returnType = void |referenceType = [lockableObject] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = lockedOutState |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = lockableObject |Type = ref |Optional = y }} ==Locked Out Flags== 
0	Not broken
1	Broken
2	Broken twice through the Infiltrator or Computer Whiz perks
}} ==See Also== *[[GetLockedOut]] *[[GetIsLockBroken]] *[[GetLocked]] [[Category:Lock_Functions]] [[Category:Lock_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Misc_Reference_Functions_(JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns whether or not the player has the key to unlock a locked door or container or the password note to unlock a terminal. |name = PlayerHasKey |returnType = bool |referenceType = [lockableForm] }} ==Notes== * This function can also be used as a condition.
==See Also== *[[GetOpenKey]] *[[GetPasswordNote]]
[[Category:Lock_Functions]] [[Category:Lock_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Misc_Reference_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]] [[Category:Player Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the lock level of a terminal's base form regardless of locked state. See [[GetLockLevel]] for the return values. |name = GetTerminalLock |returnType = int |referenceType = [terminalRef] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = terminal |Type = form |Optional = y }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetLocked]]
[[Category:Terminal_Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Misc_Reference_Functions_(JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the password note of a terminal. |name = GetPasswordNote |returnType = key:baseform |referenceType = [terminalRef] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = terminal |Type = form |Optional = y }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetOpenKey]] *[[SetPasswordNote]]
[[Category:Terminal_Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Misc_Reference_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Note Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the "locked out" state of a lock from failed hacking/picking attempt. Also includes as Return value 3 for doors flagged as inaccessible. |name = GetLockedOut |returnType = lockedOutState:int |referenceType = [lockableObject] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = lockableObject |Type = ref |Optional = y }} ==Locked Out Flags== 
0	Not broken
1	Broken
2	Broken twice through the Infiltrator or Computer Whiz perks
3	Inaccessible
}} ==See Also== *[[SetLockedOut]] *[[GetIsLockBroken]] *[[GetLocked]] [[Category:Lock_Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Misc_Reference_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Lock_Functions_(JIP)]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Lock_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the total weight of items in the inventory of a reference. The '''optional''' "playable" parameter excludes non-playable items. |name = GetInventoryWeight |alias = GetInvWeight |returnType = float |referenceType = [actorRef] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = playable |Type = bool |Optional = y }} |example = 
set fWeight to PlayerRef.GetInvWeight
Will return the combined weight of all the player's items. 
set fWeight to RoseofSharonCassidyREF.GetInvWeight 1
Will return the combined weight of all Cass's playable items. }} ==See Also== *[[GetAllItems]] *[[GetAllItemRefs]] *[[GetContainerInventoryCount]] *[[GetInvRefsForItem]] *[[GetEncumbranceRate]] *[[IsPlayable]] *[[LNIsPlayable]] *[[RefreshInventoryWeight]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Inventory_Functions_(JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the mod index of the script that calls the function. (A quick shortcut for MCM scripting.) |name = GetSelfModIndex |returnType = int |example = set iModIndex to GetSelfModIndex }} ==See Also== *[[GetSourceModIndex]] *[[GetModIndex]] *[[ValidateModIndex]] *[[SetDebugMode]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:File_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:File Functions]]

revision.comment: these examples didn't make any sense, why would you call this with a path to one specific INI file
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns an [[array]] of filenames ([[string_var]]s) in a subdirectory relative to the ".\Data" folder. Wildcards may be used in the 'filter' string, e.g. "*.nif" The filenames returned include the file type (.nif, .esp, etc.). |name = GetFilesInFolder |alias = GetFiles |returnType = array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = folderpath |Type = string |Optional = n }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = filter |Type = string |Optional = y }} }} ==Syntax and Usage== 
array_var Array

let Array := GetFilesInFolder "Config" "*.ini"

array_var Array
string_var Path

let Path := "Config/myMod"
let Array := GetFilesInFolder $Path
To get all files in the Data folder itself, pass an empty string to the folderpath parameter: 
let aFiles := GetFilesInFolder "%e"
==See Also== *[[FileExists]] *[[GetFoldersInFolder]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:File_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns an [[array]] of folder names in a subdirectory relative to the ".\Data" folder. Wildcards may be used in the 'filter' string, e.g. "vault*" |name = GetFoldersInFolder |alias = GetFolders |returnType = array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = folderpath |Type = string |Optional = n }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = filter |Type = string |Optional = y }} }} ==Syntax and Usage== 
array_var Array

let Array := GetFoldersInFolder "Config/Mod"

array_var Array
string_var Path

let Path := "Config/Mod"
let Array := GetFoldersInFolder $Path
To get all folders in the Data folder itself, pass an empty string to the folderpath parameter: let aFolders := GetFoldersInFolder "%e" ==See Also== *[[FileExists]] *[[GetFilesInFolder]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:File_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns an [[array]] containing the filenames of all plugins that contain or override the given form. |name = GetFormMods |returnType = array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = form |Type = form }} |example = 
array_var arForms
let arForms := GetFormMods Scotch
ar_Dump arForms
** Dumping Array #1 **
Refs: 1 Owner FF: 
[ 0.000000 ] : FalloutNV.esm
[ 1.000000 ] : DeadMoney.esm
[ 2.000000 ] : TaleofTwoWastelands.esm
}} ==See Also== *[[IsFormOverridden]] *[[GetFormOverrideIndex]] *[[GetSourceModIndex]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:File_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns a [[string]] containing the file name, minus the extension, of the mod at the given index. Pass '1' as an optional second parameter to keep the extension. |name = GetModName |returnType = string |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = index |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = keepext |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = 
GetModName 0
"FalloutNV" 
GetModName 0 1
"FalloutNV.esm" }} ==See Also== *[[GetNthModName]] *[[GetModTraitSTR]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:File_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Name Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Name Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns whether or not a form is overridden by a plugin lower in the load order than the calling script. |name = IsFormOverridden |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = formToCheck |Type = form }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetFormMods]] *[[GetFormOverrideIndex]] *[[GetSourceModIndex]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:File_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:File Functions]]
[[Category:Name Functions]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns whether or not a ref is only activated through its [[Reference#Activate_Parents|activate parent]]. |name = IsParentActivateOnly |returnType = bool |referenceType = [ref] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = activator |Type = ref }} |example = set bIsParentOnly to rRef.IsParentActivateOnly }} ==See Also== *[[GetParentRef]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Misc_Reference_Functions_(JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns whether or not quest items are visible in inventories (as set with vanilla function SetShowQuestItems). |name = GetShowQuestItems |returnType = bool |example = set bShowQuestItems to GetShowQuestItems }} ==See Also== *[[SetShowQuestItems]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Quest Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the base form of a reference to another form. Updates the reference's 3D data if it is loaded. |name = SetBaseForm |returnType = void |referenceType = [ref] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = newBase |Type = form }} }} ==Notes== *Does work when called on container references if [https://www.nexusmods.com/newvegas/mods/58277 JIP LN NVSE] version is 56.70 or higher. *Can be different base type if [https://www.nexusmods.com/newvegas/mods/58277 JIP LN NVSE] version is 56.70 or higher.
==See Also== *[[GetBaseForm]] *[[GetBaseObject]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Misc_Reference_Functions_(JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns an [[array]] of loaded refs for a given base form. "Loaded" in this case means that they must be found in a loaded cell/worldspace. |name = GetFormRefs |returnType = array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = base |Type = form }} |example = 
array_var arrRefs
let arrRefs := GetFormRefs TinCan01
}} ==See Also== *[[GetRefs]] *[[GetCellRefs]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns an [[array]] of regions that the player is currently located within. |name = GetPlayerRegions |returnType = array |example = 
array_var arrRegions
let arrRegions := GetPlayerRegions
}} ==See Also== *[[IsPlayerInRegion]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the parent worldspace of a worldspace. |name = GetWorldspaceParentWorldspace |alias = GetWorldParentWorld |returnType = ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = worldspace |Type = form }} |example = set rWorldspace to GetWorldspaceParentWorldspace FreesideWorld }} ==See Also== [[GetParentWorldspace]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:World_Space_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:World_Space_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the VATS special effect of a weapon form. |name = GetWeaponVATSEffect |returnType = ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = form }} |example = 
GetWeaponVATSEffect WeapSuperSledge
Will return MaulerKnockdownSpell }} ==See Also== *[[SetWeaponVATSEffect]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon_Functions]] [[Category:VATS Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the VATS special effect of a weapon form. |name = SetWeaponVATSEffect |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = effect |Type = form }} |example = 
SetWeaponVATSEffect WeapSuperSledge FaceSmashEffect
}} ==See Also== *[[GetWeaponVATSEffect]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon_Functions]] [[Category:VATS Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns whether or not a [[reference]] has the given flag. |name = GetReferenceFlag |alias = GetRefFlag |returnType = bool |referenceType = [ref] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = flag |Type = int }} }} ==Flags== 
1		ESM
32		Deleted
34		Taken
64		Hidden From Local Map
128		Turn Off Fire
256		Inaccessible
512		Motion Blur
1024		Persistent
2048		Initially Disabled
16384		Temporary
32768		Visible When Distant
65536		High Priority LOD
524288		Dead
8388608		Destroyed
16777216	Destructible
33554432	No AI Aquire
268435456	Reflected By Auto Water
536870912	Refracted By Auto Water
2147483648	Multibound
==See Also== *[[GetFormFlag]] *[[SetReferenceFlag]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Misc_Reference_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Condition_Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets a flag on a [[reference]]. |name = SetReferenceFlag |alias = SetRefFlag |returnType = void |referenceType = [ref] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = flag |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = value |Type = bool }} |example = 
rRef.SetRefFlag 256 1
Will mark a ref Inaccessible. 
rRef.SetRefFlag 8388608 0
Will mark a ref as NOT Destroyed. }} ==Flags== 
1		ESM
32		Deleted
34		Taken
64		Hidden From Local Map
128		Turn Off Fire
256		Inaccessible
512		Motion Blur
1024		Persistent
2048		Initially Disabled
16384		Temporary
32768		Visible When Distant
65536		High Priority LOD
524288		Dead
8388608		Destroyed
16777216	Destructible
33554432	No AI Aquire
268435456	Reflected By Auto Water
536870912	Refracted By Auto Water
2147483648	Multibound
==See Also== *[[GetReferenceFlag]] *[[SetFormFlag]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the Full Name of a [[Reference]] or [[Base Form]] as a [[string]]. Works with all form types that have names. Unlike [[GetName]], this works properly with references that have had their names changed via [[SetRefName]]. |name = LNGetName |returnType = string |referenceType = [reference] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = object |Type = baseForm |Optional = y }} |example = 
TerminalRef.LNGetName
Checks the reference's name and outputs "Vault 21 Terminal." 
LNGetName GSProspectorSaloonInterior
Checks the base form's name and outputs "Prospector Saloon." }} ==See Also== *[[LNSetName]] *[[GetName]] *[[SetName]] *[[SetNameEx]] *[[SetActorFullName]] *[[SetActorFullNameAlt]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Name Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Name Functions]] [[Category:String Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns a stringmap with two keys ( "x" and "y" ) with values matching the X and Y coordinates of an exterior cell. |name = GetCellCoords |returnType = array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = cell |Type = form }} |examples = 
let arrCoords := GetCellCoords
Returns the coordinates of the player's parent cell. 
set rCell to ref.GetParentCell
let arrCoords := GetCellCoords rCell
}} ==See Also== *[[GetExteriorCell]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Cell Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Cell Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the critical flags of a weapon form. The only known weapon crit flag is 1: OnDeath. |name = GetWeaponCritFlags |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = form }} |example = GetWeaponCritFlags WeapLaserRifle ==Weapon Crit Flags== 
1	OnDeath
}} ==See Also== *[[SetWeaponCritFlags]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon_Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Set the critical flags of a weapon form. The only known weapon crit flag is 1: OnDeath. |name = SetWeaponCritFlags |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flags |Type = int }} |example = SetWeaponCritFlags WeapLaserRifle 0 ==Weapon Crit Flags== 
1	OnDeath
}} ==See Also== *[[GetWeaponCritFlags]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon_Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the kill impulse value of a weapon form. Kill impulse affects how far an actor is pushed back when killed by the weapon. |name = GetWeaponKillImpulse |returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = form }} |example = 
GetWeaponKillImpulse WeapLaserRifle
Returns 0 
GetWeaponKillImpulse WeapHuntingRifle
Returns 26 }} ==See Also== *[[SetWeaponKillImpulse]] *[[GetWeaponImpulseDistance]] *[[SetWeaponImpulseDistance]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the kill impulse value of a weapon form. Kill impulse affects how far an actor is pushed back when killed by the weapon. |name = SetWeaponKillImpulse |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = value |Type = float }} |example = 
SetWeaponKillImpulse WeapLaserRifle 15.3
}} ==See Also== *[[GetWeaponKillImpulse]] *[[GetWeaponImpulseDistance]] *[[SetWeaponImpulseDistance]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the impulse distance value of a weapon form. Kill impulse affects how far an actor is pushed back when killed by the weapon. |name = GetWeaponImpulseDistance |returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = form }} |example = GetWeaponImpulseDistance WeapLaserRifle Returns 0 }} ==See Also== *[[GetWeaponKillImpulse]] *[[SetWeaponKillImpulse]] *[[SetWeaponImpulseDistance]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the impulse distance value of a weapon form. Kill impulse affects how far an actor is pushed back when killed by the weapon. |name = SetWeaponImpulseDistance |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = value |Type = float }} |example = SetWeaponImpulseDistance WeapLaserRifle 3.1 }} ==See Also== *[[GetWeaponKillImpulse]] *[[SetWeaponKillImpulse]] *[[GetWeaponImpulseDistance]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the value of a string [[Setting|GameSetting]] (NVSE function [[SetStringGameSettingEx]] doesn't work correctly). |name = SetStringSetting |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = settingName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = string }} |example = 
SetStringSetting "sLockLevelNameVeryHard" "Really Hard"
Changes the string of a level 100 lock from "Very Hard" to "Really Hard" 
SetStringSetting "sLockLevelNameHard" "Hard (75)"
Changes the string of a level 75 lock from "Hard" to "Hard (75)" }} ==See Also== *[[GetStringSetting]] *[[GetStringGameSetting]] *[[SetStringGameSettingEx]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:GameSetting Functions]]

revision.comment: MenuMode note
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the global time multiplier, as set by [[SetGlobalTimeMultiplier]]. It is also affected by the player using Turbo, and [[VATS]]. Even in [[MenuMode]], where the time mult has no effect, the time mult will have the same value it had while in [[GameMode]]. |name = GetGlobalTimeMultiplier |alias = ggtm |returnType = float |example = 
set fGTM to ggtm
}} ==Notes== {{Template:Time Mult Changes}} ==See Also== *[[SetGlobalTimeMultiplier]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Time Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Similar to [[IsPlayable]], but also works for RACE, EYES, HAIR, and PERK forms. If a [[calling reference]] is given, its [[BaseForm]] is used. {{:Playable Items}} |name = LNIsPlayable |referenceType = reference |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = playableForm |Type = form }} |example = set bIsPlayable to LNIsPlayable WeapNVCaravanShotgunCass Returns 0 (not playable). }} ==Form Types== These form types can be Playable: 
10	Hair
11	Eyes
12	Race
24	Armor
40	Weapon
41	Ammo
86	Perk
==See Also== *[[LNSetIsPlayable]] *[[IsPlayable]] *[[SetIsPlayable]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]]

revision.comment: Fix incorrect arguments
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Similar to [[SetIsPlayable]], but also works for WEAP, AMMO, RACE, EYES, HAIR, and PERK forms. If a [[calling reference]] is given, its [[BaseForm]] is used. {{:Playable Items}} |name = LNSetIsPlayable |referenceType = reference |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = playable |Type = bool }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = form |Type = BaseForm |Optional = y }} |LNSetIsPlayableWeapNVCaravanShotgunCass 1 Changes the Playable flag on Cass's shotgun to 1 (playable). }} ==Form Types== These form types can be Playable: 
10	Hair
11	Eyes
12	Race
24	Armor
40	Weapon
41	Ammo
86	Perk
==See Also== *[[LNIsPlayable]] *[[IsPlayable]] *[[SetIsPlayable]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the value of a string [[Game Setting]] (NVSE [[GetStringGameSetting]] doesn't work correctly). |name = GetStringSetting |returnType = string |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = setting |Type = string }} |example = let svSetting := GetStringSetting "sLockLevelNameVeryHard" }} ==See Also== *[[SetStringSetting]] *[[GetStringGameSetting]] *[[GetNumericGameSetting]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:GameSetting Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the default message display time (defaults to 2.0). This is the display time for UI Messages about added/removed items, critical hits, crippled limbs, etc. |name = GetDefaultMessageTime |alias = GetDMT |returnType = float |example = set fDisplayTime to GetDefaultMessageTime }} ==See Also== *[[SetDefaultMessageTime]] *[[SetMessageDisplayTime]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Message Functions]]

revision.comment: Added note about JohnnyGuitar fix.
revision.text: {{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the default message display time (defaults to 2.0). This is the display time for UI Messages about added/removed items, critical hits, crippled limbs, etc. |name = SetDefaultMessageTime |alias = SetDMT |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = displayTime |Type = float }} |example = SetDefaultMessageTime 3.5 }} ==Notes== *JohnnyGuitar NVSE 5.19 fixed a bug where this function would override all message display times even if the function wasn't used. ==See Also== *[[GetDefaultMessageTime]] *[[SetMessageDisplayTime]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Message Functions]]

revision.comment: italics
revision.text: {{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the rank of the specified perk on the calling actor, or 0 if the actor does not have the perk. *When called on the '''player character''', this function will return the perk rank on the player, unless the optional argument '''forTeammates''' is non-zero, in which case it will return the perk rank for teammates. *When called on any actor other than the player, this function will work correctly only if the '''uNPCPerks''' optional JIP LN feature is toggled in '''Data\NVSE\plugins\jip_nvse.ini'''. |name = HasPerkRank |returnType = rank:int |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = perk |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = forTeammates |Type = 1/0 |Optional = y }} |example = set iRank to actorRef.HasPerkRank Cowboy set iRank to player.HasPerkRank Cowboy 1 }} ==Notes== * For [[:Category:Functions_(Command_Extender)|Command Extender]], using the optional alt parameter will always return 0. The teammate perk list was added in ''New Vegas''. ==See Also== *[[GetPerkRank]] *[[GetAltPerkRank]] *[[SetPerkRank]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Perk Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Perk Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the required level of a perk. |name = GetPerkLevel |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = perk |Type = form }} |example = GetPerkLevel Cannibal }} ==See Also== *[[SetPerkLevel]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Perk_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Perk Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the number of forms of a given type in an array. JIP NVSE Plugin v40 merged Lutana NVSE plugin v19. '''As of JIPv40, This function's syntax was changed from Lutana v19, requiring that you pass the arrays via their IDs (set to integers) if you use the script compiler override (CO).''' |name = GetFormCountType |returnType = count:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = formsArrayID |Type = array }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = type |Type = int }} }}
== Example == array_var arrForms int iType int iCount ... let iCount := GetFormCountType arrForms, iType
== Example with CO == array_var arrForms int iType int iCount int iArrFormsID ... set iArrFormsID to arrForms let iCount := GetFormCountType iArrFormsID iType
== Old Syntax (Lutana standalone) == (int) GetFormCountType forms:array type:int
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Array_Functions]]

revision.comment: For Cell formtype, only returns interior cells
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Similar to [[GetLoadedType]], but returns an [[Array_Variables|array]] (rather than a [[FormList]]) filled with every form of the given [[Form_Type_IDs|Form Type ID]] from all loaded plugins. '''Optional Index parameter''': Passing a value for the 'index' parameter loads only forms from the plugin at that [[GetModIndex|mod index]] (default: -1 for all plugins). |name = GetLoadedTypeArray |alias = GLTA |returnType = array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = typeID |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = index |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = 
let arrItems := GLTA 24
Returns armors from all loaded plugins ==Example== 
let arrItems := GLTA 40 0
Returns weapons from mod index 0 (should be FalloutNV.esm) }} ==Notes== *When called with typeID = 57 (Cells), returns only interior cells. ==See Also== *[[GetLoadedType|GetLoadedType]] *[[Form_Type_IDs|Form Type ID]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

revision.comment: For Cell formtype, only returns interior cells
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Empties a form list and then fills it with every form of the given [[Form_Type_IDs|Form Type ID]] from all loaded plugins. '''Optional Index parameter''': Passing a value for the 'index' parameter loads only forms from the plugin at that [[GetModIndex|mod index]] (default: -1 for all plugins). '''Optional NoClear parameter''': Passing a 1 for the 'noclear' parameter prevents the function from emptying the list before adding forms to it. |name = GetLoadedType |alias = GLT |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = outlist |Type = formList }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = typeID |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = index |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = noClear |Type = bool |Optional = y }} |example = 
GLT MyFormList 24
Will empty MyFormList, then add armors from all loaded plugins to MyFormList. 
GLT MyFormList 40 0 1
Will add weapons from mod index 0 to MyFormList, retaining anything already in MyFormList. }} ==Notes== *When called with typeID = 57 (Cells), returns only interior cells. ==See Also== *[[GetLoadedTypeArray|GetLoadedTypeArray]] *[[Form_Type_IDs|Form Type ID]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:List Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Passes a formatted string to the console, enabling the use of console commands in scripts. |name = Console |returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = line |Type = string }} |example = Console "TCL" Will execute the [[TCL]] command (ToggleCollision) }}
==Notes== * This function was broken in JIP LN 57.30, but was patched in JohnnyGuitar NVSE, and [[JIP PP LN]].
==See Also== *[[:Category:Console Functions|Console Functions]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous_Functions_(JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the texture path of a [[loading screen]]. |name = GetLoadScreenTexture |returnType = string |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = screen |Type = form }} |example = GetLoadScreenTexture GeneralLoadScreen01 Returns "interface\loading\loading_billboard05.dds" }} ==See Also== *[[GetLoadScreenTypeTextRGB]] *[[SetLoadScreenTypeTextRGB]] *[[SetLoadScreenTexture]] *[[GetLoadScreenType]] *[[SetLoadScreenType]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:LoadScreen_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Loading Screen Functions]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:Texture Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the LoadScreenType form of a [[loading screen]]. |name = GetLoadScreenType |returnType = loadScreenType:baseform |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = loadScreen |Type = baseform }} |example = set rLoadScreenType to GetLoadScreenType GeneralLoadScreen01 Returns StandardLoadingTip }} ==See Also== *[[GetLoadScreenTypeTextRGB]] *[[SetLoadScreenTypeTextRGB]] *[[GetLoadScreenTexture]] *[[SetLoadScreenTexture]] *[[SetLoadScreenType]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:LoadScreen_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Loading Screen Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the texture path of a [[loading screen]]. |name = SetLoadScreenTexture |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = screen |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = path |Type = string }} |example = SetLoadScreenTexture GeneralLoadScreen01 "interface\loading\loading_billboard06.dds" }} ==See Also== *[[GetLoadScreenTypeTextRGB]] *[[SetLoadScreenTypeTextRGB]] *[[GetLoadScreenTexture]] *[[GetLoadScreenType]] *[[SetLoadScreenType]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:LoadScreen_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Loading Screen Functions]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:Texture Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the color values of text in a loading screen type form. |name = SetLoadScreenTypeTextRGB |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = loadScreenType |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = red |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = green |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = blue |Type = float }} |example = 
SetLoadScreenTypeTextRGB StandardLoadingTip 59.3 181 255
}} ==See Also== *[[GetLoadScreenTypeTextRGB]] *[[GetLoadScreenTexture]] *[[SetLoadScreenTexture]] *[[GetLoadScreenType]] *[[SetLoadScreenType]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:LoadScreen_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Loading Screen Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Similar to [[ar_Dump]], outputs all entries of a [[Levelled List]] to the console. |name = DumpLevList |alias = LListDump |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = levelledList |Type = baseform }} |example = DumpLevList LootNVGunsAll }} [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Leveled_List_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Leveled List Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the global variable governing the 'none' chance of a leveled list. This might not be set; most lists do not use a global variable to control it, so the returned ref should be verified with IsFormValid. |name = GetChanceNoneGlobal |returnType = globalVariable:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = levelledList |Type = ref }} |example = set rRef to GetChanceNoneGlobal CapsChangeFortuneFinder }} ==See Also== *[[SetChanceNoneGlobal]] *[[GetChanceNone]] *[[SetChanceNone]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Leveled_List_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Leveled List Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the global variable governing the 'none' chance of a leveled list. The global form parameter is optional. Executing without the parameter will remove the global ("none" in GECK). |name = SetChanceNoneGlobal |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = levelledList |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = global |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = 
SetChanceNoneGlobal CapsChangeFortuneFinder
Will remove FortuneFinderOdds 
SetChanceNoneGlobal CapsChangeFortuneFinder ScroungerOdds
Will change it to use ScroungerOdds instead of FortuneFinderOdds }} ==See Also== *[[GetChanceNoneGlobal]] *[[GetChanceNone]] *[[SetChanceNone]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Leveled_List_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Leveled List Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the 'none' chance of a leveled list. |name = GetChanceNone |returnType = chanceNone:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = levelledList |Type = baseform }} |example = GetChanceNone Ammo10mm75 Returns 25 }} ==See Also== *[[GetChanceNoneGlobal]] *[[SetChanceNone]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Leveled_List_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Leveled List Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the 'none' chance of a leveled list. |name = SetChanceNone |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = levelledList |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = chanceNone |Type = int }} |example = SetChanceNone Ammo10mm75 50 }} ==See Also== *[[GetChanceNone]] *[[GetChanceNoneGlobal]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Leveled_List_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Leveled List Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns a bitfield of the flags used by a leveled list. |name = GetLeveledListFlags |alias = LListGetFlags |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = levelledList |Type = baseform }} |example = GetLeveledListFlags Ammo10mm75 Returns 2 }} ==Leveled List Flags== 
1	Calculate from all levels <= player's level
2	Calculate for each item in count
4	Use All
==See Also== *[[SetLeveledListFlags]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Leveled_List_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Leveled List Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the flags used by a leveled list with a bitfield parameter. |name = SetLeveledListFlags |alias = LListSetFlags |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = levelledList |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flags |Type = int }} }} ==Leveled List Flags== 
1	Calculate from all levels <= player's level
2	Calculate for each item in count
4	Use All
==See Also== *[[GetLeveledListFlags]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Leveled_List_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Leveled List Functions]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the number of entries in a [[Leveled List]]. |name = GetNumLevItems |alias = LListGetCount |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = levelledList |Type = baseform }} |example = set iNumLevItems to GetNumLevItems CapsFortuneFinder }} ==See Also== *[[Ar_Size]] (equivalent for [[Array Variable|Arrays]]) *[[ListGetCount]] (equivalent for [[FormList]]s)
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Leveled_List_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Leveled List Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Removes all entries from a leveled list. |name = LeveledListClear |alias = LListClear |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = levelledList |Type = baseform }} |example = LeveledListClear CapsFortuneFinder }} ==See Also== *[[LeveledListAddForm]] *[[LeveledListRemoveForm]] *[[LeveledListReplaceForm]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Leveled_List_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Leveled List Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions_(FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Removes the leveled list entry at the given index for any [[LeveledItem]], [[LeveledCreature]], or [[LeveledNPC]] list. |name = RemoveNthLevItem |alias = LListRemoveNthForm |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = levelledList |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = index |Type = int }} |example = 
if GetNumLevItems CapsFortuneFinder 
    RemoveNthLevItem CapsFortuneFinder 0
endif
Would remove the first entry of CapsFortuneFinder, after checking there is an entry with GetNumLevItems }} ==See Also== *[[SetNthLevItem]] *[[GetNthLevItem]] *[[GetNthLevItemLevel]] *[[GetNthLevItemCount]] *[[SetNthLevItemCount]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Leveled_List_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Leveled List Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Creates a new entry in a [[leveled list]] with the given parameters. (Use decimal values between 0.0 and 1.0 for health percentage.) If you are calling LeveledListAddForm with a leveled list passed as the formToAdd argument, you should pass in 0.0 as the health argument. This matches with leveled lists in vanilla. Otherwise, you may see decreased spawn rates. |name = LeveledListAddForm |alias = LListAddForm |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = levelledList |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = formToAdd |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = level |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = count |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = health |Type = float }} |example = 
LeveledListAddForm CapsFortuneFinder Caps001 10 200 1.0
}} ==See Also== *[[AddItemToLeveledList]] *[[LeveledListRemoveForm]] *[[LeveledListReplaceForm]] *[[LeveledListClear]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Leveled_List_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Leveled List Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions_(FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the index of the first entry containing the given form, or -1 if the form is not found. (Equivalent to using ListGetFormIndex for a FormList) |name = GetLevItemIndexByForm |alias = LListGetFormIndex |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = levelledList |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = formToFind |Type = baseform }} |example = GetLevItemIndexByForm CapsFortuneFinder Caps001 }} ==See Also== *[[GetLevItemIndexByLevel]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Leveled_List_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Leveled List Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the index of the first entry for the given level. |name = GetLevItemIndexByLevel |alias = LListGetLevelIndex |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = levelledList |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = level |Type = int }} |example = GetLevItemIndexByLevel CapsFortuneFinder 6 }} ==See Also== *[[GetLevItemIndexByForm]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Leveled_List_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Leveled List Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the form of the entry at the given index for any [[LeveledItem]], [[LeveledCreature]], or [[LeveledCharacter]] list. |name = GetNthLevItem |alias = LListGetNthForm |returnType = baseform |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = levelledList |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = index |Type = int }} |example = set rRef to GetNthLevItem CapsFortuneFinder 3 }} ==See Also== *[[GetNthLevItemLevel]] *[[GetNthLevItemCount]] *[[RemoveNthLevItem]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Leveled_List_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Leveled List Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the count of the entry at the given index for any [[LeveledItem]], [[LeveledCreature]], or [[LeveledCharacter]] list. |name = GetNthLevItemCount |alias = LListGetNthCount |returnType = count:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = levelledList |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = index |Type = int }} |example = set iCount to GetNthLevItemCount CapsFortuneFinder 3 }} ==See Also== *[[GetNthLevItem]] *[[GetNthLevItemLevel]] *[[RemoveNthLevItem]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Leveled_List_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Leveled List Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the level of the entry at the given index for any [[LeveledItem]], [[LeveledCreature]], or [[LeveledCharacter]] list. |name = GetNthLevItemLevel |alias = LListGetNthLevel |returnType = level:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = levelledList |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = index |Type = int }} |example = set iLevel to GetNthLevItemLevel CapsFortuneFinder 3 }} ==See Also== *[[GetNthLevItem]] *[[GetNthLevItemCount]] *[[RemoveNthLevItem]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Leveled_List_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Leveled List Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Removes all entries with the given form from a leveled list. Returns the number of entries removed. |name = LeveledListRemoveForm |alias = LListRemoveForm |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = levelledList |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = formToRemove |Type = baseform }} |example = 
set iNumRemoved to LeveledListRemoveForm CapsFortuneFinder Caps001
==Example== 
LeveledListRemoveForm CapsFortuneFinder Caps001
Removes without checking how many }} ==See Also== *[[LeveledListAddForm]] *[[LeveledListReplaceForm]] *[[LeveledListClear]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Leveled_List_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Leveled List Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions_(FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Replaces all instances of a form in a leveled list with another. |name = LeveledListReplaceForm |alias = LListReplaceForm |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = levelledList |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = old |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = new |Type = form }} |example = LeveledListReplaceForm CapsFortuneFinder Caps001 TinCan01 }} ==See Also== *[[LeveledListAddForm]] *[[LeveledListRemoveForm]] *[[LeveledListClear]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Leveled_List_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Leveled List Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions_(FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the form of the entry at the given index for any [[LeveledItem]], [[LeveledCreature]], or [[LeveledCharacter]] list. |name = SetNthLevItem |alias = LListSetNthForm |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = levelledList |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = nthItem |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newForm |Type = baseform }} |example = SetNthLevItem CapsFortuneFinder 3 TinCan01 }} ==See Also== *[[GetNthLevItem]] *[[GetNthLevItemCount]] / [[SetNthLevItemCount]] *[[GetNthLevItemLevel]] / [[SetNthLevItemLevel]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Leveled_List_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Leveled List Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the count of the entry at the given index for any [[LeveledItem]], [[LeveledCreature]], or [[LeveledCharacter]] list. |name = SetNthLevItemCount |alias = LListSetNthCount |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = levelledList |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = index |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = count |Type = int }} |example = SetNthLevItemCount CapsFortuneFinder 3 1 }} ==See Also== *[[GetNthLevItem]] / [[SetNthLevItem]] *[[GetNthLevItemCount]] *[[GetNthLevItemLevel]] / [[SetNthLevItemLevel]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Leveled_List_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Leveled List Functions]]

revision.comment: typo fix
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the level of the entry at the given index for any [[LeveledItem]], [[LeveledCreature]], or [[LeveledCharacter]] list. |name = SetNthLevItemLevel |alias = LListSetNthLevel |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = levelledList |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = index |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = level |Type = int }} |example = SetNthLevItemLevel CapsFortuneFinder 3 20 }} ==See Also== *[[GetNthLevItem]] / [[SetNthLevItem]] *[[GetNthLevItemCount]] / [[SetNthLevItemCount]] *[[GetNthLevItemLevel]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Leveled_List_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Leveled List Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the LoadScreenType form of a [[loading screen]]. |name = SetLoadScreenType |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = loadscreen |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = loadScreenType |Type = baseform }} |example = SetLoadScreenType GeneralLoadScreen01 StandardLoadingTip }} ==See Also== *[[GetLoadScreenTypeTextRGB]] *[[SetLoadScreenTypeTextRGB]] *[[GetLoadScreenTexture]] *[[SetLoadScreenTexture]] *[[GetLoadScreenType]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:LoadScreen_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Loading Screen Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns a [[stringmap]] with keys 'r', 'g', and 'b' containing the color values of text in a [[loading screen]] type form. |name = GetLoadScreenTypeTextRGB |returnType = array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = loadScreenType |Type = baseform }} |example = 
array_var arrColors
let arrColors := GetLoadScreenTypeTextRGB StandardLoadingTip
ar_Dump arrColors
Dumping Array #1 Refs: 1 Owner FF: [ b ] : 53.000000 [ g ] : 180.000000 [ r ] : 255.000000 }} ==See Also== *[[SetLoadScreenTypeTextRGB]] *[[GetLoadScreenTexture]] *[[SetLoadScreenTexture]] *[[GetLoadScreenType]] *[[SetLoadScreenType]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:LoadScreen_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Loading Screen Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the [[Entry Point]] code of the nth entry in a perk's entry list. |name = GetNthPerkEntryFunction |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = perk |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = index |Type = int }} |example = 
GetNthPerkEntryFunction Cannibal 0
Returns 27 (Activate) ==Example== 
GetNthPerkEntryFunction  FastShot 1
Returns 8 (Calculate To Hit Chance) }} ==Entry Function Codes== 
0	Calculate Weapon Damage                         37	Reload Speed
1	Calculate My Critical Hit Chance                38	Equip Speed
2	Calculate My Critical Hit Damage                39	Action Point Regen
3	Calculate Weapon Attack AP Cost                 40	Action Point Cost

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the type of the nth entry in a perk's entry list. |name = GetNthPerkEntryType |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = perk |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = nth |Type = int }} |example = GetNthPerkEntryType FastShot 1 Returns 1. The 2nd entry for FastShot (index 1), is a one-value Entry Point. ==Entry Type Values== 
0	Dynamic
1	EntryPoint::OneValue
2	EntryPoint::TwoValue
