{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns a [[creature]]'s melee attack reach. |name = GetCreatureReach |returnType = reach:int |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = creature |Type = baseForm |Optional = y }} |example = 
 int iReach
 let iReach := RexRefr.GetCreatureReach

 int iReach
 let iReach := GetCreatureReach Rex
}} ==Notes== *This attribute is only applicable to creatures that are unarmed and attack using parts of their skeleton. ==See Also== *[[SetCreatureReach]] *[[GetCreatureDamage]] / [[SetCreatureDamage]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Toggles the specified object as an '''internal marker'''. This will signal the game to not render the object's 3D model, which will ther..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Toggles the specified object as an '''internal marker'''. This will signal the game to not render the object's 3D model, which will therefore not be visible.
*By omitting the 2nd argument, the function can be used to return whether the specified object is an internal marker, without making any changes. |name = SetInternalMarker |returnType = isInternal:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = object |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = toggle |Type = 1/0 |Optional = y }} |example = SetInternalMarker XMarkerBlue 1 }} [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Plays the specified '''animation sequence''' on the calling reference. The sequence must be defined in a '''NiControllerManager''', in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. The 2nd argument, '''nodeName''', is optional, and may be used for directing the function to a specific node in the 3D model, where the '''NiControllerManager''' is found. If omitted, the function will look for it in the root node. When called on an actor, this function can play any variant of any [[AnimGroups|Animation Group]] that is available for that actor. This is done by passing the path to the '''.kf''' animation file (relative to the '''meshes\''' folder) as the sequence name. This function differs from [[PlayGroup]] in that: *It enables to play any defined animation sequence, and is not restricted to sequences matching one of the [[AnimGroups|Animation Group]]. *It enabled to specify a node other than the root node. |name = PlayAnimSequence |returnType = successful:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = sequenceName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = nodeName |Type = string |Optional = y }} |example = objectRef.PlayAnimSequence "YugeKaboom" objectRef.PlayAnimSequence "YugeKaboom" "Blast_Node" actorRef.PlayAnimSequence "Characters\_Male\2hrjamk.kf" objectRef.PlayAnimSequence "Open" objectRef.PlayAnimSequence "Close" }} [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Animation Functions]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Model Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Removes all perks from the calling actor. *When called on the '''player character''', this function will remove all perks from the player, unless the optional argument '''forTeammates''' is non-zero, in which case it will remove all perks added for teammates. *When called on any actor other than the player, this function will only work if the '''uNPCPerks''' optional JIP LN feature is toggled in '''Data\NVSE\plugins\jip_nvse.ini''', otherwise it will have no effect. '''forTeammates''' will have no effect. |name = RemoveAllPerks |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = forTeammates |Type = 1/0 |Optional = y }} |example = actorRef.RemoveAllPerks player.RemoveAllPerks 1 }} [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Perk Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Perk Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the [[WaterType]] belonging to the object reference under the crosshair. This is limited to objects within activation range. |name = GetCrosshairWater |returnType = waterType:BaseForm |example = set rWaterType to GetCrosshairWater }} ==See also== * The [[IActivatePickLength]] gamesetting controls from which distance the player can activate a reference. * [[GetActivatorWaterType]] * [[GetCrosshairRefEx]] * [[GetCrosshairRef]] * [[ObjectUnderReticle]] * [[CrosshairRefInList]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Water Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Crosshair Functions]]

revision.comment: Added FO3 and ButcherPete categories.
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns whether or not a form has the given flag. |name = GetFormFlag |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = form |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flag |Type = int }} }} ==Example== 
if GetFormFlag GSSunnySmiles 1024
    ; GSSunnySmiles is persistent
endif
==Bit fields== This is adapted from FNVEdit. As a minimum, one should not make changes to anything labelled "Runtime". {| class="wikitable" |- ! Flag ! Bit ! Setting |- | 1 | 0 | ESM |- | 2 | 1 | Unknown 1 |- | 4 | 2 | Unknown 2 (Runtime) / Plugin selected (Editor) |- | 8 | 3 | Form cannot be saved (Runtime) / Plugin active (Editor) |- | 16 | 4 | Form initialized (Runtime) / Plugin cannot be active or selected (Editor) |- | 32 | 5 | Deleted |- | 64 | 6 | Border Region / Has Tree LOD / Constant / Hidden From Local Map / Plugin Endian |- | 128 | 7 | Turn Off Fire |- | 256 | 8 | Inaccessible |- | 512 | 9 | Casts shadows / On Local Map / Motion Blur |- | 1024 | 10 | Quest item / Persistent reference |- | 2048 | 11 | Initially disabled |- | 4096 | 12 | Ignored |- | 8192 | 13 | No Voice Filter |- | 16384 | 14 | Cannot Save (Runtime) |- | 32768 | 15 | Visible when distant |- | 65536 | 16 | Random Anim Start / High Priority LOD |- | 131072 | 17 | Dangerous / Off limits (Interior cell) / Radio Station (Talking Activator) |- | 262144 | 18 | Compressed |- | 524288 | 19 | Can't wait / Platform Specific Texture / Dead |- | 1048576 | 20 | Unknown 21 |- | 2097152 | 21 | Load Started (Runtime) // set when beginning to load the form from save |- | 4194304 | 22 | Unknown 23 |- | 8388608 | 23 | Unknown 24 // Runtime might use it for "Not dead" on non actors. |- | 16777216 | 24 | Destructible (Runtime) |- | 33554432 | 25 | Obstacle / No AI Acquire |- | 67108864 | 26 | NavMesh Generation - Filter |- | 134217728 | 27 | NavMesh Generation - Bounding Box |- | 268435456 | 28 | Non-Pipboy / Reflected by Auto Water |- | 536870912 | 29 | Child Can Use / Refracted by Auto Water |- | 1073741824 | 30 | NavMesh Generation - Ground |- | 2147483648 | 31 | Multibound |} ==See Also== *[[SetFormFlag]] *[[GetFlagsLow]] *[[GetFlagsHigh]] *[[GetReferenceFlag]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Flag Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]]

revision.comment: Added FO3 and ButcherPete categories.
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets a flag on a form. A flag can function differently depending on if it is active in a [[Reference]] or in a [[BaseForm]]. |name = SetFormFlag |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = form |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flag |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = value |Type = bool }} }} ==Example== 
SetFormFlag SunnySmiles 1024 0
; SunnySmiles is no longer persistent
This same flag can function differently depending on if it is active in a [[Reference]] or in a [[BaseForm]]. For example: 
SetFormFlag GSSunnySmiles 1024 0
; GSSunnySmiles is no longer a quest item (which is NOT the intended effect)
==Bit fields== This is adapted from FNVEdit. As a minimum, one should not make changes to anything labelled "Runtime". {| class="wikitable" |- ! Flag ! Bit ! Setting |- | 1 | 0 | ESM |- | 2 | 1 | Unknown 1 |- | 4 | 2 | Unknown 2 (Runtime) / Plugin selected (Editor) |- | 8 | 3 | Form cannot be saved (Runtime) / Plugin active (Editor) |- | 16 | 4 | Form initialized (Runtime) / Plugin cannot be active or selected (Editor) |- | 32 | 5 | Deleted |- | 64 | 6 | Border Region / Has Tree LOD / Constant / Hidden From Local Map / Plugin Endian |- | 128 | 7 | Turn Off Fire |- | 256 | 8 | Inaccessible |- | 512 | 9 | Casts shadows / On Local Map / Motion Blur |- | 1024 | 10 | Quest item / Persistent reference |- | 2048 | 11 | Initially disabled reference |- | 4096 | 12 | Ignored |- | 8192 | 13 | No Voice Filter |- | 16384 | 14 | Cannot Save (Runtime) |- | 32768 | 15 | Visible when distant |- | 65536 | 16 | Random Anim Start / High Priority LOD |- | 131072 | 17 | Dangerous / Off limits (Interior cell) / Radio Station (Talking Activator) |- | 262144 | 18 | Compressed |- | 524288 | 19 | Can't wait / Platform Specific Texture / Dead |- | 1048576 | 20 | Unknown 21 |- | 2097152 | 21 | Load Started (Runtime) // set when beginning to load the form from save |- | 4194304 | 22 | Unknown 23 // When used on a cell form with [https://www.nexusmods.com/newvegas/mods/58277 JIP LN] it causes the cell to forget the positions of any objects that have been moved (such as movable statics and misc items) which helps avoid save bloat. |- | 8388608 | 23 | Unknown 24 // Runtime might use it for "Not dead" on non actors. |- | 16777216 | 24 | Destructible (Runtime) |- | 33554432 | 25 | Obstacle / No AI Acquire |- | 67108864 | 26 | NavMesh Generation - Filter |- | 134217728 | 27 | NavMesh Generation - Bounding Box |- | 268435456 | 28 | Non-Pipboy / Reflected by Auto Water |- | 536870912 | 29 | Child Can Use / Refracted by Auto Water |- | 1073741824 | 30 | NavMesh Generation - Ground |- | 2147483648 | 31 | Multibound |} ==See Also== *[[GetFormFlag]] *[[SetFlagsLow]] *[[SetFlagsHigh]] *[[SetReferenceFlag]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Flag Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the string associated to the nth entry in a perk's entry list. ("Activate Prompt" for type 4) |name = GetNthPerkEntryString |returnType = string |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = perk |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = index |Type = int }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetNthPerkEntryType]] *[[GetNthPerkEntryValue1]] *[[SetNthPerkEntryValue1]] *[[GetNthPerkEntryValue2]] *[[SetNthPerkEntryValue2]] *[[SetNthPerkEntryString]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Perk_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Perk Functions]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:String Functions (JIP)]]

(Stage for type 6) |name = GetNthPerkEntryValue1 |returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = perk |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = index |Type = int }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetNthPerkEntryType]] *[[SetNthPerkEntryValue1]] *[[GetNthPerkEntryValue2]] *[[SetNthPerkEntryValue2]] *[[GetNthPerkEntryString]] *[[SetNthPerkEntryString]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Perk_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Perk Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns a [[bitmask]] of the calling actor's current movement flags. ==Actor Movement Flags== 
1	Forward
2	Backward
4	Left
8	Right
16	Turn Left
32	Turn Right
256	Walking
512	Running
1024	Sneaking
2048	Swimming
4096	Jumping
8192	Flying
16384	Falling
32768	Sliding
|name = GetActorMovementFlags |returnType = movementFlags:int |referenceType = reference |example = 
set iMovementFlags to actorRef.GetActorMovementFlags
if eval (iMovementFlags & 1280) == 1280
	; Actor is sneaking and walking
endif
}} ==See Also== * [[SetPlayerMovementFlags]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]] [[Category:Bit Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Toggles sneak mode on/off for the player character. |name = TogglePlayerSneaking |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = sneakMod..."
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Toggles sneak mode on/off for the player character. |name = TogglePlayerSneaking |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = sneakMode |Type = 1/0 }} |example = TogglePlayerSneaking 1 }} [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Player Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = When called from a '''[[OnHit]]'''/'''[[OnHitWith]]''' script block (or the corresponding NVSE event handlers), or from a '''[[ScriptEffectStart]]''' block in an '''[[Ammo]]''' form '''Impact Script''', will return the hitting weapon's base damage, including modifiers for the firing actor's skill and the weapon's condition. |name = GetHitBaseWeaponDamage |returnType = baseWpnDmg:float |referenceType = reference |example = set fBaseWpnDmg to targetRef.GetHitBaseWeaponDamage }} ==See Also== *[[GetHitHealthDamage]] *[[GetCalculatedWeaponDamage]] *[[GetHitLimbDamage]] *[[GetHitAttacker]] *[[GetHitProjectile]] *[[GetHitWeapon]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = When called from a '''[[OnHit]]'''/'''[[OnHitWith]]''' script block (or the corresponding NVSE event handlers), or from a '''ScriptEffe..."
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = When called from a '''[[OnHit]]'''/'''[[OnHitWith]]''' script block (or the corresponding NVSE event handlers), or from a '''[[ScriptEffectStart]]''' block in an '''[[Ammo]]''' form '''Impact Script''', will return the '''fatigue damage''' caused by the current hit. |name = GetHitFatigueDamage |returnType = fatigueDmg:float |referenceType = reference |example = set fFatigueDmg to targetRef.GetHitFatigueDamage }} ==See Also== *[[GetHitHealthDamage]] *[[GetHitLimbDamage]] *[[GetHitAttacker]] *[[GetHitProjectile]] *[[GetHitWeapon]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns a [[StringMap]] of [[GameSetting]]s, where the keys are the GameSetting names and the values are the settings' values. The type of gamesetting to include is filtered by ''gmstType''. ''gmstType'' is a [[Bitmask]] with the following flags: 
1 = Float/Int-value settings (starts with "i"/"f")
2 = String-value settings (starts with "s")
It is set to 3 if not specified (all gamesettings included). |name = GetGameSettings |returnVal = settings |returnType = array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = gmstType |Type = int |Optional = y |Value = 0-3 }} |example = array_var aSettings let aSettings := GetGameSettings (0b10) ;get only string-value settings The above example makes use of [[Binary Notation]] to clarify that only the second bit is being set. }} [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:GameSetting Functions]] [[Category:Bit Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the linear velocity on each of the 3 X/Y/Z '''global axes''', of the collision object (if any) attached to the specified node in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. The result XYZ are placed into the 3 specified '''Script Variables'''. *The velocity is in units of '''0.1 Meter/sec''', or '''7 game units/sec'''. *If the '''optional''' 5th argument, '''getLocal''', is non-zero, the result velocities are relative to the object's '''local axes'''. *If the 3D model does not have the specified node, or the node does not have a mobile collision object, the function returns 0, and the script variables are unchanged. |name = GetLinearVelocityAlt |returnType = success:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = nodeName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = resX |Type = ScriptVar }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = resY |Type = ScriptVar }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = resZ |Type = ScriptVar }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = getLocal |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }} |example = objectRef.GetLinearVelocityAlt "TumbleweedNV" fVelX fVelY fVelZ 1 }} ==See Also== *[[GetLinearVelocity]] *[[SetLinearVelocity]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Movement Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the angular velocity of each of the 3 X/Y/Z axes, of the collision object (if any) attached to the specified node in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. The result XYZ are placed into the 3 specified '''Script Variables'''. *By default, the function returns the velocities relative to the object's '''local axes''', unless the '''optional''' 5th argument, '''getGlobal''', is non-zero, on which case the velocities are relative to the world, '''global axes'''. *The velocity is in units of '''2π rad/s''' (essentially, '''revolutions/second'''). *If the 3D model does not have the specified node, or the node does not have a mobile collision object, the function returns 0, and the script variables are unchanged. |name = GetAngularVelocityAlt |returnType = success:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = nodeName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = resX |Type = ScriptVar }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = resY |Type = ScriptVar }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = resZ |Type = ScriptVar }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = getGlobal |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }} |example = objectRef.GetAngularVelocityAlt "TumbleweedNV" fVelX fVelY fVelZ }} ==See Also== *[[SetAngularVelocityEx]] *[[SetAngularVelocity]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Movement Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the angular velocity of each of the 3 X/Y/Z axes, of the collision object (if any) attached to the specified node in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. *By default, the function sets the velocities relative to the object's '''local axes''', unless the '''optional''' 5th argument, '''setGlobal''', is non-zero, on which case the velocities are set relative to the world, '''global axes'''. *The velocity is in units of '''2π rad/s''' (essentially, '''revolutions/second'''). *If the 3D model does not have the specified node, or the node does not have a mobile collision object, the function returns 0. |name = SetAngularVelocityEx |returnType = success:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = nodeName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = velocityX |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = velocityY |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = velocityZ |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = setGlobal |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }} |example = objectRef.SetAngularVelocityEx "TumbleweedNV" 0 15 0 }} ==See Also== *[[GetAngularVelocityAlt]] *[[GetAngularVelocity]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Movement Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the calling actor's current linear velocity on each of the 3 X/Y/Z '''global axes'''. The result XYZ are placed into the 3 specif..."
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the calling actor's current linear velocity on each of the 3 X/Y/Z '''global axes'''. The result XYZ are placed into the 3 specified '''Script Variables'''. *If the '''optional''' 4th argument, '''getLocal''', is non-zero, the result velocities are relative to the actor's '''local axes'''. |name = GetActorVelocityAlt |returnType = success:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = resX |Type = ScriptVar }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = resY |Type = ScriptVar }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = resZ |Type = ScriptVar }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = getLocal |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }} |example = actorRef.GetActorVelocityAlt fVelX fVelY fVelZ 1 }} ==Notes== *The sign of the returned value indicates direction: a negative Z velocity indicates the actor is speeding downwards. {{Template:Velocity Measurement}} ==See Also== *[[SetActorVelocity]] *[[GetObjectVelocity]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Movement Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the value of a specified property of the collision object (if any) attached to the specified node in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. |name = GetCollisionObjProperty |returnType = propertyValue:float |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = nodeName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = propertyID |Type = int |Value = 0-7 }} ==Property IDs== 
0	Friction
1	Restitution
2	Linear Damping
3	Angular Damping
4	Inertia: X
5	Inertia: Y
6	Inertia: Z
7	Body Mass
|example = set fLinearDamping to objectRef.GetCollisionObjProperty "TumbleweedNV" 2 }} ==Notes== *'''Friction''' - Surface smoothness; how easily it slides along other bodies. *'''Restitution''' - Body bounciness; how much energy it has after colliding. Less than 1.0 loses energy; greater than 1.0 gains energy. *'''Linear Damping''' - Rate of linear deceleration. A value of 0.1 will remove 10% linear velocity every second. *'''Angular Damping''' - Rate of angular deceleration. A value of 0.05 will remove 5% angular velocity every second. *'''Inertia: X/Y/Z''' - Resistance to change in velocity or direction of motion (per axis) from external forces. *'''Body Mass''' - The body's mass in kg. A mass of zero represents an immovable object. ==See Also== *[[SetCollisionObjProperty]] *[[GetCollisionObjLayerType]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the value of a specified property of the collision object (if any) attached to the specified node in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. *Alternatively, an asterisk "*" may be passed as the node name, in which case the property value will be set for any child node of the reference's node that has collision. |name = SetCollisionObjProperty |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = nodeName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = propertyID |Type = int |Value = 0-7 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = float }} ==Property IDs== 
0	Friction
1	Restitution
2	Linear Damping
3	Angular Damping
4	Inertia: X
5	Inertia: Y
6	Inertia: Z
7	Body Mass
|example = objectRef.SetCollisionObjProperty "TumbleweedNV" 2 0.15 objectRef.SetCollisionObjProperty "*" 2 0.15 }} ==Notes== *'''Friction''' - Surface smoothness; how easily it slides along other bodies. *'''Restitution''' - Body bounciness; how much energy it has after colliding. Less than 1.0 loses energy; greater than 1.0 gains energy. *'''Linear Damping''' - Rate of linear deceleration. A value of 0.1 will remove 10% linear velocity every second. *'''Angular Damping''' - Rate of angular deceleration. A value of 0.05 will remove 5% angular velocity every second. *'''Inertia: X/Y/Z''' - Resistance to change in velocity or direction of motion (per axis) from external forces. *'''Body Mass''' - The body's mass in kg. A mass of zero represents an immovable object. ==See Also== *[[GetCollisionObjProperty]] *[[SetCollisionObjLayerType]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Model Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the [[Collision Layer Types|layer type]] of the collision object (if any) attached to the specified node in the calling reference..."
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the [[Collision Layer Types|layer type]] of the collision object (if any) attached to the specified node in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. |name = GetCollisionObjLayerType |returnType = layerType:int |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = nodeName |Type = string }} |example = set iLayerType to objectRef.GetCollisionObjLayerType "TumbleweedNV" }} ==See Also== *[[SetCollisionObjLayerType]] *[[GetCollisionObjProperty]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Model Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the [[Collision Layer Types|layer type]] of the collision object (if any) attached to the specified node in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. *Alternatively, an asterisk "*" may be passed as the node name, in which case the layer type will be set for any child node of the reference's node that has collision. |name = SetCollisionObjLayerType |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = nodeName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = layerType |Type = int |Value = 1-42 }} |example = objectRef.SetCollisionObjLayerType "TumbleweedNV" 15
objectRef.SetCollisionObjLayerType "*" 15 }} ==See Also== *[[GetCollisionObjLayerType]] *[[SetCollisionObjProperty]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Model Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets the file path to the '''NIF model''' used by the [[calling reference]]. Unlike similar functions, such as [[SetModelPathEx]], the model is changed <u>for the calling reference only</u> and will not affect other instances of the same base form. *The file path is relative to '''..\Data\Meshes\'''. *Calling the function with the '''nifPath''' argument omitted will reset the model to the one defined for the reference's base form. *A call to this function should be followed by a call to [[Update3D]] on the reference, in order to apply the changes. |name = SetRefrModelPath |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = nifPath |Type = string |Optional = y }} |example = objectRef.SetRefrModelPath "Clutter\LiquorBottles\WhiskeyBottle01Empty.nif" }} ==Notes== *Using this on a [[reference]] whos baseform is a [[Static]] will also make the replacement NIF static regardless of how the NIF is setup due to how Static forms function.
==See Also== *[[SetModelPath]] *[[SetModelPathEx]] - deprecated in favor of [[SetModelPath]] (not a typo, just a weird history).
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Model Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an [[array_var]] of a quest's visible objectives. |returnType = array_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = quest |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = ShowCompletedObjectives |Type = bool }} |example = 
let aQuestObjectives := GetQuestObjectives QuestREF 0
Returns an array of the quest's visible active objectives. 
let aQuestObjectives := GetQuestObjectives QuestREF 1
Returns an array of the quest's visible completed objectives }} [[Category:Quest Functions]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Quest Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

revision.comment: Added note about combining function with GetQuestFailed.
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an [[array_var]] of active quests or completed quests. |returnType = array_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ShowCompletedQuests |Type = bool |Optional = y }} |example = 
let aQuests := GetQuests
Returns an array of active quests. 
let aQuests := GetQuests 1
Returns an array of completed quests. ==Notes== *If failed quests need to be filtered out, use [[GetQuestFailed]]. }} [[Category:Quest Functions]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Quest Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns a float of the game's current session duration in seconds. |returnType = float }} ==See Also== *[[GetTimePlayed]] - A more robu..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns a float of the game's current session duration in seconds. |returnType = float }}
==See Also== *[[GetTimePlayed]] - A more robust alternative. [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Time Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns whether or not the calling actor reference, or baseform is incapable of moving. |name = GetIsImmobile |returnType = GetIsImmobile:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = object |Type = baseform |Optional = y }} |example = CraigBooneRef.GetIsImmobile }} ==See Also== *[[IsMobile]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the header version number of the specified plugin as a float. |name = GetPluginHeaderVersion |referenceType = |returnType = f..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the header version number of the specified plugin as a float. |name = GetPluginHeaderVersion |referenceType = |returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = PluginName |Type = string }} |example = GetPluginHeaderVersion "FalloutNV.esm" }}
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:File Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the first form in the calling reference's inventory that is in the passed list. |name = PickFromList |returnType = form |referenceType = [ref] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = list |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = SearchStartPos |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = SearchHowMany |Type = int |Optional = y }}}} ==Example== 
Player.PickFromList GunsList
Will return the first form from the list that is found in the Player's inventory. Returns 0 if none of the forms is found in the Player's inventory 
Player.PickFromList GunsList 5
Will start the search from index 5 of the list (default is 0), skipping the first five forms (indexes 0,1,2,3,4). 
Player.PickFromList GunsList 5 5
Will search five forms starting from index 5 of the list (indexes 5,6,7,8,9). [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Sets whether or not the calling actor reference will wobble their gun. |name=SetNoGunWobble |referenceType = [ref] |returnType = void |alias = SetNoGunWobble |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Type = int |Value = 0/1 }} |example = CraigBooneRef.SetNoGunWobble }} [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the interrupt '''[[AI Package]]''' currently in use by the calling actor. |name = GetInterruptPackage |returnType = package:ref |referenceType = reference |example = set rPackage to actorRef.GetInterruptPackage }} ==See Also== *[[GetBasePackages]] *[[GetCurrentAIPackage]] *[[GetCurrentPackage]] *[[GetIsCurrentPackage]] *[[GetNthPackage]] *[[GetPackageCount]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:AI Functions]] [[Category:Package Functions]]

{{Function |origin = LU |summary = Sets the flag of the specified encounter zone. |name = SetZoneFlag |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = EncounterZone |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = ZoneFlag |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = NewZoneFlagValue |Type = int }} }} ==Example== 
SetZoneFlag BisonSteve 1 1
; Encounter zone will no longer reset.
==Bit fields== {| class="wikitable" |- ! Flag ! Bit ! Setting |- | 1 | 0 | Never Resets |- | 2 | 1 | Match PC level below minimum |} ==See Also== * [[GetZoneFlag]] * [[GetZone]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Encounter Zone Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Overrides the hardcoded '''Multiplier''' (1.0, by default) defining the rate and maximum penalty to armor DT/DR when item condition percent falls below the 50% threshold. Above the 50% threshold, this formula is ignored and the '''PenaltyMult''' is just 1.0. The formula: 
PenaltyMult = 1.0 - (0.5 - CondPercent[0, 1.0]) * Multiplier
*The function may be called with the argument omitted, in which case it will return the current value of the multiplier without modifying it. |name = SetArmorConditionPenalty |returnType = multiplier:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = multiplier |Type = float |Optional = y }} |example = 
SetArmorConditionPenalty 1.5
The maximum penalty mult, at 0% condition, is 0.25 (i.e. EffectiveDTDR = 0.25 * BaseDTDR). }} ==See Also== *[[SetConditionDamagePenalty]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Armor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Armor Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns 1 if the calling actor reference is currently being affected by any game effect ([[Actor Effect]], [[Object Effect]], or Ingest..."
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns 1 if the calling actor reference is currently being affected by any game effect ([[Actor Effect]], [[Object Effect]], or [[Ingestible]]) from the specified [[FormList]]. |name = IsSpellTargetList |returnType = spellTarget:0/1 |referenceType = actorRef |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = effectList |Type = FormList }} |example = set bSpellTarget to actorRef.IsSpellTargetList NVLegionBannedList }} ==See Also== *[[IsSpellTargetAlt]] *[[ActorHasEffect]] *[[HasMagicEffect]] *[[OnMagicEffectHit]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Effect Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Excludes specific combat-AI action types from being performed by the calling actor ref, when in combat. The function takes a single argu..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Excludes specific combat-AI action types from being performed by the calling actor ref, when in combat. The function takes a single argument, which is a '''[[Using Bitmasks|bitmask]]''' composed of up to 27 optional bits (see table below). A bit set to '''1''' will exclude the associated combat action from being performed. *For clarity and convenience, it is recommended to use a '''[[Binary Notation]]''' for the bitmask argument. ==Combat Action Bits== 
Bit #	Action
-----	------
0	ATTACK_RANGED_EXPLOSIVE
1	ATTACK_RANGED_EXPLOSIVE_FROM_COVER
2	ATTACK_RANGED
3	ATTACK_RANGED_FROM_COVER
4	ATTACK_GRENADE
5	ATTACK_GRENADE_FLUSH_TARGET
6	ATTACK_GRENADE_FROM_COVER
7	ATTACK_MELEE
8	ATTACK_HAND_TO_HAND
9	MOVE
10	MOVE_AND_SWITCH_TO_MELEE
11	MOVE_AND_ATTACK_RANGED
12	MOVE_AND_ATTACK_RANGED_EXPLOSIVE
13	MOVE_AND_ATTACK_GRENADE
14	DRAW_WEAPON
15	SWITCH_WEAPON
16	AVOID_THREAT
17	SEARCH
18	INVESTIGATE
19	DODGE
20	IGNORE_BLOCKED_TARGET
21	FLEE
22	ACTIVATE_COMBAT_ITEM
23	USE_COMBAT_ITEM
24	ACQUIRE_LINE_OF_SIGHT
25	HIDE
26	APPROACH_TARGET
|name = SetExcludedCombatActions |referenceType = actorRef |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = excludeMask |Type = int }} |example = 
VeronicaRef.SetExcludedCombatActions (0b000101000001000010110000000)
VeronicaRef is excluded from performing the combat actions: '''ATTACK_MELEE''', '''ATTACK_HAND_TO_HAND''', '''MOVE_AND_SWITCH_TO_MELEE''', '''SWITCH_WEAPON''', '''FLEE''' and '''USE_COMBAT_ITEM'''. 
VeronicaRef.SetExcludedCombatActions 0
VeronicaRef may perform any combat action. }} ==Notes== *The effects of this function are reset upon loading a game. ==See Also== *[[GetExcludedCombatActions]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns a '''[[Using Bitmasks|bitmask]]''' specifying combat-AI action types the calling actor is excluded from performing in combat (see..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns a '''[[Using Bitmasks|bitmask]]''' specifying combat-AI action types the calling actor is excluded from performing in combat (see '''[[SetExcludedCombatActions]]'''). |name = GetExcludedCombatActions |returnType = excludeMask:int |referenceType = actorRef |example = 
if eval (actorRef.GetExcludedCombatActions & (0b1010000))

	(Is actorRef excluded from performing the combat actions ATTACK_GRENADE or ATTACK_GRENADE_FROM_COVER ?)
}} ==See Also== *[[SetExcludedCombatActions]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns a float in the range of 0 to 100, indicating how illuminated a point is at the specified X/Y/Z, in the current world/cell. *A val..."
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns a float in the range of 0 to 100, indicating how illuminated a point is at the specified X/Y/Z, in the current world/cell. *A value of 0 indicates full darkness, whereas 100 indicates fully-lit. |name = GetLightAmountAtPoint |returnType = lightAmount:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = posX |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = posY |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = posZ |Type = float }} |example = set fLightAmount to GetLightAmountAtPoint 1123 -5057 900 }} [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Retrieves the XYZ coordinates of the position the reticle (crosshair) is pointing at, and outputs the results into the 3 specified '''Scr..."
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Retrieves the XYZ coordinates of the position the reticle (crosshair) is pointing at, and outputs the results into the 3 specified '''Script Variables'''. *If the position is a valid one, the function returns 1, otherwise 0. |name = GetReticlePosAlt |returnType = validPos:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = resPosX |Type = ScriptVar }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = resPosY |Type = ScriptVar }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = resPosZ |Type = ScriptVar }} |example = GetReticlePosAlt fPosX fPosY fPosZ }} ==See Also== *[[GetReticleRange]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Crosshair Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Calculates the X (pitch) and Z (yaw) angles between two given 3D points in space, and outputs the result angles into the 2 specified '''S..."
Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Plays a game '''Sound''' centered on a specified '''NiNode''' in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. The sound will emanate from the..."
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Plays a game '''Sound''' centered on a specified '''NiNode''' in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. The sound will emanate from the world position of the node. |name = PlaySound3DNode |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = gameSound |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = nodeName |Type = string }} |example = player.PlaySound3DNode AMBCeilingFanLP "Bip01 L Thumb1" }} [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Sound Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns whether or not the passed '''Weapon''' (1st argument), which can be either a base form, or a reference of a weapon placed in the..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns whether or not the passed '''Weapon''' (1st argument), which can be either a base form, or a reference of a weapon placed in the world, has an optional weapon mod of the specified type. If it does, the function returns the index (1-3) of the weapon mod. Otherwise, 0 is returned. |name = WeaponHasModType |returnType = modSlot:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = modType |Type = int |Value = 1-16 }} ==Weapon Mod Type IDs== 
1	Increase Damage
2	Increase Clip Capacity
3	Decrease Spread
4	Decrease Weight
5	Regenerate Ammo (Shots)
6	Regenerate Ammo (Seconds)
7	Decrease Equip Time
8	Increase Rate of Fire
9	Increase Projectile Speed
10	Increase Max Condition
11	Silencer
12	Split Beam
13	VATS Bonus
14	Increase Zoom (Scope)
16	Suppressor
|example = set iSilencerSlot to WeaponHasModType rWeapon 11 }} ==See Also== *[[EquippedWeaponHasModType]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions (JIP)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = The function takes a string, a font ID (equivalent to XML <font>), and a horizontal wrap-width (equivalent to XML <wrapwidth&..."
Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Rotates the [[Calling reference|calling reference]] around the specified X,Y,Z world position, by the specified X,Y,Z angles. The object..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Rotates the [[Calling reference|calling reference]] around the specified X,Y,Z world position, by the specified X,Y,Z angles.
The object's world position and orientation angles are modified accordingly. |name = RotateAroundPoint |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = pointX |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = pointY |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = pointZ |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = angleX |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = angleY |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = angleZ |Type = float }} |example = objectRef.RotateAroundPoint 81780 101687 -11013 0 0 30 }} ==See Also== *[[GetPosEx]] *[[SetPosEx]] *[[SetAngleEx]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Movement Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an '''array_var''' containing all '''perks''' the calling '''Actor''' reference has. *When called on '''the player''', depending on the function's 1st argument, '''forTeammates''', the result array will contain either the player's own perks, or the player's perks for active teammates. *When called on any other actor, the result depends on whether the '''uNPCPerks''' optional JIP LN feature is enabled or not. **If enabled, the array will contain all the perks owned by the actor (both startup and script-added perks). If a teammate and the argument is non-zero, the player's perks for teammates will also be included. **If disabled, the function will return a non-empty array if and only if the actor is a teammate and the argument is non-zero. *If the 2nd argument, '''includeHidden''', is non-zero, all applicable hidden perks will be included in the result, as well. |name = GetAllPerks |returnType = allPerks:array_var |referenceType = actorRef |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = forTeammates |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = includeHidden |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }} |example = let aAllPerks := player.GetAllPerks 0 1 let aAllPerks := actorRef.GetAllPerks 1 }} [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Perk Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:Perk Functions (JIP)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the special '''Worldspace Cell''' containing all '''persistent references''' in the specified '''Worldspace'''. |name = GetWorld..."
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the special '''Worldspace Cell''' containing all '''persistent references''' in the specified '''Worldspace'''. |name = GetWorldspacePersistentCell |returnType = persistentCell:form |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = worldspace |Type = form }} |example = set rPersistentCell to GetWorldspacePersistentCell FreesideWorld }} [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:World Space Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:World Space Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the cell, position and rotation of the calling reference, where it was '''originally placed in the GECK editor'''. The function outputs the X/Y/Z position and rotation values into the 6 '''Script Variable''' arguments, whereas the cell is the return value of the function.
*If the calling reference was not placed in the editor, the function returns 0. |name = GetEditorPosition |returnType = originCell:form |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = outPosX |Type = ScriptVar }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = outPosY |Type = ScriptVar }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = outPosZ |Type = ScriptVar }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = outRotX |Type = ScriptVar }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = outRotY |Type = ScriptVar }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = outRotZ |Type = ScriptVar }} |example = set rOriginCell to someRef.GetEditorPosition fPosX fPosY fPosZ fRotX fRotY fRotZ }} ==See Also== *[[MoveToEditorPosition]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Movement Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Moves the calling reference to a specified node. |name = SynchronizePosition |alias = SyncPos |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = targetRef |Type = reference |Optional = y |Value = 0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = syncRot(lock view to bone angle) |Type = 1/0 |Optional = y |Value = 0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = z_offset_from_bone |Type = float |Optional = y |Value = 0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = nodeName |Type = string |Optional = y |Value = "" }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetEditorPosition]] *[[GetStartingPos]] *[[MoveTo]] *[[MoveToCell]] *[[MoveToNode]] *[[MoveToReticle]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Movement Functions]]

{{Bc|Functions (JIP)}} <big>The JIP Extra-Data framework introduces a new dynamic and reliable method for storing extra game-data, of any basic type, on any object reference in the game. '''Most notably this also includes [[Inventory Reference|Inventory References]]'''.
The data is stored and accessed via a set of script commands listed hereunder; It is saved in the game and persists with the reference, until either manually removed (via script), or the reference is deleted.
Any and all extra-data stored on a reference is "owned" by the particular mod by which the data was stored on said reference. The data is compartmentalized, such that '''multiple mods may safely store their own extra-data on the same reference, without conflict.''' Any mod can freely access extra-data owned by other mods, but may not modify it.
For each reference, there are a total of 20 data slots available. The below table details the slot indices and which data-types may be stored in each: {|class="wikitable centercells" |- !Slot Index !Data Type |- |<nowiki>0-17</nowiki> |Either Numeric, or Form/Reference |- |<nowiki>18-19</nowiki> |String Only |} </big>
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |originVersion = 56.85 |summary = Returns whether the calling reference has any stored [[Extra Data Functions (JIP)|Extra Data]] owned by: *If the function's optional argument is omitted - the parent mod of the calling script. Otherwise *The mod whose '''mod index''' was specified. |name = RefHasExtraData |returnType = 1/0 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = altModIndex |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = 
bHasXData = objectRef.RefHasExtraData
iIndex = GetModIndex "SomeOtherMod.esp"
bHasXData = objectRef.RefHasExtraData iIndex
}} ==See Also== *[[GetRefExtraData]] *[[SetRefExtraData]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Extra Data Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |originVersion = 56.85 |summary = Returns the data stored at the specified slot index of the calling reference's [[Extra Data Functions (JIP)|Extra Data]], owned by: *If the function's 2nd, optional argument is omitted - the parent mod of the calling script. Otherwise - *The mod whose '''mod index''' was specified. |name = GetRefExtraData |returnType = special |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = slotIndex |Type = int |Value = 0-19 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = altModIndex |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = 
fNumVal = objectRef.GetRefExtraData 2
sStringVal = objectRef.GetRefExtraData 18
iIndex = GetModIndex "SomeOtherMod.esp"
rRefVal = objectRef.GetRefExtraData 12 iIndex
}} ==Notes== {|class="wikitable centercells" |- !Slot Index !Data Type |- |0-17 |Either Numeric, or Form/Reference |- |18-19 |String Only |} ==See Also== *[[RefHasExtraData]] *[[SetRefExtraData]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Extra Data Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |originVersion = 56.85 |summary = Sets the value stored at the specified slot index of the calling reference's [[Extra Data Functions (JIP)|Extra Data]]. *The value may be numeric, a form/reference, or a string. '''See table below for data-type restrictions per slot'''. *Calling the function with both arguments omitted will remove '''all''' extra-data from the calling reference. |name = SetRefExtraData |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = slotIndex |Type = int |Value = 0-19 |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = value |Type = any |Optional = y }} |example = objectRef.SetRefExtraData 2 -903.015
objectRef.SetRefExtraData 6 WeapNV357Revolver
objectRef.SetRefExtraData 15 iIntVar
objectRef.SetRefExtraData 19 "Girly Chew Hossencofft"
objectRef.SetRefExtraData }} ==Notes== {|class="wikitable centercells" |- !Slot Index !Data Type |- |<nowiki>0-17</nowiki> |Either Numeric, or Form/Reference |- |<nowiki>18-19</nowiki> |String Only |} ==See Also== *[[RefHasExtraData]] *[[GetRefExtraData]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Extra Data Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |originVersion = 56.85 |summary = When toggled on an object reference, makes it so the reference will not be saved by the game and will be deleted once its 3D is unloaded. *This function will only work on non-persistent, dynamic (mod index = '''FF''') references. *By omitting the argument, the function can be used to return whether the calling reference is marked for purging, without making any changes. |name = TogglePurgeOnUnload |returnType = isToggled:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = toggle |Type = 1/0 |Optional = y }} |example = objectRef.TogglePurgeOnUnload 1
bPurgeOnUnload = objectRef.TogglePurgeOnUnload }} [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]]

{{Deprecated}} {{Function |origin = LU |summary = Now always returns zero. '''Do not use'''. Mods using this should be updated to use [[GetPluginVersion|GetPluginVersion]]. <s>Returns the version of this plugin.</s> (deprecated by [[GetPluginVersion|GetPluginVersion]] in NVSE 4.5.7) |name = GetLNVersion |alias = GetJIPLNVersion |returnType = int }} [[Category:Deprecated Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]]

{{Deprecated}} {{Function |origin = LU |summary = Returns the default message display time (defaults to 2.0). This is the display time for UI Messages about added/removed items, critical hits, crippled limbs, etc. |name = GetMessageDisplayTime |alias = [[GetMDT]] |returnType = float }} [[Category:Deprecated Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |originVersion = 56.85 |summary = Returns the rotation value of the north marker of the specified '''interior cell'''. If no marker is present the function will return 0. |name = GetCellNorthRotation |returnType = northRotation:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = interiorCell |Type = form }} |example = fCellZRotation = GetCellNorthRotation NellisHangar2 }} [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Cell_Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Cell_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |originVersion = 56.90 |summary = Returns the specified [[Projectile]]'s emmited [[Light]] form (if any). |name = GetProjectileLight |returnType = light:form |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = projectile |Type = form }} |example = set rProjLight to GetProjectileLight 40mmGrenadeProjectile }} ==See Also== *[[SetProjectileLight]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions]] [[Category:Light Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |originVersion = 56.90 |summary = Sets the specified [[Projectile]]'s emmited [[Light]] form. *The 2nd argument is optional. When omitted, the emitted light will be set to None. |name = SetProjectileLight |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = projectile |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = light |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = SetProjectileLight 40mmGrenadeProjectile LightViolet SetProjectileLight 40mmGrenadeProjectile }} ==See Also== *[[GetProjectileLight]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions]] [[Category:Light Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |originVersion = 56.90 |summary = Returns whether the specified item form was marked as "unique" (via [[ToggleItemUnique]]). *Unique items will not be available as optional repair parts sources in the item repair menu. |name = IsItemUnique |returnType = isUnique:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = item |Type = form }} |example = set bIsUnique to IsItemUnique WeapNVMinigunUnique }} ==See Also== *[[ToggleItemUnique]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |originVersion = 56.90 |summary = Returns a [[Map Array]] containing, as '''values''', the [[Inventory Reference|Inventory References]] associated with the '''UI ListBox''' items of the current active menu. The '''keys''' of the map-array are the indices of their paired items in the '''un-filtered''' '''UI ListBox'''. These indices may be used for easy access to the '''UI Tile''' connected to the list item, for retrieving/modifying its properties. Example: 
GetUIFloatAlt "ContainerMenu/NOGLOW_BRANCH/CM_ItemsRect/CM_Items_InventoryList/*:24/x"
Supported menus: 
1002	Pip-Boy Inventory Menu
1008	Container Menu
1035	Repair Menu
1053	Barter Menu
1058	Repair Services Menu
The function takes 2 optional arguments: *'''inclFiltered''' - If non-zero, all list items will be returned in the array, including those not shown. Otherwise, only list items that are shown and selectable will be returned. *'''rightPanel''' - In menus where there are two panels (left and right), passing this argument as non-zero will return an array of the right-panel items. |name = GetMenuItemListRefs |returnType = menuItems:array_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = inclFiltered |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = rightPanel |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }} |example = let aMenuItems := GetMenuItemListRefs let aMenuItems := GetMenuItemListRefs 1 1 }} ==Notes== *For iterating over the returned [[Map Array]], use a [[ForEach]] loop. [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:UI Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Reference Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |originVersion = 56.95 |summary = When called from the script of an object that is inside a container/inventory, the function will return the object as an [[Inventory Reference]], which may then be used with any of the [[Inventory Reference Functions]] scope. |name = GetSelfAsInventoryRef |returnType = inventoryRef:ref |example = set rSelfInventoryRef to GetSelfAsInventoryRef }} [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Inventory Reference Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |originVersion = 57.10 |summary = Attaches a line mesh to a selected node in the loaded 3D model of the calling reference. The function takes 7 arguments, as follows: *'''targetNode''' - The name of the parent node where the line is attached as a child. *'''lineName''' - The name that is given to the line block (this name can later be used to transform/remove the line using any of the '''NifBlock''' functions). *'''length''' - The initial length of the line, in game units (the length can later be changed using [[SetNifBlockScale]]; '''length = scale'''). *'''red, green, blue & alpha''' - The RGB colour and alpha (opacity) of the line; each 0-255. |name = AttachLine |referenceType = reference |returnType = successful:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = targetNode |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = lineName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = length |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = red |Type = int |Value = 0-255 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = green |Type = int |Value = 0-255 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = blue |Type = int |Value = 0-255 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = alpha |Type = int |Value = 0-255 }} |example = player.AttachLine "ProjectileNode" "LaserPointer" 384 255 95 95 179 }} ==Notes== *On successful attach, the function returns 1, otherwise 0. *To remove an attached line completely, use [[RemoveNifBlock]]. ==See Also== *[[RemoveNifBlock]] *[[AttachModel]] *[[InsertNode]] *[[ModelHasBlock]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Misc Reference Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Model Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |originVersion = 57.10 |summary = Sets the maximum condition damage a single attack hit can inflict to any worn armor item, as a percentage of the armor base health. This function, along with the '''fDamageToArmorPercentage''' [[Settings|GMST]], can be used for tweaking and balancing armor condition damage. *This setting is saved in the game. *The function may be called with the argument omitted, in which case it will return the setting's current value, without modifying it. |name = SetDamageToArmorMaxPercent |returnType = maxPercent:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = maxPercent |Type = float |Value = 0.0-1.0 |Optional = y }} |example = 
SetDamageToArmorMaxPercent 0.025
Sets the maximum condition damage per hit to 2.5% of armor base health. }} ==See Also== *[[SetArmorConditionPenalty]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Armor Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Armor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |originVersion = 57.15 |summary = Registers a source file to be handled by '''[[JIP LN NVSE Script Runner Introduction|Script Runner]]'''. The main purpose of this function is to make it possible to register SR script files selectively and conditionally, on game restart. *This function may only be called from a '''Game Restart''' ('''gr_''' prefix) '''SR''' script, and will have no effect otherwise. *The '''filePath''' string argument should be the path to the source file, relative to '''..\Data\NVSE\plugins\scripts\''', and must include at least one subfolder. i.e. The file must be located in a subfolder of '''..\scripts\'''. *The file name should follow the standard naming convention of '''SR''' files - the prefix will determine when the script is executed. *If successful, the function returns '''1'''. The function will return '''0''' if: **The file could not be accessed, or **The source script failed to compile, or **A file with the same name was already registered. |name = RegisterSRScript |returnType = successful:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = filePath |Type = string }} |example = RegisterSRScript "my_optional\gl_Option2.txt" }} ==See Also== *[[RunBatchScript]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Script Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Script Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |originVersion = 57.15 |summary = Sets the casing item type for the specified [[Ammo]] type. *The casing is an item that has a chance to spawn in the player's inventory when firing the ammo type. |name = SetAmmoCasing |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ammoType |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = casingItem |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = SetAmmoCasing Ammo12GaSlug EggTimer SetAmmoCasing Ammo12GaSlug ; (Sets the casing item to None) }} ==See Also== *[[GetAmmoCasing]] [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Ammo Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Ammo Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns if a form has a specified keyword associated with it. |name = HasKeyword |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = form:form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = "keyword:string" }} |example = 
if HasKeyword rSomeRef "InvisibleDoor"         
     ; Success! This is in fact an invisible door!
endif
}} ==Notes== *If a keyword is assigned to a baseform, HasKeyword will still return true if a REF using that baseform is passed as the form to check. *If checking to see if an object is part of some group, Using '''HasKeyword''' to check for a single keyword is faster then checking a [[FormList]] full of other members of the group. ==See Also== *[[AssignKeyword]] *[[GetKeywordForms]] *[[HasKeywordCond]] [[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Keyword Functions_(JIP)]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns an [[array_var]] of forms associated with a specified keyword. |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = "keyword:string" }} |example = 
aKeyword := GetKeywordForms "My Keyword"
==See Also== *[[HasKeyword]] *[[AssignKeyword]] }} [[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Keyword Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the distance between Collision and Target X Y Z coordinates.
==Warning== The calculation is not the fastest thing on the planet, and spamming a bunch of casts every frame might be not the healthiest thing for Fallout New Vegas performance |returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = fOriginX |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = fOriginY |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = fOriginZ |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = fTargetX |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = fTargetY |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = fTargetZ |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = iColliderFlag |Type = int }} |example = distanceBetweenCollisionAndTargetXYZ = GetP2PRayCastRange fOriginX, fOriginY, fOriginZ, fTargetX, fTargetY, fTargetZ, iColliderFlag }} ==Notes== * Uses GECK's Collision flag * The flags can be located in [[Collision Flags]]
== See Also == *[[Collision Flags]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Movement Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the position of the point of collision ==Warning== The calculation is not the fastest thing on the planet, and spamming a bunch..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JIP |summary = Returns the position of the point of collision
==Warning== The calculation is not the fastest thing on the planet, and spamming a bunch of casts every frame might be not the healthiest thing for Fallout New Vegas performance |returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = fOriginX |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = fOriginY |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = fOriginZ |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = fRotX |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = fRotY |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = fXOut |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = fYOut |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = fZOut |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = iColliderFlag |Type = int }} |example = positionOfCollisionXYZ = GetPointRayCastPos fOriginX, fOriginY, fOriginZ, fRotX, fRotY, fXOut, fYOut, fZOut, iColliderFlag }} ==Notes== * Uses GECK's Collision flag * The flags can be located in [[Collision Flags]]
== See Also == *[[Collision Flags]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Movement Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Incomplete}} {{Function |origin = JIP |originVersion = 57.30 |summary = |name = |returnType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = }} |example = }} ==See..."
