{{Incomplete}} {{Function |origin = JIP |originVersion = 57.30 |summary =
|name = |returnType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = }} |example = }} ==See Also== *[[HasKeyword]]
[[Category:Functions_(JIP)]] [[Category:Keyword Functions_(JIP)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Incomplete}} {{Function |origin = JIP |originVersion = 57.30 |summary = |name = |returnType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = }} |example = }} ==See..."
Created page with "{{Incomplete}} {{Function |origin = JIP |originVersion = 57.30 |summary = |name = |returnType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = }} |example = }} ==See..."
revision.text:
{{Incomplete}} {{Function |origin = JIP |originVersion = 57.30 |summary =
|name = |returnType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = }} |example = }} ==See Also== *[[GetReticleNode]]
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)]] [[Category:Crosshair Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns the Archetype code for the specified Base Effect. Archetype codes are as follows: 
0: Value Modifier
1: Script
2: Dispel
3: Cure Disease
11: Invisibility
12: Chameleon
13: Light
16: Lock
17: Open
18: Bound Item
19: Summon Creature
24: Paralysis
30: Cure Paralysis
31: Cure Addiction
32: Cure Poison
33: Concussion
34: Value and Parts
35: Limb Condition
36: Turbo
|returnVal = code |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = baseEffect |Type = ref }} |example = set iCode to GetBaseEffectArchetype CateyeBaseEffect // returns 1 }} ==See Also== *[[GetBaseEffectAV]] *[[GetBaseEffectScript]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns the [[Actor Value code]] for the specified Value Modifier-type Base Effect. |returnVal = code |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = baseEffect |Type = ref }} |example = set iCode to GetBaseEffectAV VRegeneration // returns 15 }} ==See also== *[[Actor Value Codes]] *[[GetBaseEffectArchetype]] *[[GetBaseEffectScript]]
[[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Converts world coordinates to screen coordinates. Returns X and Y (from 0 to 1) and Z distance (in camera units) from a point in the worldspace. Also returns if the object is on the screen. If the desired values are pixel coordinates, the output X and Y values must be multiplied by the desired target resolution. The parameter HandleType works for the offscreen calculations, possible values are: 
0: Offscreen coordinates given as a square shape
1: Offscreen coordinates given as a circular shape
2: Offscreen calculations not made
If a reference is used, the input values will be used as offsets from that reference's position. |returnVal = isOnScreen |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = xOut |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = yOut |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = zOut |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = xIn |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = yIn |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = zIn |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = handleType |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = reference |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = 
int xOut
int yOut
int zOut
int isOnScreen
...

set isOnScreen to WorldToScreen xOut yOut zOut 0 0 0 0 SunnyREF ; Gives us the screen position of SunnyREF
}} [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]]

revision.comment: Add notes
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Toggles the display of [[MenuMode|Level Up Menu]]. Whenever a save is loaded, the level-up menu will be re-enabled. For this reason, checking [[GetGameLoaded]] in a quest script is recommended to continuously prevent the display of the menu. |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = disableOrEnable |Type = 0/1 }} |example = ToggleLevelUpMenu 0 }} ==Notes== *Does not prevent the display of the Level-Up Menu from functions like [[AdvLevel]], [[ShowLevelUpMenu]], [[ShowPerkMenu]], and [[ShowSkillMenu]].
==See also== *[[IsLevelUpMenuEnabled]]
[[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns 0 if Level Up Menu was disabled using [[ToggleLevelUpMenu]]. |returnVal = 0, 1 |returnType = int |example = set iEnabled to IsLevelUpMenuEnabled }} ==See also== *[[ToggleLevelUpMenu]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns 1 if the specified cell has been visited by the player, 0 otherwise. |name = IsCellVisited |returnVal = 0, 1 |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = cell |Type = form }} |example = set isVisited to IsCellVisited PrimmNashResidence }} ==Notes== *IsCellVisited will return 0 on the first visit if called in events such as [[OnCellEnter]] because the event is executed earlier than SeenData is updated. [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Cell Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Checks if a specified cell has expired and going to be reset on the next load (happens 3 days after the last visit). The return values are as follows: 
-1: Cell has not been visited or player is currently in it
0: Cell has not expired
1: Cell has expired (either 3 days have passed, or ResetInterior was called)
|name = IsCellExpired |returnVal = -1, 0, 1 |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = cell |Type = form }} |example = set isExpired to IsCellExpired PrimmNashResidence }} ==See also== *[[IsCellVisited]] *[[ResetInterior]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Cell Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns the MD5 hash of a file at the specified path relative to Fallout directory. |name = MD5File |returnType = string |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = filepath |Type = string }} |example = 
MD5File "FalloutNV.exe"
MD5File "Data\FalloutNV.esm"
}} ==Notes== *Hashing functions may cause a performance hit and should be used with caution. ==See Also== *[[SHA1File]] [[Category:Functions_(JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:File Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns the SHA1 hash of a file at the specified path relative to Fallout directory. |name = SHA1File |returnType = string |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = filepath |Type = string }} |example = 
SHA1File "FalloutNV.exe"
SHA1File "Data\FalloutNV.esm"
}} ==Notes== *Hashing functions may cause a performance hit and should be used with caution. ==See Also== *[[MD5File]] [[Category:Functions_(JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:File Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Opens or closes the Pip-Boy. Accepts one optional argument - a tab that Pip-Boy will open on. 
1002 - Inventory
1003 - Stats
1023 - Data
|returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = TabID |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = TogglePipBoy 1002 ; Pip-Boy will open on Inventory tab (or close if opened) TogglePipBoy ; Pip-Boy will open on a tab it closed on (or close if opened) }} [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:Pip-Boy Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Forces a calling actor to jump. |referenceType = actorRef |example = SunnyREF.Jump }} ==Notes== *Because why not.
[[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Movement Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Sets the duration (in seconds) and a multiplier for camera rumble. |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = shakeMult |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = shakeDuration |Type = float }} |example = SetCameraShake 2 3 }} ==See Also== *[[TriggerHUDShudder]] *[[TriggerHitShader]] *[[SetCameraShakeNoHUDShudder]]
[[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Forces VATS camera to stop playing. |example = StopVATSCam }} ==See Also== *[[EndVATScam]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:VATS Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns filepath of the map texture for a specified '''[[Worldspace]]'''. |returnType = string |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Worldspace |Type = ref }} |example = 
string_var strVar
let strVar := GetWorldSpaceMapTexture WastelandNV
}} [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Texture Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:String Functions]]

revision.comment: italics
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns RGB values of a specified pixel in a 24-bit .bmp image. The function accepts 6 arguments - an image file path relative to ''Fallout: New Vegas'' folder, 3 variables that will contain the result, and pixel coordinates - width and height. | |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = filepath |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = ROut |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = GOut |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = BOut |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = width |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = height |Type = int }} |example = 
int rOut
int gOut
int bOut
...
GetPixelFromBMP "Data/BMPs/Example.bmp" rOut gOut bOut 200 300
}} [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns the value of interior's numeric lighting trait (determined by traitID). |returnType = traitValue:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = interiorCell |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-15 }} ==Interior Lighting Numeric Trait IDs== 
0	Ambient color R
1	Ambient color G
2	Ambient color B
3	Directional color R
4	Directional color G
5	Directional color B
6	Directional rotation XY
7	Directional rotation Z
8	Directional fade
9	Fog color R
10	Fog color G
11	Fog color B
12	Fog near
13	Fog far
14	Fog power
15	Fog clip distance
|example = GetInteriorLightingTraitNumeric PrimmNashResidence 0 Will return Red value of ambient interior lighting of PrimmNashResidence. }} ==See Also== *[[SetInteriorLightingTraitNumeric]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Light_Functions]] [[Category:Cell Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Sets the value of interior's numeric lighting trait (determined by traitID). |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = interiorCell |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-15 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = float }} ==Interior Lighting Numeric Trait IDs== 
0	Ambient color R
1	Ambient color G
2	Ambient color B
3	Directional color R
4	Directional color G
5	Directional color B
6	Directional rotation XY
7	Directional rotation Z
8	Directional fade
9	Fog color R
10	Fog color G
11	Fog color B
12	Fog near
13	Fog far
14	Fog power
15	Fog clip distance
|example = SetInteriorLightingTraitNumeric PrimmNashResidence 0 35 Will set Red value of ambient interior lighting of PrimmNashResidence to 35. }} ==See Also== *[[GetInteriorLightingTraitNumeric]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Light_Functions]] [[Category:Cell Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns the total, calculated damage per second of a '''[[Weapon]]''', adjusted by any bonuses/penalties from skill, perks and special ammo effects.
If called on an [[Inventory Reference]], the damage will further be adjusted by the weapon's health, and by any weapon mods attached to it. |returnType = weaponDPS:float |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weaponForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = set fDPS to GetCalculatedWeaponDPS WeapGatlingLaser
set fDPS to inventoryRef.GetCalculatedWeaponDPS }} ==Notes== *Version 5.19 of JohnnyGuitar allows this function to be used on world references in addition to inventory references. *The function returns the calculated damage per second for the player actor only. *Any ammo effects that alter damage per second in the player's currently loaded ammo are used for the calculation, even if the weapon does not actually chamber it. The same happens when looking at the DPS info while hovering over weapons in the Pip-Boy menu.
==See Also== *[[GetCalculatedWeaponDamage]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns '''Animatable''' flag of a specified '''Imagespace Modifier'''. |returnVal = 0, 1 |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = IMOD |Type = ref }} |example = GetIMODAnimatable NVWastelandNightIS }} [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Imagespace Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns 1 if a specified optional patch is enabled in JohnnyGuitar.ini. Patch IDs are as follows: 
1: bLoadEditorIDs
3: bFixFleeing
4: bFixItemStackCount
5: bFixNPCShootingAngle
6: bNoMuzzleFlashCooldown
7: bReset3rdPersonCamera
|returnVal = 0, 1 |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = patchID |Type = int }} |example = set iLoadIDs to GetJohnnyPatch 1 }} ==Notes== *bLoadEditorIDs is no longer optional as of version 4.35. Existing scripts checking this setting will now always return 1. ==See Also== *[[GetOptionalPatch]] - JIP LN equivalent of this function. [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns the value of a numeric trait of a [[Media Set]] (determined by traitID). |returnType = traitValue:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = MediaSet |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-17 }} ==Numeric Media Set Trait IDs== 
0	Type (NAM1): 0 - Battle, 1 - Location, 2 - Dungeon, 3 - Incidental
1	Loop dB / Battle dB / Day Outer dB (NAM8)
2	Explore dB / Day Middle dB (NAM9)
3	Suspense dB / Day Inner dB (NAM0)
4	Night Outer dB (ANAM)
5	Night Middle dB (BNAM)
6	Night Inner dB (CNAM)
7	Day Outer Boundary % (JNAM)
8	Day Middle Boundary % (KNAM)
9	Day Inner Boundary % (LNAM)
10	Night Outer Boundary % (MNAM)
11	Night Middle Boundary % (NNAM)
12	Night Inner Boundary % (ONAM)
13	Flags (PNAM)
14	Wait Time / Minimum Time On / Daytime Min (DNAM)
15	Loop Fade Out / Looping/Random Crossfade Overlap / Nighttime Min (ENAM)
16	Recovery Time / Layer Crossfade Time / Daytime Max (FNAM)
17	Nighttime Max (GNAM)
Same Media Set traits can hold up to three different values depending on the Media Set type. For better understanding of how they work, take a look at Media Set records in FNVEdit. |example = 
if (GetMediaSetTraitNumeric musSetIncDesert 0 == 3) // if Media Set type is Incidental
      set fValue to GetMediaSetTraitNumeric musSetIncDesert 14 // set fValue to Daytime Min
endif
}} ==See Also== *[[SetMediaSetTraitNumeric]] *[[GetMediaSetTraitString]] *[[SetMediaSetTraitString]] *[[GetMediaSetTraitSound]] *[[SetMediaSetTraitSound]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Sound Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Sets the value of a numeric trait of a [[Media Set]] (determined by traitID). |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = MediaSet |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-17 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = float }} ==Numeric Media Set Trait IDs== 
0	Type (NAM1): 0 - Battle, 1 - Location, 2 - Dungeon, 3 - Incidental
1	Loop dB / Battle dB / Day Outer dB (NAM8)
2	Explore dB / Day Middle dB (NAM9)
3	Suspense dB / Day Inner dB (NAM0)
4	Night Outer dB (ANAM)
5	Night Middle dB (BNAM)
6	Night Inner dB (CNAM)
7	Day Outer Boundary % (JNAM)
8	Day Middle Boundary % (KNAM)
9	Day Inner Boundary % (LNAM)
10	Night Outer Boundary % (MNAM)
11	Night Middle Boundary % (NNAM)
12	Night Inner Boundary % (ONAM)
13	Flags (PNAM)
14	Wait Time / Minimum Time On / Daytime Min (DNAM)
15	Loop Fade Out / Looping/Random Crossfade Overlap / Nighttime Min (ENAM)
16	Recovery Time / Layer Crossfade Time / Daytime Max (FNAM)
17	Nighttime Max (GNAM)
Same Media Set traits can hold up to three different values depending on the Media Set type. For better understanding of how they work, take a look at Media Set records in FNVEdit. |example = 
if (GetMediaSetTraitNumeric musSetIncDesert 0 == 3) // if Media Set type is Incidental
      SetMediaSetTraitNumeric musSetIncDesert 14 20 // set Daytime Min value to 20
endif
}} ==See Also== *[[GetMediaSetTraitNumeric]] *[[GetMediaSetTraitString]] *[[SetMediaSetTraitString]] *[[GetMediaSetTraitSound]] *[[SetMediaSetTraitSound]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Sound Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns the value of a String trait (sound path) of a [[Media Set]] (determined by traitID). |returnType = traitValue:string |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = MediaSet |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-5 }} ==Numeric Media Set Trait IDs== 
0	Loop / Battle / Day Outer (NAM2)
1	Explore / Day Middle (NAM3)
2	Suspense / Day Inner (NAM4)
3	Night Outer (NAM5)
4	Night Middle (NAM6)
5	Night Inner (NAM7)
Same Media Set traits can hold up to three different values depending on the Media Set type. For better understanding of how they work, take a look at Media Set records in FNVEdit. |example = 
if (GetMediaSetTraitNumeric musSetBTTLRuralGood1Perc 0 == 0) // if Media Set type is Battle
      set sLoopPath to GetMediaSetTraitString musSetBTTLRuralGood1Perc 0 // set fValue to Loop sound path
endif
}} ==See Also== *[[GetMediaSetTraitNumeric]] *[[SetMediaSetTraitNumeric]] *[[SetMediaSetTraitString]] *[[GetMediaSetTraitSound]] *[[SetMediaSetTraitSound]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Sound Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Sets the value of a String trait (sound path) of a [[Media Set]] (determined by traitID). |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = MediaSet |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-5 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = string }} ==Numeric Media Set Trait IDs== 
0	Loop / Battle / Day Outer (NAM2)
1	Explore / Day Middle (NAM3)
2	Suspense / Day Inner (NAM4)
3	Night Outer (NAM5)
4	Night Middle (NAM6)
5	Night Inner (NAM7)
Same Media Set traits can hold up to three different values depending on the Media Set type. For better understanding of how they work, take a look at Media Set records in FNVEdit. |example = 
if (GetMediaSetTraitNumeric musSetBTTLRuralGood1Perc 0 == 0) // if Media Set type is Battle
      SetMediaSetTraitString musSetBTTLRuralGood1Perc 0 "Data/Music/Your/Path" // set Loop sound path
endif
}} ==See Also== *[[GetMediaSetTraitNumeric]] *[[SetMediaSetTraitNumeric]] *[[GetMediaSetTraitString]] *[[GetMediaSetTraitSound]] *[[SetMediaSetTraitSound]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Sound Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns the value of a Sound-type trait of a [[Media Set]] (determined by traitID). |returnType = traitValue:form |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = MediaSet |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-1 }} ==Numeric Media Set Trait IDs== 
0	Intro / Daytime(HNAM)
1	Outro / Nighttime(INAM)
Same Media Set traits can hold up to three different values depending on the Media Set type. For better understanding of how they work, take a look at Media Set records in FNVEdit. |example = 
if (GetMediaSetTraitNumeric musSetIncDesert 0 == 3) // if Media Set type is Incidental
      set rSoundForm to GetMediaSetTraitSound musSetIncDesert 0 // set reference to Daytime sound form
endif
}} ==See Also== *[[GetMediaSetTraitNumeric]] *[[SetMediaSetTraitNumeric]] *[[GetMediaSetTraitString]] *[[SetMediaSetTraitString]] *[[SetMediaSetTraitSound]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Sound Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Sets the value of a Sound-type trait of a [[Media Set]] (determined by traitID). |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = MediaSet |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-1 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newForm |Type = ref }} ==Numeric Media Set Trait IDs== 
0	Intro / Daytime(HNAM)
1	Outro / Nighttime(INAM)
Same Media Set traits can hold up to three different values depending on the Media Set type. For better understanding of how they work, take a look at Media Set records in FNVEdit. |example = 
if (GetMediaSetTraitNumeric musSetIncDesert 0 == 3) // if Media Set type is Incidental
      SetMediaSetTraitSound musSetIncDesert 0 musINCdesertday // set new Daytime sound form
endif
}} ==See Also== *[[GetMediaSetTraitNumeric]] *[[SetMediaSetTraitNumeric]] *[[GetMediaSetTraitString]] *[[SetMediaSetTraitString]] *[[GetMediaSetTraitSound]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Sound Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Sets Velocity on a reference. Same as [[SetVel]] but accepts all three axes at once and works in scripts natively. |returnType = void |referenceType = ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = X |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Y |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Z |Type = float }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetObjectVelocity]]
[[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Movement Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns the time elapsed since the game's executable was launched. Accepts one optional argument - a time unit: 
0: milliseconds (default)
1: seconds
2: minutes
|returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = unit |Type = 0/1/2 }} |example = set fTimePlayed to GetTimePlayed 1 // e.g. 50.5 seconds }} ==See Also== *[[GetTimeStamp]] *[[GetUserTime]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Time Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns the value of a "1st Person Model" trait of a Weapon (determined by traitID). |returnType = traitValue:ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Weapon |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-7 }} ==Trait IDs== 
0	1st Person Model (WNAM)
1	1st Person Model - Mod 1 (WNM1)
2	1st Person Model - Mod 2 (WNM2)
3	1st Person Model - Mod 1 and 2 (WNM3)
4	1st Person Model - Mod 3 (WNM4)
5	1st Person Model - Mod 1 and 3(WNM5)
6	1st Person Model - Mod 2 and 3 (WNM6)
7	1st Person Model - Mod 1, 2 and 3 (WNM7)
|example = 
ref rWeaponModel
set rWeaponModel to GetWeapon1stPersonModel WeapHuntingRifle 1 // set rWeaponModel to 1st person model with mod 1 attached
}} ==See Also== *[[SetWeapon1stPersonModel]] *[[GetWeaponModel]] / [[SetWeaponModel]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Sets the value of a "1st Person Model" trait of a Weapon (determined by traitID). If new model is omitted, existing model will be cleared. |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Weapon |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 0-7 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newModel |Type = ref |Optional = y }} ==Trait IDs== 
0	1st Person Model (WNAM)
1	1st Person Model - Mod 1 (WNM1)
2	1st Person Model - Mod 2 (WNM2)
3	1st Person Model - Mod 1 and 2 (WNM3)
4	1st Person Model - Mod 3 (WNM4)
5	1st Person Model - Mod 1 and 3(WNM5)
6	1st Person Model - Mod 2 and 3 (WNM6)
7	1st Person Model - Mod 1, 2 and 3 (WNM7)
|example = 
ref rWeaponModel
SetWeapon1stPersonModel WeapHuntingRifle 1 rWeaponModel // set 1st person model with mod 1 attached to rWeaponModel
}} ==See Also== *[[GetWeapon1stPersonModel]] *[[GetWeaponModel]] / [[SetWeaponModel]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns the Path trait of a specified Music Type. |returnVal = path |returnType = string |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = musicType |Type = ref }} |example = let sPath := GetMusicTypePath DefaultSuccess // "Data/Music/Special/Success.mp3" }} ==See also== *[[GetMusicTypeDB]] *[[SetMusicTypeDB]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Music]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns the dB trait of a specified Music Type. |returnVal = dB |returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = musicType |Type = ref }} |example = set fDB to GetMusicTypeDB DefaultSuccess // -6 }} ==See also== *[[SetMusicTypeDB]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Music]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Sets the dB trait of a specified Music Type. |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = musicType |Type = ref }} |example = SetMusicTypeDB DefaultSuccess -100 }} ==See also== *[[GetMusicTypeDB]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Music]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns the [[Form ID]] associated with a specified [[Editor ID]] [[string]] (case-sensitive). |returnVal = baseform |returnType = ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = editorID |Type = string }} |example = set rSunny to EditorIDToFormID "GSSunnySmiles" Or if you have an EditorID string from [[GetEditorID]]: 
let rRef := EditorIDToFormID $sEditorID
}} ==See Also== *[[GetEditorID]] *[[Ar_SortEditor]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:ID Functions]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns the array of Weather forms associated with a Region Data of a region. |returnVal = weather |returnType = array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = region |Type = ref }} |example = let aWeathers := GetRegionWeathers NewVegasRegion // { NVWastelandClear, NVWastelandClearNight } }} ==See also== *[[ClearRegionWeathers]] *[[AddRegionWeather]] *[[RemoveRegionWeather]] *[[IsWeatherInRegion]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Removes all weathers associated with a Region. |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = region..."
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Removes all weathers associated with a Region. |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = region |Type = ref }} |example = ClearRegionWeathers NewVegasRegion }} ==See also== *[[GetRegionWeathers]] *[[AddRegionWeather]] *[[RemoveRegionWeather]] *[[IsWeatherInRegion]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Adds a Weather form to a Region Data of a region. |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = region |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = weather |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = chance |Type = int(0-100) }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = global |Type = ref |Optional = y }} |example = AddRegionWeather NewVegasRegion MegatonCloudy01 100 }} ==See also== *[[ClearRegionWeathers]] *[[GetRegionWeathers]] *[[RemoveRegionWeather]] *[[IsWeatherInRegion]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns 1 if a Weather form is found in Region Data of a region. |returnType = (0/1)int |arguments = {{FunctionArgum..."
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns 1 if a Weather form is found in Region Data of a region. |returnType = (0/1)int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = region |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = weather |Type = ref }} |example = set bInRegion to IsWeatherInRegion NewVegasRegion NVWastelandClear }} ==See also== *[[GetRegionWeathers]] *[[AddRegionWeather]] *[[RemoveRegionWeather]] *[[ClearRegionWeathers]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]]

revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns true if Weather Region Data has an override flag. |returnType = (0/1)int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = region |Type = ref }} |example = set bOverride to GetRegionWeatherOverride NewVegasRegion }} ==See also== *[[SetRegionWeatherOverride]] *[[GetRegionWeatherPriority]] *[[SetRegionWeatherPriority]]
[[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Sets the Override flag of a Weather Region Data. |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = region |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flag |Type = int }} |example = SetRegionWeatherOverride NewVegasRegion 0 }} ==See also== *[[GetRegionWeatherOverride]] *[[GetRegionWeatherPriority]] *[[SetRegionWeatherPriority]]
[[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns the Priority of a Weather Region Data. |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = region |Type = ref }} |example = set iPriority to GetRegionWeatherPriority NewVegasRegion // 100 }} ==See also== *[[GetRegionWeatherOverride]] *[[SetRegionWeatherOverride]] *[[SetRegionWeatherPriority]]
[[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Sets the Priority of a Weather Region Data. |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = region |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = priority |Type = int }} |example = SetRegionWeatherPriority NewVegasRegion 5 }} ==See also== *[[GetRegionWeatherOverride]] *[[SetRegionWeatherOverride]] *[[GetRegionWeatherPriority]]
[[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Stops an instance of a Sound that is currently playing from a specified source, e.g. played using [[PlaySound3D]] or [[PlaySoundFade]]. Allows to gradually fade the sound out, if optional argument is provided. |returnType = success:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = soundForm |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = source |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = fadeTime |Type = float |Optional = y }} |example = StopSoundAlt NPCHumanDrinkingWaterToilet Player }} ==Notes== * Optional argument was introduced in JohnnyGuitar NVSE version 5.07. ==See Also== *[[StopSound]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Sound Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns the type of the primitive of the calling reference. Return values are as follows: <pre> 1	Box 2	Sphere 3	Pla..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns the type of the primitive of the calling reference. Return values are as follows: 
1	Box
2	Sphere
3	Plane (collision only)
|returnType = 1-3:int |referenceType = reference |example = set sType to objectRef.GetPrimitiveType }} ==See Also== *[[AddPrimitive]] *[[RemovePrimitive]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Removes the primitive of the calling reference. |returnType = success:0/1 |referenceType = reference |example = objectRef.Rem..."
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Removes the primitive of the calling reference. |returnType = success:0/1 |referenceType = reference |example = objectRef.RemovePrimitive }} ==See Also== *[[AddPrimitive]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns base scale of a reference. If called on an NPC, will return their base height. |returnType = float |referenceType = ref |alias = GetBasedScale |example = set fBaseScale to Player.GetBaseScale }} ==See Also== *[[SetCreatureBaseScale]] *[[GetScale]] / [[SetScale]] / [[ModScale]]
[[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Scale Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

broken warning
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Resets the specified Actor Value to its previous state before [[ForceAV]] was called on it.
'''WARNING: This function is currently broken and does nothing.''' |returnType = void |referenceType = ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = actorValue |Type = statName }} |example = Player.UnforceAV Health }}
==See Also== *[[ForceActorValue]] *[[GetActorValue]] *[[GetBaseActorValue]] *[[GetPermanentActorValue]] *[[RestoreActorValue]] *[[SetActorValue]] *[[Stats List]]
[[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Resets the specified Actor Value to its previous state before [[SetAV]] was called on it. |returnType = void |referenceType = ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = actorValue |Type = statName }} |example = Player.UnsetAV Health }} [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Sets the DDS file path for the male or female icon for the specified biped object. Its NVSE counterpart [[SetBipedIconPathEx]] is broken. |name = SetBipedIconPathAlt |returnType = void |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = NewPath |Type = string}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = isFemale |Type = int}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = form |Type = ref}} }} ==Example== 
SetBipedIconPathAlt "MyNewIcons\PipboyImages\Apparel\new_leather.dds" 0 ArmorLeather
==See Also== *[[GetBipedIconPath]] *[[SetBipedModelPathEX]] *[[SetModelPath]] *[[SetIconPath]] [[Category:Functions_(JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Texture Functions]] [[Category:Armor Functions]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Sets a specified Facegen model flag for a specified Armor. Flag IDs are as follows: 
0 - Head
1 - Torso
2 - RightHand
3 - LeftHand
|returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = armor |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagID |Type = int(0-3) }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = isFemale |Type = int(0/1) }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = onOrOff |Type = int(0/1) }} |example = SetFacegenModelFlag OutfitWasteland01 1 0 0 // disables Torso flag for male biped model }} ==See also== *[[GetFacegenModelFlag]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:FaceGen Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns the body model path of the specified Race. Model part IDs are as follows: 
0 - UpperBody
1 - LeftHand
2 - RightHand
|name = GetRaceBodyModelPath |returnType = modelPath:string_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = race |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = modelPartID |Type = int |Value = 0-2 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = isFemale |Type = int |Value = 0/1 }} |example = GetRaceBodyModelPath Caucasian 0 0 // returns "characters\_Male\UpperBody.nif" }} ==See Also== *[[GetRaceHeadModelPath]] [[Category:Functions_(JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns a specified Facegen model flag for a specified Armor. Flag IDs are as follows: 
0 - Head
1 - Torso
2 - RightHand
3 - LeftHand
|returnType = int(0/1) |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = armor |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagID |Type = int(0-3) }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = isFemale |Type = int(0/1) }} |example = set torsoFlag to GetFacegenModelFlag OutfitWasteland01 1 0 // returns Torso flag for male biped model }} ==See also== *[[SetFacegenModelFlag]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:FaceGen Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Toggles a specified Pip-Boy button light. Button IDs are as follows: 
0 - Stats
1 - Items
2 - Data
|returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = buttonID |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = onOrOff |Type = int }} |example = ToggleNthPipboyLight 0 1 // Will enable button glow on Stats button }} [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Pip-Boy Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns the head model path of the specified Race. Model part IDs are as follows: 
0 - Head
1 - Ears
2 - Mouth
3 - TeethLower
4 - TeethUpper
5 - Tongue
6 - LeftEye
7 - RightEye
|name = GetRaceHeadModelPath |returnType = modelPath:string_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = race |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = modelPartID |Type = int |Value = 0-7 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = isFemale |Type = int |Value = 0/1 }} |example = GetRaceHeadModelPath Caucasian 0 0 // returns "Characters\Head\HeadHuman.NIF" }} ==See Also== *[[GetRaceBodyModelPath]] [[Category:Functions_(JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]]

revision.comment: returns baseforms
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns the [[array]] of all [[BaseForm]] members of a specified [[Faction]], optionally filtered by Rank. |name = GetFactionMembers |returnType = array:actorBase |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = faction |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = rank |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = let aKnights := GetFactionMembers BrotherhoodSteelFaction 1 // returns the array of Brotherhood of Steel knights }}
[[Category:Functions_(JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Faction Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns the initial heap allocation size in megabytes (DefaultHeapInitialAllocMB in nvse_config.ini). |returnType = float |example = set fHeapSize to GetDefaultHeapSize }}
[[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Debugging]] [[Category:File Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns the distance between two points in a three-dimensional space. |returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = x1 |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = y1 |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = z1 |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = x2 |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = y2 |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = z2 |Type = float }} |example = set fDistance to GetVector3DDistance 100 100 100 200 200 200 }} ==See Also== *[[Get3DDistanceFromHitToNiNode]] *[[Get3DDistanceBetweenNiNodes]] *[[Get3DDistanceToNiNode]]
[[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Math Functions]] [[Category:Movement Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns the distance between a point in the world and a specified '''NiNode''' on a calling reference. |referenceType = ref |returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = NiNode |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = x |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = y |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = z |Type = float }} |example = set fDistance to easypeteRef.Get3DDistanceToNiNode "Bip01 Pelvis" 100 100 100 }} ==See Also== *[[GetVector3DDistance]] *[[Get3DDistanceFromHitToNiNode]] *[[Get3DDistanceBetweenNiNodes]]
[[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Movement Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns the distance between two NiNodes on two references. |referenceType = ref |returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ref1 |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = ref2 |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = NiNode1 |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = NiNode2 |Type = string }} |example = set fDistance to Get3DDistanceBetweenNiNodes EasyPeteRef SunnySmilesRef "Bip01 Pelvis" "Bip01 Pelvis" }} ==Notes== *If needed, references can be the same object.
==See Also== *[[GetVector3DDistance]] *[[Get3DDistanceFromHitToNiNode]] *[[Get3DDistanceToNiNode]]
[[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Movement Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = When called from a OnHit/OnHitWith script block (or the corresponding NVSE event handlers), or from a ScriptEffectStart block in an Ammo form Impact Script, returns the distance between hit location and a specified NiNode on a calling reference. |returnType = float |referenceType = ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = NiNode |Type = string }} |example = set fDistance to EasyPeteRef.Get3DDistanceFromHitToNiNode "Bip01 Pelvis" }} ==See Also== *[[GetVector3DDistance]] *[[Get3DDistanceBetweenNiNodes]] *[[Get3DDistanceToNiNode]]
[[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Movement Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns the life state of an actor. Return values are as follows:
0 - Alive
1 - Dying
2 - Dead
3 - Unconscious
4 - Reanimated (unused)
5 - Restrained
6 - Unconscious essential
|returnType = int |referenceType = actor |example = set iLifeState to sunnyref.GetLifeState }} ==Notes== *'''Dying''' state starts when actor's health reaches 0 and lasts for the amount of time determined by ''fDyingTimer'' setting. [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns the specified flag of a Race. Flag IDs are as follows:
0 - Playable
1 - Child
|returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = race |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagID |Type = int(1/4) }} |example = set iFlag to GetRaceFlag Caucasian 0 }} == See Also == *[[SetRaceFlag]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Flag Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Sets the specified flag of a [[Race]]. Flag IDs are as follows:
0 - Playable
1 - Child
|returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = race |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagID |Type = int(1/4) }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = setOrRemove |Type = int(0/1) }} |example = SetRaceFlag Caucasian 0 0 }} == See Also == *[[GetRaceFlag]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Flag Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns the linear velocity (in game length [[Units]]/sec) of the collision object (if any) attached to the specified node in the calling reference's loaded 3D model.
The velocity is in units of '''0.1 Meter/sec''', or 7 '''game [[Units]]/sec'''. |name = GetLinearVelocity |returnType = void |referenceType = ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = nodeName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = xOut |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = yOut |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = zOut |Type = float }} |example = easypeteref.GetLinearVelocity "Bip01 L Hand" xOut yOut zOut ;* Function result is stored in xOut, yOut and zOut }}
==See Also== *[[GetLinearVelocityAlt]] *[[GetObjectVelocity]] *[[SetLinearVelocity]]
[[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Movement Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns a [[Sound]] form attached to a specified [[container]]. Sound IDs are as follows:
0 - Open sound
1 - Close sound
2 - Random/Looping sound
|returnType = form |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = container |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = whichSound |Type = int }} |example = set rSound to GetContainerSound MyContainer 0 ==See Also== *[[SetContainerSound]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Sound Functions]] }}

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Sets a [[Sound]] form attached to a specified [[container]]. Sound IDs are as follows:
0 - Open sound
1 - Close sound
2 - Random/Looping sound
|returnType = form |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = container |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = whichSound |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newSound |Type = form }} |example = SetContainerSound MyContainer 0 rSound ==See Also== *[[GetContainerSound]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Sound Functions]] }}

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns combat skill of a creature. |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = creature |Type = actorBase }} |example = set iSkill to GetCreatureCombatSkill Molerat01 }} ==See Also==
[[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]] [[Category:Creature Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''UDF Script''' as a handler that will be invoked when a specified Actor (or any Actor) is killed. Formlists are also accepted as filters. Compared to vanilla [[OnDeath]] event, this will fire instantly, while vanilla fires when ''fDyingTimer'' seconds elapses. Filtering uses Actor base forms, not references (if a reference is specified, its base form is used for filtering). When invoked, the script is passed with a single argument: Actor that was killed. |name = SetJohnnyOnDyingEventHandler |alias = SetOnDyingEventHandler |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flag |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = actor |Type = filter |Optional = y }} |example = 
SetJohnnyOnDyingEventHandler 1 OnDyingUDF 0 rActor
'''Sets''' the OnDyingUDF script as a handler to be invoked when '''rActor''' is killed. 
SetJohnnyOnDyingEventHandler 0 OnDyingUDF 0 rActor
'''Removes''' the OnDyingUDF script as a handler of the above. }} ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn OnDyingUDF

ref rActor
ref rKiller

begin Function {rActor} 

set rKiller to rActor.GetKiller
...

end
==Notes== *Event must be registered in [[GameMode]] block (not [[MenuMode|MenuMode 4]]) as it is flushed every game load. This also means [[GetGameRestarted]] is NOT a viable option to set this event for the session. Instead consider using [[GetGameLoaded]] to (re)set the event handler. *Flag argument is reserved for future use and should always be 0. ==See Also== *[[GetKiller]] *[[GetLifeState]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''UDF Script''' as a handler that will be invoked when a specified Quest (or any Quest) is started. Formlists are also accepted as filters. When invoked, the script is passed with a single argument: a Quest that started. |name = SetJohnnyOnStartQuestEventHandler |alias = SetOnStartQuestEventHandler |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flag |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = quest |Type = filter |Optional = y }} |example = 
SetJohnnyOnStartQuestEventHandler 1 OnStartQuestUDF 0 rQuest
'''Sets''' the OnStartQuestUDF script as a handler to be invoked when '''rQuest''' is started. 
SetJohnnyOnStartQuestEventHandler 0 OnStartQuestUDF 0 rQuest
'''Removes''' the OnStartQuestUDF script as a handler of the above. }} ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn OnStartQuestUDF

ref rQuest

begin Function {rQuest} 

...

end
==Notes== *Flag argument is reserved for future use and should always be 0. ==See Also== *[[SetJohnnyOnCompleteQuestEventHandler]] *[[SetJohnnyOnFailQuestEventHandler]] *[[SetJohnnyOnStopQuestEventHandler]] *[[SetOnQuestStageEventHandler]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Quest Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''UDF Script''' as a handler that will be invoked when a specified Quest (or any Quest) is stopped. Formlists are also accepted as filters. When invoked, the script is passed with a single argument: a Quest that stopped running. |name = SetJohnnyOnStopQuestEventHandler |alias = SetOnStopQuestEventHandler |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flag |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = quest |Type = filter |Optional = y }} |example = 
SetJohnnyOnStopQuestEventHandler 1 OnStopQuestUDF 0 rQuest
'''Sets''' the OnStopQuestUDF script as a handler to be invoked when '''rQuest''' is stopped. 
SetJohnnyOnStopQuestEventHandler 0 OnStartQuestUDF 0 rQuest
'''Removes''' the OnStopQuestUDF script as a handler of the above. }} ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn OnStartQuestUDF

ref rQuest

begin Function {rQuest} 

...

end
==Notes== *Flag argument is reserved for future use and should always be 0. ==See Also== *[[SetJohnnyOnCompleteQuestEventHandler]] *[[SetJohnnyOnFailQuestEventHandler]] *[[SetJohnnyOnStartQuestEventHandler]] *[[SetOnQuestStageEventHandler]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Quest Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Disables muzzle flash lights in the game. Intended to be a performance boost in combat. disableMode argument supports the following modes:
0 - re-enable
1 - disable for all actors
2 - disable for the player only
3 - disable for NPCs only
|returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = disableMode |Type = bool |Optional = y }} |example = 
DisableMuzzleFlashLights 1 // Disables muzzle flash lights
DisableMuzzleFlashLights 0 // Enables them back
}} ==Notes== *This function does not persist through saves. [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Sets the custom map marker icon of a [[Map Marker]] [[reference]] or [[formlist]] of references using a specified path, relative to Data/Textures. |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = markerReforList |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = iconPath |Type = string }} |example = SetCustomMapMarkerIcon IconFormList "CustomIcons\icon.dds" }} ==Notes== *The effect of this function lasts for the entire game session and is not [[savebaked]].
==See Also== *[[GetCustomMapMarker]]
[[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Map Functions]] [[Category:Texture Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:String Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Sets new sound for a specified [[Projectile]]. Sound IDs are as follows: 
1 - Projectile sound
2 - Countdown sound
3 - Disable sound
|returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = projectile |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = soundID |Type = int(1-3) }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = sound |Type = form }} |example = SetProjectileSound MineFragProjectile 2 newSound }} [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Sound Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Sets new sound for a specified [[Explosion]]. Sound IDs are as follows: 
1 - Sound 1
2 - Sound 2
|returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = explosion |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = soundID |Type = int(1-2) }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = sound |Type = form }} |example = SetExplosionSound BloatFlyExplosion 1 newSound }} [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Sound Functions]] [[Category:Explosion Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Sets world model path of a Weapon, relative to Fallout New Vegas\Data\Meshes. |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = path |Type = string }} |example = SetWeaponWorldModelPath Weap10mmPistol "weapons\hand2hand\bladedgauntlet_GO.NIF" }} ==See Also== *[[GetWeaponWorldModelPath]] *[[SetModelPath]] *[[SetWeapon1stPersonModel]]
[[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Clamps the value to a specified range. |returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = value |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = min |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = max |Type = float }} |example = set fVal to Clamp 5 10 20 // sets fVal to 10 set fVal to Clamp 75 100 50 // sets fVal to 100 set fVal to Clamp 1000 500 50 // sets fVal to 50 }} [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Math Functions]]

revision.comment: Big ol zipline example, thanks to Xilandro
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns an interpolation between two inputs (v0, v1) for a parameter (t) in the closed unit interval [0, 1]. |returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = v0 |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = v1 |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = t |Type = float }} |example = set fVal to Lerp v0 v1 t To bring the player from one point to another in a linear fashion, starting by activating a "zipline" object: 
scn ZipLineScript

float oldX 
float oldY
float oldZ

float newX
float newY
float newZ

float zipX 
float zipY
float zipZ

short playerusingzipline  

float fCurrentTime
float fTime
float fTimeInSecondsHowLongShouldPlayerUseZipLine  ;could be a global

begin OnActivate Player

  if playerusingzipline == 0
    set oldX to ZipLineStartREFR.GetPos X
    set oldY to ZipLineStartREFR.GetPos Y
    set oldZ to ZipLineStartREFR.GetPos Z

    set newX to ZipLineEndREFR.GetPos X
    set newY to ZipLineEndREFR.GetPos Y
    set newZ to ZipLineEndREFR.GetPos Z

    set fTimeInSecondsHowLongShouldPlayerUseZipLine to 5
    set playerusingzipline to 1
    set fCurrentTime to 0
    DisablePlayerControls 1, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0  ;prevent player from doing weird stuff.
  endif

end


begin GameMode

	if playerusingzipline

           ;* Prevent player from dying due to falling.
           Player.ResetFallTime
           Player.SetActorVelocity Z 0
	
           ;* Displace the player.
	   set fCurrentTime to fCurrentTime + GetSecondsPassed
	   set fTime to fCurrentTime / fTimeInSecondsHowLongShouldPlayerUseZipLine
	
	   set zipX to Lerp oldX newX fTime
	   set zipY to Lerp oldY newY fTime
	   set zipZ to Lerp oldZ newZ fTime
	
	   player.SetPosEx zipX zipY zipZ
	     if fTime >= 1
	        set playerusingzipline to 0
	        EnablePlayerControls
	    endif
	endif
end
}} [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Math Functions]]

revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = '''The function currently conflicts with [https://www.nexusmods.com/newvegas/mods/64596 JIP Minimap]. The event will not fire if the user has it installed.'''
Sets/removes the specified '''UDF Script''' as a handler that will be invoked when a specified Cell (or any Cell) is marked as seen. Formlists are also accepted as filters. When invoked, the script is passed with a single argument: a seen Cell. |name = SetJohnnySeenDataEventHandler |alias = SetSeenDataEventHandler |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flag |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = cell |Type = filter |Optional = y }} |example = 
SetJohnnySeenDataEventHandler 1 OnSeenDataUDF 0 rCell
'''Sets''' the OnSeenDataUDF script as a handler to be invoked when '''rCell''' is marked as seen. 
SetJohnnySeenDataEventHandler 0 OnSeenDataUDF  0 rCell
'''Removes''' the OnSeenDataUDF script as a handler of the above. }} ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn OnSeenDataUDF 

ref rCell

begin Function {rCell} 

...

end
==Notes== *Flag argument is reserved for future use and should always be 0. [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''UDF Script''' as a handler that will be invoked when a specified Actor (or any Actor) loses a specified Limb (or any limb). Formlists are also accepted as filters. Limb IDs are as follows:
Torso 	       0
Head 1 	       1
Head 2 	       2
Left Arm 1     3
Left Arm 2     4
Right Arm 1    5
Right Arm 2    6
Left Leg 1     7
Left Leg 2     8
Left Leg 3     9
Right Leg 1    10
Right Leg 2    11
Right Leg 3    12
Brain          13
Hand or Weapon 14
When invoked, the script receives two arguments: Actor and a Limb ID. |name = SetJohnnyOnLimbGoneEventHandler |alias = SetOnLimbGoneEventHandler |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flag |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = actor |Type = filter |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = limbID |Type = filter |Optional = y }} |example = 
SetJohnnyOnLimbGoneEventHandler 1 OnLimbGoneUDF 0 rActor 1
'''Sets''' the OnLimbGoneUDF script as a handler to be invoked when '''rActor''''s head gets detached from his body. 
SetJohnnyOnLimbGoneEventHandler 0 OnLimbGoneUDF 0 rActor 1
'''Removes''' the OnLimbGoneUDF script as a handler of the above. }} ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn OnLimbGoneUDF

ref rActor
int limbID

begin Function {rActor, limbID} 

...

end
==Notes== *Event must be registered in [[GameMode]] block (not [[MenuMode|MenuMode 4]]) as it is flushed every game load. This also means [[GetGameRestarted]] is NOT a viable option to set this event for the session. Instead consider using [[GetGameLoaded]] to (re)set the event handler. *Flag argument is reserved for future use and should always be 0. [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns 1 if a specified [[Quest]] was failed. |returnVal = 0, 1 |returnType = int |alias = GetQF |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = quest |Type = form }} |example = set bFailed to GetQuestFailed VCG01 }}

[[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Quest Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''[[UDF|UDF Script]]''' as a handler that will be invoked when a specified '''[[Challenge]]''' (or any Challenge) is completed. '''[[Formlist]]s''' are also accepted as filters. When invoked, the script is passed with a single argument: a Challenge that was completed. |name = SetJohnnyOnChallengeCompleteEventHandler |alias = SetOnChallengeCompleteEventHandler |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flag |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = challenge |Type = filter |Optional = y }} |example = 
SetJohnnyOnChallengeCompleteEventHandler 1 OnChallengeCompleteUDF 0 rChallenge
'''Sets''' the OnChallengeCompleteUDF script as a handler to be invoked when '''rChallenge''' is started. 
SetJohnnyOnChallengeCompleteEventHandler 0 OnChallengeCompleteUDF 0 rChallenge
'''Removes''' the OnChallengeCompleteUDF script as a handler of the above. }} This handler runs after the associated script, but before the challenge is marked as completed. To run the handler UDF after the challenge has been marked completed, simply delay the call by a frame. 
SetJohnnyOnChallengeCompleteEventHandler 0 ({ref rChallenge} => CallAfterFrames 1 OnChallengeCompleteUDF 0 rChallenge) 0 rChallenge
==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn OnChallengeCompleteUDF

ref rChallenge

begin Function {rChallenge} 

...


end
==Notes== *Flag argument is reserved for future use and should always be 0. ==See Also== *[[GetChallengeCompleted]] *[[ShowOff:OnChallengeProgress]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Challenge Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns the number of menu entries in a specified [[Terminal]]. |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = terminal |Type = form }} |example = set iCount to GetTerminalMenuItemCount rTerminal }} [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Terminal Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns current Pip-Boy mode. Return values are as follows:

|returnType = int |example = set iMode to GetPipBoyMode }} [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Pip-Boy Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns the value of a weapon's numeric VATS Data trait (determined by traitID). |returnType = traitValue:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 1-5 }} ==Weapon VATS Data Trait IDs== 
1	Skill
2	Damage Mult
3	AP
