|example = GetWeaponVATSTraitNumeric WeapBaseballBat 2 Will return the Damage Multiplier value for special attacks in VATS when using WeapBaseballBat. }} ==See Also== *[[SetWeaponVATSTraitNumeric]] [[Category:Functions_(JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:VATS Functions]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Sets the value of a weapon's numeric VATS Data trait (determined by traitID). |returnType = traitValue:float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int |Value = 1-5 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = float }} ==Weapon VATS Data Trait IDs== 
1	Skill
2	Damage Mult
3	AP
4	Silent
5	Mod Required
|example = SetWeaponVATSTraitNumeric WeapBaseballBat 2 5 Will set the Damage Multiplier value for special attacks in VATS when using WeapBaseballBat to 5. }} ==See Also== *[[GetWeaponVATSTraitNumeric]] [[Category:Functions_(JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:VATS Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Removes [[Terminal]] Menu entry at a specified index. |returnType = success:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = terminal |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = index |Type = int }} |example = RemoveTerminalMenuItem rTerminal 3 }} [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Terminal Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Sets map texture for a specified '''Worldspace''' using string filepath relative to "Data\Textures". |returnType = void |argum..."

revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns index of a last mod overriding a specific form. |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = form }} |example = set iIdx to GetFormOverrideIndex SunnyREF }} ==Notes== *This function was broken prior to version 5.08.
==See Also== *[[IsFormOverridden]] *[[GetFormMods]] *[[GetSourceModIndex]]
[[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:File Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns sequence anim group ID of the calling reference. Sequence IDs are as follows: 
0 - Idle
1 - Movement
2 - Left Arm
3 - Left Hand
4 - Weapon
5 - Weapon Up
6 - Weapon Down
7 - Special idle
|returnType = int |referenceType = ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = sequenceID |Type = int(0-7) }} |example = set groupID to player.GetSequenceAnimGroup 1 }} ==Notes== *Possible returned values are listed at the [[AnimGroups|Anim Groups]] page [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Animation Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''[[UDF|UDF Script]]''' as a handler that will be invoked whenever the activation prompt appears on the object. [[Base Form]]s, [[references]] and [[formlist]]s are accepted as filters, as well as [[Form Type IDs]]. When invoked, the script receives one argument: reference under crosshair. |name = SetJohnnyOnCrosshairEventHandler |alias = SetOnCrosshairEventHandler |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flag |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = form |Type = filter |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = formType |Type = filter |Optional = y }} |example = 
SetJohnnyOnCrosshairEventHandler 1 OnCrosshairUDF 0 rActor
'''Sets''' the OnCrosshairUDF script as a handler to be invoked when '''rActor''' is under crosshair. 
SetJohnnyOnCrosshairEventHandler 0 OnCrosshairUDF 0 rActor
'''Removes''' the OnCrosshairUDF script as a handler of the above. 
SetJohnnyOnCrosshairEventHandler 1 OnCrosshairUDF 0 xMarker 27
'''Sets''' the OnCrosshairUDF script as a handler to be invoked when any Container is under crosshair. xMarker is used as stub argument to allow use of second filter without first. }} ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn OnCrosshairUDF

ref rForm

begin Function {rForm} 

...

end
==Notes== *Compared to other crosshair events, this one runs before the activation prompt is loaded, so any manipulations with the form will be reflected on activation prompt. *Event must be registered in [[GameMode]] block (not [[MenuMode|MenuMode 4]]) as it is flushed every game load. This also means [[GetGameRestarted]] is NOT a viable option to set this event for the session. Instead consider using [[GetGameLoaded]] to (re)set the event handler. *Flag argument is reserved for future use and should always be 0. ==See Also== *[[OnCrosshairOn]] *[[OnCrosshairOff]] *[[SetOnPCTargetChangeEventHandler]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Event Handlers]] [[Category:Crosshair Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Displays cinematic text with fade out effect on the screen, similar to "Quest started" text. Optional arguments can be used to configure the display. Queue priority argument accepts the following values:
0 - Append to queue
1 - Prepend to queue
2 - Clear queue and display immediately
Justification argument accepts the following values:
0 - Left
1 - Center
2 - Right
Title and subtitle fonts accept integer values corresponding to font slots in [Fonts] section in Fallout.ini. |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = title |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = subtitle |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = soundID |Type = string |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = queuePriority |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = justification |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = titleFont |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = subtitleFont |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = QueueCinematicText "Title" "Subtitle" "UIPopUpQuestComplete" 2 2 1 1 }} ==See Also== *[[QueueObjectiveText]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Quest Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Sorts elements of an [[array]] of forms ([[Reference]]s or [[Base Form]]s) alphabetically (or reverse-alphabetically) using their [[Editor ID]]s, and returns a new array containing the elements in sorted order. |returnType = array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = source |Type = formArray}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = sortReverse |Type = bool |Optional = y}} |example = 
array_var aOriginal
array_var aSorted

let aOriginal := Ar_List SunnyREF, EasyPeteRef, GSChetRef

let aSorted := Ar_SortEditor aOriginal ; EasyPeteRef, GSChetRef, SunnyREF

let aSorted := Ar_SortEditor aOriginal, 1 ; SunnyREF, GSChetRef, EasyPeteRef
}} ==See Also== *[[Array Variables]] *[[Ar_CustomSort]] *[[Ar_SortAlpha]] *[[Ar_Sort]] *[[GetEditorID]] [[Category:Functions_(JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Replaces one of the following hardcoded UI sounds with a [[Sound]] form:
1 - Quest Failed
2 - Quest Added
3 - Quest Completed
4 - Location Discovered
|arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = sound |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = type |Type = int(1-4) }} |example = SetUIUpdateSound MyNewQuestCompleteSound 3 }} [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Sound Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions]]

{{Deprecated | xNVSE allows using Actor Value codes without the need for alternative functions.
See [[Actor Value Functions]]}} {{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns the current, modified value of the specified stat. GetActorValueAlt uses a numeric [[Actor Value Codes|Actor Value code]] instead of a stat name. |name = GetActorValueAlt |alias = GetAVAlt |returnVal = Stat |returnType = int |referenceType = [Actor] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = actorValueCode |Type = int }} |example = GetActorValueAlt 16 }} =='''See Also'''== *[[Actor Value Codes]] *[[ForceActorValueAlt]] *[[ModActorValueAlt]] *[[RestoreActorValueAlt]] *[[SetActorValueAlt]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]]

{{Deprecated | xNVSE allows using Actor Value codes without the need for alternative functions.
See [[Actor Value Functions]]}} {{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = SetActorValueAlt sets the stat's base value to the new value using a numeric [[Actor Value Codes|Actor Value code]] instead of a Stat name. |name = SetActorValueAlt |alias = SetAVAlt |referenceType = Actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ActorValueCode |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Value |Type = int }} |example = Player.SetActorValueAlt 16 3 }} == See Also == *[[ForceActorValueAlt]] *[[GetActorValueAlt]] *[[ModActorValueAlt]] *[[RestoreActorValueAlt]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]]

{{Deprecated | xNVSE allows using Actor Value codes without the need for alternative functions.
See [[Actor Value Functions]]}} {{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = ForceActorValueAlt modifies the current stat to the specified value (not to be confused with SetActorValue, which changes the BASE value of the stat), using a numeric [[Actor Value Codes|Actor Value code]] instead of a stat name. |name = ForceActorValueAlt |alias = ForceAVAlt |returnType = void |referenceType = [ActorRefID] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ActorValueCode |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Value |Type = int }} |example = ForceActorValueAlt 16 50 }} ==See Also== *[[ForceActorValue]] *[[UnforceAV]] *[[DamageActorValueAlt]] *[[GetActorValueAlt]] *[[ModActorValueAlt]] *[[RestoreActorValueAlt]] *[[SetActorValueAlt]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]]

{{Deprecated | xNVSE allows using Actor Value codes without the need for alternative functions.
See [[Actor Value Functions]]}} {{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = DamageActorValueAlt alters the damage modifier of a given actor value using a numeric [[Actor Value Codes|Actor Value code]] instead of a stat name. The damage modifier is always a negative number, or zero if there is no damage to the actor value. DamageActorValueAlt will decrease the modifier by the given amount. |name = DamageActorValueAlt |alias = DamageAVAlt |returnType = void |referenceType = [ActorRefID] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ActorValueCode |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Value |Type = int|float }} |example = BuddyRef.DamageActorValueAlt 16 5 For example, suppose you have an NPC with 10 points of health as the current reference. If Buddy has 10 points of health, then this would subtract 5 from the damage modifier and now [[GetActorValue]] health returns 5. }} ==See Also== *[[DamageActorValue]] *[[ForceActorValueAlt]] *[[GetActorValueAlt]] *[[ModActorValueAlt]] *[[RestoreActorValueAlt]] *[[SetActorValueAlt]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]]

{{Deprecated | xNVSE allows using Actor Value codes without the need for alternative functions.
See [[Actor Value Functions]]}} {{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Permanently alters the actor value using a numeric [[Actor Value Codes|Actor Value code]] instead of a Stat name. The modifier may be positive or negative. |name = ModActorValueAlt |alias = ModAVAlt |returnType = void |referenceType = [Actor] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ActorValueCode |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Value |Type = int }} |example = Player.ModActorValueAlt 5 3 This will permanently increase Player's Strength by 3 }} ==See Also== *[[ModActorValue]] *[[DamageActorValueAlt]] *[[ForceActorValueAlt]] *[[GetActorValueAlt]] *[[RestoreActorValueAlt]] *[[SetActorValueAlt]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]]

{{Deprecated | xNVSE allows using Actor Value codes without the need for alternative functions.
See [[Actor Value Functions]]}} {{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = RestoreActorValueAlt alters the damage modifier of a given actor value using a numeric [[Actor Value Codes|Actor Value code]] instead of a stat name. The damage modifier is always a negative number, or zero if there is no damage to the actor value. RestoreActorValueAlt will increase the modifier by the given amount. |name = RestoreActorValueAlt |alias = RestoreAVAlt |returnType = void |referenceType = [ActorRefID] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ActorValueCode |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Value |Type = int|float }} |example = BuddyRef.RestoreActorValueAlt 16 5 }} ==See Also== *[[RestoreActorValue]] *[[ForceActorValueAlt]] *[[GetActorValueAlt]] *[[ModActorValueAlt]] *[[DamageActorValueAlt]] *[[SetActorValueAlt]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]]

revision.comment: session persistent
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Highlights the body part of an actor. Can be used in scripts, unlike [[HighlightBodyPart]]. Like [[HighlightBodyPart]], only a single actor can be highlighted at a time. This function is '''session-persistent'''. Body part IDs are:
No Parameter - Everything except Weapon
0 - Torso
1 - Head1
2 - Head2
3 - LeftArm1
4 - LeftArm2
5 - RightArm1
6 - RightArm2
7 - LeftLeg1
8 - LeftLeg2
9 - LeftLeg3
10 - RightLeg1
11 - RightLeg2
12 - RightLeg3
13 - Brain
14 - Weapon
15 - Everything except Weapon
|referenceType = Actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = bodyPartID |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = SunnyREF.HighlightBodyPartAlt 0 }} ==See Also== *[[HighlightBodyPart]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]]

revision.comment: Fixed typo
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''[[UDF|UDF Script]]''' as a handler that will be invoked when a specified [[Quest]] (or any Quest) is completed. [[Formlist]]s are also accepted as filters. When invoked, the script is passed with a single argument: a Quest that was completed. |name = SetJohnnyOnCompleteQuestEventHandler |alias = SetOnCompleteQuestEventHandler |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flag |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = quest |Type = filter |Optional = y }} |example = 
SetJohnnyOnCompleteQuestEventHandler 1 OnCompleteQuestUDF 0 MyQuest
'''Sets''' the OnCompleteQuestUDF script as a handler to be invoked when '''MyQuest''' is completed. 
SetJohnnyOnCompleteQuestEventHandler 0 OnCompleteQuestUDF 0 MyQuest
'''Removes''' the OnCompleteQuestUDF script as a handler of the above. }} ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn OnCompleteQuestUDF

ref rQuest

begin Function {rQuest} 

...

end
==Notes== *Flag argument is reserved for future use and should always be 0. ==See Also== *[[SetJohnnyOnStartQuestEventHandler]] *[[SetJohnnyOnFailQuestEventHandler]] *[[SetJohnnyOnStopQuestEventHandler]] *[[SetOnQuestStageEventHandler]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Quest Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''UDF Script''' as a handler that will be invoked when a specified Quest (or any Quest) is failed. Formlists are also accepted as filters. When invoked, the script is passed with a single argument: a Quest that was failed. |name = SetJohnnyOnFailQuestEventHandler |alias = SetOnFailQuestEventHandler |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flag |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = quest |Type = filter |Optional = y }} |example = 
SetJohnnyOnFailQuestEventHandler 1 OnFailQuestUDF 0 MyQuest
'''Sets''' the OnFailQuestUDF script as a handler to be invoked when '''MyQuest''' is failed. 
SetJohnnyOnFailQuestEventHandler 0 OnFailQuestUDF 0 MyQuest
'''Removes''' the OnFailQuestUDF script as a handler of the above. }} ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn OnFailQuestUDF

ref rQuest

begin Function {rQuest} 

...

end
==Notes== *Flag argument is reserved for future use and should always be 0. ==See Also== *[[SetJohnnyOnCompleteQuestEventHandler]] *[[SetJohnnyOnStartQuestEventHandler]] *[[SetJohnnyOnStopQuestEventHandler]] *[[SetOnQuestStageEventHandler]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Quest Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns the Script Processing Delay of a quest. |returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = quest |Type = form }} |example = set fDelay to GetQuestDelay VCG01 }} ==See Also== *[[SetQuestDelay]] *[[GetDelayElapsed]] *[[SetDelayElapsed]]
[[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Quest Functions]] [[Category:Script Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns 1 if a specified DLL is currently loaded into the game's executable. Optional parameter controls whether the function looks for any matching dlls, or only dlls originating from game folder. |returnType = 0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = dllName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = checkOutsideOfGameFolder |Type = 0/1 }} |example = 
IsDLLLoaded "d3dx9_38" // returns 1 if New Vegas Heap Replacer is loaded
IsDLLLoaded "d3d9" // returns 1 if dxvk is loaded
IsDLLLoaded "usvfs_x86" 1 // returns 1 if the gsme was started through MO2
}} ==See Also== *[[IsModLoaded]] *[[IsPluginInstalled]] *[[GetFilesInFolder]] - can be used to retrieve DLL files in a specific folder. *[[IsProgramRunning]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Debugging]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''[[UDF|UDF Script]]''' as a handler that will be invoked when the user makes changes in [[MenuMode|Settings Menu]] and closes it. |name = SetJohnnyOnSettingsUpdateEventHandler |alias = SetOnSettingsUpdateEventHandler |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flag |Type = int }} |example = 
SetJohnnyOnSettingsUpdateEventHandler 1 OnSettingsUpdateUDF 0
'''Sets''' the OnSettingsUpdateUDF script as a handler to be invoked when settings are changed. 
SetJohnnyOnSettingsUpdateEventHandler 0 OnSettingsUpdateUDF 0
'''Removes''' the OnSettingsUpdateUDF script as a handler of the above. }} ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn OnSettingsUpdateUDF 

begin Function {} 

...

end
==Notes== *Flag argument is reserved for future use and should always be 0. ==See Also== *[[SetOnMenuOpenEventHandler]] *[[SetOnMenuCloseEventHandler]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns the direction of a nearest hostile compass target as one of the following values:
0 - front
1 - front right
2 - right
3 - back right
4 - back
5 - back left
6 - left
7 - front left
|returnType = int(0-8) ||arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = skipInvisibleHostiles |Type = int |Value = 0/1 |Optional = y }} |example = set iDirection to GetNearestCompassHostileDirection 1 // Gets the direction of a nearest hostile actor, skipping invisible hostiles }} [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Combat Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Compass Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns the nearest hostile compass target. |returnType = ref ||arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = skipInvisibleHostiles |Type = int |Value = 0/1 |Optional = y }} |example = set rHostile to GetNearestCompassHostile 1 // Gets the nearest hostile compass target, skipping invisible hostiles }} ==See Also== *[[GetNearestCompassHostileDirection]] *[[IsCompassHostile]]
[[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Combat Functions]] [[Category:Compass Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Marks a '''Note''' as read or unread. If the "serialize" parameter is passed and set to 1, the change will persist in the save (since version 5.17). |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = note |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = isRead |Type = int |Value = 0/1 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = serialize |Type = int |Optional = y |Value = 0/1 }} |example = SetNoteRead MyNote 1 1 ==See Also== * [[GetNoteRead]] }} [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Note Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Sets HUD visibility flags bitmask that will be used when player controls are disabled. HUD Visibility flags are as follows:
AP Meter = 1 (bit 0),
HP Meter = 2 (bit 1),
Radiation Meter = 4 (bit 2),
Enemy Health = 8 (bit 3),
Quest Reminder = 16 (bit 4),
Region Location = 32 (bit 5),
Reticle = 64 (bit 6),
Sneak Meter = 128 (bit 7),
Messages = 256 (bit 8),
Info = 512 (bit 9),
Subtitles = 1024 (bit 10),
Hotkeys = 2048 (bit 11),
XP Meter = 4096 (bit 12),
Breath Meter = 8192 (bit 13),
Grenade Indicator = 16384 (bit 14),
Crippled Limb Indicator = 32768 (bit 15),
Hardcore Mode Meters = 65536 (bit 16)
|alias = SetDPCHUDFlags |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = flags |Type = bitmask }} |example = SetDPCHUDFlags iBitMask }} ==See Also== *[[SetBit]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns local or world camera position. |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = x |Type = returnVariable }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = y |Type = returnVariable }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = z |Type = returnVariable }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = worldOrLocal |Type = int |Value = 0/1 }} |example = 
float xOut
float yOut
float zOut
GetCameraTranslation xOut yOut zOut 1 // Stores local camera position in xOut, yOut and zOut variables
}} [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]]

revision.comment: add note for invisible actors
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns true if a calling actor reference is a hostile compass target.
This function can return true even if the calling actor is invisible and the player does not have the Improved Detection [[Perk Entry Point]]. |returnVal = 0, 1 |returnType = int |referenceType = Actor |example = SunnyREF.IsCompassHostile }} [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Combat Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Compass Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Disables or enables combat music until the game is restarted. |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = toggle |Type = int |Value = 0/1 }} |example = ToggleCombatMusic 0 }} [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Sound Functions]] [[Category:Music]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns 1 if combat music is enabled, 0 if it was disabled with [[ToggleCombatMusic]]. |returnType = int |example = set bMusicOn to IsCombatMusicEnabled }} [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Sound Functions]] [[Category:Music]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns 1 if there are hostile actors near the player. Maximum distance is controlled by '''fHostileActorExteriorDistance''' and '''fHostileActorInteriorDistance''' settings. |returnVal = 0, 1 |returnType = int |example = set bHostilesNear to IsHostilesNearby }} ==Notes== *The function utilizes internal subroutine which is called when Sleep/Wait key is pressed in the game.
==See Also== *[[IsInCombat]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''[[UDF|UDF Script]]''' as a handler that will be invoked when a specified '''[[:Category:Perk|Perk]]''' (or any '''Perk''') is added to the player, or its Perk Rank is changed. [[FormList]]s are also accepted as filters. When invoked, the script is passed with three arguments: the added perk, previous perk rank and new perk rank. |name = SetJohnnyOnAddPerkEventHandler |alias = SetOnAddPerkEventHandler |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flag |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = perk |Type = filter |Optional = y }} |example = 
SetJohnnyOnAddPerkEventHandler 1 OnAddPerkUDF 0 BloodyMess
'''Sets''' the OnAddPerkUDF script as a handler to be invoked when '''Bloody Mess''' perk is added to the player (or any other actor). 
SetJohnnyOnAddPerkEventHandler 0 OnAddPerkUDF 0 BloodyMess
'''Removes''' the OnAddPerkUDF script as a handler of the above. }} ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn OnAddPerkUDF 

ref rPerk
int iOldRank
int iNewRank

ref rPerkOwner

begin Function {rPerk, iOldRank, iNewRank} 

	set rPerkOwner to this

	(code)

end
==Notes== *Flag argument is reserved for future use and should always be 0. *To differentiate between player and companion perks, use [[GetAltPerkRank]] in the handler script. *Since [[:Category:Functions_(JIP)|JIP LN]] version 56.24, it is possible for actors other than the player character to receive and hold perks directly. And since JohnnyGuitar version 4.0, this handler can thus fire for any actor and not just the player. Since it cannot be filtered for a specific actor that received the perk, you may want to check inside the script which actor did through [[GetSelf]]. ==See Also== *[[SetJohnnyOnRemovePerkEventHandler]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Perk Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''[[UDF|UDF Script]]''' as a handler that will be invoked when a specified '''[[:Category:Perk|Perk]]''' (or any '''Perk''') is removed from the player. [[FormList]]s are also accepted as filters. When invoked, the script is passed with a single argument - a perk than was removed. |name = SetJohnnyOnRemovePerkEventHandler |alias = SetOnRemovePerkEventHandler |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flag |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = perk |Type = filter |Optional = y }} |example = 
SetJohnnyOnRemovePerkEventHandler 1 OnRemovePerkUDF 0 BloodyMess
'''Sets''' the OnRemovePerkUDF script as a handler to be invoked when '''Bloody Mess''' perk is removed from the player. 
SetJohnnyOnRemovePerkEventHandler 0 OnRemovePerkUDF 0 BloodyMess
'''Removes''' the OnRemovePerkUDF script as a handler of the above. }} ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn OnRemovePerkUDF

ref rPerk

ref rPerkOwner

begin Function {rPerk} 

	set rPerkOwner to this

	(code)

end
==Notes== *Flag argument is reserved for future use and should always be 0. *Since [[:Category:Functions_(JIP)|JIP LN]] version 56.24, it is possible for actors other than the player character to receive and hold perks directly. And since JohnnyGuitar version 4.0, this handler can thus fire for any actor and not just the player. Since it cannot be filtered for a specific actor on which the perk is removed, you may want to check inside the script through [[GetSelf]]. ==See Also== *[[SetJohnnyOnAddPerkEventHandler]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Perk Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

revision.comment: Reordering
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Modifies the '''time left''' of Nth temporary effect-entry of the calling actor. [[GetTempEffects]] should be called before calling this function, and on the same frame, in order to determine the index of the effect-entry to be modified. |name = ModNthTempEffectTimeLeft |returnType = success:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = index |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = modifier |Type = float }} |example = actorRef.ModNthTempEffectTimeLeft 2 -5 ; decreases the time left for the effect to run by 5 }} ==Notes== *Internally the function modifies "time elapsed" variable, which counts from 0 to the effect's duration. This means that you can't change the time left by a value bigger than the current time elapsed, or it would go into negatives and cause undefined behavior. Consider this example: 
 ; timeElapsed 0, duration 240
actorRef.ModNthTempEffectTimeLeft 0 100
; timeElapsed -100, duration 240
==See Also== *[[RemoveNthTempEffect]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns World Model path of a weapon. |returnType = string_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = form }} |example = set sPath to GetWeaponWorldModelPath WeapShishkebab }} ==See Also== *[[GetScopeModelPath]] *[[GetWeapon1stPersonModel]] *[[SetWeaponWorldModelPath]]
[[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns String trait of a Body Part Data form, using body part type ID and trait ID. ==Body-Part Type IDs== <pre>0	Torso 1&#..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns String trait of a Body Part Data form, using body part type ID and trait ID. ==Body-Part Type IDs== 
0	Torso
1	Head 1
2	Head 2
3	Left Arm 1
4	Left Arm 2
5	Right Arm 1
6	Right Arm 2
7	Left Leg 1
8	Left Leg 2
9	Left Leg 3
10	Right Leg 1
11	Right Leg 2
12	Right Leg 3
13	Brain
14	Weapon
==Body-Part Trait IDs== 
1	Part Node
2	VATS Target
3	Start Node
4	Part Name
5	Target Bone
|returnType = string_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = bodyPartData |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = typeID |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int }} |example = set sTrait to GetBodyPartTraitString NVGecko 0 3 }} ==See Also== *[[GetBodyPartTraitNumeric]] *[[GetBodyPartTraitForm]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns the type of an actor effect form. Return values are as follows:
-1 - Invalid type
0 - Actor Effect
1 - Disease
2 - Power (unused)
3 - Lesser Power (unused)
4 - Ability
5 - Poison
10 - Addiction
|returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = actorEffect |Type = form }} |example = set iType to GetActorEffectType rEffect }} [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns width of a .dds texture file using its filepath relative to Data\Texures folder. |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = path |Type = string }} |example = set iWidth to GetTextureWidth "myTexture.dds" }} ==Notes== * Since version 5.06, the function supports textures packed into BSA archives as well as loose files. ==See Also== *[[GetTextureHeight]] *[[GetTextureFormat]] *[[GetTextureMipMapCount]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Texture Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns height of a .dds texture file using its filepath relative to Data\Textures folder. |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = path |Type = string }} |example = set iHeight to GetTextureHeight "myTexture.dds" }} ==Notes== * Since version 5.06, the function supports textures packed into BSA archives as well as loose files. ==See Also== *[[GetTextureWidth]] *[[GetTextureFormat]] *[[GetTextureMipMapCount]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Texture Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns mipmap count of a .dds texture file using its filepath relative to Data\Textures folder. |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = path |Type = string }} |example = set iMipMapCount to GetTextureMipMapCount "myTexture.dds" }} ==Notes== * Since version 5.06, the function supports textures packed into BSA archives as well as loose files. ==See Also== *[[GetTextureWidth]] *[[GetTextureHeight]] *[[GetTextureFormat]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Texture Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns format of a .dds texture file using its filepath relative to Data\Textures folder. Returns values 1 to 5 corresponding to DXT1 to DXT5. |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = path |Type = string }} |example = set iFormat to GetTextureFormat "myTexture.dds" }} ==Notes== * Since version 5.06, the function supports textures packed into BSA archives as well as loose files. ==See Also== *[[GetTextureWidth]] *[[GetTextureHeight]] *[[GetTextureMipMapCount]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Texture Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Sends an alarm to the owner of an item or a container reference as if it was stolen. If the actor is not being detected by the owner of an item, it will result in a karma loss.

When using a container reference, optional argument can be used for alternative behavior - when set to 1, function will first scan calling actor's inventory for items stolen from the container's owner and dispatch the alarm if any were found. |referenceType = thiefActor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = stolenItemOrContainer |Type = refr }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = scanInventory |Type = int(0/1) |Optional = y }} |example = Player.SendStealingAlarm itemRefr }} ==See Also== *[[SendAssaultAlarm]] *[[SendTrespassAlarm]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Crime_Functions]]

revision.comment: 4.93 changes: can now remove poison
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Applies specified poison to the calling inventory reference. If calling reference is an Actor, its equipped weapon will be used.
===New in 4.93=== The ''poison'' arg is now optional; if omitted or set to a null ref, will remove the poison from the weapon, if there is any. |referenceType = weaponInventoryRefOrActor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = poison |Type = baseform |Optional = y |Value = 0 }} |example = Player.ApplyWeaponPoison BleakVenom Will apply Bleak Venom to player's equipped weapon. inventoryRefr.ApplyWeaponPoison BleakVenom Will apply Bleak Venom to the inventoryRefr, provided it's a weapon. }} [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Calculates spread for an Actor and its equipped weapon, accounting for movement types, perk modifiers, ammo and weapon mod effects. |returnType = float |referenceType = actor |example = Player.GetCalculatedSpread }} ==See Also== *[[GetCalculatedActorSpread]] *[[Gun Spread Formula (New Vegas)]]
[[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns the actor speaking through the talking activator. |returnType = form |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = tal..."

revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns either player's karma title (e.g. Vault Martyr) or karma tier (e.g. Very Good) as a string variable. |returnType = string_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = titleOrTier |Type = int(0/1) |Optional = y }} |example = set sTitle to GetPlayerKarmaTitle 0 }}
[[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Karma Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns the array of all hostile compass targets. Optional parameter allows skipping invisible targets. |returnType = array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = skipInvisible |Type = int(0/1) |Optional = y }} |example = let aEnemies := GetCompassHostiles 1 }} ==See Also== *[[GetNearestCompassHostile]] *[[GetNearestCompassHostileDirection]] *[[IsCompassHostile]] *[[GetCompassTargets]]
[[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Combat Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Compass Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Prevents saving until the save is reloaded. |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = onOrOff |Type = int(1/0) }} |example = ToggleDisableSaves 1 // will disable saves }} ==Notes== * Save disabling is stored on a per-mod basis, so if two mods disable saving at some point, it will be disabled until both of them will call '''ToggleDisableSaves 0''', or until the save is reloaded. *Does not work when set from the console or a scriptrunner script, since those share one mod index. *Prevents both [[ForceSave]] and [[SystemSave]] from creating saves as well.
[[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Time Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''[[UDF|UDF Script]]''' as a handler that will be executed every frame before the frame is rendered.
'''Due to the nature of this event, it should only be used for situations where frame-wide synchronization is required. Any other action shall be done outside it, i.e. using [[SetGameMainLoopCallback]].''' |alias = SetOnRenderUpdateEventHandler |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flag |Type = int }} |example = 
SetJohnnyOnRenderUpdateEventHandler 1 OnRenderUpdateUDF 0
'''Sets''' the OnRenderUpdateUDF script as a handler to be executed every frame before it's rendered. 
SetJohnnyOnRenderUpdateEventHandler 0 OnRenderUpdateUDF 0
'''Removes''' the OnRenderUpdateUDF script as a handler of the above. }} ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn OnRenderUpdateUDF 

begin Function {} 

...

end
==Notes== *Flag argument is reserved for future use and should always be 0. ==See Also== *[[SetGameMainLoopCallback]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 4.10 |summary = Returns an array of perks that are available to the player on its current level (same perks would be available in Level Up Menu). |returnType = array |example = array_var aPerks = GetAvailablePerks }} [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Perk Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 4.10 |summary = Returns true if the game considers interacting with a calling reference a "hostile" action, performing the same checks that are used to determine red color of the crosshair or the activation prompt. |returnVal = 0/1 |returnType = int |referenceType = reference |example = int isHostile = JoeCobbREF.IsCrimeOrEnemy }} [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 4.20 |summary = Returns the value of a numeric '''Effect Shader''' trait. |returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = effectShader |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int }} |example = float fFallOff = GetEffectShaderTraitNumeric NukeAtomShader 13 }} ==Effect Shader trait IDs== 
0 - Flags (values below)
1 - Membrane Shader - Source Blend Mode (values below)
2 - Membrane Shader - Blend Operation (values below)
3 - Membrane Shader - Z Test Function (values below)
4 - Fill/Texture Effect - Color (RGB)
5 - Fill/Texture Effect - Alpha Fade In Time
6 - Fill/Texture Effect - Full Alpha Time
7 - Fill/Texture Effect - Alpha Fade Out Time
8 - Fill/Texture Effect - Presistent Alpha Ratio
9 - Fill/Texture Effect - Alpha Pulse Amplitude
10 - Fill/Texture Effect - Alpha Pulse Frequency
11 - Fill/Texture Effect - Texture Animation Speed (U)
12 - Fill/Texture Effect - Texture Animation Speed (V)
13 - Edge Effect - Fall Off
14 - Edge Effect - Color (RGB)
15 - Edge Effect - Alpha Fade In Time
16 - Edge Effect - Full Alpha Time
17 - Edge Effect - Alpha Fade Out Time
18 - Edge Effect - Persistent Alpha Ratio
19 - Edge Effect - Alpha Pulse Amplitude
20 - Edge Effect - Alpha Pusle Frequence
21 - Fill/Texture Effect - Full Alpha Ratio
22 - Edge Effect - Full Alpha Ratio
23 - Membrane Shader - Dest Blend Mode (values below)
24 - Particle Shader - Source Blend Mode (values below)
25 - Particle Shader - Blend Operation (values below)
26 - Particle Shader - Z Test Function (values below)
27 - Particle Shader - Dest Blend Mode (values below)
28 - Particle Shader - Particle Birth Ramp Up Time
29 - Particle Shader - Full Particle Birth Time
30 - Particle Shader - Particle Birth Ramp Down Time
31 - Particle Shader - Full Particle Birth Ratio
32 - Particle Shader - Persistant Particle Birth Ratio
33 - Particle Shader - Particle Lifetime
34 - Particle Shader - Particle Lifetime +/-
35 - Particle Shader - Initial Speed Along Normal
36 - Particle Shader - Acceleration Along Normal
37 - Particle Shader - Initial Velocity #1
38 - Particle Shader - Initial Velocity #2
39 - Particle Shader - Initial Velocity #3
40 - Particle Shader - Acceleration #1
41 - Particle Shader - Acceleration #2
42 - Particle Shader - Acceleration #3
43 - Particle Shader - Scale Key 1
44 - Particle Shader - Scale Key 2
45 - Particle Shader - Scale Key 1 Time
46 - Particle Shader - Scale Key 2 Time
47 - Color Key 1 (RGB)
48 - Color Key 2 (RGB)
49 - Color Key 3 (RGB)
50 - Color Key 1 Alpha
51 - Color Key 2 Alpha
52 - Color Key 3 Alpha
53 - Color Key 1 Key Time
54 - Color Key 2 Key Time
55 - Color Key 3 Key Time
56 - Particle Shader - Initial Speed Along Normal +/-
57 - Particle Shader - Initial Rotation (deg)
58 - Particle Shader - Initial Rotation (deg) +/-
59 - Particle Shader - Rotation Speed (deg/sec)
60 - Particle Shader - Rotation Speed (deg/sec) +/-
61 - Addon Models (form ID)
62 - Holes - Start Time
63 - Holes - End Time
64 - Holes - Start Val
65 - Holes - End Val
66 - Edge Width (alpha units)
67 - Edge Color
68 - Explosion Wind Speed
69 - Texture Count U
70 - Texture Count V
71 - Addon Models - Fade In Time
72 - Addon Models - Fade Out Time
73 - Addon Models - Scale Start
74 - Addon Models - Scale End
75 - Addon Models - Scale In Time
76 - Addon Models - Scale Out Time
==Flags== 
0 - No Membrane Shader
3 - No Particle Shader
4 - Edge Effect Inverse
5 - Affect Skin Only)
==Blend Mode values== 
1 - Zero
2 - One
3 - Source Color
4 - Source Inverse Color
5 - Source Alpha
6 - Source Inverted Alpha
7 - Dest Alpha
8 - Dest Inverted Alpha
9 - Dest Color
10 - Dest Inverse Color
11 - Source Alpha SA
==Blend Operation values== 
1 - Add
2 - Subtract
3 - Reverse Subtract
4 - Minimum
5 - Maximum
==Z Test Function values== 
3 - Equal To
4 - Normal
5 - Greater Than
7 - Greater Than or Equal To
8 - Always Show
==See Also== *[[SetEffectShaderTraitNumeric]] *[[GetEffectShaderTexturePath]] *[[SetEffectShaderTexturePath]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]]

Most changes to effect shaders will not take effect while the shader effect is playing. |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = effectShader |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = value |Type = float }} |example = SetEffectShaderTraitNumeric NukeAtomShader 13 1.5 }} ==Effect Shader trait IDs==

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 4.20 |summary = Sets the specified texture path of an '''Effect Shader'''. |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = effectShader |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = textureID |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = path |Type = string }} |example = SetEffectShaderTexturePath NukeAtomShader 0 "my/path/to/texture.dds" }} ==Effect Shader texture IDs== 
0 - Fill Texture
1 - Particle Shader Texture
2 - Holes Texture
==See Also== *[[SetEffectShaderTraitNumeric]] *[[GetEffectShaderTraitNumeric]] *[[GetEffectShaderTexturePath]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 4.20 |summary = Returns the specified texture path of an '''Effect Shader'''. |returnType = string_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = effectShader |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = textureID |Type = int }} |example = string_var sFillTexture = GetEffectShaderTexturePath NukeAtomShader 0 }} ==Effect Shader texture IDs== 
0 - Fill Texture
1 - Particle Shader Texture
2 - Holes Texture
==See Also== *[[SetEffectShaderTraitNumeric]] *[[GetEffectShaderTraitNumeric]] *[[SetEffectShaderTexturePath]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 4.20 |summary = Returns the global '''RGB color''' of the specified interface. 
1	HUD - Main
2	HUD - Alt.
3	Terminal
4	Pip-Boy
5	Main/Pause Menu
|returnType = hex |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = interfaceID |Type = int |Value = 1-5 }} |example = int iColor = GetSystemColor 1 }} == Retrieving individual RGB values == The function returns a combined RGB value. To separate it into three channels, bitwise operations can be used, as follows: 
int iRgb
int iRed
int iGreen
int iBlue

set iRgb to GetSystemColor 1

set iRed to RightShift iRgb 16
set iRed to LogicalAnd iRed 255

set iGreen to RightShift iRgb 8
set iGreen to LogicalAnd iGreen 255

set iBlue to LogicalAnd iRgb 255

printc "%g %g %g" iRed iGreen iBlue
== See Also == *[[SetSystemColor]] *[[GetSystemColorAlt]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 4.20 |summary = Returns thresholded (capped) value of an '''Actor Value'''. Various actor values have flags specifying their minimum and maximum values, such as 1 and 10 for Charisma or 0 and 100 for Barter, even though these stats can have values outside of this range. The game uses thresholded actor values for calculations, such as detection formula. |alias = GetThresholdAV |returnType = float |referenceType = actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = name |Type = ActorValue }} |example = Player.GetThresholdAV Barter }} [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 4.20 |summary = Takes numeric values of Red, Green and Blue channels (from 0 to 255) and returns Hue (from 0 to 360 degrees), Saturation and Value (percentage from 0 to 1). Uses script variables as inputs and outputs. |name = RGBtoHSV |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = rIn |Type = scriptVar }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = gIn |Type = scriptVar }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = bIn |Type = scriptVar }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = hOut |Type = scriptVar }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = sOut |Type = scriptVar }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = vOut |Type = scriptVar }} |example = 
int rIn = 120
int gIn = 255
int bIn = 13
float hOut
float sOut
float vOut
RGBtoHSV rIn gIn bIn hOut sOut vOut
}} ==See Also== *[[HSVtoRGB]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 4.20 |summary = Takes values of Hue (from 0 to 360 degrees), Saturation and Value (percentage from 0 to 1) and returns va..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 4.20 |summary = Takes values of Hue (from 0 to 360 degrees), Saturation and Value (percentage from 0 to 1) and returns values of Red, Green and Blue channels (from 0 to 255). Uses script variables as inputs and outputs. |name = HSVtoRGB |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = hIn |Type = scriptVar }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = sIn |Type = scriptVar }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = vIn |Type = scriptVar }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = rOut |Type = scriptVar }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = gOut |Type = scriptVar }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = bOut |Type = scriptVar }} |example = 
float hIn = 300
float sIn = 0.5
float vIn = 0.3
int rOut
int gOut
int bOut
HSVtoRGB hIn sIn vIn rOut gOut bOut
}} ==See Also== *[[RGBtoHSV]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 4.20 |summary = Returns 1 if the game is currently in a state where only load screens specifically associated with the cu..."
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 4.20 |summary = Returns 1 if the game is currently in a state where only load screens specifically associated with the current cell are displayed ([[SetLocationSpecificLoadScreensOnly]] was called). |returnVal = 0, 1 |returnType = int |example = int iSpecificLSCR = GetLocationSpecificLoadScreensOnly }} ==See Also== *[[SetLocationSpecificLoadScreensOnly]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 4.30 |summary = Returns an array of alternate texture forms of a specified Armor model. Model IDs are as follows:
1 - Male Biped model
2 - Female Biped model
3 - Male World model
4 - Female World model
|returnType = array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = armor |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = modelID |Type = int }} |example = array_var altTextures = GetArmorAltTextures MS18RangerBattlearmor 1 }} ==See Also== *[[GetWeaponAltTextures]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Armor Functions]] [[Category:Texture Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 4.30 |summary = Returns specified numeric trait of an Idle Marker. Trait IDs are as follows:
1 - Flags
2 - Idle Timer
3 - Animation Count
Flags bitmask can include the following bits:
bit 0 - Run in Sequence (1)
bit 2 - Do Once (4)
|returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = idleMarker |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int }} |example = GetIdleMarkerTraitNumeric DefaultBrahminIdleMarker 2 }} ==See Also== *[[SetIdleMarkerTraitNumeric]] *[[GetIdleMarkerAnimations]] *[[SetIdleMarkerAnimation]] *[[SetIdleMarkerAnimations]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 4.30 |summary = Sets specified numeric trait of an Idle Marker. Trait IDs are as follows:<pre> 1 - Flags (bitmask) 2 - Id..."

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 4.30 |summary = Sets specified numeric trait of an Idle Marker. Trait IDs are as follows:
1 - Flags (bitmask)
2 - Idle Timer
Flags bitmask can include the following bits:
bit 0 - Run in Sequence (1)
bit 2 - Do Once (4)
|arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = idleMarker |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = traitID |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = float }} |example = SetIdleMarkerTraitNumeric DefaultBrahminIdleMarker 2 50 }} ==Notes== *Animation Count trait is not available here as it is updated automatically when using [[SetIdleMarkerAnimations]]. ==See Also== *[[GetIdleMarkerTraitNumeric]] *[[GetIdleMarkerAnimations]] *[[SetIdleMarkerAnimation]] *[[SetIdleMarkerAnimations]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]]

revision.text: {{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 4.30 |summary = Replaces an animation of a specified Idle Marker using a numeric index. |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = idleMarker |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = index |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newIdle |Type = baseform }} |example = SetIdleMarkerAnimation DefaultStandingTerminalMarker 0 LooseStandingArmsFolded }} ==See Also== *[[GetIdleMarkerTraitNumeric]] *[[SetIdleMarkerTraitNumeric]] *[[SetIdleMarkerAnimations]] *[[GetIdleMarkerAnimations]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 4.30 |summary = Returns flags bitmask of a Terminal menu entry using its index. Flags bitmask can include the following bits:
bit 0 - Add Note (1)
bit 1 - Force Redraw (2)
|arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = terminal |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = entryIndex |Type = int }} |example = int terminalFlags = GetTerminalMenuItemFlags MyTerminal 0 }} ==See Also== *[[SetTerminalMenuItemFlags]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

Returns -1 for non-skill books. |returnType = avCode |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = book |Type = baseform }} |example = int iSkill = GetBookSkill MyBook }} ==See Also== *[[SetBookSkill]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Book Functions]]

Accepts [[Actor Value Codes|actor value codes]] between 32 and 46 (skills), and -1 to make the book not teach any skill. |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = book |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = avCode |Type = int }} |example = SetBookSkill MyBook 35 }} ==See Also== *[[GetBookSkill]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Book Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 4.55 |alias = SetJohnnyOnActorValueChangeEventHandler |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''[[UDF|UDF Script]]''' as a handler that will be invoked when a specified '''[[Actor Value]]''' (or ''any'' '''Actor Value''', if the optional parameter is omitted) is changed. The event is limited to Player's actor value changes and can be further filtered using an [[Actor_Value_Codes|Actor Value code]]. When invoked, the script receives three arguments: Actor Value code, its previous value and its new value. |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flag |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = avCode |Type = filter |Optional = y }} |example = 
SetOnActorValueChangeEventHandler 1 OnHealthChangeUDF 0 16
'''Sets''' the OnHealthChangeUDF script as a handler to be invoked when player's Health changes 
SetOnActorValueChangeEventHandler 0 OnHealthChangeUDF 0 16
'''Removes''' the OnHealthChangeUDF script as a handler of the above. }} ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn OnHealthChangeUDF

int avCode
float previousValue
float newValue

begin Function {avCode, previousValue, newValue} 

	(code)

end
==Notes== *Flag argument is reserved for future use and should always be 0. *Changes to ''InventoryWeight'' actor value do not dispatch this event, because internally this actor value is only updated when ''GetActorValue'' is called on it. Similarly, the event does not dispatch when derived skills get increased by their associated SPECIAL, although direct increases to skills do dispatch it. *Prior to version 5.08, the event was being dispatched with incorrect previousValue and newValue. ==See Also== [[SetOnNPCActorValueChangeEventHandler]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

16	Fog power

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 4.65 |summary = Removes model path of a Weapon scope. |returnVal = isSuccess |returnType = 0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = baseform }} |example = RemoveScopeModelPath WeapSniperRifle ==Notes== To successfully remove a scope from a scoped weapon via script, a number of other operations must be carried out. First of all, the 'Has Scope' flag must be removed from the weapon in question, as well as the 'Scope From Mod' and 'Scope Has Night Vision' flags (if applicable). You must also increase the weapon's FOV if you don't want it to zoom in as far as it did with the scope equipped, and remove the mod-added scope FOV reduction (if applicable). However, to successfully fully remove a scope from a weapon in game these operations must take place AFTER you call RemoveScopeModelPath on the weapon. If the scoped weapon is currently equipped to the player character Player.ReloadEquippedModels must be called to apply the changes to the weapon. ==Example 2, Removing Scope From Current Equipped Weapon== 
let refWeap := Player.GetEquippedObject 5

RemoveScopeModelPath refWeap

let iWeapFlags := GetWeaponFlags1 refWeap
let iWeapFlags := ClearBit iWeapFlags, 2
SetWeaponFlags1 iWeapFlags, refWeap

let iWeapFlags := GetWeaponFlags1 refWeap
let iWeapFlags := ClearBit iWeapFlags, 12
let iWeapFlags := ClearBit iWeapFlags, 13
SetWeaponFlags2 iWeapFlags, refWeap

SetWeaponSightFOV 65 refWeap
Player.ReloadEquippedModels 5
==See Also== *[[GetWeaponFlags1]] *[[GetWeaponFlags2]] *[[SetWeaponFlags1]] *[[SetWeaponFlags2]] *[[GetWeaponSightFOV]] *[[SetWeaponSightFOV]] *[[GetWeaponItemModValue1]] }} [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 4.70 |summary = Sets a custom icon path for the icon that appears in the message box when player's reputation tier changes. Changes are unique to the reputation form passed as an argument. Reputation icons have 4 tiers used to indicate changes (tier names are derived from default icons): 
1	in pain
2	sad
3	neutral
4	very happy
Changes affect corner messages for reputation as well - tier 2 is used for negative changes, and 4 for positive. |returnType = success(0/1) |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = reputation |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = tier |Type = int(1-4) }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = iconPath |Type = string }} |example = SetCustomReputationChangeIcon GoodspringsReputation 2 "path/to/my/icon.dds" ; will change the icon appearing in the message when reputation with Goodsprings decreases by small amount }} [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Reputation Functions]]
