64 - PC murdered a faction member (bit 6)
256 - Track crime (bit 8)
512 - Allow sell (bit 9)
|returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = faction |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flags |Type = int }} |example = SetFactionFlags Lucky38Faction 1 }} ==See Also== *[[GetFactionFlags]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Faction Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''UDF Script''' as an event handler that will be executed when GetActorsByProcessingLevel#Processi..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''UDF Script''' as an event handler that will be executed when [[GetActorsByProcessingLevel#Processing-Levels|processing level]] of an actor changes.
When invoked, the script receives three arguments: Actor reference, old processing level and new processing level.

The following optional filters are accepted:
1. NPC reference/base form/formlist of references/formlist of baseforms;
2. New processing level. |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flag |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = NPC |Type = filter |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newLevel |Type = filter |Optional = y }} |example = 

SetOnProcessLevelChangeEventHandler 1 OnProcessChangeUDF 0 SunnyREF 0
'''Sets''' the OnProcessChangeUDF script as a handler to be executed when '''SunnyREF''''s processing level changes to High. 
SetOnProcessLevelChangeEventHandler 0 OnProcessChangeUDF 0 SunnyREF 0
'''Removes''' the OnProcessChangeUDF script as a handler of the above. }} ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn OnProcessChangeUDF

ref rActor
int iOldLevel
int iNewLevel

begin Function {rActor, iOldLevel, iNewLevel} 

...

end
==Notes== *Event must be registered in [[GameMode]] block (not [[MenuMode|MenuMode 4]]), because it's reset every game load. This also means [[GetGameRestarted]] is NOT a viable option to set this event for the session. Instead consider using [[GetGameLoaded]] to (re)set the event handler. *Flag argument is reserved for future use and should always be 0. [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 4.80 |summary = Returns the value of a custom miscellaneous stat previously created with [[ModExtraMiscStat]]. |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = statName |Type = string }} |example = GetExtraMiscStat "Pepeglares posted" }} ==Notes== * Starting from version 4.98 [[InitExtraMiscStat]] is required to use custom misc stats. [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category: Stat Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 4.80 |summary = Modifies the value of a custom miscellaneous stat. If the stat doesn't exist, it will be created and added to the Pip-Boy menu. Stats and their values are stored in the save. |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = statName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = modValue |Type = int }} |example = ModExtraMiscStat "Pepeglares posted" 1 ; Will increase a misc by 1 }} ==Notes== * Starting from version 4.98 [[InitExtraMiscStat]] is required to use custom misc stats. ==See Also== *[[GetExtraMiscStat]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category: Stat Functions]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 4.85 |summary = Returns current moon phase.
0 - Full moon
1 - Waning gibbous
2 - Third quarter
3 - Waning crescent
4 - New moon
5 - Waxing crescent
6 - First quarter
7 - Waxing gibbous
|returnVal = 0-7 |returnType = int |example = GetMoonPhase }} [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns an [[array]] of recipes that produce (output) a given [[item]]. |name = GetFormRecipesAlt |returnType = array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = outputItem |Type = baseform }} |example = GetFormRecipesAlt Ammo5mm }} [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:Recipe Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Allows you to add new dialogue topics. Response types:
0 - Link From
1 - Choices
2 - Follow Ups
|arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = topicInfoResponse |Type = INFO }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = topic |Type = DIAL }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = type |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = position |Type = int }} |example = DialogResponseAddRelatedTopic 001418C4 BoSTruceTopic1 1 0 // Adds a new related topic to the response }} [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Dialogue Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns an array of dialogue responses. Response types:
0 - Link From
1 - Choices
2 - Follow Ups
|arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = TopicInfo |Type = topic }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Response Type |Type = int }} |example = DialogResponseRelatedGetAll 001418C4 }} [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Dialogue Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 4.96b |summary = Returns a float of the player's camera FOV. If ''worldOr1stOrScene'' is set to 0, will return the World FOV (3rd person FOV). If it is 1, will return the first-person FOV. If it is 2, will return the FOV according to the current Scene being displayed by the SceneGraph. |name = GetPlayerCamFOV |returnVal = fov |returnType = float |referenceType = reference |conditionFunc = Both |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = int |Type = Integer }} |example = float fPlayerFPSFOV = Player.GetPlayerCamFOV 1 Returns the player's first-person FOV. }}
[[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 4.98 |summary = Initializes a custom miscellaneous stat. This function is required for a custom miscellaneous stat to be displayed in the Pip-Boy. |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = statName |Type = string }} |example = InitExtraMiscStat "Pepeglares posted" ; Will create a new misc stat and set its value to 0. }} ==Notes== * This function needs to be used in a MenuMode 4 block if being used in a plugin. JIP Script Runner files must use the function in a file prefixed with gr_. ==See Also== *[[ModExtraMiscStat]] *[[GetExtraMiscStat]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category: Stat Functions]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 4.97 |summary = An extended version [[ShowBarberMenu]] that can take a [[FormList]] to blacklist/whitelist certain beards and hairs. |name = ShowBarberMenuEx |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = flag |Type = int }} {{FunctionArgument |Name = FormList |Type = baseform }} |example = ShowBarberMenuEx 3 MyHairList }} ==Notes== * 0 blacklists beards and hairs. * 1 blacklists beards and whitelists hairs. * 2 whitelists beards and blacklists hairs. * 3 whitelists both hairs and beards. ==See Also== *[[ShowPlasticSurgeonMenu]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Hair Functions]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Overrides the near clip distance for the viewmodel (player's hands) camera, without affecting the clip distance of the world camera. Setting the value to 0 resets the value. |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = distance |Type = float }} |example = SetViewmodelClipDistance 0.00001 }} ==See Also== *[[SetClipDist]] *[[GetViewmodelClipDistance]]
[[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns the value of the near clip distance for the viewmodel (player's hands) camera. Returns 0 if value is default (SetViewmodelClipDistance is inactive). |returnType = float |arguments = |example = let GetViewmodelClipDistance := fClipDistance }} ==See Also== *[[SetClipDist]] *[[SetViewmodelClipDistance]]
[[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns whether or not a radio or jukebox reference is playing. |name = IsRadioRefPlaying |referenceType = reference |example = let bRadioPlaying := MyRadioRef.IsRadioRefPlaying }} ==Notes== *This function is currently broken and will crash the game. [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Radio Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns an [[array_var]] of all radio talking activator references in the base game and installed mods. |returnType = array_var |example = let aRadios := GetAllGameRadios }} [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Radio Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns an [[array_var]] of all talking activator refs available to the specified radio/jukebox reference. |returnType = array_var |referenceType = reference |example = MyRadioRef.GetAvailableRadios }} [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Radio Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Tunes a radio reference to the specified activator baseform. |name = TuneRadioRef |returnType = void |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = activator |Type = baseForm |Optional = y }} |example = MyRadioRef.TuneRadioRef NewVegasRadio }} ==Notes== *This function only works while you're in the same cell. It persists across saves, but as soon as you leave, the change is undone. [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Radio Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''UDF Script''' as a handler that will be invoked on a specified target radio ref after a sound is attached. |name = SetJohnnyOnRadioPostSoundAttachEventHandler |alias = SetOnRadioPostSoundHandler |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flag |Type = int }} |example = SetJohnnyOnRadioPostSoundAttachEventHandler 1 RadioPostSoundAttachUDF 0 }} ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn RadioPostSoundAttachUDF

ref rTarget
int mode

begin Function {rTarget, mode} 

...

end
==Notes== *Flag argument is reserved for future use and should always be 0. [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Radio Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Sets the media location controller on the specified audio marker reference. |name = AudioMarkerSetController |alias = AMKSetC..."
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Sets the media location controller on the specified audio marker reference. |name = AudioMarkerSetController |alias = AMKSetCtrl |referenceType = reference |conditionFunc = Script |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = form |Type = media location controller }} |example = MyAudioMarkerRef.AudioMarkerSetController musCtrlGnrcDeserted }} [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Audio Markers Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns the media location controller on the specified audio marker reference. |name = AudioMarkerGetController |alias = AMKGetCtrl |referenceType = reference |conditionFunc = Both |example = let rMediaLocationController := MyAudioMarkerRef.AudioMarkerGetController }} [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Audio Markers Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Sets the property on the specified audio marker reference. |name = AudioMarkerSetProperty |alias = AMKSetProp |referenceType..."
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Sets the property on the specified audio marker reference. |name = AudioMarkerSetProperty |alias = AMKSetProp |referenceType = reference |conditionFunc = Script |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = int |Type = Integer }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Property |Type = Float }} }} [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Audio Markers Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns the currently active audio marker. |name = AudioMarkerGetCurrent |alias = AMKGetCur |referenceType = reference |con..."
revision.text: {{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns the currently active audio marker. |name = AudioMarkerGetCurrent |alias = AMKGetCur |referenceType = reference |conditionFunc = Both |example = let rMediaLocationController := AudioMarkerGetCurrent }} [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Audio Markers Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns the furniture reference ID currently being used by the specified NPC reference. |name = GetCurrentFurnitureRef |returnVal = [Insert return val name here, or remove this arg] |referenceType = reference |conditionFunc = Script |example = let rCurrentChair = PlayerRef.GetCurrentFurnitureRef }} [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Actor State Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns the time elapsed since the game's last frame. |name = GameGetSecondsPassed |alias = GGetSecPass |returnVal = Seconds |returnType = Float |conditionFunc = Both |example = let fSeconds := GameGetSecondsPassed }} ==See Also== *[[GetSecondsPassed]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Time Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Hides the specified item in the barter menu with optional arguments to determine which side of the menu the item is hidden on and what vendor it applies to. Omitting the optional arguments will cause the item to be hidden entirely from the barter menu. Note that it is recommended to not specify a vendor unless necessary. |name = HideItemBarterEx |conditionFunc = Script |example = HideItemBarterEx WeapNVDriverUnique 1 2 PlayerRef Prevents the player from selling the weapon. |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = item |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Hide |Type = 0/1 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Don't hide on side |Type = 1/2 |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = vendor |Type = reference |Optional = y }} }} ==Notes== * 1 don't hide item on the left (player) * 2 don't hide item on the right (vendor) ==See Also== *[[IsItemBarterHiddenEx]] *[[ToggleItemUnique]] - Similar function for repair lists. [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns the flag value from [[HideItemBarterEx]]. |name = IsItemBarterHiddenEx |returnVal = ishidden |conditionFunc = Script |example = let iItemHidden := IsItemBarterHiddenEx WeapNVDriverUnique PlayerRef |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = form |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = form |Type = reference |Optional = y }} }} ==Notes== * 0 Item is not hidden for either side. * 1 Item is not hidden for player (left). * 2 Item is not hidden for vendor (right). * 3 Item is hidden for player and vendor (both sides) [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns 1 if a specified BSA archive (relative to Data folder) is currently loaded in the game. |returnType = 0/1 |arguments =..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Returns 1 if a specified BSA archive (relative to Data folder) is currently loaded in the game. |returnType = 0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = bsaName |Type = string }} |example = 
IsBSALoaded "Fallout - Misc.bsa"
}} ==See Also== *[[IsModLoaded]] *[[IsDLLLoaded]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Debugging]] [[Category:File Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 5.07 |summary = Plays a sound using calling reference as its source and position, fading it in for a specified number of seconds. |returnVal = isSuccess |returnType = 0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = sound |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = fadeTime |Type = float }} |example = Player.PlaySoundFade NPCMothmanCreepy 2 }} ==Notes== * By default, if calling reference is not provided, Player is used. ==See Also== *[[PlaySound3D]] *[[StopSoundAlt]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Sound Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 5.07 |summary = Returns a Map-type array containing alternate texture forms of a specified Armor or Weapon model as value..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 5.07 |summary = Returns a Map-type array containing alternate texture forms of a specified Armor or Weapon model as values, and their 3D indices as keys. If Armor form is passed, an optional argument should be used to specify model ID, as follows:
1 - Male Biped model
2 - Female Biped model
3 - Male World model
4 - Female World model
|returnType = map |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = armorOrWeapon |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = modelID |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = array_var altTextures = GetAltTexturesEx MS18RangerBattlearmor 1 }} ==See Also== [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Armor Functions]] [[Category:Texture Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 5.07 |summary = Given coordinates within currently loaded Navmesh, returns an array containing coordinates of the center of the Navmesh triangle, and distance to it, as follows: 
Index	Attribute
-----	---------
0	X - float
1	Y - float
2	Z - float
3	Distance - float
Two additional arguments allow skipping disabled navmesh triangles and (optionally) limit the search on Z-axis. |returnType = coords:array_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = posX |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = posY |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = posZ |Type = float |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = skipDisabled |Type = int(0/1) }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = zLimit |Type = float |Optional = y }} |example = let aCoords := GetPointInNavMesh fX fY fZ 1 100 }} ==See Also== *[[GetNearestNavMeshTriangle]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Navmesh Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Renders a texture over the player camera. * The Alpha Texture should be an atlas of all the splatters you would like to render ** White color makes it opaque ** Black makes it solid and visible * The Color Texture should be an atlas of all the colors you would like to to have for the splatters * The Flare Texture should be a single texture to display the lens flare when vision is blocked/blurred by the splatter |returnVal = 0/1 |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = splatterCount |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = splatterDuration |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = sizeMultiplier |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = opacityMultiplier |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = alphaTexture |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = colorTexture |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flareTexture |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = fade |Type = int }} |example = TriggerScreenSplatterEx 12 5 1 1 "Textures\Alpha.dds" "Textures\Color.dds" "Textures\Flare.dds" 0 }} ==See Also== *[[TriggerScreenSplatter]]
[[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:UI Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 5.07 |summary = Given coordinates within currently loaded cell grid, returns an array containing coordinates of the center of the nearest Navmesh triangle, and distance to it, as follows: 
Index	Attribute
-----	---------
0	X - float
1	Y - float
2	Z - float
3	Distance - float
Two additional arguments allow skipping disabled navmesh triangles and (optionally) limit the search on Z-axis. |returnType = coords:array_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = posX |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = posY |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = posZ |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = skipDisabled |Type = int(0/1) }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = zLimit |Type = float |Optional = y }} |example = let aCoords := GetNearestNavMeshTriangle fX fY fZ 1 100 }} ==See Also== *[[GetPointInNavMesh]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Navmesh Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Sets the duration (in seconds) and a multiplier for camera rumble, without rumbling the HUD. |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = shakeMult |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = shakeDuration |Type = float }} |example = SetCameraShakeNoHUDShudder 2 3 }} ==See Also== *[[TriggerHUDShudder]] *[[TriggerHitShader]] *[[SetCameraShake]]
[[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 5.10 |summary = Returns 1 if Actor is currently under a health-damaging effect. |returnType = int:0/1 |referenceType = actor |example = Player.HasHealthDamageEffect }} ==See Also== *[[GetTempEffects]] [[Category:Functions_(JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 5.10 |summary = Turns '''Auto Move''' on or off for the player |returnType = success:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = toggle |Type = int(0/1) }} |example = SetAutoMove 1 1 }} ==See Also== *[[GetAutoMove]] [[Category:Functions_(JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 5.10 |summary = Turns '''Always Run''' on or off for the player, optionally updating the Running movement flag. |name = SetAlwaysRun |returnType = success:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = toggle |Type = int(0/1) }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = updateRunningFlag |Type = int(0/1) |Optional = y }} |example = SetAlwaysRun 1 1 }} ==See Also== *[[GetAlwaysRun]] *[[GetActorMovementFlags]] [[Category:Functions_(JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 5.07 |summary = Returns a two-dimensional '''[[array_var]]''' containing all Ingestible forms currently having an effect on the player, their duration and remaining time. The elements in the array are structured as follows: 
Index	Attribute
-----	---------
0	Ingestible: form
1	Time Left : float
2	Duration : float
|returnType = tempEffects:array_var |example = let aTempEffects := GetTempIngestibleEffects let iIndex := 0 while iIndex < ar_Size aTempEffects let rIngestible := aTempEffects[iIndex][0] let fTimeLeft := aTempEffects[iIndex][1] let fDuration := aTempEffects[iIndex][2] let iIndex += 1 loop }} ==Notes== * As each Ingestible usually contains multiple Effect entries with different duration, the Effect with the longest duration is used to reflect it in the array. ==See Also== *[[GetTempEffects]] [[Category:Functions_(JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Sets the custom map marker at the specified coordinates within the player's current interior cell or worldspace. Functions the s..."
Created page with "{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |summary = Unsets the custom map marker placed in the Pip-Boy Map tab (or using [[SetCustomMapMarker]] function). |returnType = suc..."

revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 5.11 |summary = Sets/removes the specified UDF Script as a handler that will be invoked when the player goes into the sleep/wait cycle. The event takes an integer. 0 for both rest and wait, 1 for wait, and 2 for rest. |name = SetJohnnyOnSleepWaitEventHandler |alias = SetONSleepWEventHandler |conditionFunc = Script |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = setOrRemove |Type = Integer }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Script |Type = OnSleepWaitUDF }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flags |Type = Integer |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = filter |Type = Integer |Optional = y }} |example = 
SetJohnnyOnSleepWaitEventHandler 1 OnSleepWaitUDF 0 1
'''Sets''' the OnSleepWaitUDF script as a handler to be invoked when the player is waiting. 
SetJohnnyOnSleepWaitEventHandler 0 OnSleepWaitUDF 0
'''Removes''' the OnSleepWaitUDF script as a handler of the above. }} ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn OnSleepWaitUDF

int iMode

begin Function {iMode} 

...

end
==Notes== *Flag argument is reserved for future use and should always be 0. [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

[[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Note Functions]]

This is required for it to play, and for the Pip-Boy "Show notes for current quest only" button to work.

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 5.06 |summary = This function sets up an event handler that triggers when keyboard or controller input causes UI navigation changes in menus. Unlike [[SetOnMouseoverChangeEventHandler]] which only works with mouse input, this function responds to keyboard and controller navigation.

[[GetSelectedItemRef]], [[GetRecipeMenuSelection]], or [[GetMenuTargetRef]] can be used to get the currently selected item The delta parameter's exact meaning may depend on the specific menu being monitored. |name = SetJohnnyOnKeyboardControllerSelectionChangeEventHandler |alias = SetOnKBCTrlUIDeltaHandler |conditionFunc = Script |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = setOrRemove |Type = Integer }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = Ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Flag |Type = Integer |Optional = y |Value = 0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = MenuID |Type = Integer |Optional = y |Value = 0 }} |example = 
SetJohnnyOnKeyboardControllerSelectionChangeEventHandler 1 OnKeyboardOrControllerEventUDF 0 1008
'''Sets''' the OnKeyboardOrControllerEventUDF script as a handler to be invoked when '''Container''' menu is open. 
SetOnKBCTrlUIDeltaHandler 0 OnKeyboardOrControllerEventUDF 0 1008
'''Removes''' the OnKeyboardOrControllerEventUDF script as a handler of the above. }} ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn OnKeyboardOrControllerEventUDF

int iDelta ; iDelta: Integer representing the UI change/delta (exact meaning may vary by menu)
           ; The delta parameter's exact meaning may depend on the specific menu being monitored
begin Function {iDelta} 
	ref rItem = GetSelectedItemRef
	(code)
end
==Notes== *Flag argument is reserved for future use and should always be 0. *MenuID can be set to 0 to apply to every menu *The event doesn't apply when switching menu categories, it only fires when scrolling between items in a category. [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 5.17 |summary = Plays a sound file using its path relative to ''New Vegas'' folder. |returnVal = isSuccess |returnType = 0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = path |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = fadeTime |Type = float |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = isVoice |Type = int(0/1) |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = systemSound |Type = int(0/1) |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = shouldLoop |Type = int(0/1) |Optional = y }} |example = PlaySoundFromPath "Data\Sound\fx\YourCustomSound.mp3" 2.5 1 1 }} ==Notes== *Using this function is preferable to using the '''[[PlaySoundFile]]''' function, which has several limitations.
==See Also== *[[StopSoundFromPath]] *[[PlaySound3DFromPath]] *[[StopSound3DFromPath]] *[[IsSoundPlayingFromPath]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Sound Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 5.17 |summary = Stops all instances of a sound from playing, specified by its path relative to ''New Vegas'' folder. |returnVal = isSuccess |returnType = 0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = path |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = fadeTime |Type = float |Optional = y }} |example = StopSoundFromPath "Data\Sound\fx\YourCustomSound.mp3" 2.5 }} ==Notes== *Using this function is preferable to using the '''[[StopSoundFile]]''' function, which has several limitations.
==See Also== *[[PlaySoundFromPath]] *[[PlaySound3DFromPath]] *[[StopSound3DFromPath]] *[[IsSoundPlayingFromPath]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Sound Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 5.17 |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''UDF Script''' as a handler that will be invoked in the event a NPC responds to the player while in the DialogMenu. Can be filtered by a specified '''Emotion ID'''. When invoked, the script is passed with five arguments: the response string, voiceline sound path, emotion id, emotion value, and response number. |name = SetOnNPCResponseEventHandler |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flag |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = emotionID |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = 
SetOnNPCResponseEventHandler 1 OnNPCResponseUDF 0 1
'''Sets''' the OnNPCResponseUDF script as a handler to be invoked whenever the a NPC responds to the player with the '''Anger''' dialogue emotion. 
SetOnNPCResponseEventHandler 0 OnNPCResponseUDF
'''Removes''' the OnNPCResponseUDF script as a handler of the above. }} ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn OnNPCResponseUDF

string_var sResponse
string_var sVoicePath
int iEmotionID
int iEmotionValue
int iResponseNumber

begin Function {sResponse, sVoicePath, iEmotionID, iEmotionValue, iResponseNumber}

	(code)

end
==Expression IDs== 
0:   Neutral
1:   Anger
2:   Disgust
3:   Fear
4:   Sad
5:   Happy
6:   Surprise
7:   Pained
==See Also== [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Event Handlers]] [[Category:Dialogue Functions]]

{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 5.17 |summary = Returns the casino chip form used by the specified casino form. |name = GetCasinoChip |conditionFunc = Script |example = GetCasinoChip GomorrahCasinoData }} ==See Also== *[[SetCasinoChip]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Casino Functions]]

revision.comment: Add SetHUDVisibilityOverride
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 5.20 |summary = Sets specified flags to game HUD Visibility flags allowing to easily hide\show game UI elements. |name = SetHUDVisibilityOverride |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = iVisFlags |Type = int == HUD Flags == 
kActionPoints = 0x1,// BIT - 0
kHitPoints = 0x2,// BIT - 1
kRadiationMeter = 0x4,// BIT - 2
kEnemyHealth = 0x8,// BIT - 3
kQuestReminder = 0x10,// BIT - 4
kRegionLocation = 0x20,// BIT - 5
kReticleCenter = 0x40,// BIT - 6
kSneakMeter = 0x80, // BIT - 7
kMessages = 0x100, // BIT - 8
kInfo = 0x200, // BIT - 9
kSubtibles = 0x400,// BIT - 10
kHotkeys = 0x800,// BIT - 11
kXpMeter = 0x1000,// BIT - 12
kBreathMeter = 0x2000,// BIT - 13
kExplosivePositioning = 0x4000,// BIT - 14
kCrippledLimbIndicator = 0x8000,// BIT - 15
kHardcoreMode = 0x10000,// BIT - 16
}} |example = 
SetHUDVisibilityOverride 0
Hides all UI elements on screen. 
SetHUDVisibilityOverride 131071
Restores all UI elements on screen. 
if GetBit CurrentFlags 9 == 1
  set CurrentFlags to ClearBit CurrentFlags, 9
  SetHUDVisibilityOverride CurrentFlags
endif
Checks if INFO Hud flag is already cleared. If not - clears it thus hiding crosshair prompt. 
if GetBit CurrentFlags 9 == 0
    set CurrentFlags to SetBit CurrentFlags, 9
    SetHUDVisibilityOverride CurrentFlags
endif
Checks if INFO HUD flag is already set. If not - sets it thus showing crosshair prompt. }} == Notes == * Before setting flags you should always check their value with [[GetHUDVisibilityOverride]], then set/clear a bit to retain compatibility with other mods. * Hiding some flags might not disable UI elements, for instance radiation meter will still be visible until you exit\enter any menu after you walked into an irradiated area. ==See Also== *[[GetHUDVisibilityOverride]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

kHitPoints = 0x2,// BIT -1

kSneakMeter = 0x80, // BIT -7
kMessages = 0x100, // BIT - 8

kSubtibles = 0x400,// BIT - 10
kHotkeys = 0x800,// BIT - 11
kXpMeter = 0x1000,// BIT - 12
kBreathMeter = 0x2000,// BIT - 13
kExplosivePositioning = 0x4000,// BIT - 14
kCrippledLimbIndicator = 0x8000,// BIT - 15
kHardcoreMode = 0x10000,// BIT - 16
}} ==See Also== *[[SetHUDVisibilityOverride]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 5.20 |alias = SetJohnnyOnNPCActorValueEventHandler |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''[[UDF|UDF Script]]''' as a h..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 5.20 |alias = SetJohnnyOnNPCActorValueEventHandler |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''[[UDF|UDF Script]]''' as a handler that will be invoked when a specified '''[[Actor Value]]''' (or ''any'' '''Actor Value''', if the optional parameter is omitted) is changed for the specified '''[[Actor]]''' (or ''any'' '''Actor''', if the optional parameter is omitted). The event can be further filtered using an [[Actor_Value_Codes|Actor Value code]]. When invoked, the script receives three arguments: Actor Value code, its previous value and its new value. |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flag |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Actor |Type = filter |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = avCode |Type = filter |Optional = y }} |example = 
SetOnNPCActorValueChangeEventHandler 1 OnHealthChangeUDF 0 SunnySmiles 16
'''Sets''' the OnHealthChangeUDF script as a handler to be invoked when Sunny's Health changes 
SetOnNPCActorValueChangeEventHandler 0 OnHealthChangeUDF 0 SunnySmiles 16
'''Removes''' the OnHealthChangeUDF script as a handler of the above. }} ==Handler Script== A skeleton handler script for this event: 
scn OnHealthChangeUDF

ref actor
int avCode
float previousValue
float newValue

begin Function {actor, avCode, previousValue, newValue} 

	(code)

end
==Notes== *Flag argument is reserved for future use and should always be 0. *This handler will '''not''' trigger for the Player. It can trigger for any other actor. For the Player, use [[SetOnActorValueChangeEventHandler]] *Changes to ''InventoryWeight'' actor value do not dispatch this event, because internally this actor value is only updated when ''GetActorValue'' is called on it. Similarly, the event does not dispatch when derived skills get increased by their associated SPECIAL, although direct increases to skills do dispatch it. ==See Also== [[SetOnActorValueChangeEventHandler]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Event Handlers (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

revision.text:
{{Function |origin = JohnnyGuitar |originVersion = 5.11 |summary = Sets the Players Movement Flag |name = SetPlayerMovementFlags |returnType = Float |conditionFunc = Script |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = int |Type = Integer }} }} ==Actor Movement Flags== 
1	Forward
2	Backward
4	Left
8	Right
16	Turn Left
32	Turn Right
256	Walking
512	Running
1024	Sneaking
2048	Swimming
4096	Jumping
8192	Flying
16384	Falling
32768	Sliding
==See Also== * [[GetActorMovementFlags]] [[Category:Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = kNVSE |summary = Plays the specified anim at the specified priority and weight, with optional parameters. All arguments except the sequence path are optional. This is a low-level animation function that must be used with care. Use [[PlayAnimationPath]], [[ForcePlayIdle]] or [[PlayGroup]] if you don't know what you're doing. This function plays the specified animation sequence path at the specified priority and weight. The animation will overlay any other animations playing on the actor. Use [[DeactivateAnim]], [[CrossFadeAnims]] or [[BlendToAnim]] to deactivate or transition the animation to a different one. * '''bIsFirstperson''' specifies if the animation should be played on the player character's first person or third person skeleton. If the target actor is an NPC it will default to 0. If no value is specified and the reference is the player character it will default to the current point of view of view of the player. * '''priority''' specifies the [[#Priorities|priority]]. The default value is 1. * '''bStartOver''' specifies if the animation should start from it's "start" key or resume from it's last offset. The default value is 1. * '''fWeight''' specifies the final [[#Weights|weight]] of the animation after easing in. Default value is the sequence weight of the NiControllerSequence which can be retrieved with [[GetAnimationAttributes]]. * '''fEaseInTime''' specifies a time period over which the weight will be ramped up from 0.0 to the weight specified by fWeight. This is used for easing in sequences so that they do not pop when activated over other running sequences. The default value is the max of the animation's '''Blend''' and '''BlendIn''' text keys divided by 30. If the animation is missing both of those keys, the default ease in time will be 0. For more info see [[#Easing In and Out|Easing In and Out]]. * '''time sync sequence path''' is an optional sequence with which the time for the activated sequence is [[#Time Synchronization|synchronized]]. This synchronization is done via the morph text keys in each sequence. If at least one pair of identical morph text keys is not found in each sequence, this function will return 0. The path of the sequence must be based from '''Meshes\''', but should not include it in the string for most anim group based animations but should contain the prefix for idle animations. The anim must be [[DeactivateAnim|deactivated]] or otherwise nothing will happen. Animations do not deactivate by themselves after they finish. |name = ActivateAnim |referenceType = actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = anim sequence path |Type = string }} {{FunctionArgument |Name = bIsFirstPerson |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }} {{FunctionArgument |Name = iPriority |Type = integer |Optional = y }} {{FunctionArgument |Name = bStartOver |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }} {{FunctionArgument |Name = fWeight |Type = float |Optional = y }} {{FunctionArgument |Name = fEaseInTime |Type = float |Optional = y }} {{FunctionArgument |Name = time sync sequence path |Type = string |Optional = y }} |example = 
ActivateAnim "Characters\Recoil.kf" 1
}} ==Priorities== Each sequence that is activated is assigned a priority. The priority indicates what precedence the sequence has over other active sequences. The default priority for all sequences is 0. If a sequence is activated with a higher priority, it will take complete precedence over the lower priority sequences once it is at full strength (i.e. not easing in or out). This is useful for activating sequences that control part of a character while a full body sequence plays at a lower priority. For example, a full body walk sequence could be active with a priority of 0 and an arm wave sequence could be activated with a priority of 1. The arm wave sequence would take precedence over the walk sequence for the bones it controls, causing the arm to wave while the remainder of the body continues to walk. ==Weights== Upon activation each sequence is assigned a weight in the range from 0.0 to 1.0. This value defines the maximum weight the sequence will have once it reaches full strength (i.e. not easing in or out). If multiple sequences are active on a given bone, sequence weights are normalized at each priority level before blending with sequences at other priorities. Effectively, this means the weight of a sequence describes how it blends with other sequences at the same priority. For example, "look right" and "look left" sequences can be activated at priority 0 with a weight of 0.5 each to achieve an end result of looking straight ahead. These same sequences can both be weighted 1.0 (or even 0.0) to produce an identical outcome due to the normalization process that occurs at each priority level. ==Easing In and Out== When sequences are activated and deactivated their effective weight values can be eased in and out to achieve smooth transitions in the final result. The durations for this easing are specified in the [[ActivateAnim]] and [[DeactivateAnim]] functions as well as most transition functions. When being eased in, the weight value is ramped up from 0.0 to the weight that was specified in the activation function. Similarly, when being eased out, the weight value is ramped down from the specified weight to 0.0. ==Time Synchronization== With optional time synchronization, the update time for one sequence is determined by another sequence. This results in time compression being performed for the first sequence so that its timing directly corresponds with the second sequence. This allows sequences with different lengths and frequencies to be run simultaneously, with one sequence synchronized to the other. This eliminates potential artifacts of animation blending, such as having foot falls come at different points in each sequence, which is common with walk and run sequences. Any activated sequence can be optionally synchronized to any other sequence. Circular dependencies are detected and rejected by the activation functions. In order for time synchronization to be used between two sequences, the sequences must have been marked with identical pairs of morph text keys. For example, a walk animation and a run animation could each have a morph text key named "morph: knees together" at the point in each animation where the knees are together. Additionally, they could also have an identical pair of morph text keys called "morph: left foot down" at the point in each animation when the left foot is on the ground. All identical pairs of morph text keys that are found in the two sequences are used for synchronization so the animation looks correct even if the two sequences are of different lengths. ==See Also== *[[DeactivateAnim]] *[[CrossFadeAnims]] *[[BlendToAnim]] *[[BlendToAnimFromPose]] *[[IsAnimSequencePlaying]] [[Category:Functions (kNVSE)]] [[Category:Animation Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = kNVSE |summary = Sets the current [[GetAnimAction|Anim Action]] of the calling reference to 0, allowing attack action while another animation action is..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = kNVSE |summary = Sets the current [[GetAnimAction|Anim Action]] of the calling reference to 0, allowing attack action while another animation action is playing. This function can also used to enable new actions even when other animation actions are in progress, such as reloading while attack animation is playing. |name = AllowAttack |referenceType = reference |arguments = |example = 
player.AllowAttack
}} ==See Also== *[[GetAnimAction]] [[Category:Functions (kNVSE)]] [[Category:Animation Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = kNVSE |summary = Performs a blend transition from the specified source animation sequence to the specified offset in the destination animation sequence..."

revision.text:
{{Function |origin = kNVSE |summary = Performs a blend transition from the current scene graph state of the character to the specified frame in the destination sequence. Optional ''fDestFrame'' argument allows specifying the offset of the animation in which it should start. |name = BlendToAnimFromPose |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = sequence path |Type = string }} {{FunctionArgument |Name = bFirstPerson |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }} {{FunctionArgument |Name = fDuration |Type = float |Optional = y }} {{FunctionArgument |Name = fDestFrame |Type = float |Optional = y }} {{FunctionArgument |Name = iPriority |Type = integer |Optional = y }} {{FunctionArgument |Name = time sync sequence path |Type = string |Optional = y }} |example = 
BlendToAnimFromPose "Characters\Animation.kf" 1 0.5
}} ==Blend Transitions== A blend transition moves between static frames in two sequences and is activated with the [[BlendToAnim]] function. The source sequence is eased out while the destination sequence is eased in over the duration of the blend, with each sequence being locked to a particular frame. Blend transitions are good for smoothly transitioning between two sequences with very different styles of movement (e.g. between an idle animation and a walk animation). Note that when accumulating transformations for the character, using a blend transition will cause all forward movement to stop for the duration of the blend, since only a static frame in each sequence is used. An alternate type of blend is one that blends from the current scene graph pose of the character to a specified frame in some destination sequence. This type of blend is activated with the [[BlendToAnimFromPose]] function. This can be useful when the character is in a random position (e.g. after a physics simulation) and a smooth transition is needed back to a sequence. Note, however, that a temporary sequence is created for BlendFromPose that contains current scene graph data for the character, such as bone transforms, so there is some processing and memory overhead associated with this type of blend. Whenever possible, it is recommended that BlendToAnim be used instead. BlendToAnimFromPose should only be used if the character is not currently in a position corresponding to a frame in another sequence. ==See Also== *[[ActivateAnim]] *[[DeactivateAnim]] *[[CrossFadeAnims]] *[[BlendToAnim]] *[[IsAnimSequencePlaying]] *[[SetAnimOffset]] [[Category:Functions (kNVSE)]] [[Category:Animation Functions]]

{{Function |origin = kNVSE |summary = Deactivates the specified animation sequence. This is a low level animation function that must be used on an animation path played with [[ActivateAnim]], [[BlendToAnim]], [[BlendToAnimFromPose]] or [[CrossFadeAnims]]. Do not use it if you don't know what you're doing. Returns 1 if the sequence was successfully deactivated, and 0 otherwise. * Optional argument '''fEaseOutTime''' specifies a time period over which the sequence weight will be ramped down from its set value to 0.0. This is used for easing out the sequence to avoid a pop when other sequences are still running. The default value is the max of the NiControllerSequence text keys '''Blend''' and '''BlendOut''' divided by 30. |name = DeactivateAnim |referenceType = actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = anim sequence path |Type = string }} {{FunctionArgument |Name = fEaseOutTime |Type = float |Optional = y }} |example = 
DeactivateAnim "Characters\Recoil.kf"
}} ==See Also== *[[ActivateAnim]] *[[CrossFadeAnims]] *[[BlendToAnim]] *[[BlendToAnimFromPose]] *[[IsAnimSequencePlaying]] *[[SetAnimOffset]] [[Category:Functions (kNVSE)]] [[Category:Animation Functions]]

{{Function |origin = kNVSE |summary = Fires the currently equipped weapon of the specified actor. |name = ForceFireWeapon |referenceType = actor |arguments = |example = 
player.ForceFireWeapon
}} ==See Also== *[[FireWeapon]] *[[FireWeaponEx]] [[Category:Functions (kNVSE)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Animation Functions]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

{{Function |origin = kNVSE |summary = Plays an idle animation on an actor immediately. This function works consistently in all script types (and not just effect scripts) and in the in-game console, unlike [[PlayIdle]] and [[PlayIdleEx]]. An optional player point of view argument can be passed where 1 represents 3rd person and 2 represents 1st person. |name = ForcePlayIdle |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = idleAnim |Type = ref |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = playerPOV (optional) |Type = 1/2 |Optional = y }} |example = 
actorRef.ForcePlayIdle MTHitHeadA
}} ==See Also== *[[ForceHitStaggerReaction]] *[[ForceRecoilAnim]] *[[ForceStopIdle]] [[Category:Functions (kNVSE)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Animation Functions]]

{{Function |origin = kNVSE |summary = Forces the currently playing idle animation on the calling reference to stop. This function works consistently in all script types (and not just effect scripts) and in the in-game console, unlike [[StopIdle]]. An optional player point of view argument can be passed where 1 represents 3rd person and 2 represents 1st person. |name = ForceStopIdle |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = playerPOV (optional) |Type = 1/2 |Optional = y }} |example = 
actorRef.ForceStopIdle
}} ==See Also== *[[ForcePlayIdle]] [[Category:Functions (kNVSE)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Animation Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = kNVSE |summary = Returns an array of strings containing the paths of all currently active animation sequences on the specified actor. |name = GetActive..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = kNVSE |summary = Returns an array of strings containing the paths of all currently active animation sequences on the specified actor. |name = GetActiveAnims |referenceType = actor |returnType = array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = bIsFirstPerson |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }} |example = 
array_var aAnims = player.GetActiveAnims
}} The GetActiveAnims function returns an array of strings containing the paths of all currently active animation sequences playing on the specified actor. * '''bIsFirstperson''' specifies whether to get animations from the player character's first person or third person skeleton. If the target actor is an NPC it will default to 0. If no value is specified and the reference is the player character it will default to the current point of view of the player. If there are no active animations, an empty array is returned. ==See Also== *[[ActivateAnim]] *[[DeactivateAnim]] *[[PlayAnimationPath]] *[[IsAnimSequencePlaying]] [[Category:Functions (kNVSE)]] [[Category:Animation Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = kNVSE |summary = Retrieves attributes and information for the specified animation sequence as a [[String Map]]. When called on an actor, retrieves additional data such as the state, current offset, calculated time relative to the actor if it has been played on him. |name = GetAnimationAttributes |alias = GetAnimAttrs |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = anim sequence path |Type = string }} |example = 
array_var mAttributes = GetAnimationAttributes "Characters\Dance.kf"
Print mAttributes->sequenceName
|documentation = Retrieves attributes and information for the specified animation sequence. }} ==Attributes== 
- "accumRootName"
- "animGroupId"
- "sequenceType"
- "keyType"
- "animGroupName"
- "sequenceName"
- "seqWeight"
- "cycleType"
- "frequency"
- "beginKeyTime"
- "endKeyTime"
- "textKeyTimes"
- "textKeyValues"
When called on an actor, additionally these attributes are returned: 
- "lastTime"
- "weightedLastTime"
- "lastScaledTime"
- "state"
- "offset"
- "startTime"
- "endTime"
- "calculatedTime"
- "multiplier"
==Enum Values== ===State=== 
kState_Inactive = 0,
kState_Animating = 1,
kState_EaseIn = 2,
kState_EaseOut = 3,
kState_TransSource = 4,
kState_TransDest = 5,
kState_MorphSource = 6
===Sequence Type=== 
kSequence_None = -1,
kSequence_Idle = 0,
kSequence_Movement = 1,
kSequence_LeftArm = 2,
kSequence_LeftHand = 3,
kSequence_Weapon = 4,
kSequence_WeaponUp = 5,
kSequence_WeaponDown = 6,
kSequence_SpecialIdle = 7,
===Cycle Type=== 
kCycle_Loop = 0,
kCycle_Reverse = 1,
kCycle_Clamp = 2,
===Key Type=== 
kAnimKeyType_ClampSequence = 0,
kAnimKeyType_LoopingSequenceOrAim = 1,
kAnimKeyType_SpecialIdle = 2,
kAnimKeyType_Equip = 3,
kAnimKeyType_Unequip = 4,
kAnimKeyType_Attack = 5,
kAnimKeyType_PowerAttackOrPipboy = 6,
kAnimKeyType_AttackThrow = 7,
kAnimKeyType_PlaceMine = 8,
kAnimKeyType_SpinAttack = 9,
kAnimKeyType_LoopingReload = 10,
==See Also== *[[ActivateAnim]] *[[DeactivateAnim]] *[[CrossFadeAnims]] *[[BlendToAnim]] *[[BlendToAnimFromPose]] *[[IsAnimSequencePlaying]] *[[SetAnimOffset]] [[Category:Functions (kNVSE)]] [[Category:Animation Functions]]

{{Function |origin = kNVSE |summary = Returns the animation paths, including variants, that would be played if the specified animation group is played on the actor as an [[Array]]. The array is sorted where animations with higher priority are at the start of the array, but bear in mind kNVSE condition scripts can skip animations with higher priority, instead playing animations with a lower priority. This function also takes into account any [[kNVSE Plugin|kNVSE]] overridden animations. |name = GetAnimationsByAnimGroup |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = anim group id |Type = animation group }} |example = 
player.GetAnimationsByAnimGroup AttackRight
}} ==See Also== *[[GetAnimationAttributes]] *[[ActivateAnim]] *[[DeactivateAnim]] *[[CrossFadeAnims]] *[[BlendToAnim]] *[[BlendToAnimFromPose]] *[[IsAnimSequencePlaying]] *[[SetAnimOffset]] *[[SetAnimationTextKeys]] [[Category:Functions (kNVSE)]] [[Category:Animation Functions]]

{{Function |origin = kNVSE |summary = This function retrieves the current time of a specified animation. It is functionally equivalent to accessing the ''calculatedTime'' field found in the [[GetAnimationAttributes]] function. Alternatively, it can be considered as the sum of the ''offset'' field in the same function and the value obtained from [[GetGlobalAnimTime]]. |name = GetAnimCurrentTime |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = sAnimPath |Type = string }} |example = 
player.GetAnimCurrentTime "Characters\MyAnimation.kf"
|documentation = Retrieves the current time of the specified animation. This function allows you to obtain the current playback time of a specific animation, providing valuable information for animation-related operations. }} ==See Also== *[[SetAnimCurrentTime]] *[[GetAnimationAttributes]] *[[GetGlobalAnimTime]] *[[SetGlobalAnimTime]] [[Category:Functions (kNVSE)]] [[Category:Animation Functions]]

{{Function |origin = kNVSE |summary = Retrieves the time passed value for animations on the actor. This function can be used to obtain the current time passed, which is useful for controlling playback with [[SetGlobalAnimTime]]. |name = GetGlobalAnimTime |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = bFirstPerson |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }} |example = 
player.GetGlobalAnimTime
}} ==See Also== *[[SetGlobalAnimTime]] [[Category:Functions (kNVSE)]] [[Category:Animation Functions]]

{{Function |origin = kNVSE |summary = Retrieves the playing animation attributes or a null array for the specified sequence type on an actor. This function returns a [[String Map]] in the same format as [[GetAnimationAttributes]]. |name = GetPlayingAnimBySequenceType |alias = GetAnimByType |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = sequence type |Type = integer }} |example = 
GetPlayingAnimBySequenceType 1
}} ==Sequence Types== 
kSequence_Idle = 0,
kSequence_Movement = 1,
kSequence_LeftArm = 2,
kSequence_LeftHand = 3,
kSequence_Weapon = 4,
kSequence_WeaponUp = 5,
kSequence_WeaponDown = 6,
kSequence_SpecialIdle = 7
==See Also== *[[GetAnimationAttributes]] *[[GetPlayingAnimSequencePaths]] [[Category:Functions (kNVSE)]] [[Category:Animation Functions]]

{{Function |origin = kNVSE |summary = Retrieves the paths of currently playing animation sequences as an [[Array]]. If no animation is playing for a sequence type of some index, the string in the array will be empty. |name = GetPlayingAnimSequencePaths |alias = GetAnimsByType |referenceType = reference |arguments = |example = 
player.GetPlayingAnimSequencePaths
}} ==Sequence Types== 
kSequence_Idle = 0,
kSequence_Movement = 1,
kSequence_LeftArm = 2,
kSequence_LeftHand = 3,
kSequence_Weapon = 4,
kSequence_WeaponUp = 5,
kSequence_WeaponDown = 6,
kSequence_SpecialIdle = 7
==See Also== *[[GetAnimationAttributes]] *[[GetAnimationsByAnimGroup]] *[[GetPlayingAnimBySequenceType]] [[Category:Functions (kNVSE)]] [[Category:Animation Functions]]

{{Function |origin = kNVSE |originVersion = 24 |summary = Returns 1 if the specified [[AnimGroups|Animation Group]] is playing on the specified actor. This function will actually check if the anim group is playing unlike [[IsAnimPlaying]] which just checks if there is any anim playing at the sequence type of the anim group. |name = IsAnimPlayingAlt |returnType = 1/0 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = anim group |Type = AnimationGroup }} }} [[Category:Functions (kNVSE)]] [[Category:Animation Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = kNVSE |summary = Returns whether an animation with the specified path is playing on the specified actor. Shorthand for calling [[GetAnimationAttributes]..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = kNVSE |summary = Returns whether an animation with the specified path is playing on the specified actor. Shorthand for calling [[GetAnimationAttributes]] on an actor for a path and checking if the state is not 0. |name = IsAnimSequencePlaying |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = path |Type = string }} |example = 
IsAnimSequencePlaying "Characters\MyAnimation.kf"
}} ==See Also== *[[ActivateAnim]] *[[DeactivateAnim]] *[[CrossFadeAnims]] *[[BlendToAnim]] *[[BlendToAnimFromPose]] *[[SetAnimOffset]] [[Category:Functions (kNVSE)]] [[Category:Animation Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = kNVSE |summary = Plays the specified animation sequence path. The animation will automatically deactivate when it reaches the end key, unlike ActivateAn..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = kNVSE |summary = Plays the specified animation sequence path. The animation will automatically deactivate when it reaches the end key, unlike ActivateAnim which requires manual deactivation. This function is safer to use than [[ActivateAnim]] for most animation needs. PlayAnimationPath is a good option for activating [[SetAnimAdditive|additive animations]]. |name = PlayAnimationPath |referenceType = actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = anim sequence path |Type = string }} {{FunctionArgument |Name = bIsFirstPerson |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }} |example = 
PlayAnimationPath "Characters\Recoil.kf" 1
}} The PlayAnimationPath function plays the specified animation sequence path. Unlike [[ActivateAnim]], this function is designed to automatically deactivate the animation after it reaches its end key, making it safer and more convenient for most animation needs. * '''anim sequence path''' is the path to the animation sequence. The path must be based from '''Meshes\''', but should not include it in the string for most anim group based animations but should contain the prefix for idle animations. * '''bIsFirstperson''' specifies if the animation should be played on the player character's first person or third person skeleton. If the target actor is an NPC it will default to 0. If no value is specified and the reference is the player character it will default to the current point of view of the player. The animation will play on top of all other animations that are currently playing. Which nodes are affected are determined by each interpolator's priority within the animation. Although the animation will automatically deactivate when it reaches its end key, you can still manually deactivate it using [[DeactivateAnim]] if needed. ==Behavior== * The animation will automatically deactivate after reaching its end key (non-looping animations only) * The function will return 1 if the animation was successfully activated, 0 otherwise * If the animation is already playing, it will be deactivated first and then reactivated * The animation uses its default sequence weight and ease-in time values ==When to Use PlayAnimationPath vs. ActivateAnim== PlayAnimationPath is generally safer to use than ActivateAnim for most animation needs because: * It automatically deactivates when the animation completes * It has fewer parameters to manage * It handles common animation tasks with sensible defaults ActivateAnim should be used when you need more precise control over: * Animation priorities * Weight values * Ease-in timing * Time synchronization with other animations * Looping animations that need to persist ==See Also== *[[ActivateAnim]] *[[SetAnimAdditive]] *[[DeactivateAnim]] *[[IsAnimSequencePlaying]] [[Category:Functions (kNVSE)]] [[Category:Animation Functions]]

{{Function |origin = kNVSE |summary = Sets an additive animation for an actor, providing a reference pose animation and an optional time point for blending. This function is used to apply a secondary animation on top of an actor's base animation. Returns 1 if the additive animation is successfully applied, and 0 otherwise. * The argument '''first person''' controls whether the animation should apply to the first-person model (if applicable). If not specified and the actor is the player, the player's current perspective is used. * The argument '''reference pose anim path''' allows specifying a reference pose animation to blend from. If not provided, a default idle animation will be used. * '''reference pose time point''' specifies a time point within the reference pose animation to blend from, allowing fine control over the blending process. Defaults to 0.0 if not specified. To play the anim use [[ActivateAnim]] or any other of kNVSE's low level animation functions. |name = SetAnimAdditive |referenceType = actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = additive anim path |Type = string }} {{FunctionArgument |Name = first person |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }} {{FunctionArgument |Name = reference pose anim path |Type = string |Optional = y }} {{FunctionArgument |Name = reference pose time point |Type = float |Optional = y }} |example = 
Player.SetAnimAdditive "Characters\HitReact.kf" 1 "Characters\IdlePose.kf" 0.5
}} ==See Also== *[[SetAnimWeight]] *[[ActivateAnim]] *[[DeactivateAnim]] *[[CrossFadeAnims]] *[[BlendToAnimFromPose]] *[[IsAnimSequencePlaying]] [[Category:Functions (kNVSE)]] [[Category:Animation Functions]]

{{Function |origin = kNVSE |summary = Sets an current time of the specified animation sequence. The current time allows you to control the time for the animation, effectively shifting it forward or backward in time. |name = SetAnimCurrentTime |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = anim sequence path |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = fCurrentTime |Type = float }} ==Example== ===Example 1: Freezing Animation Frame=== 
player.SetAnimCurrentTime "Characters\Dance.kf" 1.1
This command will freeze the frame at 1.1 seconds into the 'Dance.kf' animation. ===Example 2: Playing Animation Backwards=== 
string_var sAnim = "Characters\MyAnim.kf"
player.SetAnimCurrentTime (sAnim) (player.GetAnimCurrentTime - GetSecondsPassed * 2)
In this script, [[GetSecondsPassed]] is multiplied by 2. This adjustment is necessary because the game engine will add the time delta to the offset each frame. Without this multiplication, the animation would remain static. ===Important Note:=== For both examples to work effectively, they must be invoked within the [[SetGameMainLoopCallback]] function and with the [[GameMode]] modeFlag active. }} ==See Also== *[[GetAnimCurrentTime]] *[[ActivateAnim]] *[[DeactivateAnim]] *[[CrossFadeAnims]] *[[BlendToAnim]] *[[BlendToAnimFromPose]] *[[IsAnimSequencePlaying]] *[[GetAnimationAttributes]] [[Category:Functions (kNVSE)]] [[Category:Animation Functions]]

{{Function |origin = kNVSE |summary = Sets text keys for the specified animation sequence. Text keys allow you to define specific points in the animation timeline and associate text values with those points. These text values can be used for synchronization, labeling, or other purposes. Requires a reference if called after the game is loaded for the first time. If called from the main menu before the game is loaded, all actors using this animation will inherit the text keys from the animation. This function does nothing if the actor is currently playing the animation. To get the current text keys and values, use [[GetAnimationAttributes]]. |name = SetAnimTextKeys |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = anim path |Type = string }} {{FunctionArgument |Name = text key times |Type = array }} {{FunctionArgument |Name = text key values |Type = array }} |example = 
player.SetAnimTextKeys "Characters\MyAnimation.kf" (Ar_List 0.0, 1.0) (Ar_List "Start", "End")
}} ==See Also== *[[ActivateAnim]] *[[DeactivateAnim]] *[[CrossFadeAnims]] *[[BlendToAnim]] *[[BlendToAnimFromPose]] *[[IsAnimSequencePlaying]] *[[GetAnimationAttributes]] *[[SetAnimOffset]] [[Category:Functions (kNVSE)]] [[Category:Animation Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = kNVSE |summary = Sets the [[ActivateAnim#Weights|weight]] of the specified animation sequence. The weight affects the influence or contribution of the a..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = kNVSE |summary = Sets the [[ActivateAnim#Weights|weight]] of the specified animation sequence. The weight affects the influence or contribution of the animation sequence to the overall animation blend. A higher weight value means the animation has a stronger influence on the character's animation. |name = SetAnimWeight |referenceType = actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = anim sequence path |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = weight |Type = float }} |example = 
player.SetAnimWeight "Characters\MyAnimation.kf" 0.75
}} ==See Also== *[[ActivateAnim]] *[[DeactivateAnim]] *[[CrossFadeAnims]] *[[BlendToAnim]] *[[BlendToAnimFromPose]] *[[GetAnimationAttributes]] *[[SetAnimOffset]] *[[GetPlayingAnimSequencePaths]] [[Category:Functions (kNVSE)]] [[Category:Animation Functions]]

{{Function |origin = kNVSE |summary = Sets the time passed value on the actor, which affects all animations they are currently playing. This function provides a way to control and manipulate the playback of animations. You can use it to pause, reverse, or alter the speed of animations, allowing for dynamic animation control to alter the playback of animations. |name = SetGlobalAnimTime |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = timePassed |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = bFirstPerson |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }} |example = 
player.SetGlobalAnimTime ((player.GetGlobalAnimTime) - GetSecondsPassed)
Freeze frames the player in a fixed position when called from [[SetGameMainLoopCallback]] with [[GameMode]] as modeFlag. }} ==See Also== *[[GetGlobalAnimTime]] *[[SetAnimCurrentTime]] *[[SetGameMainLoopCallback]] [[Category:Functions (kNVSE)]] [[Category:Animation Functions]]

{{Function |origin = kNVSE |summary = Replaces an [[AnimGroups|animation]] for a specified [[weapon]] [[base form]]. The anim group that is overridden is specified in the KF file of the given path. |name = SetWeaponAnimationPath |returnType = success:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = baseForm }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = firstPerson |Type = 0/1 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = enable |Type = 0/1 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = path |Type = string }} |example = SetWeaponAnimationPath WeapNV9mmPistol 0 1 "characters\_male\TestAnims\1hpAttack7.kf" }}
==Notes== *This function stacks so if two mod override the same anim group on the same weapon, the previous override will be restored once the other mod disables it's animation override.
==See Also== *[[SetActorAnimationPath]]
[[Category:Functions_(kNVSE)]] [[Category:Animation Functions]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the localization appropriate string representing the actor value. Useful for supporting multiple languages. |name = ActorValueToString |alias = AVString |referenceType = |returnType = string |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ActorValue |Type = string }} }} ==Example== 
string_var my_string
let my_string := ActorValueToString "Strength"
==See Also== *[[ActorValueToStringC]] (for AV int codes) *[[String Variable]] *[[Let]] *[[GetActorValue]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the localization appropriate string representing the actor value, which is specified by its [[Actor Value Codes|int code.]] Useful for supporting multiple languages. The 2nd argument '''typeFilter''' is only available if using [[:Category:Functions_(JIP)|JIP LN]], and may be used to control the type of string, defined in the corresponding AVInfo entry, that is returned: *0: Default name *1: Full name (localized) *2: Abbreviated (localized) |name = ActorValueToStringC |alias = AVStringC |referenceType = |returnType = string |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = AVCode |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = typeFilter |Type = int |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== 
string_var my_string
let my_string := ActorValueToStringC 5
''my_string'' now contains "Strength" 
let my_string := ActorValueToStringC 5 2
''my_string'' now contains "STR" (in the English version) ==See Also== *[[ActorValueToString]] (for AV string) *[[StringToActorValue]] *[[String Variable]] *[[Let]] *[[GetActorValue]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Similar to [[AddItemHealthPercent]], but allows ownership information to be specified.
Does nothing if the item cannot be given health (like Caps). Use [[AddItemOwnership]] for that instead. |name = AddItemHealthPercentOwner |referenceType = Actor/Container |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Item |Type = ObjectID }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Count |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = HealthPercent |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Owner |Type = NPC/Faction }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Rank |Type = int }} |example = SomeChest.AddItemHealthPercentOwner WeapHuntingRifle, 1, 0.35, BoomerFaction, 1 }} ==See Also== *[[AddItemHealthPercent]] *[[AddItemOwnership]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Object Functions]] [[Category:Ownership Functions]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Similar to [[AddItem]], but allows ownership information to be specified. |name = AddItemOwnership |referenceType = Actor/Container |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Item |Type = ObjectID }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Count |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Owner |Type = NPC/Faction }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Rank |Type = int }} |example = SomeChest.AddItemOwnership WeapHuntingRifle, 1, BoomerFaction, 1 }} ==See Also== *[[AddItem]] *[[AddItemHealthPercentOwner]] *[[IsOwned]] *[[IsOwner]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Object Functions]] [[Category:Ownership Functions]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Appends to the end of an existing regular array (list) a new element. The regular array must have been initialized prior, for example by using [[Ar_Construct]] or [[Ar_List]]. This function can not be used to add elements to map or stringmap arrays. |name = Ar_Append |returnType = None |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = list |Type = array}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = element |Type = multi}} }} ==Example== 
array_var MyList

let MyList := Ar_Construct "array"

Ar_Append MyList, SunnyREF
Ar_Append MyList, 1.5

; * MyList now contains: SunnyREF, 1.5
==See Also== *[[Array Variables]] *[[Ar_Insert]] *[[Ar_Cat]] can be used to append all elements from one array to another. *[[Ar_Resize]] *[[Ar_Construct]] *[[Ar_List]] *[[Let]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the constant -99999, which represents an invalid numeric array index. It can be used to compare the results of other array functions. |name = Ar_BadNumericIndex |returnType = -99999:int |arguments = }} ==Example== 
array_var aBeatles

let aBeatles := Ar_List JohnREF, PaulREF, GeorgeREF, RingoREF ; * example context

if eval (Ar_Find SunnyREF, aBeatles) == Ar_BadNumericIndex
    ; * Sunny Smiles is not a member of the Beatles array

endif
==See Also== *[[Array Variables]] *[[Let]] *[[Eval]] *[[Ar_List]] *[[Ar_Find]] *[[Ar_BadStringIndex]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns a constant (an empty string) which represents an invalid index for string indexed arrays (string maps). It can be used to compare the results of other array functions. |name = Ar_BadStringIndex |returnType = "":str |arguments = }} ==Example== 
if eval (Ar_Find SomeValue, MyStringMap) == Ar_BadStringIndex
    ; * SomeValue is not an element found in MyStringMap
endif
==See Also== *[[Ar_BadNumericIndex]] (equivalent for numeric indexed arrays) *[[Ar_Find]] *[[Ar_Map]] *[[Ar_Construct]] *[[Array Variables]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns an empty array of the specified type. To assign the function return to a variable, the [[let]] command must be used, rather than 'set'. Type is specified as one of three strings; 'array', 'map' or 'stringmap'. This type can later be checked using [[TypeOf]]. |name = Ar_Construct |returnType = Array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = type |Type = string}} }} ==Example== 
array_var MyList
array_var MyMap
array_var MyStringMap

let MyList := Ar_Construct "array"
let MyMap := Ar_Construct "map"
let MyStringMap := Ar_Construct "stringmap"
==Notes== * If the array is created from Script Runner, you should call [[Ar_Null]] after you done using the array. ==See Also== *[[Array Variable]] *[[Let]] *[[Ar_List]] *[[Ar_Map]] *[[Ar_Range]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns an identical copy of the specified [[array]]. If the specified array contains elements that are themselves arrays, the returned copy will contain references to the same sub arrays, not duplicates. (Meaning, changes to one sub array will affect both). If you need to duplicate sub arrays too, use [[Ar_DeepCopy]]. |name = Ar_Copy |returnType = array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Source |Type = array}}}} ==Example== 
array_var aOriginal
array_var aClone

let aClone := Ar_Copy aOriginal
==See Also== *[[Ar_DeepCopy]] *[[Ar_Cat]] *[[Array Variables]] *[[Let]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sorts the elements of an array based on the return of a specified [[Lambda|lambda]] or [[User Defined Function]]. Ar_CustomSort will repeatedly take 2 elements from the array to sort, and pass them to the UDF as parameters in the form of two single element arrays. Your UDF determines how the values of the 2 array var parameters should be sorted in relation to each other. This is useful if you wish to sort forms according to anything other than their name/formID (eg weight, size, skills etc), or sub-arrays. You let the function know that one element should be considered 'earlier than' or 'less than' in the resulting array's order by letting the UDF return 'true', ie: [[SetFunctionValue]]. You can define the sorting method any way you like, but it must be definitive, otherwise an infinite loop (CTD) may result. |name = Ar_CustomSort |returnType = array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Source |Type = array}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = ComparisonUDF |Type = script}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = ReverseSort |Type = bool}}}} ==Example (lambda)== I want to sort a bunch of Goodsprings residents according to their medicine skill, with those with higher skill earlier in the array, ie 'less than'. Utilizing [[Lambda|lambdas]] this becomes trivial: 
scn MyCallingScript
    
array_var array1
array_var array2
array_var a
array_var b
let array1 := Ar_List TrudyRef, SunnyRef, DocMitchellRef, EasyPeteRef, GSJoeCobbRef, GSChetRef

let array2 := ar_CustomSort array1, ({a, b} => (*a).GetAV Medicine < (*b).GetAV Medicine)
Now array2 is a regular array custom sorted, which in our case means in descending order of medicine skill: 
    0    DocMitchellRef    ; Doc has 33 medicine
    1    GSChetRef         ; Chet has 15
    2    TrudyRef          ; These three all have 12
    3    SunnyRef
    4    EasyPeteRef
    5    GSJoeCobbRef      ; Joe Cobb has 10
If the Ar_CustomSort function was called with the optional ReverseSort boolean, it'll sort the array in reverse order, which in our case means in ascending order of medicine skill (due to implementation of the UDF): 
    0    GSJoeCobbRef
    1    EasyPeteRef
    2    SunnyRef
    3    TrudyRef
    4    GSChetRef
    5    DocMitchellRef
==Example (UDF Script)== Before the introduction of lambdas you needed to define a new [[User Defined Function|UDF]] script for sorting. The comparison UDF: 
scn MyComparisonUDF
    
array_var a        ; parameters
array_var b

ref rA            ; local ref vars
ref rB

Begin Function {a, b}
    let rA := a[0]
    let rB := b[0]
    if rA.GetAV Medicine > rB.GetAV Medicine
        SetFunctionValue 1    
        ; * SetFunctionValue non-zero (true) marks the comparison as returning:
        ; * element value a < element value b
    endif
End
Usage of Ar_CustomSort in another script: 
scn MyCallingScript
    
array_var array1
array_var array2
    
let array1 := Ar_List TrudyRef, SunnyRef, DocMitchellRef, EasyPeteRef, GSJoeCobbRef, GSChetRef

let array2 := ar_CustomSort array1, MyComparisonUDF
==See Also== *[[Array Variable]] *[[User Defined Function]] *[[Ar_SortAlpha]] *[[Ar_Sort]] *[[Tutorial: Array Variables 1]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:User Defined Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns an identical copy of the specified array. Unlike [[Ar_Copy]], if the specified array contains nested arrays then these are also copied (not just referenced) as well, recursively. |name = Ar_DeepCopy |returnType = array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Source |Type = array}}}} ==Example== 
array_var aOriginal
array_var aClone

let aClone := Ar_DeepCopy aOriginal
==See Also== *[[Ar_Copy]] *[[Array Variable]] *[[Let]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

revision.comment: Changing category to Array Functions
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Identically to [[Ar_Dump]], prints the contents of an array to the console in a human readable format.
