However, the array is specified by its int ID instead of variable name. Intended usage is in-game debugging via the console. You can find out the ID number for all arrays attached to a quest in game via the console command, [[ShowQuestVars]]. |name = Ar_DumpID |returnType = None |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ArrayID |Type = int}}}} ==Example== 
Ar_DumpID 1
==See Also== *[[Ar_Dump]] *[[SCOF]] *[[Array Variables]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:Debugging]]

revision.comment: Add "optional" to ToErase arg
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Erases the entry at the specified index, if it exists, from the specified array. The index may be specified using range notation to delete all elements with indices within the range, inclusively. Alternatively, the index argument may be omitted to erase all entries in an array. Returns the count of entries erased. The array may be of any type, but the index must be appropriate to it (int or string). Arrays of type "array" ([[Ar_List]]) are always indexed consecutively, so any entries above the erased will have their keys shifted down. |name = Ar_Erase |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Parent |Type = array }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = ToErase |Type = index-or-range |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== 
Ar_Erase SomeArray, SomeKey ; erases the entry at SomeKey from SomeArray
==Additional Context== 
array_var aBeatles

let aBeatles := Ar_List JohnREF, PaulREF, GeorgeREF, RingoREF

Ar_Erase aBeatles, 4 ; does nothing because 4 is not a valid index

Ar_Erase aBeatles, 1:2 ; erases entries from 1 to 2

; aBeatles now contains: JohnREF, RingoREF

Ar_Erase aBeatles, 0 ; erases entry at key 0

; aBeatles now only contains: RingoREF
==Notes== * Any kind of forwards loop through an array, may or stringmap can encounter issues if you erase the element currently under iteration inside the loop. This includes any [[ForEach | ForEach]] loop and forwards [[While | While]] loops, eg "while iSize > (iKey += 1)".For regular arrays, the best method is to do a backwards while loop, eg "While -1 < (iKey -= 1)". For maps and stringmaps, you can either use [[Ar_Append | Ar_Append]] to add the keys that need erased to a separate array that you loop through afterwards, or merely exclude them from being added to a new map or stringmap that you intend to replace the old one with. *It doesn't appear necesssary to check if the key you pass actually exists in the array, although it should be of the expected type. This means that when an [[Ar_Find]] call returns a bad numeric or bad string index, passing it as a parameter to Ar_Erase will not cause an error. So you don't have to check if it is valid before calling Ar_Erase. ==See Also== *[[Array Variables]] *[[Ar_Resize]] *[[Let]] *[[While]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the key of the first occurrence of a value in an array, or optionally within a sub range of the array. If the value searched for is not found, it returns the constant [[Ar_BadNumericIndex]] (-99999.0) for numeric indexed arrays, or the constant [[Ar_BadStringIndex]] (empty string) for those that are string indexed. |name = Ar_Find |returnType = int-OR-string |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Value |Type = multi}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = ToSearch |Type = array}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Subrange |Type = range}}}} ==Example== A numeric indexed array ("array" or "map"): 
if eval (Ar_Find SomeValue, SomeArray) != Ar_BadNumericIndex
    ; then SomeValue is an element in SomeArray
endif
A string indexed array ("stringmap"): 
if eval (Ar_Find SomeValue, MyStringMap) != Ar_BadStringIndex
    ; then SomeValue is an element in MyStringMap
endif
Another numeric example, with extra context: 
array_var aBeatles

let aBeatles := Ar_List JohnREF, PaulREF, GeorgeREF, RingoREF ; * example context

if eval (Ar_Find RingoREF, aBeatles) != Ar_BadNumericIndex
    ; * Ringo is found somewhere in the array aBeatles

elseif eval (Ar_Find GeorgeREF, aBeatles, 1:2) != Ar_BadNumericIndex
    ; * George is found within the subrange (second to third elements) of array

endif
==See Also== *[[Ar_HasKey]] to check if a key is in an array, instead of a value. *[[Array Variables]] *[[Let]] *[[Eval]] *[[Ar_List]] *[[Ar_BadNumericIndex]] *[[Ar_BadStringIndex]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the first key of an array. For regular arrays (list arrays) this is just equivalent to SomeRegArray[0]. This function is intended for use with map and stringmap arrays, where the first key is dynamic. Note that map and stringmap arrays are always sorted in ascending numeric or alphanumeric order, regardless of the order of element insertion. |name = Ar_First |returnType = key:int-or-float-or-string |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Source |Type = array}}}} ==Example== 
array_var MyMap
int iKey ; * or float/string_var as appropriate for array

let iKey := Ar_First MyMap

if eval MyMap[iKey] == SunnyREF
    ; * SunnyREF is the value corresponding to first key in MyMap
endif
==See Also== *[[Ar_Last]] *[[Ar_Next]] *[[Ar_Prev]] *[[Array Variable]] *[[Ar_Map]] *[[Let]] *[[Eval]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns True (1) if the specified array contains the specified key, or otherwise False (0). |name = Ar_HasKey |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ToCheck |Type = array}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Key |Type = arraykey}}}} ==Example== 
if eval Ar_HasKey SomeArray, SomeKey
   ; SomeArray has SomeKey
endif
==Examples With Extraneous Context== 
array_var aMagicNumberToString
string_var slot

let aMagicNumberToString := Ar_Map 2::"Upper Body", 5::"Weapon"

if eval Ar_HasKey aMagicNumberToString, 2
    ; True, the array has the key: 2
    let slot := aMagicNumberToString[2] ; I know this is safe
endif

if eval Ar_HasKey aMagicNumberToString, 5
    ; True, the array has the key: 5
endif

if eval Ar_HasKey aMagicNumberToString, 13
    ; False, the array has no key: 13 (or any except 2 and 5 in this case)
endif
Exactly the same with a "stringmap" 
array_var aStringToMagicNumber
int iMN

let aStringToMagicNumber := Ar_Map "Upper Body"::2, "Weapon"::5

if eval Ar_HasKey aStringToMagicNumber, "Upper Body"
    ; True, the array has the key: "Upper Body"
    let iMN := aStringToMagicNumber["Upper Body"] ; I know this is safe
endif

if eval Ar_HasKey aStringToMagicNumber, "Weapon"
    ; True, the array has the key: "Weapon"
endif

if eval Ar_HasKey aStringToMagicNumber, "Another"
    ; False, the array has no key: "Another" (or any except previous two)
endif
==Notes== *It will work with any type of array, although it is most appropriate for "map" and "stringmap" types, since with the regular "array" type ([[Ar_List]]) the keys are always consecutive from 0, so you could simply use [[Ar_Size]] instead. ==See Also== *[[Array Variables]] *[[Ar_Map]] *[[Ar_Size]] *[[Ar_Construct]] *[[Ar_BadNumericIndex]] *[[Ar_BadStringIndex]] *[[Let]] *[[Eval]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Inserts a single element into an Array-type array (list) at the specified index, provided the index is not greater than the current size of the array. Elements at and above the index are shifted up by one index. Usable only with Array-type arrays. Note that this modifies an array in place- it returns a boolean, not a new array. |name = Ar_Insert |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Array |Type = array}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Index |Type = int}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Element |Type = multi}} }} ==Example== 
array_var MyList

let MyList := Ar_List SunnyREF, CheyenneREF

Ar_Insert MyList, 1, EasyPeteREF

; * MyList now contains: SunnyREF, EasyPeteREF, CheyenneREF
==See Also== *[[Ar_Append]] *[[Ar_InsertRange]] *[[Ar_Construct]] *[[Ar_List]] *[[Let]] *[[Array Variables]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Similar to [[Ar_Insert]], but allows for multiple new elements to be inserted at once. Inserts a range of elements into an Array-type array (list) at the specified index, provided the index is not greater than the current size of the array. Elements above the insertion index are shifted up by the number of elements inserted. The range is itself passed as an Array, and each element of the range is inserted into the target Array in order. Usable only with Array-type (regular/list) arrays. Note that this function modifies an array in place and returns a boolean, not a new array. |name = Ar_InsertRange |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Array |Type = array}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Index |Type = int}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = ToInsert |Type = array}} }} ==Example== 
array_var MyList
array_var MyInsertion

let MyList := Ar_List SunnyREF, CheyenneREF

let MyInsertion := Ar_List Beer, Jet, Knife

Ar_InsertRange MyList, 1, MyInsertion

; * MyList now contains: SunnyREF, Beer, Jet, Knife, CheyenneREF
==See Also== *[[Ar_Insert]] (for single elements) *[[Ar_Append]] *[[Ar_Construct]] *[[Ar_List]] *[[Let]] *[[Array Variables]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns a regular array (list array) containing all of the keys found in the specified array. This may be useful for map and stringmap arrays. |name = Ar_Keys |returnType = array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Source |Type = array}}}} ==Example== 
array_var TypeCodes
array_var MyKeys

let TypeCodes := Ar_Map "Armor"::24, "Weapon"::40, "Ammo"::41, "Alchemy"::47

let MyKeys := Ar_Keys TypeCodes

; MyKeys is a regular array containing: "Alchemy", "Ammo", "Armor", "Weapon"
; Note: map keys are always sorted in ascending order
==See Also== *[[Array Variable]] *[[Ar_HasKey]] *[[Ar_Find]] *[[Ar_Map]] *[[Ar_Range]] *[[Let]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the last key of an array. For regular arrays (list arrays) this is equivalent to (Ar_Size RegArray - 1). This function is most useful for map and stringmap arrays, where the last key is dynamic. Note that map and stringmap arrays are always sorted in ascending numeric or alphanumeric order, regardless of the order of element insertion. |name = Ar_Last |returnType = key:int-or-float-or-string |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Source |Type = array}}}} ==Example== 
array_var MyMap
int iKey ; * or float/string_var as appropriate for array

let iKey := Ar_Last MyMap

if eval MyMap[iKey] == SunnyREF
    ; * SunnyREF is the value corresponding to the last key in MyMap
endif
==See Also== *[[Array Variable]] *[[Ar_First]] *[[Ar_Next]] *[[Ar_Prev]] *[[Ar_Size]] *[[Ar_Map]] *[[Let]] *[[Eval]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns a list array variable with the specified elements as its entries in the same order. Up to 20 elements may specified during creation, although more can be added using alternative functions on subsequent lines. Array elements may be of any type, and in any combination. To assign the function return to a variable, the [[let]] command must be used, rather than 'set'. |name = Ar_List |returnType = Array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = entry0 |Type = multi}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = entry1 |Type = multi}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = ...entry19 |Type = multi }} }} ==Example== 
array_var aBeatles

let aBeatles := Ar_List JohnREF, PaulREF, GeorgeREF, RingoREF
==See Also== *[[Array Variables]] *[[Let]] *[[Foreach]] *[[Ar_Construct]] *[[Ar_Map]] *[[Ar_Range]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

revision.comment: TypeOf shill
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns a "map" or "stringmap" array variable with the specified key::element pairs as its entries. Up to 20 key::element pairs may be specified during creation, although more can be added using [[Let]] or alternative functions on subsequent lines. All keys must be of the same type, either numeric (ints and floats can be mixed) or string, which will cause the function to return an array of the type "map" or "stringmap", respectively. The array's type can be checked using [[TypeOf]]. Array elements may be of any type in any combination. To assign the function return to a variable, the Let .. := .. command must be used, rather than 'set .. to ..'. |name = Ar_Map |returnType = array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Key(int/float/string) |Type = :Value(multi) }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = ...Key(int/float/string) |Type = :Value(multi) |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== 
array_var aOutfit
ref rItem

let aOutfit := Ar_Map 2::ArmorLeather, 10::CowboyHat05, 5::WeapNV357Revolver

; I've created a "map" with those 3 key::element entries

let rItem := aOutfit[10] ; this ref now points to CowboyHat05
Identical style for a "stringmap": 
array_var aContacts
ref rActor

let aContacts := Ar_Map "Nemesis"::EasyPeteREF, "Saviour"::DocMitchellREF, "Bestie"::SunnyREF

; I've created a "stringmap" with those 3 key::element entries

let rActor := aContacts["Nemesis"] ; rActor is my nemesis, EasyPeteREF
The next example illustrates a possible mistake- it is incorrect and will fail: 
array_var aPartners

let aPartners := Ar_Map SunnyREF::EasyPeteREF, VeronicaREF::CraigBooneREF

; FAILURE- you can not use a ref as a key, it must be either a string or number. 
; Side note: NVSE4 adds ToNumber and ToString to convert types

let aPartners := Ar_Map "key"::SunnyREF, 45::VeronicaREF

; FAILURE- all keys must be the same type, either strings or numbers, not a combination of both
==See Also== *[[Array Variables]] *[[Ar_List]] *[[Ar_Construct]] *[[Let]] *[[Foreach]] *[[ToString]] *[[ToNumber]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the next key of an array, after the one specified. For regular arrays (list arrays) this is just equivalent to (SpecifiedKey + 1). This function is most useful for map and stringmap arrays, where keys are dynamic. Note that map and stringmap arrays are always sorted in ascending numeric or alphanumeric order, regardless of the order elements were inserted. Returns a bad index ([[Ar_BadNumericIndex]] or [[Ar_BadStringIndex]]) if there is no next key in the array. |name = Ar_Next |returnType = key:int/float/string |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Source |Type = array}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Key |Type = int/float/string}}}} ==Example== 
array_var MyMap
float MapKey ; * or string_var as appropriate for array

let MapKey := Ar_First MyMap ; * The First Key

let MapKey := Ar_Next MyMap, MapKey ; * The Next Key

if MapKey == Ar_BadNumericIndex
    ; Then there is no next key in the map
endif
==See Also== *[[Array Variable]] *[[Ar_First]] *[[Ar_Last]] *[[Ar_Prev]] *[[Ar_Map]] *[[Let]] *[[Eval]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns a constant, an invalid [[array]]. This is used to clear up array variables when they are no longer necessary, such as when a script terminates. As of NVSE 4.6.2, arrays defined in [[User Defined Function|UDF]] scripts are cleaned up automatically, but those held in other scripts are not. Therefore it is essential to use Ar_Null to clear any local array variables when other scripts terminate to avoid save game bloat. Once the array is cleaned up, it will be set to 0. |name = Ar_Null |returnType = NullArray:array |arguments = }} ==Example== 
let SomeArray := Ar_Null
==Notes== * Should be called on all arrays that were created from Script Runner ==See Also== *[[Array Variable]] *[[Sv_Destruct]] (Equivalent to clear redundant [[String Variable|string variables]]) *[[Ar_Resize]] *[[Ar_BadNumericIndex]] *[[Ar_BadStringIndex]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns 1 if the specified array is packed (a regular array), or 0 if not (a map). Useful when using an [[array_var]] defined in another plugin. Added by NVSE 5.0.1. |name = Ar_Packed |returnType = bool |referenceType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Target |Type = array}} |example = }} ==See Also== *[[TypeOf]] is a more versatile function which can perform the same task. [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the previous key of an array, after that specified. For regular arrays (list arrays) this is just equivalent to (SpecifiedKey - 1). This function is most useful for map and stringmap arrays, where keys are dynamic. Note that map and stringmap arrays are always sorted in ascending numeric or alphanumeric order, regardless of the order of element insertion. Returns a bad index ([[Ar_BadNumericIndex]] or [[Ar_BadStringIndex]]) if there is no previous key in the array. |name = Ar_Prev |returnType = key:int-or-float-or-string |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Source |Type = array}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Next |Type = key}}}} ==Example== 
array_var MyMap
int iKey ; * or float/string_var as appropriate for array

let iKey := Ar_Last MyMap ; * get last element

while iKey != Ar_BadNumericIndex
    ; do something for every valid key in array, in reverse order
    let iKey := Ar_Prev MyMap, iKey
loop
==See Also== *[[Array Variable]] *[[Ar_First]] *[[Ar_Last]] *[[Ar_Next]] *[[Ar_Map]] *[[Let]] *[[Eval]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns a regular array (list) containing all int values between the specified start and end (inclusive), at the specified step interval. The step argument is optional and defaults to 1. A negative step interval can be used to order elements in reverse. To assign the function return to a variable, the [[let]] command must be used, rather than 'set'. |name = Ar_Range |returnType = array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Start |Type = int}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = End |Type = int}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = step |Type = int }} }} ==Example== 
array_var arr

let arr := Ar_Range 1, 5

 ; arr contains: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5

let arr := Ar_Range 0, 30, 10

 ; arr contains: 0, 10, 20, 30

let arr := Ar_range 10, 5, -1

 ; arr contain: 10, 9, 8, 7, 6, 5
==See Also== *[[Foreach]] *[[Let]] *[[Array Variables]] *[[Ar_Construct]] *[[Ar_List]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Usable only with "array" type array variables (array Lists). Resizes an existing array to the specified size. If the new size is a reduction, excess elements are deleted, starting from the last. If the resize is an increase, new elements are appended which each have the optional padding value specified (any item is permitted), or 0 if none was specified. It does not return an array, it resizes one in place. It returns true (1) if successful and false (0) if it it fails; although it is unlikely one will ever need to bother checking this. |name = Ar_Resize |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = List |Type = array}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = NewSize |Type = int}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = PadValue |Type = multi}}}} ==Example== 
array_var aBeatles

let aBeatles := Ar_List JohnREF, PaulREF, GeorgeREF, RingoREF

; aBeatles now contains: JohnREF, PaulREF, GeorgeREF, RingoREF

Ar_Resize aBeatles, 2

; aBeatles now contains: JohnREF, PaulREF

Ar_Resize aBeatles, 5, 42

; aBeatles now contains: JohnREF, PaulREF, 42, 42, 42
==Notes== *Alternatively, you can also use [[Ar_Append]] to add new elements to an existing array, and [[Ar_Erase]] to remove elements. Both these alternatives also resize an array in place. ==See Also== *[[Array Variables]] *[[Ar_Append]] *[[Ar_Erase]] *[[Ar_List]] *[[Ar_Size]] *[[Let]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the number of elements contained in an [[Array Variables|Array]], or -1 if the array has not been initialized |name = Ar_Size |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ToCheck |Type = array}}}} ==Example== 
array_var aBeatles

Ar_Size aBeatles ; returns -1, not initialized

let aBeatles := Ar_List JohnREF, PaulREF, GeorgeREF, RingoREF

Ar_Size aBeatles ; now returns 4
Conditional example: 
if eval (Ar_Size SomeArray) > 5
   ; SomeArray contains more than 5 elements
endif
==See Also== *[[Array Variables]] *[[Ar_Construct]] *[[Ar_Resize]] *[[Ar_List]] *[[Let]] *[[Eval]] *[[ListGetCount]] (equivalent for [[FormList]]s) *[[GetNumLevItems]] (equivalent for [[:Category:Leveled_Lists|Leveled Lists]]) [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Attempts to sort the elements of an array in ascending or descending order and returns a new regular array (list) containing the elements in sorted order (keys associated with the elements are lost, if source is a map type). In order to be sorted all elements of an array must be of the same type (strings, numbers, or objects). If this condition is not met an empty array is returned. Strings are sorted alphabetically and case-insensitively, numbers are sorted numerically, and objects are sorted by formID. By default the elements are sorted in ascending order, the optional flag sorts descending. |name = Ar_Sort |returnType = array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Source |Type = anyarray}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = SortDescending |Type = bool}}}} ==Example== 
array_var aOriginal
array_var aSorted

let aOriginal := Ar_List 1, 5, 2

let aSorted := Ar_Sort aOriginal ; * 1, 2, 5

let aSorted := Ar_Sort aOriginal, 1 ; * 5, 2, 1
==Notes== *If the order of values is not important, Ar_Sort can function as a quick way to pass all values in a map or stringmap to a regular array, comparable to Ar_Keys for keys. ==See Also== *[[Array Variables]] *[[Ar_CustomSort]] *[[Ar_SortAlpha]] *[[Let]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sorts the elements of an array alphabetically in ascending or descending order and returns a new Array containing the elements in sorted order. Ordering is case-insensitive. Unlike [[Ar_Sort]], does not require elements to be of the same type - numeric elements are [[ToString|converted to a string]] representation, and forms are sorted by their names (if named) or the string representation of their formIDs. Omitting the optional parameter causes elements to be sorted in ascending order. |name = Ar_SortAlpha |returnType = array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Source |Type = anyarray}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = SortDescending |Type = bool}}}} ==Example== 
array_var aOriginal
array_var aSorted

let aOriginal := Ar_List 5, 1, Jet, "xylophone", SunnyREF

let aSorted := Ar_SortAlpha aOriginal

; aSorted contains: 1, 5, Jet, SunnyREF, "xylophone"
==See Also== *[[Array Variable]] *[[Ar_CustomSort]] *[[Ar_Sort]] *[[Let]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the single character associated with an ASCII code as a string. |name = AsciiToChar |returnType = string |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ASCIICode |Type = int (Decimal)}} }}
==See Also== *[[CharToAscii]] *[[ToString]] *[[Sv_GetChar]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = An alternative to [[GetFormFromMod]]. Takes a mod index as an integer set by [[GetModIndex]] and the '''decimal converted''' [[formID]] or in [[Inline Expression|hexadecimal 0x format form ID]] of an object, which may be either a [[reference]] or [[base form]], and returns a reference to it. This can be used to reference forms contained in non-dependent external modules, but great care must be taken as invalid usage may crash the game. |name = BuildRef |returnType = form |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ModIndex |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = DecimalFormID |Type = int }}}} ==Example== 
int iModIndex
ref rRainySmiles

if IsModLoaded "SomePopularCompanionMod.esp"
    set iModIndex to GetModIndex "SomePopularCompanionMod.esp"
    set rRainySmiles to BuildRef iModIndex, (0x104E85)
endif
==Avoiding CTDs== If one uses an invalid decimal form ID then it will likely crash the game when you try to use your (mis)built reference. This may be an issue if the form is non existent or different in past or future versions of the referenced module, or if two distinct modules are given the same file name. You can avoid CTDs by ensuring the return is both a valid form, and of the type you expect it to be, using [[IsFormValid]], and functions like [[IsReference]], [[IsActor]] or [[GetType]]. For example, following on from the code above, where we want to get a companion added by an external mod: 
if eval !(IsFormValid rRainySmiles)
   ; The Form is NOT valid, do not proceed!
   return
elseif eval !(IsReference rRainySmiles)
   ; The form is NOT a reference, its something else, do not proceed!
   return
elseif eval !(rRainySmiles.IsActor)
   ; The form is a reference, but NOT an actor (maybe its a door or something)!
   return
else
   ; Its all good, we got an actor from the buildref so we can proceed
endif

; "if eval !(condition)" just means "if conditon is false"
Or you can use [[GetType]] for any types: 
if eval !(IsFormValid SomeBuildRefSpell)
    ; The Form is NOT valid, do not proceed!
    return
elseif GetType SomeBuildRefSpell != 20 ; * 20 is the code for 'spell'
    ; I was expecting a spell but got something else, do not proceed!
    return
else
    ; Its safe, I got a spell like I expected
    PlayerREF.CIOS SomeBuildRefSpell
endif
Note: You must use multiple lines for the if statements, not &&, because the GECK always evaluates all conditons on a line. *Since xNVSE 6.0, using [[Eval]] enables condition short-circuiting behavior, making it safe to use a single if statement line. ==See Also== *[[GetFormFromMod]] *[[FnGetExternalForm]] - A convenient UDF for safe build reffing. *[[IsModLoaded]] *[[GetModIndex]] *[[GetLocalRefIndex]] *[[Causes of CTDs]] *[[IsFormValid]], [[IsReference]], [[IsActor]], [[GetType]] *[[Eval]] *[[GetVariable]] *[[AddScriptVariable]] *[[SetVariable]] *[[SetRefVariable]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:ID Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Calls a [[User Defined Function]]. Should be followed by a list of arguments matching the types expected by the function. If a calling reference is specified, commands within the function body will operate on that reference. Call returns the value specified by the function. |name = Call |returnType = multi |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = function |Type = script }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = multi |Type = arg(up to 15) |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== 
Call MyUDF

int iValue

let iValue := Call MyOtherUDF SunnyREF

if eval (Call MySuitableUDF)
    ; * My UDF return evaluated to true
endif

player.Call MyPlayerUDF
==See Also== *[[CallAfterFrames]] *[[CallAfterSeconds]] *[[Eval]] *[[GetCallingScript]] *[[Let]] *[[RunScript]] *[[SetFunctionValue]] *[[User Defined Function]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:User Defined Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the ASCII code of a single character passed as a string. Additional characters in the string are ignored. |name = CharToASCII |returnType = ASCIICode:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = SourceCharacter |Type = string}} }} ==See Also== *[[AsciiToChar]] *[[ToNumber]] *[[Sv_GetChar]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the specified int with the specified bit cleared. |name = ClearBit |returnType = int |referenceType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ValueToChange |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = BitToClear |Type = int }} }} ==Example== 
int a
set a to 15		; bits 0-3 set ; %00001111 binary
set a to ClearBit a, 2	; clear bit 2 (value 4) ;%00000100 binary
; a is now 11
==See Also== *[[SetBit]] *[[SetEquipmentSlotsMask]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Bit Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Clears the item association of a hotkey. |name = ClearHotkey |returnType = none |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = WhichHotKey |Type = int }} |referenceType = }} ==Example== 
ClearHotKey 1
==See Also== *[[GetHotkeyItem]] *[[SetHotkeyItem]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Hotkey Functions]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

revision.comment: +cat
revision.text: {{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns true (1) if the compiled scripts are identical. |name = CompareScripts |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Script |Type = script }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Script |Type = script }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetCurrentScript]] *[[GetCallingScript]] *[[GetScript]] *[[SetScript]] *[[RemoveScript]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Script Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Runs the console command [[ShowInventory|inv]] via script, to print the inventory of the calling container to the console. Requires [[SetConsoleEcho|Console Echo]] to be toggled on. This function is useful for creating [[Debug Dumps]] with [[Con_SCOF]]. |name = Con_Inv |returnType = None |referenceType = container |arguments = }} ==Example== 
SetConsoleEcho 1
PlayerREF.Con_Inv
==See Also== *[[ShowInventory]] (inv) - Console version *[[GetConsoleEcho]] *[[SetConsoleEcho]] *[[Con_SCOF]] (to write console output to a text file) *[[Debug Dumps]] *[[Con_SQV]] *[[Con_ShowVars]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Debugging]]

revision.comment: +see also
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Runs the console command [[SCOF]] (Set Console Output File) via a script. This saves all subsequent console output during a game session to an external text file in the main game directory. The file name is specified as a string argument, and if it does not exist then it will be created, otherwise it will be appended to.
This function can be used to create [[Debug Dumps]]. |name = Con_SCOF |returnType = none |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = FileName |Type = string }} |example = con_SCOF "debuglog.txt" }} ==See Also== *[[SCOF]] *[[HasConsoleOutputFile]] *[[GetConsoleOutputFile]] *[[Print]] / [[DebugPrint]] *[[PrintF]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Debugging]]

revision.comment: link to debug dumps
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Added by NVSE v4. Shows the specified quest's variables in the console window. Requires [[SetConsoleEcho|Console Echo]] to be toggled on. This function is useful for creating [[Debug Dumps]] with [[Con_SCOF]]. |name = Con_SQV |returnType = None |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ToShowVarsOf |Type = Quest}} }} ==Example== 
SetConsoleEcho 1
Con_SQV HolyGrailQuest
Will output to the console something like: 
->iNumFollowers = 7
->HasFoundGrail = 1
->HasSiegedCastle = 0
->fGameDayFound= 21.0524
->aSomeArray = 188.0000
->SomeStringVar = 165
--- Quest state -----------------------------
Running?       Yes
Current stage: 0
Priority:      50
==Notes== *String and array variablea print their ID number rather than contents. ==See Also== *[[Debug Dumps]] *[[ShowQuestVars]] - Console version *[[Con_SCOF]] *[[GetConsoleEcho]] *[[SetConsoleEcho]] *[[Ar_Dump]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Debugging]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Runs the console command [[SetUFOCamSpeedMult]] via a script, to adjust the speed multiplier on the UFO Cam. |name = Con_SetUFOCamSpeedMult |returnType = None |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = SpeedMult |Type = float }} |example = Con_SetUFOCamSpeedMult 2.5 }} ==See Also== *[[SetUFOCamSpeedMult]] *[[Con_TFC]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Prints the name and value of all variables in the script attached to the [[calling reference]]. Requires [[SetConsoleEcho|Console Echo]] to be toggled on. This function is useful for creating [[Debug Dumps]] with [[Con_SCOF]]. |name = Con_ShowVars |referenceType = reference |returnType = None |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = |Type = }} }} ==Example== 
SetConsoleEcho 1
SunnyREF.Con_ShowVars
==Notes== *String and array variables print their ID number rather than contents. ==See Also== *[[ShowVars]] - Console version *[[Debug Dumps]] *[[Con_SQV]] (to show the variables for a quest instead) *[[Ar_Dump]] (to show contents of an array) *[[Con_SCOF]] (to write console output to a text file) *[[GetConsoleEcho]] *[[SetConsoleEcho]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Debugging]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Runs the console command [[ToggleCollision|ToggleCollision (TCL)]] via a script, to toggle on or off collision (NoClip). |name = Con_TCL |returnType = None |example = Con_TCL }} ==See Also== *[[ToggleCollision|ToggleCollision (TCL)]] *[[Con_TFC]] *[[ToggleObjectCollision]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Runs the console command [[ToggleDebugText|ToggleDebugText (TDT)]] via a script, to toggle on or off debug display. |name = Con_TDT |returnType = None |example = Con_TDT }} ==See Also== *[[ToggleDebugText]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Debugging]]

revision.comment: created
revision.text: {{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Runs the console command [[ToggleFlyCam|ToggleFlyCam (TFC)]] via a script, to toggle on or off the fly camera. |name = Con_TFC |returnType = None |example = Con_TFC ; then do something that needs fly cam Con_TFC ; repeat to revert to previous state }} ==See Also== *[[ToggleFlyCam]] *[[GetTFC]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Runs the console command [[ToggleFootIK]] (TFIK) via a script. |name = Con_TFIK |returnType = none |arguments = |example = Con_TFIK }} ==See Also== *[[ToggleFootIK]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Runs the console command [[ToggleMenus]] via a script, to toggle on or off menus. |name = Con_ToggleMenus |returnType = None |example = Con_ToggleMenus }} ==See Also== *[[ToggleMenus]] *[[Con_TFC]] *[[CloseActiveMenu]] *[[Con_CloseAllMenus]] *[[CloseAllMenus]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:UI Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Added by NVSE V4. Used only inside of a [[While]] or [[Foreach]] loop block to bypass subsequent lines and return code execution to its beginning, repeating the loop if it's condition evaluates true. See the loop function pages for further information. |name = Continue |example = While(iCount > 0) ; do things if (someConditon == true) Continue endif ; do additional things loop
foreach Element <- Array ; do things if (someConditon == true) Continue endif ; do additional things loop }}
==See Also== *[[While]] *[[ForEach]] *[[Break]] *[[Return]] to entirely stop the execution of the script.
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Copies an [[inventory reference]] to the specified container. The calling object needn't be in a container and remains valid after the command is called. If the calling object is equipped, the copy will not be equipped. Returns true if it succeeds, although it is not necessary to check this.
|name = CopyIR |referenceType = InvRef |returnType = Success:bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Location |Type = Container }} |example = ; Within an object script: CopyIR PlayerREF }} ==See Also== *[[CopyIRAlt]] - Same as this but returns the newly copied inventory reference *[[RemoveMeIR]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category: Object Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Reference Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Creates a (temporary) [[inventory reference]] to the specified form. This reference does not exist in the gameworld or in a container, and remains valid for only one frame.
It is mostly useful for creating a stack of one or more items to be added to a container with [[CopyIR]].
|name = CreateTempRef |referenceType = |returnType = ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ToReference |Type = form }} }} ==See Also== *[[TempCloneForm]] *[[IsClonedForm]] *[[CopyIR]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Reference Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Unselects any target and quit VATS mode, allowing current attack to finish executing. |name = EndVATScam |returnType = none |referenceType = |arguments = |example = EndVATScam }} ==See Also== *[[Con_CloseAllMenus]] *[[StopVATSCam]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:VATS Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Forces the [[calling reference]] to equip the specified object, and runs its OnEquip block. Optionally disallows unequipping the object and hides messages. |name = EquipItem2 |returnType = none |referenceType = Actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Object |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = NoUnequipFlag |Type = bool |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = HideEquipMessageFlag |Type = bool |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== 
SunnyREF.EquipItem2 SomeItem
==Notes== *This function is identical to the JIP function [[EquipItemAlt]]. ==See Also== *[[EquipItemAlt]] *[[EquipItem]] *[[EquipMe]] *[[UnequipItemAlt]] *[[UnequipMe]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category: Object Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Equips the calling object on its owner.
Also works on [[Inventory Reference]]s. |name = EquipMe |returnType = none |referenceType = reference |arguments = |example = ; Within an object script EquipMe }} ==See Also== *[[UnequipMe]] *[[EquipItem2]] *[[RemoveMe]] *[[RemoveMeIR]] *[[RemoveMeIRAlt]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category: Object Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Reduces an expression to a boolean for use in if conditional statements. Used to permit the usage of any function or expression that may be so reduced for this purpose. Eval significantly expands the scripting functionality of if-statements as it supports [[NVSE Expressions]]. With xNVSE 6, Eval supports [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Short-circuit_evaluation short-circuiting] the logical operators && and {{!}}{{!}}. Eval is required to test the value of [[User Defined Function|user defined functions]], and those related to [[Array Variable|arrays]] and [[String Variable|strings]] in if conditionals, as the vanilla compiler will otherwise misunderstand them. This is because these were added by NVSE, and hence were not present when the original scripting language was created. |name = eval |returnType = boolean |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = AnyReducable |Type = expression }} }} ==Example== 

if eval SomeStringVar == "this string"
   ; SomeStringVar has contents "this string"
endif


if eval SomeArray[3] == SomeValue 
    ; Element at index 3 of SomeArray equals SomeValue
endif


if eval (call SomeSuitableUDF) 
    ; The user defined function returns true
endif


if eval SomeInt == 5 
   ; * including 'eval' here is not necessary, but is harmless

elseif eval !(SomeActor.GetDead) ; * elseif can be used as usual
   ; SomeActor is _not_ dead, (note '!')

else
   ; else can be used as usual
endif
==Notes== *If struggling to understand the purpose of 'eval', then by analogy think of 'if eval' as simply telling the GECK compiler the line is written in NVSE language, to prevent it complaining with an error. *Eval is not required for NVSE aware conditions, such as [[While]]. *Whilst not necessary, it is harmless to include 'eval' in any 'if' statement (when testing vanilla functions, for example), however it may be slightly slower than using a regular if statement. There is a possible advantage to this, in that when an illegitimate eval statement fails (example, testing certain functions on an empty ref var), an [[NVSE Error|error message]] will be printed to the game console, rather than silently failing. ==See Also== *[[TestExpr]] *[[User Defined Function]] *[[Array Variable]] *[[String Variable]] *[[Script Compiler Override]] ==External Links== *[https://obse.silverlock.org/obse_command_doc.html#OBSE_Expressions List of supported expression found in OBSE, these are equivalent in NVSE] *[https://www.loverslab.com/topic/26749-tutorial-nvse4-part-1-syntax-and-expressions/ Tutorial on syntax and expressions in NVSE 4] [[Category:Commands]] [[Category:Scripting]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:User Defined Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

revision.comment: https
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Used to repeat a script block for each entry in a collection. The collection may be either a container, [[Array Variable|array]] or [[String Variable|string]]. Since [[xNVSE]] 6.2.5, ForEach can also be used to iterate directly over a [[FormList]]; the past method was to convert the formlist to an array using [[GetListForms]]. ForEach loops should only be used with stringmap arrays due to taking lot of overhead, since it is necessary to create a temp associative single-pair stringmap array for each of the iterations. Use [[while]] loops for arrays with indexes instead to avoid this extra overhead. '''NOTE:''' since xNVSE v6+, ForEach now only performs slightly worse than While, so it is far more acceptable to use. Within foreach loops, the command continue may be used to skip any remaining loop code for that entry, and move on to the next. In the same context, the command break may be used to end the loop immediately, ignoring any remaining code and entries. "<-" is part of the ForEach expression and is synonymous with the "in" operator in python. }} ==Syntax and Usage== In the case of a container, an entry is a temporary reference to an item in its inventory. This means it may be used as a [[calling reference]], but becomes invalid once the loop ends. (See also: [[GetInvRefsForItem]], [[CopyIR]], [[RemoveMeIR]], [[IsEquipped]]). 
ref Item
ref Container
foreach Item:tempref <- Container:ref
    ; Item is a temporary reference to an item in the container
loop
For an [[Array Variable|array]], each entry is a stringmap with two fields, "key" and "value". The key is the index of the entry in the collection (0, 1, 2... for regular list arrays). (Note: you can replace 'entry["value"]' with the shorthand '*entry', see [[NVSE Expressions]]) 
array_var Entry
array_var Collection

foreach Entry:array <- Collection:array
    ; Entry["key"] is the key (index) of each entry (0, 1, 2... for lists)
    ; Entry["value"] is the value of each entry in the array
loop
For a string, each entry is a string containing a single character. 
string_var Char
string_var Source

foreach Char:string <- Source:string
    ; Char is a single character in String
loop
For a [[FormList]] source, each entry is a form. 
ref rForm

ForEach rForm <- ExampleFormList
    ; rForm is the nth form in the ExampleFormList
loop
==Example== 
ref rItem
ref rActor

foreach rItem <- rActor
   if rItem.IsEquipped
        rItem.UnequipMe
   endif
loop

; Will unequip all equipped items from rActor

array_var Beatles
array_var Entry
ref rMusician
int iPosition

let Beatles := ar_List JohnREF, PaulREF, GeorgeREF, RingoREF

foreach Entry <- Beatles
    let iPosition := Entry["key"]
    let rMusician := Entry["value"]
    Print "Entry #" + $iPosition + " is " + $rMusician
loop

; Will print in game:
; Entry #0 is John Lennon
; Entry #1 is Paul McCartney
; Entry #2 is George Harrison
; Entry #3 is Ringo Starr
Using continue: 
foreach Entry <- Beatles
    let rMusician := Entry["value"]
    if rMusician.GetDead
        continue ; * Go direct to next entry: we ignore dead members
    endif
    rMusician.AddItem Beer, 1
loop

; Every living member of the Beatles is given a beer
Using break: 
foreach Entry <- Beatles
    let rMusician := Entry["value"]
    if rMusician.GetInWorldSpace Liverpool
        rMusician.AddItem Beer, 1
    else
        break ; * End Loop immediately if we find a member is not in Liverpool
    endif
loop

; Give a beer to each member of the Beatles until one is found not to be in Liverpool- assume that all Beatles except Paul are in Liverpool; since Paul is the second entry of the array, only John, the first, gets a beer.
You can also use the [[Ar_Range]] command to approximate the traditional 'C' style for loop. The code below prints the numbers 0-10: 
foreach Entry <- (Ar_Range 0, 10)
    Print $Entry["value"]
loop
Note that above we reference Entry["value"] in the [[Print]] function directly, rather than use an intermediary variable (let SomeVar := Entry["value"]..). This is only possible when using NVSE aware functions or the [[Script Compiler Override]]. ==Notes== * When you use Foreach to loop through an array type, using [[Ar_Erase | Ar_Erase]] on the key that is currently being iterated, while inside the loop, will cause issues. If you're looping through a regular array, consider using a backwards [[While | While]] loop instead. For maps and stringmaps, you can either append the keys that need removing to a separate array first and do the erasing there afterwards: 
let aSomeArray := ar_construct "array"
foreach entry <- aSomeMap
   if somecondition
     ar_append aSomeArray, entry["key"]
   endif
loop
let iKey := ar_size aSomeArray
while -1 < (iKey -= 1)
   ar_erase aSomeMap, aSomeArray[iKey]
loop
or use the foreach loop to populate a new map or stringmap that excludes the keys you need erased, and then replace the old one with the new one: 
let aSomeMap2 := ar_construct "map"
foreach entry <- aSomeMap
   if somecondition
     continue
   endif
   let aSomeMap2[entry["key"]] := entry["value"]
loop
let aSomeMap := aSomeMap2
* The above also holds true for erasing characters from a string when you're ForEach-ing through a string. Use similar techniques to avoid the issue. ==See Also== *[[While]] *[[Ar_ForEach]] *[[ForEachInList]] *[[Array Variables]] *[[Let]] *[[Eval]] *[[Label]]/[[Goto]] *[[GetListForms]] (converts a form list to an array, so you can use foreach with it if you can't use [[xNVSE]] 6.2.5 or greater) *[[NVSE Expressions]] ==External Links== *[https://obse.silverlock.org/obse_command_doc.html#OBSE_Expressions List of supported expression found in OBSE, these are equivalent in NVSE] *[https://www.loverslab.com/topic/26749-tutorial-nvse4-part-1-syntax-and-expressions/ Tutorial on syntax and expressions in NVSE 4] (this link requires pre-setting user details for this site) [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Commands]] [[Category:Scripting]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Adds a form to an embedded list of forms associated with an object at a given index; passing -2 will add to the end of the list. |name = GenericAddForm |returnType = none |referenceType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ListCode |Type = int |Optional = }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Target |Type = object |Optional = }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = ToAdd |Type = form |Optional = }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Index |Type = int |Optional = }} }} ==Embedded List Codes== *0 = Race Hair (see also: [[GetRaceHairs]] *1 = Race Eyes ([[GetRaceEyes]]) *5 = Actor Base (NPC or creature) Packages ([[GetBasePackages]]) *6 = Actor Base Spells ([[GetBaseSpellListSpells]]) *7 = Actor Base Levelled Spells ([[GetBaseSpellListLevSpells]]) *10 = NPC Head Part ([[GetHeadParts]]) *13 = Form List form (redundant given existing functions, such as [[ListAddForm]]) ==Example== To add a new head part to Easy Pete: 
GenericAddForm 10, EasyPeteREF, EyePatch, -2
Note: Changes to an actor's 3D are not visible until it reloads, which can be forced using [[Update3D]]. ==See Also== *[[GenericReplaceForm]] *[[GenericDeleteForm]] *[[GenericGetForm]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Deletes a form within an embedded list of forms associated with an object at a given index. Returns the removed form. |name = GenericDeleteForm |returnType = RemovedForm:ref |referenceType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ListCode |Type = int |Optional = }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Target |Type = object |Optional = }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Index |Type = int |Optional = }} }} ==Embedded List Codes== *0 = Race Hair (see also: [[GetRaceHairs]] *1 = Race Eyes ([[GetRaceEyes]]) *5 = Actor Base (NPC or creature) Packages ([[GetBasePackages]]) *6 = Actor Base Spells ([[GetBaseSpellListSpells]]) *7 = Actor Base Levelled Spells ([[GetBaseSpellListLevSpells]]) *10 = NPC Head Part ([[GetHeadParts]]) *13 = Form List form (redundant given existing functions, such as [[ListAddForm]]) ==Example== To remove Easy Pete's beard: 
ref OldForm

let OldForm := GenericDeleteForm 10, EasyPeteRef, 0

; OldForm is 'Old Beard'.
Note: Changes to an actor's 3D are not visible until it reloads, which can be forced using [[Update3D]]. ==See Also== *[[GenericAddForm]] *[[GenericReplaceForm]] *[[GenericGetForm]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the form within an embedded list of forms associated with an object at a given index. |name = GenericGetForm |returnType = form |referenceType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ListCode |Type = int |Optional = }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Target |Type = object |Optional = }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Index |Type = int |Optional = }} }} ==Embedded List Codes== *0 = Race Hair (see also: [[GetRaceHairs]] *1 = Race Eyes ([[GetRaceEyes]]) *5 = Actor Base (NPC or creature) Packages ([[GetBasePackages]]) *6 = Actor Base Spells ([[GetBaseSpellListSpells]]) *7 = Actor Base Levelled Spells ([[GetBaseSpellListLevSpells]]) *10 = NPC Head Part ([[GetHeadParts]]) *13 = Form List form (redundant given existing functions, such as [[ListAddForm]]) ==Example== To return Easy Pete's first head part (index 0): 
ref HeadPart

let HeadPart := GenericGetForm 10, EasyPeteREF, 0

; HeadPart is 'Old Beard'.
==See Also== *[[GenericAddForm]] *[[GenericReplaceForm]] *[[GenericDeleteForm]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Replaces a form within an embedded list of forms associated with an object at a given index with a new one. Returns the replaced form. |name = GenericReplaceForm |returnType = OldForm:ref |referenceType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ListCode |Type = int |Optional = }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Target |Type = object |Optional = }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = New |Type = form |Optional = }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Index |Type = int |Optional = }} }} ==Embedded List Codes== *0 = Race Hair (see also: [[GetRaceHairs]] *1 = Race Eyes ([[GetRaceEyes]]) *5 = Actor Base (NPC or creature) Packages ([[GetBasePackages]]) *6 = Actor Base Spells ([[GetBaseSpellListSpells]]) *7 = Actor Base Levelled Spells ([[GetBaseSpellListLevSpells]]) *10 = NPC Head Part ([[GetHeadParts]]) *13 = Form List form (redundant given existing functions, such as [[ListAddForm]]) ==Example== To give Easy Pete a new beard: 
ref OldForm

let OldForm := GenericReplaceForm 10, EasyPeteREF, BeardChopper, 0

; OldForm is 'Old Beard', now he's got a chopper.
Note: Changes to an actor's 3D are not visible until it reloads, which can be forced using [[Update3D]]. ==See Also== *[[GenericAddForm]] *[[GenericDeleteForm]] *[[GenericGetForm]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns an [[Array Variable|array]] containing all factions to which the specified or calling actor belongs. |name = GetActiveFactions |referenceType = actor |returnType = array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Target |Type = actor |Optional = y}} }} ==Example== 
array_var Sunny_Factions

let Sunny_Factions := SunnyREF.GetActiveFactions
Ar_Dump Sunny_Factions: 
** Dumping Array #19 **
Refs: 1 Owner 0A: nvse-test.esp
[ 0.000000 ] : Sunny Smiles Faction (001691EA)
[ 1.000000 ] :  (0013F8A4)
[ 2.000000 ] :  (0013F89B)
[ 3.000000 ] : Goodsprings Militia (0015EC58)
[ 4.000000 ] : Goodsprings Faction (00104C6E)
[ 5.000000 ] : Goodsprings Ringo Allies Faction (0015F8DB)
==See Also== *[[GetActiveRanks]] *[[GetBaseFactions]] *[[GetBaseNumFactions]] *[[GetBaseNthFaction]] *[[GetBaseNthRank]] *[[GetBaseNthFaction]] *[[GetNumRanks]] *[[Array Variables]] [[Category:Faction Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

revision.comment: italics
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns an [[Array Variable|array]] containing the current (active) ranks for each faction to which the the [[calling reference]] belongs. The order of ranks corresponds to the factions returned by [[GetActiveFactions]]. Note that in ''Fallout: New Vegas'', ranks are largely unused are usually zero. |name = GetActiveRanks |returnType = array |referenceType = actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Target |Type = Actor |Optional = y}} }} ==Example== 
array_var Sunny_Ranks

Let Sunny_Ranks := SunnyREF.GetActiveRanks
Ar_Dump Sunny_Ranks would print (as in most vanilla cases, all zero): 
** Dumping Array #16 **
Refs: 1 Owner 0B: nvse-test.esp
[ 0.000000 ] : 0.000000
[ 1.000000 ] : 0.000000
[ 2.000000 ] : 0.000000
[ 3.000000 ] : 0.000000
[ 4.000000 ] : 0.000000
[ 5.000000 ] : 0.000000
==See Also== *[[GetActiveFactions]] *[[GetBaseRanks]] *[[GetNumRanks]] *[[Array Variables]] [[Category:Faction Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the high base flags of the specified actor or calling reference as a combined value of the set bits (int). |name = GetActorBaseFlagsHigh |returnType = int |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = NPC |Type = BaseForm |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== 
int FlagsMask
set FlagsMask to GetActorBaseFlagsHigh SomeNPC
==Bit fields== {| class="wikitable" |- ! Flag ! Bit ! NPC ! Creature |- | 1 | 0 | Unknown 16 | No Right Arm |- | 2 | 1 | Unknown 17 | No Left Arm |- | 4 | 2 | Unknown 18 | No Combat In Water |- | 8 | 3 | Unknown 19 | No Shadow |- | 16 | 4 | No VATS Melee | No VATS melee |- | 32 | 5 | Unknown 21 | Allow PC Dialogue |- | 64 | 6 | Can Be All Races | Can't Open Doors |- | 128 | 7 | Auto-Calc Service | Immobile |- | 256 | 8 | Unknown 24 | Tilt Front/Back |- | 512 | 9 | Unknown 25 | Tilt Left/Right |- | 1024 | 10 | No Knockdowns | No knockdowns |- | 2048 | 11 | Not Pushable | Not Pushable |- | 4096 | 12 | Unknown 28 | Allow Pickpocket |- | 8192 | 13 | Unknown 29 | Is Ghost |- | 16384 | 14 | No Rotating to Head-track | No Rotating to Head-track |- | 32768 | 15 | Unknown 31 | Invulnerable |} ==See Also== *[[GetActorBaseFlagsLow]] *[[SetActorBaseFlagsHigh]] *[[SetBit]] *[[ClearBit]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the low base flags of the specified actor or calling reference, as a combined value of the set bits (int). |name = GetActorBaseFlagsLow |returnType = int |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = actor |Type = BaseForm |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== 
int FlagsMask
set FlagsMask to GetActorBaseFlagsLow SomeActor
==Bit fields== {| class="wikitable" |- ! Flag ! Bit ! NPC ! Creature |- | 1 | 0 | Female | Biped |- | 2 | 1 | Essential | Essential |- | 4 | 2 | Is CharGen Face Preset | Weapon & Shield? |- | 8 | 3 | Respawn | Respawn |- | 16 | 4 | Auto-calc stats | Swims |- | 32 | 5 | Unknown 5 | Flies |- | 64 | 6 | Unknown 6 | Walks |- | 128 | 7 | PC Level Mult | PC Level Mult |- | 256 | 8 | Unknown 8 | Unknown 8 |- | 512 | 9 | No Low-Level Processing | No Low-Level Processing |- | 1024 | 10 | Unknown 10 | Unknown 10 |- | 2048 | 11 | No Blood Spray | No Blood Spray |- | 4096 | 12 | No Blood Decal | No Blood Decal |- | 8192 | 13 | Unknown 13 | Unknown 13 |- | 16384 | 14 | Unknown 14 | Unknown 14 |- | 32768 | 15 | Unknown 15 | No Head |} ==Notes== * Bit 8 (256) was described as "use template", but checking it on a templated base form or a base form used as a template always returns 0. * Bits 11 & 12 operate in the opposite way compared to the Geck. While in the geck, NPCs are flagged if they have blood spray and decal, the bits are only 1 if they don't. ==See Also== *[[SetActorBaseFlagsLow]] *[[GetActorBaseFlagsHigh]] *[[GetBit]] *[[SetBit]] *[[ClearBit]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

revision.comment: Removed note about function being broken and fixed.
revision.text: {{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns a [[Array Variable|String Map]] consisting of all of the key-value entries for the [[Mod Local Data]] associated with the mod to which the calling script belongs. This is a copy of the local data, so modifying the array contents will not modify the stored data. |name = GetAllModLocalData |returnType = stringmap |referenceType = |arguments = |example = array_var AllModLocalData let AllModLocalData := GetAllModLocalData }} =='''See Also'''== *[[Mod Local Data]] *[[GetModLocalData]] *[[SetModLocalData]] *[[ModLocalDataExists]] *[[RemoveModLocalData]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the rank of the specified companion [[:Category:Perk|Perk]]. This function may only be called on the player. Returns -1 if the perk argument or [[calling reference]] is null. |name = GetAltPerkRank |returnType = int |referenceType = PlayerREF |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Target |Type = perk}} }} ==See Also== *[[GetPerkRank]] (for normal perks) *[[HasPerk]] *[[AddPerk]] [[Category:Perk Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the casing item given by the specified ammo or calling reference. |name = GetAmmoCasing |returnType = form |referenceType = ammoReference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ammo |Type = baseForm |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== 
ref rAmmoCase
set rAmmoCase to GetAmmoCasing Ammo20Ga
==See Also== *[[SetAmmoCasing]] *[[GetWeaponAmmo]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Ammo Functions]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the percentage chance of providing an empty case item on use for the specified [[ammo]] or [[calling reference]] of an ammo item. |name = GetAmmoConsumedPercent |returnType = float |referenceType = ammoReference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ammo |Type = baseform |Optional = y }} |example = float fCaseChance set fCaseChance to GetAmmoConsumedPercent Ammo308 }} =='''See Also'''== *[[SetAmmoTraitNumeric]] can be used to set the consumed percent *[[SetAmmoConsumedPercent]] was added in [[xNVSE]] 6.3.1 *[[GetAmmoCasing]] *[[GetWeaponAmmo]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Ammo Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the speed (velocity) of the specified ammo or [[calling reference]]. |name = GetAmmoSpeed |returnType = float |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Ammo |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = float fSpeed set fSpeed to GetAmmoSpeed Ammo308 }} =='''See Also'''== *[[GetWeaponAmmo]] *[[GetAmmoCasing]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Ammo Functions]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

revision.comment: italics
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the Damage Threshold of the specified [[armor]] [[base form]], or a [[calling reference]] |name = GetArmorDT |returnType = int |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Armor |Type = form |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== 
int iDT

set iDT to GetArmorDT ArmorLeather
==Notes== * Armors in ''Fallout: New Vegas'' primarily use Damage Threshold, unlike ''Fallout 3'' which use Damage Resistance, or 'Armor Rating', this can be retrieved using [[GetArmorArmorRating|GetArmorAR]]. ==See Also== *[[GetArmorAR]] (Damage Resistance) *[[SetArmorAR]] *[[SetArmorDT]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Armor Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

revision.comment: Removed note about function being broken and fixed.
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the array that corresponds to the array variable specified by name for the calling reference or specified scriptable object / quest. |name = GetArrayVariable |returnType = array |alias = GetArrayVar |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = VarName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Parent |Type = Object |Optional = y }} |referenceType = ref }} ==Example== 
ref rActor
array_var aData

let aData := rActor.GetArrayVariable "aData"

; Alternatively, (or required if rActor holds a base form): 

let aData := GetArrayVariable "aData", rActor
==Note== *Since two array variables point to the same array contents if one is let to the other, a 'set' version of this function is not necessary- changes to one will affect the other, because they both refer to the same data. Use [[Ar_Copy]] or [[Ar_DeepCopy]] if you wish to duplicate arrays. ==See Also== *[[GetVariable]] (To get a numeric variable value instead) *[[GetRefVariable]] (and for ref variables) *[[HasVariable]] (to check if a variable exists at all) *[[SetVariable]] *[[SetRefVariable]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Script Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns an [[Array Variable|array]] containing all factions to which the base form of the specified actor (which may be base) or [[calling reference]] belongs. |name = GetBaseFactions |returnType = array |referenceType = actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Target |Type = actor |Optional = y}} }} ==Example== 
array_var Sunny_Factions

Let Sunny_Factions := GetBaseFactions GSSunnySmiles
Ar_Dump Sunny_Factions would print: 
** Dumping Array #17 **
Refs: 1 Owner 0A: nvse-test.esp
[ 0.000000 ] : Sunny Smiles Faction (001691EA)
[ 1.000000 ] :  (0013F8A4)
[ 2.000000 ] :  (0013F89B)
[ 3.000000 ] : Goodsprings Militia (0015EC58)
[ 4.000000 ] : Goodsprings Faction (00104C6E)
[ 5.000000 ] : Goodsprings Ringo Allies Faction (0015F8DB)
==See Also== *[[GetBaseRanks]] *[[GetActiveFactions]] *[[GetBaseNumFactions]] *[[GetBaseNthFaction]] *[[GetBaseNthRank]] *[[GetBaseNthFaction]] *[[GetFactionMembers]] *[[GetNumRanks]] *[[Array Variables]] [[Category:Faction Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the actual [[Base Form]] of a [[leveled]] [[calling reference]]. |name = GetBaseForm |alias = gbf |returnType = form |referenceType = reference }} ==See Also== *[[GetBaseObject]] *[[SetBaseForm]] *[[GetStaticForm]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Leveled_List_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the Nth faction of the baseform of the [[calling reference]] or specified form. The order of factions matches that of [[GetBaseNthRank]]. |name = GetBaseNthFaction |returnType = faction |referenceType = actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = NthFaction |Type = int}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Target |Type = actor-character-creature |Optional = y}} }} ==Example== 
ref rFaction

let rFaction := SunnyREF.GetBaseNthFaction 1
==See Also== *[[GetBaseFactions]] *[[GetBaseRanks]] *[[GetBaseNthRank]] [[Category:Faction Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the Nth faction rank of the [[base form]] of the [[calling reference]] or specified form. The order of ranks returned matches that of [[GetBaseNthFaction]]. |name = GetBaseNthRank |returnType = int |referenceType = actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = NthFaction |Type = int}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Target |Type = actor-character-creature |Optional = y}} }} ==Example== 
int iRank

let iRank := SunnyREF.GetBaseNthRank 1
==See Also== *[[GetBaseFactions]] *[[GetBaseRanks]] *[[GetBaseNthFaction]] [[Category:Faction Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the number of factions to which the base form of the [[calling reference]] or specified NPC belongs. |name = GetBaseNumFactions |returnType = int |referenceType = Actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Target |Type = NPC |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== 
int NumFactions

let NumFactions := SunnyREF.GetBaseNumFactions ; 6
==See Also== *[[GetBaseFactions]] *[[GetNumRanks]] *[[GetBaseNthFaction]] [[Category:Faction Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns an [[Array Variable|array]] containing all [[Package]]s attached to the base form of the specified actor or [[calling reference]]. |name = GetBasePackages |returnType = array |referenceType = actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Target |Type = actor |Optional = y}} }} ==Example== 
array_var Sunny_Packages

let Sunny_Packages := SunnyREF.GetBasePackages
==See Also== *[[Array Variables]] *[[GetCurrentPackage]] *[[GetPackageCount]] *[[GenericAddForm]] *[[GenericDeleteForm]] *[[GenericGetForm]] *[[GenericReplaceForm]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:AI Functions]] [[Category:Package Functions]]

revision.comment: italics
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns an [[Array Variable|array]] containing the ranks for each faction to which the [[base form]] of the [[calling reference]] or specified [[NPC]] belongs. The order of ranks corresponds to the factions returned by [[GetBaseFactions]]. Note that in ''Fallout: New Vegas'', ranks are largely unused are usually zero. |name = GetBaseRanks |returnType = array |referenceType = actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Target |Type = NPC-or-Actor |Optional = y}} }} ==Example== 
array_var Sunny_Ranks

Let Sunny_Ranks := SunnyREF.GetBaseRanks
Ar_Dump Sunny_Ranks would print (as in most vanilla cases, all zero): 
** Dumping Array #14 **
Refs: 1 Owner 0B: nvse-test.esp
[ 0.000000 ] : 0.000000
[ 1.000000 ] : 0.000000
[ 2.000000 ] : 0.000000
[ 3.000000 ] : 0.000000
[ 4.000000 ] : 0.000000
[ 5.000000 ] : 0.000000
==See Also== *[[GetBaseFactions]] *[[GetActiveRanks]] *[[GetBaseNthRank]] *[[GetNumRanks]] *[[Array Variables]] [[Category:Faction Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

revision.comment: italics
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns an [[Array Variable|array]] containing all leveled spells attached to the base form of the specified actor or [[calling reference]]. Note that leveled spells may be unused in vanilla ''Fallout: New Vegas''. |name = GetBaseSpellListLevSpells |returnType = array |referenceType = actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Target |Type = actor |Optional = y}} }} ==Example== 
array_var Base_Spells

let Base_Spells := PlayerREF.GetBaseSpellListLevSpells
==See Also== *[[GenericAddForm]] *[[GenericReplaceForm]] *[[GenericDeleteForm]] *[[GenericGetForm]] *[[Array Variables]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns an [[Array Variable|array]] containing all spells attached to the base form of the specified actor or [[calling reference]]. |name = GetBaseSpellListSpells |returnType = array |referenceType = actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Target |Type = actor |Optional = y}} }} ==Example== 
array_var Base_Spells

let Base_Spells := PlayerREF.GetBaseSpellListSpells
Ar_Dump Base_Spells would print something like: 
** Dumping Array #10 **
Refs: 1 Owner 0B: nvse-test.esp
[ 0.000000 ] :  (00000147)
Note: not all spells have names, but id 147 refers to the 'PipBoyLight' actor effect. ==See Also== *[[GenericAddForm]] *[[GenericReplaceForm]] *[[GenericDeleteForm]] *[[GenericGetForm]] *[[Array Variables]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the DDS file path to the specified biped icon for the specified object or [[calling reference]]. |name = GetBipedIconPath |returnType = Path:string |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = BipedPathCode |Type = int}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Object |Type = form |Optional = y}} }} ==Example== 
string_var male_biped_icon_path

let male_biped_icon_path := GetBipedIconPath 0, ArmorLeather

; male_biped_icon_path == "Interface\Icons\PipboyImages\Apparel\appearal_leather_armor.dds"
==Biped Path Codes== Specifies icon/model paths associated with biped objects, such as armor *0: Male Biped Path *1: Female Biped Path *2: Male Ground Path *3: Female Ground Path ==See Also== *[[SetBipedIconPathEx]] *[[GetModelPath]] *[[GetIconPath]] *[[String Variable]] *[[Let]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:Armor Functions]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Texture Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the NIF file path to the specified biped model for the specified object or [[calling reference]]. |name = GetBipedModelPath |returnType = Path:string |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = BipedPathCode |Type = int}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Object |Type = form |Optional = y}} }} ==Example== 
string_var male_biped_path

let male_biped_path := GetBipedModelPath 0, ArmorLeather

; male_biped_path == "Armor\LeatherArmor\M\OutfitM.NIF"
==Biped Path Codes== Specifies icon/model paths associated with biped objects, such as armor *0: Male Biped Path *1: Female Biped Path *2: Male Ground Path *3: Female Ground Path ==See Also== *[[SetBipedModelPathEx]] *[[GetModelPath]] *[[GetIconPath]] *[[String Variable]] *[[Let]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Armor Functions]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the value of the bit at the specified position in a bitmask. Bit indices start at 0 and should not be greater than 20- higher values are unreliable and prone to conversion. |name = GetBit |returnType = bool |referenceType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = BitMask |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = BitIndex |Type = int }} }} ==Example== 
int BitMask
int BitTwo

set BitMask to 11		  ; binary: 00001011
set BitTwo to GetBit BitMask, 1   ; 1
==See Also== *[[SetBit]] *[[ClearBit]] *[[GetEquipmentSlotsMask]] *[[GetWeaponFlags1]] *[[GetActorBaseFlagsLow]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Bit Functions]]

revision.comment: note + cat
revision.text: {{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Usable only within a [[User Defined Function]] script to return the other script that called the UDF. This could be useful for debugging or converted for use as an [[Array Variable|array]] map key. |name = GetCallingScript |returnType = script |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = |Type = }} |example = ref rCallingScript let rCallingScript := GetCallingScript PrintC "My UDF just got called by %i", rCallingScript }} ==Notes== *If the UDF is called within a dialogue or stage script of a quest, this function will return zero. ==See Also== *[[Call]] *[[User Defined Function]] *[[ToString]] *[[ToNumber]] *[[PrintToConsole|PrintC]] *[[GetCurrentScript]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Debugging]] [[Category:Script Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns true if the engine echoes to the console, else false. |name = GetConsoleEcho |returnType = EchoState:bool |arguments = |example = if GetConsoleEcho ; do some echoing endif }} ==See Also== *[[SetConsoleEcho]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the file name of current [[Con_SCOF|console output file]], or null (empty string: "") if there is none. |name = GetConsoleOutputFilename |alias = GetCOF |returnType = FileName:string |arguments = |example = string_var file_name let file_name := GetCOF }} ==See Also== *[[Con_SCOF]] *[[HasConsoleOutputFile]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Debugging]] [[Category:File Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the current package of the [[calling reference|calling]] or specified actor. |name = GetCurrentPackage |referenceType = Actor |returnType = package |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Target |Type = actorREF |Optional = optional }} }} ==Example== 
ref current_package

let current_package := SunnyREF.GetCurrentPackage
==See Also== *[[GetBasePackages]] *[[GetCurrentAIPackage]] *[[GetCurrentStablePackage]] *[[GetIsCurrentPackage]] *[[GetNthPackage]] *[[GetPackageCount]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:AI Functions]] [[Category:Package Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns an array of the path to the current objective targets. The path is an array of zero or more doors (called teleportLink) followed by the target itself. Works only on the current objective of the current quest for the player, so no parameters required. Will build the teleportLinks if needed, but activating the quest should have done that already. |name = GetCurrentQuestObjectiveTeleportLinks |returnType = array |arguments = }} ==Example== 
array_var Path

let Path := GetCurrentQuestObjectiveTeleportLinks
==See Also== *[[Array Variables]] *[[Let]] *[[GetCurrentQuestObjectiveTeleportLinksAlt]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

revision.comment: +cat
revision.text: {{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the current script, the form of the script that called this function. |name = GetCurrentScript |returnType = script |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = |Type = }} |example = ref rMe let rMe := GetCurrentScript }} ==See Also== *[[GetCallingScript]] (for the script that called a [[User Defined Function|UDF]]) [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Script Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the reference last selected in the game console. |name = GetDebugSelection |returnType = Selection:reference |referenceType = |arguments = }} ==Example== 
ref LastClicked

set LastClicked to GetDebugSelection
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Debugging]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns an [[Array Variable|array]] with elements matching all the [[Default Objects]] (forms). Default objects are viewable under the 'Gameplay' section of the GECK, or in GameForm.h (kDefaultObjectNames). |name = GetDefaultForms |returnType = array |referenceType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = |Type = }} }} ==Example== 
array_var Default_Forms

let Default_Forms := GetDefaultForms
Ar_Dump Default_Forms would print something like: 
** Dumping Array #32 **
Refs: 1 Owner 0B: nvse-test.esp
[ 0.000000 ] : Stimpak (00015169)
[ 1.000000 ] : Sugar Bombs (0008C551)
[ 2.000000 ] : Rad-X (00015168)
[ 3.000000 ] : RadAway (00015167)
[ 4.000000 ] : Med-X (00050F8F)
[ 5.000000 ] : Paralyzing Palm (0006B20B)
[ 6.000000 ] : Player Faction (0001B2A4)
[ 7.000000 ] : Mysterious Stranger (0005F9CE)
[ 8.000000 ] : Mysterious Stranger Faction (0007F505)
...
[ 33.000000 ] :  (0015FF5F)
==See Also== *[[GetNthDefaultForm]] *[[SetNthDefaultForm]] *[[Array Variables]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the character who is currently speaking in a conversation with the [[calling reference]]. The ''Actor'' argument is unused. When this function is used as a condition, will still return the FormID of the Dialogue Speaker, which may not be very useful. |name = GetDialogueSpeaker |alias = GDK |returnType = ref |referenceType = actorRefr |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Actor |Type = reference |optional = y}} |example = set MyQuest.Speaker to GetDialogueSpeaker }} =='''See Also'''== *[[GetDialogueTarget]] *[[GetDialogueSubject]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Dialogue Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = These 4 similar functions return the X/Y/Z coordinate or rotation to which the [[calling reference|calling]] [[IsLoadDoor|load door]] reference teleports. |name = GetDoorTeleportX/Y/Z/Rot |returnType = X/Y/Z/Rot:float |referenceType = LoadDoorRef |arguments = |example = }} ==Example== 
float fX
float fY
float fZ
float fRot

let fX   := SomeDoorRef.GetDoorTeleportX
let fY   := SomeDoorRef.GetDoorTeleportY
let fZ   := SomeDoorRef.GetDoorTeleportZ
let fRot := SomeDoorRef.GetDoorTeleportRot
== Notes == * This returns the coordinates of the marker this door teleports you to, ''not'' the teleport marker that is spawned outside of the door. ==See Also== *[[IsLoadDoor]] *[[SetDoorTeleport]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Movement Functions]] [[Category:Door Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the equipment flags used by the specified [[Armor]] as a combined value of the bits (int). Masks may be built by using [[ClearBit]]/[[SetBit]]. |name = GetEquipmentBipedMask |alias = GetEBM |returnType = Mask:int |referenceType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ToGetMaskOf |Type = armor }} }} ==Bit Fields== {| class="wikitable" |- ! Flag ! Bit ! Equipment Mask |- | 4 | 2 | HasBackpack |- | 8 | 3 | Medium Armor |- | 32 | 5 | Power Armor |- | 64 | 6 | Non-playable |- | 128 | 7 | HeavyArmor |} Other bits are either unused or unknown. ==Example== 
int Mask
let Mask := GetEquipmentBipedMask ArmorTeslaPower ; * 160 (Heavy + Power)
To check if some item has the "HeavyArmor" flag, you could use: 
if Mask == (SetBit Mask, 7)
   ; It is "Heavy armor". 
   ; (mask compares equal to self with bit7 set, so bit7 is already set)
endif
You can alternatively use [[NVSE Expressions|'C' style bit operators]] in NVSE aware contexts, such as [[let]] or [[eval]]: 
if eval Mask & (1 << 7)
    ; Bit7 is set, so the item is flagged as "Heavy armor"
endif
==See Also== *[[SetEquipmentBipedMask]] *[[GetEquipmentSlotsMask]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Armor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the slots used by the biped form as a [[bitmask]] int, for the specified [[Base Form]]. If a [[calling reference]] is passed, its base form will be used instead. The mask is a bitmask that should be built by using [[ClearBit]]/[[SetBit]].
