The order of bits is as specified for the slot id of [[GetEquippedObject]] or as defined in the GECK slot list of an armor, 0="head" to 19="body addon 3". |name = GetEquipmentSlotsMask |alias = GetESM |returnType = BitMask:int |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ToGetMaskOf |Type = baseForm |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== To check if some equipment is using the "eyeglasses" slot (bit 11): 
int BitMask
set BitMask to GetEquipmentSlotsMask SomeItemBaseForm

if (GetBit BitMask, 11)
    ; SomeItemBaseForm uses the "eyeglasses" slot.
endif
NVSE aware functions, such as [[Let]] and [[Eval]] also allow 'C' style bit operators (see [[NVSE Expressions]]), an alternative method to check if the 11th bit is set is: 
if eval BitMask & (1 << 11)
    ; Bit 11 is set, so the item uses the "eyeglasses" slot
endif
==See Also== *[[SetEquipmentSlotsMask]] *[[GetEquipmentBipedMask]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Armor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns a combined value of the bits(int) of the equipped [[weapon mod]]s for the currently equipped [[weapon]] of the [[calling reference]]. |name = GetEquippedWeaponModFlags |returnType = BitMask:int |referenceType = actorReference |arguments = }} ==Example== 
int ModsBitMask
set ModsBitMask to PlayerREF.GetEquippedWeaponModFlags

if ModsBitMask == 3  ;first two weapon mods are equipped.

elseif ModsBitMask == 7  ;all weapon mods are equipped.

elseif ModsBitMask == 2  ;only the second weapon mod is equipped.

endif
==Weapon Mod Flags== {| class="wikitable" |- ! Flag ! Bit ! Weapon Mod |- | 1 | 0 | Weapon Mod 1 |- | 2 | 1 | Weapon Mod 2 |- | 4 | 2 | Weapon Mod 3 |} ==Notes== *If you're looking for a compatible general use case function, consider using [[GetAllSlotMods]] instead, as it supports both the Vanilla Item Mod system, as well as the [[Extended Item Mods System]] introduced in [[VanillaPlugins+|Plugins+]] ==See Also== *[[SetBit]] *[[ClearBit]] *[[GetWeaponFlags1]] *[[GetWeaponFlags2]] *[[SetEquippedWeaponModFlags]] *[[SetWeaponRefModFlags]] *[[GetWeaponRefModFlags]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns an [[Array Variable|array]] containing all the female rank names (as [[string]]s) of the specified [[Faction]]. Note that most factions in ''Fallout: New Vegas'' do not have rank names. |name = GetFactionRankFemaleNames |returnType = array |referenceType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Target |Type = actor |Optional = y}} }} ==Example==

let Rank_Names := GetFactionRankNames VBrotherhoodOfSteelFaction
Ar_Dump Rank_Names would print: 
** Dumping Array #18 **
Refs: 1 Owner 0B: nvse-test.esp
[ 0.000000 ] : Initiate
[ 1.000000 ] : Senior Initiate
[ 2.000000 ] : Apprentice
[ 3.000000 ] : Journeyman Scribe or Knight
[ 4.000000 ] : Scribe or Knight
[ 5.000000 ] : Senior Scribe or Knight
[ 6.000000 ] : Paladin

[ 8.000000 ] : Elder
==See Also== *[[GetFactionRankNames]] *[[GetBaseRanks]] *[[Array Variables]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Faction Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:Name Functions]]

revision.comment: italics
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the path to the main directory of ''Fallout: New Vegas'' on the user's system. |name = GetFalloutDirectory |returnType = string |arguments = }} ==Example== 
string_var FO_dir

let FO_dir := GetFalloutDirectory

; FO_dir == "E:\STEAM\steamapps\common\Fallout New Vegas\"
==See Also== *[[String Variable]] *[[Let]] *[[Debug Dumps]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:Debugging]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the first entry in an array of [[inventory reference]]s to all objects of the specified type in the calling container. |name = GetFirstRefForItem |referenceType = container |returnType = ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Type |Type = object}} }} ==Example== 
ref rTemp

let rTemp := PlayerREF.GetFirstRefForItem WeapNV9mmPistol

; rTemp might equal: '9mm Pistol (FF001376)'
==See Also== *[[GetNextRefForItem]] *[[GetInvRefsForItem]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Reference Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = As [[GetFlagsLow]], but returns the second set of individual flags for the specified form, which may be of any type. Beware that not all flags should be modified, or have a (known) purpose, and their effects may differ dependent on form type. |name = GetFlagsHigh |returnType = Mask:int |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Target |Type = form |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== 
int FlagsMask
set FlagsMask to GetFlagsHigh Weap10mmPistol
==Bit fields== This is adapted from FNVEdit. As a minimum, one should not make changes to anything labelled "Runtime". {| class="wikitable" |- ! Flag ! Bit ! Setting |- | 1 | 0 | Random Anim Start / High Priority LOD |- | 2 | 1 | Dangerous / Off limits (Interior cell) / Radio Station (Talking Activator) |- | 4 | 2 | Compressed |- | 8 | 3 | Can't wait / Platform Specific Texture / Dead |- | 16 | 4 | Unknown 21 |- | 32 | 5 | Load Started (Runtime) // set when beginning to load the form from save |- | 64 | 6 | Unknown 23 |- | 128 | 7 | Unknown 24 // Runtime might use it for "Not dead" on non actors. |- | 256 | 8 | Destructible (Runtime) |- | 512 | 9 | Obstacle / No AI Acquire |- | 1024 | 10 | NavMesh Generation - Filter |- | 2048 | 11 | NavMesh Generation - Bounding Box |- | 4096 | 12 | Non-Pipboy / Reflected by Auto Water |- | 8192 | 13 | Child Can Use / Refracted by Auto Water |- | 16384 | 14 | NavMesh Generation - Ground |- | 32768 | 15 | Multibound |} ==See Also== *[[SetFlagsHigh]] *[[GetFlagsLow]] *[[GetFormFlag]] *[[GetWeaponFlags1]] *[[GetWeaponFlags2]] *[[GetActorBaseFlagsLow]] *[[GetActorBaseFlagsHigh]] *[[SetBit]] *[[ClearBit]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the first set of individual flags for the specified form, which may be of any type as a combined value of the bits (int). Beware that not all flags should be modified, or have a (known) purpose, and their effects may differ dependent on form type. See [[GetEquipmentSlotsMask]] for an example of checking for a specific bit. |name = GetFlagsLow |returnType = Mask:int |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Target |Type = form |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== 
int FlagsMask
set FlagsMask to GetFlagsLow Weap10mmPistol
==Bit fields== This is adapted from FNVEdit. As a minimum, one should not make changes to anything labelled "Runtime". {| class="wikitable" |- ! Flag ! Bit ! Setting |- | 1 | 0 | ESM |- | 2 | 1 | Unknown 1 |- | 4 | 2 | Unknown 2 (Runtime) / Plugin selected (Editor) |- | 8 | 3 | Form cannot be saved (Runtime) / Plugin active (Editor) |- | 16 | 4 | Form initialized (Runtime) / Plugin cannot be active or selected (Editor) |- | 32 | 5 | Deleted |- | 64 | 6 | Border Region / Has Tree LOD / Constant / Hidden From Local Map / Plugin Endian |- | 128 | 7 | Turn Off Fire |- | 256 | 8 | Inaccessible |- | 512 | 9 | Casts shadows / On Local Map / Motion Blur |- | 1024 | 10 | Quest item / Persistent reference |- | 2048 | 11 | Initially disabled |- | 4096 | 12 | Ignored |- | 8192 | 13 | No Voice Filter |- | 16384 | 14 | Cannot Save (Runtime) |- | 32768 | 15 | Visible when distant |} ==See Also== *[[SetFlagsLow]] *[[GetFlagsHigh]] *[[GetFormFlag]] *[[GetWeaponFlags1]] *[[GetWeaponFlags2]] *[[GetActorBaseFlagsLow]] *[[GetActorBaseFlagsHigh]] *[[SetBit]] *[[ClearBit]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the [[FormID]] number as a [[string]] in hexadecimal for the specified form ([[Reference]] or [[Base Form]]). |name = GetFormIDString |returnType = string |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ToGet |Type = form }} |referenceType = |example = string_var formid let formid := GetFormIDString SunnyREF
; if you wanted to convert this to an int, you can use: int intFormID let intFormID := ToNumber formid, 1 }} ==Notes== *You can use this to convert a form to a string/int for use as an array map key. However, beware that FormIDs may change on game load, dependent on load order. *Will not compile when you pass an explicit form or ref as the parameter, only a [[ref var]]. ==See Also== *[[StringToRef]] *[[GetRawFormIDString]] *[[ToNumber]] *[[ToString]] *[[String Variable]] *[[Array Variable]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:ID Functions]] [[Category:String Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the effective value of the uGridsToLoad ini setting. |name = GetGridsToLoad |returnType = int |arguments = }} ==See Also== *[[OutputLocalMapPicturesOverride]] *[[SetOutputLocalMapPicturesGrids]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the specified RGB int value (0=All, 1=Red, 2=Green, 3=Blue) for the specified NPC or [[calling reference]]. |name = GetHairColor |returnType = int |referenceType = Actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ColorCode |Type = int}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Actor |Type = NPC |Optional = y}} |example = }} ==Example== 
int HairRedValue

let HairRedValue := SunnyREF.GetHairColor 1
==Note== The number returned by GetHairColor 0, ie what one would expect to be the full RGB value, is a 7-digit decimal representation of a 6-digit hexadecimal number. This in turn is a concat of 3 separate 2-digit hex numbers, each representing a color - ordered blue, green, red (BGR). It's this way in the vanilla game as well. To convert the result of GetHairColor 0 to separate integers between 0 and 255 for each color, you have to convert it to hex and re-convert the three 2-digit hex numbers back to decimal: 
let iResult := someRef.GetHairColor 0
let svHex := NumToHex iResult, 6
let iBlue := ToNumber svHex[0:1], 1
let iGreen := ToNumber svHex[2:3], 1
let iRed := ToNumber svHex[4:5], 1
==See Also== *[[SetHairColor]] *[[GetHair]] *[[GetHairLength]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Hair Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns an [[Array Variable|array]] containing all the head parts attached the [[calling reference]] or specified NPC. |name = GetHeadParts |returnType = array |referenceType = Actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Target |Type = NPC |Optional = y}} }} ==Example== 
array_var Head_Parts

let Head_Parts := EasyPeteRef.GetHeadParts
Ar_Dump Head_Parts would print something like: 
** Dumping Array #24 **
Refs: 1 Owner 0B: nvse-test.esp
[ 0.000000 ] : Old Beard (0009F923)
==See Also== *[[GenericAddForm]] *[[GenericReplaceForm]] *[[GenericDeleteForm]] *[[GenericGetForm]] *[[GetHair]] *[[GetEyes]] *[[Array Variables]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the file path to the DDS icon texture for the specified object or [[calling reference]]. |name = GetIconPath |returnType = Path:string |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Object |Type = form |Optional = y}} }} ==Example== 
string_var icon_path

let icon_path := GetIconPath Vodka

; icon_path == "Interface\Icons\PipboyImages\Items\items_vodka.dds"
==See Also== *[[SetIconPath]] *[[GetTexturePath]] *[[GetModelPath]] *[[GetBipedModelPath]] *[[String Variable]] *[[Let]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:Texture Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Similar to [[GetInSameCell]], but checks a grid of cells, making it useful for exteriors. Returns true (1) if the specified [[reference]] is within the grid of cells, the size of which is specified by depth. Depth defaults to 0, a value of -1 will use the uGridsToLoad setting. |name = GetInGrid |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Target |Type = reference}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = CellDepth |Type = int |Optional = y}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = IncludeTakenRefs |Type = bool |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== 
if GetInGrid SunnyREF, 1
   ; Sunny is in the same or a next cell to the player (depth 1 = 3x3 cell grid)

if GetInGrid SunnyREF, -1
   ; Sunny is within uGrid range of the player
==Notes== *This function works when checking if a reference is in a interior cell. *For a 3x3 grid (depth 1), the reference is in the middle cell of an edge of the grid, not the center cell of the grid itself. For a 5x5 grid (depth 2), another layer of cells are added around the 3x3 grid and the reference will be one cell away from the edge of the grid that is checked - again, not the center of the grid itself. ==See Also== *[[GetInGridInCell]] *[[GetInSameCell]] / [[GetInCell]] *[[GetRefs]] / [[GetFirstRef]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]]

revision.comment: add Jazzisparis' note
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = As [[GetInGrid]], but the center cell of the grid is specified rather than using that of the player. |name = GetInGridInCell |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Center |Type = cell}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Target |Type = reference}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = CellDepth |Type = int |Optional = y}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = IncludeTakenRefs |Type = bool |Optional = y }} }} ==Notes== * Note that exterior cells are non-persistent and are only valid when within the current loaded grid. Calling the function with a cell that isn't currently loaded would therefore crash the script. To avoid this, check if the exterior cell is loaded before calling the function: if eval (Springvale && GetInGridInCell Springvale PlayerREF 1) ; ... endif
==See Also== *[[GetInGrid]] *[[GetInCell]] *[[GetRefsInCell]] / [[GetFirstRefInCell]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Cell Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns an [[array]] of [[Inventory Reference]]s to objects that are instances of the specified [[Base Form]] in the calling container. |name = GetInvRefsForItem |referenceType = container |returnType = array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Type |Type = baseform}} }} ==Example== 
array_var MyArray

let MyArray := PlayerREF.GetInvRefsForItem WeapNV9mmPistol

; ar_dump might, for example return:
; [ 0.000000 ] : 9mm Pistol (FF001373)
; [ 1.000000 ] : 9mm Pistol (FF001374)
; [ 2.000000 ] : 9mm Pistol (FF001375)
==See Also== *[[GetFirstRefForItem]] *[[GetNextRefForItem]] *[[RemoveItem]] *[[RemoveItemTarget]] *[[RemoveAllTypedItems]] *[[RemoveAllItems]] *[[Array Variables]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Reference Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the name of the key specified by its [[IsKeyPressed|DirectInput scan code number]]. |name = GetKeyName |returnType = string |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = KeyCode |Type = int}} }} ==Example== 
string_var key_name

let key_name := GetKeyName 45 ; 'X'
==See Also== *[[IsKeyPressed]] *[[GetKeyPress]] *[[String Variable]] *[[Let]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:Debugging]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns an [[Array Variable|array]] with entries matching the contents of a specified leveled character. |name = GetLevCharacterRefs |returnType = array |referenceType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Source |Type = LeveledCharacter}} }} ==Example== 
array_var Freeside_Encounters

let Freeside_Encounters := GetLevCharacterRefs VarFreesideThug
Ar_Dump Freeside_Encounters would print: 
** Dumping Array #22 **
Refs: 1 Owner 0B: nvse-test.esp
[ 0.000000 ] : Freeside Thug (00118A8B)
[ 1.000000 ] : Freeside Thug (00118A8C)
[ 2.000000 ] : Freeside Thug (00118A82)
[ 3.000000 ] : Freeside Thug (00118A83)
[ 4.000000 ] : Freeside Thug (00118A84)
[ 5.000000 ] : Freeside Thug (00118A8D)
[ 6.000000 ] : Freeside Thug (00118A89)
[ 7.000000 ] : Freeside Thug (00118A8A)
==See Also== *[[GetLevCreatureRefs]] *[[GetListForms]] *[[GenericAddForm]] *[[GenericReplaceForm]] *[[GenericDeleteForm]] *[[GenericGetForm]] *[[Array Variables]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns an [[Array Variable|array]] with entries matching the contents of a specified [[LeveledCreature]]. |name = GetLevCreatureRefs |returnType = array |referenceType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Source |Type = LeveledCreature}} }} ==Example== 
array_var Gecko_Encounters

let Gecko_Encounters := GetLevCreatureRefs EncWastelandNVGecko
Ar_Dump Gecko_Encounters would print: 
** Dumping Array #21 **
Refs: 1 Owner 0B: nvse-test.esp
[ 0.000000 ] : Gecko (0010CD73)
[ 1.000000 ] : Golden Gecko (00118D61)
[ 2.000000 ] : Fire Gecko (00113246)
==See Also== *[[GetLevCharacterRefs]] *[[GetListForms]] *[[GenericAddForm]] *[[GenericReplaceForm]] *[[GenericDeleteForm]] *[[GenericGetForm]] *[[Array Variables]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:Creature Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns an [[Array Variable|array]] with entries matching the specified form list. |name = GetListForms |returnType = array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ToMatch |Type = formlist}} }} ==Example== 
array_var Ammo_Forms

let Ammo_Forms := GetListForms AmmoList357Magnum
This function is convenient for looping through form lists: 
array_var Entry
ref rForm

foreach Entry <- (GetListForms SomeFormList)
    let rForm := *Entry
    ; rForm will be each form in the form list
loop
==See Also== *[[Foreach]] *[[Let]] *[[Array Variables]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:List Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns as an int, the local index (form ID without mod index prefix) for the [[calling reference]] or passed object. |name = GetLocalRefIndex |returnType = int |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Target |Type = object |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== 
int iIndex

set iIndex to SunnyREF.GetLocalRefIndex
==See Also== *[[GetFormIDString]] *[[GetSourceModIndex]] *[[BuildRef]] *[[NumToHex]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]]

revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the [[Mod Local Data]] value associated with the specified key for the mod to which the calling script belongs. If no data exists for the key, returns 0. |name = GetModLocalData |returnType = Multi |referenceType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Key |Type = string}} |example = ref rActor let rActor := GetModLocalData "Player's Bestie" }}
=='''See Also'''== *[[Mod Local Data]] *[[SetModLocalData]] *[[ModLocalDataExists]] *[[RemoveModLocalData]] *[[GetAllModLocalData]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the file path to the NIF model for the specified object or [[calling reference]]. |name = GetModelPath |returnType = Path:string |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Object |Type = form |Optional = y}} }} ==Example== 
string_var model_path

let model_path := GetModelPath Vodka

; model_path == "Clutter\Food\Vodka01.NIF"
==See Also== *[[SetModelPath]] *[[GetBipedModelPath]] *[[GetGrassModel]] / [[SetGrassModel]] *[[GetImpactDataModel]] *[[GetRaceBodyModelPath]] *[[GetRaceHeadModelPath]] *[[GetScopeModelPath]] *[[GetSkeletonModel]] *[[GetIconPath]] *[[String Variable]] *[[Let]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the height attribute of the specified NPC or [[calling reference]]. |name = GetNPCHeight |returnType = float |referenceType = Actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Actor |Type = NPC |Optional = y}} |example = }} ==Example== 
float SunnyHeight
let SunnyHeight := SunnyREF.GetNPCHeight
==See Also== *[[SetNPCHeight]] *[[GetNPCWeight]] *[[GetScale]] *[[GetHairLength]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]]

It is unclear if this attribute has any affect in the vanilla game. |name = GetNPCWeight |returnType = float |referenceType = Actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Actor |Type = NPC |Optional = y}} |example = }} ==Example== 
float SunnyWeight
let SunnyWeight := SunnyREF.GetNPCWeight

revision.comment: Added to Category:Condition Functions
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the beta number of the installed version of NVSE. A non beta version will return 0. |name = GetNVSEBeta |returnType = int |example = if GetNVSEBeta != 0 && GetNVSEBeta < 3 MessageBoxEx "This mod requires beta 3 or later of NVSE 4, please upgrade" endif }} ==See Also== *[[GetNVSEVersion]] *[[GetNVSERevision]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

revision.comment: Added to Category:Condition Functions
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the revision number of the installed version of NVSE. |name = GetNVSERevision |returnType = int |example = if GetNVSEVersion == 4 && GetNVSERevision < 3 MessageBoxEx "This mod requires revision 3 or later of NVSE 4, please upgrade" endif }} ==See Also== *[[GetNVSEVersion]] *[[GetNVSEBeta]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the installed version of NVSE. |name = GetNVSEVersion |returnType = int |example = if GetNVSEVersion < 4 MessageBoxEx "This mod requires NVSE 4 or later, please upgrade" endif }} ==Usage Warning== Scripts will fail during game if they reference any features added by an NVSE version later than that installed by the user, so this function is typically used to warn about this. Therefore, this check should be added to a script that does not use any other NVSE functions, otherwise it may never run. Example: 
let x := 5
; since 'let' is added by NVSE 4, this script crashes without it

if GetNVSEVersion < 4 ; * pointless
    MessageBoxEx "This mod requires NVSE 4, but you won't see this warning without it because the containing script fails"
endif
==See Also== *[[Debug Dumps]] *[[GetNVSERevision]] *[[GetNVSEBeta]] *[[GetPluginVersion]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Debugging]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the name of the specified object as a [[string]]. Returns "" if a name for the form could not be found. |name = GetName |returnType = string |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Named |Type = object }} |referenceType = }} ==Example== 
string_var my_name
let my_name := GetName SunnyREF ; * "Sunny Smiles"
==Notes== * This function doesn't return reference names set by [[SetRefName]]. [[LNGetName]] should be used for this instead. ==See Also== *[[ToString]] (shorthand: '$') often does the same thing. *[[SetName]] *[[LNGetName]] *[[LNSetName]] *[[CompareNames]] *[[SetActorFullName]] *[[SetActorFullNameAlt]] *[[String Variable]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:Name Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the next entry in an array of [[inventory reference]]s to all objects of the specified type in the calling container, and should be called after [[GetFirstRefForItem]], and during the same frame. |name = GetNextRefForItem |referenceType = container |returnType = temp ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Type |Type = object}} }} ==Example== 
ref rTemp

let rTemp := PlayerREF.GetFirstRefForItem WeapNV9mmPistol

while rTemp != 0
    ; do something 
    let rTemp := PlayerREF.GetNextRefForItem WeapNV9mmPistol
loop
==See Also== *[[GetFirstRefForItem]] *[[GetInvRefsForItem]] (to get an array with all inventory refs) [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Reference Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the form at the specified index from the list of [[Default Objects]] (forms). Default objects are viewable under the 'Gameplay' section of the GECK, or in GameForm.h (kDefaultObjectNames). |name = GetNthDefaultForm |returnType = form |referenceType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Index |Type = int}} }} ==Example== 
ref DefForm

let DefForms := GetNthDefaultForm 5
==See Also== *[[GetDefaultForms]] *[[SetNthDefaultForm]] *[[Array Variables]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

revision.comment: Created. Source OBSE docs.
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the Nth explicit ref in the calling script or passed script object. Explicit references are any objects mentioned by name in the script; for instance, if a script contains an "OnHit Player" block or a "Player.AddItem" command, its explicit references will include the player. |name = GetNthExplicitRef |returnType = ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Index |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Script |Type = script |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== 
ref rFive
let rFive := GetNthExplicitRef 5, SomeScript
==See Also== *[[GetNumExplicitRefs]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Script Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the title of the nth rank in [[Faction]]. By default returns the male title, the optional [[boolean]] argument returns the female one. |name = GetNthFactionRankName |returnType = RankName:string |referenceType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Target |Type = faction}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = WhichRank |Type = int}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Female |Type = bool |Optional = y}} }} ==Example== 
string_var rank_name

let rank_name := GetNthFactionRankName KadathFaction, 3
==See Also== *[[SetNthFactionRankNameEX]] *[[GetFactionRankNames]] *[[GetFactionRankFemaleNames]] *[[GetNumRanks]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:Faction Functions]] [[Category:Name Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the filename of the active mod loaded at the specified index as a [[string]]. |name = GetNthModName |returnType = string |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ModIndex |Type = int}}}} ==Example== 
string_var mod_2A
let mod_2A := GetNthModName 42

; mod_2A == "SomeFoxyModName.esp"
==Note== *The index is specified as an int, not a hexadecimal string. ==See Also== *[[GetModName]] *[[GetModIndex]] (the reverse, to get the index from the name) *[[GetSourceModIndex]] (get index for a form) *[[GetNumLoadedMods]] *[[GetModLocalData]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Debugging]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Name Functions]] [[Category:String Functions]]

revision.comment: Created. Source OBSE docs
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the number of explicit references in the calling script or passed script object. Explicit references are any objects mentioned by name in the script; for instance, if a script contains an "OnHit Player" block or a "Player.AddItem" command, its explicit references will include the player. |name = GetNumExplicitRefs |returnType = NumRefs:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Script |Type = script |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== 
int iNumRefs
let iNumRefs := GetNumExplicitRefs ; * for this script

; or

let iNumRefs := GetNumExplicitRefs SomeOtherScript
==See Also== *[[GetNthExplicitRef]] *[[GetCurrentScript]] *[[GetCallingScript]] *[[GetScript]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Script Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the number of ranks for the specified faction. |name = GetNumRanks |returnType = int |referenceType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Target |Type = faction |Optional = }} }} ==Example== 
int NumRanks

let NumRanks := GetNumRanks VBrotherhoodOfSteelFaction
==See Also== *[[GetActiveFactions]] *[[GetBaseRanks]] *[[GetBaseNthRank]] [[Category:Faction Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the value of a numeric setting. Setting name is of the format "SettingName:CategoryName" where CategoryName is the header under which the setting appears in the .ini file. |name = GetNumericIniSetting |returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = SettingName |Type = string }} |example = }} ==See Also== *[[SetNumericIniSetting]] *[[GetStringGameSetting]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:INI Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the start location of a [[package]]. |name = GetPackageLocation |alias = GetPackageLocationReference |returnType = ref |referenceType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ToCheck |Type = package }} |example = ref rStart set rStart to GetPackageLocation SomePackage }} ==See Also== *[[SetPackageLocationReference]] *[[GetPackageLocationRadius]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:AI Functions]] [[Category:Package Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the start location radius of a [[package]]. |name = GetPackageLocationRadius |returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = package |Type = form }} |example = float fRadius set fRadius to GetPackageLocationRadius SomePackage }} ==See Also== *[[SetPackageLocationRadius]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:AI Functions]] [[Category:Package Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the distance/count of a [[package]]. |name = GetPackageTargetDistance |alias = GetPackageTargetCount |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = package |Type = form }} |example = int iDist set iDist to GetPackageTargetDistance MyEscortPackage }} ==See Also== *[[SetPackageTargetDistance]] *[[GetPackageLocationRadius]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:AI Functions]] [[Category:Package Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns true (1) if the [[calling reference]] and passed reference cross reference each other, through the form variables of the specified [[:Category:Tokens|token]] in their inventories. |name = GetPaired |referenceType = actor |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Token |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Another |Type = actor }} }} ==Example== 
; Context:
SunnyREF.SetTokenRef MarriageToken, EasyPeteREF
EasyPeteREF.SetTokenRef MarriageToken, SunnyREF

; Example:
SunnyREF.GetPaired MarriageToken, EasyPeteREF ; * will return 1
==See Also== *[[SetTokenRef]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Token Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

revision.comment: Added to Category:Condition Functions
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the rank of the specified [[:Category:Perk|Perk]]. This function may only be called on the player. Returns -1 if the perk argument or [[calling reference]] is null. Returns 0 if the player does not have the perk, otherwise returns an int with the rank starting at 1. |name = GetPerkRank |returnType = int |referenceType = PlayerREF |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Target |Type = perk}} }} ==Example== 
int iRank

let iRank := PlayerREF.GetPerkRank LordDeathChallengePerk
==See Also== *[[GetAltPerkRank]] (for companion perks) *[[HasPerk]] *[[AddPerk]] [[Category:Perk Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the [[base form]] for the [[ammo]] currently held in the [[player]]'s [[weapon]]. |name = GetPlayerCurrentAmmo |returnType = Ammo:baseform |referenceType = |arguments = |example = ref currentAmmo set currentAmmo to GetPlayerCurrentAmmo }} =='''See Also'''== *[[GetCurrentAmmo]] *[[GetPlayerCurrentAmmoRounds]] *[[SetPlayerCurrentAmmoRounds]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:Ammo Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the current number of rounds in the clip of the player's weapon. |name = GetPlayerCurrentAmmoRounds |returnType = int |referenceType = ref |arguments = |example = int currentAmmo set currentAmmo to GetPlayerCurrentAmmoRounds }} [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:Ammo Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the version number of the specified NVSE plugin, or -1 if the plugin is not loaded. Note that plugin names are set during registration and displayed in the file, nvse.log, it is not the file name. |name = GetPluginVersion |referenceType = |returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = PluginName |Type = string }} |example =
if (GetPluginVersion "JIP NVSE Plugin") < 56
    MessageBoxEx "You need to install version 56 of JIP LN NVSE Plugin"
endif
}} ==Note== *The return value is usually an integer, i.e. it has no decimal point. For example, to check if the version of [[ShowOff NVSE]] is greater or equal to 1.80, one should check for that value * 100 to get rid of the decimal points, which would look like this: 
 if (GetPluginVersion "ShowOffNVSE Plugin") >= 180
    ; do stuff
 endif
**However, there is a sole exception with [[JIP LN NVSE]], which returns a float. For example, if checking for version 57.30 or greater, then 57.30 is the number that must be used in the script. *Below is a list of known plugin names: {{NVSE_Plugins}} ==See Also== *[[IsPluginInstalled]] *[[GetNVSEVersion]] *[[IsModLoaded]] *[[GetNexusModVersion]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:File Functions]]

revision.comment: Added Actor Functions category.
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the race of the specified actor |name = GetRace |returnType = race |referenceType = Actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Actor |Type = NPC |Optional = y}} }} ==Example== 
ref rRace
let rRace := GetRace SomeNPC
if rRace == Ghoul
    ; do something ghoulish
endif

let rRace := SomeNPCRef.GetRace
if rRace == Hispanic
  ; whatever
endif
==See Also== *[[GetIsRace]] (to return a boolean) *[[GetRaceName]] (to return a string) [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns an [[Array Variable|array]] containing all the eyes available to the specified race. |name = GetRaceEyes |returnType = array |referenceType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Target |Type = race}} }} ==Example== 
array_var Race_Eyes

let Race_Eyes := GetRaceEyes Caucasian
Ar_Dump Race_Eyes would print something like: 
** Dumping Array #20 **
Refs: 1 Owner 0B: nvse-test.esp
[ 0.000000 ] : Blue (00004253)
[ 1.000000 ] : Dark Brown (00004256)
[ 2.000000 ] : Hazel (00004255)
[ 3.000000 ] : Green (00004252)
==See Also== *[[GetRaceHairs]] *[[GenericAddForm]] *[[GenericReplaceForm]] *[[GenericDeleteForm]] *[[GenericGetForm]] *[[GetRace]] *[[Array Variables]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:Eye Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns an [[Array Variable|array]] containing all the [[Hair]]s available to the specified race. |name = GetRaceHairs |returnType = array |referenceType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Target |Type = race}} }} ==Example== 
array_var Race_Hairs

let Race_Hairs := GetRaceHairs Caucasian
[[Ar_Dump]] Race_Hairs would print something like: 
** Dumping Array #19 **
Refs: 1 Owner 0B: nvse-test.esp
[ 0.000000 ] : Clean Cut (00014B90)
[ 1.000000 ] : Tunnel Snake (000218C6)
[ 2.000000 ] : Pompadour (000218C7)
[ 3.000000 ] : Sarge (0002242B)
[ 4.000000 ] : Domestic Goddess (00022E4E)
[ 5.000000 ] : No Nonsense (00022E4F)
[ 6.000000 ] : Prim 'N Proper (00022E50)
... and so on
==See Also== *[[GetRaceEyes]] *[[GenericAddForm]] *[[GenericReplaceForm]] *[[GenericDeleteForm]] *[[GenericGetForm]] *[[GetRace]] *[[Array Variables]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:Hair Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the name of the [[Race]] of the specified [[Actor]] ([[Base Form]]), as a [[string]]. |name = GetRaceName |returnType = string |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Actor |Type = NPC }} }} ==Example== 
string_var my_string
let my_message := GetRaceName SomeNPC
MessageEx "The race of %n is %z", SomeNPC, my_string
==See Also== *[[GetRace]] *[[String Variable]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:Name Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the [[FormID]] number as a [[string]] in hexadecimal for the specified [[ref variable]] or [[array]] element, regardless of whether or not it is a valid FormID. |name = GetRawFormIDString |returnType = string |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ToGet |Type = Multi }} |referenceType = |example = string_var formid let formid := GetRawFormIDString MyArray[0] }} ==Notes== *You can use this to convert a form for use as an array map key. However, beware that FormIDs may change on game load, dependent on load order. *The function will fail in-game (return 00000000) when you pass an explicit form or reference as the parameter. It will work with a ref var or an array element. ==See Also== *[[GetFormIDString]] *[[StringToRef]] *[[ToNumber]] *[[ToString]] *[[String Variable]] *[[Array Variable]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:ID Functions]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the value of a 'ref' variable specified by name for the calling reference or specified scriptable object / quest. |name = GetRefVariable |returnType = form |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = VarName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Parent |Type = Object |Optional = y }} |referenceType = ref }} ==Example== 
ref rActor
ref rHome

let rHome := rActor.GetRefVariable "rHomeMarker"

; Alternatively, (or required if rActor holds a base form): 

let rHome := GetRefVariable "rHomeMarker", rActor
==See Also== *[[GetVariable]] (To get a numeric variable value instead) *[[GetArrayVariable]] (and for arrays) *[[HasVariable]] (to check if a variable exists at all) *[[ShowVars]] / [[Con_ShowVars]] (to print all variables/values to console) *[[SetVariable]] (Added by NVSE 4.5.6) *[[SetRefVariable]] (Aded by NVSE 4.5.6) [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Script Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Similar to using [[GetFirstRef]] and [[GetNextRef]], and follows the same syntax. Returns an [[Array Variable|array]] containing all the [[Reference|references]] in the currently loaded cell(s), optional only of a specific [[Form Type IDs|form type]]. {{Template:GetRefsEtc_Description}} |name = GetRefs |referenceType = reference |returnType = array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = FormTypeCode |Type = int |Optional = y |Value = 0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = CellDepth |Type = int |Optional = y |Value = 0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = IncludeTakenRefs |Type = bool |Optional = y |Value = 0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = maxDistance |Type = float |Optional = y |Value = 0 }} }} ==Example== 
; Simple example
array_var Cell_Refs

let Cell_Refs:= GetRefs

; Suppose you want all the actors currently loaded

array_var All_Actors

let All_Actors := GetRefs 200, -1
==See Also== *[[GetActorsByProcessingLevel]] (New method to loop through actors) *[[GetRefsInCell]] (for cells other than the player's current) *[[GetFirstRef]] / [[GetNextRef]] (the 'old' method) *[[While]] (Useful for looping through the returned array) *[[Let]] *[[Array Variables]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

revision.comment: add maxDistance arg
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Similar to using [[GetFirstRefInCell]] and [[GetNextRef]], and follows the same syntax. Returns an [[Array Variable|array]] containing all the [[Reference|references]] in the specified cell(s), optional only of a specific [[Form Type IDs|form type]]. {{Template:GetRefsEtc_Description}} |name = GetRefsInCell |referenceType = reference |returnType = array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Target |Type = Cell }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = FormTypeCode |Type = int |Optional = y |Value = 0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = CellDepth |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = IncludeTakenRefs |Type = bool |Optional = y |Value = 0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = maxDistance |Type = float |Optional = y |Value = 0 }} }} ==Example== 
; To get all the actors in the Goodsprings prospector saloon
array_var Saloon_Refs

let Saloon_Refs := GetRefsInCell GSProspectorSaloonInterior, 200
==See Also== *[[GetRefs]] (to use the player's current cell) *[[GetFirstRefInCell]] / [[GetNextRef]] (the 'old' method) *[[Foreach]] (Useful for looping through the returned array) *[[Let]] *[[Array Variables]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:Cell Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the path to the scope model of the specified weapon. |name = GetScopeModelPath |returnType = string |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ToGetScopeOf |Type = weapon |Optional = }} |referenceType = }} ==Example== 
string_var scope_path

let scope_path := GetScopeModelPath WeapSniperRifle
==See Also== *[[GetWeaponHasScope]] *[[SetScopeModelPath]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the value of the specified string game setting. |name = GetStringGameSetting |returnType = string |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = SettingName |Type = string }} |referenceType = }} ==See Also== *[[GetNumericGameSetting]] *[[SetStringGameSettingEx]] *[[GetStringIniSetting]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:GameSetting Functions]]

revision.comment: fix ini setting format style
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the value of a string ini setting found in '''Fallout.ini''' / '''FalloutPrefs.ini''' / '''FalloutCustom.ini''', which is specified as a single string in the format: "settingName:settingGroup". The ':' seems to be expected by the engine. |name = GetStringIniSetting |returnType = string |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Setting |Type = string }} |referenceType = }} ==Example== 
string_var pad_language

let pad_language := GetStringIniSetting "sDPad:LANGUAGE"
==See Also== *[[SetStringIniSetting]] *[[GetStringGameSetting]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:INI Functions]]

revision.comment: Added to Category:Condition Functions
revision.text: {{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns whether or not the [[ToggleFlyCam|fly camera]] is currently active. |name = GetTFC |returnType = int |example = if GetTFC ; do something that needs fly camera endif }} ==See Also== *[[ToggleFlyCam]] *[[Con_TFC]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the file path to the texture for the specified object or [[calling reference]]. This command is identical to [[GetIconPath]], but also works for other object types such as skills, classes, and miscellaneous objects. |name = GetTexturePath |returnType = Path:string |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Object |Type = form |Optional = y}} }} ==Example== 
string_var texture_path

let texture_path := GetTexturePath Vodka
==See Also== *[[SetTexturePath]] *[[GetIconPath]] *[[GetModelPath]] *[[GetBipedModelPath]] *[[String Variable]] *[[Let]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Texture Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the form value of the specified [[:Category:Tokens|token]] in the inventory of the [[calling reference]]. When trying to use this as a condition function, no forms can be selected, so it ends up being useless. |name = GetTokenRef |referenceType = Actor |returnType = form |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Token |Type = baseform }} }} ==Example== 
ref rPartner

let rPartner := SunnyREF.GetTokenRef MarriageToken
==See Also== *[[SetTokenRef]] *[[GetTokenValue]] *[[GetPaired]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Token Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the numeric value of the specified [[:Category:Tokens|token]] in the inventory of the [[calling reference]]. |name = GetTokenValue |referenceType = Actor |returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Token |Type = baseform }} }} ==Example== 
float fMood

let fMood := SunnyREF.GetTokenValue MoodToken
==See Also== *[[SetTokenValue]] *[[GetTokenRef]] *[[GetPaired]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Token Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns a StringMap [[array]] containing information about the time and date on the player's system.
The returned StringMap has the following key/value pairs, all of which are integers:
*Year (1601 - 30827) *Month (1-12) *DayOfWeek (1:Sunday - 7:Saturday) *Day (1-31) *Hour (0-23) *Minute (0-59) *Second (0-59) *Millisecond (0-999) |name = GetUserTime |returnType = StringMap |referenceType = |arguments = |example = array_var UT let UT := GetUserTime
if eval UT["Month"] == 12 && UT["Day"] == 25 PlayerREF.AddItem Weap44MagnumRevolver, 1 PlayerREF.AddItem Ammo44Magnum, 1 MessageBoxEx "Merry Christmas, don't play Fallout all day ;)" endif }} =='''See Also'''== *[[GetTimeStamp]] *[[GetTimePlayed]] *[[Array Variable]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Time Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the value of the numeric variable specified by name for the calling reference or specified scriptable object / quest. |name = GetVariable |returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = VarName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Parent |Type = Object |Optional = y }} |referenceType = ref }} ==Example== 
ref rActor
int WaitState

let WaitState := rActor.GetVariable "Waiting"

; Alternatively, (or required if rActor holds a base form): 

let WaitState := GetVariable "Waiting", rActor
For accessing quest variables from a mod without having to add that mod as a master, using [[GetFormFromMod]]: 
float fFloat
ref rQuest

let rQuest := GetFormFromMod "Some Plugin.esp" "AED"
let fFloat := GetVariable "varName" rQuest
==See Also== *[[GetRefVariable]] (For ref rather than numeric variables) *[[GetArrayVariable]] (and for arrays) *[[HasVariable]] (to check if a variable exists at all) *[[ShowVars]] / [[Con_ShowVars]] (to print all variables/values to console) *[[SetVariable]] *[[SetRefVariable]] *[[Associating data with a reference]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Script Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the first set of flags for the specified weapon or calling reference, as a combined value of the bits (int). See [[GetEquipmentSlotsMask]] for an example of checking for a specific bit. |name = GetWeaponFlags1 |returnType = Mask:int |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Weapon |Type = form |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== 
int FlagsMask
set FlagsMask to GetWeaponFlags1 WeapNV9mmPistol
==Bit fields== {| class="wikitable" |- ! Flag ! Bit ! Equipment Mask |- | 1 | 0 | ignores normal weapon resist |- | 2 | 1 | is automatic |- | 4 | 2 | has scope |- | 8 | 3 | can't drop |- | 16 | 4 | hide backpack |- | 32 | 5 | embedded weapon |- | 64 | 6 | don't use first-person IS animations |- | 128 | 7 | nonplayable |} ==See Also== *[[SetWeaponFlags1]] *[[GetWeaponFlags2]] *[[GetEquipmentSlotsMask]] *[[SetBit]] *[[ClearBit]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the second set of flags for the specified [[weapon]] or [[calling reference]], as a combined value of the bits (int). See [[GetEquipmentSlotsMask]] for an example method of checking for a specific bit. |name = GetWeaponFlags2 |returnType = Mask:int |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Weapon |Type = form |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== 
int FlagsBitMask
set FlagsBitMask to GetWeaponFlags2 WeapNV9mmPistol
==Weapon Flags 2 Bit Fields== {| class="wikitable" |- ! Flag ! Bit ! Equipment Mask |- | 1 | 0 | player only |- | 2 | 1 | NPCs use ammo |- | 4 | 2 | no jam after reload |- | 8 | 3 | override action points |- | 16 | 4 | minor crime |- | 32 | 5 | range fixed |- | 64 | 6 | not used in normal combat |- | 128 | 7 | override damage to weapon mult |- | 256 | 8 | don't use third-person IS animations |- | 512 | 9 | short burst |- | 1024 | 10 | rumble alternate |- | 2048 | 11 | long burst |- | 4096 | 12 | scope has night vision |- | 8192 | 13 | scope from mod |} ==See Also== *[[SetWeaponFlags2]] *[[GetWeaponFlags1]] *[[GetWeaponHasFlag]] *[[SetBit]] *[[ClearBit]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Engine Differences (Fallout)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the base form of the weapon mod at the specified slot index (1, 2 or 3) for the specified weapon or [[calling reference]]. |name = GetWeaponItemMod |returnType = form |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ItemMod |Type = int}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Weapon |Type = form |Optional = y}} }} ==Example== 
ref rMod

set rMod to GetWeaponItemMod 1, SomeWeapon
==See Also== *[[GetWeaponItemModEffect]] *[[GetWeaponItemModValue1]] *[[GetWeaponItemModValue2]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the 1st value for the mod at the specified slot (1, 2 or 3) on the specified weapon or [[calling reference]]. This is the 1st modifier amount for the weapon mod, which will impact the primary magnitude of the effect.
The 2nd modifier amount ([[GetWeaponItemModValue2]]) is rarely, if ever, used. If a calling ''weaponReference'' is passed, then its [[BaseForm]] will be used automatically instead. |name = GetWeaponItemModValue1 |returnType = value1:float |referenceType = weaponReference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = whichItemMod |Type = int}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Weapon |Type = BaseForm |Optional = y}} }} ==Example== 
float value1

let value1 := GetWeaponItemModValue1 1, SomeWeapon
==See Also== *[[GetWeaponItemModValue2]] *[[GetEquippedWeaponModFlags]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

revision.comment: fix wrong documentation on results
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the 2nd value for the mod at the specified slot (1, 2 or 3) on the specified weapon or [[calling reference]]. This is the 2nd modifier amount for the weapon mod, which will impact the secondary magnitude of the effect. This is rarely, if ever, used. If a calling ''weaponReference'' is passed, then its [[BaseForm]] will be used automatically instead. |name = GetWeaponItemModValue2 |returnType = EffectCode:int |referenceType = weaponReference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ModSlot |Type = int}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Weapon |Type = form |Optional = y}} }} ==Example== 
float value2 

let value2 := GetWeaponItemModValue2 1, SomeWeapon
==See Also== *[[Weapon Mod Codes]] *[[GetWeaponItemModValue1]] (for the first effect) *[[GetEquippedWeaponModFlags]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the long bursts value of the specified weapon or calling reference. |name = GetWeaponLongBursts |returnType = int |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Weapon |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = short bBursts set bBursts to GetWeaponLongBurst WeapNVAssaultCarbine }} =='''See Also'''== *[[SetWeaponLongBursts]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the the required skill level for the specified weapon or [[calling reference]] to one. |name = GetWeaponRequiredSkill |alias = GetReqSkill |returnType = int |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Weapon |Type = form |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== 
int iReqSkill
set iReqSkill to GetWeaponRequiredSkill WeapNV9mmPistol
==See Also== *[[SetWeaponRequiredSkill]] *[[GetWeaponRequiredStrength]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the the required strength for the specified weapon or calling reference to one. |name = GetWeaponRequiredStrength |alias = GetReqStr |returnType = int |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Weapon |Type = form |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== 
int iReqStrength
set iReqStrength to GetWeaponRequiredStrength WeapNV9mmPistol
==See Also== *[[SetWeaponRequiredStrength]] *[[GetWeaponRequiredSkill]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the rumble wavelength for the specified weapon or calling reference. |name = GetWeaponRumbleWavelength |alias = GetRumbleWavelen |returnType = float |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Weapon |Type = form |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== 
float fWaveLen
set fWaveLen to GetRumbleWavelen WeapNV9mmPistol
==See Also== *[[GetWeaponRumbleDuration]] *[[GetWeaponRumbleLeftMotor]] *[[GetWeaponRumbleRightMotor]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns true (1) if console output is currently being [[Con_SCOF|written to a file]]. |name = HasConsoleOutputFileName |alias = HasCOF |returnType = bool |arguments = |example = if HasCOF ... }} ==See Also== *[[Con_SCOF]] *[[GetConsoleOutputFile]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Debugging]] [[Category:File Functions]]

revision.comment: Added to Category:Condition Functions
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Checks whether an [[NPC]] owns a [[reference]] or has the required [[faction]] rank to own said reference. |name = HasOwnership |referenceType = Actor |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Item |Type = ref }} }} ==Example== 
if SunnyREF.HasOwnership SomeItemREF
    ; Sunny Smiles owns SomeItemREF
endif
=='''See Also'''== *[[AddItemOwnership]] *[[IsOwned]] *[[IsOwner]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Ownership Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns true (1) if the specified object's script has a variable of the specified name. Works for any scriptable object for all variable types. |name = HasVariable |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = VarName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Parent |Type = Object |Optional = y }} |referenceType = ref }} ==Example== 
ref rActor

if rActor.HasVariable "Waiting"
     ; Set or get it, etc
endif

; Alternatively, (or required if rActor holds a base form): 

if HasVariable "Waiting", rActor
==See Also== *[[ShowVars]] / [[Con_ShowVars]] to print all variables and values to the console *[[GetVariable]] (to retrieve the actual value of a numeric variable) *[[GetRefVariable]] (as above for ref variables) *[[GetArrayVariable]] (and for arrays) *[[SetVariable]] (Added by NVSE 4.5.6) *[[SetRefVariable]] (Aded by NVSE 4.5.6) *[[Associating data with a reference]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Script Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns True (1) if the specified character (represented by an ASCII-coded int) is a digit. |name = IsDigit |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = SourceCharacter |Type = int}} }} ==See Also== *[[IsLetter]] *[[IsPunctuation]] *[[Sv_GetChar]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns true (1) if the [[calling reference|calling item reference]] is currently equipped by its owner. |name = IsEquipped |returnType = bool |referenceType = reference |arguments = |example = }} ==Example== 
ref rItem

foreach rItem <- PlayerREF
    if rItem.IsEquipped
     ; do something
    endif
loop
==Usage Note== *As of NVSE 4.5.7, this function must be called on an [[inventory reference]]. You can obtain a (temporary) inventory reference using a [[foreach]] loop on a container, or functions such as [[GetInvRefsForItem]], [[GetFirstRefForItem]], or [[CreateTempRef]]. ==See Also== *[[EquipMe]] *[[UnequipMe]] *[[EquipItem2]] *[[GetEquippedObject]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Reference Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns True (1) if the specified character is alphabetic. |name = IsLetter |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = SourceCharacter |Type = int}} }} ==See Also== *[[IsDigit]] *[[IsPunctuation]] *[[Sv_GetChar]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns true (1) if the [[calling reference]] is a load door, meaning a [[Door]] that leads to a different [[Cell]]. |name = IsLoadDoor |returnType = bool |referenceType = reference |arguments = |example = }} ==Example== 
GSDocMitchellHouseIntDoorREF.IsLoadDoor ; returns 1, it goes to Goodsprings.
==See Also== *[[GetDoorTeleportX/Y/Z/Rot]] *[[SetDoorTeleport]] *[[GetType]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Door Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Checks whether a [[reference]] is owned by an [[NPC]] or that said NPC has the required faction rank to own the reference. |name = IsOwned |referenceType = ref |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Owner |Type = ref }} }} ==Example== 
if SomeItemRef.IsOwned SunnyREF
    ; SomeItemRef is owned by Sunny Smiles
endif
=='''See Also'''== *[[AddItemOwnership]] *[[HasOwnership]] *[[IsOwner]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Ownership Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

revision.comment: Function is actually a FOSE function.
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns True (1) if the calling [[reference]] is persistent. |name = IsPersistent |referenceType = reference |returnType = bool |arguments = }} ==Example== 
if SomeRef.IsPersistent
    ; it is safe to proceed
endif
==See Also== *[[SetPersistent]] *[[IsFormValid]] *[[IsReference]] *[[TypeOf]] *[[GetType]] *[[GetPermanent]] *[[GetReferenceFlag]] / [[SetReferenceFlag]] [[Category:Debugging]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:Functions (FOSE)]]

revision.comment: Removed double ==Form Types==
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns true (1) if the specified form or [[calling reference]], which may be an armor, weapon, ammo or race, has the playable flag. Added by NVSE 4.2 (see notes). {{:Playable Items}} |name = IsPlayable |referenceType = reference |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ToCheck |Type = form |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== 
if IsPlayable Weap10mmPistol
    ; The weapon is playable
endif
==Form Types== These form types can be Playable: 
10	Hair (not supported by this function; see LNIsPlayable)
11	Eyes (not supported by this function; see LNIsPlayable)
12	Race
24	Armor
40	Weapon
41	Ammo
86	Perk (not supported by this function; see LNIsPlayable or GetPerkFlag)
==Note== * In NVSE 4.2, this function only worked for armor- 4.5.7 has expanded it to work with weapons and ammo as well. * Since NVSE 5.0.2, this function also supports races. ==See Also== *[[SetIsPlayable]] *[[LNIsPlayable]] *[[LNSetIsPlayable]] *[[IsQuestItem]] *[[GetWeaponFlags1]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns true if the player is swimming. |name = IsPlayerSwimming |returnType = bool |example = if IsPlayerSwimming ; Do something endif }} ==See Also== *[[IsSwimming]] - The equivalent for NPCs, which does not work for player. *[[IsInWater]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns true (1) if the specified NVSE plugin is loaded. Note that plugin names are set during registration and displayed in the file, nvse.log, it is not the file name. |name = IsPluginInstalled |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = PluginName |Type = string }}}} ==Example== 
if IsPluginInstalled "MCM Extensions" ; MCM
...
if IsPluginInstalled "Extensions for NVSE" ; NX
...
if IsPluginInstalled "JIP NVSE Plugin" ; Jazzisparis
...
if IsPluginInstalled "JohnnyGuitarNVSE" ; JohnnyGuitar
...
if IsPluginInstalled "SUP NVSE Plugin" ; SUP NVSE
==Notes== * Below is a list of known plugin names: {{NVSE_Plugins}} ==See Also== *[[GetPluginVersion]] *[[GetNVSEVersion]] *[[IsModLoaded]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Debugging]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns True (1) if the specified character is printable (not a non-printable control character). |name = IsPrintable |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = SourceCharacter |Type = int}} }} ==See Also== *[[IsLetter]] *[[IsDigit]] *[[Sv_GetChar]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns True (1) if the specified character is punctuation. |name = IsPunctuation |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = SourceCharacter |Type = int}} }} ==See Also== *[[IsLetter]] *[[IsDigit]] *[[Sv_GetChar]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]]

revision.comment: Function is actually a FOSE function.
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns true if the specified object is marked as a [[Quest Item]]. |name = IsQuestItem |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Item |Type = Object }} |referenceType = |example = if IsQuestItem SomePlayerItem ; Don't remove it from the player endif }} ==Form Types== These form types can be Quest items: 
22: Talking Activator
24: Armor
25: Book
31: Misc
40: Weapon
41: Ammo
42: NPC
43: Creature
46: Key
47: Ingestible
103: Weapon Mod
==Notes== *Does not compile when passing a form to it that isn't able to be kept in inventory, ie actors. When passing such forms to it via a ref var, it will always return false in-game. You can, however, determine if such forms are flagged as quest items by using [[GetFlagsLow]] with bit 10. ==See Also== *[[GetPermanent]] *[[SetQuestObject]] *[[SetQuestItem]] *[[SetShowQuestItems]] [[Category:Functions (FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Quest Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the index of an object in a form list. If the object is contained in the list exactly, this index is returned, otherwise if the base form or a leveled form of the object is contained, this index is returned. If the object is not contained in the list in either reference, base, or leveled form, the constant -1 is returned. |name = IsRefInList |returnType = Index:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ToCheck |Type = formlist }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = For |Type = anyform }} }} ==Example== 
; Form list FL contains: SunnyREF, GSSunnySmiles, GSCheyenne 

IsRefInList FL, SunnyREF ; * returns 0

IsRefInList FL, CheyenneREF ; * returns 2

IsRefInList FL, EasyPeteREF ; * returns -1, for Not Found.
== Notes == *[[Reference]]s must be flagged persistent if included in a [[formlist]] otherwise they will be cleared. ==See Also== *[[IsInList]] (Checks for base objects only) *[[ListGetFormIndex]] (Returns index of exact matches only) *[[ListAddForm]] *[[ListRemoveForm]] *[[ListClear]] *[[Array Variables]] (a more powerful alternative to using form lists) [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:List_Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns True (1) if the specified character is an upper case letter. |name = IsUpperCase |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = SourceCharacter |Type = int}} }} ==See Also== *[[IsLetter]] *[[IsDigit]] *[[Sv_GetChar]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = let is used for variable assignments and is best thought of as a more powerful version of the vanilla set .. to .. command. Compared to set, it has the following advantages: * It supports a wider range of [[NVSE Expressions|operators]], such as += and -= for convenietly incrementing or decrementing numeric variables. * It supports [[Array Variables|array variables]], [[String Variable|string variables]] and [[User Defined Function|user defined functions]]. * When an assignment fails, a [[NVSE Error|runtime error]] message is printed to the console, naming the guilty mod and script, and the script will continue to run. By comparison, set will cause the script to fail silently, and so is harder to debug. * It performs faster than set. Let can also be called implicitly by using a [[Let Macro|Macro]]: iCount = 10 ;is equivalent to let iCount := 10 ;in original syntax |name = let |returnType = assignment |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Variable |Type = Any }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Operator |Type = Any }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Value |Type = Any }} }} ==Example== Simple assignments: 
ref MyRef
int MyInt

let MyInt := 5        ; equivalent to 'set MyInt to 5'
let MyRef := SunnyREF ; equivalent to 'set MyRef to SunnyREF'
Incrementing, etc 
let MyInt := 5  ; 'set MyInt to 5'
let MyInt += 1  ; 'set MyInt to MyInt + 1'
let MyInt *= 10 ; 'set MyInt to MyInt * 10'
let MyInt /= 5  ; 'set MyInt to MyInt / 5'

let MyVeryLongNamedQuest.SomeVariable += 1 'set MyVeryLongNamedQuest.SomeVariable to MyVeryLongNamedQuest.SomeVariable + 1'
Strings, arrays and UDFs: 
string_var my_string
array_var Beatles
int FoodCount

let my_string := "string variables are great"
let my_string += " and you can concatanate them with += too!"

let Beatles := Ar_List JohnREF, PaulREF, GeorgeREF, RingoREF

let Beatles[3] := KeithMoonREF ; swap element 3 (RingoREF for KeithMoonREF, probably a bad move ;))

let MyRef := Beatles[0] ; first element is 0, so MyRef == JohnREF

let FoodCount := call MyFoodCountingUDF PlayerREF ; call a UDF
Multiple ''':=''' operators can be chained together to assign multiple variables to a single variable: 
int iTemp
int iVar

let iTemp := iVar := 2

print $iTemp
print $iVar 
; * Will print "2" twice in console.
This chaining works for arrays, and any other variable type: 
array_var aFolders 
array_var aFiles

;(assume both arrays were previously filled)

let aFolders := aFiles := ar_null  

ar_dump aFiles
ar_dump aFolders
; * Nothing will print to console, since both arrays are now empty.
==See Also== *[[NVSE Expressions]] - Complete list of supported operators *[[TestExpr]] - useful when worried about [[NVSE Error|Runtime errors]] *[[Eval]] - useful for if statements *[[Array Variable]] *[[String Variable]] *[[Script Compiler Override]] ==External Links== *[https://obse.silverlock.org/obse_command_doc.html#OBSE_Expressions List of supported expression found in OBSE, these are equivalent in NVSE] [[Category:Commands]] [[Category:Scripting]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:User Defined Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware_Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Removes all entries from the specified form list. |name = ListClear |returnType = None |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ToClear |Type = FormList }} }} ==Example== 
ListClear SomeFormList
if ListGetCount SomeFormList == 0
    ; It worked
endif
==See Also== *[[ListGetCount]] *[[ListGetNthForm]] *[[ListAddForm]] *[[Array Variables]] (an alternative to using form lists) [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:List_Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions_(FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Added by NVSE, used to mark the end of a [[While]] or [[Foreach]] loop section, similar to the 'endif' in condition statements. See the loop function pages for usage information. |name = Loop |returnType = void }} ==See Also== *[[While]] *[[Foreach]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Similar to [[ShowMessage]] but accepts a string directly rather than via a message form. Variables can be passed using [[String Formatting|string formatting]], or [[String Variable|string variables]] via [[ToString]]($). The message will be displayed in a box that prompts for user confirmation.
Supports up to 10 custom buttons separated by "|". To determine which button was selected, [[GetButtonPressed]] can be used. |name = MessageBoxEx |returnType = none |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = string |Type = formatable }} |example= }} ==Example== 
MessageBoxEx "This message will in a box on screen|Ok|Cancel"
set iBtn to GetButtonPressed
if (iBtn == 1)
   MessageEx "Cancel was selected"
endif
==See Also== *[[ShowMessage]] *[[MessageBoxExAlt]] *[[MessageEx]] (without the box) *[[Print]] *[[ToString|ToString($)]] *[[String Variable]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Debugging]] [[Category:Message Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

revision.comment: Added link to MessageBoxExAlt
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Similar to [[ShowMessage]] but accepts a string directly rather than via a message form. Variables can be passed using [[String Formatting|string formatting]], or [[String Variable|string variables]] via [[ToString|ToString ($)]]. |name = MessageEx |returnType = none |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = string |Type = formatable }} |example= }} ==Example== 
MessageEx "This message will be shown in the top left of the screen in game"

int X
...
MessageEx "Sunny's name is %n, and the value of X is %g" SunnyREF, X

string_var my_string
let my_string := "Use $ to convert string_vars, etc to strings"
...
MessageEx $my_string
==Notes== *Unlike ShowMessage, the icon and duration can not be specified, and are always default. *[[MessageExAlt]], a function from the [[:Category:Functions_(JIP)|JIP NVSE Plugin]], may be used to specify the icon and duration. ==See Also== *[[ShowMessage]] *[[MessageBoxEx]] *[[MessageBoxExAlt]] *[[MessageExAlt]] *[[Print]] *[[ToString|ToString ($)]] *[[String Variable]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Debugging]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Message Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns true (1) if [[Mod Local Data]] exists associated with the specified key for the mod to which the calling script belongs. |name = ModLocalDataExists |returnType = Exists:bool |referenceType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Key |Type = string}} |example = if eval ModLocalDataExists "Something Important" ; do something endif }} =='''See Also'''== *[[Mod Local Data]] *[[GetModLocalData]] *[[SetModLocalData]] *[[RemoveModLocalData]] *[[GetAllModLocalData]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Converts a specified integer to a hexadecimal string, optionally padded to the specified width. If the specified width is smaller than the minimum width to represent the number, will use the minimum width. The maximum width is 8 characters. If ''AddPrefix'' is set to true (non-zero), will prepend "0x" to the string. * This arg is added in [[xNVSE]] 6.2.9. The ''IntToHex'' alias was added in [[xNVSE]] 6.2.9. |name = NumToHex |alias = IntToHex |returnType = string |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ToConvert |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = WidthToPadTo |Type = int |Optional = y |Value = 8 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = AddPrefix |Type = bool |Optional = y |Value = 0 }} }} ==Example== 
string_var hex_index

let hex_index := NumToHex 42, 2

; hex_index == "2A"
==See Also== *[[IntToBin]] *[[String Variable]] *[[ToNumber|ToNumber(#)]] *[[ToString|ToString($)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]]

revision.comment: some more see also
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Called on a reference, returns one random item from the intersection subset of items that are contained both in their inventory and the specified form list. Returns 0 if no items are common to both. |name = PickOneOf |returnType = form |referenceType = actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Intersection |Type = formlist }}}} ==Example== 
; SunnyREF inventory contains: Beer, Jet, Knife
; MyFormList contains: Beer, Jet, GeckoSteak

SunnyREF.PickOneOf MyFormList ; returns either Beer or Jet, at random
==See Also== *[[GetInventoryObject]] *[[ListGetNthForm]] *[[Rand]] *[[GetItemCount]] *[[IsRefInList]] *[[ListGetFormIndex]] *[[IsInList]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Prints a string expression to the console. This is mainly a convenience function to eliminate the need for temporary string variables when printing complex strings using [[PrintToConsole]], as it supports string concatenation and the [[ToString]] function, also making [[String Formatting|string formatting]] mostly unnecessary, although it can still be helpful to do things like ending the line with "%r".
Like [[Let]] and [[Eval|if eval]], NVSE expressions and nested function calls are supported without [[Script Compiler Override|compiler override]].
|name = Print |returnType = none |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = string |Type = expression }} |example = Print "My mod version is " + $MyMainQuest.Version + " and NVSE version is " + $(GetNVSEVersion) Console --> My mod version is 1.04 and NVSE version is 4 ref rRef Print $(GetEditorID rRef) Will output the EditorID of the form held in rRef. }} ==See Also== *[[PrintDebug]] (only prints if mod has debug mode enabled) *[[PrintF]] (Outputs to a file) *[[PrintToConsole]] (PrintC, the old version which relies upon string formatting) *[[PrintVar]] *[[ToString|ToString($)]] *[[String Variable]] *[[Con_SCOF]] *[[Debug Dumps]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Debugging]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = As [[Print]], but outputs to console only if the calling mod has [[SetDebugMode|debug mode enabled]]. |name = PrintDebug |alias = PrintD |returnType = none |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = string |Type = expression }}}} ==Example== 
SetDebugMode 1

PrintDebug "SomeVariable = " + $SomeVariable
==See Also== *[[Print]] *[[SetDebugMode]] *[[GetDebugMode]] *[[DebugPrint]] (the old equivalent which requires string formatting) *[[Con_SCOF]] to transfer console outputs into Debug Logs. *[[ToString|ToString($)]] *[[String Variable]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Debugging]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = As [[PrintF]], but only outputs if the calling mod has [[SetDebugMode|Debug Mode enabled]]. |name = PrintDebugF |alias = PrintDF |returnType = none |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = OutputFileName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = OutputText |Type = string }} |example = SetDebugMode 1 PrintDebugF "MyLog.txt", "That Gum you like is going to come back in style."
MyLog.txt --> That Gum you like is going to come back in style. }} ==See Also== *[[PrintF]] (Regular version) *[[SetDebugMode]] *[[GetDebugMode]] *[[PrintDebug]] *[[ToString|ToString($)]] *[[String Variable]] *[[Debug Dumps]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Debugging]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Similar to [[Print]], but outputs the string to the specified file (in the [[GetFalloutDirectory|Fallout directory]]) instead of the console. If the file exists it will be appended, otherwise created. |name = PrintF |returnType = none |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = OutputFileName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = OutputText |Type = string }} |example = PrintF "MyLog.txt", "The owls are not what they seem..."
MyLog.txt --> The owls are not what they seem... }} ==See Also== *[[PrintDebugF]] (only outputs if mod has debug mode enabled) *[[Print]] (Outputs to console instead) *[[ModLogPrint]] *[[Con_SCOF]] *[[ToString|ToString($)]] *[[String Variable]] *[[Debug Dumps]] *[[WriteStringToFile]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Debugging]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Converts a given quaternion to euler angles. Two methods are available: the default is normalized linear interpolation, and optionally spherical linear interpolation. |name = QToEuler |alias = QToE |returnType = none |referenceType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Elevation |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Bank |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Heading |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Quat_W |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Quat_X |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Quat_Y |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Quat_Z |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = UseSLI |Type = bool |Optional = y }} }}
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Algohol Functions]] [[Category:Math Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns a random float between specified minimum and maximum. |name = Rand |returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Minimum |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Maximum |Type = float }} |example = float fCount set fCount to Rand 1, 10
int iRand set iRand to Rand 0, 100 ; * for 0-99, equivalent to using GetRandomPercent }} ==Notes== *To get an int, either use [[Floor]] or just store the return in an int variable. *To round to the ''nearest'' int, add 0.5 to the return and then either [[Floor]] it or store it in an int variable.
==See Also== *[[GetRandomInRange]] *[[GetRandomPercent]] *[[NX_GetRandom]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Math Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Removes an [[inventory reference]] from its container, optionally transferring it to a different container, in much the same way as the vanilla [[RemoveMe]] command. The inventory reference becomes invalid once this command is called and should no longer be used. |name = RemoveMeIR |referenceType = InvRef |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Transfer |Type = Container |Optional = y }} |example = ; Within an object script: RemoveMeIR ; or RemoveMeIR SomeContainerREF }} ==See Also== *[[RemoveMeIRAlt]] *[[RemoveMe]] *[[CopyIR]]
