[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category: Object Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Reference Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Deletes the [[Mod Local Data]] entry associated with the specified key for the mod to which the calling script belongs. Returns false (0) if no data existed for the specified key, otherwise true (1). |name = RemoveModLocalData |returnType = Existed:bool |referenceType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Key |Type = string}} |example = RemoveModLocalData "Some Key" }} =='''See Also'''== *[[Mod Local Data]] *[[GetModLocalData]] *[[SetModLocalData]] *[[ModLocalDataExists]] *[[GetAllModLocalData]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Resets the values (to zero/null) of all variables on a reference with a script attached. |name = ResetAllVariables |returnType = none |referenceType = [Reference] |arguments = }} ==Example== 
SunnyREF.ResetAllVariables
==See Also== *[[ResetQuest]] *[[SetVariable]] *[[SetRefVariable]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Script Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Runs a script, which may be passed literally or attached to a form. This allows scripts to be run without a reference.
Returns true or false depending on what the script returns. *''Prior to xNVSE 6.2.9, this function did not return a value, as it instead printed its result in console.'' |name = RunScript |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Script |Type = Script-or-ScriptedForm }} |referenceType = }} ==Example== 
RunScript MyScript ; to run script MyScript

; or
RunScript MyToken ; to run the script attached to the item, MyToken
==Unreliable?== *As of NVSE 4.6.3, this function seems to run the first block of the script once, and only if it is appropriate (eg: first block is GameMode). ==See Also== *[[GetCurrentScript]] *[[ResetAllVariables]] *[[Call]] with [[User Defined Function]] (an alternative way to run scripts without a reference) [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Script Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the high base flags of the specified actor or calling reference to the specified combined bit value (int). Using unknown flags may cause random behavior. |name = SetActorBaseFlagsHigh |returnType = none |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = FlagsMask |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = NPC |Type = BaseForm |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== 
int FlagsMask
SetActorBaseFlagsHigh FlagsMask, SomeNPC
==Bit fields== {| class="wikitable" |- ! Flag ! Bit ! NPC ! Creature |- | 1 | 0 | Unknown 16 | No Right Arm |- | 2 | 1 | Unknown 17 | No Left Arm |- | 4 | 2 | Unknown 18 | No Combat In Water |- | 8 | 3 | Unknown 19 | No Shadow |- | 16 | 4 | No VATS Melee | No VATS melee |- | 32 | 5 | Unknown 21 | Allow PC Dialogue |- | 64 | 6 | Can Be All Races | Can't Open Doors |- | 128 | 7 | Auto-Calc Service | Immobile |- | 256 | 8 | Unknown 24 | Tilt Front/Back |- | 512 | 9 | Unknown 25 | Tilt Left/Right |- | 1024 | 10 | No Knockdowns | No knockdowns |- | 2048 | 11 | Not Pushable | Not Pushable |- | 4096 | 12 | Unknown 28 | Allow Pickpocket |- | 8192 | 13 | Unknown 29 | Is Ghost |- | 16384 | 14 | No Rotating to Head-track | No Rotating to Head-track |- | 32768 | 15 | Unknown 31 | Invulnerable |} ==See Also== *[[SetActorBaseFlagsLow]] *[[GetActorBaseFlagsHigh]] *[[SetBit]] *[[ClearBit]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the low base flags of the specified actor or calling reference to the specified combined bit value (int). Using unknown flags may cause random behavior. |name = SetActorBaseFlagsLow |returnType = none |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = FlagsMask |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = NPC |Type = BaseForm |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== 
int FlagsMask
SetActorBaseFlagsLow FlagsMask, SomeNPC
==Bit fields== {| class="wikitable" |- ! Flag ! Bit ! NPC ! Creature |- | 1 | 0 | Female | Biped |- | 2 | 1 | Essential | Essential |- | 4 | 2 | Is CharGen Face Preset | Weapon & Shield? |- | 8 | 3 | Respawn | Respawn |- | 16 | 4 | Auto-calc stats | Swims |- | 32 | 5 | Unknown 5 | Flies |- | 64 | 6 | Unknown 6 | Walks |- | 128 | 7 | PC Level Mult | PC Level Mult |- | 256 | 8 | Unknown 8 | Unknown 8 |- | 512 | 9 | No Low-Level Processing | No Low-Level Processing |- | 1024 | 10 | Unknown 10 | Unknown 10 |- | 2048 | 11 | No Blood Spray | No Blood Spray |- | 4096 | 12 | No Blood Decal | No Blood Decal |- | 8192 | 13 | Unknown 13 | Unknown 13 |- | 16384 | 14 | Unknown 14 | Unknown 14 |- | 32768 | 15 | Unknown 15 | No Head |} ==Notes== * Bit 8 (256) was described as "use template", but checking it on a templated base form or a base form used as a template always returns 0. * Bits 11 & 12 operate in the opposite way compared to the Geck. While in the geck, NPCs are flagged if they have blood spray and decal, the bits are only 1 if they don't. ==See Also== *[[GetActorBaseFlagsLow]] *[[SetActorBaseFlagsHigh]] *[[SetBit]] *[[ClearBit]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

revision.comment: Baseform note, clarify the % weirdness.
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the Damage Resistance (Armor Rating) of the specified [[armor]] [[BaseForm]].
The ''newDR'' argument is 100x the value of the effective DR. Example: to set the armor to 20 DR, the ''newDR'' int must be 2000. If called on an armor reference, will modify that armor's BaseForm. |name = SetArmorArmorRating |alias = SetArmorAR |returnType = none |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = newDR |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Armor |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = SetArmorAR 2000, ArmorLeather }} ==See Also== *[[GetArmorAR]] *[[GetArmorDT]] *[[SetArmorDT]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Armor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the damage threshold of the specified armor or calling reference. |name = SetArmorDT |returnType = none |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = DT |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Armor |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = SetArmorDT 5.0, ArmorLeather }} ==See Also== *[[GetArmorAR]] (Damage Resistance) *[[SetArmorAR]] *[[GetArmorDT]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Armor Functions]]

{{Deprecated}} {{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the DDS file path to a [[String Formatting|formatable string]] for the male or female icon for the specified biped object or [[calling reference]] (nothing) reference.
'''NOTE: Use [[SetBipedIconPathAlt]] instead, as this is currently broken.''' |name = SetBipedIconPathEx |returnType = nothing |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = NewPath |Type = string}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Formatting |Type = }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = BipedPathCode |Type = int}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Object |Type = form |Optional = y}} }} ==Example== 
SetBipedIconPathEx "MyNewIcons\PipboyImages\Apparel\new_leather.dds" 0, ArmorLeather
==Biped Path Codes== Specifies icon/model paths associated with biped objects, such as armor *0: Male Biped Path *1: Female Biped Path *2: Male Ground Path *3: Female Ground Path ==See Also== *[[SetBipedIconPathAlt]] *[[GetBipedIconPath]] *[[SetBipedModelPathEx]] *[[SetModelPath]] *[[SetIconPath]] *[[String Variable]] *[[Let]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Texture Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Deprecated Functions]]

{{Deprecated}} {{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the NIF file path to a [[String Formatting|formatable string]] for the male or female model for the specified biped object or [[calling reference]]. '''NOTE: this function is broken, use [[SetBipedModelPathAlt]] instead.''' |name = SetBipedModelPathEx |returnType = nothing |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = NewPath |Type = string}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Formatting |Type = }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = BipedPathCode |Type = int}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Object |Type = form |Optional = y}} }} ==Example== New male biped path for leather armor: 
SetBipedModelPathEx "MyMod\Armor\M\NewLeather.NIF" %e 0 ArmorLeather
==Biped Path Codes== Specifies icon/model paths associated with biped objects, such as armor *0: Male Biped Path *1: Female Biped Path *2: Male Ground Path *3: Female Ground Path ==Notes:== *Not including the formatting stops the script from compiling. ==See Also== *[[GetBipedModelPath]] *[[SetBipedModelPathAlt]] *[[SetIconPath]] *[[SetModelPath]] *[[String Formatting]] *[[String Variable]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:Armor Functions]] [[Category:Deprecated Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the specified [[Int]] with the specified bit set to 1, or optionally 0 (cleared). This function is useful for manipulating bitmasks. |name = SetBit |returnType = int |referenceType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Target |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = BitToSet |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = NewBitValue |Type = bool |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== 
int a
set a to 11		; bits 0, 1, 3 set ; %00001011 binary
set a to SetBit a, 2	; sets bit 2 (value 4) ; %00000100 binary
; a is now 15
set a to SetBit a, 1, 0	; would set bit 1 (value 2), but the third parameter is 0 so instead it clears the bit
; a is now 13
==Notes== *SetBit considers the first bit be numbered, 0, and the final bit to be 31; however, it should not be used to change bits greater than 29 to avoid sign and float conversion issues. ==See Also== *[[GetBit]] *[[ClearBit]] *[[SetEquipmentSlotsMask]] *[[SetWeaponFlags1]] *[[SetActorBaseFlagsLow]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Bit Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Toggles whether the engine echoes to the console and returns the previous state.
This is essentially a script version of [[Verbose]]. |name = SetConsoleEcho |returnType = PreviousEchoState:bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = EchoState |Type = bool}} |example = SetConsoleEcho 1 ; or int PreviousState let PreviousState := SetConsoleEcho 1 }} ==See Also== *[[GetConsoleEcho]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the current health of the calling [[Reference]]. |name = SetCurrentHealth |returnType = none |referenceType = ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = NewHealth |Type = float |Optional = }} |example = }} ==Notes== This function should not be used on Actors, use [[RestoreAV]] / [[DamageAV]] instead
==See Also== *[[GetCurrentHealth]] *[[ResetHealth]] *[[SetEquippedCurrentHealth]] *[[SetItemRefCurrentHealth]] *[[SetObjectHealth]] to set the health for all references belonging to a Base Form.
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Object Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the reference, coordinates, and rotation to which the [[calling reference|calling]] load door teleports. Only the reference argument is required; the values for the others will be taken from the specified reference's coordinates and rotation if omitted. |name = SetDoorTeleport |returnType = none |referenceType = LoadDoorRef |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = TargetDoor |Type = reference |Optional = }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = X |Type = float |Optional = y}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Y |Type = float |Optional = y}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Z |Type = float |Optional = y}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Rotation |Type = float |Optional = y}} |example = }} ==Example== 
MagicDoorRef.SetDoorTeleport HallOfMirrorsDoorRef
==Notes== *Changes made using this function will not persist between game sessions, unlike the OBSE version of this function, in which this is optional. You can workaround this by recalling the function within a [[GetGameRestarted]] condition. ==See Also== *[[IsLoadDoor]] *[[GetDoorTeleportX/Y/Z/Rot]] [[Category:Door Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the equipment flags used by the specified armor to a mask (int). Masks may be built by using [[ClearBit]]/[[SetBit]]. |name = SetEquipmentBipedMask |alias = SetEBM |returnType = none |referenceType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = NewBitMask |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = ToModify |Type = armor }} }} ==Bit Fields== {| class="wikitable" |- ! Flag ! Bit ! Equipment Mask |- | 4 | 2 | HasBackpack |- | 8 | 3 | Medium Armor |- | 32 | 5 | Power Armor |- | 64 | 6 | Non-playable |- | 128 | 7 | HeavyArmor |} Other bits are either unused or unknown. ==Example== 
int NewMask

SetEquipmentBipedMask NewMask, ArmorLeather
==See Also== *[[GetEquipmentBipedMask]] *[[SetEquipmentSlotsMask]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Armor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the slots used by the biped form from a [[bitmask]] int, for the specified [[Base Form]]. If a [[calling reference]] is passed, its base form will be used instead. The mask is a bitmask that should be built by using [[ClearBit]]/[[SetBit]]. The order is as returned by [[GetEquippedObject]] or as defined in the GECK slot list of an armor, and starts at 0. |name = SetEquipmentSlotsMask |alias = SetESM |returnType = BitMask:int |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = BitMask |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = ToSetSlotsOf |Type = baseForm |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== 
int BitMask
SetEquipmentSlotsMask BitMask, ArmorLeather
==See Also== *[[GetEquipmentSlotsMask]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Armor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the mask (int) of the equipped weapon mods for the currently equipped weapon of the [[calling reference]]. |name = SetEquippedWeaponModFlags |returnType = none |referenceType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = NewMask |Type = int}} }} ==Example== 
int ModsMask
; ...

SetEquippedWeaponModFlags ModsBitMask
==Weapon Mod Flags== *1 (bit 0) = Weapon Mod 1 *2 (bit 1) = Weapon Mod 2 *4 (bit 2) = Weapon Mod 3 ==Notes== *If you're looking for a compatible general use case function, consider using [[AttachMod]] instead, as it supports both the Vanilla Item Mod system, as well as the [[Extended Item Mods System]] introduced in [[VanillaPlugins+|Plugins+]] ==See Also== *[[GetEquippedWeaponModFlags]] *[[SetBit]] *[[ClearBit]] *[[SetWeaponFlags1]] *[[SetWeaponFlags2]] *[[SetWeaponRefModFlags]] *[[GetWeaponRefModFlags]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Registers a [[UDF|User-Defined Function (UDF)]] as an [[Event Handling|Event Handler]] for the specified event. This means that whenever the specified event occurs in-game, if a UDF was set as an Event Handler for that event, the UDF's script will run, allowing for instant reactions to certain events. Note that Event Handlers only stay set for the current session, and are not tied to saves in any way. This means that in most cases, it should be reset each time a new session starts. See [[GetGameRestarted]] for further details on sessions and how new ones can be detected. To track if an event has been set for the session, consider using a Temporary [[Auxiliary Variable]], which is also tied to the session. |name = SetEventHandler |returnType = success:bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = eventID |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = functionScript |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = filters(up to 2) |Type = pair |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = priority |Type = pair |Optional = y |Value = "priority"::1 }} }} ==Specifying The Event== To specify the event, the function accepts a [[string]] containing the event's name. See [[Event Handling]] for standard NVSE event names. For example, '''"OnHitWith"''' is a valid event specifier. ==Arguments== The Handler script must be able to receive the standard arguments for the specified event. See [[Event Handling]] to learn which arguments come standard with which events. For example, a Handler script for '''"OnHitWith"''' must have the following arguments, specified in the '''"{ }"''' brackets: 
scn OnHitWithHandlerScript

ref rTarget
ref rWeapon

begin Function {rTarget, rWeapon}
Note that "rTarget" and "rWeapon" could be named differently. With this, when the Handler script is called, the provided arguments will be filled with the relevant information. For instance, when "OnHitWithHandlerScript" is called when the [[Player]] attacks SunnyREF with a 10mm Pistol, rTarget will be SunnyREF and rWeapon will be the 10mm Pistol. After the Handler script runs from an event triggering, all [[Variables]] are cleared, just as with regular [[UDF]]s. Note that the issue with [[string_var]]s and [[array_var]]s not being properly cleared is still pertinent here. While xNVSE 6.0+ fixes the issue, in order to maintain backwards compatibility with older versions of NVSE, string_vars should be cleared using [[Sv_Destruct]], and array_vars should be cleared using [[Ar_Null]]. ==Filters== Depending on the given event's arguments, up to two optional arguments can be supplied to [[SetEventHandler]] to filter events. Filters prevent event handlers being called unnecessarily. It is more efficient to set up a filter for the event than it is to filter inside the UDF Handler script. These filters need to be specified in "key"::value pairs. The key to the first filter, generally what the event happens to, should be '''"first"'''. The key to the second filter, generally what causes the event, should be '''"second"'''. If the filter's value is a [[Formlist]], the handler script will run if an event happens to or because of any element in the list, including elements in sublists. NVSE doesn't attempt to detect loops in Formlists, so avoid any variation of placing Formlist A in or under Formlist B after you've already placed Formlist B in or under Formlist A. Attempting to pass an array as a filter will fail, and the event handler will simply bypass the filter. When passing the [[Player]] as a filter, '''playerref''' must be used. For example, the following line will set up "OnHitWithHandlerScript" as a Handler script for the "OnHitWith" event, which will only run if the Target is SunnyREF and the Weapon is a 10mm Pistol: 
 SetEventHandler "OnHitWith" OnHitWithHandlerScript "first"::SunnyREF "second"::Weap10mmPistol
Note that in the above example, '''SunnyREF''' is a [[Reference]] and '''Weap10mmPistol''' is a [[Base Form]]. It is important to test and verify if an event's arguments return either References or Base Forms, and therefore if filters should be References or Base Forms. To this end, [[IsReference]] and [[IsFormValid]] can be useful. ==Priority== To determine when an event handler should run relative to other handlers, ''priority'' can be specified with the following syntax: '''"priority"::SomeInt'''. More information is in the '''Priority System''' section. ==Examples== 
scn FnOnHitByPlayer
ref rTarget
ref attacker

begin Function { rTarget, attacker }
	; we know the attacker must be the player, but the argument is still required
	print $rTarget+ " was hit by the player"
end
scn FnOnPlayerRestoreHealth
ref rTarget
int effectCode

begin Function { rTarget, effectCode }
	print "The player has been hit by a restore health effect"
end
scn FnOnLoadGame
string_var filename
int iSaveFileNameLength

begin Function { filename, iSaveFileNameLength }
	print "Loading game from " + $filename
        print "Save file name length: " + $iSaveFileNameLength 

        ;code
     
        sv_Destruct filename  ;not destroying strings in UDFs can cause savebloat, unless using newer versions of xNVSE. 
end
scn SomeQuestScript
begin gamemode
	if getGameRestarted
		SetEventHandler "OnHit" FnOnHitByPlayer "first"::playerref
		SetEventHandler "OnMagicEffectHit" FnOnPlayerRestoreHealth "first"::playerref "second"::RestoreHealth
		SetEventHandler "LoadGame" FnOnLoadGame
	endif
end
===Lambda example=== [[Lambda|Lambdas]] can be used instead of creating new scripts with SetEventHandler: 
begin GameMode
	if GetGameRestarted
		; multiline
		SetEventHandler "OnHit" (begin function {rTarget, rAttacker}
			print $rTarget + " was hit by the player"
		end) "first"::playerref

		; single-line only shorthand
		SetEventHandler "OnHit" ({rTarget, rAttacker} => print $rTarget + " was hit by the player") "first"::playerref
	endif
end
===Priority System (New in xNVSE 6.2.9)=== If unspecified, default priority while setting an event is 1.
Highest (greatest) priority means the handler will run first before other handlers, lowest means it will run last.
Handlers on the same priority will execute based on registration order. To debug priority conflicts, use [[IsEventHandlerFirst]] / [[IsEventHandlerLast]]. '''0''' is reserved as an '''invalid priority''', for use in other functions where we don't want to filter by priority.
It cannot be used for this function, as you must register a handler with a valid priority.
Besides that, any number, even a negative number, can be used as a priority. Priority CANNOT be set for LN event handlers, such as [[OnCellEnter]]. For more details, see [[Event Handler Priority System]]. Examples for how to specify priority: SetEventHandler "SomeEvent" SomeUDF "priority"::2 Will set an event handler with priority 2, which will be guaranteed to execute sooner than any handler that didn't specify a priority. The ''priority'' arg can be swapped around with the ''filter'' args. For example, these lines do the same thing: SetEventHandler "SomeEvent" SomeUDF "priority"::2 "first"::Player SetEventHandler "SomeEvent" SomeUDF "first"::Player "priority"::2 ==See Also== *[[Event_Handling]] *[[RemoveEventHandler]] *[[GetCurrentEventName]] *[[DispatchEvent]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets all of the second set of individual flags for a form to those specified by the bitmask int. See [[GetFlagsHigh]] for explanation of what the bits mean, and various warnings. |name = SetFlagsHigh |returnType = none |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = FlagsBitMask |Type = int |Optional = }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Target |Type = form |Optional = y }} }} ==Bit fields== This is adapted from FNVEdit. As a minimum, one should not make changes to anything labelled "Runtime". {| class="wikitable" |- ! Flag ! Bit ! Setting |- | 1 | 0 | Random Anim Start / High Priority LOD |- | 2 | 1 | Dangerous / Off limits (Interior cell) / Radio Station (Talking Activator) |- | 4 | 2 | Compressed |- | 8 | 3 | Can't wait / Platform Specific Texture / Dead |- | 16 | 4 | Unknown 21 |- | 32 | 5 | Load Started (Runtime) // set when beginning to load the form from save |- | 64 | 6 | Unknown 23 |- | 128 | 7 | Unknown 24 // Runtime might use it for "Not dead" on non actors. |- | 256 | 8 | Destructible (Runtime) |- | 512 | 9 | Obstacle / No AI Acquire |- | 1024 | 10 | NavMesh Generation - Filter |- | 2048 | 11 | NavMesh Generation - Bounding Box |- | 4096 | 12 | Non-Pipboy / Reflected by Auto Water |- | 8192 | 13 | Child Can Use / Refracted by Auto Water |- | 16384 | 14 | NavMesh Generation - Ground |- | 32768 | 15 | Multibound |} ==See Also== *[[GetFlagsHigh]] *[[SetFlagsLow]] *[[SetFormFlag]] *[[SetWeaponFlags1]] *[[SetWeaponFlags2]] *[[SetActorBaseFlagsLow]] *[[SetActorBaseFlagsHigh]] *[[SetBit]] *[[ClearBit]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets all of the first set of individual flags for a form to those specified by the bitmask int. See [[GetFlagsLow]] for explanation of what the bits mean, and various warnings. |name = SetFlagsLow |returnType = none |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = FlagsBitMask |Type = int |Optional = }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Target |Type = form |Optional = y }} }} ==Bit fields== This is adapted from FNVEdit. As a minimum, one should not make changes to anything labelled "Runtime". {| class="wikitable" |- ! Flag ! Bit ! Setting |- | 1 | 0 | ESM |- | 2 | 1 | Unknown 1 |- | 4 | 2 | Unknown 2 (Runtime) / Plugin selected (Editor) |- | 8 | 3 | Form cannot be saved (Runtime) / Plugin active (Editor) |- | 16 | 4 | Form initialized (Runtime) / Plugin cannot be active or selected (Editor) |- | 32 | 5 | Deleted |- | 64 | 6 | Border Region / Has Tree LOD / Constant / Hidden From Local Map / Plugin Endian |- | 128 | 7 | Turn Off Fire |- | 256 | 8 | Inaccessible |- | 512 | 9 | Casts shadows / On Local Map / Motion Blur |- | 1024 | 10 | Quest item / Persistent reference |- | 2048 | 11 | Initially disabled |- | 4096 | 12 | Ignored |- | 8192 | 13 | No Voice Filter |- | 16384 | 14 | Cannot Save (Runtime) |- | 32768 | 15 | Visible when distant |} ==See Also== *[[GetFlagsLow]] *[[SetFlagsHigh]] *[[SetFormFlag]] *[[SetWeaponFlags1]] *[[SetWeaponFlags2]] *[[SetActorBaseFlagsLow]] *[[SetActorBaseFlagsHigh]] *[[SetBit]] *[[ClearBit]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Used only within the 'Function' block type of a [[User Defined Function]], sets the return value to that specified. A UDF may return only a single value, but it may be of any type, including an array. If the function is called multiple times in the same function, the most recent is used by the call; this function is not a substitute for the keyword 'return', script execution will continue to the end of the block as normal. In another script, to bind the UDF function value to a variable, [[Let]] must be used rather than set, and to use in a conditional, [[Eval|if eval]] rather than just if. |name = SetFunctionValue |returnType = None |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = UDFReturnValue |Type = multi}} }} ==Example== 
scn MyUDF

Begin Function { }

    SetFunctionValue SunnyREF ; * this function simply returns Sunny Smiles

End
Then in some other script: 
ref rActor

let rActor := call MyUDF 

; rActor == SunnyREF
Another UDF example: 
Begin Function { }
   
    if GameHour > 12
        SetFunctionValue 1

    elseif PlayerREF.GetAV Strength > 5
        SetFunctionValue 1

    else
        SetFunctionValue 0

    endif

    ; * return true if it is after midday or player has more than 5 strength

End
==See Also== *[[User Defined Function]] *[[Call]] *[[Let]] *[[Eval]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:User Defined Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Sets the hair on the [[NPC]] BaseForm to a [[Hair|hairstyle]] predefined in the GECK. The change will not be apparent until the 3D reloads, which you can force using [[Update3D]]. Returns 1 on a successful change, 0 otherwise. |name = SetHair |returnType = bool |returnVal = success |referenceType = NPCRefr |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = NewHair |Type = form}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = NPC |Type = BaseForm |Optional = y}} |example = }} ==Example== 
SunnyREF.SetHair HairBun
SunnyREF.Update3D
==Notes== *According to the OBSE docs for this function: "Changes alter the base actor and are not saved in the savegame unless called on the player... Changes do appear to be saved for the player, but may not be visible until the game is reloaded." ==See Also== *[[GetHair]] *[[SetHairColor]] *[[SetEyes]] [[Category:Functions_(FO3)]] [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Hair Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the hair color RGB value, specified as a single int, for the specified NPC or [[calling reference]]. To see the changes, you must call [[Update3D]]. |name = SetHairColor |returnType = none |referenceType = Actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = NewRGB |Type = int}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Actor |Type = NPC |Optional = y}} |example = }} ==Example== 
SunnyREF.SetHairColor 1044735
SunnyREF.Update3D
==Note== In order to calculate the 7-digit integer that SetHairColor accepts based on regular RGB integers between 0 and 255 (see [[GetHairColor]] to have the system explained more fully), each color's integer needs to be converted to a 2-digit hex string, the hex strings concatted in BGR order, and the result converted to decimal: 
let svBlue := NumToHex iBlue, 2
let svGreen := NumToHex iGreen, 2
let svRed := NumToHex iRed, 2
let svFull := svBlue + svGreen + svRed
let iFull := ToNumber svFull, 1
==See Also== *[[GetHairColor]] *[[SetHair]] *[[SetHairLength]] *[[Update3D]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Hair Functions]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Sets the hair length on the [[NPC]] [[BaseForm]]. The hair length attribute is apparently unused by the vanilla game.
If an NPC reference is passed, its BaseForm is derived. |name = SetHairLength |returnType = none |referenceType = NPCRefr |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = HairLength |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = NPC |Type = BaseForm |Optional = y}} |example = }} ==Example== SunnyREF.SetHairLength 1.0 This is equivalent to: SetHairLength 1.0 GSSunnySmiles
==See Also== *[[GetHairLength]] *[[SetHair]] *[[SetHairColor]] *[[SetEyes]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(FO3)]] [[Category:Hair Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the file path to the DDS icon texture for the specified object or [[calling reference]]. |name = SetIconPath |returnType = none |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = NewPath |Type = string}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Object |Type = form |Optional = y}} }} ==Example== 
SetIconPath "MyCoolMod\Icons\new_icon_vodka.dds", Vodka
==See Also== *[[GetIconPath]] *[[SetTexturePath]] *[[SetModelPath]] *[[SetBipedModelPath]] *[[String Variable]] *[[Let]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Texture Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets a simple icon file path to a [[String Formatting|formatable string]] for the specified object. Although ported to NVSE, this function was deprecated in OBSE- it is better to use [[SetTexturePath]] instead. |name = SetIconPathEx |returnType = none |referenceType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = NewPath |Type = string}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Formatting |Type = }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Object |Type = form}} }} ==See Also== *[[SetTexturePath]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:Texture Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]]

revision.comment: This function is broken for everything but Biped forms
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets whether the object or [[calling reference]] can be used by the player. If a calling reference is given, its [[BaseForm]] is modified. Only works on Biped forms. |name = SetIsPlayable |returnType = none |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = IsPlayable |Type = bool }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Item |Type = Object |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== 
SetIsPlayable 1, SomeArmor
==Form Types== These form types can be Playable: 
10	Hair (not supported by this function; see LNSetIsPlayable)
11	Eyes (not supported by this function; see LNSetIsPlayable)
12	Race (not supported by this function; see LNSetIsPlayable)
24	Armor
40	Weapon (not supported by this function; see LNSetIsPlayable)
41	Ammo (not supported by this function; see LNSetIsPlayable)
86	Perk (not supported by this function; see LNSetIsPlayable or SetPerkFlag)
==See Also== *[[IsPlayable]] *[[LNIsPlayable]] *[[LNSetIsPlayable]] *[[SetPerkFlag]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the [[Mod Local Data]] value associated with the specified key for the mod to which the calling script belongs. The key must be a string; the data can be a string, number or object, but not an array. Keys are case-insensitive and unique - only one data value can be associated with a particular key. Returns true if the function succeeds, although it should not necessary to check this.
|name = SetModLocalData |returnType = Success:bool |referenceType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Key |Type = string}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Data |Type = multi}} |example = SetModLocalData "Sunny's old package", SomePackage }} =='''See Also'''== *[[Mod Local Data]] *[[GetModLocalData]] *[[ModLocalDataExists]] *[[RemoveModLocalData]] *[[GetAllModLocalData]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the model of the specified [[BaseForm]]. *If a [[calling reference]] is passed, then its [[BaseForm]] will be used instead. *This change is NOT savebaked. *The file path is relative to '''..\Data\Meshes\'''. *If the replaced model is already loaded, then it will need to be reloaded via functions like [[ReloadEquippedModels]] or [[Update3D]]. |name = SetModelPath |returnType = 1/0 |returnVal = success |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = NewPath |Type = string}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = form |Type = baseForm |Optional = y}} }} ==Example== This will set the model for all instances of '''GSSunnySmiles''' (the baseform of '''SunnyREF'''): 
sunnyREF.SetModelPath "MyCoolMod\Creatures\SomeModelFile.NIF"
==Notes== *This function was previously deprecated and removed in favor of [[SetModelPathEx]], however that function has multiple issues related to its baseForm arg not working properly in most cases. **'''SetModelPath''' will be re-introduced in [[xNVSE]] 6.3.6, and made to return 1/0 for success. ==See Also== *[[SetModelPathEx]] (newer, '''more broken''' version. Not recommended.) *[[GetModelPath]] *[[SetRefrModelPath]] - only works on references, but enables per-reference unique changes. *[[SetIconPath]] *[[SetBipedModelPathAlt]] (the non-alt versions are broken) *[[String Variable]] *[[Let]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]]

{{Deprecated | This function has been deprecated as it is bugged, but is still preserved in its original state as multiple mods depend on its behavior. Once xNVSE 6.3.6 is released to the public, '''[[SetModelPath]]''' can be used instead (not a typo, the original version was brought back and is simply more reliable).}} {{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the file path to the NIF model for the specified [[ref variable]] containing a [[BaseForm]]. If a [[calling reference]] is used instead, its BaseForm is modified. *This change is NOT savebaked. *The file path is relative to '''..\Data\Meshes\'''. *If the replaced model is already loaded, then it will need to be reloaded via functions like [[ReloadEquippedModels]] or [[Update3D]]. |name = SetModelPathEx |returnType = none |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = NewPath |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseForm |Type = refVar |Optional = y}} }} ==Example== This will set the model for all instances of '''GSSunnySmiles''' (the baseform of '''SunnyREF'''): 
sunnyREF.SetModelPathEx "MyCoolMod\Creatures\SomeModelFile.NIF"
=Bugs= Trying to pass an EditorID to the ''baseForm'' arg results in a compilation error. This happens due to the function supporting [[String Formatting]] and trying to have args that are placed after the format args, since all the format args are required to be variables, and an EditorID is not a variable. When using this function with a base form, the script won't compile unless a formatted string with all possible 20 format variables is used before passing the base form. You can use an empty string variable for such, like so: 
string_var s = ""
ref rVodka = Vodka
SetModelPathEx "MyCoolMod\Food\NewVodka.NIF%z%z%z%z%z%z%z%z%z%z%z%z%z%z%z%z%z%z%z%z" s s s s s s s s s s s s s s s s s s s s rVodka
The following example compiles, BUT DOES NOT WORK: 
ref rVodkaRef = Vodka
SetModelPathEx "MyCoolMod\Food\NewVodka.NIF" rVodkaRef
Indeed, the only method that works normally is passing a calling reference (where the function will use its baseform only). ==See Also== *[[SetModelPath]] - less buggy, re-introduced in xNVSE 6.3.6. *[[GetModelPath]] *[[SetBipedModelPath]] for armors. *[[SetIconPath]] *[[String Variable]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the height attribute of the specified NPC or [[calling reference]]. |name = SetNPCHeight |returnType = float |referenceType = Actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = NewHeight |Type = float}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Actor |Type = NPC |Optional = y}} |example = }} ==Example== 
SunnyREF.SetNPCHeight 25.0
==See Also== *[[GetNPCHeight]] *[[SetNPCWeight]] *[[SetScale]] *[[SetHairLength]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the weight attribute of the specified NPC or [[calling reference]]. It is unclear if this attribute has any affect in the vanilla game. |name = SetNPCWeight |returnType = none |referenceType = Actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = NewWeight |Type = float}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Actor |Type = NPC |Optional = y}} |example = }} ==Example== 
SunnyREF.SetNPCWeight 55.0
==See Also== *[[GetNPCWeight]] *[[SetScale]] *[[SetHairLength]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the name of the specified [[Base Object]], held by a [[Ref Variable]], to the [[string Variable|string]]. |name = SetNameEx |returnType = None |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = NewName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = ToRename |Type = ref }} }} ==Notes== * The function does not accept string variables directly. String variables must be converted to a string: ** Example: SetNameEX $sv_NewName rFormToRename * The base object to rename must be held in a [[Ref Variable]]. Example: set rFormToRename to Weap10mmSubmachineGun * The object to rename is not limited to Inventory items. [[Base Object]]s with a 'FULL - Name' sub record can be renamed, including Quest topics (Topic Text), etc. ==See Also== *[[SetName]] *[[GetName]] *[[LNSetName]] *[[LNGetName]] *[[SetActorFullName]] *[[SetFullNameAlt]] *[[String Formatting]] *[[String Variable]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:Name Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the form at the specified index in the list of [[Default Objects]] (forms). Warning: There is no check that the new form is valid. Default objects are viewable under the 'Gameplay' section of the GECK, or in GameForm.h (kDefaultObjectNames). |name = SetNthDefaultForm |returnType = none |referenceType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Index |Type = int}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = New |Type = form}} }} ==Example== 
SetNthDefaultForm 5, SomeValidForm
==See Also== *[[GetDefaultForms]] *[[GetNthDefaultForm]] *[[Array Variables]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the title of the nth rank in [[Faction]] to a [[String Formatting|formatable string]]. By default sets the male title, the optional [[boolean]] argument sets the female one. |name = SetNthFactionRankNameEx |returnType = none |referenceType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = NewName |Type = string}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Formatting |Type = }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Target |Type = faction}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = WhichRank |Type = int}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Female |Type = bool |Optional = y}} }} ==Example== 
SetNthFactionRankNameEx "Mistress of Dreams", KadathFaction, 3, 1
==See Also== *[[GetNthFactionRankName]] *[[GetFactionRankNames]] *[[GetFactionRankFemaleNames]] *[[GetNumRanks]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:Faction Functions]] [[Category:Name Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the required [[faction]] rank for ownership of the calling reference. |name = SetOwningFactionRequiredRank |referenceType = ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Rank |Type = int }} |example = SomeItemREF.SetOwningFactionRequiredRank 1 }} [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Faction Functions]] [[Category:Ownership Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the start location radius of a [[package]]. |name = SetPackageLocationRadius |returnType = none |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = package |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument|Name = radius |Type = float }} |example = SetPackageLocationRadius SomePackage, 200 }} ==See Also== *[[GetPackageLocationRadius]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:AI Functions]] [[Category:Package Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the distance/count of a [[package]]. |name = SetPackageTargetDistance |alias = SetPackageTargetCount |returnType = none |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = package |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = value |Type = int }} |example = SetPackageTargetDistance MyEscortPackage, 500 }} ==See Also== *[[GetPackageTargetDistance]] *[[SetPackageTargetReference]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:AI Functions]] [[Category:Package Functions]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Sets the quest item flag for the specified [[BaseForm]]. If only a [[calling reference]] is passed, its BaseForm is used. This change is save baked. |name = SetQuestItem |returnType = none |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = QuestFlag |Type = bool }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Item |Type = Object |Optional = y }} |referenceType = ref |example = SetQuestItem 1, MyToken }} ==Form Types== These form types can be Quest items: 
22: Talking Activator
24: Armor
25: Book
31: Misc
40: Weapon
41: Ammo
42: NPC
43: Creature
46: Key
47: Ingestible
103: Weapon Mod
==See Also== *[[IsQuestItem]] *[[SetQuestFlag]] *[[SetQuestObject]] *[[SetShowQuestItems]] [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Quest Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]]

revision.comment: now safe for Inventory Refs
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the number of items in a stack of items in the world. Note that while this function will change the count of any type of reference, it may have unpredictable results when used on references like NPCs which are not logically stackable. |name = SetRefCount |returnType = void |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = NewCount |Type = int }} }} ==Example== 
SomeAmmoClipREF.SetRefCount 30
==Notes== *If using an (x)[[NVSE]] version older than 6.2.6, this function should not be used to modify the quantity of an [[inventory reference]]. ==See Also== *[[GetRefCount]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:UI Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the value of the 'ref' variable specified by name for the calling reference or specified scriptable object. |name = SetRefVariable |returnType = none |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = VarName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = NewValue |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Parent |Type = Object |Optional = y }} |referenceType = ref }} ==Example== 
ref rActor

rActor.SetRefVariable "rHome", MyHomeXMarker

; Alternatively, (or required if rActor holds a base form, such as a quest): 

SetRefVariable "rHome", MyHomeXMarker, rActor
==See Also== *[[SetVariable]] (To set a numeric rather than ref var) *[[HasVariable]] (to check if a variable exists at all) *[[GetVariable]] (to return the existing value) *[[GetRefVariable]] (For ref rather than numeric variables) *[[GetArrayVariable]] (and for arrays) [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Script Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the path to the scope model of the specified weapon. |name = SetScopeModelPath |returnType = none |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ModelPath |Type = string |Optional = }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = ToSetFor |Type = weapon |Optional = y }} |referenceType = ref }} ==Example== 
SetScopeModelPath "path/to/model", WeapSniperRifle
==See Also== *[[GetWeaponHasScope]] *[[GetScopeModelPath]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]]

{{Deprecated | This function is broken. Use '''[[SetStringSetting]]''' instead.}} ---- {{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the value of a string [[Game Setting]], which is specified as a single [[String Formatting|formatable string]], separated using the pipe character ('{{!}}'): "SettingName{{!}}NewValue". When calling from the console, use '@' instead of the pipe character. |name = SetStringGameSettingEx |returnType = none |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = SettingName-and-NewValue |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Formatting |Type = }} |referenceType = }} ==Example== 
SetStringGameSettingEx "SettingName|NewValue"
==See Also== *[[GetStringGameSetting]] *[[SetStringIniSetting]] *[[SetStringSetting]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:GameSetting Functions]] [[Category:Deprecated Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the value of a string ini setting found in '''Fallout.ini''' / '''FalloutPrefs.ini''' / '''FalloutCustom.ini''', which is specified as a string in format: "settingName:settingGroup". |name = SetStringIniSetting |returnType = none |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Setting |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = NewValue |Type = string }} |referenceType = }} ==Example== 
SetStringIniSetting "settingName:settingGroup", "new value"
==Notes== *This function was '''broken''' prior to [[xNVSE]] 6.3.1, but has since been fixed. Existing scripts will need to be recompiled in order to make use of the fixed function behavior. ==See Also== *[[GetStringIniSetting]] *[[SetNumericINISetting]] / [[GetNumericINISetting]] *[[SetStringSetting]] / [[GetStringGameSetting]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:INI Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the form value of the specified [[:Category:Tokens|token]] in the inventory of the [[calling reference]]. |name = SetTokenRef |referenceType = Actor |returnType = none |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Token |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = NewValue |Type = anyform }} }} ==Example== 
ref rPartner
let rPartner := EasyPeteRef

SunnyREF.SetTokenRef MarriageToken, rPartner
==Note== *As of NVSE 4.5.6, the compiler disallows passing references directly to this function. However, it works correctly for base forms, or if you use an intermediary ref variable, or use the [[Script Compiler Override]]. ==See Also== *[[GetTokenRef]] *[[SetTokenValue]] *[[SetTokenValueAndRef]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Token Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the numeric value of the specified [[:Category:Tokens|token]] in the inventory of the [[calling reference]]. |name = SetTokenValue |referenceType = Actor |returnType = none |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Token |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = NewValue |Type = float }} }} ==Example== 
SunnyREF.SetTokenValue DiseaseToken, 65.1
==See Also== *[[GetTokenValue]] *[[SetTokenRef]] *[[SetTokenValueAndRef]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Token Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the numeric and form values of the specified [[:Category:Tokens|token]] in the inventory of the [[calling reference]]. |name = SetTokenValueAndRef |referenceType = Actor |returnType = none |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Token |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = NewValue |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = NewValue |Type = form }} }} ==Example== 
SunnyREF.SetTokenValueAndRef DiseaseToken, 65.1, DiseaseSpell
==See Also== *[[SetTokenRef]] *[[SetTokenValue]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Token Functions]]

{{Deprecated | This function has been deprecated as [[Bug: Children Tiles|it is bugged]], but is still preserved in its original state as multiple mods depend on its behavior. Use '''[[SetUIStringAlt]]''' instead.}} ---- {{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the value of a string UI trait. Trait name is of the format used by [[GetUIFloat]]. The path argument has two new capabilities. A path segment of "*" matches any child tile, and a path segment suffix of ":N" matches the N'th child tile. This is useful when accessing list box items, where the tile names are either identical, or unpredictable, or both. |name = SetUIStringEx |returnType = None |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = TraitName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = NewValue |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Formatting |Type = }} }} ==See Also== *[[String Formatting]] *[[SetUIString]] *[[SetUIFloat]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:Deprecated Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the value of the numeric variable specified by name for the calling reference or specified scriptable object. |name = SetVariable |returnType = none |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = VarName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = NewValue |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Parent |Type = Object |Optional = y }} |referenceType = ref }} ==Example== 
ref rCompanion

rCompanion.SetVariable "Waiting", 1

; Alternatively, (or required if rCompanion holds a base form, such as a quest): 

SetVariable "Waiting", 1, rCompanion
==See Also== *[[SetRefVariable]] (To set a ref rather than numeric var) *[[HasVariable]] (to check if a variable exists at all) *[[GetVariable]] (to return the existing value) *[[GetRefVariable]] (For ref rather than numeric variables) *[[GetArrayVariable]] (and for arrays) *[[Associating data with a reference]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Script Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the fire rate for the specified weapon or [[calling reference]]. |name = SetWeaponFireRate |alias = SetFireRate |returnType = none |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = FireRate |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Weapon |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = SetWeaponFireRate 20, WeapNVAssaultCarbine }} =='''See Also'''== *[[GetWeaponFireRate]] *[[SetWeaponLongBursts]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the first set of flags for a passed weapon or [[calling reference]] to the specified mask (int). Masks may be constructed using [[SetBit]] / [[ClearBit]]. |name = SetWeaponFlags1 |returnType = Mask:int |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = FlagsMask |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Weapon |Type = form |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== 
int FlagsMask

SetWeaponFlags1 FlagsMask, WeapNV9mmPistol
==Weapon Flags 1 Bit Fields== {| class="wikitable" |- ! Flag ! Bit ! Equipment Mask |- | 1 | 0 | ignores normal weapon resist |- | 2 | 1 | is automatic |- | 4 | 2 | has scope |- | 8 | 3 | can't drop |- | 16 | 4 | hide backpack |- | 32 | 5 | embedded weapon |- | 64 | 6 | don't use first-person IS animations |- | 128 | 7 | nonplayable |} ==See Also== *[[GetWeaponFlags1]] *[[SetWeaponFlags2]] *[[SetEquipmentSlotsMask]] *[[SetBit]] *[[ClearBit]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the second set of flags for a passed [[weapon]] or [[calling reference]] to the specified mask of the combined value of the bits (int). Bitmasks should be built using [[SetBit]] / [[ClearBit]]. |name = SetWeaponFlags2 |returnType = none |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = FlagsMask |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Weapon |Type = form |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== 
int FlagsMask

SetWeaponFlags2 FlagsMask, WeapNV9mmPistol
==Weapon Flags 2 Bit Fields== {| class="wikitable" |- ! Flag ! Bit ! Equipment Mask |- | 1 | 0 | player only |- | 2 | 1 | NPCs use ammo |- | 4 | 2 | no jam after reload |- | 8 | 3 | override action points |- | 16 | 4 | minor crime |- | 32 | 5 | range fixed |- | 64 | 6 | not used in normal combat |- | 128 | 7 | override damage to weapon mult |- | 256 | 8 | don't use third-person IS animations |- | 512 | 9 | short burst |- | 1024 | 10 | rumble alternate |- | 2048 | 11 | long burst |- | 4096 | 12 | scope has night vision |- | 8192 | 13 | scope from mod |} ==See Also== *[[GetWeaponFlags2]] *[[SetWeaponFlags1]] *[[SetEquipmentSlotsMask]] *[[SetBit]] *[[ClearBit]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Engine Differences (Fallout)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the long bursts value of the specified weapon or calling reference. |name = SetWeaponLongBursts |returnType = none |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = bLongBursts |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Weapon |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = SetWeaponLongBurst 1, WeapNVAssaultCarbine }} =='''See Also'''== *[[GetWeaponLongBursts]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the the required skill level for the specified weapon or calling reference to one. |name = SetWeaponRequiredSkill |alias = SetReqSkill |returnType = none |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = NewSkillLevel |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Weapon |Type = form |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== 
SetWeaponRequiredSkill 50, WeapNV9mmPistol
==See Also== *[[GetWeaponRequiredSkill]] *[[SetWeaponRequiredStrength]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the required strength for the specified weapon or calling reference to one. |name = SetWeaponRequiredStrength |alias = SetReqStr |returnType = none |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = NewStrength |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Weapon |Type = form |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== 
SetWeaponRequiredStrength 5, WeapNV9mmPistol
==See Also== *[[GetWeaponRequiredStrength]] *[[SetWeaponRequiredSkill]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the resist type for the [[Weapon]] [[BaseForm]]. If ''weapon'' is omitted and a [[calling reference]] is provided, that reference's BaseForm is used.
The new Resist Type is specified by its [[Actor Value Codes|AV code number]]. |name = SetWeaponResistType |alias = SetWeaponResist |returnType = none |referenceType = weaponRefr |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ResistanceCode |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = baseForm |Optional = y }} |example = SetWeaponResistType 61, Weap10mmPistol ; * 61 is EMP resistance. }} =='''See Also'''== *[[GetWeaponResistType]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the the skill type, which is specified by its [[Actor Value Codes|Actor Value code number]], for the specified weapon or [[calling reference]]. |name = SetWeaponSkill |returnType = none |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = SkillAVCode |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Weapon |Type = form |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== 
SetWeaponSkill 41, WeapNV9mmPistol ; * 41 corresponds to 'Guns' (FNV) or 'Small Guns' (FO3)
==See Also== *[[GetWeaponSkill]] *[[SetWeaponRequiredSkill]] (for the level of the skill required) [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Compares a formatable string to a string variable. Returns 0 if the strings are equal, 1 if the string variable occurs alphabetically before the formatted string, -1 if the string variable occurs alphabetically after the formatted string, or -2 if the comparison fails. Comparison is case-insensitive by default, with the optional flag reversing this. |name = Sv_Compare |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Formatable |Type = string}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Formatting |Type = |Optional = y}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = ToCompareWith |Type = string_var}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = CaseSensitive |Type = bool |Optional = y}} }} ==Example== 
string_var my_string

let my_string := "Alpha"

sv_Compare "Beta", my_string ; * returns 1
==Notes== * With [[Eval]] you can use the == operator to compare strings, which is the modern way to do string comparison. ==See Also== *[[String Formatting]] *[[String Variable]] *[[Let]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]]

revision.comment: Aligning category name with standards
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns a string variable constructed from the specified string and up to 20 [[String Formatting|formatting]] arguments. If no formatting is required, this function is not necessary, as the [[Let]] command can be used to assign string variables. Note that Let supports concatanation and the [[ToString|ToString($)]] function which can usually achieve the same result as formatting. |name = Sv_Construct |returnType = string_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Literal |Type = string}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Formatting |Type = }} }} ==Example== 
string_var my_string
int iValue
ref rActor

; Used to construct a formatted string:

let my_string := Sv_Construct "iValue equals %g and rActor's name is %n", iValue, rActor
Typically you can skip Sv_Construct and use concatanation with [[ToString|ToString($)]] instead of formatting, if you prefer: 
let my_string := "iValue equals " + $iValue + " and rActor's name is " + $rActor
==See Also== *[[String Variable]] *[[String Formatting]] *[[Let]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the number of occurrences of some sub string within a string variable. By default it is case insensitive, but an optional flag is available to change this. The first argument is a string, which may have optional formatting information thereafter. This is what you want to count. The second argument (ignoring the optional formatting) is a string variable within which to search for the first string. There are three optional int arguments at the very end: a start position and end position, which allow searching of a substring of the string_var, and finally, a boolean flag to enable case sensitivity. |name = Sv_Count |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = toFind |Type = string}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = 20xOptional |Type = formatting |Optional = y}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = within |Type = string_var}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = startPos |Type = int |Optional = y}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = numChars |Type = int |Optional = y}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = caseSensitiveFlag |Type = int |Optional = y}} }} ==Example== 
string_var my_message

let my_message := "The owls are not what they seem"

Sv_Count "owls", my_message ; will return 1
Sv_Count "foxes", my_message ; will return 0
Sv_Count "the", my_message ; will return 2: found in words 'The' and 'they'
Another example, maybe you want to be able to specify complex script instructions as a single string? 
string_var my_instructions
ref rActor

if eval (Sv_Count "sunny smiles", my_instructions)
    let rActor := SunnyREF
else
    let rActor := PlayerREF
endif

if eval (Sv_Count "kill", my_instructions) > 2
    ; kill actor if I get asked 3 times
    rActor.Kill
elseif eval (Sv_Count "move", my_instruction)
    ; move actor if I get asked one or more times. 
    rActor.MoveTo PlayerREF 
endif
==See Also== *[[String Variable]] *[[Let]] *[[Eval]] *[[Sv_Find]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Destroys the contents of up to ten [[String Variable]]s, resetting their value to NULL (empty, zero). Unlike other variables, string variables might not be automatically destroyed when their script is, and potentially cause bloat if not destructed. All string variables created by a mod are automatically destroyed when the mod is unloaded, however. |name = Sv_Destruct |returnType = None |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ToDestruct |Type = string_var}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = optional-1 |Type = string_var}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = ... |Type = }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = optional-9 |Type = string_var}} }} ==Example== 
string_var my_string

let my_string := "whatever"

Sv_Destruct my_string

; my_string == 0 (not initialized)

string_var s1
string_var s2
string_var s3

Sv_Destruct s1, s2, s3

; * destructed all 3 at once.
==See Also== *[[String Variable]] *[[Ar_Null]] (Equivalent to clear redundant [[array_var]]s) [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

revision.comment: Aligning category name with standards
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Erases the specified number of characters from the specified string starting at the specified position. Omitting the HowMany argument will erase all characters from startPos to the end of the string variable. Note that this function modifies a string variable in place, and returns nothing. |name = Sv_Erase |returnType = none |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Source |Type = string_var}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = StartPos |Type = int}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = HowMany |Type = int}} }} ==Example== 
string_var my_string

let my_string := "He never waves when he goes by"

Sv_Erase my_string, 2, 6

; my_string == "He waves when he goes by"

Sv_Erase my_string 8

; my_string == "He waves"
==See Also== *[[String Variable]] *[[Let]] *[[Sv_Destruct]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the position of the first occurrence of a string in a [[string variable]]. The first argument is a string, which may have optional formatting information thereafter. This is what you want to find. The second argument (ignoring the optional formatting) is a string variable within which to search for the first string. There are three optional int arguments at the very end: a start position and end position, which allow searching of a substring of the string_var, and finally, a boolean flag to enable case sensitivity. |name = Sv_Find |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = toFind |Type = string}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = 20xOptional |Type = formatting |Optional = y}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = within |Type = string_var}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = startPos |Type = int |Optional = y}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = numChars |Type = int |Optional = y}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = caseSensitiveFlag |Type = int |Optional = y}} }} ==Example== 
Sv_Find "this", MyStringVar
==Additional Context== 
string_var my_message

let my_message := "The owls are not what they seem"

Sv_Find "e", my_message ; will return 2, the first 'e'

Sv_Find "the", my_message ; will return 0, note case insensitivy- it found 'The'

Sv_Find "owls", my_message ; will return 4

Sv_Find "something that isn't in the string", my_message ; will return -1
Warning, remember it returns the position, not true or false. Hence: 
string_var my_instructions

let my_instructions := "Do Something"

if eval (Sv_Find "Do Something", my_instructions)
    ; Is False, because it returns zero as the found position
endif

if (Sv_Find "Not in string", my_instructions)
    ; Is True, because it returns -1 for 'not found', and that reduces to true
endif

; * If you just want to know if something was found, use this:

if eval (Sv_Find "Do Something", my_instructions) > -1
    ; Do something is somewhere in the variable
endif
==See Also== *[[String Variable]] *[[Let]] *[[Eval]] *[[Sv_Count]] *[[Sv_RegexSearch]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the ASCII code (0-127) for a single character in a string variable. |name = Sv_GetChar |returnType = ASCIICode:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Source |Type = string_var}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Position |Type = int}} }} ==Notes== *Although ported to NVSE, according to the OBSE docs this function is deprecated by [[Let]], which allows return of a single character: let MyChar := SomeStringVar[Pos]. ==See Also== *[[String Variable]] *[[Let]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:Deprecated Functions]]

revision.comment: Aligning category name with standards
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Inserts a formattable string (or string variable) into a target string variable at a given position. The target string variable is modified in place, and this function returns nothing. |name = Sv_Insert |returnType = none |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Insertion |Type = string}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Formatting |Type = }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Target |Type = string}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Position |Type = int}} }} ==Example== 
string_var my_string

let my_string := "he building in there?"

Sv_Insert "What's ", my_string, 0

; * my_string == "What's he building in there?" ; (length = 28)

Sv_Insert ", %n" SunnyREF, my_string, 26

; * my_string == "What's he building in there, Sunny Smiles?"
==See Also== *[[String Variable]] *[[String Formatting]] *[[Let]] *[[Sv_Replace]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]]

revision.comment: returns -1 for error
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the length of a string variable, which is the number of characters it contains. Returns -1 if the string was invalid. |name = Sv_Length |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ToMeasure |Type = string_var}} }} ==Example== 
string_var my_string

let my_string := "Ringo"

Sv_Length my_string ; returns 5
==Notes== *Remember that strings are indexed from 0, so the location of the final character is always: 
Sv_Length my_string - 1
==See Also== *[[String Variable]] *[[Let]] *[[Eval]] *[[Sv_Count]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the input [[string]] with each '%' character replaced by two '%' characters. The argument can be any expression evaluating to a string value. |name = sv_Percentify |returnType = string |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Source |Type = string}} }} ==Example== 
string_var my_string

let my_string := "100% gold"

let my_string := Sv_Percentify my_string

; my_string == "100%% gold"
==See Also== *[[String Variable]] *[[Let]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Replaces occurrences of an old [[string]] with a new string within a string variable. Both the old and new text is passed in a single format string argument, separated by a pipe character as in "Old{{!}}New" (when calling from the console, use '@' in place of the pipe character). By default this function replaces all occurrence in the entire string and is case insensitive. Optionally, a range may be specified by start character and length thereafter, a case sensitive flag and a maximum occurrences to replace may be provided. Note that this function modifies a string_var in place, and returns the count of number of replacements as an int, not a new string. |name = Sv_Replace |returnType = NumReplaced:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Old{{!}}New |Type = string}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Formatting |Type = }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Source |Type = string_var}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = StartPos |Type = int |Optional = y}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = SearchLen |Type = int |Optional = y}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = CaseSensitive |Type = bool |Optional = y}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = MaxReplacements |Type = int |Optional = y}} }} ==Example== 
string_var my_string

let my_string := "the cat is great"

Sv_Replace "great|terrible", my_string

; my_string is now: "the cat is terrible"
==See Also== *[[Sv_Find]] *[[Sv_RegexReplace]] *[[String Variable]] *[[String Formatting]] *[[Let]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the contents of a string variable to the passed formattable string. Although ported to NVSE, this function was deprecated in OBSE. It is simpler and better easier to use [[Let]] instead. |name = Sv_Set |returnType = none |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Literal |Type = string}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Formatting |Type = }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Target |Type = string_var}} }} ==Example== 
string_var my_string
int SomeVariable

let my_string := "You don't need to use sv_set to do this " + $SomeVariable

sv_set "but if you really want to, its like this %g" SomeVariable, my_string
==See Also== *[[Let]] *[[String Variable]] *[[String Formatting]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:Deprecated Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Splits the specified string based on the specified delimiter(s) and returns a regular array (list array) containing all substrings. Each character in the set of delimiters is matched individually, not as the complete string it is passed as. |name = Sv_Split |returnType = array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ToSplit |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Delimiters |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = JoinDelimiters |Type = bool |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== 
string_var my_string
array_var substrings

let my_string := "What's he building in there?"

let substrings := Sv_Split my_string, " " ; * split on 'space'

; * substrings contains: "What's", "he", "building", "in", "there?"

;2 delimiters

let my_string := "He never waves when he goes by. He's hiding something from the rest of us, and I think I know why."

let substrings := Sv_Split my_string, ",." 

; * substrings contains: "He never waves when he goes by", " He's hiding something from the rest of us", " and I think I know why"

;Join delimiters bool to split at a string.

let my_string := "He never waves when he goes by. He's hiding something from the rest of us, and I think I know why."

let substrings := Sv_Split my_string, "rest" 1

; * substrings contains: "He never waves when he goes by. He's hiding something from the ", " of us, and I think I know why."
==See Also== *[[String Variable]] *[[Let]] *[[Sv_SubString]] *[[Sv_Replace]] *[[Sv_Find]] *[[Sv_Erase]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns a substring of a string variable. Although ported to NVSE, this function was deprecated in OBSE. It is simpler and better to use [[NVSE Expressions|slice notation]] instead. |name = Sv_SubString |returnType = string |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Source |Type = string_var}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = StartPos |Type = int |Optional = y}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = HowMany |Type = int |Optional = y}} }} ==Example== 
string_var my_string
string_var my_substring

let my_string := "This is my string"

let my_substring := my_string[0:3] ; returns "This" the easy way (the first four characters in the string)

let my_substring := my_string[11:-1] ; returns "string" (a negative value such as -1 proceed from the right end of the string so -1 == string length)

let my_substring := Sv_SubString my_string 0, 4  ; returns "This" using this function
==See Also== *[[Let]] *[[String Variable]] *[[String Formatting]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:Deprecated Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the input string with all letters converted to lower case. |name = sv_ToLower |returnType = string |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Source |Type = string}} }} ==Example== 
string_var my_string
string_var my_other_string

let my_string := "Hello There"

let my_other_string := Sv_ToLower my_string

; my_other_string == "hello there"
==See Also== *[[Sv_ToUpper]] *[[ToUpper]], [[ToLower]] - returns the converted character code *[[String Variable]] *[[Let]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Attempts to convert the contents of a string variable to a number. Valid characters include digits, a leading sign, and an 'e' to indicate scientific notation. Conversion halts at the first invalid character. |name = Sv_ToNumeric |returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ToConvert |Type = string_var}} }} ==Example== 
string_var my_string
float MyFloat

let my_string := "-55"

let MyFloat := sv_ToNumeric my_string ; * -55.0
==See Also== *[[ToNumber]] (shorthand: '#') *[[String Variable]] *[[Let]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the input string with all letters converted to upper case. |name = sv_ToUpper |returnType = string |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Source |Type = string}} }} ==Example== 
string_var my_string
string_var my_other_string

let my_string := "What's he building?"

let my_other_string := Sv_ToUpper my_string

; my_other_string == "WHAT'S HE BUILDING?"
==See Also== *[[Sv_ToLower]] *[[String Variable]] *[[Let]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = TestExpr is passed any expression and returns True (1) if it succeeds, or False (0) if it fails; meaning it generates an [[NVSE Error]], and the error message is suppressed from appearing in the console. This is an alternative to validating one's data, which might require multiple lines. Essentially it is saying; "I know this line of code might fail, so tell me if it does and I will handle it. In this case the 'error' is normal, so don't alert the user". Comparable to the 'try', 'catch/except' found in some programming languages. Added by NVSE 4.1. |name = TestExpr |returnType = Result:bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = AnyLine |Type = expression }}}} ==Example== Simple example, instead of bothering to check a key exists with [[Ar_HasKey]]: 
if TestExpr SomeArray[SomeKey]
    ; SomeArray has SomeKey
else
    ; SomeArray does not have SomeKey, but don't show an error about that.
endif
The advantage that testexpr has over other validity checks is that the expression is actually performed inside the check if it can be. Suppose you want to keep a tally of the number of times the player encounters every NPC of a particular name: 
array_var aTally
ref rActor
string_var actor_name

let aTally := Ar_Construct "stringmap"
; ...

let actor_name := $rActor

if TestExpr aTally[actor_name] += 1
   ; do nothing. The increment worked because the actor_name is in the array already
else
    let aTally[actor_name] := 1 ; * first time we met actor_name, so create the entry
endif
Rather than 'do nothing' if testexpr returns true, consider only doing something if it returns false: 
if 0 == testexpr aTalley[actor_name] += 1 ; if "actor_name" is a valid key, the actual increase already happens here
   let aTalley[actor_name] := 1
endif
Suppose you are worried a ref variable might be empty for some reason, so your functions would fail: 
ref rActor
int iStrengthStat

if TestExpr iStrengthStat := rActor.GetAV "Strength"
    ; do nothing. The function worked, so rActor must be valid
else
    ; rActor isn't valid, so don't proceed further yet
    return
endif
TestExpr's return of a bool telling you if the expression succeeded or not doesn't mean that you can only use it in an if-condition: 
let iKey := ar_size aSomeArray
while -1 < (iKey -= 1)
   testexpr ar_erase aSomeArray[iKey][2], 3 ; if [2][3] exists for any aSomeArray[iKey], will do the erasure. If not, then not.
loop
==Notes== *Variables can be assigned using ':=', '+=', etc, using any NVSE aware command (such as TestExpr, [[Eval]], [[While]]...), not just with [[Let]]. ==See Also== *[[NVSE Error]] *[[Let]] *[[ToString|ToString($)]] *[[Array Variable]] *[[IsFormValid]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Debugging]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the ASCII code of the specified character converted to lower case. |name = ToLower |returnType = LowerCharacter:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = SourceCharacter |Type = int}} }} ==See Also== *[[ToUpper]] *[[Sv_ToLower]] *[[Sv_GetChar]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Converts a [[string]] that contains a number, to a number. You can use '#' as shorthand alias.
If ''reportError'' is true, an [[NVSE Error]] will be reported if converting the string to a number was unsuccessful. The error can be caught using [[TestExpr]]. * ''This arg was added in xNVSE 6.2.9.'' |name = ToNumber |returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ToConvert |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = hexidecimal |Type = bool |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = reportError |Type = bool |Optional = y |Value = 0 }} |referenceType = |example = int iNum string_var MyString let MyString := "1969" let iNum := #MyString ; 1969
float fNum let MyString := "3.14" let fNum := #MyString ; 3.14
let MyString := "00000ADD" let iNum := ToNumber MyString, 1 ; 2781
let MyString := "0x00000ADD" ;* will know it's a hex number if it starts with "0x" let iNum := ToNumber MyString ; 2781 }}
==See Also== *[[ToString]] (shorthand: '$') *[[NumToHex]] *[[GetFormIDString]] *[[String Variable]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category: String Functions]] [[Category: ID Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Attempts to convert an expression to a string. For numeric expressions, the result is a string representation of the numeric value. For objects, it is the name of the object if available; otherwise it is the [[formID]] expressed in hexadecimal notation. For string variables, conversion is only necessary for vanilla and old NVSE functions (eg: It is needed for [[PrintToConsole]], but not for [[Print]]) You can also use the shorthand alias '$', which is an [[NVSE Expressions|NVSE Expression]] |name = ToString |returnType = string |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ToConvert |Type = exp}} }} ==Alias== 
(string) $ToConvert:exp
==Example== 
string_var my_string
int iValue

let my_string := $SunnyREF

my_string == "Sunny Smiles" ; thats the name of the reference

let iValue := 13
let my_string := $iValue + " and " + $VCG01 ; an int and a quest

my_string == "13 and Ain't That a Kick in the Head"
You only need to use it with string variables for vanilla and old NVSE functions: 
string_var my_string

let my_string := "whatever"
MessageEx $my_string
