==See Also== *[[String Variable]] *[[Tutorial: String Variables 6|String Variables Tutorial: Passing strings as parameters]] *[[ToNumber]] *[[GetFormIDString]] (if you want the formid of a named object) *[[NumToHex]] *[[ActorValueToStringC]] *[[TypeOf]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category: String Functions]] [[Category: ID Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the ASCII code of the specified character converted to uppercase. |name = ToUpper |returnType = UpperCharacter:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = SourceCharacter |Type = int}} }} ==See Also== *[[ToLower]] *[[Sv_ToUpper]] *[[Sv_GetChar]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns a [[string]] representing the type of an expression. This is mainly useful for determining the type of an [[Array]] element at run-time. Possible return values: "String", "Number", "Form", "Array", "StringMap", "Map". |name = TypeOf |referenceType = |returnType = string |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = expression |Type = multi }} }} ==Example== 
if eval (TypeOf SomeArray[SomeKey]) == "Number"
    ; Do something that works on numbers
endif
==See Also== *[[GetType]] *[[IsReference]] *[[IsFormValid]] *[[Eval]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Debugging]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Unequips the calling object on its owner. |name = UnequipMe |returnType = none |referenceType = reference |arguments = |example = ; Within an object script UnequipMe }} ==See Also== *[[EquipMe]] *[[EquipItem2]] *[[RemoveMe]] *[[RemoveMeIR]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category: Object Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Updates the 3D visual representation of the [[calling reference]], which is useful to reflect changes to its models or textures. |name = Update3D |returnType = none |referenceType = reference |arguments = }} ==Example== 
SunnyREF.Update3D
==Notes== *This function '''does not work on the player character'''. Use [[UpdatePlayer3D]], instead. *Using Update3D on an NPC will cause it to re-evaluate its inventory. *Using this in a code block that updates frequently (Such as gamemode in object scripts) can cause the object to become invisible, be sure to condition your code properly so that this command doesn't run endlessly. ==See Also== *[[UpdatePlayer3D]] *[[ReloadEquippedModels]] *[[Reset3DState]] *[[SetHair]] *[[SetEyes]] *[[AttachModel]] *[[InsertNode]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Actor_Functions]] [[Category:Model Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the length of a vector3. |name = V3Length |alias = V3Len |returnType = Length:float |referenceType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Vector_X |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Vector_Y |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Vector_Z |Type = float }} }}
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Algohol Functions]] [[Category:Math Functions]]

revision.comment: Add Math Category
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Calculates a normalized vector3. |name = V3Normalize |alias = V3Norm |returnType = none |referenceType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Out_X |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Out_Y |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Out_Z |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Vector_X |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Vector_Y |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Vector_Z |Type = float }} }} ==Example== 
float vX
float vY
float vZ

V3Normalize "vX" "vY" "vZ" vX vY vZ

; The result will be the normalized (vX; vY; vZ) vector.
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Algohol Functions]] [[Category:Math Functions]]

revision.comment: https
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Whilst a specified condition is met, the code section below, until the [[loop]] command, will repeat. Within while loops, the command [[continue]] may be used to skip remaining loop code and return to the while line. In the same context, the command [[break]] may be used to end the loop immediately, regardless of the while condition. The same effect could be achieved using [[label]], [[goto]], if and endif statements, but this method allows for fewer lines and improved readability. Care must be taken to avoid the possibility of infinite loops- a while condition that never becomes false will immediately [[Causes of CTDs|crash the game]]. |name = while |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Condition |Type = expression }} }} ==Examples== 
while SomeCondition  ; * this is similar to an 'if' statement
    ; do something
loop ; * this is similar to an 'endif' statement
The code below will [[Print|print]] the numbers 1, 2, 3, 4 and 5 to the console: 
int iCount
let iCount := 0      ; * 'set iCount to 0...'

while iCount < 5     ; * 'if iCount is less than 5...'
    let iCount += 1  ; * 'add 1 to iCount...'
    Print $iCount    ; * 'then print it to the console...'
loop                 ; * 'then go back to the while line.'
The code below will print the numbers 1 and 3 to the console. The number 2 is skipped due to the continue, whilst numbers 4 and 5 are skipped because of the break. 
set iCount to 0
while iCount < 5
    let iCount += 1
    if iCount == 2
        continue ; go back to the loop start
    elseif iCount == 4
        break ; end the loop immediately
    endif
    Print $iCount
loop
While allows NVSE expressions, including assignments, in the expression that follows it, so a shorter alternative for the example above is: 
set iCount to 0
while 5 > (iCount += 1)
   if iCount == 2
     continue
   elseif iCount == 4
     break
   endif
   Print $iCount
loop
'''NOTE:''' for `while myNumber > (iCount += 1)`, if this while loop is checked repeatedly (i.e. is placed in a `begin GameMode block`, iCount will keep increasing even after the loop is done, unless it's reset like in the example. If left unattended, this could lead to the iCount variable store a really high numbers. However, this does not cause savebloat, as internally it will use the same amount of space. While is often used to loop through a regular array, formlist or string var - all types of data that use consecutive integer indices starting at 0 and ending at (total size - 1). The most efficient forwards loop for those is written like 
let iSize := ar_size aArray  ; use ListGetCount for formlists, and sv_length for string vars
let iKey := -1
while iSize > (iKey += 1)
  ; do stuff with the value held at aArray[iKey]
loop
However, all those data types also automatically shift their indices when an element is erased, so erasing an element during a forwards loop will not only skip the next element if you perform it on the element being currently iterated, but will also end up with errors as the iSize variable will be no longer up to date. You can avoid that by using a backwards loop, since any shifting of the indices will not affect the next elements that will be iterated: 
let iKey := ar_size aArray
while -1 < (iKey -= 1)
  if somecondition
    ar_erase aArray, iKey
  endif
loop
==See Also== *[[Foreach]] *[[Eval]] *[[Label]] *[[Goto]] *[[Let]] ==External Links== *[https://obse.silverlock.org/obse_command_doc.html#OBSE_Expressions List of supported expression found in OBSE, these are equivalent in NVSE] *[https://www.loverslab.com/topic/26749-tutorial-nvse4-part-1-syntax-and-expressions/ Tutorial on syntax and expressions in NVSE 4] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Commands]] [[Category:Scripting]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the [[package]]'s target to the chosen [[reference]]. If ''target'' is not specified, then the [[calling reference]] will be used instead. |name = SetPackageTargetReference |returnType = none |referenceType = targetRefr |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = package |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = target |Type = ref }} |example = SetPackageTargetReference MyPackage, MyRef }} ==See Also== *[[GetPackageTargetDistance]] *[[SetPackageTargetDistance]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:AI Functions]] [[Category:Package Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the current number of rounds in the clip of the player's weapon. |name = SetPlayerCurrentAmmoRounds |returnType = none |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument | Name = count | Type = int }} |example = SetPlayerCurrentAmmoRounds 512 }} [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(Command Extender)]] [[Category:Ammo Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the mod effect used by a weapon in a specified mod slot (1-3). |name = GetWeaponItemModEffect |returnType = effect:int |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = modSlot |Type = int(1-3) }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = form }} }} ==Example== 
int ModEffect
set ModEffect to GetWeaponItemModEffect 1 WeapNV9mmPistol
Returns the following values: ==Mod Effect== *0 = None *1 = Increase Weapon Damage *2 = Increase Clip Capacity *3 = Decrease Spread *4 = Decrease Weight *5 = Regenerate Ammo (shots) *6 = Regenerate Ammo (seconds) *7 = Decrease Equip Time *8 = Increase Rate of Fire *9 = Increase Projectile Speed *10 = Increase Max. Condition *11 = Silence *12 = Split Beam *13 = VATS Bonus *14 = Increase Zoom *15 = *16 = Suppressor ==See Also== *[[Weapon Mod Codes]] *[[SetWeaponItemModEffect]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the NPCs age class. |name = GetAgeClass |returnType = int |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = actor |Type = reference |Optional = y }} |example = int AgeClass set AgeClass to GetAgeClass MyNPC }} ==Class== *-1 = Undefined *0 = Child *1 = Default *2 = Old *3 = Old Aged
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Dispatches a [[User-Defined Event]] that any mod can register to with [[SetEventHandler]] / [[SetEventHandlerAlt]]. This allows for easy communication between mods. The listening mod receives a [[StringMap|string map array]] in its handler script. By default it will contain the event name under the "eventName" key, and the dispatching mod name under the "eventSender" key, both as strings. The dispatching mod can choose to override this behavior by * setting an optional ''infomap'' parameter to a custom stringmap that listening mod will receive. * setting an optional ''AlternativeSenderString'' string that will replace "eventSender" key. |name = DispatchEvent |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = eventID |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = infomap |Type = stringmap |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = AlternativeSenderString |Type = string |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== This example script dispatches a custom event to all listeners. Dispatching mod name is '''"MyMod.esp"''' 
scn BasicDispatch

Begin GameMode

  DispatchEvent "My Mod Is Ready"

endif

End
This example listener script registers handler scripts to receive two custom events - '''"My Mod Is Ready"''' and '''"Sending Local Actor Refs"''' 
scn RegisteringHandlers

Begin GameMode

  if GetGameLoaded
    SetEventHandler "My Mod Is Ready", fnBasicEventHandler
    SetEventHandler "Sending Local Actor Refs", fnExtendedEventHandler
  endif

End
This example handler script receives the custom event '''"My Mod Is Ready"''' and parses its default parameters: ''eventName'' and ''eventSender''. 
scn fnBasicEventHandler

array_var aArgs

Begin Function {aArgs}

  Print "Received '" + $aArgs["eventName"] + "' from '" + $aArgs["eventSender"] + "'"
  ; "Received 'My Mod Is Ready' from 'MyMod.esp'
  ; do stuff

End
This example script dispatches a custom event with an optional ''infomap'' and a custom string for ''eventSender'' parameter. 
scn ExtendedDispatch

array_var aArgs

Begin GameMode

    let aArgs := ar_construct "stringmap"
    let aArgs["list"] := GetRefs 200
    DispatchEvent "Sending Local Actor Refs", aArgs, "ExtendedDispatch"

End
This example handler script receives the custom '''"Sending Local Actor Refs"''' event with a custom ''infomap'' and ''eventSender''. 
scn fnExtendedEventHandler
array_var aArgs

array_var aRefs

Begin Function {aArgs}

  Print "Received '" + $aArgs["eventName"] + "' from script '" + $aArgs[eventSender] + "'"
  ; "Received 'Sending Local Actor Refs' from script 'ExtendedDispatch'
  let aRefs := aArgs["list"]
  ; do stuff

End
==See Also== *[[NVSE Event Handling]] *[[SetEventHandler]] / [[SetEventHandlerAlt]] *[[RemoveEventHandler]] *[[GetCurrentEventName]] ==External Links== *[http://www.loverslab.com/topic/39417-tutorial-nvse4-part-5-event-handlers-user-defined-events-udes/ Tutorial on Event Handling and User-Defined Events] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the [[Class]] of the specified [[Actor]]. |name = GetClass |returnType = class |referenceType = Actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Actor |Type = NPC |Optional = y}} }} ==Example== 
ref rClass
let rClass := GetClass SomeNPCBaseForm
if rClass == BoomerClass
    ; blow something up
endif

let rClass := SomeNPCRef.GetClass
if rClass == Drunk
  ; play a drinking anim
endif
==See Also== *[[GetIsClass]] (to return a boolean) *[[GetNameOfClass]] (to return a string) *[[SetClass]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the name of the [[Class]] of the specified [[Actor]] ([[Base Form]] or [[Reference]]), as a [[string]]. |name = GetNameOfClass |returnType = string |referenceType = Actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Actor |Type = NPC }} }} ==Example== 
string_var my_string
let my_string := GetNameOfClass SomeNPCBaseForm
MessageEx "The race of %n is %z", SomeNPCBaseForm, my_string

string_var my_string
let my_string := EasyPeteRef.GetNameOfClass
MessageEX "The class of %n is %z", EasyPeteRef, my_string
==See Also== *[[GetClass]] *[[String Variable]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Name Functions]] [[Category:String Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = When called on a reference with ref syntax, returns True (1) if the calling reference is persistent. When passing a reference as parameter, returns true if the reference is flagged as a quest item. |name = GetPermanent |referenceType = reference |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ItemOrActor |Type = Ref }} }} ==Example== 
if SomeRef.GetPermanent
    ; ref is persistent
endif

if GetPermanent SomeRef
    ; ref is quest item
endif
==Note== *In contrast with [[IsQuestItem]], GetPermanent does work with actors in order to figure out if they're flagged as quest items. It only accepts references, however, not base forms. ==See Also== *[[SetPermanent]] *[[IsPersistent]] *[[IsQuestItem]] [[Category:Debugging]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the flags of the specified [[Eyes]] form as a mask of the combined value of the bits (int). |name = GetEyesFlags |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = eyes |Type = BaseForm |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== 
int FlagsMask
set FlagsMask to GetEyesFlags rEyes
if FlagsMask == (SetBit FlagsMask, 1)
  ; "not male"
endif
==Bits== *1 (bit 0) = playable *2 (bit 1) = not male *4 (bit 2) = not female ==See Also== *[[SetEyesFlags]] *[[GetBit]] *[[SetBit]] *[[ClearBit]] *[[GetEquipmentSlotsMask]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Eye Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the flags of the specified eyes form to the specified mask of the combined value of the bits (int). |name = SetEyesFlags |returnType = none |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = eyes |Type = BaseForm |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = FlagsMask |Type = int }} }} ==Example== 
int FlagsMask
SetEyesFlags rEyes, FlagsMask
==Bits== *1 (bit 0) = playable *2 (bit 1) = not male *4 (bit 2) = not female ==See Also== *[[GetEyesFlags]] *[[GetBit]] *[[SetBit]] *[[ClearBit]] *[[GetEquipmentSlotsMask]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Eye Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns the flags of the specified hair form as a mask of the combined value of the bits (int). |name = GetHairFlags |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = hair |Type = BaseForm |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== 
int FlagsMask
set FlagsMask to GetHairFlags rHair
if FlagsMask == (SetBit FlagsMask, 1)
  ; "not male"
endif
==Bits== *1 (bit 0) = playable *2 (bit 1) = not male *4 (bit 2) = not female *8 (bit 3) = fixed color ==See Also== *[[SetHairFlags]] *[[GetBit]] *[[SetBit]] *[[ClearBit]] *[[GetEquipmentSlotsMask]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Hair Functions]] [[Category:Flag Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the flags of the specified hair form to the specified mask of the combined value of the bits (int). |name = SetHairFlags |returnType = none |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = hair |Type = BaseForm |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = FlagsMask |Type = int }} }} ==Example== 
int FlagsMask
SetHairFlags rHair, FlagsMask

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the attack animation of the [[Weapon]] [[BaseForm]]. If ''weapon'' is omitted and a [[calling reference]] is provided, that reference's BaseForm is used. Return values are as follows:
  0:    Default
  1:    Attack 3
  2:    Attack 4
  3:    Attack 5
  4:    Attack 6
  5:    Attack 7
  6:    Attack 8
  7:    Attack Left
  8:    Attack Loop
  9:    Attack Right
 10:    Attack Spin
 11:    Attack Spin 2
 12:    Attack Throw
 13:    Attack Throw 2
 14:    Attack Throw 3
 15:    Attack Throw 4
 16:    Attack Throw 5
 17:    Place Mine
 18:    Place Mine 2
 19:    Attack 9
 20:    Attack Throw 6
 21:    Attack Throw 7
 22:    Attack Throw 8

revision.comment: italics
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the [[ammo]] (or [[FormList]] of Ammo) used by the specified [[Weapon]] [[BaseForm]]. If ''weapon'' is omitted and a [[calling reference]] is provided, that reference's BaseForm is used. |name = GetWeaponAmmo |alias = GetAmmo |returnType = baseForm |returnVal = ammoOrFormlist |referenceType = weaponRefr |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = baseform |optional = y }} }} ==Example== 
ref rAmmo
set rAmmo to GetWeaponAmmo Weap10mmPistol
==Notes== *Beware that in ''Fallout: New Vegas'', weapons may use either an ammo ([[Form Type IDs|typecode: 41]]) or a form list (typecode: 85) containing multiple ammos for this field. To be safe, one can use [[GetType]] to check the return. Also, Weapons that do not use ammo will return 0. ==See Also== *[[SetWeaponAmmo]] *[[GetPlayerCurrentAmmo]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Engine Differences (Fallout)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:Ammo Functions]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

revision.comment: add priority system documentation
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Removes the specified script as an event handler for the specified event.
Two optional filter arguments can be supplied as '''key::value''' pairs to filter events according to the target and/or object.
If both filters are omitted, all event handlers matching the script and event will be removed. If only one filter is omitted, all handlers matching the script, event, and the other filter will be removed.
An optional priority argument can be supplied as '''"priority"::value'''. More information is in the '''Priority System''' section.
Returns true if at least one matching event handler was found and removed, false otherwise. |name = RemoveEventHandler |returnType = bool |returnVal = Removed |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = eventID |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = FunctionScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Filter1 |Type = pair |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Filter2 |Type = pair |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Priority |Type = pair |Optional = y |Value = "priority"::0 }} }} ==Priority System (New in xNVSE 6.2.9)== If unspecified, default priority while removing an event is 0, which is reserved as an '''invalid priority''' (meaning we don't want to filter by any priority). So by default, priority is ignored and all handlers with matching filters will get removed.
For more details, see [[Event Handler Priority System]].
===Examples=== Say there was an event handler registered with priority = 2: SetEventHandler "SomeEvent" SomeUDF "priority"::2
To remove this handler, we could use: RemoveEventHandler "SomeEvent" SomeUDF This would remove all handlers with ''SomeUDF'', regardless of priority
We could also use: RemoveEventHandler "SomeEvent" SomeUDF "priority"::2 This would only remove all handlers with ''SomeUDF'' that have priority = 2.
The ''priority'' arg can be swapped around with the ''filter'' args. For example, these lines do the same thing: RemoveEventHandler "SomeEvent" SomeUDF "priority"::2 "first"::Player RemoveEventHandler "SomeEvent" SomeUDF "first"::Player "priority"::2
==See Also== *[[Event_Handling]] *[[SetEventHandler]] / [[SetEventHandlerAlt]] *[[DispatchEvent]] *[[GetCurrentEventName]]
==External Links== *[http://www.loverslab.com/topic/39417-tutorial-nvse4-part-5-event-handlers-user-defined-events-udes/ Tutorial on Event Handling and User-Defined Events]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = When called from within a [[SetEventHandler]]-style event handler, returns the name of the event currently being handled, as [[Event_Handling|defined]]. |name = GetCurrentEventName |returnVal = string |returnType = EventName |example = Let sv_event := GetCurrentEventName if eval sv_event == "OnPackageStart" ; do something elseif eval sv_event == "OnPackageChange" ; do something else endif }} ==See Also== *[[Event Handling]] *[[SetEventHandler]] *[[RemoveEventHandler]] *[[DispatchEvent]]
==External Links== *[http://www.loverslab.com/topic/39417-tutorial-nvse4-part-5-event-handlers-user-defined-events-udes/ Tutorial on Event Handling and User-Defined Events]
[[Category:Functions (NVSE)]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Reorders a UI list box such as the item list of the Barter menu. |name = SortUIListBox |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = pathName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = sortSpec |Type = string }} |example = SortUIListBox "BarterMenu/*:0/*:5/*:9" "-_IsBarterSelected,@string,-#_value" Sorts the barter menu alphabetically, and then by item condition, with all items selected for barter before all other items. }} ==Notes== * ''pathName'' gives the name of the list box to sort, using the same syntax as [[GetUIFloat]]. * ''sortSpec'' defines how the list box items should be sorted. A sort specification looks like "keySpec0[,keySpec1[,...]][$destinationPath[$incrementPath]]" where [] indicate optional parts. * A ''keySpec'' looks like "[-][#][@]path" where "-" reverses the normal sort order for this key "#" sorts this string field numerically (default is alphabetic sort) "@" sorts this item name disregarding quanties and mod indicators E.g. "9mm Pistol (2)" and "9mm Pistol+" sort as "9mm Pistol" * ''destinationPath'' describes which trait to modify. The default is ''_y''. * ''incrementPath'' describes what value to increment ''destinationPath'' by. The default is ''height''. ==See Also== *[[GetUIFloat]]
[[Category:Functions (NVSE)]] [[Category:Console Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions]]

{{Incomplete}} {{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Adds an animation to the base actor's or creature's animation list. |referenceType = actorRef |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = animName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseActor |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = npcRef.AddAnimation "AnimationName" }} ==See Also== *[[GetNthAnimation]] *[[DelAnimation]] *[[DelAnimations]] [[Category:Functions (NVSE)]] [[Category:Animation Functions]]

{{Incomplete}} {{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Removes an animation from the base actor's or creature's animation list. |referenceType = actorRef |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = animName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseActor |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = npcRef.DelAnimation "AnimationName" }} ==See Also== *[[AddAnimation]] *[[DelAnimations]] *[[GetNthAnimation]] [[Category:Functions (NVSE)]] [[Category:Animation Functions]]

{{Incomplete}} {{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Removes all animations from the base actor's or creature's animation list. |referenceType = actorRef |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = baseActor |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = npcRef.DelAnimations }} ==See Also== *[[GetNthAnimation]] *[[DelAnimation]] *[[AddAnimation]] [[Category:Functions (NVSE)]] [[Category:Animation Functions]]

{{Incomplete}} {{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Returns name of Nth animation of the base actor's or creature's animation list. |referenceType = actorRef |returnType = string_var |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = index |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = baseActor |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = npcRef.GetNthAnimation 0 }} ==See Also== *[[AddAnimation]] *[[DelAnimation]] *[[DelAnimations]] [[Category:Functions (NVSE)]] [[Category:Animation Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Identical to the [[OutputLocalMapPictures]] console command, but overrides the [[uGridsToLoad]] ini setting acording to the value set by [[SetOutputLocalMapPicturesGrids]]. |name = OutputLocalMapPicturesOverride |alias = OLMPOR |returnType = void |arguments = }} ==Notes== *This function is broken, and behaves exactly like [[OutputLocalMapPictures]], no matter the value set by SetOutputLocalMapPicturesGrids.
==See Also== *[[SetOutputLocalMapPicturesGrids]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the value with which to override [[uGridsToLoad]] when generating local maps with [[OLMPOR]]. |name = SetOutputLocalMapPicturesGri..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the value with which to override [[uGridsToLoad]] when generating local maps with [[OLMPOR]]. |name = SetOutputLocalMapPicturesGrids |alias = SetOLMPGrids |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = uGridsOverride |Type = int }} }} ==See Also== *[[OutputLocalMapPicturesOverride]] (OLMPOR). *[[OutputLocalMapPictures]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.0.8 |summary = Sets the numeric value of a [[float]] UI trait.
The trait name is of the format "MenuName/ElementName/ElementName/.../TraitName" as defined in the XML hierarchy for the desired menu, i.e. "MessageMenu/NoGlow_Branch/MM_Title/x".
Returns 1 (true) on successful change, 0 (false) otherwise. *Prior to [[xNVSE]] 6.2.5, this function did not return anything.
Supersedes [[SetUIFloat]], which is [[Bug: Children Tiles|bugged for templated children tiles]] |name = SetUIFloatAlt |returnType = bool |returnVal = success |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = traitName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = float }} }} ==Notes== *The traitName argument to Get/SetUI(Float/String) has two extra capabilities. A path segment of "*" matches any child tile, and a path segment suffix of ":N" matches the N'th child tile. This is useful when accessing list box items, where the tile names are either identical, or unpredictable, or both.
*Do not use this command with [[MCM]] since it depends on bugged behaviour. Instead use [[SetUIFloat]].
==See Also== *[[GetUIFloatAlt]] *[[SetUIStringAlt]] *[[GetUIString]] *[[SetUIFloatMultiple]]
[[Category:Functions (NVSE)]] [[Category:UI Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.0.8 |summary = Looks up a UI trait by name and returns its numeric [[float]] value. The trait name is of the format "MenuName/ElementName/ElementName/.../TraitName" as defined in the XML hierarchy for the desired menu, i.e. "MessageMenu/NoGlow_Branch/MM_Title/x". Returns -999 if UI trait was not found. Supersedes [[GetUIFloat]], which is [[Bug: Children Tiles|bugged for templated children tiles]].
|name = GetUIFloatAlt |returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = traitName |Type = string }} |example = GetUIFloatAlt "RepairServicesMenu/NOGLOW_BRANCH/RSM_MainRect/RSM_RepairList/RSM_RepairListTemplateRect:4/string" Returns the name of the 4th item on the Repair Services menu. GetUIFloatAlt "BarterMenu/*:0/*:5/*:9/lb_scrollbar/_number_of_items" Returns the number of items in the Barter menu. }} ==Notes== *The traitName argument to Get/SetUI(Float/String) has two extra capabilities. A path segment of "*" matches any child tile, and a path segment suffix of ":N" matches the N'th child tile. This is useful when accessing list box items, where the tile names are either identical, or unpredictable, or both. *Do not use this command with [[MCM]] since it depends on bugged behaviour. Instead use [[GetUIFloat]].
==See Also== *[[SetUIFloatAlt]] *[[SetUIStringAlt]] *[[GetUIString]]
[[Category:Functions (NVSE)]] [[Category:UI Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.0.8 |summary = Sets the value of a [[string]] UI trait to a [[Formatted String]]. The trait name is of the format "MenuName/ElementName/ElementName/.../TraitName" as defined in the XML hierarchy for the desired menu, i.e. "MessageMenu/NoGlow_Branch/MM_Title/x". Returns 1 (true) on successful change, 0 (false) otherwise. *Prior to [[xNVSE]] 6.2.5, this function did not return anything. Supersedes [[SetUIString]] and [[SetUIStringEx]], which are [[Bug: Children Tiles|bugged for templated children tiles]]. |name = SetUIStringAlt |returnType = bool |returnVal = success |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = TraitName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = NewValue |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Formatting |Type = }} }} ==Notes== *The path argument has two extra capabilities. A path segment of "*" matches any child tile, and a path segment suffix of ":N" matches the N'th child tile. This is useful when accessing list box items, where the tile names are either identical, or unpredictable, or both. *Do not use this command with [[MCM]] since it depends on bugged behaviour. Instead use [[SetUIStringEx]]. ==See Also== *[[SetUIFloatAlt]] / [[ModUIFloat]] *[[GetUIFloatAlt]] *[[GetUIString]] [[Category:Functions (NVSE)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:String Functions]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Identical to [[AddSpell]] but without the UI message. |name = AddSpellNS |returnType = void |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = spell |Type = baseform }} }}
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the nearest whole number that is greater than a float |name = Ceil |returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = float |Type = float }} |example = float fMyNum
let fMyNum := Ceil 0.1 fMyNum == 1.0
let fMyNum := Ceil 1.6 fMyNum == 2.0
let fMyNum := Ceil 2.0 fMyNum == 2.0
let fMyNum := Ceil 3.5 fMyNum == 4.0 }} ==See Also== *[[Floor]] *[[Round]] *[[Ceil (UI Directive)]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Math Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Compares a form's FullName to another form's FullName.
''firstForm'' can be provided as either a reference, or a baseForm, depending on where it is supplied. In either case, the function uses ''firstForm'''s [[BaseForm]] to get its name.
If ''firstForm'' and ''secondForm'''s names match completely, returns 0. Else, returns either 1 or -1, depending on which form had the longest name. If the ''firstForm'' is the longest, will return -1, and if ''secondForm'' is longest, will return 1. |name = CompareNames |returnType = float |referenceType = [firstForm:reference] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = secondForm |Type = baseForm }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = firstForm |Type = baseForm |Optional = optional }} }} ==See Also== *[[SetName]] *[[SetNameEx]] *[[GetName]] *[[LNSetName]] *[[LNGetName]] *[[SetActorFullName]] *[[SetActorFullNameAlt]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Name Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Closes all open menus. |name = con_CloseAllMenus |returnType = void }} ==Notes== * DO NOT use this command for when the player has their PipBoy up. This will cause many of the keys to become unresponsive, and likely force the player to close the game to fix the issue. ** A viable workaround is to simulate the user tapping their Pip-Boy key, by default with ''[[TapKey]] 15'' (FOSE/NVSE).
==See Also== *[[CloseActiveMenu]] (a safer alternative). *[[CloseAllMenus]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Setting string is of the same format as that used by [[con_SetINISetting]]. Does not return a value but merely prints the value to the console. |name = con_GetINISetting |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = settingName |Type = string }} }} =='''See Also'''== *[[Con SetINISetting]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:INI Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Loads the savegame with the specified filename if it exists. Passing 1 in the optional parameter will dump debug information about a save to a text file. |name = con_LoadGame |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = filename |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Integer |Type = int |Optional = optional }} }} [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Exit game without going through menus. |name = con_QuitGame |returnType = void }} ==See Also== *[[QuitGame]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Refresh INI settings from files. |name = con_RefreshINI |returnType = void }} ==See Also== *[[RefreshINI]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:INI Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Saves the current game with the specified filename. If the second parameter is non-zero, generates a text file containing information about the saved data. |name = con_Save |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = saveName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Integer |Type = int |Optional = optional }} }} [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Sets the value of an INI setting. Setting string is of the format "SettingName:CategoryName" where CategoryName is the header under which the setting appears in the '''Fallout.ini''' / '''FalloutPrefs.ini''' / '''FalloutCustom.ini''' files. Does not accept variables as arguments. |name = con_SetINISetting |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = setting |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = string }} }} =='''See Also'''== *[[Con GetINISetting]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:INI Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Writes all the .ini files. |name = con_SaveINI |returnType = void }} ==See Also== *[[SaveINIFiles]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:INI Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Sets a game setting. Does not accept variables as arguments. |name = con_SetGameSetting |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = settingName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = string }} }} =='''See Also'''== *[[GetNumericGameSetting]] *[[SetNumericGameSetting]] which does accept variables as arguments.
*[[SetStringSetting]] [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:GameSetting Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

revision.comment: https
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Disables the key and/or mouse button bound to a control. *With the [https://www.nexusmods.com/newvegas/mods/58277 JIP LN NVSE Plugin] installed, this function will also work when using a controller. |name = DisableControl |alias = dc |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = controlCode |Type = int |Value = 0-27 }} }} {{Control_Codes}} ==See Also== *[[EnableControl]] *[[DisablePlayerControls]] / [[EnablePlayerControls]] / [[GetPlayerControlsDisabled]] *[[GetControl]] / [[GetAltControl]] *[[HoldControl]] / [[ReleaseControl]] *[[IsControlDisabled]] *[[IsControlPressed]] *[[OnControlDown]] / [[OnControlUp]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Identical to [[PrintToConsole]], but message is only printed if [[SetDebugMode|Debug mode]] is enabled for the calling mod. |name = DebugPrint |alias = DBPrintC |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Message |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Formatting |Type = |Optional = optional }} }} ==Notes== *Requires use of [[ToString|ToString($)]] to print [[String Variable|string variables]]. ==See Also== *[[PrintDebug]] *[[String Formatting]] *[[PrintToConsole]] *[[SetDebugMode]] *[[SCOF]] / [[Con_SCOF]] (to write console output to a file) *[[Print]] [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Debugging]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Prevents a player from using a key |name = DisableKey |alias = dk |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = scanCode |Type = int }} }} {{DirectX Scancodes}} ==See Also== *[[EnableKey]] *[[GetKeyPress]] *[[HoldKey]] *[[IsKeyDisabled]] *[[IsKeyPressed]] *[[MenuHoldKey]] *[[MenuReleaseKey]] *[[MenuTapKey]] *[[OnKeyDown]] *[[OnKeyUp]] *[[ReleaseKey]] *[[TapKey]] [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

revision.comment: https
revision.text: {{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Enables the key and/or mouse button bound to a control. *With the [https://www.nexusmods.com/newvegas/mods/58277 JIP LN NVSE Plugin] installed, this function will also work when using a controller. |name = EnableControl |alias = ec |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = controlCode |Type = int |Value = 0-27 }} }} [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Reenables a key previously disabled with DisableKey |name = EnableKey |alias = ek |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = scanCode |Type = int }} }} {{DirectX Scancodes}} ==See Also== *[[DisableKey]] *[[GetKeyPress]] *[[HoldKey]] *[[IsKeyDisabled]] *[[IsKeyPressed]] *[[MenuHoldKey]] *[[MenuReleaseKey]] *[[MenuTapKey]] *[[OnKeyDown]] *[[OnKeyUp]] *[[ReleaseKey]] *[[TapKey]] [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

revision.comment: Added mention of arithmetic operator '%' modulo meant for integers.
revision.text: {{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the result of a floating point operated on by a floating point modulous. (As opposed to the arithmetic operator '%' modulo, for integers.) |name = fmod |returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = x |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = n |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = offset |Type = float |Optional = optional }} }} [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Math Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Calculates the exponential of a value |name = Exp |returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = float |Type = float }} }} [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Math Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

revision.comment: Add examples
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the nearest whole number that is less than a float |name = Floor |alias = flr |returnType = float |returnVal = flooredVal |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = float |Type = float }} |example = float fMyNum
let fMyNum := Floor 0.1 fMyNum == 0.0
let fMyNum := Floor 1.6 fMyNum == 1.0
let fMyNum := Floor 2.0 fMyNum == 2.0
let fMyNum := Floor 3.5 fMyNum == 3.0 }} ==See Also== *[[Ceil]] *[[Round]] *[[Floor (UI Directive)]]
[[Category:Math Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the [[Template:DirectX Scancodes|mouse button]] assigned to a [[Template:Control_Codes|control]]. |name = GetAltControl |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = controlCode |Type = int }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetControl]] [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the attack damage of the specified [[Base Form]]. If no Base Form is specified, will instead get the attack damage of the [[calling reference]]'s Base Form. |name = GetAttackDamage |alias = GetDamage |returnType = int |referenceType = [[reference]] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = [[BaseForm]] |Optional = optional }} }} ==Notes== *This function works on [[Weapon]] forms, Water forms, and [[Creature]] forms.
==See Also== *[[SetAttackDamage]] *[[GetCalculatedWeaponDamage]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the base health of an object. If ''form'' is specified, will check that [[BaseForm]]. Otherwise, if a [[calling reference]] is specified, will derive the BaseForm of that object in order to check its base health. |name = GetBaseHealth |alias = GetHealth |returnType = int |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = form |Type = baseform |Optional = optional }} }} =='''See Also'''== *[[SetObjectHealth]] *[[GetCurrentHealth]] *[[SetCurrentHealth]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the key (as its [[Template:DirectX_Scancodes|directx scancode]]) assigned to a control, specified by it's [[Template:Control_Codes|control code]]. |name = GetControl |returnType = KeyCode:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = controlCode |Type = int }} }} ==Example== 
int iPOVKey
set iPOVKey to GetControl 13
==See Also== *[[GetAltControl]] *[[TapControl]] *[[IsKeyPressed]] *[[TapKey]] *[[HoldKey]] [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the current health (condition) of the calling reference. Used for item condition, such as for armors/weapons.
Also works on destructible objects. Returns 0 if the reference cannot have health. |name = GetCurrentHealth |returnType = float |referenceType = reference |arguments = |example = float ItemHealth set ItemHealth to ItemRefr.GetCurrentHealth }} ==Notes== * If this function is used on an actor, it will always return their base health. To get an actor's current health, use `[[GetActorValue]] Health` instead.
==See Also== *[[GetBaseHealth]] *[[GetDestructionDataHealth]] / [[SetDestructionDataHealth]] *[[GetEquippedCurrentHealth]] *[[GetItemRefCurrentHealth]] *[[SetCurrentHealth]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the [[Base Object]] id of the [[calling reference]]. |name = GetBaseObject |alias = gbo |returnType = form |referenceType = reference }} ==Example== 
SunnyREF.gbo ; # returns GSSunnySmiles
==Notes== *Will return a dynamic (created in-game) form if called on references spawned by a base form that is templated to a levelled list. Use [[GetBaseForm]] to return the 'spawning' NPC/creature base form instead, ie the one that was placed in the cell. ==See Also== *[[GetBaseForm]] *[[GetObjectType]] *[[SetBaseForm]] *[[GetStaticForm]] [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the reference under the crosshair - i.e., the reference that would be activated if the player pressed the Activate button. Will return non-activatable items (like tables and trees) as long as they are within activation distance. Otherwise returns zero.
Ignores objects with no collision. |name = GetCrosshairRef |returnType = ref }} ==Notes== *If the crosshair ref is Cell Water, this function returns an invalid temporary reference. *The [[IActivatePickLength]] gamesetting controls from which distance the player can activate a reference.
==See also== * [[GetCrosshairRefEx]] ignores activation range. * [[ObjectUnderReticle]] * [[CrosshairRefInList]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:Crosshair Functions]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns whether debug mode is set for the mod. If the mod index parameter is omitted, uses the mod index of the calling script. |name = GetDebugMode |alias = GetDBMode |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = modIndex |Type = int |Optional = optional }} }} ==See Also== *[[SetDebugMode]] *[[GetDebugModeState]] *[[PrintDebug]] *[[DebugPrint]] *[[Con_SCOF]] *[[ModLogPrint]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Debugging]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = For the calling [[Actor]] [[reference]], returns the [[BaseForm]] of the [[item]] equipped in the specified slot. Returns 0 (nothing) if no object is equipped there. |name = GetEquippedObject |alias = GetEqObj |returnVal = equippedObj |returnType = BaseForm |referenceType = actorReference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = SlotID |Type = int }} }} ==Example== 
ref rClothes
set rClothes to PlayerREF.GetEquippedObject 2 ; 'Upper Body'
== Equipment Slot IDs == 0: head 1: hair 2: upper body (usual slot for armor and clothing) 3: left hand 4: right hand 5: weapon 6: pip boy 7: backpack 8: necklace 9: headband 10: hat 11: eyeglasses 12: nosering 13: earrings 14: mask 15: choker 16: mouth object 17: body addon 1 18: body addon 2 19: body addon 3 ==See Also== *[[Drop]] *[[GetEquippedCurrentHealth]] *[[GetEquippedItemRef]] *[[GetItemValue]] *[[IsInList]] *[[IsQuestItem]] *[[RemoveItem]] *[[UnequipItem]] [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the first reference in the player's current cell. A type can optionally be supplied by its [[Form Type IDs|ID code]] to return only references matching that type. For example, you can pass 200 for actors and 201 for inventory items. Returns nothing (zero) if there is no ref matching the type in the cell. This function will normally be used only prior to a loop including [[GetNextRef]] to scan actors in a cell. An optional cell depth can be supplied to specify the number of adjacent cells to scan in exteriors: a cell depth of 1 scans the player's current cell plus 8 adjacent cells, a depth of 2 scans the player's cell plus 25 adjacent cells. By default, inactive references to items which were previously picked up by an actor are ignored; passing 1 for the third parameter will force those references to be included. |name = GetFirstRef |returnType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = FormTypeID |Type = int |Optional = optional }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = CellDepth |Type = int |Optional = optional }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = IncludeTakenRefs |Type = bool |Optional = optional }} }} ==Example== Suppose you want to make all the humans in a cell dance? 
ref rActor
set rActor to GetFirstRef 200 ; * 200 == actors
while rActor != 0
    if rActor.GetDead || rActor.GetDisabled || rActor.GetIsCreature
        ; skip
    else
        rActor.PlayIdle MyDancingIdle
    endif
    set rActor to GetNextRef ; * make sure this is within the loop or CTD
loop
This script grabs the first actor in the cell, and makes them dance if they are a living, enabled, human. It then repeats this for every other actor in the cell ==Notes== *The function will pick up dead and disabled references, note the example checks for this. *Infinite loops immediately crash the game, so make sure you avoid this. GetFirstRef should be before the loop starts (outside it), whilst GetNextRef must be within it (it must run before it repeats). *At one point the OBSE version of this function had a bug which was commonly worked around by using 'set rActor to Pencil01' immediately prior to 'set rActor to GetNextRef'. A fix was added, so that this workaround should no longer be necessary. ==See Also== *[[GetFirstRefInCell]] *[[GetNextRef]] *[[While]] *[[GetRefs]] (to return an array) [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = For the calling [[Actor]] [[reference]], returns the current health (condition) of the object equipped in the specified slot. This does NOT return the item's health percentage. |name = GetEquippedCurrentHealth |alias = GetEqCurHealth |returnType = float |referenceType = actorRefr |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = equipmentSlotID |Type = int }} }} ==Equipment Slot IDs== 0: Head 1: Hair 2: Upper body 3: Left hand 4: Right hand 5: Weapon 6: Pip boy 7: Backpack 8: Necklace 9: Headband 10: Hat 11: Eyeglasses 12: Nosering 13: Earrings 14: Mask 15: Choker 16: Mouth object 17: Body addon 1 18: Body addon 2 19: Body addon 3
==See Also== *[[GetCurrentHealth]] *[[SetEquippedCurrentHealth]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Identical to [[GetFirstRef]], except takes an additional parameter specifying which cell to scan, rather than using the current. Note that if the specified cell is not loaded in memory, this function will only return persistent references. |name = GetFirstRefInCell |returnType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Cell |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = FormTypeID |Type = int |Optional = optional }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = CellDepth |Type = int |Optional = optional }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = IncludeTakenRefs |Type = bool |Optional = optional }} }} ==Example== 
ref rActor
set rActor to GetFirstRefInCell GSProspectorSaloonInterior, 200
==See Also== *[[GetFirstRef]] - includes a more detailed example on using scanners. *[[GetNextRef]] *[[GetRefs]] [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Cell Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns 1 when the game is restarted on a per-script basis. For each script that calls GetGameRestarted, the command returns true exactly once per game session. Useful for situations in which a script must set certain object properties or settings each time the game is started. |name = GetGameRestarted |returnType = int }} ==Notes== * To clarify, "restarted" means whenever the game is launched. The time after the game is launched and before it is closes is known as a "session". This functions returns true once per session, per-script. **Therefore, [[GetGameRestarted]] does not re-trigger on reloading a save after death or from the pause menu, nor does it re-trigger each time a new game is started in the same session. See [[GetGameLoaded]] if that functionality is needed. * "Per-script" means that if this function is used more than once in a script, the second instance of it will return false. Therefore it's crucial that any relevant parts of the script are kept within a single instance of this function. **On the flipside, [[GetGameRestarted]] can return true for multiple scripts (but only once per each script, per session).
==See Also== *[[GetGameLoaded]] *[[GetGameHotReloaded]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the [[Eyes]] of an [[NPC]]. |name = GetEyes |returnType = eyes:BaseForm |referenceType = NPCRefr |arguments = |example = ref playerEyes set playerEyes to Player.GetEyes }}
==Notes== *With NVSE, this also works on a base form with base syntax: GetEyes GSSunnySmiles
==See Also== *[[SetEyes]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:Eye Functions]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the [[Hair]] [[BaseForm]] of an [[NPC]]. |name = GetHair |returnVal = hair |returnType = baseForm |referenceType = NPCRefr |arguments = |example = ref playerHair set playerHair to Player.GetHair }}
==Notes== *Under NVSE, this also works with base syntax on a base form: GetHair GSSunnySmiles
==See Also== *[[SetHair]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:Hair Functions]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns 1 after a game is loaded on a per-script basis. For each script that calls GetGameLoaded, the command will return true exactly once each time the game is loaded and false thereafter. |name = GetGameLoaded |returnType = int }}
==Notes== * To clarify, this function will return true when loading a game from the main menu, reloading after death or loading a game from the pause menu. But will NOT return true if using [[CenterOnCell]] from the main menu after previously being in game. * If this function is used more than once in a single script, the second instance of it will return false. Therefore it's crucial that any relevant parts of the script are kept within a single instance of this function.
==See Also== *[[GetGameRestarted]] *[[GetGameHotReloaded]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the length of an [[NPC]] BaseForm's [[Hair]]. |name = GetHairLength |returnType = float |referenceType = NPCRefr |arguments = |example = int playerHairLength set playerHairLength to Player.GetHairLength }}
==Notes== *With [[NVSE]], this also works with base syntax on a base form: GetHairLenth GSSunnySmiles
==See Also== *[[SetHairLength]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:Hair Functions]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the item (as a [[BaseForm]]) assigned to the specified hotkey from 1 to 8. |name = GetHotkeyItem |returnType = BaseForm |returnVal = item |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = hotkey |Type = int }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetHotkeyItemRef]] *[[SetHotkeyItem]] / [[SetHotkey]] / [[SetHotkeyItemRef]] *[[SaveHotkeys]] + [[RestoreHotkeys]] *[[ClearAllHotkeys]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Hotkey Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the [[Template:DirectX_Scancodes|DirectX Key Scancode]] of the nth key which is currently being held down. |name = GetKeyPress |alias = gkp |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = index |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = IgnoreKeyStatus |Type = int(0/2) |Optional = y }} }} ==Examples== Returns the first key being held down. 
set iScancode to GetKeyPress 0
Returns the first key being held down, even if disabled by [[DisableKey]] or [[DisableControl]]. 
set iScancode to GetKeyPress 0 2
==Notes== *The optional ''IgnoreKeyStatus'' arg was added in xNVSE version 6.4.5. {{DirectX Scancodes}} ==See Also== *[[DisableKey]] *[[EnableKey]] *[[HoldKey]] *[[IsControlPressed]] *[[IsKeyDisabled]] *[[IsKeyPressed]] *[[GetDisabledKeys]] *[[MenuHoldKey]] *[[MenuReleaseKey]] *[[MenuTapKey]] *[[OnKeyDown]] *[[OnKeyUp]] *[[ReleaseKey]] *[[TapKey]] [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the base form of the Nth item in a [[Container]]. |name = GetInventoryObject |alias = GetNthObject |returnType = baseform |referenceType = Container |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = index |Type = int }}}} ==Example== 
ref rItem

set rItem to PlayerREF.GetInventoryObject 21
Often you may wish to use this in a loop, to go through every item in an inventory: 
int x
ref rItem

let x := PlayerREF.GetNumItems

while x > 0
    let x -= 1 ; decrement x, the first item is '0'
    let rItem := PlayerREF.GetInventoryObject x
    ; do something
loop
==Notes== *Beware removing items from a container within a loop, because it may change the index of those later. *Multiple instances of the same base form in a container are considered as just one item by the function. ==See Also== *[[ForEach]] *[[GetNumItems]] *[[GetEquippedObject]] *[[GetRefs]] *[[while]], [[let]] *[[ShowInventory]] / [[Con_Inv]] [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the [[Door]] [[reference]] to which the calling reference is linked. Linked doors are also known as teleport doors - they are linked in order to determine the back-and-forth teleport locations. |name = GetLinkedDoor |returnVal = linkedDoor |returnType = reference |referenceType = doorRefr }} ==Notes== *This function is superseded by [[GetTeleportDoor]], which performs more safety checks.
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:Door Functions]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the index of the specified mod. The NVSE version of this function returns a value of 255 if the mod is not loaded. |name = GetModIndex |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = modName |Type = string }} }} ==Example== 
int iIndex
set iIndex to GetModIndex "MyModFileName.esp"
PrintC "My mod version X.Y is loaded at decimal '%g' or hex '%x2'" iIndex, iIndex
==See Also== *[[IsModLoaded]] *[[GetNthModName]] (the reverse, to get the name from an index) *[[BuildRef]] [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the code of the nth mouse button which is currently being held down |name = GetMouseButtonPress |alias = gmbp |returnType = FixMe |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = index |Type = int }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetNumMouseButtonsPressed]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the base value of the specified [[BaseForm]] or [[calling reference]]. If a calling reference is specified, its BaseForm is derived. |name = GetItemValue |alias = GetValue |returnType = int |referenceType = itemRefr |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = item |Type = baseForm |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== 
int iValue

set iValue to GetValue Weap10mmPistol
==Notes== *This function will not return a value for [[Ingestible]]s, as their value is specified internally in a separate location. *Does not appear as a Condition Function for [[Perk Entry Point|Perk Entry Points]]. ==See also== *[[GetValueAlt]] - fixes the aforementioned issue with Ingestibles. [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Object Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Usable only following [[GetFirstRef]] or [[GetFirstRefInCell]], to return the next reference matching the same parameters of the previous function. This command should only be used within a loop that executes within a single frame.
Returns 0 (nothing) if there are no new references left to return that frame. |name = GetNextRef |returnType = reference }} ==Example== See [[GetFirstRef]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the value of a numeric [[GameSetting]]. |name = GetNumericGameSetting |returnType = IntegerOrFloat |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = settingName |Type = string }} }} =='''Notes'''== *The compiler will not catch typing errors for the ''settingName'' string, so take care to type accurately. =='''See Also'''== *[[Con SetGameSetting]] *[[GetGameSetting]]
*[[SetNumericGameSetting]] *[[SetStringSetting]] [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:GameSetting Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the value of a numeric setting found in '''Fallout.ini''' / '''FalloutPrefs.ini''' / '''FalloutCustom.ini'''. Setting name is of the format "SettingName:CategoryName" where CategoryName is the header under which the setting appears in the .ini file. |name = GetNumericINISetting |returnType = IntegerOrFloat |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = settingName |Type = string }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetStringIniSetting]] / [[SetStringIniSetting]] *[[Con_GetINISetting]] / [[Con_SetINISetting]]
----
For [[Settings|GameSettings]]: *[[GetNumericGameSetting]] / [[SetNumericGameSetting]] *[[GetGameSetting]] / [[Con_SetGameSetting]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:INI Functions]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the number of unique items in the reference's inventory. |name = GetNumItems |alias = GetNumObjects |returnType = int |returnVal = numItems |referenceType = ContainerRefr |arguments = |example = int total set total to player.GetNumItems }} ==See Also== *[[GetNthObject]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

revision.comment: Add IgnoreKeyStatus arg
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns how many keyboard keys are currently being held down. |name = GetNumKeysPressed |alias = gnkp |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = IgnoreKeyStatus |Type = int(0/2) |Optional = y }} }} ==Examples== Returns number of keys currently being held down, even if disabled by [[DisableKey]] or [[DisableControl]]. 
set iNumKeys to GetNumKeysPressed 2
==Notes== *The optional ''IgnoreKeyStatus'' arg was added in xNVSE version 6.4.5. [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the number of loaded mods |name = GetNumLoadedMods |returnType = int }} ==See Also== *[[GetNthModName]] *[[While]] *[[IsModLoaded]] *[[GetModIndex]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns how many mouse buttons are currently being held down |name = GetNumMouseButtonsPressed |alias = gnmbp |returnType = int }} ==See Also== *[[GetMouseButtonPress]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the [[Object Effect]] of the [[Item]] [[BaseForm]]. If an item reference is passed, that reference's BaseForm is used. |name = GetObjectEffect |alias = GetEnchantment |returnType = baseForm |returnVal = effect |referenceType = itemRefr |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = item |Type = baseform |Optional = optional }} }} ==See Also== *[[SetObjectEffect]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the number of references of a given type in the currently loaded cell(s). {{Template:GetRefsEtc_Description}} |name = GetNumRefs |returnType = int |returnVal = numRefs |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = formType |Type = int |Optional = optional }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = cellDepth |Type = int |Optional = optional }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = includeTakenRefs |Type = 0/1 |Optional = optional }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetNumRefsInCell]] *[[GetRefs]] / [[GetRefsInCell]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Cell Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the [[Key]] BaseForm associated with the calling lockable object reference. |name = GetOpenKey |alias = GetKey |returnType = baseForm |returnVal = openKey |referenceType = lockableRefr }} ==See Also== *[[SetOpenKey]] *[[SetOpenState]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Lock Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the Owner ([[BaseForm]]) of the [[calling reference]]. The owner can be a [[Faction]] or an [[NPC]]. |name = GetOwner |returnVal = owner |returnType = baseForm |referenceType = reference |example = ref owner set owner to someItem.GetOwner }} ==See Also== *[[GetOwnerOfCell]] / [[SetCellOwnership]] *[[SetOwnership]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Ownership Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the number of references of a given type in the specified cell. {{Template:GetRefsEtc_Description}} |name = GetNumRefsInCell |returnType = int |returnVal = numRefs |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = cell |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = formtype |Type = int |Optional = optional }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = cellDepth |Type = int |Optional = optional }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = includeTakenRefs |Type = 0/1 |Optional = optional }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetNumRefs]] *[[GetRefs]] / [[GetRefsInCell]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Cell Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the required [[Faction]] rank for ownership of the [[calling reference]]. |name = GetOwningFactionRequiredRank |alias = GetOwningFactionRank |returnType = int |referenceType = reference |example = int requirement set requirement to someItem.GetOwningFactionRequiredRank }} [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Faction Functions]] [[Category:Ownership Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the parent cell of the [[calling reference]] (also known as the cell the reference is in). |name = GetParentCell |alias = gpc |returnType = BaseForm |returnVal = cell |referenceType = reference }} ==See Also== *[[GetParentWorldspace]] *[[GetInCell]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Cell Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the owner of the [[cell]] that the [[calling reference]] is in. |name = GetParentCellOwner |alias = GetCellOwner |returnVal = cellOwner |returnType = BaseForm |referenceType = reference |example = ref parent set parent to someRef.GetParentCellOwner }} ==See Also== *[[GetOwnerOfCell]] *[[GetParentCell]] *[[GetOwner]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Cell Functions]] [[Category:Ownership Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the rank required for ownership of the calling reference's [[GetParentCell|parent cell]]. |name = GetParentCellOwningFactionRequiredRank |alias = CetCellFactionRank |returnType = int |returnVal = requiredRank |referenceType = reference |example = int requirement set requirement to someItem.GetParentCellOwningFactionRequiredRank ==Notes== *The abbreviation of this function is not incorrect. The FOSE developers have the typo written in the code, so "CetCellFactionRank" works, but "GetCellFactionRank" doesn't. }} [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Faction Functions]] [[Category:Cell Functions]] [[Category:Ownership Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the parent [[WorldSpace]] of the [[calling reference]] (also known as the WorldSpace which the reference is in). |name = GetParentWorldspace |alias = gpw |returnType = baseForm |returnVal = worldSpace |referenceType = reference |example = set rWorldspace to Player.GetParentWorldspace }} ==See Also== *[[GetParentCell]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:World_Space_Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the count of items in a stacked reference, such as an [[inventory reference]] or a reference placed/dropped in the world. |name = GetRefCount |returnType = int |referenceType = reference }} ==See Also== *[[SetRefCount]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the repair list for the [[Item]] BaseForm. If ''item'' is unspecified and a calling reference is passed, that reference's BaseForm is used. |name = GetRepairList |alias = grl |returnType = baseForm |returnVal = repairList |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = item |Type = baseform |Optional = optional }} }} [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the script of a scriptable [[BaseForm]]. If ''ScriptableObject'' is omitted and a [[calling reference]] is provided, that reference's BaseForm is used. |name = GetScript |returnType = BaseForm |returnVal = script |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ScriptableObject |Type = baseform |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== 
ref MyScript

set MyScript to SunnyREF.GetScript

; alternatively or necessarily for non-references:

set MyScript to GetScript SunnyREF
==See Also== *[[SetScript]] *[[CompareScripts]] *[[GetCurrentScript]] [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Script Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the index (load order) of the mod to which the specified form belongs. |name = GetSourceModIndex |returnType = int |referenceType = worldRefr |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = item |Type = baseform |Optional = optional }} }} ==Example== 
int iModIndex
set iModIndex to GetSourceModIndex SomeBaseForm
set iModIndex to SomeRef.GetSourceModIndex
==See Also== *[[GetSelfModIndex]] *[[GetModIndex]] *[[GetNthModName]] *[[GetCallingScript]] *[[ValidateModIndex]] [[Category:Debugging]] [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the [[Form Type IDs|type code]] of the specified [[Base Form]], or [[calling]] [[Reference]]. If a reference is passed, its BaseForm is derived. |name = GetObjectType |alias = GetType |returnType = TypeCode:int |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = BaseForm |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== Calling on a known reference: 
int iType

let iType := SunnyRef.GetType
print $iType 
;* Should print "42", which is the NPC form type.
Calling on a known Base Form: 
int iType

let iType := GetType GSSunnySmiles
print $iType 
;* Should print "42", which is the NPC form type.
===Working With [[Ref Variable]]s:=== *Reminder: While Ref Variables sound awfully like "Reference Variable", they can hold ''either'' Base Forms or References. '''If the ref variable is guaranteed to be a Base Form:''' 
ref rRef
int iType

let iType := GetType rRef
'''If the ref variable is guaranteed to be a Reference:''' 
ref rRef
int iType

let iType := rRef.GetType
If you're not sure if the ref variable will be valid, '''it is always important to verify''' using [[IsFormValid]] / [[IsReference]]. Not doing so can cause problems; this applies to other functions as well. In the interest of debugging such issues, it is recommended to use [[Let|"Let :="]] over '''"Set to"''', as Let just as fast (if not faster in some cases) and will report errors if attempting to call functions with the wrong syntax / with invalid forms. The following will cover a few examples of how to use these functions in conjunction with [[GetType]], in order to avoid errors and crashes. '''To verify if the ref variable holds either a Base Form or a Reference:''' 
ref rRef

if IsFormValid rRef
   ; Unless we can assume rRef will be a Reference or a Base Form,
   ;... it is unsafe to proceed without further filtering.
endif
'''To verify if the ref variable holds a Base Form:''' 
ref rRef
int iType

if IsFormValid rRef
   if IsReference rRef != 1
      ; rRef is a Base Form.
      let iType := GetType rRef
      if iType == 41 ; ammo
         ; do something appropriate for ammo
      endif
   endif
endif
'''To verify if the ref variable holds a Reference:''' 
ref rRef

if IsReference rRef
  if (rRef.GetType) == 24 ; Armor
     ; do something appropriate for Armor
  endif
endif
'''To verify if the ref variable holds either a Base Form or a Reference, ''and to be able to act in either case'':''' 
ref rRef
int iType

if IsFormValid rRef
   if IsReference rRef != 1
      ; rRef is a Base Form.
      let iType := GetType rRef
      if iType == 41 ; ammo
         ; do something appropriate for ammo
      endif
   else
      ; rRef is a Reference.
      let iType := rRef.GetType 
      if iType == 41 ; ammo
         ; do something appropriate for ammo
      endif
   endif
endif
==See Also== *[[Form Type IDs]] *[[GetBaseObject]] *[[GetEquipType]] *[[GetWeaponType]] *[[IsReference]] *[[TypeOf]] [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Debugging]] [[Category:ID Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the [[Cell]] to which the calling [[Door]] reference teleports. |name = GetTeleportCell |returnType = baseForm |returnVal = cell |referenceType = reference }} ==See Also== *[[GetTeleportDoor]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Cell Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

revision.comment: italics

revision.text:
{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the base weight of the specified [[base form]]. If a reference is passed, its baseform is derived. |name = GetWeight |returnVal = weight |returnType = float |referenceType = refr |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = item |Type = baseform |Optional = y }} }} ==See Also== *[[SetWeight]] *[[GetInventoryWeight]] *[[RefreshInventoryWeight]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Jump to ("go to") a labeled position in the script previously defined by a call to Label. Execution will return to the label, simulating a loop. Be careful to provide an exit condition to avoid creating infinite loops. |name = Goto |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = labelID |Type = int |Value = 0-255 }} |example = int GetNextActor set GetNextActor to 1 Label GetNextActor ; Do something if SomeCondition GoTo GetNextActor endif }}
==Notes== * There are 256 available slots for Labels, enabling IDs from 0 to 255 inclusively. Values outside of this range will not work. * If using NVSE since version 4, Label/Goto should be replaced by [[While]] or [[Foreach]] in all but exceptional cases, as these are more powerful, readable and reliable. Use of Label/Goto for creating loops should be considered outdated and bad form.
==See Also== * [[Label]] * [[While]] * [[Foreach]]
==External Links== * [http://www.cipscis.com/fallout/tutorials/loops.aspx Loops] * [http://fose.silverlock.org/fose_command_doc.html#Goto FOSE Documentation]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Fakes a key press (specified by [[IsKeyPressed|int code]]) indefinitely, or until [[ReleaseKey]] is called. |name = HoldKey |alias = hk |returnType = none |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = scanCode |Type = int }} }} ==Example== 
HoldKey 31 ; 'S' key
; ...
; later, after a delay 
ReleaseKey 31
{{DirectX Scancodes}} ==See Also== *[[DisableKey]] *[[EnableKey]] *[[GetKeyPress]] *[[IsKeyDisabled]] *[[IsKeyPressed]] *[[MenuHoldKey]] *[[MenuReleaseKey]] *[[MenuTapKey]] *[[OnKeyDown]] *[[OnKeyUp]] *[[ReleaseKey]] *[[TapKey]] [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns 1 if key is a game control or 2 if a custom control set by SetIsControl. |name = IsControl |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = scanCode |Type = int }} }} [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns True (1) if the object passed in the [[ref variable]] is valid, meaning it holds either a [[Reference]] or a [[Base Form]]. Returns False (0) if the object stored in the variable is not currently loaded in memory (for instance, a non-persistent reference) or if the variable doesn't hold any object. This is useful in cases where an invalid ref variable will cause the script or game to crash. |name = IsFormValid |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = refVar |Type = ref }} }} ==Example== *If one expects a ref variable to hold either a Base Form or a Reference, and that either should be used to call functions, [[IsReference]] can be used to further narrow down the results after [[IsFormValid]] has confirmed that the ref variable is valid. Implementation: 
ref SomeRef
int iType

if IsFormValid SomeRef
   if IsReference SomeRef != 1
      ; SomeRef is a valid Base Form.
      let iType := GetType SomeRef
   else
      ; SomeRef is a valid Reference.
      let iType := SomeRef.GetType
   endif
endif
