==See Also== *[[IsBaseForm]] *[[IsFormLoading]] *[[IsPersistent]] *[[IsReference]] *[[TypeOf]] *[[Causes of CTDs]] [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Debugging]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns True (1) if a [[Template:DirectX_Scancodes|DirectX Key Scancode]] has been disabled by [[DisableKey]] or [[DisableControl]]. |name = IsKeyDisabled |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = scanCode |Type = int }} }} {{DirectX Scancodes}} ==See Also== *[[DisableKey]] *[[EnableKey]] *[[DisableControl]] *[[EnableControl]] *[[GetDisabledKeys]] *[[GetKeyPress]] *[[IsKeyPressed]] *[[HoldKey]] *[[ReleaseKey]] *[[MenuHoldKey]] *[[MenuReleaseKey]] *[[TapKey]] *[[MenuTapKey]] *[[OnKeyDown]] *[[OnKeyUp]] [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

revision.comment: Add note that function can be used on vanilla DLCs
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns whether the specified mod is loaded. |name = IsModLoaded |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = modName |Type = string }} }} ==Example== 
if IsModLoaded "SomeKnownConflictingMod.esp"
    ; do some workaround
endif
==Notes== * This function can also be used to check if vanilla DLCs are installed. ==See Also== *[[GetModIndex]] *[[GetNumLoadedMods]] [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns whether the specified [[BaseForm]] has the mod index of 0xFF (not owned by any mod). Baseforms with a mod index of 0xFF include those created with [[TempCloneForm]], hence this function's name.
This also returns true for clones made with the ''indexToCallingScript'' arg set to 1 in [[TempCloneForm]].
If ''form'' argument is omitted and a [[calling reference]] is provided, that reference's BaseForm is used. |name = IsClonedForm |alias = IsCloned |returnVal = isCloned |returnType = 0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = form |Type = baseForm |Optional = optional }} }} ==See Also== *[[IsReferenceCloned]] *[[TempCloneForm]] *[[CreateTempRef]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns True (1) if a [[Template:DirectX_Scancodes|DirectX Key Scancode]] is currently being pressed. |name = IsKeyPressed |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = scanCode |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = IgnoreKeyStatus |Type = int(0/2) |Optional = y }} }} ==Examples== If IsKeyPressed 28 == 1 ;The enter key has been pressed EndIf
If IsKeyPressed 28 2 == 1 ;The enter key has been pressed, even if disabled using DisableKey or DisabledControl EndIf
{{DirectX Scancodes}} ==See Also== *[[IsButtonPressed]] - Similar function but for controller buttons. *[[DisableKey]] *[[EnableKey]] *[[DisableControl]] *[[EnableControl]] *[[IsKeyDisabled]] *[[GetDisabledKeys]] *[[GetKeyPress]] *[[HoldKey]] *[[ReleaseKey]] *[[MenuHoldKey]] *[[MenuReleaseKey]] *[[TapKey]] *[[MenuTapKey]] [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

revision.comment: https
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns 1 if the control has been disabled by a script using [[DisableControl]]. *With the [https://www.nexusmods.com/newvegas/mods/58277 JIP LN NVSE Plugin] installed, this function will also work when using a controller. (This feature appears to be broken at the moment. With a connected controller, the function returns an inversed value.) |name = IsControlDisabled |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = controlCode |Type = int |Value = 0-27 }} }} ==See Also== *[[IsControlPressed]] *[[DisableControl]] *[[EnableControl]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns true (1) if the [specified [[BaseForm]] has a script attached to it, false (0) otherwise. If ''ScriptableForm'' is omitted and a [[calling reference]] is provided, that reference's BaseForm is used. |name = IsScripted |returnVal = isScripted |returnType = bool |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ScriptableForm |Type = baseForm |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== 
if SunnyREF.IsScripted
   ; do something
endif

; or (required for non-references):

if (IsScripted SunnyREF)
==See Also== *[[GetScript]] *[[SetScript]] *[[HasScriptBlock]] [[Category:Script Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns True (1) if the passed [[ref variable]] stores a valid [[Reference]]. Particularly useful for validation in cases where a non reference might cause a script to crash. |name = IsReference |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = reference |Type = ref }} }} ==Example== 
if IsReference RefVar
    ; it is safe to proceed
endif
==Notes== *IsReference ALWAYS returns true in a script block that has the CO on ([[Script Compiler Override]]). ==See Also== *[[IsFormValid]] *[[IsBaseForm]] *[[TypeOf]] *[[GetType]] *[[IsPersistent]] [[Category:Debugging]] [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns 1 if the key or mouse button assigned to a control is pressed. With [[JIP LN NVSE]] installed, also returns 1 if the controller button assigned to a control is pressed (the default controller button layout is assumed). |name = IsControlPressed |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = controlCode |Type = int }} }} ==Notes:== Control Codes: 
0:     Forward
1:     Backward
2:     Left
3:     Right
4:     Attack
5:     Activate
6:     Block/Aim
7:     Ready Item
8:     Crouch
9:     Run
10:    Always Run
11:    Auto-Move
12:    Jump
13:    TogglePOV
14:    MenuMode
15:    Rest
16:    VATS
17-24: Hotkeys 1-8
25:    Quicksave
26:    Quickload
27:    Grab
==See Also== *[[GetKeyPress]] *[[HoldKey]] *[[IsKeyPressed]] *[[MenuHoldKey]] *[[MenuReleaseKey]] *[[MenuTapKey]] *[[OnControlDown]] *[[OnControlUp]] *[[OnKeyDown]] *[[OnKeyUp]] *[[TapKey]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Keyboard Mappings]] [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns whether the biped [[BaseForm]] (such as [[Armor]]) is considered power armor. If ''form'' is omitted and a [[calling reference]] is provided, that reference's BaseForm is used. |name = IsPowerArmor |alias = IsPA |returnVal = isPowerArmor |returnType = 0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = form |Type = baseform |Optional = y }} }} ==See Also== *[[SetIsPowerArmor]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Armor Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Defines a labeled position in a script which subsequent calls to Goto can use to create loops. The label must be defined before Goto is called. |name = Label |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = labelID |Type = int }} |example = int GetNextActor ; * or some appropriate name set GetNextActor to 1 Label GetNextActor }} ==Notes== *There are 256 available slots for Labels, enabling IDs from 0 to 255 inclusively. Values outside of this range will not work. *If using NVSE since version 4, label/goto should be replaced by [[While]] or [[Foreach]] in all but exceptional cases, as this is more powerful, readable and reliable. ==See Also== *[[Goto]] *[[While]] *[[Foreach]] [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Adds the form to the list at the given index (or at the end if not provided, or if -2 is the given index). If the function fails to do anything, returns -1, otherwise returns the index for where the form was added.
Added in [[xNVSE]] v6.2.5, the ''checkForDupes'' arg can set to 1 (true) to prevent adding duplicate forms to the formlist.
This function is subject to [[:Category:List Functions|warnings]] concerning being used on non-empty lists. |name = ListAddForm |returnVal = indexAddedAt |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = formlist |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = formToAdd |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = index |Type = int |Optional = y |Value = -2 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = checkForDupes |Type = bool |Optional = y |Value = 0 }} }} ==Notes== *'''[[FOSE]] version of the function suffers from a memory corruption bug and can cause various issues when used.''' The bug was addressed in early versions of [[xNVSE]] for New Vegas, and in [[ButcherPete FOSE]] plugin for Fallout 3.
*Forms added to a list with this function will not persist across savegames. If you need to add items to a list that will be preserved in a saved game, use [[AddFormToFormList]].
==See Also== *[[ListAddReference]] *[[ListAddList]] *[[ListAddArray]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:List_Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Shifts a 32-bit integer left the specified number of bits |name = LeftShift |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Value |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = ShiftAmount |Type = int }} }} [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Adds the calling reference at the given index (or at the end if not provided, or if -2 is the given index). If the function fails to do anything, returns -1, otherwise returns the index for where the reference was added.
Added in [[xNVSE]] v6.2.5, the ''checkForDupes'' arg can set to 1 (true) to prevent adding duplicate forms to the formlist.
This function is subject to [[:Category:List Functions|warnings]] concerning being used on non-empty lists. |name = ListAddReference |alias = ListAddRef |returnVal = indexAddedAt |returnType = int |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = formlist |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = index |Type = int |Optional = optional |Value = -2 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = checkForDupes |Type = bool |Optional = y |Value = 0 }} }} ==Notes== *'''[[FOSE]] version of the function suffers from a memory corruption bug and can cause various issues when used.''' The bug was addressed in early versions of [[xNVSE]] for New Vegas, and in [[ButcherPete FOSE]] plugin for Fallout 3. *Forms added to a list with this function will not persist across savegames. If you need to add items to a list that will be preserved in a saved game, use [[AddFormToFormList]].
==See Also== *[[ListAddForm]] *[[ListAddList]] *[[ListAddArray]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:List Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the count of forms in the specified [[FormList]]. |name = ListGetCount |returnType = Count:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = formlist |Type = baseForm }} }} ==Example== 
int iCount

set iCount to ListGetCount AmmoList10mm ; * 3
== Notes == *Will not properly return the count of a list of [[reference]]s if they are not flagged persistent. ==See Also== *[[ListGetNthForm]] *[[ListRemoveNthForm]] *[[Ar_Size]] (equivalent for [[Array Variable|Arrays]]) *[[GetNumLevItems]] (equivalent for [[:Category:Leveled_Lists|Leveled Lists]]) [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:List_Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the index for the specified form ([[reference]] ''or'' [[BaseForm]]) if it is found exactly in the specified form list, otherwise returns constant -1 (not found). |name = ListGetFormIndex |returnVal = formIndex |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = formlist |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = form |Type = ref }} }} ==Example== 
int iIndex
set iIndex to ListGetFormIndex GoodspringersList, SunnyREF
To determine if SunnyRef is in GoodspringersList, one could check: if ListGetFormIndex GoodspringersList SunnyREF > -1 ==Notes== *The first form in the list is at index 0, and the last is equal to ([[ListGetCount|ListGetCount - 1]]). ==See Also== *[[IsInList]] (Checks for base objects only) *[[IsRefInList]] (Indexes both exact and base/leveled matches) *[[ListGetCount]] [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:List_Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the nth form in the form list |name = ListGetNthForm |returnVal = nthForm |returnType = reference/BaseForm |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = formlist |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = index |Type = int }} |example = let rForm := ListGetNthForm ExampleFormList 0 ;Will return the first element in the list. }}
==Looping With ListGetCount== To cycle through each form in a FormList using this function and [[ListGetCount]], here's an example:
let iSize := ListGetCount ExampleFormList
while ( (iSize -= 1) >= 0) let rForm := ListGetNthForm ExampleFormList iSize ;do anything with this form. loop
Note that a backwards [[While]] loop was chosen here, since unlike with a forwards loop, forms can be safely removed from the FormList without causing the loop to fail. Besides that, forwards loops are also perfectly fine to use.
==See Also== *[[ListGetCount]] *[[ListClear]] *To find an element: [[ListGetFormIndex]], [[IsInList]], [[IsRefInList]], [[IsWeaponInList]] *To remove an element: [[ListRemoveForm]], [[ListRemoveNthForm]] *To replace an element: [[ListReplaceForm]], [[ListReplaceNthForm]] *To add an element: [[ListAddForm]], [[ListAddReference]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:List_Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Removes the nth form from the list |name = ListRemoveNthForm |alias = ListRemoveNth |returnType = ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = formlist |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = index |Type = int |Optional = optional }} }} [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:List Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Replaces the nth form of the list with the specified form. |name = ListReplaceNthForm |alias = ListReplaceNth |returnType = ref |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = formlist |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = replaceWith |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = formIndex |Type = int |Optional = optional }} }} [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:List Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Replaces the specified form with another. |name = ListReplaceForm |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = formlist |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = replaceWith |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = formToReplace |Type = ref }} }} [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:List Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Calculates the base 10 log of a value |name = Log10 |returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = float |Type = float }} }} [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Math Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Performs a logical AND between two 32-bit integers. |name = LogicalAnd |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = int |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = int |Type = int }} }} ==See Also== *[[LogicalNot]] *[[LogicalOr]] *[[LogicalXor]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Performs a logical NOT on a 32-bit integer. |name = LogicalNot |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = int |Type = int }} }} ==See Also== *[[LogicalAnd]] *[[LogicalOr]] *[[LogicalXor]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Releases a key held down by MenuHoldKey |name = MenuReleaseKey |alias = mrk |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = scanCode |Type = int }} }} {{DirectX Scancodes}} ==See Also== *[[DisableKey]] *[[EnableKey]] *[[GetKeyPress]] *[[HoldKey]] *[[IsKeyDisabled]] *[[IsKeyPressed]] *[[MenuHoldKey]] *[[MenuTapKey]] *[[OnKeyDown]] *[[OnKeyUp]] *[[ReleaseKey]] *[[TapKey]] [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Fakes a key press for one frame in menu mode |name = MenuTapKey |alias = mtk |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = scanCode |Type = int }} }} {{DirectX Scancodes}} ==See Also== *[[DisableKey]] *[[EnableKey]] *[[GetKeyPress]] *[[HoldKey]] *[[IsKeyDisabled]] *[[IsKeyPressed]] *[[MenuHoldKey]] *[[MenuReleaseKey]] *[[OnKeyDown]] *[[OnKeyUp]] *[[ReleaseKey]] *[[TapKey]] [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Performs a logical OR between two 32-bit integers. |name = LogicalOr |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = int |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = int |Type = int }} }} ==See Also== *[[LogicalAnd]] *[[LogicalNot]] *[[LogicalXor]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = When given two numbers will return the value of the first to the power of the second. |name = Pow |returnType = float |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = num1 |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = num2 |Type = float }} |referenceType = }} ==Examples== float x float y float answer
set x to 2.0 set y to 5.0
set answer to pow x y ;answer will contain 2^5 which is 32.0
==See Also== *[[Abs]] *[[Cos]] *[[Log]] *[[Sin]] *[[Tan]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Math Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Prints a string to console. Optionally with up to 20 [[String Formatting|format specifiers]]. |name = PrintToConsole |alias = PrintC |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Message |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Formatting ... |Type = |Optional = optional }} }} ==Example== 
PrintC "This message will appear in the game console"
With formatting: 
int iVal
set iVal to 42
PrintC "The value of iVal is: %g" iVal
==Notes== *Requires use of [[ToString|ToString($)]] to print [[String Variable|string variables]]. ==See Also== *[[DebugPrint]] *[[MessageEx]] *[[Print]] (NVSE 4 version) *[[String Formatting]] *[[ToString]] [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Debugging]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Performs a logical XOR between two 32-bit integers. |name = LogicalXor |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = int |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = int |Type = int }} }} ==See Also== *[[LogicalAnd]] *[[LogicalNot]] *[[LogicalOr]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Prints name of highlighted UI component for debug purposes |name = PrintActiveTile |returnType = void }}
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Debugging]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Shifts a 32-bit integer right the specified number of bits |name = RightShift |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = int |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = int |Type = int }} }} [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Bit Functions]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Changes the value of the base form passed in. |name = SetBaseItemValue |alias = SetValue |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = form |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = int }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetItemValue]] *[[SetValueAlt]] *[[GetValueAlt]] *[[SetItemValue]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category: Object Functions]]

revision.comment: Fix documentation for how this works on references
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Removes the [[Script]] from the specified [[BaseForm]], and returns it. If ''ScriptableForm'' is omitted and a [[calling reference]] is provided, that reference's BaseForm is modified, and the reference's script [[ExtraData]] will be cleared, meaning the old script will stop running immediately for the reference. |name = RemoveScript |returnType = Script:baseForm |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ScriptableObject |Type = baseForm |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== 
ref OldScript

set OldScript to SunnyREF.RemoveScript

; or (required for non-references):

set OldScript to RemoveScript GSSunnySmiles
==See Also== *[[IsScripted]] *[[GetScript]] *[[SetScript]] [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Script Functions]] [[Category:ExtraData Functions]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Assigns a new keycode to the specified keyboard control. Changes are saved to the .ini when the user exits the game. |name = SetControl |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = controlCode |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = scanCode |Type = int }} }} [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Input Functions]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Assigns a new mouse button code to the specified mouse control. Changes are saved to the ini when the user exits the game. |name = SetAltControl |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = controlCode |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = scanCode |Type = int }} }} [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Input Functions]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Releases a key (specified by [[IsKeyPressed|int code]]) which was held down by a prior call to [[HoldKey]]. |name = ReleaseKey |alias = rk |returnType = none |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = scanCode |Type = int }} }} {{DirectX Scancodes}} ==See Also== *[[DisableKey]] *[[EnableKey]] *[[GetKeyPress]] *[[HoldKey]] *[[IsControlPressed]] *[[IsKeyDisabled]] *[[IsKeyPressed]] *[[MenuHoldKey]] *[[MenuReleaseKey]] *[[MenuTapKey]] *[[OnKeyDown]] *[[OnKeyUp]] *[[TapKey]] [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Input Functions]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Removes the specified form from the form list. Returns the index of the removed form. |name = ListRemoveForm |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = formlist |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = form |Type = ref }} }} ==See Also== *[[ListRemoveNthForm]] *[[ListClear]] *[[Ar_Erase]] [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:List Functions]] [[Category:Functions_(FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Toggles debug mode for all scripts belonging to a mod. When called in the console, the mod index of the mod for which debug messages are to be toggled can be specified by the second parameter (in decimal, not hexadecimal, format). |name = SetDebugMode |alias = DBMode |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = bEnableDebugMessages |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = modIndex |Type = int |Optional = optional }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetDebugMode]] *[[PrintDebug]] / [[DebugPrint]] *[[GetDebugModeState]] *[[GetSelfModIndex]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Debugging]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Sets the current health of the equipped object at the given slot for the calling [[Actor]] [[reference]]. |name = SetEquippedCurrentHealth |alias = SetEqCurHealth |returnType = void |referenceType = actorRefr |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = newHealth |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = equipmentSlotID |Type = int }} }} ==Equipment Slot IDs== 0: Head 1: Hair 2: Upper body 3: Left hand 4: Right hand 5: Weapon 6: Pip boy 7: Backpack 8: Necklace 9: Headband 10: Hat 11: Eyeglasses 12: Nosering 13: Earrings 14: Mask 15: Choker 16: Mouth object 17: Body addon 1 18: Body addon 2 19: Body addon 3
==See Also== *[[GetEquippedCurrentHealth]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Sets the attack damage of the specified [[Base Form]]. If no ''BaseForm'' is specified, will instead set the attack damage of the [[calling reference]]'s Base Form. |name = SetAttackDamage |alias = SetDamage |returnType = void |referenceType = [[reference]] |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = damage |Type = [[int]] }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = [[BaseForm]] |Optional = optional }} |example = SetAttackDamage 10 Weap10mmPistol This sets the damage of every single 10mm Pistol references that belongs to this base form to 10.
player.SetAttackDamage 10 Weap10mmPistol This performs the same task as the above; "player." is ignored.
player.SetAttackDamage 10 This does nothing, given that internally, the "player" class does not have an attack damage class.
SetAttackDamage 20 CrSuperMutant2Nightkin This sets the damage of the CrSuperMutant2Nightkin base form to 20, meaning every single reference belonging to this base form will now deal 20 damage in an unarmed attack (see [[Stats Tab - Creatures]]). }} ==Notes== *This function works on [[Weapon]] forms, Water forms, and [[Creature]] forms.
==See Also== *[[GetAttackDamage]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Sets the name of the [[Base Form]] to the specified [[string]]. If ''form'' is omitted and a [[calling reference]] is passed, will use that reference's BaseForm (as returned by [[GetBaseForm]]). |name = SetName |returnType = void |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = name |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = form |Type = baseform |Optional = optional }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetName]] *[[SetNameEx]] *[[LNSetName]] *[[LNGetName]] *[[SetActorFullName]] *[[SetFullNameAlt]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Name Functions]] [[Category:String Functions]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Sets whether the [[armor]]'s [[Base Form]] is considered Power Armor or not. If ''form'' is omitted and a [[calling reference]] is provided, that reference's BaseForm is used. |name = SetIsPowerArmor |alias = SetIsPA |returnType = void |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = isPA |Type = 0/1 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = form |Type = baseForm |Optional = optional }} }} ==See Also== *[[IsPowerArmor]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Armor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Fakes a key press indefinitely in [[Menu Mode]]. |name = MenuHoldKey |alias = mhk |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = scanCode |Type = int }} }} {{DirectX Scancodes}} ==See Also== *[[DisableKey]] *[[EnableKey]] *[[GetKeyPress]] *[[HoldKey]] *[[IsKeyDisabled]] *[[IsKeyPressed]] *[[MenuReleaseKey]] *[[MenuTapKey]] *[[OnKeyDown]] *[[OnKeyUp]] *[[ReleaseKey]] *[[TapKey]] [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Flags a key or mouse button as a control, allowing modders to define custom controls - other scripts can then use [[IsControl]] to detect conflicts in which two mods try to use the same key for different actions. |name = SetIsControl |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = scanCode |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = isControl |Type = int }} }} [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Input Functions]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Sets a numeric [[Game Setting]]. Accepts variables as arguments. Returns 1 for a successful change, and 0 on a failed one. For example, it may fail if the GameSetting could not be found. |name = SetNumericGameSetting |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = settingName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = float |Type = float }} }} =='''Notes'''== *The compiler will not catch typing errors for the ''settingName'' string, so take care to type accurately. *A game setting changed by this function is not save baked but rather is session based. =='''See Also'''== *[[Con_SetGameSetting]]
*[[GetNumericGameSetting]] *[[GetStringSetting]] *[[SetStringSetting]] [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:GameSetting Functions]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Sets the health of the specified [[Base Form]]. If a reference is passed, will modify the Base Form of that reference. |name = SetObjectHealth |alias = SetHealth |returnType = void |referenceType = refr |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = newHealth |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = form |Type = baseform |Optional = optional }} }} =='''See Also'''== *[[GetBaseHealth]] *[[GetCurrentHealth]] *[[SetCurrentHealth]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Object Functions]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Sets the [[Key]] used to unlock the lockable [[calling reference]]. |name = SetOpenKey |alias = SetKey |returnType = void |referenceType = refr |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = keyItem |Type = baseform }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetOpenKey]] *[[SetOpenState]] *[[GetOpenState]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Lock Functions]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Sets a numeric ini setting, using the same format for the setting name as [[GetNumericIniSetting]]. Accepts variables as arguments.
Returns 1 for a successful change, and 0 on a failed one. For example, it may fail if the INI setting could not be found. |name = SetNumericINISetting |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = settingName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = float }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetNumericINISetting]] *[[SetStringINISetting]] / [[GetStringINISetting]] *[[GetNumericGameSetting]] / [[SetNumericGameSetting]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:INI Functions]]

revision.comment: Fix documentation for how this works on references
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Sets the specified [[Script]] onto the specified [[BaseForm]], and returns any previous script. If ''ScriptableObject'' is omitted and a [[calling reference]] is provided, that reference's BaseForm is modified. |name = SetScript |returnType = previousScript:baseForm |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = NewScript |Type = baseForm }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = ScriptableObject |Type = baseForm |Optional = optional }} }} ==Example== 
ref OldScript

set OldScript to SunnyREF.SetScript MyNewSunnyScript

; or (required if used for non-reference)

SetScript MyNewSunnyScript, SunnyREF
==Warning== * If the target object already has a script attached to it, after changing the script the stored values of local variables for references to that object will not match the variables in the new script. If called on a reference, the variable list for the calling reference will be updated to match the new script, but other references to the same base object will not be updated. * When changing the script of a Base Form, the references created from it will retain the original script until the game loads again. You can run SetScript in a [[MenuMode]] 4 block so that the base form's script is updated before references are loaded from it. **If a reference is passed, the script that reference uses will be refreshed instantly. * SetScript is safe to use with quests, with previously unscripted objects, and on unique references (for which no other references exist to the same base object). Using the command on base objects for which non-unique references exist may result in undefined behavior. ==See Also== *[[GetScript]] *[[ResetAllVariables]] *[[CompareScripts]] *[[GetCurrentScript]] [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Script Functions]]

{{Deprecated | This function has been deprecated as [[Bug: Children Tiles|it is bugged]], but is still preserved in its original state as multiple mods depend on its behavior. Use '''[[SetUIStringAlt]]''' instead.}}
----
{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Sets the value of a string UI trait. Trait name is of the format used by [[GetUIFloat]]. |name = SetUIString |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = traitName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = string }} }} ==See Also== *[[SetUIStringEx]] *[[GetUIFloat]] *[[SetUIFloat]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:String Functions]] [[Category:Deprecated Functions]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Sets the repair list for the specified item. If ''itemForm'' is omitted and a [[calling reference]] is passed, will use that reference's BaseForm. |name = SetRepairList |returnType = void |referenceType = itemRefr |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = repairFormList |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = itemForm |Type = baseForm |Optional = y }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetRepairList]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Sets the base weight of the specified [[Base Form]]. If a [[reference]] is passed, its baseform is derived. |name = SetWeight |returnType = void |referenceType = refr |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = float |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = item |Type = baseform |Optional = optional }} }} ==Notes== * If Item mods are given a weight, they will crash when loaded into the world or removed from your inventory - unless you have lStewieAl's Tweaks, or your itemMod was implemented via [[Devkit]].
==See Also== *[[GetWeight]] *[[RefreshInventoryWeight]]
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]]

revision.comment: https
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Taps the key or mouse button assigned to a control, which is specified by its int code. *With the [https://www.nexusmods.com/newvegas/mods/58277 JIP LN NVSE Plugin] installed, this function will also work when using a controller. |name = TapControl |alias = tc |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = controlCode |Type = int |Value = 0-27 }} }} ==Example== Suppose you wanted to force third person if the player is in first: 
if IsPC1stPerson
    TapControl 13 ; POV key
endif
==Control Codes== *0:Forward *1:Backward *2:Left *3:Right *4:Attack *5:Activate *6:Block *7:Ready Item *8:Crouch *9:Run *10:Always Run *11:Auto-Move *12:Jump *13:TogglePOV *14:MenuMode *15:Rest *16:VATS *17:Hotkey 1 *18:Hotkey 2 (Ammo swap key) *19:Hotkey 3 *20:Hotkey 4 *21:Hotkey 5 *22:Hotkey 6 *23:Hotkey 7 *24:Hotkey 8 *25:Quicksave *26:Quickload *27:Grab ==See Also== *[[GetControl]] *[[TapKey]] *[[HoldKey]] *[[Con_CloseAllMenus]] [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Fakes the key press specified by [[IsKeyPressed|int scancode]] for one frame. |name = TapKey |alias = tk |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = scanCode |Type = int }} }} ==Example== 
TapKey 20
{{DirectX Scancodes}} ==See Also== *[[DisableKey]] *[[EnableKey]] *[[GetKeyPress]] *[[HoldKey]] *[[IsKeyDisabled]] *[[IsKeyPressed]] *[[MenuHoldKey]] *[[MenuReleaseKey]] *[[MenuTapKey]] *[[OnKeyDown]] *[[OnKeyUp]] *[[ReleaseKey]] [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Input Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

revision.comment: NVSE 6.2.6
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Clones the specifies [[BaseForm]], returning a new base form. If ''ToClone'' is omitted and a [[calling reference]] is provided, that reference's BaseForm is used. By default, a cloned form's ID has the '''FF''' mod index, like dynamic objects generated during the course of gameplay. If ''indexToCallingScript'' is true, it will instead use the index of the mod to which the calling script belongs, taking the first available form ID in it. Either way, this cloned base form will not be persisted in the save game but the ref var containing the temporary form ID will be (which will be invalid on game load) so you must reinitialize the form on [[GetGameRestarted|game restart]]. |name = TempCloneForm |returnType = Clone:baseForm |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ToClone |Type = baseForm |Optional = optional }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = indexToCallingScript |Type = bool |Optional = optional }} }} ==Example== 
ref MyClone

set MyClone to TempCloneForm Vodka
''MyClone'' now points to a clone of the 'Vodka' ingestible, which uses a form ID with a '''FF''' prefix. 
ref MyClone

set MyClone to TempCloneForm Vodka 1
''MyClone'' now points to a clone of the 'Vodka' ingestible, with a form ID using the same prefix as the mod to which the script belongs. ==See Also== *[[IsClonedForm]] *[[CreateTempRef]] *[[Mod Local Data]] (A method to store data independent of save games) *[[Auxiliary Variables]] (Another method) *[[IsScriptMarkedOnLoad]] (Another method) [[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.1 |summary = Executes a [[UDF|User Defined Function]] or an inline [[Lambda|Lambda script]] each frame until specified amount of seconds have elapsed in-game. Only runs in [[GameMode]] by default, or if ''runWhen'' is set to 0. If ''runWhen'' is set to 1, will '''also''' run in [[MenuMode]]. If it is set to 2, will '''also''' pause the callback while the main menu / pause menu / console menu MenuModes are open ''(new in 6.3.0)''. Every queued call is removed when loading the game ([[GetGameLoaded]]), so they may need to be reset during then. *NOTE: This means that timers created using this function are '''NOT savebaked''', which could potentially cause bugs if a timer was expected to keep running after saving and reloading a save while the timer was running. Arguments can be passed like [[Call]] to the function (since [[xNVSE]] 6.2.6). If an arg is an array, then its lifetime will be extended until the callbacks have stopped. |name = CallForSeconds |alias = CallFor |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = seconds |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = udf |Type = script }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = runWhen |Type = int |Optional = y |Value = 0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = multi |Type = arg(up to 15) |Optional = y }} }} ==Example with an inline Lambda script== 
CallForSeconds 10 (begin function {} 
    print "You look so shiftless"
end)
Or 
CallForSeconds 10 ({} => print "You look so shiftless")
Calls UDF each frame for 10 seconds while in GameMode. See [[Lambda]]. ==Example with a UDF== 
scn MyUDF
Begin Function { ref rPlayer }
    print "la la la ", + $rPlayer 
End
CallForSeconds 10 MyUDF 1 PlayerRef
==Notes== *The seconds are '''not''' counted in real-life seconds; they are affected by the game's Time Mult ([[GetGlobalTimeMultiplier]]). **The time mult affects the callback '''even while inside [[MenuMode]]'''. ==See Also== *[[CallWhile]] *[[CallLoop]] *[[CallAfterSeconds]] *[[SetGameMainLoopCallback]] *[[Lambda]] *[[Inline Expression]] *[[Call]] [[Category:Functions (NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.1 |summary = Executes a [[UDF|User Defined Function]] or an inline [[Lambda|Lambda script]] '''each frame''' while specified condition UDF (or a Lambda) returns true. If it returns false, then the handler will never be re-evaluated again, unless it is reset by another call to [[CallWhile]]. Every queued call is removed when loading the game ([[GetGameLoaded]]), so they may need to be reset during then. *NOTE: This means that callbacks created using this function are '''NOT savebaked''', which could potentially cause bugs if a callback was expected to keep running after saving and reloading a save while the callback was running. Runs in both [[GameMode]] and [[MenuMode]], unless ''DontRunInMenuMode'' and/or ''DontRunInGameMode'' flags are enabled (see below). Will also run while paused (meaning while main menu / pause menu / console menu MenuModes are open) unless ''DontRunWhilePaused'' is enabled. The following features have been added starting from [[xNVSE]] 6.2.6. ''flags'' is a [[bitmask]], whose bits toggle on/off the following features: 
PassArgsToTheCalledFunction    = 1 (bit #0)  - xNVSE 6.2.6
PassArgsToTheConditionFunction = 2 (bit #1)  - xNVSE 6.2.6
DontRunInMenuMode              = 4 (bit #2)  - xNVSE 6.2.8
DontRunInGameMode              = 8 (bit #3)  - xNVSE 6.2.8        
DontRunWhilePaused             = 16 (bit #4) - xNVSE 6.3.0
For example, if flags = 0, then none of these features are on. If flags = 0b11 ([[Binary Notation]], or 3 in decimal), then the first two are on. By default, all flags are off (''flags'' = 0). Arguments can be passed like [[Call]] to the function. If an arg is an array, then its lifetime will be extended until the callbacks have stopped. If ''PassArgsToTheCalledFunction'' is on, then the [[Call]]-like args will be passed to the ''udf'', and if ''PassArgsToTheConditionFunction'' is on, they'll be passed to the ''condition'' UDF. Both flags can be on at the same time. |name = CallWhile |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = udf |Type = script }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = condition |Type = script }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flags |Type = int |Optional = y |Value = 0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = multi |Type = arg(up to 15) |Optional = y }} |example = CallWhile ({} => ivar += 1) ({} => ivar < 1000) Calls left UDF each frame until value of ivar reaches 1000 }} ==See Also== *[[CallWhen]] *[[CallLoop]] *[[CallForSeconds]] *[[CallAfterSeconds]] *[[SetGameMainLoopCallback]] *[[Lambda]] *[[Inline Expression]] *[[Call]] [[Category:Functions (NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.1 |summary = Executes a [[UDF|User Defined Function]] or an inline [[Lambda|Lambda script]] after specified amount of seconds have elapsed in-game. Only runs in [[GameMode]] by default, or if ''runWhen'' is set to 0. If ''runWhen'' is set to 1, will '''also''' run in [[MenuMode]]. If it is set to 2, will '''also''' pause the callback while the main menu / pause menu / console menu MenuModes are open ''(new in 6.3.0)''. Every queued call is removed when loading the game ([[GetGameLoaded]]), so they may need to be reset during then. *NOTE: This means that timers created using this function are '''NOT savebaked''', which could potentially cause bugs if a timer was expected to keep running after saving and reloading a save while the timer was running. Arguments can be passed like [[Call]] to the function (since [[xNVSE]] 6.2.6). If an arg is an array, then its lifetime will be extended until the callbacks have stopped. |name = CallAfterSeconds |alias = CallAfter |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = seconds |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = udf |Type = script }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = runWhen |Type = int |Optional = y |Value = 0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = multi |Type = arg(up to 15) |Optional = y }} }} ==Example with an inline Lambda script== 
CallAfterSeconds 10 (begin function {}
    print "10 seconds later..."
end)
Or 
CallAfterSeconds 5.5 ({} => print "5.5 seconds later...")
Calls UDF after 5.5 seconds, but only in GameMode. See [[Lambda]]. ==Example with a UDF== 
scn MyUDF
Begin Function { ref rPlayer }
    print "5.5 seconds later... My name is " + $rPlayer
End
CallAfterSeconds 5.5 MyUDF 1 PlayerRef
==Notes== *The seconds are '''not''' counted in real-life seconds; they are affected by the game's Time Mult ([[GetGlobalTimeMultiplier]]). **The time mult affects the callback '''even while inside [[MenuMode]]'''. *If you pass 0 as an argument for seconds, the callback is called next frame. This is similar behaviour to [[CallFunctionNextFrame]]. ==See Also== *[[Call]] *[[CallAfterFrames]] *[[CallWhile]] *[[CallForSeconds]] *[[CallLoop]] *[[Inline Expression]] *[[Lambda]] *[[SetGameMainLoopCallback]] [[Category:Functions (NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.1 |summary = Like [[Ar_Dump]], but prints the contents of the array to the specified file (in the [[GetFalloutDirectory|Fallout directory]]). Intended as a debugging tool.
If ''bAppend'' is omitted or set to 1, the contents of the file are appended to the file's previous text. Otherwise, all previous content is erased. |name = Ar_DumpF |returnType = None |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ToDump |Type = array }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = fileName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = bAppend |Type = bool |Value = 0/1 |Optional = y }} |example = Ar_DumpF SomeArray "MyFile.txt" }}
==See Also== *[[DumpFormList]] Similar function but for [[FormList]]s. *[[DumpLoadOrder]] *[[Ar_Dump]] / [[Ar_DumpID]] *[[WriteArrayToFile]] can be used for larger/multidimensional arrays that the console may not be able to fully display. *[[Con_SCOF]] (writes console output to an external text file) *[[Con_SQV]] (prints all variables attached to a quest) *[[Debug Dumps]] *[[Print]] *[[DebugPrint]] *[[Array Variables]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:Debugging]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.1 |summary = Prints the given variable's name and value to the console. Primary use case is debugging while scripting.
Since [[xNVSE]] 6.3.0, up to 14 additional variables can be passed to the function. This will act the same way as calling '''PrintVar''' multiple times with those different variables. |name = PrintVar |alias = PrintV |returnType = none |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = variableName |Type = variable }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = otherVariableNames |Type = variable(up to 14) |Optional = y }} |example = let fMyVariable := 2.5 printv fMyVariable Console >> fMyVariable: 2.5
'''Printing multiple variables in one line:''' let fMyVariable := 2.5 let aMyVariable = ar_List 2, 4 printv fMyVariable, aMyVariable Console >> fMyVariable: 2.5 >> aMyVariable: [2, 4] }} ==See Also== *[[DebugPrintVar]] *[[Print]] *[[PrintDebug]] *[[PrintF]] *[[Con_SCOF]] *[[Debug Dumps]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Debugging]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.1.6 |summary = Takes an [[array]] and a function and returns a new array that containing elements that pass the test implemented by the provided filter function. |name = Ar_Filter |returnType = array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = source |Type = array}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = filter |Type = function/lambda}} }} ==Example== 
array_var aWords = ar_List "spray", "limit", "elite", "exuberant", "destruction", "present"

array_var aResult = ar_Filter aWords ({array_var aIter} => sv_Length (*aIter) > 6)
Content of aResult: "exuberant", "destruction", "present" 
array_var aResult = ar_Filter aActors (begin function { array_var aIter }
    ref rActor = aIter["value"]
    if rActor.GetDead == 0
        SetFunctionValue 1
    endif
end)
; equivalent to ar_Filter aActors ({array_var aIter} => !(*aIter).GetDead)
; filters out all dead actors
==Notes== * To see why the * operator is used and why aIter is an array_var, see Dereference/Unbox in [[NVSE Expressions]]. * aIter["key"] will contain the index of aWords. If aWords was a [[String Map]] then it would contain the string key. ==See Also== *[[Ar_MapTo]] *[[Ar_FindWhere]] *[[Lambda]] *[[Array Variables]] *[[NVSE Expressions]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

revision.comment: Add note about return array type
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.1.6 |summary = Creates a new [[Array]] populated with the results of calling a provided function on every element in the calling array. The [[TypeOf|type]] of the new array will be the same as the source array. |name = Ar_MapTo |returnType = array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Source |Type = array}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = MapFunction |Type = function/lambda}} }} ==Example== 
array_var aIter
array_var aNumbers = ar_List 1, 4, 9, 16

array_var aResult = ar_MapTo aNumbers ({aIter} => *aIter * 2);
Content of aResult: 2, 8, 18, 32 ==Notes== * To see why the '''*''' operator is used and why '''aIter''' is an array_var, see Dereference/Unbox in [[NVSE Expressions]]. * '''aIter["key"]''' will contain the index of '''aNumbers'''. If '''aNumbers''' was a [[String Map]] then it would contain the string key. ==See Also== *[[Ar_Filter]] *[[Ar_FindWhere]] *[[Lambda]] *[[Array Variables]] *[[NVSE Expressions]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

revision.comment: add example
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.1.6 |summary = Returns the value of the first element in the provided array that satisfies the provided testing function. If no values satisfy the testing function, 0 is returned. |name = Ar_FindWhere |returnType = multi |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Source |Type = array}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = FindFunction |Type = function/lambda}} }} ==Example== 
array_var aIter
array_var aNumbers = ar_List 5, 12, 8, 130, 44

int iResult = ar_FindWhere aNumbers ({aIter} => *aIter > 10);
Content of iResult: 12 ===Checking if no matches were found=== If it's possible that no matches will be found, then a "not-found" result can be detected in the following way.
This assumes that the number 0 is NOT a valid value inside the array; if it is, then there is no way to discern if the search was successful or not. 
ref rBaseActor = GSSunnySmiles
array_var aIter

array_var aResult = &(ar_FindWhere aDetectingActors ({aIter} => (*aIter).GBF == rBaseActor))
if eval ((TypeOf *aResult) != "Number" || (*aResult != 0))
     let ref rActorRef = *aResult
else
     ;* Couldn't find a detecting actor with that actor baseform.
endif
==Notes== * To see why the * operator is used and why aIter is an array_var, see Dereference/Unbox in [[NVSE Expressions]]. * aIter["key"] will contain the index of aNumbers. If aWords was a [[String Map]] then it would contain the string key. ==See Also== *[[Ar_Filter]] *[[Ar_MapTo]] *[[Lambda]] *[[Array Variables]] *[[NVSE Expressions]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.1.6 |summary = Returns true if the specified script at any point calls the specified command. |name = HasScriptCommand |returnType = success:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = CommandOpcode |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Script/Form |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = EventID |Type = int |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== 
; Activate is 4109
if HasScriptCommand 4109 SunnyRef  
   ; do stuff
endif
or 
if HasScriptCommand (GetCommandOpcode "Activate") TestScript 2 ; Check if TestScript has activate command inside OnActivate block
   ; do stuff
endif
==See Also== *[[HasScriptBlock]] *[[GetCommandOpcode]] *[[Begin|Event IDs]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Script Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.1.6 |summary = Returns the command opcode for the specified command |name = GetCommandOpcode |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = CommandName |Type = string}} }} ==Example== 
int iActivateCommandOpcode = GetCommandOpcode "Activate"
Content of iActivateCommandOpcode: 4109 ==See Also== *[[ScriptHasCommand]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.1.6 |summary = Decompiles a script to a file (path is in this format: Fallout New Vegas\DecompiledScripts\ModName\ScriptName.gek). Meant to be used as a console function. Handy if a script source has been removed from a script. |name = DecompileScript |returnType = array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Script |Type = form}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = FileExtension |Type = string (optional)}} }} ==Example== 
DecompileScript ArcadeScript
Creates and populates \Fallout New Vegas\DecompiledScripts\FalloutNV.esm\ArcadeScript.gek with 
ScriptName ArcadeScript

Int Waiting
Int CombatStyleMelee
Int CombatStyleRanged
Int IsFollowingDefault
Int IsFollowingLong
Int FollowerSwitchAggressive

Begin OnDeath
	If VNPCFollowers.ArcadeHired == 1
		Set VNPCFollowers.nCurrentFollowers To VNPCFollowers.nCurrentFollowers - 1

		If VNPCFollowers.nCurrentFollowers == 0
			Set VNPCFollowers.bPlayerHasFollower To 0
		EndIf

		Set VNPCFollowers.ArcadeHired To 0
		Set VNPCFollowers.bHumanoidInParty To 0
		ShowMessage FollowerMessageDeadArcade
		RemovePerk BetterHealing
		ShowMessage FollowerMessagePerkArcadeRemove
	EndIf

	Set VNPCFollowers.bArcadeDead To 1

	If GetQuestCompleted VMS54 == 0
		SetStage VMS54 110
	EndIf

	If GetObjectiveDisplayed VMS35 50 == 1 && GetObjectiveCompleted VMS35 50 == 0
		SetObjectiveDisplayed VMS35 50 0
	EndIf

End

Begin OnCombatEnd
	If GetPlayerTeammate == 1
		ResetHealth
		RestoreActorValue PerceptionCondition 100
		RestoreActorValue EnduranceCondition 100
		RestoreActorValue LeftAttackCondition 100
		RestoreActorValue LeftMobilityCondition 100
		RestoreActorValue RightAttackCondition 100
		RestoreActorValue RightMobilityCondition 100
	EndIf

End

Begin OnLoad
	If CombatStyleMelee == 1
		SetCombatStyle FollowersCombatStyleMelee
	Else 
		SetCombatStyle FollowersCombatStyleRanged
	EndIf

End
==Decompile All Scripts For A Specific Mod== 
foreach aIter <- GetLoadedTypeArray 17 (GetModIndex "INSERT MOD NAME HERE.esp")
    DecompileScript (*aIter)
loop
This should needless to be said placed behind a [[GetGameRestarted]] check or it will decompile all the files every frame. ==Notes== * This function can also take a TESPackage form, and can decompile it's Begin, End and Change scripts. * As of v6.3.3, it can now decompile result scripts for any applicable form type. ==See Also== *[[ScriptHasCommand]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Script Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.1.7 |summary = Creates a new [[Array]] populated with a specified amount of elements. For each element, starting from the first element to the last one, ''valueGenerator'' is called and an element is set to that result.
If ''keyGenerator'' is omitted, then the resulting array will be of '''Array''' type, and each value being generated is being appended to that array.
Otherwise, each Value being generated is tied to the Key generated by ''keyGenerator''. In the latter case, the type of the returned array will be a '''StringMap''' / '''Map''', depending on the type of value that the ''keyGenerator'' first returns.
Key generation and Value generation are evaluated side-by-side, with the ''valueGenerator'' function being called first. Returns [[Ar_Null]] if the ''keyGenerator'' function ever returns a non-string or non-numeric element. |name = Ar_Generate |returnType = array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = numElems |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = valueGenerator |Type = function/lambda }} {{FunctionArgument |Name = keyGenerator |Type = function/lambda |Optional = y }} |example = let array_var aTest := ar_Generate 5 ({} => 0) '''aTest''' is filled with 5 elements, which are each initialized to 0. *Note that this is less efficient than `[[ar_Init]] 5 0`, as the result has to be re-evaluated for each element, even though it will always be zero. let array_var aNumbers := ar_Generate 13 ({} => (Rand 0, 100)) '''aNumbers''' is filled with 13 elements, which are each assigned a random float number between 0 and 99. 
let int iCounter := -1
let array_var aArray := Ar_Generate 20, ({} => (player.GetEquippedObject (iCounter += 1)))
'''aArray''' is filled with forms; from index 0 to 19, the array's elements are set to the equipped object (if any) occupying the equip slot determined by that index. *Note that an object can occupy multiple equip slots, so this method is not preferable to retrieve each unique equipped form. Use [[GetEquippedItems]] for that instead. 
scn DumpLimbHealthUDF

begin Function { }

    let ref rActor := this
    let int iCounter := 24
    let array_var aLimbHealths := ar_Generate 7 ({} => rActor.GetAV (iCounter += 1)) ({} => ActorValueToStringC iCounter)
    ar_dump aLimbHealths 

end
This function dumps the health of each limb for the calling actor.
It does this by generating a StringMap by calling Ar_Generate, which loops through each AV code for limb health (25-31) and calling some functions on those limb IDs. Example output: 
** Dumping Array #1 **
Refs: 1 Owner 0F: Some Mod.esp
[ BrainCondition ] : 100.000000
[ EnduranceCondition ] : 54.111355
[ LeftAttackCondition ] : 0.000000
[ LeftMobilityCondition ] : 0.000000
[ PerceptionCondition ] : 100.000000
[ RightAttackCondition ] : 100.000000
[ RightMobilityCondition ] : 100.000000
}} ==Notes== *The ''keyGenerator'' argument was only added in xNVSE v6.2.1. ==See Also== *[[Ar_Init]] *[[Ar_Construct]] *[[Ar_MapTo]] *[[Ar_Filter]] *[[Ar_FindWhere]] *[[Lambda]] *[[Array Variables]] *[[NVSE Expressions]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.1.7 |name = ar_Init |summary = Creates a new (numerically-indexed) [[Array]] populated with a specified amount of an element. Every element of that array is set to the provided ''elem'', which can be of any type. |returnType = array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = numElems |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = initElem |Type = anyType }} }} ==Example== let array_var aTest := ar_Init 5 0 '''aTest''' is filled with 5 elements, which are all initialized to 0.
let array_var aRandomNumber := ar_Init 13 (Rand 0, 100) '''aRandomNumber''' is filled with 13 elements, which are all assigned to the same random number that was generated once by `Rand 0, 100`.
let array_var aString := ar_Init 50 "SomeString" '''aString ''' is filled with 50 elements, which are all assigned to "SomeString".
==See Also== *[[Ar_Generate]] *[[Ar_Construct]] *[[Ar_MapTo]] *[[Ar_Filter]] *[[Ar_FindWhere]] *[[Lambda]] *[[Array Variables]] *[[NVSE Expressions]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.2.1 |name = Ar_DeepEquals |alias = Ar_DeepCompare |summary = Compares the contents of two arrays, including key/element pairs from nested sub-arrays (though the arrays do not need to have sub-arrays). Returns 1 (true) if the arrays have the same contents in the same order, 0 (false) otherwise. If both arrays are null, also returns 1. In xNVSE v6.2.1, with [[NVSE Expressions]] enabled via [[Let]], [[Eval]], etc, the Equality/Inequality operators (==/!=) can be used to compare the surface contents of two arrays. However, any sub-arrays are compared via checking their numeric IDs only, and not by checking their contents, so this function is made for comparing potentially multidimensional arrays in an extensive manner. |returnVal = isEqual |returnType = bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = array1 |Type = array_var }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = array2 |Type = array_var }} }} ==Example== 
array_var arr1
array_var arr2

let arr1 := ar_List 1, 2
let arr2 := ar_List 1, 2
print $(Ar_DeepEquals arr1, arr2)
Prints "1" (true), since both arrays are equal. 
let arr1 := ar_List 1, 2
let arr2 := ar_List 1, 100
print $(Ar_DeepEquals arr1, arr2)
Prints "0" (false), since both arrays have different elements. 
let arr1 := ar_List 1, 2, (ar_list SunnyREF, 1, (ar_list 2, (ar_list 5, (Ar_Map 2::ArmorLeather, 10::CowboyHat05))))
let arr2 := ar_List 1, 2, (ar_list SunnyREF, 1 (ar_list 2, (ar_list 5, (Ar_Map 2::ArmorLeather, 10::CowboyHat05))))
print $(Ar_DeepEquals arr1, arr2)
This prints "1" (true), since both arrays have identical key/element pairs in the same order. 
let arr1 := ar_List 1, 2, (ar_list SunnyREF, 1, (ar_list 2, (ar_list 5, (Ar_Map 2::ArmorLeather, 10::CowboyHat05))))
let arr2 := ar_List 1, 2, (ar_list SunnyREF, 1 (ar_list 2, (ar_list 5, (Ar_Map 2000::ArmorLeather, 10::CowboyHat05))))
print $(Ar_DeepEquals arr1, arr2)
This prints "0" (false), since the second array has a different key for the ''ArmorLeather'' element. ==See Also== *[[Ar_DeepCopy]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.2.1 |summary = Sets the [[ammo]] '''Regen Rate''' for the [[Weapon]] [[BaseForm]]. If ''weapon'' is omitted and a calling ref..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.2.1 |summary = Sets the [[ammo]] '''Regen Rate''' for the [[Weapon]] [[BaseForm]]. If ''weapon'' is omitted and a [[calling reference]] is provided, that reference's BaseForm is used. |name = SetWeaponRegenRate |returnType = bool |returnVal = success |referenceType = weaponRefr |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = regenRate |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = baseform |Optional = optional }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetWeaponRegenRate]]
[[Category:Functions (NVSE)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.2.1 |summary = Returns the [[ammo]] '''Regen Rate''' for the [[Weapon]] [[BaseForm]]. If ''weapon'' is omitted and a [[calling reference]] is provided, that reference's BaseForm is used. |name = GetWeaponRegenRate |conditionFunc = Both |returnType = float |returnVal = regenRate |referenceType = weaponRefr |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = baseform |Optional = optional }} }} ==See Also== *[[SetWeaponRegenRate]]
[[Category:Functions (NVSE)]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.2.1 |summary = Takes an [[array]] and a function and executes the function once for each array element. |name = Ar_ForEach |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = source |Type = array}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = function |Type = function/lambda}} }} ==Example== 
array_var aIter
array_var aWords = ar_List "spray", "limit", "elite", "exuberant", "destruction", "present"

ar_ForEach aWords ({aIter} => print *aIter);
; prints all items in the list

array = ar_list 4 9 100 1 43

; get the lowest value of an array
lowest = ar_first array
ar_ForEach array ({aIter} => lowest = GetMinOf (*aIter) lowest)
; lowest is now 1

; multiple lines (see Lambda wiki page)
ar_ForEach array (begin function {array_var aIter}
  print "the value of aIter is on the next line"
  print $(*aIter)
end)
==Notes== * To see why the * operator is used and why aIter is an array_var, see Dereference/Unbox in [[NVSE Expressions]]. * aIter["key"] will contain the index of aWords. If aWords was a [[String Map]] then it would contain the string key. * This is an alternative to [[ForEach]] which may be less verbose, but is less flexible (can't use control-flow statements like [[Break]]). ==See Also== *[[ForEach]] *[[ForEachInList]] - Equivalent for [[FormList]]s *[[Ar_MapTo]] *[[Ar_FindWhere]] *[[Lambda]] *[[Array Variables]] *[[NVSE Expressions]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.2.1 |summary = Takes an [[array]] and a function and returns true if any of the elements satisfy the function condition. |name..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.2.1 |summary = Takes an [[array]] and a function and returns true if any of the elements satisfy the function condition. |name = ar_Any |returnType = 0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = source |Type = array}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = conditionFunction |Type = function/lambda}} }} ==Example== 
array_var aIter
array_var aWords = ar_List "spray", "limit", "elite", "exuberant", "destruction", "present"

if ar_Any aWords ({aIter} => *aIter == "spray") ; returns 1
  print "success!"
elseif ar_Any aWords ({aIter} => *aIter == "Aunt Mary") ; returns 0
  print "failure!"
endif
==Notes== * To see why the * operator is used and why aIter is an array_var, see Dereference/Unbox in [[NVSE Expressions]]. * aIter["key"] will contain the index of aWords. If aWords was a [[String Map]] then it would contain the string key. ==See Also== *[[Ar_All]] *[[Ar_ForEach]] *[[Ar_MapTo]] *[[Ar_FindWhere]] *[[Lambda]] *[[Array Variables]] *[[NVSE Expressions]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.2.1 |summary = Takes an [[array]] and a function and returns true if all of the elements satisfy the function condition. |name..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.2.1 |summary = Takes an [[array]] and a function and returns true if all of the elements satisfy the function condition. |name = ar_All |returnType = 0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = source |Type = array}}{{FunctionArgument |Name = conditionFunction |Type = function/lambda}} }} ==Example== 
array_var aIter
array_var aNumbers = ar_List 1, 30, 39, 29, 10, 13

if ar_All aNumbers ({aIter} => *aIter < 40 ) ; returns 1
  print "success!"
endif
==Notes== * To see why the * operator is used and why aIter is an array_var, see Dereference/Unbox in [[NVSE Expressions]]. * aIter["key"] will contain the index of aNumbers. If aNumbers was a [[String Map]] then it would contain the string key. ==See Also== *[[Ar_Any]] *[[Ar_ForEach]] *[[Ar_MapTo]] *[[Ar_FindWhere]] *[[Lambda]] *[[Array Variables]] *[[NVSE Expressions]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.2.3 |summary = The [[condition]]-function equivalent of [[Call]]. Calls every [[UDF]] script in the specified '''UDFList''' formlist, and returns the [[SetFunctionValue|value returned by the last-called UDF]]. If no values are returned, then it returns 0. If the return value is a number ([[TypeOf]] == "Number"), then it returns that directly. Otherwise, the value is converted into a boolean. For example, if an array is the last return value, then it returns if the array is valid or not (1 or 0, respectively). The UDFs are executed in the order they are arranged in the formlist. The "Run On" subject for the condition is passed to the UDF, and can be retrieved using [[GetSelf]]/[[This]]. No function arguments are passed, so the scripts should begin with: 
begin Function { }
If '''bBreakIfFalse''' is set to non-zero, then if a UDF returns a false-y value during formlist loop, it will prevent further UDFs from being called and return 0. Returns 0 when called from a script (not meant for use there). |name = Call |returnType = int |returnVal = lastUDFResult |referenceType = runOnSubject |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = UDFList |Type = FormList }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = bBreakIfFalse |Type = int |Optional = y }} }} ==Notes== *Warning: some conditions are evaluated each frame, while others are not. ==See Also== *[[Call]] *[[User Defined Function]] *[[SetFunctionValue]] *[[GetCallingScript]] *[[RunScript]] *[[Let]] *[[Eval]] *[[ConditionPrint]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:User Defined Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.2.4 |summary = Executes a [[UDF|function]] when a specified condition function returns true exactly once. Condition is evaluated '''each frame''' until it becomes true. Every queued call is removed when loading the game ([[GetGameLoaded]]), so they may need to be reset during then. *NOTE: This means that callbacks created using this function are '''NOT savebaked''', which could potentially cause bugs if a callback was expected to keep running after saving and reloading a save while the callback was running. Runs in both [[GameMode]] and [[MenuMode]], unless ''DontRunInMenuMode'' and/or ''DontRunInGameMode'' flags are enabled (see below). Will also run while paused (meaning while main menu / pause menu / console menu MenuModes are open) unless ''DontRunWhilePaused'' is enabled. The following features have been added starting from [[xNVSE]] 6.2.6. ''flags'' is a [[bitmask]], whose bits toggle on/off the following features: 
PassArgsToTheCalledFunction    = 1 (bit #0)  - xNVSE 6.2.6
PassArgsToTheConditionFunction = 2 (bit #1)  - xNVSE 6.2.6
DontRunInMenuMode              = 4 (bit #2)  - xNVSE 6.2.8
DontRunInGameMode              = 8 (bit #3)  - xNVSE 6.2.8   
DontRunWhilePaused             = 16 (bit #4) - xNVSE 6.3.0
For example, if flags = 0, then none of these features are on. If flags = 0b11 ([[Binary Notation]], or 3 in decimal), then the first two are on. By default, all flags are off (''flags'' = 0). Arguments can be passed like [[Call]] to the function. If an arg is an array, then its lifetime will be extended until the callbacks have stopped. If ''PassArgsToTheCalledFunction'' is on, then the [[Call]]-like args will be passed to the ''udf'', and if ''PassArgsToTheConditionFunction'' is on, they'll be passed to the ''condition'' UDF. Both flags can be on at the same time. |name = CallWhen |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = udf |Type = script }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = condition |Type = script }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flags |Type = int |Optional = y |Value = 0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = multi |Type = arg(up to 15) |Optional = y }} |example = CallWhen ({} => print "Alert! You're almost dying!") ({} => player.GetAV Health < 10) When player health goes below 10, left [[User Defined Function|function]] is called exactly once and never again (unless CallWhen is executed again later). }} ==See Also== *[[CallWhile]] *[[CallForSeconds]] *[[CallAfterSeconds]] *[[SetGameMainLoopCallback]] *[[Lambda]] *[[Inline Expression]] *[[Call]] [[Category:Functions (NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = RemoveAllPackages removes the specified actor's AI packages. |name = RemoveAllPackages |returnType = void |referenceType = [ActorRefID] |example = BuddyRef.RemoveAllPackages }}
==Notes== * You can reset the actor's AI packages using [[EvaluatePackage]] or [[ResetAI]].
==See Also== *[[AddScriptPackage]] *[[EvaluatePackage]] *[[RemovePackageAt]] *[[RemoveScriptPackage]]
[[Category:Functions (NVSE)]] [[Category:AI Functions]] [[Category:Package Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Adds a package to calling actor's AI package list at a specified index. Using -1 as an index appends the package to the end of the list. Existing packages are not replaced when adding at the same index, indices are shifted up instead.

This only affects the targeted actor ref, so the base actor form and other refs using it are unaffected This is perfect for dynamically adding packages to spawned actors without having a mess of packages with equally messy conditions on the base actor form. |name = AddPackageAt |returnType = int:index |referenceType = actor |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = package |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = index |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = actor |Type = reference |Optional = y }} |example = SunnyREF.AddPackageAt MyPackage 0 }} ==See Also== *[[AddScriptPackage]] *[[RemovePackageAt]] [[Category:Functions (NVSE)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:AI Functions]] [[Category:Package Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Gets the count of packages from an actor base form. |name = GetPackageCount |returnType = void |referenceType = [ActorRefID] |example = BuddyRef.GetPackageCount }}
==Notes== * Prior to xNVSE 6.2.5, this function always printed in console that the result was 0. This was merely visual, as the actual result was accurate.
==See Also== *[[AddScriptPackage]] *[[EvaluatePackage]] *[[GetBasePackages]] *[[GetCurrentAIPackage]] *[[GetCurrentPackage]] *[[GetIsCurrentPackage]] *[[RemoveAllPackages]] *[[RemoveScriptPackage]]
[[Category:Functions (NVSE)]] [[Category:AI Functions]] [[Category:Package Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.2.3 |summary = Returns a ([[Ar_Copy|non-deeply]]) copied version of the source array that has no duplicate values. The first instance of a value in the array is what's kept in the new array; all other duplicate values following that are discarded. |name = Ar_Unique |returnType = array |conditionFunc = Script |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = source |Type = array_var }} |example = array_var aTest 
 let aTest := ar_List 1, 2, 3, 2, 2, 2, 3
 let aTest := Ar_Unique aTest
The ''aTest'' array will be set to contain [1, 2, 3]. }} ==See Also== *[[Ar_List]] *[[Ar_Map]] *[[Array Variables]] *[[NVSE Expressions]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Makes a specified quest the active quest. |name = SetCurrentQuest |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = quest |Type = form }} |example = SetCurrentQuest VMS18 }}
==Notes== *This function is identical to the vanilla function [[ForceActiveQuest]].
==See Also== *[[GetActiveQuest]] *[[GetCurrentQuest]]
[[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:Functions (FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Quest Functions]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the objectiveID of the Nth objective of the quest |name = GetNthQuestObjective |alias = GetNthObjective |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = QuestID |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = index |Type = int }} |example = int myObjective set myObjective to GetNthQuestObjective someQuest 2 }}
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Quest Functions]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

{{Function |origin = FOSE1 |summary = Returns the number of objectives for the specified [[Quest]]. |name = GetQuestObjectiveCount |alias = GetObjectiveCount |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Quest |Type = baseform |Optional = no }} |example = int totalObjectives set totalObjectives to GetQuestObjectiveCount someQuest }}
[[Category:Functions_(FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Quest Functions]] [[Category:Engine_Differences_(Fallout)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

revision.comment: add return val
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.2.5 |summary = Modifies the numeric value of a [[float]] UI trait.
The trait name is of the format "MenuName/ElementName/ElementName/.../TraitName" as defined in the XML hierarchy for the desired menu, i.e. "MessageMenu/NoGlow_Branch/MM_Title/x". Returns 1 (true) upon successful change, 0 (false) otherwise. |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = traitName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = float }} |returnType = bool |returnVal = success |example = ModUIFloat "MessageMenu/NoGlow_Branch/MM_Title/x" -5 }} ==Notes== *The traitName argument to Get/Set/ModUI(Float/String) has two extra capabilities. A path segment of "*" matches any child tile, and a path segment suffix of ":N" matches the N'th child tile. This is useful when accessing list box items, where the tile names are either identical, or unpredictable, or both. ==See Also== *[[GetUIFloatAlt]] *[[SetUIStringAlt]] *[[GetUIString]] *[[SetUIFloatMultiple]] *[[SetUIFloatAlt]] [[Category:Functions (NVSE)]] [[Category:UI Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.2.5 |summary = Takes a [[FormList]] and a [[UDF]] and executes the function once for each element in the list. |name = ForEachInList |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = source |Type = formList }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = function |Type = udf/lambda}} }} ==Example== ref rIter ForEachInList MyFormList ({rIter} => printvar rIter) Will print each element in the formlist to console.
==Notes== * This is an alternative to [[ForEach]] which may be less verbose, but is less flexible (can't use control-flow statements like [[Break]]).
==See Also== *[[ForEach]] - supports more sophisticated FormList looping *[[Ar_ForEach]] - equivalent for [[Array Variables]] *[[Lambda]] *[[NVSE Expressions]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]] [[Category:List Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.2.6 |summary = Sets the Editor ID of a form. This function is NOT to be used on existing forms in the game, but is intended for usage when creating new forms such as lambdas and cloned forms which need to be referenced outside of the mod. |name = SetEditorID |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = form |Type = form }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = editor id |Type = string }} }} == Example == 
ref rLambda = {} => print "Hello world"
SetEditorID rLambda "NamedLambda"

ref rClonedForm = TempCloneForm Vodka
SetEditorID rClonedForm "ClonedVodka"
==See also== *[[GetEditorID]] [[Category:Functions (NVSE)]] [[Category:ID Functions]] [[Category:String Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the permanent flag on a base form. This change is [[savebaked]]. |name = SetPermanent |returnType = Float |conditionFunc = Script |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = flag |Type = Integer }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = ref |Type = ObjectRef |Optional = y }} }} ==Example== 
SetPermanent 1 SomeRef

SetPermanent 0
==See Also== *[[GetPermanent]] *[[IsPersistent]] *[[IsQuestItem]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.2.8 |summary = Allows up to 16 value filters of any type (numeric, form, string, array), as well as array-of-filters filters. Also allows specifying the event handler's priority amongst other handlers, more information in the '''Priority System''' section. Details on how filter types are handled are available here: [[New xNVSE Event Filtering System]]. '''CAUTION:''' '''''All''''' event handlers registered with this function will be '''cleared/flushed on load'''. LN events CANNOT be used with this function, since [[xNVSE]] does not directly manage those events. For example, [[OnCellEnter]] is an invalid event to use for this function. Each ''filter'' arg is a pair. In this case, the numeric index of the nth argument to filter is paired with the actual filter. Example: 
1::"I am a string filter"
Assuming that the first arg dispatched is a string, then this will filter that arg; if the two strings match, then this event handler is dispatched, otherwise it isn't.
