Index 0 is a special case; it is reserved to filter the [[calling reference]], also known as what is returned by [[GetSelf]] / [[GetSelfAlt]]. The other 15 value filters (indexes 1-15) are for filtering the args that are passed. Lastly, a priority value can be specified, see the '''Priority System''' section for more information. Multiple event handlers can be set for the same script [[UDF]], though one should avoid having redundant handlers, otherwise the ''handlerFunction'' could be called multiple times for the same event dispatch. To remove these handlers, use [[RemoveEventHandler]], which now supports numeric-type arg indexes. Like [[SetEventHandler]], this works with '''User-Defined Events''', which can be dispatched via [[DispatchEvent]] and [[DispatchEventAlt]]. |name = SetEventHandlerAlt |returnType = success:bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = eventName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerFunction |Type = [[UDF]] }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = filters(up-to-16) |Type = pair |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = priority |Type = pair |Optional = y |Value = "priority"::1 }} |example = 
scn OnHitUDF
begin Function { ref rTarget, ref rAttacker }
    printvar rTarget
    printvar rAttacker 
    print "OnHitUDF >> RAN!"
end
The following code uses the [[NVSE Event Handling|OnHit NVSE event]]. 
SetEventHandlerAlt "OnHit" OnHitUDF
; Unfiltered, so OnHitUDF will run whenever the NVSE OnHit event is fired.

SetEventHandlerAlt "OnHit" OnHitUDF 1::Player
; The 1st arg is filtered, so OnHitUDF will only run if rTarget is the player.

SetEventHandlerAlt "OnHit" OnHitUDF 2::SunnyREF
; The 2nd arg is filtered, so OnHitUDF will only run if rAttacker is SunnyREF.

SetEventHandlerAlt "OnHit" OnHitUDF 1::Player 2::SunnyREF
; The 1st and 2nd args are filtered, so OnHitUDF will only run if rTarget is the player and rAttacker is SunnyREF.

SetEventHandlerAlt "OnHit" OnHitUDF 1::(Ar_List Player, SunnyREF)
; The 1st arg is filtered, so OnHitUDF will only run if rTarget is either the player or SunnyREF.

scn NVSELoadGameUDF
begin Function { string_var sSaveFileName }
    printvar sSaveFileName
    print "NVSELoadGameUDF >> RAN!"
end
The following code uses the [[NVSE Event Handling|LoadGame NVSE event]]. 
SetEventHandlerAlt "LoadGame" NVSELoadGameUDF 1::"Save 185   c69  Primm  01 54 17.fos"
; The 1st arg is filtered, so NVSELoadGameUDF will only run if sSaveFileName is equal to that string.
===Priority System (New in xNVSE 6.2.9)=== If unspecified, default priority while setting an event is 1.
Highest (greatest) priority means the handler will run first before other handlers, lowest means it will run last.
Handlers on the same priority will execute based on registration order. To debug priority conflicts, use [[IsEventHandlerFirst]] / [[IsEventHandlerLast]]. '''0''' is reserved as an '''invalid priority''', for use in other functions where we don't want to filter by priority.
It cannot be used for this function, as you must register a handler with a valid priority.
Besides that, any number, even a negative number, can be used as a priority. For more details, see [[Event Handler Priority System]]. Examples for how to specify priority: SetEventHandlerAlt "SomeEvent" SomeUDF "priority"::2 Will set an event handler with priority 2, which will be guaranteed to execute sooner than any handler that didn't specify a priority. The ''priority'' arg can be swapped around with the ''filter'' args. For example, these lines do the same thing: SetEventHandlerAlt "SomeEvent" SomeUDF "priority"::2 1::Player SetEventHandlerAlt "SomeEvent" SomeUDF 1::Player "priority"::2 }} ==See Also== *[[SetEventHandler]] - uses the old filtering system. *[[NVSE Event Handling]] *[[RemoveEventHandler]] - works with both the old and new filtering system. *[[GetCurrentEventName]] *[[DispatchEvent]] / [[DispatchEventAlt]] [[Category:Functions (NVSE)]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.2.8 |summary = Similar to [[Call]], but executes multiple Event Handler [[UDF]]s that were registered for the [[User-Defined Event]] of a certain name, assuming the filters for those event handlers match with the dispatched arguments. Can dispatch up to 15 arguments of any type (numeric, form, string, [[array]]), as well as passing an optional [[calling reference]] to invoke the [[UDF]]s with. Users can register to these custom events via [[SetEventHandler]] / [[SetEventHandlerAlt]], though [[SetEventHandlerAlt]] is more suited for this task, as it allows filters that dictate when the handler should run for each individual arg. |name = DispatchEventAlt |referenceType = callingRef |returnType = success:bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = eventName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = multi |Type = arg(up to 15) |Optional = y }} |example = player.DispatchEventAlt "MyCustomEvent" SunnyREF, (Ar_List 1, 2), "test", 2.5 If a user were to register to this event, their event handler [[UDF]] script would look like this: 
scn MyEventHandlerUDF
begin Function { ref rArg1, array_var aArg2, string_var aArg3, float fArg4 }
   ; do stuff
end
And they could register their event handler like so: SetEventHandlerAlt "MyCustomEvent" MyEventHandlerUDF Since that event handler is unfiltered, it will run for this dispatch. However, if it has filters, then it depends on if the filters match the provided args for the handler to run. For example, SetEventHandlerAlt "MyCustomEvent" MyEventHandlerUDF 1::SunnyREF Since the first arg dispatched is SunnyREF, and the filter for the 1st arg (1::) is SunnyREF, the filters match. However, if we had: SetEventHandlerAlt "MyCustomEvent" MyEventHandlerUDF 1::EasyPeteREF EasyPeteREF does not match with SunnyREF, so this registered event handler will not run for this particular dispatch. Though it may run for another one in the future, if the first arg dispatched becomes EasyPeteREF. More information on this filtering system is available on [[SetEventHandlerAlt]] and [[New xNVSE Event Filtering System]]. }} ==Notes== *The [[UDF]]s are not forced to have the same amount of arguments as the event dispatches; they may choose to receive less. This property allows events to increase the amount of args they pass without fearing to break existing event handlers that were set for that event. ==See Also== *[[SetEventHandlerAlt]] - can set an event handler with filters for each arg, and the calling reference. *[[SetEventHandler]] - uses the old filtering system, i.e won't support non-form filters, and can only have up to 2 filters. *[[RemoveEventHandler]] - works with both the old and new filtering system. *[[GetCurrentEventName]] *[[DispatchEvent]] - passes a StringMap of arguments, instead of any number of differently-typed arguments. [[Category:Functions (NVSE)]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.2.8 |summary = Returns an [[Inventory Reference]] of the currently sold item for an OnSell event.
Does NOT work inside the [[OnSell]] blocktype. |name = GetSoldItemInvRef |returnType = soldItem:reference |example = ref rSoldItemRef = GetSoldItemInvRef }}
==See Also== *[[NVSE Event Handling]] - provides more information on OnSell event.
[[Category:Functions (NVSE)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Reference Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.2.8 |summary = Calls UDF every couple of seconds, until the condition UDF returns false. Every queued call is removed when loading the game ([[GetGameLoaded]]), so they may need to be reset during then. *NOTE: This means that callbacks created using this function are '''NOT savebaked''', which could potentially cause bugs if a callback was expected to keep running after saving and reloading a save while the callback was running. ''flags'' is a [[bitmask]], whose bits toggle on/off the following features: 
PassArgsToTheCalledFunction    = 1 (bit #0)
PassArgsToTheConditionFunction = 2 (bit #1)
DontRunInMenuMode              = 4 (bit #2)
DontRunInGameMode              = 8 (bit #3)      
DontRunWhilePaused             = 16 (bit #4) - xNVSE 6.3.0
For example, if flags = 0, then none of these features are on. If flags = 0b11 ([[Binary Notation]], or 3 in decimal), then the first two are on.
By default, all flags are off (''flags'' = 0). ''DontRunWhilePaused'' means that the callback will be delayed while main menu / pause menu / console menu [[MenuMode]]s are open. Arguments can be passed to the function, just like [[Call]]. If an arg is an array, then its lifetime will be extended until the callbacks have stopped. If ''PassArgsToTheCalledFunction'' is on, then the [[Call]]-like args will be passed to the ''udf'', and if ''PassArgsToTheConditionFunction'' is on, they'll be passed to the ''condition'' UDF. Both flags can be on at the same time. |name = CallWhilePerSeconds |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = timeInterval |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = udf |Type = script }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = condition |Type = script }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flags |Type = int |Optional = y |Value = 0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = multi |Type = arg(up to 15) |Optional = y }} }} ==See Also== *[[CallWhile]] *[[CallWhen]] *[[CallForSeconds]] *[[CallAfterSeconds]] *[[CallAfterFrames]] *[[SetGameMainLoopCallback]] *[[Lambda]] *[[Inline Expression]] *[[Call]] [[Category:Functions (NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.2.8 |summary = Executes a [[UDF|User Defined Function]] or an inline [[Lambda|Lambda script]] after the specified amount of frames have elapsed in-game
Only runs in [[GameMode]] by default, or if ''runWhen'' is set to 0. If ''runWhen'' is set to 1, will '''also''' run in [[MenuMode]]. If it is set to 2, will '''also''' pause the callback while the main menu / pause menu / console menu MenuModes are open ''(new in 6.3.0)''.
Every queued call is removed when loading the game ([[GetGameLoaded]]), so they may need to be reset during then.
Arguments can be passed like [[Call]] to the function. If an arg is an array, then its lifetime will be extended until the callbacks have stopped.
|name = CallAfterFrames |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = frames |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = udf |Type = script }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = runWhen |Type = int |Optional = y |Value = 0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = multi |Type = arg(up to 15) |Optional = y }} }} ==See Also== *[[Call]] *[[CallAfterSeconds]] *[[CallForSeconds]] *[[CallWhen]] *[[CallWhile]] *[[CallLoop]] *[[Inline Expression]] *[[Lambda]] *[[SetGameMainLoopCallback]]
[[Category:Functions (NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.2.9 |summary = Tests whether the specified [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Regular_expression Regular Expression] pattern is valid or not. This is only useful to test regex that is dynamically retrieved from a file or other dynamic source. If the regex is known beforehand, consider using an online validator. Possible return values are listed below: 
Error_FailedToExtractArgs = -1,
NoErrors = 0,

Error_Collate = 1,
Error_Ctype = 2,
Error_Escape = 3,
Error_Backref = 4,
Error_Brack = 5,
Error_Paren = 6,
Error_Brace = 7,
Error_Badbrace = 8,
Error_Range = 9,
Error_Space  10,
Error_Badrepeat = 11,
Error_Complexity = 12,
Error_Stack = 13,
Error_Unknown = 14
If an error occurs and ''outErrorStrVar'' is set to a valid string variable, then that string variable will be set to contain information explaining the nature of the error. |name = ValidateRegex |returnType = match:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = regEx |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = outErrorStrVar |Type = string_var |Optional = Y }} |example = 
string_var sOutError = ""
int iError = ValidateRegex "[" sOutError

printvar iError
; Will print "5" (Error_Brack)

printvar sOutError 
; Will print a string explaining that the error is caused by mismatched brackets (missing closing bracket).
}} ==See Also== *[[sv_RegexMatch]] *[[sv_RegexSearch]] *[[sv_RegexReplace]] *To test Regex expressions, consider using this website: [https://regex101.com/ Regex101] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]] [[Category:Functions (NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.2.9 |summary = Converts a specified integer to a binary string, optionally padded to the specified width. If the specified width is smaller than the minimum width to represent the number, will use the minimum width. The maximum width is 32 bits. If ''AddPrefix'' is set to true (non-zero), will prepend "0b" to the string. |name = IntToBin |returnType = string |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ToConvert |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = WidthToPadTo |Type = int |Optional = y |Value = 32 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = AddPrefix |Type = bool |Optional = y |Value = 0 }} }} ==Example== 
string_var sBinary = IntToBin 42, 1
; sBinary  == "0b101010"
==See Also== *[[IntToHex]] *[[String Variable]] *[[ToNumber|ToNumber(#)]] *[[ToString|ToString($)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.2.9 |summary = Returns an array containing all event handlers matching the filter args (or all event handlers, if unfiltered). Only includes event handlers registered using [[SetEventHandler]] / [[SetEventHandlerAlt]], or are generally managed by [[NVSE]]. *Does NOT include the LN event handlers, such as [[OnCellEnter]], as those handlers aren't managed by NVSE. *Events such as [[SetOnHitEventHandler]] are not managed by NVSE. If '''not''' filtering by event name, will return a [[StringMap]], where the keys are the event names and the values are arrays of handlers (potentially sorted by priority).
Otherwise, filtering by event name will just return the array of handlers for that event. '''An array of handlers sorted by priority''' is a [[Map Array]], where the keys are priority values and the values are arrays of handlers for that priority.
If a priority filter is used, then this array is skipped, and the array of handlers for the priority is used directly instead. An '''array of handlers''' is a regular array containing an array for each handler. A '''handler array''' contains: *Index [0]: Either a script handler, or an array representation of a plugin handler. **The latter is a [[StringMap]] containing the plugin name at ["Plugin"] and the handler name at ["Handler"]. These strings may be empty. *Index [1]: A [[StringMap]] of all the filters used by the handler. |name = GetEventHandlers |returnType = array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = eventNameFilter |Type = string |Optional = y |Value = "" }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerFilter |Type = [[UDF]] |Optional = y |Value = 0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = priorityFilter |Type = int |Optional = y |Value = 0 }} }} ==See Also== *[[DumpEventHandlers]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.2.9 |summary = Dumps all event handlers matching the filter args (or dumps all event handlers, if unfiltered).
<u>Only includes event handlers registered using [[SetEventHandler]] / [[SetEventHandlerAlt]], or are generally managed by [[NVSE]].</u> *Does NOT include the LN event handlers, such as [[OnCellEnter]], as those handlers aren't managed by NVSE. *Events such as [[SetOnHitEventHandler]] are not managed by NVSE.
Event handlers will be listed according to the event they belong to and the [[Event Handler Priority System|priority]] they belong to.
Includes event handlers registered from inside [[NVSE]] plugins.
|name = DumpEventHandlers |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = eventNameFilter |Type = string |Optional = y |Value = "" }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerFilter |Type = [[UDF]] |Optional = y |Value = 0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = priorityFilter |Type = int |Optional = y |Value = 0 }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetEventHandlers]] - Same information in [[Array]] form.
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.2.9 |summary = Returns a [[Map Array]] with all the event handlers that have a '''higher or equal''' priority than the specified ''handler'' at ''startPriority''. This is for debugging priority conflicts between handlers.
Each key in the map is a priority, and each value is an array representing a handler.
A '''handler array''' is a regular array containing: *Index [0]: Either a script handler, or an array representation of a plugin handler. **The latter is a [[StringMap]] containing the plugin name at ["Plugin"] and the handler name at ["Handler"]. <u>These strings may be empty.</u> *Index [1]: A [[StringMap]] of all the filters used by the handler.
If the ''handler'' could not be found at ''startPriority'', returns [[Ar_Null]].
<u>Only includes event handlers registered using [[SetEventHandler]] / [[SetEventHandlerAlt]], or are generally managed by [[NVSE]].</u> *Does NOT include the LN event handlers, such as [[OnCellEnter]], as those handlers aren't managed by NVSE. *Events such as [[SetOnHitEventHandler]] are not managed by NVSE.
|name = GetHigherPriorityEventHandlers |returnType = array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = eventName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = handler |Type = [[UDF]] }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = startPriority |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = scriptsToIgnore |Type = scriptOrFormlist |Optional = y |Value = 0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = pluginsToIgnore |Type = array |Optional = y |Value = [[Ar_Null]] }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = pluginHandlersToIgnore |Type = array |Optional = y |Value = [[Ar_Null]] }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetLowerPriorityEventHandlers]] *[[GetEventHandlers]] *[[DumpEventHandlers]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.2.9 |summary = Returns a [[Map Array]] with all the event handlers that have a '''lower or equal''' priority than the specified ''handler'' at ''startPriority''. This is for debugging priority conflicts between handlers.
Each key in the map is a priority, and each value is an array representing a handler.
A '''handler array''' is a regular array containing: *Index [0]: Either a script handler, or an array representation of a plugin handler. **The latter is a [[StringMap]] containing the plugin name at ["Plugin"] and the handler name at ["Handler"]. <u>These strings may be empty.</u> *Index [1]: A [[StringMap]] of all the filters used by the handler.
If the ''handler'' could not be found at ''startPriority'', returns [[Ar_Null]].
<u>Only includes event handlers registered using [[SetEventHandler]] / [[SetEventHandlerAlt]], or are generally managed by [[NVSE]].</u> *Does NOT include the LN event handlers, such as [[OnCellEnter]], as those handlers aren't managed by NVSE. *Events such as [[SetOnHitEventHandler]] are not managed by NVSE.
|name = GetLowerPriorityEventHandlers |returnType = array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = eventName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = handler |Type = [[UDF]] }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = startPriority |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = scriptsToIgnore |Type = scriptOrFormlist |Optional = y |Value = 0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = pluginsToIgnore |Type = array |Optional = y |Value = [[Ar_Null]] }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = pluginHandlersToIgnore |Type = array |Optional = y |Value = [[Ar_Null]] }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetHigherPriorityEventHandlers]] *[[GetEventHandlers]] *[[DumpEventHandlers]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.2.9 |summary = Returns 1 (true) if there are no event handlers at a '''higher or equal''' priority than the specified ''handler'' at ''startPriority''. This is for debugging priority conflicts between handlers.
If the ''handler'' could not be found at ''startPriority'', returns 0 (false).
<u>Only accounts for event handlers registered using [[SetEventHandler]] / [[SetEventHandlerAlt]], or are generally managed by [[NVSE]].</u> *Does NOT include the LN event handlers, such as [[OnCellEnter]], as those handlers aren't managed by NVSE. *Events such as [[SetOnHitEventHandler]] are not managed by NVSE.
|name = IsEventHandlerLast |returnType = bool |returnVal = isLast |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = eventName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = handler |Type = [[UDF]] }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = startPriority |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = scriptsToIgnore |Type = scriptOrFormlist |Optional = y |Value = 0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = pluginsToIgnore |Type = array |Optional = y |Value = [[Ar_Null]] }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = pluginHandlersToIgnore |Type = array |Optional = y |Value = [[Ar_Null]] }} }} ==See Also== *[[IsEventHandlerFirst]] *[[GetHigherPriorityEventHandlers]] / [[GetLowerPriorityEventHandlers]] *[[GetEventHandlers]] *[[DumpEventHandlers]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.2.9 |summary = Returns 1 (true) if there are no event handlers at a '''lower or equal''' priority than the specified ''handler'' at ''startPriority''. This is for debugging priority conflicts between handlers.
If the ''handler'' could not be found at ''startPriority'', returns 0 (false).
<u>Only accounts for event handlers registered using [[SetEventHandler]] / [[SetEventHandlerAlt]], or are generally managed by [[NVSE]].</u> *Does NOT include the LN event handlers, such as [[OnCellEnter]], as those handlers aren't managed by NVSE. *Events such as [[SetOnHitEventHandler]] are not managed by NVSE.
|name = IsEventHandlerFirst |returnType = bool |returnVal = isFirst |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = eventName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = handler |Type = [[UDF]] }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = startPriority |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = scriptsToIgnore |Type = scriptOrFormlist |Optional = y |Value = 0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = pluginsToIgnore |Type = array |Optional = y |Value = [[Ar_Null]] }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = pluginHandlersToIgnore |Type = array |Optional = y |Value = [[Ar_Null]] }} }} ==See Also== *[[IsEventHandlerLast]] *[[GetHigherPriorityEventHandlers]] / [[GetLowerPriorityEventHandlers]] *[[GetEventHandlers]] *[[DumpEventHandlers]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Event Handlers]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.3.0 |summary = Returns 1 if a specific [[ammo]] is currently equipped by the [[calling reference|calling actor reference]], 0 otherwise. |name = HasAmmoEquipped |alias = IsAmmoEquipped |conditionFunc = Both |returnType = 0/1 |referenceType = actorRefr |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Ammo |Type = form }} }} =='''See Also'''== *[[GetCurrentAmmo]] *[[IsEquippedAmmoInList]] *[[GetEquippedWeaponCanUseAmmo]] *[[GetEquippedWeaponUsesAmmoList]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Ammo Functions]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.3.0 |summary = Returns 1 if the [[calling reference|calling actor reference]]'s equipped weapon can use the specified [[ammo]], 0 otherwise. |name = GetEquippedWeaponCanUseAmmo |conditionFunc = Both |returnType = 0/1 |referenceType = actorRefr |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Ammo |Type = form }} }} =='''See Also'''== *[[GetWeaponCanUseAmmo]] *[[GetEquippedWeaponUsesAmmoList]] *[[HasAmmoEquipped]] *[[IsEquippedAmmoInList]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Ammo Functions]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.3.0 |summary = Returns 1 if the currently equipped [[ammo]] of the [[calling reference|calling actor reference]] is inside the..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.3.0 |summary = Returns 1 if the currently equipped [[ammo]] of the [[calling reference|calling actor reference]] is inside the specified [[FormList]], 0 otherwise. |name = IsEquippedAmmoInList |conditionFunc = Both |returnType = 0/1 |referenceType = actorRefr |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ammoList |Type = formList }} }} =='''See Also'''== *[[IsAmmoEquipped]] *[[GetEquippedWeaponCanUseAmmo]] *[[GetEquippedWeaponUsesAmmoList]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Ammo Functions]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.3.0 |summary = Returns 1 if the [[calling reference|calling actor reference]]'s equipped weapon uses the specified [[ammo]] [[FormList]], 0 otherwise. |name = GetEquippedWeaponUsesAmmoList |conditionFunc = Both |returnType = 0/1 |referenceType = actorRefr |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ammoList |Type = formList }} }} =='''See Also'''== *[[GetEquippedWeaponCanUseAmmo]] *[[HasAmmoEquipped]] *[[IsEquippedAmmoInList]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Ammo Functions]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.3.0 |summary = Returns true (1) if the [[calling reference]] is an [[Inventory Reference]], false (0) otherwise. |name = IsInventoryRef |returnVal = isInvRef |returnType = bool |referenceType = reference |arguments = |example = }}
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Reference Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.3.0 |summary = As [[PrintVar]], but outputs to console only if the calling mod has [[SetDebugMode|debug mode enabled]]. Primary use case is debugging while scripting. |name = DebugPrintVar |alias = DPrintV |returnType = none |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = variableName |Type = variable }} }} ==See Also== *[[PrintVar]] *[[SetDebugMode]] *[[GetDebugMode]] *[[Print]] *[[PrintDebug]] *[[PrintF]] *[[Con_SCOF]] *[[Debug Dumps]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Debugging]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.3.1 |summary = Returns the X angle between the calling reference and the specified object in a range from -180 to 180 degrees. Unlike [[GetHeadingAngle]], this can be called on non-actor references. |name = GetHeadingAngleX |returnVal = -180 to 180 |returnType = float |referenceType = callingReference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = target |Type = reference }} |example = 
    ;* This code makes the player face SunnyREF (assuming they're nearby)
    ref rRefToFace = SunnyREF
    ref rRefToChangeAngle = Player

    float fNewXAngle = rRefToChangeAngle.GetHeadingAngleX rRefToFace 
    fNewXAngle += rRefToChangeAngle.GetAngle X

    float fNewZAngle = rRefToChangeAngle.GetAngle Z + rRefToChangeAngle.GetHeadingAngle rRefToFace

    rRefToChangeAngle.SetAngle Z fNewZAngle
    rRefToChangeAngle.SetAngle X fNewXAngle
}} ==Notes== * Here is an alternate method to make the player face a ref, using [[V3Length]] and [[fATan2]], provided by Xilandro: 
    float deltaX = (Player.GetPos X) - (rRefToFace.GetPos X)
    float deltaY = (Player.GetPos Y) - (rRefToFace.GetPos Y)
    float deltaZ = (Player.GetPos Z) - (rRefToFace.GetPos Z)

    float vLen = V3Length deltaX deltaY 0

    float newX = fATan2 deltaZ vLen
    Player.SetAngle X newX 

    float newZAngle = Player.GetAngle Z + Player.GetHeadingAngle rRefToFace
    Player.SetAngle Z newZAngle
==See Also== *[[GetHeadingAngle]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category: Movement Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.3.1 |summary = Changes the percentage chance of providing an empty case item on use for the specified [[ammo]] or calling ref..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.3.1 |summary = Changes the percentage chance of providing an empty case item on use for the specified [[ammo]] or [[calling reference]] of an ammo item. |name = SetAmmoConsumedPercent |referenceType = ammoReference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = newPercent |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = ammo |Type = baseForm |Optional = y }} |example = SetAmmoConsumedPercent 2.5 Ammo308 }} =='''See Also'''== *[[GetAmmoConsumedPercent]] *[[SetAmmoTraitNumeric]] can also be used to set the consumed percent *[[GetAmmoCasing]] *[[GetWeaponAmmo]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Ammo Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.3.1 |summary = Returns 1 if the [[weapon]] [[BaseForm]] can use the specified [[ammo]] baseform, 0 otherwise. If a ''weaponRefe..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.3.1 |summary = Returns 1 if the [[weapon]] [[BaseForm]] can use the specified [[ammo]] baseform, 0 otherwise. If a ''weaponReference'' is passed instead of a baseForm, its baseform is used instead. |name = GetWeaponCanUseAmmo |conditionFunc = Both |returnType = 0/1 |referenceType = weaponReference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = baseForm }} }} =='''See Also'''== *[[GetEquippedWeaponCanUseAmmo]] *[[GetEquippedWeaponUsesAmmoList]] *[[HasAmmoEquipped]] *[[IsEquippedAmmoInList]]
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Ammo Functions]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.2.7 |summary = A debugging function to make test assertions, usually to verify if a function is properly functioning so as to c..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.2.7 |summary = A debugging function to make test assertions, usually to verify if a function is properly functioning so as to catch bugs and regressions in code. If ''assertion'' is false, will print an error message and show the script line that isn't working in console. |name = Assert |returnType = none |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = assertion |Type = bool }} |example = 
 ;* From xNVSE's unit tests: test if ar_Erase properly erases all entries
 array_var aVar = ar_list 1 2 3 4
 ar_Erase aVar
 Assert (ar_Size aVar) == 0
}} ==See Also== *[[PrintVar]] / [[DebugPrintVar]] *[[Print]] *[[PrintDebug]] *[[PrintF]] *[[Con_SCOF]] *[[Debug Dumps]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Debugging]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.3.3 |summary = Copies an [[inventory reference]] to the specified container. The calling object needn't be in a container and r..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.3.3 |summary = Copies an [[inventory reference]] to the specified container. The calling object needn't be in a container and remains valid after the command is called. If the calling object is equipped, the copy will not be equipped.
Returns the newly copied inventory reference if it succeeds.
|name = CopyIRAlt |referenceType = InvRef |returnType = copiedInvRef:reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Location |Type = Container }} |example = ref rCopiedInvRef = someInvRef.CopyIRAlt PlayerREF }} ==See Also== *[[RemoveMeIR]] *[[CopyIR]] - Same as this but returns 1/0 for success
[[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category: Object Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Reference Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.3.3 |summary = Returns a compiled [[script]] from a text file pointed by the ''path'' arg. The ''path'' arg is relative to '''"data/nvse/user_defined_functions/.."'''. If it was formatted like a [[UDF]], then the returned script can be used like a regular UDF, meaning it can be passed to functions like [[Call]]. *This makes it a great way to store long lines of code that wouldn't fit in a [[Lambda]] due to the line character limit. **Of course, it's only useful if you're running scripts without wanting to use an esp/esm file that could store an actual UDF. The compiled script is cached for performance, so it won't be recompiled after the first call.
To "hot-reload" and recompile the script to account for mid-game changes, likely for testing purposes, set ''forceRecompile'' to 1. |name = CompileScript |alias = GetUDFFromFile |returnType = script:form |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = path |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = forceRecompile |Type = bool |Optional = y |Value = 0 }} }} ==Example== Here is what my example script text file, "TestReturnRefUDF.txt" contains: 
begin Function { ref rRefToReturn }

    print "Test UDF ran!"
    printvar rRefToReturn

end
This text file is placed at this path: "data/nvse/user_defined_functions/SomeSubFolder/TestReturnRefUDF.txt" To compile this text file UDF and test it, this can be done in script: 
ref rUDF = CompileScript "SomeSubFolder/TestReturnRefUDF.txt"
printvar rUDF
call rUDF SunnyREF  ; should see a SunnyREF print in console
Note: If "Begin Function block not found in compiled script data" is output in the console as an error but your function still compiles, check to make sure you have not used ''scn YourScriptNameHere'' for any function you are using with CompileScript, as ''scn'' should not be used inside a [[UDF]]. ==See Also== *[[DecompileScript]] *[[RunScriptSnippet]] *[[RunBatchScript]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Script Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.3.6 |summary = Dumps GECK Wiki-style documentation for the specified command to '''nvse.log'''. Useful for documenting new func..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.3.6 |summary = Dumps GECK Wiki-style documentation for the specified command to '''nvse.log'''. Useful for documenting new functions. Note that for certain plugins, the '''origin''' name and '''Category:Functions (?)''' category name may be incorrect due to naming scheme inconsistencies. In those cases, create an issue for xNVSE on github so that the correct strings may be hardcoded. |name = DumpCommandWikiDoc |alias = DumpWikiDoc |conditionFunc = Script |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = commandName |Type = string }} }} ==Example== *From console: DumpWikiDoc "Ar_Cat" Resulting text in nvse.log: 
{{Function
 |origin = JIP
 |originVersion = [TO SPECIFY]
 |summary = [TO DOCUMENT]
 |name = ar_Cat
 |alias = pinto_Cat
 |returnVal = [Insert return val name here, or remove this arg]
 |returnType = Float [remove this arg if func returns nothing]
 |conditionFunc = Script
 |arguments =
  {{FunctionArgument
   |Name = [Enter Arg Name]
   |Type = Integer
  }}{{FunctionArgument
   |Name = [Enter Arg Name]
   |Type = Integer
  }}{{FunctionArgument
   |Name = [Enter Arg Name]
   |Type = Integer
   |Optional = y
  }}
}}
[[Category:Functions (JIP)]]
[[Category:? [Add other relevant categories] ]]
[[Category:Functions (NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.3.6 |summary = Dumps out all specified registered script functions in a detailed format, or a simpler format if ''showQuickList'' is set to 1.
Useful for collecting information on functions, for example for making geck script syntax highlighters.
Docs are dumped to '''nvse.log'''.
By default, ''startingOpcode'' is set to 5120 to only include functions from [[NVSE]] and NVSE plugins. Set this arg to 0 to get functions from the vanilla game as well. |name = DumpDocs |conditionFunc = Script |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = showQuickList |Type = 1/0 |Optional = y |Value = 0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = startingOpcode |Type = int |Optional = y |Value = 5120 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = showIfConditionOnly |Type = 1/0 |Optional = y |Value = 0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = showIfDeprecated |Type = 1/0 |Optional = y |Value = 0 }} }} [[Category:Functions (NVSE)]] [[Category:Debugging]]

revision.comment: Undo revision 38462 by [[Special:Contributions/Stentorious|Stentorious]] ([[User talk:Stentorious|talk]])
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = NVSE |summary = Sets the item associated with a hotkey. Specified item form must already be present in player's inventory. |name = SetHotkeyItem |returnType = none |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = WhichHotKey |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = Item |Type = ref }} |referenceType = }} ==Example== 
ref rOldHotkey1

SetHotkeyItem 1, rOldHotKey1
==See Also== *[[SetHotkey]] / [[SetHotkeyItemRef]] *[[GetHotkeyItem]] / [[GetHotkeyItemRef]] *[[SaveHotkeys]] + [[RestoreHotkeys]] *[[ClearAllHotkeys]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Hotkey Functions]]

{{Function |origin = NVSE |originVersion = 6.4.5 |summary = Returns an [[array_var]] containing all [[Template:DirectX_Scancodes|DirectX Key Scancodes]] currently disabled by [[DisableKey]] or [[DisableControl]]. |returnType = array_var |example = let aScancodes := GetDisabledKeys }}
==See Also== *[[DisableKey]] *[[EnableKey]] *[[DisableControl]] *[[EnableControl]] *[[IsControlPressed]] *[[IsKeyDisabled]] *[[IsKeyPressed]] [[Category:Functions_(NVSE)]] [[Category:Input Functions]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |summary = Modifies a numeric [[Game Setting]] by the given value.
Returns 1 for a successful change, and 0 on a failed one. For example, it may fail if the GameSetting could not be found. |name = ModNumericGameSetting |returnType = success:bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = settingName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = valueModifier |Type = float }} |example = ModNumericGameSetting "fAbsorbBoltGrowWidth" 2 Will increment "fAbsorbBoltGrowWidth" by 2 ModNumericGameSetting "fAbsorbBoltGrowWidth" -5 Will decrease "fAbsorbBoltGrowWidth" by 5 }} =='''Notes'''== *The compiler will not catch typing errors for the ''settingName'' string, so take care to type accurately.
=='''See Also'''== *[[GetNumericGameSetting]] / [[SetNumericGameSetting]] *[[Con_SetGameSetting]] *[[GetStringSetting]] / [[SetStringSetting]] *[[GetNumericINISetting]] / [[SetNumericINISetting]] / [[ModNumericINISetting]]
[[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:GameSetting Functions]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |summary = Modifies a numeric ini setting from Fallout.ini / FalloutPrefs.ini by the given value, using the same format for the setting name as [[GetNumericIniSetting]].
Returns 1 for a successful change, and 0 on a failed one. For example, it may fail if the INI setting could not be found. |name = ModNumericINISetting |returnType = success:bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = settingName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = valueModifier |Type = float }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetNumericINISetting]] / [[SetNumericINISetting]] *[[GetNumericGameSetting]] / [[SetNumericGameSetting]]
[[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:INI Functions]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |summary = Sets on/off the ability to pickpocket equipped items. |name = SetPlayerCanPickpocketEquippedItems |alias = SetPCCanStealEqItems |returnType = none |referenceType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument | Name = bOn | Type = 0/1 }} |example = SetPlayerCanPickpocketEquippedItems 1 The player will now be able to pickpocket equipped items. }} ==Notes== *By default, the player cannot pickpocket equipped items (unless `bPickpocketWornItems` from [[lStewieAl's Tweaks]] is toggled on). *Child actors still cannot have their equipped items stolen.
==See Also== *[[GetPlayerCanPickpocketEquippedItems]]
[[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

revision.comment: Added to Category:Condition Functions
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = ShowOff |summary = Returns 1 if the player can pickpocket equipped items, 0 otherwise. |name = GetPlayerCanPickpocketEquippedItems |alias = GetPCCanStealEqItems |returnType = bool |example = GetPlayerCanPickpocketEquippedItems }} ==Notes== *By default, the player cannot pickpocket equipped items, unless they have `bPickpocketWornItems` enabled in [[lStewieAl's Tweaks]]. *To avoid conflicts with [[lStewieAl's Tweaks]], most notably its `bPickpocketWornItems` option, this function decides whether or not the player can pickpocket equipped items by checking if ANY call hook has been made to one of the game's internal functions. This means that if another plugin also modifies that address, this function could break.
==See Also== *[[SetPlayerCanPickpocketEquippedItems]]
[[Category: Player Functions]] [[Category: Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |summary = Returns 1 if the player has a flag preventing them from sleeping/waiting, 0 otherwise. |name = GetPCHasSleepWaitOverride |returnType = bool |example = 
short bHasSleepWaitOverride
let bHasSleepWaitOverride := GetPCHasSleepWaitOverride
}} ==Notes== *[[EnableFastTravel]] can modify this "canSleepWait" override flag. *[[GetCellFlag]] - cells have their own separate flag that prevents the player from sleeping/waiting while inside them. ==See Also== *[[SetPCHasSleepWaitOverride]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |summary = If set to 1, sets a flag that prevents the player from sleeping/waiting. Setting it to 0 removes this flag. |name = SetPCHasSleepWaitOverride |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = bSetSleepWaitOverrideOn |Value = 0,1 }} |example = SetPCHasSleepWaitOverride 1 Will prevent the player from sleeping/waiting. }} ==Notes== *[[EnableFastTravel]] modifies the same "canSleepWait" flag. *By default, the prevention message refers to not being able to wait in the cell. *This prevention message is controlled by the [[sNoWaitInCell]] gamesetting. *This flag is save-baked.
==See Also== *[[GetPCHasSleepWaitOverride]]
[[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |summary = Returns the amount of actors that are under or at a certain distance (in game [[Units]]) from the calling reference. The type of actor to be counted can be optionally filtered via the '''flagsBitMask''' argument (bit values are written under '''Notes'''). |name = GetNumActorsInRangeFromRef |returnType = numActors:int |referenceType = reference |conditionFunc = Both |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = maxDistance |Type = float }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagsBitMask |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = int iNumActors let iNumActors := player.GetNumActorsInRangeFromRef 500 Will return the number of actors that are in 500 units distance from the player. Equivalent to `player.GetNumActorsInRangeFromRef 500 0`. let iNumActors := player.GetNumActorsInRangeFromRef 500 1 Same as above, except that the amount of actors is further filtered by only counting alive actors. let iNumActors := player.GetNumActorsInRangeFromRef 500 3 Returns the amount of actors that are alive, not invisible, and in 500 units distance from the player.
Thanks to [[Inline Expression]]s, this could be more clearly written as: 
let iNumActors := player.GetNumActorsInRangeFromRef 500 (1 << 0 | 1 << 1)
The above notation makes it clear that bit 0 and 1 (flag 1 and 2) are enabled. }} ==Notes== '''flagsBitMask''' works with the following values: {| class="wikitable" |- ! Flags ! Bit ! Decimal Value
|- | No dead actors | 0 | 1 |- | No invisible actors | 1 | 2 |- | Only detected actors | 2 | 4 |} *''Only detected actors'' was added in version 1.25. With it toggled, only combat actors that ''actorRefr'' can [[GetDetected|detect]] are counted (further filters still apply). *''No invisible actors'' works by checking if either the actor's Invisibility or Chameleon [[actor value]]s are greater than 0. ==See Also== *[[GetDistance3D]] *[[GetNumCombatActorsFromActor]] *[[GetActorsByProcessingLevel]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |summary = Returns the amount of in-combat actors that are allies/enemies to the calling actor reference. If the first argument (''maxDistance'') is omitted, it defaults to 0. If set to a number above 0, the distance (in game [[Units]]) between the calling actor and each combat actor is checked; if the distance is smaller or equal to ''maxDistance'', the combat actor can make its way into the resulting ''numActors'' count. The type of actor to be counted can be optionally filtered via the ''flagsBitMask'' argument (bit values are written under '''Notes''').
If ''flagsBitMask'' is omitted, it defaults to 0. If left at 0, ''flagsBitMask'' is internally set to 3, meaning both combat allies and targets are counted. |name = GetNumCombatActorsFromActor |returnType = numActors:int |referenceType = actorRefr |conditionFunc = Both |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = maxDistance |Type = float |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagsBitMask |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = 
int iNumActors

let iNumActors := player.GetNumCombatActorsFromActor
Will return the number of actors that are targeting or allied to the player. Equivalent to `player.GetNumCombatActorsFromActor 0 0`. let iNumActors := player.GetNumCombatActorsFromActor 500 Same as above, except that the amount of actors is further filtered by checking if the distance between each actor to the player is less or equal to 500. let iNumActors := SunnyREF.GetNumCombatActorsFromActor 0 6 Returns the amount of actors that are not invisible and are combat targets to SunnyREF. }} ==Notes== '''flagsBitMask''' works with the following values: {| class="wikitable" |- ! Flags ! Bit ! Decimal Value |- | Include combat allies | 0 | 1 |- | Include combat targets | 1 | 2 |- | No invisible actors | 2 | 4 |- | Only detected actors | 3 | 8 |} *''Only detected actors'' was added in version 1.25. With it toggled, only combat actors that ''actorRefr'' can [[GetDetected|detect]] are counted (further filters still apply). *''No invisible actors'' works by checking if either the actor's Invisibility or Chameleon [[actor value]]s are greater than 0. ==See Also== *[[GetDistance3D]] *[[GetNumActorsInRangeFromRef]] *[[GetCombatTargets]] *[[GetCombatAllies]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |summary = A convenience function that returns true if the [[weapon]]'s [[BaseForm]] is of melee type. Can be used as a condition. If the ''weapon:baseForm'' argument is omitted, the function instead checks the weapon [[reference]]'s base form. |name = IsWeaponMelee |returnType = isMelee:bool |referenceType = weapRefr |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = baseForm |Optional = y }} |example = 
short bIsMelee

let bIsMelee := IsWeaponMelee BMWeapNailBoard
Returns true, since BMWeapNailBoard's weapon type is "TwoHandMelee". }} ==Notes== *Internally, this function does nothing more than check if the weapon's type is <= 2. The same result could be achieved with [[GetWeaponType]]. ==See Also== *[[GetWeaponType]] *[[IsEquippedWeaponMelee]] *[[IsWeaponRanged]] / [[IsEquippedWeaponRanged]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |summary = A convenience function that returns true if the [[weapon]]'s [[BaseForm]] is of ranged (non-melee) type. Can be used as a condition. If the ''weapon:baseForm'' argument is omitted, the function instead checks the weapon [[reference]]'s base form. |name = IsWeaponRanged |returnType = isRanged:bool |referenceType = weapRefr |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = weapon |Type = baseForm |Optional = y }} |example = 
short bIsRanged

let bIsRanged := IsWeaponRanged BMWeapNailBoard
Returns false, since BMWeapNailBoard's weapon type is "TwoHandMelee". let bIsRanged := IsWeaponRanged EDEZapGun Returns true, since EDEZapGun's weapon type is "OneHandPistol". }} ==Notes== *Internally, this function does nothing more than check if the weapon's type is >= 3 && <= 13. The same result could be achieved with [[GetWeaponType]]. ==See Also== *[[GetWeaponType]] *[[IsWeaponMelee]] / [[IsEquippedWeaponMelee]] *[[IsEquippedWeaponRanged]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |summary = A convenience function that returns true if the actor's currently equipped [[weapon]] is of melee type. If no weapon is equipped, the function defaults to 1 (for default fists). |name = IsEquippedWeaponMelee |conditionFunc = Both |returnType = isMelee:bool |referenceType = actorRefr |arguments = |example = 
short bIsMelee

let bIsMelee := player.IsEquippedWeaponMelee
Will return true if the player has a melee weapon equipped. }} ==Notes== *Internally, this function does nothing more than check if the [[GetWeaponType|weapon's type]] is <= 2. *Not recommended to use this function for certain perk effects, like Calculate Weap. Damage, since it will affect the DAM preview that appears in the UI for other non-equipped weapons. ==See Also== *[[GetWeaponType]] *[[IsWeaponMelee]] *[[IsWeaponRanged]] / [[IsEquippedWeaponRanged]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |summary = A convenience function that returns true if the actor's currently equipped [[weapon]] is of Ranged type. If no weapon is equipped, the function defaults to 0. |name = IsEquippedWeaponRanged |conditionFunc = Both |returnType = isRanged:bool |referenceType = actorRefr |arguments = |example = 
short bIsRanged

let bIsRanged := player.IsEquippedWeaponRanged
Will return true if the player has a Ranged weapon equipped. Will return false if no weapon is equipped / a melee weapon is equipped. }} ==Notes== *Internally, this function does nothing more than check if the [[GetWeaponType|weapon's type]] is >= 3 && <= 13. *Not recommended to use this function for certain perk effects, like Calculate Weap. Damage, since it will affect the DAM preview that appears in the UI for other non-equipped weapons. ==See Also== *[[GetWeaponType]] *[[IsWeaponMelee]] / [[IsEquippedWeaponMelee]] *[[IsWeaponRanged]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |summary = Returns the progress the player has made on a certain challenge. Available as a condition. |name = GetChallengeProgress |returnType = progress:int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = challenge |Type = baseForm }} |example = 
int iProgress
let iProgress := GetChallengeProgress NVChallengeAbom
}} ==Notes== *If the challenge is beaten... **...and is NOT set to "Recurring", will return the max Threshold value. **...and is set to "Recurring", will return to 0. Afterwards, will increment as normal. ==See Also== *[[GetChallengeThreshold]] *[[GetChallengeCompleted]] [[Category:Challenge Functions]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |summary = Returns 1 and prints a message to console. Meant for use as a debugging [[condition]], in order to see when a condition is evaluated and also check if previous conditions passed.
The printed message includes information about the reference which is passed by the game to the condition function, such as its refID and editorID. The latter only shows up if an editorID was set for the reference and if [[JohnnyGuitar NVSE]]'s bLoadEditorIDs is set to 1. |name = ConditionPrint |returnType = 1 }} ==Notes== *This function should not be included into a mod's finished build, seeing as conditions are usually evaluated every frame. Otherwise, it could result in a lot of console spam.
==See Also== *[[Print]] *[[PrintDebug]] / [[DebugPrint]]
[[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Debugging]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |summary = Returns the amount of corner messages (those with icons that display on the top-left corner) that are in queue. This includes the currently displaying corner message. |name = GetNumQueuedCornerMessages |returnType = numQueuedMsg:int }} ==See Also== *[[ClearMessageQueue]] *[[ShowMessage]] *[[MessageEx]] / [[MessageExAlt]] *[[MessageBoxEx]] / [[MessageBoxExAlt]]
[[Category:Message Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |summary = Returns the Turning Speed of a '''[[Creature]]''' [[BaseForm]] or [[reference]].
Returns -1 if the form was not a valid creature. |name = GetCreatureTurningSpeed |returnType = turningSpeed:float |referenceType = creatureRefr |conditionFunc = Both |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = creatureBase |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = set fTurningSpeed to rCreatureRef.GetCreatureTurningSpeed set fTurningSpeed to GetCreatureTurningSpeed CrCentaurEvolved }} ==Notes== *This was made available as a condition function in version 1.25. ==See Also== *[[SetCreatureTurningSpeed]] *[[GetIsCreatureType]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Creature Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |summary = Returns the Foot Weight of a '''[[Creature]]''' [[BaseForm]]. If ''creatureRefr'' is passed, its BaseForm is checked.
Returns -1 if the form was not a valid creature. |name = GetCreatureFootWeight |returnType = footWeight:float |referenceType = creatureRefr |conditionFunc = Both |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = creatureBase |Type = form |Optional = y }} |example = set fFootWeight to rCreatureRef.GetCreatureFootWeight set fFootWeight to GetCreatureFootWeight CrCentaurEvolved }} ==Notes== *This was made available as a condition function in version 1.25. ==See Also== *[[SetCreatureFootWeight]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Creature Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |summary = Sets the melee Attack Reach of a '''[[Creature]]''' [[BaseForm]] or [[reference]]. The new value must be above or equal to 0. |name = SetCreatureReach |returnType = success:bool |referenceType = creatureRefr |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = newVal |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = creatureBase |Type = form |Optional = y }}
|example = rCreatureRef.SetCreatureReach 1
SetCreatureReach 10 CrCentaurEvolved }} ==Notes== *This attribute is only applicable to creatures that are unarmed and attack using parts of their skeleton.
==See Also== *[[GetCreatureReach]]
[[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Creature Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |summary = Dumps the contents of a [[FormList]]. If ''fileName'' is omitted, the contents are dumped into console.
Otherwise, it dumps to the specified file (in the [[GetFalloutDirectory|Fallout directory]]). If the file does not exist, it is created. If it exists and ''bAppend'' is omitted / is set to 1, the contents are appended to the file. Otherwise, all previous text is erased. |name = DumpFormList |alias = FListDump |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = FormList |Type = baseForm }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = fileName |Type = string |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = bAppend |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y }} |example = DumpFormList Everything Will output the contents of the "Everything" formlist in console. DumpFormList Everything "Test.txt" Will output the contents of the "Everything" formlist into "Fallout New Vegas/Test.txt". The content is appended to previous content. }} ==See Also== *[[Ar_Dump]] / [[Ar_DumpF]] - same principle, but for [[array]]s. *[[Print]] / [[PrintF]] *[[WriteArrayToFile]]
[[Category:List Functions]] [[Category:Debugging]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |summary = Adds the forms from an array to the [[FormList]] at the given index (or at the end if not provided). The array's contents are appended in the array key order. Returns 1 for success, 0 if an error is encountered. This does the same changes to the formlist as multiple [[ListAddForm]]s would, and is therefore subject to the [[:Category:List Functions|same warnings]] as that function. This also means the changes are ''not'' savebaked. |name = ListAddArray |alias = AddArrayToFormList |returnType = success:bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = formlist |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = array |Type = array_var }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = index |Type = int |Optional = optional }} |example = 
array_var aArr

let aArr := ar_List 1, 2, SunnyREF, player
ListAddArray MyEmptyFormList aArr
DumpFormList MyEmptyFormList
Will show that the MyEmptyFormList now has "SunnyREF" and "player". Non-forms and invalid forms are skipped. }} ==See Also== *[[ListAddForm]] *[[ListAddList]] *[[DumpFormList]] [[Category:List_Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |summary = Adds the forms from ''sourceList'' to ''destinationList'' at the given index (or at the end if not provided). The contents are appended in the order they appear in ''sourceList''. Returns 1 for success, 0 if an error is encountered.
This does the same changes to the formlist as multiple [[ListAddForm]]s would, and is therefore subject to the [[:Category:List Functions|same warnings]] as that function. This also means the changes are ''not'' savebaked. |name = ListAddList |alias = AddFormListToFormList |returnType = success:bool |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = destinationList |Type = baseform }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = sourceList |Type = baseForm }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = index |Type = int |Optional = optional }} }} ==See Also== *[[ListAddForm]] *[[ListAddArray]] *[[DumpFormList]]
[[Category:List_Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |summary = Like [[IsAnimPlayingEx]], but has one less argument in order to be available as a [[Condition]] function, since conditions can only have two arguments. Not meant for use in scripts - simply use [[IsAnimPlayingEx]] for that use case. |name = IsAnimPlayingExCond |returnType = animPlaying:0/1 |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = category |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = subType |Type = int |Optional = y }} ==1st Argument: Animation Categories== 
1	Miscellaneous
2	Movement
3	Attack
4	Reload
5	Weapon Jam
==2nd Argument (Optional): Animation Sub-Types== '''Miscellaneous:''' 
0	Any
1	Idle
2	(Un-)Equip/Holster
3	Block
4	Handgrip
'''Movement:''' 
0	Any
1	Forward
2	Backward
3	Left
4	Right
5	Jump
'''Attack:''' 
0	Any
1	AttackLeft
2	AttackRight
3	Attack3
4	Attack4
5	Attack5
6	Attack6
7	Attack7
8	Attack8
9	Attack9
10	AttackLoop
11	AttackSpin
12	AttackSpin2
13	AttackThrow
14	AttackThrow2
15	AttackThrow3
16	AttackThrow4
17	AttackThrow5
18	AttackThrow6
19	AttackThrow7
20	AttackThrow8
21	PlaceMine
22	PlaceMine2
23	AttackPower
'''Reload''': N/A '''Weapon Jam''': N/A |example =Below are some examples showing how to use this function in the GECK for conditions, such as Perk conditions. [[File:IsAnimPlayingExCondExample1.PNG]] The above checks if the reference is playing any '''Movement''' animation group. [[File:IsAnimPlayingExCondExample2.PNG]] The above checks if the reference is playing a '''Movement''' animation group of the '''Forward''' sub-type. [[File:IsAnimPlayingExCondExample3.PNG]] The above checks if the reference is playing an '''Attack''' animation group of the '''AttackLoop''' sub-type. [[File:IsAnimPlayingExCondExample4.PNG]] The above checks if the reference is playing any '''Weapon Jam''' animation group. }} [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Animation Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |summary = Returns the amount of hostile compass targets, optionally filtered by ''maxRange'' and ''flags''. If ''maxRange'' is unspecified / set to 0, no additional distance check is performed. Otherwise, the actor will have to be within the specified distance (in game [[Units]]) to be counted. ''flags'' is a bitmask. If ''flags'' is unspecified / set to 0, nothing else happens. Otherwise, additional filters will be applied to the count if certain bits are set.
Possible filter values: 
1	No invisible actors
|returnType = numCompassHostiles ||arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = maxRange |Type = float |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flags |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = int iNum let iNum := GetNumCompassHostiles Will return the amount of compass hostiles. Equivalent to `GetNumCompassHostiles 0 0`. let iNum := GetNumCompassHostiles 500 Will return the amount of compass hostiles that are 500 units from the player. Equivalent to `GetNumCompassHostiles 500 0`. let iNum := GetNumCompassHostiles 500 1 Will return the amount of compass hostiles that are 500 units from the player ''and'' are not invisible. }} ==Notes== *''No invisible actors'' works by checking if either the actor's Invisibility or Chameleon [[actor value]]s are greater than 0. ==See Also== *[[GetNearestCompassHostile]] / [[GetNearestCompassHostileDirection]] *[[IsCompassHostile]] *[[GetNumActorsInRangeFromRef]] *[[GetNumCombatActorsFromActor]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

revision.comment: Codes are those of the limb AVs not the body part locations
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = ShowOff |summary = Returns 1 if a limb (specified by ''LimbIndex'') is crippled. If no ''LimbIndex'' is specified, or if it is set to -1, instead returns 1 if any limb is crippled.
The "isCrippled" check is performed by checking if the [[Actor Value]] for that limb's condition is at or below 0. The minimum amount of health for a limb to be considered "crippled" by this function can be modified by changing ''healthThreshold''. |name = IsLimbCrippled |referenceType = ActorRefr |returnVal = 0/1 |returnType = isCrippled |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = LimbIndex |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = healthThreshold |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = SunnyRef.IsLimbCrippled 0 Tests if SunnyRef's head is crippled.
Player.IsLimbCrippled Tests if the Player has any limbs crippled or not. }}
==Notes== *The body locations and corresponding numbers are: {| {{Wikitable}} ! Body Part !! Return Value |- | Any Limb | -1 |- | Head | 0 |- | Torso | 1 |- | Left Arm | 2 |- | Right Arm | 3 |- | Left Leg | 4 |- | Right Leg | 5 |- | Brain | 6 |} ==See Also== *[[GetCrippledLimbsAsBitMask]] *[[GetNumCrippledLimbs]] *[[IsLimbGone]] *[[DismemberLimb]]
[[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = ShowOff |summary = Returns the amount of crippled limbs the calling actor has. The "isCrippled" check is performed by checking if the [[Actor Value]] f..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = ShowOff |summary = Returns the amount of crippled limbs the calling actor has.
The "isCrippled" check is performed by checking if the [[Actor Value]] for that limb's condition is at or below 0. The minimum amount of health for a limb to be considered "crippled" by this function can be modified by changing ''healthThreshold''. |name = GetNumCrippledLimbs |referenceType = ActorRefr |returnVal = numCrippledLimbs |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = healthThreshold |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = SunnyRef.GetNumCrippledLimbs Returns the amount of crippled limbs SunnyRef has.
Player.GetNumCrippledLimbs 50 Returns the Player's amount of limbs which which are at or below 50% health. }}
==See Also== *[[IsLimbCrippled]] *[[IsLimbGone]] *[[DismemberLimb]]
[[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

revision.comment: table formatting
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = ShowOff |summary = Returns the calling actors' equipped [[weapon]]'s Type (as seen in the Art and Sound tab). If no weapon is equipped, defaults to 0.
Note that while the GECK labels this as "Animation Type", weapon animation types are defined internally by converting this Weapon type into an Anim Type, as seen with [[GetWeaponAnimType]]. |name = GetEquippedWeaponType |returnVal = weapType |returnType = int |referenceType = ActorRefr }} ==Notes:== {|{{Wikitable}} |- ! Index ! Weapon Type |- |0 |HandToHandMelee |- |1 |OneHandMelee |- |2 |TwoHandMelee |- |3 |OneHandPistol |- |4 |OneHandPistolEnergy |- |5 |TwoHandRifle |- |6 |TwoHandAutomatic |- |7 |TwoHandRifleEnergy |- |8 |TwoHandHandle |- |9 |TwoHandLauncher |- |10 |OneHandGrenade |- |11 |OneHandMine |- |12 |OneHandLunchboxMine |- |13 |OneHandThrown (NV only; Spears) |} ==See Also== *[[GetWeaponType]] *[[GetWeaponAnimType]] *[[GetWeaponSkill]] *[[SetWeaponType]]
[[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |summary = Returns a bitmask detailing an actor's currently crippled limbs.
The "isCrippled" check is performed by checking if the [[Actor Value]] for that limb's condition is at or below 0. The minimum amount of health for a limb to be considered "crippled" by this function can be modified by changing ''healthThreshold''. |name = GetCrippledLimbsAsBitMask |referenceType = ActorRefr |returnVal = crippledLimbsMask |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = healthThreshold |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = if Player.GetCrippledLimbsAsBitMask == 3 Checks if the player has both their head and their torso crippled or not. }} ==Notes== *The limb flags and corresponding numbers are: {| class="wikitable" |- ! Crippled Limb Flag ! Value |- | Head | 1 |- | Torso | 2 |- | Left Arm | 4 |- | Right Arm | 8 |- | Left Leg | 16 |- | Right Leg | 32 |- | Brain | 64 |} ==See Also== *[[IsLimbCrippled]] *[[GetNumCrippledLimbs]] *[[IsLimbGone]] *[[DismemberLimb]]
[[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

revision.comment: GetNumBrokenEq new alias
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = ShowOff |summary = Returns the number of broken items the calling actor has equipped.
By default, the "isBroken" check is performed by checking if the health for the item's condition is at or below 0%. This minimum health percentage can be modified by changing ''healthPercThreshold''. *'''BUG: Does not currently account for weapons which have the "Increase Max Condition" [[Weapon Mod]].'''
''flagsMask'' is a bitmask containing filters for what kinds of items to get. If ''flags'' is omitted / set to 0, the function will check for every [[IsPlayable|playable]] equipped item. Otherwise, it can be used to specify which equip slots to check for, and further filters. |name = GetNumBrokenEquippedItems |alias = GetNumBrokenEq |referenceType = ActorRefr |returnVal = numBrokenItems |returnType = int |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = healthPercThreshold |Type = int |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagsMask |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = Player.GetNumBrokenEquippedItems Returns the player's total amount of broken, playable equipped items. Equivalent to `player.GetNumBrokenEquippedItems 0`.
Playerref.GetNumBrokenEquippedItems 30 32 Returns if the player's weapon is at or below 30% health. Since 32 seems a bit like a random number, this is more clearly written as the following, using bit shifting: Playerref.GetNumBrokenEquippedItems 30 (1 << 5) This is clearer since the 5th bit is the ''Weapon'' equip slot flag. }} ==Notes== {{Template:ShowOffEquippedFunctionFlags}} *The ''GetNumBrokenEq'' alias was only added in version 1.25.
==See Also== *[[GetEquippedItemsAsBitMask]]
[[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Armor Functions]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |summary = Returns a bitmask containing which equip slots are currently occupied. Note that certain items can occupy multiple equip slots. Non-playable items are checked. If called from console, will display the return value in hexadecimal format. |alias = GetOccupiedEquipSlots |referenceType = actorRefr |returnVal = equippedItemsMask |returnType = int |example = if Player.GetEquippedItemsAsBitMask == 12 Checks if the player has both something equipped for the ''Upper Body'' and the ''Left Hand'' equip slots (and nowhere else!).
if eval player.GetEquippedItemsAsBitMask & (1 << 5) Checks if the player has a weapon equipped. Equivalent to the following: if eval player.GetEquippedItemsAsBitMask & 32 }} ==Notes== {{Template:ShowOffEquippedFunctionFlags PARTIAL}}
==See Also== *[[GetEquippedItems]] *[[GetNumBrokenEquippedItems]]
[[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Armor Functions]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = ShowOff |summary = Returns the reference's Radiation ExtraData, if it has any. If it has no Radiation ExtraData, returns 0. This data is mostly used fo..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = ShowOff |summary = Returns the reference's Radiation ExtraData, if it has any. If it has no Radiation ExtraData, returns 0.
This data is mostly used for [[Radiation Marker]]s, where it determines the amount of rads gained while standing in the marker's radius. |name = GetRadiationExtraData |returnType = float |referenceType = reference |example = MyRadiationMarkerREF.GetRadiationExtraData }} ==See Also== *[[SetRadiationExtraData]] *[[Radiation Marker]]
[[Category:ExtraData Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |summary = Sets the reference's Radiation ExtraData, if it has any. If it has no Radiation ExtraData, it is added and created. The changes are savebaked.
This data is mostly used for [[Radiation Marker]]s, where it determines the amount of rads gained while standing in the marker's radius. |name = SetRadiationExtraData |returnType = bool |returnVal = success |referenceType = reference |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = newVal |Type = float }} |example = MyRadiationMarkerREF.SetRadiationExtraData 20 }} ==See Also== *[[GetRadiationExtraData]] *[[Radiation Marker]]
[[Category:ExtraData Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |summary = Returns true if Night Vision is active. This can be triggered at [[IsNight|night]], if the player is [[GetPCUsingScope|aiming down their scope]] with a [[weapon]] that has the '''[[GetWeaponFlags2|Scope Has Night Vision flag]]''' checked. |name = PlayerHasNightVisionActive |returnType = bool |returnVal = isNightVisionActive }}
[[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |summary = Returns true if the player is currently drinking placed water, false otherwise. |name = PlayerIsDrinkingPlacedWater |returnType = bool |returnVal = isDrinkingPlacedWater }} [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = ShowOff |summary = Sets whether or not the player is a murderer. |name = SetIsPCAMurderer |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = isMurderer..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = ShowOff |summary = Sets whether or not the player is a murderer. |name = SetIsPCAMurderer |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = isMurderer |Type = 0/1 }} |example = SetIsPCAMurderer 0 The player is set to not be a murderer. }} ==See Also== *[[IsPCAMurderer]]
[[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |summary = Returns true (1) if it is currently night-time according to the current [[Climate]]'s day/night cycle, false (0) otherwise.
If a climate is passed in the ''climate'' argument, instead checks it is currently night-time for that specific weather. |name = IsNight |alias = BloodyTears |returnType = bool |returnVal = isNight |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = climate |Type = baseForm |Optional = y }} |example = if IsNight This check succeeds if it is currently night-time for the current Climate. if IsNight NVDefaultClimate This check succeeds if it is currently night-time for NVDefaultClimate. }} ==Notes== *The Climate's day/night cycle is determined by the ''[[GetClimateTraitNumeric|Sunrise/Sunset Begin/End]]'' weather settings. *The game uses a global known as '''vNight''' to determine whether it is night or not, but this global is controlled by a script which uses a fixed day/night time, meaning it ignores the day/night cycle of the current Climate. *Note that vanilla commonly uses ''vNight'' to determine the day/night Weather, meaning that vNight essentially controls the day/night visual cycle. *The check that '''IsNight''' performs is used by the game to determine whether or not to toggle the Night Vision scope effect.
==See Also== *[[GetClimateTraitNumeric]]
[[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Weather Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |summary = Returns the total amount of base weight for the items that the calling [[Actor]] [[reference]] has equipped. This ignores weight modification effects, such as the "Modify Light Items" [[Perk Entry Point]].
''minWeight'' is the minimum weight an equipped item must have in order to be included in the total. If omitted, it defaults to 0.
''flagsMask'' is a bitmask containing filters for what kinds of items to get. If ''flags'' is omitted / set to 0, the function will check for every [[IsPlayable|playable]] equipped item. Otherwise, it can be used to specify which equip slots to check for, and further filters. *If an equipped item occupies multiple equip slots, the function will not count duplicates. |name = GetBaseEquippedWeight |returnType = float |returnVal = totalWeight |referenceType = actorRefr |conditionFunc = Both |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = minWeight |Type = float |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagsMask |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = player.GetBaseEquippedWeight 0 12 Returns the combined base weight of the items the player has equipped on the ''Upper Body'' and the ''Left Hand'' equip slots. }} ==Notes== {{Template:ShowOffEquippedFunctionFlags}}
==See Also== *[[GetCalculatedEquippedWeight]] *[[GetCalculatedMaxCarryWeight]] *[[GetEquippedObject]]
[[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |summary = Returns the total amount of Weight for the items that the calling [[Actor]] [[reference]] has equipped.
This accounts for weight modification effects, including: *"Modify Light Items" [[Perk Entry Point]] *"Decrease Weight" [[Weapon Mod]] effect - note that it does not properly account for it if more than 1 weap. mod with this effect is equipped. *"Adjust Heavy Weapon Weight" [[Perk Entry Point]]
''minWeight'' is the minimum weight an equipped item must have in order to be included in the total. If omitted, it defaults to 0.
''flagsMask'' is a bitmask containing filters for what kinds of items to get. If ''flags'' is omitted / set to 0, the function will check for every [[IsPlayable|playable]] equipped item. Otherwise, it can be used to specify which equip slots to check for, and further filters. *If an equipped item occupies multiple equip slots, the function will not count duplicates. |name = GetCalculatedEquippedWeight |returnType = float |returnVal = totalWeight |referenceType = actorRefr |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = minWeight |Type = float |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flagsMask |Type = int |Optional = y }} |example = player.GetCalculatedEquippedWeight Returns the total weight that the player has equipped on all slots, <u>except for unplayable items</u>, such as the Pip-Boy.
player.GetCalculatedEquippedWeight 0 (0b00000000000000000001100) Returns the combined current weight of the items the player has equipped on the ''Upper Body'' and the ''Left Hand'' equip slots. If either of those slots are occupied by Unplayable items, they are still counted.
'''0b00000000000000000001100''' (a number in [[Binary Notation]]) is equal to 12 in decimal (4 + 8). Bit #2 and #3 are both enabled, which represent ''Upper Body'' and ''Left Hand'', respectively. }} ==Notes== {{Template:ShowOffEquippedFunctionFlags}} *This function does NOT account for most, if not all of the ''bWeightless(...)'' options from [[lStewieAl's Tweaks]].
==See Also== *[[GetBaseEquippedWeight]] *[[GetCalculatedMaxCarryWeight]] *[[GetEquippedObject]]
[[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Bit Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

revision.comment: Fix error, thanks Phoenix
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = ShowOff |summary = Returns the calling [[actor]] [[reference]]'s current Max Carry Weight. This accounts for the "Get Max Carry Weight" [[Perk Entry Point]]. |name = GetCalculatedMaxCarryWeight |returnType = float |returnVal = maxCarryWeight |referenceType = actorRefr |example = player.GetCalculatedMaxCarryWeight Returns the player's current Max Carry Weight. }} ==See Also== *[[GetAV]] - CarryWeight and InventoryWeight are both [[Stats_List|Actor Values]]
[[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |summary = If set to a non-0 value, toggles debug print messages for certain [[ShowOff NVSE]] functions. Not particularly useful for most modmakers; this exists more-so to aid in ShowOff's development. |name = SetShowOffDebugMode |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = isDebugEnabled |Type = int |Value = 0/1 }} |example = SetShowOffDebugMode 1 }} ==See Also== *[[GetShowOffDebugMode]]
[[Category:Debugging]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |summary = Returns 1 (true) if print messages are enabled for certain [[ShowOff NVSE]] functions, 0 (false) otherwise. Not particularly useful for most modmakers; this exists more-so to aid in ShowOff's development. |name = GetShowOffDebugMode |returnVal = isDebugEnabled |returnType = 0/1 |example = int isDebugEnabled let isDebugEnabled := GetShowOffDebugMode }} ==See Also== *[[SetShowOffDebugMode]]
[[Category:Debugging]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |summary = Unequips certain [[item]]s from the calling [[Actor]] [[reference]]. When called without specifying any arguments, will unequip all equipped [[Playable Items]]. Otherwise, filters and effects can be used to determine what items will be unequipped, and how.
==Parameters== '''''flagsMask''''': A bitmask containing filters for what kinds of items to get. If ''flags'' is omitted / set to 0, the function will check for every [[IsPlayable|playable]] equipped item.
