Value |- | [https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8259#section-2 Standard JSON] | 0 |- | [https://github.com/stand-art/jaxn/blob/main/Specification.md JAXN - Extended JSON] | 1 |- | [https://github.com/taocpp/config/blob/main/doc/Writing-Config-Files.md TaoConfig] | 2 |} ==Examples== No matter the contents of "myFile.json" (assuming it is at least a valid standard JSON file): 
array_var aJSON = &(ReadFromJSON "myFile.json")
WriteToJSON (*aJSON) "myFile.json"
array_var aNewJSON = &(ReadFromJSON "myFile.json")
''aNewJSON'' will be the exact same as ''aJSON'', showing that '''WriteToJSON''' takes inputs in the same format as what is outputted by '''ReadFromJSON'''. ===Writing Forms=== Forms are written as JSON strings following a specific format. * They will be prefixed with '@', followed by the source file name, then ':', and finally the formID (without the mod index). '''Example:''' After the following code runs, 
WriteToJSON NVDLC04Ulysses "myFile.json"
Then the file's contents will be the following JSON string: 
"@LonesomeRoad.esm:00003E43"
==Notes== *If using unvalidated user inputs, error messages can be prevented using [[TestExpr]]. *The optional ''compactFormatting'' arg was added in version 1.81. ==Bugs== *Prior to version 1.80, specifying a value for ''jsonPointer'' would always result in a crash. *Prior to version 1.81, multibyte characters were written with the incorrect text encoding. *Prior to version 1.83, the cache could not be overwritten on subsequent writes. ==See Also== *[[ReadFromJSON]] *[[ClearFileCacheShowOff]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:JSON Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Cached File Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.45 |summary = Sets the numerical/string value of an INI key.
'''Only affects a cached version of the INI file!''' To save the changes to the actual file, [[SaveCachedIniFile]] must be called. *If the file was not previously cached, then it becomes cached. *Caching eats memory, but allows faster access to the data. *Once the cache is no longer needed, [[ClearFileCacheShowOff]] should be called to free the memory.
If an error has occured, will return -1. Otherwise, returns 1 if the key existed previously and was updated, or 2 if it was inserted.
If the value is created and ''comment'' is not an empty string, will add the comment above the created key:value pair. |name = SetINIValue_Cached |alias = SetINIValC |returnType = int |returnVal = iResult |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = sectionAndKey |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = stringOrFloat }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = iniPath |Type = string |Optional = y |Value = "" }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = comment |Type = string |Optional = y |Value = "" }} }} ==Example== SetINIValue_Cached "GamePlay:fMeleeDamageMultiplier" 2 "MyFolder\MyFile.ini" Once this function is called it should automatically make an INI file (If not created already) named MyFile.ini in "Data\Config\MyFolder"
The INI file should now contain the following: [GamePlay] fMeleeDamageMultiplier = 2
==Notes== {{Template:INI Function Notes}} ==See Also== *[[GetINIFloatOrDefault_Cached]] / [[GetINIFloatOrCreate_Cached]] *[[GetINIStringOrDefault_Cached]] / [[GetINIStringOrCreate_Cached]] *[[HasINISetting_Cached]] *[[SetINIInteger_Cached]] *[[SaveCachedIniFile]] *[[ReloadIniCache]] *[[ClearFileCacheShowOff]]
[[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:INI Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Cached File Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.45 |summary = Returns the string value of an INI key. If it does not exist or could not be found, ''defaultValue'' is returned.
'''Reads the INI file data from a memory cache.''' *If the file was not previously cached, then it becomes cached. *Caching eats memory, but allows faster access to the data. *Once the cache is no longer needed, [[ClearFileCacheShowOff]] should be called to free the memory.
|name = GetINIStringOrDefault_Cached |alias = GetINIString_Cached |returnType = string |returnVal = value |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = sectionAndKey |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = iniPath |Type = string |Optional = y |Value = "" }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = defaultValue |Type = string |Optional = y |Value = "" }} }}
==Notes== {{Template:INI Function Notes}} ==See Also== *[[GetINIFloatOrDefault_Cached]] / [[GetINIFloatOrCreate_Cached]] *[[GetINIStringOrCreate_Cached]] *[[HasINISetting_Cached]] *[[SetINIValue_Cached]] *[[SetINIInteger_Cached]] *[[SaveCachedIniFile]] *[[ReloadIniCache]] *[[ClearFileCacheShowOff]]
[[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:INI Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Cached File Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.45 |summary = Returns the float value of an INI key. If it does not exist or could not be found, ''defaultValue'' is returned.
'''Reads the INI file data from a memory cache.''' *If the file was not previously cached, then it becomes cached. *Caching eats memory, but allows faster access to the data. *Once the cache is no longer needed, [[ClearFileCacheShowOff]] should be called to free the memory.
|name = GetINIFloatOrDefault_Cached |alias = GetINIFloat_Cached |returnType = float |returnVal = value |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = sectionAndKey |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = iniPath |Type = string |Optional = y |Value = "" }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = defaultValue |Type = Float |Optional = y |Value = 0 }} }}
==Notes== {{Template:INI Function Notes}} ==See Also== *[[GetINIFloatOrCreate_Cached]] *[[GetINIStringOrDefault_Cached]] / [[GetINIStringOrCreate_Cached]] *[[HasINISetting_Cached]] *[[SetINIValue_Cached]] *[[SetINIInteger_Cached]] *[[SaveCachedIniFile]] *[[ReloadIniCache]] *[[ClearFileCacheShowOff]]
[[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:INI Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Cached File Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.45 |summary = Returns the float value of an INI key. If it does not exist or could not be found, ''createValue'' is returned and is written in the INI file cache.
'''Reads/writes the INI file data from a memory cache.''' *If the file was not previously cached, then it becomes cached. *Caching eats memory, but allows faster access to the data. *To save changes to the actual file, use [[SaveCachedIniFile]]. *Once the cache is no longer needed, [[ClearFileCacheShowOff]] should be called to free the memory.
If the value is created and comment is not an empty string, will add the comment above the created key:value pair.
|name = GetINIFloatOrCreate_Cached |alias = GetINIFltOrCreateC |returnType = float |returnVal = value |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = sectionAndKey |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = iniPath |Type = string |Optional = y |Value = "" }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = createValue |Type = float |Optional = y |Value = 0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = comment |Type = string |Optional = y |Value = "" }} }}
==Notes== {{Template:INI Function Notes}} ==See Also== *[[GetINIFloatOrDefault_Cached]] *[[GetINIStringOrDefault_Cached]] / [[GetINIStringOrCreate_Cached]] *[[HasINISetting_Cached]] *[[SetINIValue_Cached]] *[[SetINIInteger_Cached]] *[[SaveCachedIniFile]] *[[ReloadIniCache]] *[[ClearFileCacheShowOff]]
[[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:INI Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Cached File Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.45 |summary = Returns the string value of an INI key. If it does not exist or could not be found, ''createValue'' is returned and is written in the INI file cache.
'''Reads/writes the INI file data from a memory cache.''' *If the file was not previously cached, then it becomes cached. *Caching eats memory, but allows faster access to the data. *To save changes to the actual file, use [[SaveCachedIniFile]]. *Once the cache is no longer needed, [[ClearFileCacheShowOff]] should be called to free the memory.
If the value is created and comment is not an empty string, will add the comment above the created key:value pair.
|name = GetINIFloatOrCreate_Cached |alias = GetINIStrOrCreateC |returnType = string |returnVal = value |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = sectionAndKey |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = iniPath |Type = string |Optional = y |Value = "" }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = createValue |Type = string |Optional = y |Value = "" }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = comment |Type = string |Optional = y |Value = "" }} }}
==Notes== {{Template:INI Function Notes}} ==See Also== *[[GetINIFloatOrDefault_Cached]] / [[GetINIFloatOrCreate_Cached]] *[[GetINIStringOrDefault_Cached]] *[[HasINISetting_Cached]] *[[SetINIValue_Cached]] *[[SetINIInteger_Cached]] *[[SaveCachedIniFile]] *[[ReloadIniCache]] *[[ClearFileCacheShowOff]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.45 |summary = Returns true (1) if the specified INI setting exists, false (0) otherwise.
'''Reads the INI file data from a memory cache.''' *If the file was not previously cached, then it becomes cached. *Caching eats memory, but allows faster access to the data. *Once the cache is no longer needed, [[ClearFileCacheShowOff]] should be called to free the memory.
|name = HasINISetting_Cached |alias = HasINISettingC |returnType = bool |returnVal = exists |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = sectionAndKey |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = iniPath |Type = string |Optional = y |Value = "" }} }}

[[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:INI Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Cached File Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.45 |summary = Saves (writes) the contents of a cached INI file to the actual file. This recreates it entirely, and as such can be a bit costly to do constantly; one should only call this function after finishing consecutive changes.
'''Details on caching INI files:''' *Caching eats memory, but allows faster access to the data. *Once the cache is no longer needed, [[ClearFileCacheShowOff]] should be called to free the memory.
|name = SaveCachedIniFile |alias = SaveCachedIni |returnType = bool |returnVal = success |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = iniPath |Type = string |Optional = y |Value = "" }} }}
==Notes== {{Template:INI Function Notes}} ==See Also== *[[GetINIFloatOrDefault_Cached]] / [[GetINIFloatOrCreate_Cached]] *[[GetINIStringOrDefault_Cached]] / [[GetINIStringOrCreate_Cached]] *[[HasINISetting_Cached]] *[[SetINIValue_Cached]] *[[SetINIInteger_Cached]] *[[ReloadIniCache]] *[[ClearFileCacheShowOff]]
[[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:INI Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Cached File Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.45 |summary = Reloads an existing INI file cache; all data in the cache is lost in favor of the file's current contents.
Returns true (1) for success, false (0) otherwise.
'''Details on caching INI files:''' *Caching eats memory, but allows faster access to the data. *Once the cache is no longer needed, [[ClearFileCacheShowOff]] should be called to free the memory.
|name = ReloadIniCache |returnType = bool |returnVal = success |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = iniPath |Type = string |Optional = y |Value = "" }} }}
==Notes== {{Template:INI Function Notes}} ==See Also== *[[GetINIFloatOrDefault_Cached]] / [[GetINIFloatOrCreate_Cached]] *[[GetINIStringOrDefault_Cached]] / [[GetINIStringOrCreate_Cached]] *[[HasINISetting_Cached]] *[[SetINIValue_Cached]] *[[SetINIInteger_Cached]] *[[SaveCachedIniFile]] *[[ClearFileCacheShowOff]]
[[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:INI Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Cached File Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.45 |summary = Clears the cached data of a given file. Supported file cache functions are listed in [[Cached File Functions (ShowOff)]].
The type of the cached file must be specified via ''toClearType''. *Valid values are listed in '''Notes'''.
Returns true (1) if the cache was found and cleared, false (0) otherwise.
'''Details on caching files:''' *Caching eats memory, but allows faster access to the data. *Caches should be cleared when they cease to be useful.
|name = ClearFileCacheShowOff |alias = CloseFileSO |returnType = bool |returnVal = success |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = filePath |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = toClearType |Type = int }} }} ==Notes== *Valid values for ''toClearType'': {| class="wikitable" |- style="font-weight:bold; text-decoration:underline;" ! To Clear Type ! Value |- | INI | 0 |- | JSON | 1 |} *If the Clear Type is INI, then ''filePath'' will be relative to "Data\Config". *If the Clear Type is JSON, then ''filePath'' will be relative to the root folder.
==Bugs== *Prior to version 1.81, this function did not normalize JSON filepaths.
==See Also== *[[GetINIFloatOrDefault_Cached]] / [[GetINIFloatOrCreate_Cached]] *[[GetINIStringOrDefault_Cached]] / [[GetINIStringOrCreate_Cached]] *[[HasINISetting_Cached]] *[[SetINIValue_Cached]] *[[SetINIInteger_Cached]] *[[SaveCachedIniFile]] *[[ReloadIniCache]] *[[ReadFromJSON]] *[[WriteToJSON]]
[[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:INI Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Cached File Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:NVSE-Aware Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.45 |summary = Returns the item's reference's value modified by condition and [[Weapon Mod]]s. Returns -1 in case of an err..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.45 |summary = Returns the item's reference's value modified by condition and [[Weapon Mod]]s.
Returns -1 in case of an error.
If ''accountForBarterChanges'' is true AND a valid [[Inventory Reference]] is passed, will also account for Barter menu sell/buy multipliers. It should return the same value as what appears on screen. *May not work reliably if the function is being used as a Condition; it may be evaluated before the '''Calculate Buy Price''' [[Perk Entry Point]] effects are applied.
|name = GetCalculatedItemValue |returnType = float |returnVal = value |referenceType = itemRefr |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = accountForBarterChanges |Type = bool |Optional = y |Value = false }} |conditionFunc = Both }}
[[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

revision.comment: Trooper moved page [[Category:Cached File Functions (ShowOff)]] to [[Category:Cached File Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]: Moving ShowOff subcategories so that they are under a consistent (ShowOff NVSE)
revision.text:
[[ShowOff NVSE]] functions that work with cached file data.
'''Details on caching files:''' *Caching eats memory, but allows faster access to the data. *Caches should be cleared when they cease to be useful using [[ClearFileCacheShowOff]].
[[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:Function Types]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.50 |summary = Returns an array containing player's compass targets. Includes either all of them (default, ''includeWhat'' = 0), or optionally only hostile actors (''includeWhat'' = 1), or only detected actors (''includeWhat'' = 2). |returnVal = actors |returnType = array |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = includeWhat |Type = int |Value = 0/1/2 |Optional = y }} |example = array_var aTargets = GetCompassTargets 1 ; array will contain all hostile compass targets }} ==Notes== *This function was originally included in the [[JohnnyGuitar NVSE Plugin]], but it has been since moved to [[ShowOff]]. *During the transition, the function lost its ''skipInvisible'' arg, as that is now always being accounted for; all targets that actually appear in the compass are considered.
==Bugs== *This function can sometimes return invalid forms, although this may only happen on some machines. The reason is unknown.
==See Also== *[[GetCompassHostiles]]
[[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Compass Functions]] [[Category:Array Functions]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.50 |summary = Sets the integer value of an INI key.
'''Only affects a cached version of the INI file!''' To save the changes to the actual file, [[SaveCachedIniFile]] must be called. *If the file was not previously cached, then it becomes cached. *Caching eats memory, but allows faster access to the data. *Once the cache is no longer needed, [[ClearFileCacheShowOff]] should be called to free the memory.
If an error has occured, will return -1. Otherwise, returns 1 if the key existed previously and was updated, or 2 if it was inserted.
If the value is created and ''comment'' is not an empty string, will add the comment above the created key:value pair. |name = SetINIInteger_Cached |alias = SetINIIntC |returnType = int |returnVal = iResult |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = sectionAndKey |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newValue |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = iniPath |Type = string |Optional = y |Value = "" }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = comment |Type = string |Optional = y |Value = "" }} }} ==Example== SetINIInteger_Cached "SomeSection:iSomeSetting" 2 "MyFolder\MyFile.ini" Once this function is called it should automatically make an INI file (If not created already) named MyFile.ini in "Data\Config\MyFolder"
The INI file should now contain the following: [SomeSection] iSomeSetting = 2
==Notes== {{Template:INI Function Notes}} ==See Also== *[[GetINIFloatOrDefault_Cached]] / [[GetINIFloatOrCreate_Cached]] *[[GetINIStringOrDefault_Cached]] / [[GetINIStringOrCreate_Cached]] *[[HasINISetting_Cached]] *[[SetINIValue_Cached]] *[[SaveCachedIniFile]] *[[ReloadIniCache]] *[[ClearFileCacheShowOff]]
[[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:INI Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Cached File Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.50 |summary = Returns the [[calling reference|calling actor reference]]'s currently equipped weapon, as an [[Inventory Reference]]. If no weapon is equipped, returns 0. This is equivalent to calling [[GetEquippedItemRef]] for the Weapon slot. |name = GetEquippedWeaponRef |referenceType = actorRefr |returnType = invRef |returnVal = equippedWeap }} ==See also== *[[GetEquippedWeapon]] *[[GetEquippedItemRef]] *[[GetEquippedObject]]
[[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.50 |name = GetCalculatedAPCost |alias = GetCalculatedWeaponAttackAPCost |summary = Returns the Action Point cost for the player doing the specified VATS attack with their currently equipped weapon (or their fists, if no weapon is equipped). Here are the possible values for ''whatAttack'', which determines what VATS attack to query: 
DefaultAttack = 0,
SpecialAttack1 = 1, (does not work correctly outside of VATS)
SpecialAttack2 = 2 (does not work correctly outside of VATS)
If ''weaponOverride'' is set to a valid [[Weapon]] baseform, will instead use that weapon for the calculation. |returnType = int |returnVal = cost |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = whatAttack |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = weaponOverride |Type = baseForm |Default = y |Optional = y |Value = 0 }} }} ==Notes== *''SpecialAttack1'' is either the VATS attack override for Melee Weapons (equivalent to [[GetWeaponVATSTraitNumeric]] 3), or the uppercut or stomping moves when using Unarmed weapons (as per the game setting '''fUpperCutAPCost'''). *''SpecialAttack2'' is the cross attack move when using Unarmed weapons, as per the game setting '''fCrossAPCost'''. [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]] [[Category:VATS Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.50 |name = GetPosArray |summary = Outputs the XYZ position values of the [[calling reference]] into an array, where 1st ele..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.50 |name = GetPosArray |summary = Outputs the XYZ position values of the [[calling reference]] into an array, where 1st element is the X position, 2nd is Y position, and 3rd is Z position. |returnType = array |returnVal = XYZPos }}
[[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Array Functions]] [[Category:Movement Functions]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.50 |name = IsOutsideMainThread |summary = Returns true if the function is being called from outside the game's main thread.
This can be used to avoid calling code that may be unsafe when outside the main thread. For example, when outside the main thread, the [[Print]] functions can cause a crash if [[ToggleDebugText]] is on.
In most cases, the only way to run code from outside the main thread is by using certain [[Event Handlers]], which may be invoked from code residing in a helper thread. |returnType = bool |returnVal = isOutside }}
[[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Debugging]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.50 |name = GetActorValueName |alias = GetAVName |summary = Returns the name string associated to an actor value. This is useful to convert an [[Actor Value Codes|actor value code]] into its actor value name. |returnType = string |returnVal = name |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = actorValue |Type = statName }} |example = 
 string_var sName = GetActorValueName (1)
 printvar sName
 ;* Prints sName as containing "Confidence"
The above example uses an [[Inline Expression]] to allow passing an integer as an actor value code. 
 ;* Prints the values of all limb condition AVs, along with their names, for readability.

 int iPerceptionCondition = 25
 int iBrainCondition = 31

 int i = iPerceptionCondition 
 while (i <= iBrainCondition)
    float fCondition = player.GetAV (i)
    string_var sAVName = player.GetAVName (i)
    print $sName + ": " + $fCondition 
    i += 1
 loop
}} [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Actor Value Functions]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.55 |summary = Returns if the player character is overencumbered or not (1 or 0, true or false). If ''ignoreGodMode'' is set to true (non-0), will ignore the player's [[TGM|GodMode]] status to determine if they are overencumbered. By default, if they are in GodMode, they cannot be considered overencumbered. |name = GetIsPlayerOverencumbered |alias = IsPCOverencumbered |returnType = isOverencumbered:bool |conditionFunc = Both |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = ignoreGodMode |Type = bool |Optional = y |Value = 0 }} |example = 
int bIsOverencumbered = IsPCOverencumbered
Will return 1\0 depending on whether PC is overencumbered or not. If the player is in GodMode, will return 1 no matter what. }} ==Notes== *This function accounts for both the "Modify Light Items" and "Get Max Carry Weight" [[Perk Entry Point]]s. ==See Also== *[[GetEncumbranceRate]] *[[GetInventoryWeight]] *[[GetActorValue]] (since max CarryWeight is an AV) *[[GetPerkModifier]] (since max carry weight is also affected by "Get Max Carry Weight" perk entries (#22)) [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.55 |summary = Creates and starts [[Auxiliary Timer]] on an owning form to start counting down from ''timeToCountdown'' with the specified ''flags''. If the timer exists already, will instead restart it, and it will be unpaused if it was paused. If no owner form for the AuxTimer is specified, ''PlayerRef'' will be used. Once the timer for an AuxTimer runs out, it will will trigger the [[OnAuxTimerStop]] event. Afterwards, the AuxTimer will be deleted automatically unless the '''Auto Restarts''' flag is on. In that case, the timer will restart, triggering the [[OnAuxTimerStart]] event. '''AuxTimer Flags:''' 
Bit# 	 Value 	 Description
0 	 1 	 Run In MenuMode
1 	 2 	 Run In GameMode
2 	 4 	 Count In Seconds (if off, counts down in frames)
3 	 8 	 Auto-Remove On Load And MainMenu (has no effect on permanent AuxTimers, only temporary ones)
4 	 16 	 Auto Restarts
5 	 32 	 Is Paused
6 	 64 	 Ignore TimeMult In MenuMode (only used for seconds-based timers)
7 	 128 	 Don't Run When Paused (won't run in main menu, pause menu, console menu)
8 	 256 	 Run OnTimerUpdate Event (see SetOnAuxTimerUpdateHandler) - v1.65
9 	 512 	 Ignore Turbo - If on, seconds-based timers that don't ignore TimeMult won't be slowed down by turbo - v1.80
10 	 1024 	 Ignore TimeMult In GameMode (only used for seconds-based timers) - v1.80
The default-enabled flags are '''Run In MenuMode''', '''Run In GameMode''', and '''Count In Seconds'''.
Therefore, default timers obey [[GetGlobalTimeMultiplier|TimeMult]], and are slowed down by the Turbo slow-mo effect (unlike the player). The '''Ignore Turbo''' flag is recommended to be enabled if the timer should run according to the player's perspective of time, not the world's. This is because the player isn't slowed down by Turbo, but the rest of the world is. *This flag only has an effect if the '''Ignore TimeMult''' flags aren't enabled. Otherwise, it is ignored. For UI mods, it is usually recommended to enable the '''Ignore TimeMult''' flags, as most UI features in the vanilla game aren't affected by TimeMult. If the AuxTimer's owner form becomes invalid at any time while it is counting down, the AuxTimer will be deleted. This will NOT fire the [[OnAuxTimerStop]] event. |name = AuxTimerStart |returnType = success:bool |referenceType = ownerRefr |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = timerName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = timeToCountdown |Type = float |Value = -1.0 |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = flags |Type = int |Optional = y |Value = 1+2+4 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = owner |Type = baseForm |Optional = y }} |example = RaulRef.AuxTimerStart "someTimerName" 5 Will create a private-permanent AuxTimer belonging to RaulRef that will count down for 5 seconds before stopping. It will count down in both GameMode and MenuMode. AuxTimerStart "*_someTimerName" 3 2 ; 2 means only the "Run In GameMode" flag is on. Will create a temporary-public AuxTimer belonging (implicitly) to PlayerRef that will count down for 3 frames before stopping. It will count down only in GameMode. }} ==See Also== *[[SetOnAuxTimerStartHandler]] / [[SetOnAuxTimerStopHandler]] *[[SetOnAuxTimerUpdateHandler]] - Requires the '''Run OnTimerUpdate Event''' flag to be enabled to run. *[[AuxTimerStop]] *[[AuxTimerPaused]] *[[AuxTimerTimeElapsed]] *[[AuxTimerTimeToCountdown]] *[[AuxTimerTimeLeft]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Aux Functions]] [[Category:Auxiliary-Timer Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]]

revision.comment: /* Properties */
revision.text:
The '''Auxiliary-Timers''' interface is a utility added by the [[ShowOff NVSE Plugin]] in Fallout: New Vegas, and is an alternative method for dynamically storing and managing timers that automatically count down, unless paused. Auxiliary-Timers (abbreviated as '''AuxTimers''') are created, accessed, modified and deleted using a set of script commands. AuxTimers are a specialized form of [[Auxiliary-Variable Functions (JIP)|Auxiliary-Variables]] and share much of the same syntax. Specifically, this interface serves as a replacement for writing timers inside scripts and manually counting them down using [[GetSecondsPassed]]. Since AuxTimers can be savebaked depending on the duration chosen for it, they typically serve as savebaked script callbacks. In comparison, callbacks like [[CallAfterSeconds]] are NOT savebaked. ==Properties== *An AuxTimer is defined by a Name (string, case-insensitive, limit of 79 characters including any prefix) and an Object (object reference/base form) that "owns" it. This ''Object:Name'' pair must, therefore, be unique. Note that different Objects may "own" a timer with the same Name and still count as a unique pair. Any Object may "own" any number of timers. *AuxTimers store a timer value that gets counted down and flags for how the timer should behave. ==Duration & Accessibility== '''Duration:''' An AuxTimer's duration setting determines its "life-span", i.e. whether it is permanent or temporary. Permanent AuxTimers are saved with the game. In contrast, temporary AuxTimers are not saved with the game. Instead, they are persistent for the duration of the current game session (much like global variables) but are discarded when the game is exited. Consider using [[File Functions|file functions]] such as [[SetINIFloat]] to save information across multiple sessions. '''Accessibility:''' The accessibility setting determines which mods can "see" and manipulate the AuxTimer. A private AuxTimer is "private" to the mod it was created by, and can only be accessed/modified by scripts originating in that mod. This allows different mods to use the same timer names, associated with the same objects, without overwriting each other. A public AuxTimer, on the other hand, can be accessed/modified by any mod. This is an effective method for inter-mod communication and exchange of timer data, without any master-dependencies. Enabling each property is done by adding a special prefix to the AuxTimer's name. The two properties, and their combinations, define four classes of AuxTimer, as summarized in the following table: {|class="wikitable centercells" |- !Class !Prefix !Example !Saved w/ Game !Persistent in Session !Accessible By |- |Temporary-Public |*_ |"*_TimerName" |No |Yes |Any mod |- |Permanent-Public |_ |"_TimerName" |Yes |No |Any mod |- |Temporary-Private |* |"*TimerName" |No |Yes |Creating mod |- |Permanent-Private |None |"TimerName" |Yes |No |Creating mod |} ==Examples== Here we have a [[UDF]] script that will act as an event handler for [[SetOnTimerStopHandler]]: 
scn TestAuxTimerCallback

string_var sTimerName
ref rOwnerForm

begin Function { sTimerName, rOwnerForm }
	print "TestAuxTimerCallback >> RAN!"
	printvar sTimerName
	printvar rOwnerForm 
end
Its purpose will be to automatically run whenever our timer has counted all the way down and is stopped.
In order to register this event handler, we can use the following line: SunnyREF.SetOnTimerStopHandler TestAuxTimerCallback "Test" For this example, we chose '''"Test"''' as our AuxTimer's name, and SunnyRef as an arbitrary form to associate the timer with. Note that event handlers registered with [[SetOnTimerStopHandler]] get cleared automatically when a save is loaded, so if the handlers are being registered in a quest script, calling [[SetOnTimerStopHandler]] every time [[GetGameLoaded]] returns true is recommended. Finally, to start and create our AuxTimer, we can use [[AuxTimerStart]]: SunnyRef.AuxTimerStart "Test" 5 This will create a private-permanent AuxTimer named "Test" belonging to SunnyRef that will count down for 5 seconds before stopping. It will count down in both GameMode and MenuMode by default, unless with specify other flags (see [[AuxTimerStart]]'s page). When the time has fully passed, the ''TestAuxTimerCallback'' event handler will automatically be called, allowing us to react to the timer being stopped. And with that, our goal of setting up a save-baked callback is done! ==See Also== *[[Auxiliary-Variable_Functions_(JIP)|Auxiliary-Variables]] (Auxiliary Variables, with which any basic data can be stored on a form) *[[Aux Functions]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Aux Functions]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.55 |summary = Stops and erases an [[Auxiliary Timer]] on a form. This will only fire the [[OnAuxTimerStop]] event if ''fireEvent'' is set to non-0.
If no owner form for the AuxTimer is specified, ''PlayerRef'' will be used.
|name = AuxTimerStop |alias = AuxTimerErase |returnType = success:bool |referenceType = ownerRefr |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = timerName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = fireEvent |Type = bool |Value = false |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = owner |Type = baseForm |Optional = y }} |example = RaulRef.AuxTimerStop "someTimerName" Will stop and erase the private-permanent AuxTimer belonging to RaulRef. If RaulRef did not have such a timer, will do nothing and return 0.
AuxTimerStop "*_someTimerName" 1 Will stop and erase the temporary-public AuxTimer belonging (implicitly) to PlayerRef. This will fire the [[OnAuxTimerStop]] event. }} ==See Also== *[[SetOnAuxTimerStartHandler]] / [[SetOnAuxTimerStopHandler]] *[[AuxTimerStart]] *[[AuxTimerPaused]] *[[AuxTimerTimeElapsed]] *[[AuxTimerTimeToCountdown]] *[[AuxTimerTimeLeft]]
[[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Aux Functions]] [[Category:Auxiliary-Timer Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.55 |summary = Returns 1 if the specified [[Auxiliary Timer]] on the specified form is currently paused, 0 otherwise. Afterwards, if ''setPaused'' is specified, will set if the timer is paused or not.
If the timer does not exist, returns -1.
If no owner form for the AuxTimer is specified, ''PlayerRef'' will be used.
|name = AuxTimerPaused |returnType = wasPaused:bool |referenceType = ownerRefr |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = timerName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = setPaused |Type = int |Value = -1 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = owner |Type = baseForm |Optional = y }} |example = RaulRef.AuxTimerPaused "someTimerName" Will return either 1 or 0, depending on if the private-permanent AuxTimer belonging to RaulRef is paused.
AuxTimerStop "*_someTimerName" 1 Will pause the temporary-public AuxTimer belonging (implicitly) to PlayerRef. }} ==See Also== *[[AuxTimerStart]] *[[AuxTimerStop]] *[[AuxTimerTimeElapsed]] *[[AuxTimerTimeToCountdown]] *[[AuxTimerTimeLeft]]
[[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Aux Functions]] [[Category:Auxiliary-Timer Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.55 |summary = Returns the amount of time passed since the specified [[Auxiliary Timer]] started. If the timer does not exist or was fully stopped before this is called, returns -1.
Does NOT account for time elapsed while the timer is paused, if it becomes paused by [[AuxTimerPaused]].
If no owner form for the AuxTimer is specified, ''PlayerRef'' will be used.
|name = AuxTimerTimeElapsed |returnType = timeElapsed:float |referenceType = ownerRefr |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = timerName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = unused |Type = int |Value = -1 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = owner |Type = baseForm |Optional = y }} |example = RaulRef.AuxTimerTimeElapsed "someTimerName" Will return the amount of time passed since the private-permanent AuxTimer belonging to RaulRef was started, assuming it is still running. }} ==See Also== *[[AuxTimerStart]] *[[AuxTimerStop]] *[[AuxTimerPaused]] *[[AuxTimerTimeToCountdown]] *[[AuxTimerTimeLeft]]
[[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Aux Functions]] [[Category:Auxiliary-Timer Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.55 |summary = Returns the original amount of time that the [[Auxiliary Timer]] was ordered to start counting down. If the timer doesn't exist or was fully stopped before this is called, returns -1.
If no owner form for the AuxTimer is specified, ''PlayerRef'' will be used.
|name = AuxTimerTimeToCountdown |returnType = timeToCountdown:float |referenceType = ownerRefr |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = timerName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = owner |Type = baseForm |Optional = y }} |example = RaulRef.AuxTimerTimeToCountdown "someTimerName" Will return the original amount of time that the the private-permanent AuxTimer belonging to RaulRef was ordered to count down from, assuming such a timer is still running. }} ==See Also== *[[AuxTimerStart]] *[[AuxTimerStop]] *[[AuxTimerPaused]] *[[AuxTimerTimeElapsed]] *[[AuxTimerTimeLeft]]
[[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Aux Functions]] [[Category:Auxiliary-Timer Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.55 |summary = Returns the time left for an [[Auxiliary Timer]] before it stops on its own. If the timer doesn't exist or was fully stopped before this is called, returns -1.
If no owner form for the AuxTimer is specified, ''PlayerRef'' will be used.
|name = AuxTimerTimeLeft |returnType = timeLeft:float |referenceType = ownerRefr |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = timerName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = owner |Type = baseForm |Optional = y }} |example = RaulRef.AuxTimerTimeLeft "someTimerName" Returns the amount of time left for the private-permanent AuxTimer belonging to RaulRef, assuming such a timer is still running. }} ==See Also== *[[AuxTimerStart]] *[[AuxTimerStop]] *[[AuxTimerPaused]] *[[AuxTimerTimeElapsed]] *[[AuxTimerTimeToCountdown]]
[[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Aux Functions]] [[Category:Auxiliary-Timer Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.55 |name = SetOnAuxTimerStopHandler |alias = SetOnTimerStopHandler |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''[[UDF|UDF Script]]''' as a handler that will be invoked when a specified [[Auxiliary Timer]] owned by a specified form is stopped.
'''Handlers will be removed when the game is loaded or sent to the Main Menu.''' *Consider using [[GetGameLoaded]] to (re)set the event handler.
If no owner form is specified, will assume ''PlayerRef''.
When invoked, the script is passed with two arguments: the AuxTimer string name, and the owner form. |referenceType = ownerRefr |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = timerName |Type = string_var }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = ownerForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} }} ==See Also== *[[SetOnAuxTimerStartHandler]] *[[SetOnAuxTimerUpdateHandler]] *[[AuxTimerStart]] *[[AuxTimerStop]] *[[AuxTimerPaused]] *[[AuxTimerTimeElapsed]] *[[AuxTimerTimeToCountdown]] *[[AuxTimerTimeLeft]]
[[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Auxiliary-Timer Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Event Handlers]] [[Category:Event Handlers (ShowOff NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.55 |name = SetOnAuxTimerStartHandler |alias = SetOnTimerStartHandler |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''[[UDF|UDF Script]]''' as a handler that will be invoked when a specified [[Auxiliary Timer]] owned by a specified form is (re)started.
'''Handlers will be removed when the game is loaded or sent to the Main Menu.''' *Consider using [[GetGameLoaded]] to (re)set the event handler.
If no form is specified, will assume ''PlayerRef''.
When invoked, the script is passed with two arguments: the AuxTimer string name, and the owner form. |referenceType = ownerRefr |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = timerName |Type = string_var }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = ownerForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} }} ==See Also== *[[SetOnAuxTimerStopHandler]] *[[SetOnAuxTimerUpdateHandler]] *[[AuxTimerStart]] *[[AuxTimerStop]] *[[AuxTimerPaused]] *[[AuxTimerTimeElapsed]] *[[AuxTimerTimeToCountdown]] *[[AuxTimerTimeLeft]]
[[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Auxiliary-Timer Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Event Handlers]] [[Category:Event Handlers (ShowOff NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.55 |name = ClearShowoffSavedData |summary = Deletes all data of the specified types:
'''auxTimers''' - 1: Permanent-Private (to the mod calling this function), or 2: ALL Permanent-Public [[Auxiliary Timer]]s.
(Deleted data will not be saved with the game) |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = unused |Type = 0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = auxTimers |Type = 0/1/2 }} |example = ClearShowoffSavedData 0 1 Will clear the calling mod's saved AuxTimers. }} ==See Also== *[[ClearJIPSavedData]]
[[Category:Functions_(ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Savebaked Functions]] [[Category:Aux Functions]] [[Category:Auxiliary-Timer Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.55 |summary = A debug-oriented function to give the player more ammo for their currently equipped weapon. If a specific type of ammo is equipped, will add that ammo type instead of the weapon's default ammo type. Additionally, this ammo will be used to refill the player's clip as much as possible, with the rest ending up in the backup ammunition count. This is useful to avoid potentially long reload animations. Will not work with equipped weapons that have regenerating ammo. This acts as a shortcut to using [[AddItem]] to recover ammo for your current weapon. |name = RefillPlayerAmmo |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = numAmmoToAdd |Type = int }} |example = 
RefillPlayerAmmo 10
If the player has ammo currently equipped, will add 10 more of that ammo, possibly refilling part of their clips. If they don't but they have a weapon equipped, will add 10 of that weapon's default ammo. }} [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Debugging]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.60 |summary = Sets the name of the [[Ammo]] [[BaseForm]]. If a [[reference]] is passed, its BaseForm will be modified. The ''nameTypeToChange'' arg allows specifying which type of name to change for the ammo: 
FullName = 0,
ShortName = 1,
AbbreviationName = 2
|name = SetAmmoName |referenceType = ammoRefr |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = newName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = nameTypeToChange |Type = int |Optional = optional |Value = 0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = form |Type = baseform |Optional = optional }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetAmmoName]] *[[SetName]] / [[GetName]] *[[LNSetName]] / [[LNGetName]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Name Functions]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.60 |summary = Gets the name of the [[Ammo]] [[BaseForm]]. If a [[reference]] is passed, its BaseForm will be modified. The ''nameTypeToGet'' arg allows specifying which type of name to retrieve from the ammo: 
FullName = 0,
ShortName = 1,
AbbreviationName = 2
|name = GetAmmoName |referenceType = ammoRefr |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = nameTypeToGet |Type = int |Optional = optional |Value = 0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = form |Type = baseform |Optional = optional }} }} ==See Also== *[[SetAmmoName]] *[[SetName]] / [[GetName]] *[[LNSetName]] / [[LNGetName]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Name Functions]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.65 |summary = Returns the damage that will be dealt by the explosion that triggered the [[ShowOff:OnExplosionHit]] event. Unusable outside of that event. |name = GetExplosionHitDamage
If [[SetExplosionHitDamage]] was previously called, will return the newly updated damage-to-deal. |returnType = float |returnVal = damage }} ==See Also== *[[SetExplosionHitDamage]] *[[ShowOff:OnExplosionHit]]
[[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Explosion Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.65 |summary = Sets the damage that will be dealt by the explosion that triggered the [[ShowOff:OnExplosionHit]] event. Unusa..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.65 |summary = Sets the damage that will be dealt by the explosion that triggered the [[ShowOff:OnExplosionHit]] event. Unusable outside of that event. |name = SetExplosionHitDamage |returnType = void |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = newDamage |Type = float }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetExplosionHitDamage]] *[[ShowOff:OnExplosionHit]]
[[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Explosion Functions]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.65 |summary = Returns 1 if the [[calling reference|calling actor reference]] is aiming or zooming in, 0 otherwise.
In most cases, this is equivalent to checking if the aiming control is being pressed via [[IsControlPressed]], though this function will be future-proofed in case a mod adds a toggle mode to aiming instead of requiring the control to be held. |name = IsAiming |returnType = 1/0 |returnVal = isAiming |referenceType = actorRefr }} ==See Also== *[[IsAimingOrBlocking]]
[[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.65 |summary = Returns 1 if the [[calling reference|calling actor reference]] is aiming, zooming in, or holding a block. Returns 0 otherwise.
Equivalent to checking [[IsAiming]] plus [[GetAnimAction]] to check for ANIM_ACTION_BLOCK. |name = IsBlockingOrAiming |returnType = 1/0 |referenceType = actorRefr }} ==See Also== *[[IsAiming]]
[[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.65 |name = SetOnAuxTimerUpdateHandler |alias = SetOnTimerUpdateHandler |summary = Sets/removes the specified '''[[UDF|UDF Script]]''' as a handler that will be invoked when a specified [[Auxiliary Timer]] owned by a specified form is updated. This happens every frame that it is running. '''NOTE''': this event will only run if the AuxTimer has the '''Run OnTimerUpdate Event''' flag enabled; see [[AuxTimerStart]]. '''Handlers will be removed when the game is loaded or sent to the Main Menu.''' *Consider using [[GetGameLoaded]] to (re)set the event handler. If no form is specified, will assume ''PlayerRef'' as the AuxTimer owner. When invoked, the script is passed with three arguments: the AuxTimer string name, the owner form, and the time passed since the last update (float). *The time passed will always be 1 for frame-based timers, otherwise it will be a delta in seconds. |referenceType = ownerRefr |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = setORremove |Type = 1/0 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = handlerScript |Type = ref }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = timerName |Type = string }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = ownerForm |Type = ref |Optional = y }} }} ==Example Handler== 
begin Function { string_var sTimerName, ref rOwnerForm, float fTimeDelta }

        print "OnAuxTimerUpdate>> RAN!"
        printvar sTimerName, rOwnerForm
        printvar fTimeDelta 

        ;* ASSUMES rOwnerForm is a reference!!!!
        float fTimeLeft = rOwnerForm.AuxTimerTimeLeft $sTimerName
        float fTimeElapsed = rOwnerForm.AuxTimerTimeElapsed $sTimerName
        printvar fTimeLeft, fTimeElapsed 

end
==See Also== *[[SetOnAuxTimerStartHandler]] / [[SetOnAuxTimerStopHandler]] *[[AuxTimerStart]] *[[AuxTimerStop]] *[[AuxTimerPaused]] *[[AuxTimerTimeElapsed]] *[[AuxTimerTimeToCountdown]] *[[AuxTimerTimeLeft]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Auxiliary-Timer Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Event Handlers]] [[Category:Event Handlers (ShowOff NVSE)]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.65 |summary = Sets a texture path for an item to override the item's icon that will be displayed in the hotkey menu.
If ''newPath'' is an empty string, will reset the override and the item will start using the regular icon again. |name = SetItemHotkeyIconPath |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = item |Type = baseForm }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = newPath |Type = string }} }} ==See Also== *[[GetItemHotkeyIconPath]]
[[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Texture Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.65 |summary = Returns the item's hotkey override texture path set by [[SetItemHotkeyIconPath]], if present. If not, will return the regular path (i.e what [[GetTexturePath]] would return).
If ''bOnlyReturnOverride'' is 1, will return the override texture path set by [[SetItemHotkeyIconPath]], or an empty string if no override was made. |name = GetItemHotkeyIconPath |returnVal = path |returnType = string |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = item |Type = baseForm }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = bOnlyReturnOverride |Type = int |Optional = y |Value = 0 }} }} ==See Also== *[[SetItemHotkeyIconPath]]
[[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Inventory Functions]] [[Category:Texture Functions]] [[Category:File Functions]] [[Category:String Functions]]

revision.comment: Broken
revision.text:
{{Deprecated}} {{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.65 |summary = Returns true if the [[calling reference|calling actor reference]] is jumping, 0 if not.
Unlike with checking if the actor is playing a jumping animation with [[IsAnimPlayingEx]], this function will return 0 immediately when the actor lands, which allows detecting continuous jumps by holding down spacebar.
<u>'''Broken'''. This function returns true even if the player never pressed the jump control, meaning it functions equivalently to [[IsInAir]] despite checking for a different flag.</u> |name = IsJumping |referenceType = actorRefr }}
[[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Movement Functions]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.65 |summary = Toggles if ammo regeneration should be frozen, preventing ammo from being regenerated. |name = FreezeAmmoRegen |returnType = wasAmmoRegenFrozen:0/1 |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = toggle |Type = 1/0 |Optional = y }} |example = FreezeAmmoRegen 1
set bAmmoRegenFrozen to FreezeAmmoRegen }} ==Notes== * By omitting the argument, the function can be used to return whether this mode is enabled, without making any changes.
[[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Time Functions]] [[Category:Weapon Functions]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.80 |summary = A work-in-progress function to try to fix loading-related bugs for [[FreezeTime]].
Currently, all it does is make it so loading new cells in by walking around is not prevented while in [[FreezeTime]]. |name = PatchFreezeTime |conditionFunc = Script }} [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)]] [[Category:Functions (FO3)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.80 |summary = Applies an [https://easings.net/ easing function] to an input value (assumed to be between 0-1), to specify th..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.80 |summary = Applies an [https://easings.net/ easing function] to an input value (assumed to be between 0-1), to specify the rate of change of that input over time, and returns that eased value. The easing function to use is specified via ''easingFuncCode''. Possible values: 
Sine = 0
Quad = 1
Cubic = 2
Quart = 3
Quint = 4
Circ = 5
Expo = 6
Back = 7
Elastic = 8
Bounce = 9
''easingMode'' is used to specify the mode of the easing function. Possible values: 
0 = EaseIn
1 = EaseOut
2 = EaseInOut
Returns -1 if the operation was not successful (example: invalid ''easingFuncName'' string). |name = ApplyEasingAlt |alias = EaseAlt |returnVal = easedValue |returnType = float{0-1} |conditionFunc = Script |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = input |Type = float{0-1} }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = easingFuncCode |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = easingMode |Type = int{0,1,2} }} }} ==See Also== *[[ApplyEasing]] - uses a string to specify the easing function instead (recommended for readability). *[[Lerp]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Math Functions]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.80 |summary = Returns true (1) if the actor is considered invisible to the player in the compass, which happens if they have Invisibility or Chameleon [[Actor Values]] greater than 0 and the player does not have the Improved Detection [[Perk Entry Point]] effect to see through that. Returns false (0) otherwise. |name = IsActorInvisibleToPlayer |returnVal = isInvisible |returnType = 1/0 |referenceType = actorRefr |conditionFunc = Both }} [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:Player Functions]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.80 |summary = Returns 1 (true) if the player is moving the camera around, 0 (false) otherwise.
Accounts for [[DisablePlayerControls]]'s option to disable camera-changing inputs. |name = IsPlayerLookingAround |returnVal = isLookingAround |returnType = 1/0 |conditionFunc = Both }} [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Player Functions]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.80 |summary = Forces the calling actor reference to play the hit stagger reaction animation for the limb they just got hit on.
Only works inside [[OnHit]]-style event handlers, and only if [[GetHitLocation]] is not -1.
It will only work for limb stagger idle anims that check for [[GetForceHitReaction]], which vanilla has only set up for third person. A mod to set them up for first person would be recommended ''(recommendation pending)''.
If ''checkForIgnoreCrippledLimbsAV'' is set to 1, will not play the hit stagger reaction if the actor has the '''Ignore Crippled Limbs''' [[Actor Value]] set to > 0. |name = ForceHitStaggerReaction |alias = ForceHitReaction |returnVal = success |returnType = 0/1 |referenceType = actorReference |conditionFunc = Script |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = checkForIgnoreCrippledLimbsAV |Type = 0/1 |Optional = y |Value = 0 }} }} ==See Also== *[[ForceRecoilAnim]] *[[ForceJamAnim]]
[[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Animation Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.80 |summary = Forces the calling actor reference to play the weapon recoil animation, which typically only works for melee/u..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.80 |summary = Forces the calling actor reference to play the weapon recoil animation, which typically only works for melee/unarmed weapons.
Does nothing if stagger animation is playing. |name = ForceRecoilAnim |returnVal = success |returnType = 0/1 |referenceType = actorReference |conditionFunc = Script }} ==See Also== *[[ForceHitStaggerReaction]] *[[ForceJamAnim]]
[[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Animation Functions]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.80 |summary = Returns 1 (true) if the ''isStuck'' flag is set on the calling [[projectile]] reference, false (0) otherwise...."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.80 |summary = Returns 1 (true) if the ''isStuck'' flag is set on the calling [[projectile]] reference, false (0) otherwise.
Note that this flag is set on some unexpected projectiles, so using some sort of filtering will likely be required, such as checking if the projectile does not have an explosion via [[GetProjectileExplosion]]. |name = GetProjectileRefIsStuck |returnVal = 0/1 |returnType = isStuck |referenceType = projectileReference |conditionFunc = Script }} [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.80 |summary = Returns 1 (true) if the [[calling reference]] is targetable in VATS, 0 (false) otherwise.
Note that this accounts for more than just if the reference was set to be non-targetable via [[SetVATSTargetable]]; it checks if the reference could be targeted in general. For example, if passing a [[GetDisabled|disabled]] reference, this function returns false. |name = GetVATSTargetable |returnVal = isVatsTargetable |returnType = 1/0 |referenceType = reference |conditionFunc = Both }} ==See Also== *[[SetVATSTargetable]]
[[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]] [[Category:VATS Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.80 |summary = Returns 1 (true) if an activation prompt is being shown on-screen, 0 (false) otherwise. |name = GetIsActivati..."
revision.text: {{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.80 |summary = Returns 1 (true) if an activation prompt is being shown on-screen, 0 (false) otherwise. |name = GetIsActivationPromptShown |returnVal = isShown |returnType = 1/0 |conditionFunc = Both }} [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.80 |summary = Unlike the [[GetHitLocation]] condition function, the hit location returned by this condition function is allo..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.80 |summary = Unlike the [[GetHitLocation]] condition function, the hit location returned by this condition function is allowed to linger after the initial hit.
Useful as a condition for the '''Calculate Weapon Damage''' [[Perk Entry Point]], since otherwise a regular [[GetHitLocation]] condition doesn't linger long enough to alter the damage. |name = GetHitLocationLingering |returnVal = hitLocation |returnType = int |referenceType = reference |conditionFunc = Condition }} [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Actor Functions]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.80 |summary = Returns the max VATS engage distance.
Accounts for [[lStewieAl's Tweaks]]' '''bMaxVATSDistanceUsesWeaponRange''' setting. |name = GetVATSMaxEngageDistance |returnVal = engageDist |returnType = Float |conditionFunc = Script }} [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:VATS Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.80 |summary = Spawns a projectile following the calling [[projectile]] reference. It will have the same position and rotat..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.80 |summary = Spawns a projectile following the calling [[projectile]] reference.
It will have the same position and rotation data by default. If ''copyDataFromSourceProjectile'' is specified, it will copy other data from the projectile, such as the projectile's source reference and source weapon. |name = SpawnTracingProjectile |returnVal = tracingProj |returnType = reference |referenceType = projectileRefr |conditionFunc = Script |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = projToSpawn |Type = baseForm }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = ignoreGravity |Type = 1/0 |Optional = y |Value = 1 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = copyDataFromSourceProjectile |Type = 1/0 |Optional = y |Value = 0 }} }} [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.80 |summary = Sets or removes an override determining when to draw projectile tracers.
Possible values for ''chanceOverride'': If -1, remove override (returns to default chance-based behavior. If 0, never draw tracers. If 1, always draw tracers if the base [[Projectile]] has a tracer chance > 0 to start with.
Calling this function with no args or with a value of -2 or lower will produce no changes.
Returns the status of the tracer chance override BEFORE the function was called. |name = SetProjectileTracerChanceOverride |returnVal = chanceOverride |returnType = int |conditionFunc = Script |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = chanceOverride |Type = int |Optional = y |Value = -2 }} }} [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.80 |summary = If ''forceDraw'' is set to 1, forces hitscan projectiles to be rendered, similar to how they are in [[VATS]]...."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.80 |summary = If ''forceDraw'' is set to 1, forces hitscan projectiles to be rendered, similar to how they are in [[VATS]].
If ''forceDraw'' is set to 0, will go back to default vanilla behavior.
If the ''forceDraw'' arg is omitted or set to -1, will do nothing. This is useful for just checking if hitscan projectiles are forced to be drawn.
Returns if hitscan projectiles were being forced to be drawn. |name = SetForceDrawHitscanProjectiles |returnVal = wasForcingProjectilesToDraw |returnType = 1/0 |conditionFunc = Script |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = forceDraw |Type = int |Optional = y |Value = -1 }} }} [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Projectile Functions]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.80 |summary = Draws VATS-like flickering highlight over the calling reference. Unlike [[HighlightAdditionalReference]], this can be called from scripts directly.
Also unlike [[HighlightAdditionalReference]], this function checks if the target is invisible and the player can't detect them via Improved Detection [[Perk Entry Point]], unless ''checkInvisibility'' is set to 0.
There is also an option to prevent setting a flashing ref by setting ''setFlashing'' to 0. This is useful for highlighting multiple references and not having any be flashing more than any other.
This function is '''session-persistent'''.
Multiple actors can be highlighted this way, unlike with [[HighlightBodyPart]].
The last actor to be highlighted will be flashing.. |name = HighlightAdditionalReferenceAlt |returnVal = success |returnType = 1/0 |referenceType = reference |conditionFunc = Script |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = checkInvisibility |Type = 1/0 |Optional = y |Value = 1 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = setFlashing |Type = 1/0 |Optional = y |Value = 1 }} }}
==See Also== *[[HighlightAdditionalReference]]
[[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.25 |summary = If passing 1 to the function, the level up menu can be exited early without requiring all points in the curren..."
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.25 |summary = If passing 1 to the function, the level up menu can be exited early without requiring all points in the current page to be allotted.
Returns 1 for success (level up menu was open), 0 otherwise.
'''NOTE:''' Trying to revert the change by calling this function with ''canExitEarly'' = 0 does not currently work, though this usually isn't desired anyways. |name = SetLevelUpMenuCanExitEarly |returnVal = bSuccess |returnType = 1/0 |conditionFunc = Script |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = canExitEarly |Type = int(1/0) }} }} [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:UI Functions]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.20 |summary = Opens the Level-Up menu to the Skills page, and sets that this is the last page (no visiting perks).
If ''numSkills'' is specified, will override the amount of skills that could be allotted once the menu is open.
If ''showMenuDespiteNoPoints'' is set to 1, which it is by default, it will allow opening the skills menu despite having no skill points to allocate, were that to be the case.
''menuTitleOverride'' allows setting a custom title for the skill menu that is opened. |name = ShowSkillMenu |alias = ShowSkillsMenu |returnVal = bSuccess |returnType = 1/0 |conditionFunc = Script |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = numSkills |Type = int |Optional = y |Value = -1 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = showMenuDespiteNoPoints |Type = int |Optional = y |Value = 1 }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = menuTitleOverride |Type = string |Optional = y |Value = "" }} }} ==See Also== *[[ShowPerkMenu]] *[[ShowLevelUpMenu]]
[[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Player Functions]] [[Category:UI Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Incomplete}} {{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.80 |summary = |name = |returnType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = }} |example = }} ==..."
Created page with "{{Incomplete}} {{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.80 |summary = |name = |returnType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = }} |example = }} ==..."

revision.text: {{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.80 |summary = Places the given object at the spot the crosshair is pointing at. |name = PlaceAtReticleAlt |referenceType = reference |conditionFunc = Script |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = formToPlace |Type = AnyForm }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = count |Type = Integer |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = minDistance |Type = Float |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = maxDistance |Type = Float |Optional = y }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = healthPercent |Type = Float |Optional = y }} }} ==Examples== Player.PlaceAtReticleAlt EuclidTarget 1 0 20000 100 ==See Also== *[[GetReticlePos]] *[[PlaceAtReticle]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Crosshair Functions]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Incomplete}} {{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.74 |summary = |name = |returnType = |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = }} |example = }} ==..."
Add ToANSIChar
revision.text:
{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.83 |summary = Converts a [[Template:DirectX_Scancodes|DirectX Key Scancode]] into its corresponding character string based on the user's active system keyboard layout. If the ''ignoreShift'' arg is true, the function ignores the current state of the Shift key and returns the non-shifted character. |name = ToANSIChar |returnType = string |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Scancode |Type = int }}{{FunctionArgument |Name = ignoreShift |Optional = y |Type = bool}} }} ==Examples== 
let sChar := ToANSIChar 16

; sChar == "Q" (QWERTY keyboard layout)
; sChar == "Й" (JCUKEN Cyrillic keyboard layout)
let sChar := ToANSIChar 2

; sChar == "1" (Shift not pressed)
; sChar == "!" (Shift pressed)
let sChar := ToANSIChar 2 1

; sChar == "1" (Shift not pressed)
; sChar == "1" (Shift pressed)
==See Also== *[[ToString]] [[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:String Functions]]

{{Function |origin = ShowOff |originVersion = 1.83 |summary = Similar to the vanilla function [[GetSpellUsageNum]] - returns the number of times an [[Ingestible]] has been used by an actor.
The function works on ingestibles that have a [[UMON|Usage Monitor Effect]] attached to them, and return value is multiplied by effect magnitude.
However, unlike the vanilla version, which only returns a value while the ingestible's effect is active, this function allows checking the value at any time. It also correctly accounts for any [[UMON|Usage Monitor Effect]] set to decrease with time.
|name = GetSpellUsageNumEx |returnType = float |referenceType = Actor |conditionFunc = Both |arguments = {{FunctionArgument |Name = Ingestible |Type = form }} |example = Player.GetSpellUsageNumEx Psycho // returns the number of times Psycho has been used, multiplied by 30 }}
==See Also== *[[UMON|Usage Monitor Effect]] *[[Addiction Formula]]
[[Category:Functions (ShowOff NVSE)]] [[Category:Game Effect Functions]] [[Category:Condition Functions]]
